items.title: PONS IP launches AI, software and quantum tech group
items.title_zh: PONS IP成立人工智能、软件及量子技术业务组
items.title_en: PONS IP Launches AI, Software and Quantum Tech Group in Spain
items.summary: The Spain-based firm has appointed an industry veteran to lead the group, which it hopes will strengthen its ability to support clients in ‘disruptive technologies’
items.ai_summary_zh: 总部位于西班牙的PONS IP宣布成立专门的人工智能、软件和量子技术业务组。该所任命了一位行业资深人士领导该团队，旨在加强其支持客户应对“颠覆性技术”相关知识产权需求的能力。
items.ai_summary_en: Spain-based firm PONS IP has established a new practice group focused on artificial intelligence, software, and quantum technologies. The firm appointed an industry veteran to lead this initiative. This structural change aims to strengthen the firm's capacity to support clients navigating intellectual property matters related to disruptive technologies. The move reflects a targeted effort to address the specific legal needs emerging within these high-growth technical sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，西班牙后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights a trend among European firms to create specialized units for emerging technologies. For rights holders, it signals increased availability of dedicated expertise in complex fields like quantum computing and AI. Stakeholders should monitor how such specialized groups influence local prosecution practices and client support models in Spain.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PONS IP成立专注于AI、软件及量子技术的新型业务组", "已任命一位行业资深专家担任该新业务组的负责人", "此举旨在提升律所服务颠覆性技术领域客户的能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PONS IP formed a new group for AI, software, and quantum tech.", "An industry veteran was appointed to lead the new practice area."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["律所针对新兴技术领域进行专业化分工的趋势明显", "需关注AI与量子技术在欧洲知识产权保护中的实务难点", "观察该团队后续是否发布相关技术领域的法律指引或案例"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders in disruptive tech sectors may find specialized support structures more aligned with their complex IP portfolios.", "Practice note: Firms are increasingly segmenting services by technology type rather than traditional IP right categories to address sector-specific nuances.", "What to monitor: Observe whether this specialization leads to distinct procedural approaches or published guidance on AI and quantum IP in Spain."]
core_points_en: PONS IP formed a new group for AI, software, and quantum tech.
An industry veteran was appointed to lead the new practice area.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders in disruptive tech sectors may find specialized support structures more aligned with their complex IP portfolios.
Practice note: Firms are increasingly segmenting services by technology type rather than traditional IP right categories to address sector-specific nuances.
What to monitor: Observe whether this specialization leads to distinct procedural approaches or published guidance on AI and quantum IP in Spain.

items.title: [Guest Book Review] Análisis Jurídico de la Denominación de Origen Protegida – El caso del queso manchego
items.title_zh: 书评：受保护原产地名称法律分析——曼切戈奶酪案
items.title_en: [Guest Book Review] Análisis Jurídico de la Denominación de Origen Protegida – El caso del queso manchego
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host this review of the Spanish-language monograph, Análisis Jurídico de la Denominación de Origen Protegida – El caso del queso manchego (Legal Analysis of the Protected Designation of Origin – The case of Manchego cheese) by Anastasiia Kyrylenko. This book review has been prepared by Rebeca Ferrero Guillén, an IP lawyer at Garrigues and PhD
items.ai_summary_zh: IPKat刊登了由Garrigues律所知识产权律师Rebeca Ferrero Guillén撰写的书评。评论对象为Anastasiia Kyrylenko所著的西班牙语专著《受保护原产地名称法律分析——曼切戈奶酪案》。原文仅提供了书评的引言部分，未包含具体的法律分析细节或结论。
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文仅为书评导语，缺乏具体法律观点。需关注该专著对曼切戈奶酪PDO侵权判定及欧盟地理标志保护边界的论述，以了解西班牙在GI执法层面的最新学理动态。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPKat发布了关于曼切戈奶酪PDO法律分析专著的书评", "书评作者为Garrigues律所律师Rebeca Ferrero Guillén", "被评专著作者为Anastasiia Kyrylenko，聚焦西班牙地理标志案例"]

items.title: CRISPR‑Cas9 in Europe: the Current Situation and What Comes Next
items.title_zh: 欧洲CRISPR-Cas9现状与展望
items.title_en: CRISPR-Cas9 in Europe: Current Patent Landscape and Future Outlook
items.summary: The discovery of CRISPR/Cas9, a groundbreaking biotechnology platform enabling precise DNA modification, ignited one of the most complex patent battles in recent memory. Multiple groups filed patents relating to CRISPR in 2012–2013, leading to overlapping claims and years of legal wrangling over patent rights covering the technology. Two camps emerged as the principal rivals:
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅提供标题及摘要片段，指出CRISPR/Cas9技术引发复杂专利战。2012至2013年间多方申请导致权利重叠，形成两大主要竞争阵营。因正文缺失，无法提供具体法律状态或后续走向的详细事实。
items.ai_summary_en: The emergence of CRISPR-Cas9 technology has triggered one of the most complex patent disputes in recent history. Initial filings by multiple groups between 2012 and 2013 resulted in overlapping claims, leading to prolonged legal conflicts over rights. Two primary rival camps have dominated this landscape. This analysis reviews the current status of these European patent battles and outlines potential future developments for stakeholders monitoring this high-stakes biotechnology sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于原文仅为导语片段，缺乏具体案件进展、法院裁决或欧洲专利局审查动态等实质内容。目前仅能确认该技术在欧洲存在长期的权属争议背景，需等待全文发布以获取关于“当前状况”及“未来走向”的具体法律分析或实务建议。
items.ai_insight_en: Given the limited source text, specific legal outcomes or procedural updates are not detailed. However, the historical context of overlapping claims remains critical for rights holders. Stakeholders should recognize that the European patent environment for CRISPR-Cas9 is still shaped by these foundational disputes. Monitoring the resolution of conflicts between the two main rival camps is essential for understanding the stability of patent rights in this domain.

items.title: Nouveaux mécanismes de la mise en état dans les litiges de propriété intellectuelle
items.title_zh: 知识产权诉讼中案件管理的新机制
items.title_en: Nouveaux mécanismes de la mise en état dans les litiges de propriété intellectuelle
items.summary: Les pratiques de la procédure civile dans les litiges de propriété intellectuelle connaissent une mutation profonde. Alors que la Juridiction Unifiée des Brevets (JUB) propose un nouvel arsenal procédural dans les contentieux des brevets, en imposant de nouveaux standards aux praticiens, la pratique processuelle nationale des litiges de propriété intellectuelle est elle aussi en (suite) L’article Nouveaux mécanismes de la mise en état dans les litiges de propriété intellectuelle est apparu en pr
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出知识产权诉讼的民事诉讼实践正经历深刻变革。统一专利法院（UPC）在专利争议中引入了新的程序工具包，为从业者设定了新标准。同时，各国国内知识产权诉讼的程序实践也在发生相应变化。文章标题暗示重点在于“案件管理”（mise en état）阶段的新机制。
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC新程序规则对传统诉讼节奏的影响，以及国内法院是否跟进类似案件管理机制。从业者应适应UPC设定的更高程序合规标准，并监测各国程序实践的协同或差异趋势。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["统一专利法院（UPC）在专利诉讼中推行新程序工具并确立新执业标准", "知识产权领域的民事诉讼实践正在经历结构性的深刻转变", "除UPC外，各国国内知识产权诉讼的程序实践也在同步演变"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC新程序标准可能重塑专利诉讼的案件准备与管理流程", "需观察国内诉讼程序是否与UPC机制出现趋同或保持独立特色", "后续应持续追踪具体案件管理规则在实务中的适用细节与判例"]

items.title: Simmons & Simmons litigator Priya Nagpal has joined Wiggin
items.title_zh: Simmons & Simmons律师Priya Nagpal加入Wiggin
items.title_en: Patent Litigator Priya Nagpal Joins Wiggin from Simmons & Simmons
items.summary: Experienced patent litigation partner Priya Nagpal is moving to UK competitor Wiggin after nine years at Simmons & Simmons. Wiggin is a full-service law firm with offices in London, Brussels and Cheltenham. The firm specialises in media, tech and IP advice and litigation. It concentrates on patent cases in the UK and has no UPC […] The post Simmons & Simmons litigator Priya Nagpal has joined Wiggin appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 拥有九年经验的专利诉讼合伙人Priya Nagpal从Simmons & Simmons离职，加入英国竞争对手Wiggin。Wiggin是一家在伦敦、布鲁塞尔和切尔滕纳姆设有办公室的全服务律所，专注于媒体、科技及知识产权建议与诉讼，重点处理英国专利案件。
items.ai_summary_en: Priya Nagpal, an experienced patent litigation partner, has joined UK firm Wiggin after nine years at Simmons & Simmons. Wiggin operates offices in London, Brussels, and Cheltenham, specializing in media, technology, and intellectual property advice and litigation. The firm focuses its patent practice on UK cases. According to the source, Wiggin currently has no presence or activity in the Unified Patent Court. This lateral move highlights ongoing personnel shifts within the UK patent litigation sector, bringing senior expertise to a firm with a distinct domestic focus rather than a pan-European UPC footprint.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This appointment strengthens Wiggin’s UK patent litigation capabilities while maintaining its current absence from the Unified Patent Court. For rights holders, this distinction is relevant when selecting counsel for national versus unitary patent disputes. The move underscores the competitive landscape for senior talent in London. Parties with existing matters should verify continuity protocols. Observers may note how firms without UPC involvement position themselves against full-service competitors in the post-UPC environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["专利诉讼合伙人Priya Nagpal从Simmons & Simmons加入Wigg…", "Wiggin专注英国专利案件，目前在统一专利法院无业务布局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Partner Priya Nagpal moves to Wiggin after nine years at Simmons & Simmons.", "Wiggin specializes in UK patent cases and currently has no UPC presence."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注Wiggin在英国专利诉讼领域的市场份额变化", "观察新加入合伙人对律所 UPC 业务策略的潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should note Wiggin’s specialized UK focus and current lack of UPC activity when instructing counsel.", "Practice note: Senior lateral hires signal firm capacity changes; verify team continuity for ongoing UK patent litigation matters.", "What to monitor: Future developments in Wiggin’s approach to cross-border IP disputes given its stated domestic concentration."]
core_points_en: Partner Priya Nagpal moves to Wiggin after nine years at Simmons & Simmons.
Wiggin specializes in UK patent cases and currently has no UPC presence.

What to monitor: Future developments in Wiggin’s approach to cross-border IP disputes given its stated domestic concentration.

items.title: Preliminary Objections Before The UPC: A Clear Boundary Between Jurisdiction And Substance
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院初步异议：管辖权与实体问题的明确界限
items.title_en: UPC Preliminary Objections: Strict Boundary Between Jurisdiction and Substance
items.summary: What do the Rules of Procedure of the Unified Patent Court stipulate about raising a preliminary objection and providing a response to such objections? Sofiane Belhadj-Kacem provides the answer in...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析统一专利法院在REEL International诉Fives ECL案中的裁定。法院依据《程序规则》第19.1条，驳回被告基于诉讼利益和德国判决既判力提出的初步异议。法院明确初步异议仅限管辖权、分庭管辖权及语言三类情形，其他实体争议应合并审理以符合程序经济原则。
items.ai_summary_en: In a dispute between REEL International and Fives ECL, the Unified Patent Court rejected a defendant’s preliminary objection based on lack of interest to sue and res judicata from German proceedings. Relying on Rule 19.1 of the Rules of Procedure, the Court clarified that preliminary objections are strictly limited to challenges regarding court jurisdiction, division competence, or language of proceedings. The Court held that issues such as standing and res judicata require substantive examination of facts and evidence, rather than serving as procedural filters. This decision reinforces a clear structural distinction between early jurisdictional checks and full merit-based adjudication within the UPC framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC通过严格界定初步异议范围，防止程序碎片化，强调诉讼经济与快速裁决。当事人需注意管辖权异议与实体抗辩的程序分流，避免试图通过初步异议拖延实体审理。此裁判思路有助于确立UPC高效审理的司法惯例。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling confirms the UPC’s commitment to procedural efficiency by preventing the fragmentation of cases through broad preliminary objections. Practitioners should note that defenses like res judicata will be addressed during merits proceedings, not as threshold jurisdictional bars. This approach reduces delays but requires parties to prepare comprehensive factual arguments early in the litigation process.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC程序规则限定初步异议仅适用于管辖权、分庭管辖权及诉讼语言三类情形", "诉讼利益及既判力抗辩不属初步异议范围，需结合事实证据进行实体审查", "法院拒绝分离审理或中间命令请求，主张单次听证以提升效率并实现一年内结案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 19.1 limits preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language issues.", "Claims regarding interest to sue and res judicata fall outside the scope of preliminary objections.", "Substantive defenses require full factual examination and cannot be resolved via procedural dismissal."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确初步异议的封闭列表性质，排除诉讼利益等实体前置抗辩", "确立合并审理原则，拒绝将既判力等问题从实体审理中剥离", "观察UPC后续案件是否持续贯彻这一效率优先的程序管理逻辑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expect faster initial jurisdictional rulings, but anticipate that substantive defenses like res judicata will proceed to full trial rather than causing early dis…", "Practice note: Do not rely on Rule 19.1 for non-jurisdictional defenses; prepare detailed factual evidence for merits hearings instead of seeking procedural shortcuts.", "What to monitor: Future UPC case law to confirm if this strict interpretation of Rule 19.1 remains consistent across different divisions and fact patterns."]
core_points_en: Rule 19.1 limits preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language issues.
Claims regarding interest to sue and res judicata fall outside the scope of preliminary objections.
Substantive defenses require full factual examination and cannot be resolved via procedural dismissal.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Expect faster initial jurisdictional rulings, but anticipate that substantive defenses like res judicata will proceed to full trial rather than causing early dis…
Practice note: Do not rely on Rule 19.1 for non-jurisdictional defenses; prepare detailed factual evidence for merits hearings instead of seeking procedural shortcuts.
What to monitor: Future UPC case law to confirm if this strict interpretation of Rule 19.1 remains consistent across different divisions and fact patterns.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Looking for a smoking gun in the EPO file wrapper for UPC claim interpretation?
items.title_zh: UPC周报：在EPO审查档案中寻找权利要求解释的关键证据
items.title_en: UPC Weekly - Looking for a smoking gun in the EPO file wrapper for UPC claim interpretation?
items.summary: Matthew Naylor reviews Agathon v. Intercom to see how the UPC uses the EPO file wrapper to help interpret the claims.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析Agathon诉Intercom案，探讨UPC如何利用EPO审查档案解释权利要求。米兰地方法院虽遵循上诉先例，但认定专利权人在EPO审查期间的自认陈述构成不利证据，直接影响侵权判定。案件涉及成型工具对中装置的专利EP 2363263 B1，核心争议在于滚珠轴承在模具闭合时的位置及受力方式。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案表明UPC在特定事实下可能突破审查档案仅作为辅助解释工具的局限，直接将EPO阶段的自认陈述作为侵权判定的不利证据。实务中需重新评估EPO审查意见答复策略对后续UPC诉讼的潜在风险，特别是涉及技术特征明确性陈述时。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院已确认审查档案历史可影响诉讼，通常仅用于证实技术人员理解", "米兰地方法院在Agathon案中，依据专利权人在EPO审查期间的自认陈述作出不利认定", "涉案专利EP 2363263 B1核心争议为模具闭合时两排滚珠轴承同时接触内表面的技术…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO审查阶段的陈述可能在UPC侵权诉讼中转化为对专利权人不利的直接证据", "需关注UPC法院如何界定“自认陈述”与一般技术解释之间的界限", "后续应观察该案是否引发对审查档案证据效力范围的进一步司法澄清"]

items.title: Munich confirms PI in Silimed vs Polytech breast implant dispute
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑法院确认Silimed诉Polytech乳房植入物案临时禁令
items.title_en: Munich confirms PI in Silimed vs Polytech breast implant dispute
items.summary: At the centre of a complex and years-long dispute lies EP 2 581 193. The patent protects a process for manufacturing implants. Since 2017 the Brazilian manufacturer Silimed had fought with competitor Polytech over the ownership of the technology. In December 2024, the Higher Regional Court Frankfurt awarded EP 193 to Silimed. Polytech then filed an appeal […] The post Munich confirms PI in Silimed vs Polytech breast implant dispute appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 争议围绕EP 2 581 193号专利展开，该专利保护植入物制造工艺。自2017年起，巴西制造商Silimed与竞争对手Polytech就技术所有权发生纠纷。2024年12月，法兰克福高等地区法院将EP 193判给Silimed。随后Polytech提起上诉。标题显示慕尼黑法院在此案中确认了临时禁令（PI）。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案呈现多地并行诉讼特征，需关注法兰克福判决与慕尼黑禁令之间的程序互动及最终效力。实务中应留意不同法域法院对同一涉案专利权利归属及侵权行为的认定差异及其对全球策略的影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["争议核心为EP 2 581 193号专利，涉及植入物制造工艺的技术所有权", "年12月法兰克福高等地区法院裁定将该专利授予Silimed", "Polytech针对上述裁决提起上诉，且慕尼黑法院确认了相关临时禁令"]

items.title: Lamborghini reveals the hidden race to protect supercar secrecy
items.title_zh: 兰博基尼揭秘保护超跑机密的设计权争夺战
items.title_en: Lamborghini Legal Head Addresses New Threats to Supercar Design Protection
items.summary: From spy shots and AI-generated images to the EU’s repair clause, Lamborghini’s head of legal is confronting new threats to design protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，兰博基尼法务负责人正应对设计保护面临的新威胁，包括间谍照片、AI生成图像以及欧盟的维修条款（repair clause）。文章主要探讨这些新兴因素对超级跑车外观设计保密性及法律保护构成的挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: The head of legal at Lamborghini has highlighted emerging challenges to design protection in the automotive sector. Key concerns include the proliferation of spy shots and AI-generated images, which threaten the secrecy of new vehicle launches. Additionally, the EU's repair clause is identified as a significant legal factor impacting the scope of design rights for spare parts and visible components.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察：需关注欧盟维修条款在具体司法实践中的适用边界，以及AI生成内容对现有外观设计新颖性判断标准的潜在冲击。后续应监测兰博基尼及相关车企在应对此类非传统侵权手段时的具体合规策略与诉讼动向。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary highlights the intersection of technological disruption and evolving EU design law. For rights holders, understanding how non-traditional disclosures like AI imagery interact with novelty requirements is essential. The mention of the repair clause underscores the need to monitor legislative impacts on component-level protection.

items.title: IP system must adapt to survive, say EUIPO leaders
items.title_zh: EUIPO领导层称IP体系必须适应变化以生存
items.title_en: EUIPO Leaders Warn IP System Must Adapt to AI and Online Enforcement Challenges
items.summary: From AI-generated content to online enforcement disputes, Europe’s top IP officials say the legal framework is being tested as never before.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，欧洲顶级知识产权官员指出，从人工智能生成内容到在线执法纠纷，现有的法律框架正面临前所未有的考验。EUIPO领导层强调IP系统必须适应这些挑战才能生存。由于正文材料仅为摘要片段，未提供具体适应措施或详细政策细节。
items.ai_summary_en: Senior officials at the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) have stated that the existing intellectual property legal framework is facing unprecedented pressure. The challenges stem primarily from the rise of artificial intelligence-generated content and complex disputes regarding online enforcement, necessitating significant adaptation for the system's continued viability.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 鉴于正文信息有限，目前仅能确认欧盟IP高层对AI和在线执法挑战的关注。后续需观察EUIPO是否发布具体的政策更新、指南或立法建议以应对上述挑战，以及这些动态对商标权利人在数字环境下的维权策略可能产生的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary highlights high-level recognition within the EUIPO that current regulations may be insufficient for emerging digital realities. For rights holders and practitioners, this signals potential future shifts in policy or guidance. As specific measures are not detailed in this brief excerpt, stakeholders should track subsequent official communications for concrete regulatory responses.

items.title: UPC ADR: Arbitration, Mediation And Expert Determination
items.title_zh: UPC替代性争议解决：仲裁、调解与专家裁决
items.title_en: UPC ADR Mechanisms: Scope of PMAC Arbitration, Mediation and Expert Determination
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) forms part of the Unified Patent Court (UPC). Its rules of operation are available here. It operates independently, but carries out its tasks in close contact and cooperation with the committees/bodies of the UPC which will have to take decisions in relation to the operation of the Centre.
items.ai_summary_zh: 专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）作为统一专利法院（UPC）的一部分，在斯洛文尼亚卢布尔雅那和葡萄牙里斯本设有席位。其运作独立但与UPC机构紧密合作。PMAC旨在促进涉及UPC管辖权范围内的专利纠纷的替代性争议解决（ADR），包括具有事实、法律或商业联系的“相关纠纷”。当事人可选择单独或与UPC诉讼并行进行ADR程序。
items.ai_summary_en: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC), operating independently within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) framework, facilitates alternative dispute resolution for patent matters. With seats in Ljubljana and Lisbon, PMAC handles proceedings separately or in parallel with UPC litigation. Its jurisdiction extends to disputes involving European patents, unitary patents, and supplementary protection certificates, as well as related IP or commercial disputes sharing a factual, legal, or commercial nexus with UPC-competent cases. This structure allows parties to address broader portfolio issues alongside specific UPC matters.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注PMAC规则对UPC诉讼策略的影响。当纠纷涉及未选择退出的欧洲专利或单元专利时，当事人可考虑利用PMAC进行并行或独立的ADR程序，以可能更高效地解决包含非UPC管辖专利在内的关联争议。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing European portfolios, PMAC offers a structured avenue to resolve complex, multi-jurisdictional IP conflicts efficiently. The ability to handle related non-UPC disputes alongside UPC matters provides strategic flexibility in settlement negotiations. Legal teams should evaluate how integrating PMAC processes might streamline resolution for portfolios containing both opted-out and unitary patents, particularly where commercial relationships extend beyond strict patent validity or infringement issues.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC是UPC组成部分，设在斯洛文尼亚和葡萄牙，运作独立但与UPC机构紧密合作", "ADR程序可单独进行或与UPC诉讼并行，适用于UPC拥有部分管辖权的专利及相关纠纷", "“相关纠纷”涵盖与欧洲专利或补充保护证书有事实、法律或商业联系的其他IP或商业争议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PMAC operates independently but cooperates closely with UPC bodies, offering mediation and arbitration services from its seats in Slovenia and Portugal.", "ADR proceedings may be conducted separately or in parallel with UPC litigation, provided a connection exists to UPC jurisdiction.", "Scope includes 'related disputes' involving other patents or IP rights that share a factual, legal, or commercial nexus with UPC-competent cases."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enables efficient resolution of mixed IP portfolios by addressing UPC-competent and related non-UPC disputes in a single forum.", "Practice note: Parties can initiate PMAC proceedings independently or concurrently with UPC actions, allowing for flexible procedural strategies.", "What to monitor: Developments in PMAC case law and procedural rules to assess effectiveness in resolving cross-border patent and commercial nexus disputes."]
core_points_en: PMAC operates independently but cooperates closely with UPC bodies, offering mediation and arbitration services from its seats in Slovenia and Portugal.
ADR proceedings may be conducted separately or in parallel with UPC litigation, provided a connection exists to UPC jurisdiction.
Scope includes 'related disputes' involving other patents or IP rights that share a factual, legal, or commercial nexus with UPC-competent cases.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enables efficient resolution of mixed IP portfolios by addressing UPC-competent and related non-UPC disputes in a single forum.
Practice note: Parties can initiate PMAC proceedings independently or concurrently with UPC actions, allowing for flexible procedural strategies.
What to monitor: Developments in PMAC case law and procedural rules to assess effectiveness in resolving cross-border patent and commercial nexus disputes.

items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院就选择性分销在线限制发布指引
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
items.summary: Restrictions in a selective distribution model affecting online channels or discounting are not automatically illegal but need to be carefully assessed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers案中裁定，品牌方阻止授权零售商在无品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认，选择性分销中针对在线渠道的限制需结合法律与经济背景评估是否造成“足够损害”，而非自动违法。此举重申了Coty案原则，但强调转售价格维持等核心限制仍受严厉执法。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清了选择性分销中在线限制的合法性边界，强调个案经济分析的重要性。企业应区分合法的品牌定位保护措施与非法的核心限制（如固定转售价格）。尽管判决有利于品牌方维护渠道形象，但需警惕欧盟委员会及成员国机构对硬性价格管控的持续高压执法态势。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英上诉法院裁定Deckers阻止授权商在无品牌折扣网销售不违竞争法，推翻原判", "在线销售限制需结合具体背景评估是否造成足够损害，不 presumptively 违法", "转售价格维持及全面禁止在线销售仍属核心限制，面临欧盟及各国严厉处罚"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["选择性分销在线限制需基于品牌定位、网络完整性或防止搭便车等客观标准", "避免从事转售价格维持或全面禁止在线销售等被认定为“核心”的反竞争行为", "关注欧盟及各成员国竞争执法机构对奢侈品及零售行业价格管控的最新处罚动态"]

items.title: UPC update: PI claim loses bite | ADR open for business
items.title_zh: UPC更新：临时禁令威力减弱，替代性争议解决机制启动
items.title_en: UPC update: PI claim loses bite | ADR open for business
items.summary: Clarification concerning jurisdictional reach and latest stats released by the court were also among the top talking points in recent weeks
items.ai_summary_zh: 近期统一专利法院（UPC）动态显示，其发布的最新统计数据及管辖权澄清成为焦点。文章指出，UPC的临时禁令（PI）申请效力似乎有所减弱，可能影响权利人的策略选择。同时，替代性争议解决（ADR）机制已正式开放运营，为当事人提供了诉讼之外的新选项。这些变化反映了UPC在实践中的演进，对欧洲专利诉讼格局产生深远影响。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利诉讼时，应关注UPC临时禁令效力减弱的趋势，这可能降低被诉方立即停止运营的紧迫性。建议积极利用新开放的ADR机制进行和解谈判，以控制成本。同时，密切关注管辖权澄清带来的程序变化，优化在欧洲的知识产权维权与应诉布局。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC临时禁令申请效力减弱，权利人需重新评估诉讼策略与预期效果", "替代性争议解决机制正式开放，为专利纠纷提供高效、低成本的解决路径", "法院发布最新统计数据并澄清管辖权范围，增强司法透明度与可预测性"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC临时禁令效力减弱趋势，调整应对紧急禁令的策略", "利用新开放的ADR机制寻求和解，降低跨境专利诉讼成本与风险"]

items.title: A Matter of Time: Issues in determining priority dates for trade mark applications made in the wake of Brexit
items.title_zh: 脱欧后英国商标申请优先权日期认定问题解析
items.title_en: A Matter of Time: Issues in determining priority dates for trade mark applications made in the wake of Brexit
items.summary: In Parabolica Limited v Tesla Holding AS, Parabolica Limited appealed the initial decision in the opposition filed by Tesla Holding AS to Parabolica Limited’s UK trade mark filing. The crux of the issue was identifying the relevant filing date of Parabolica Limited’s UK trade mark application to determine if this application was made in bad faith.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案争议焦点为Parabolica在英国脱欧后提交的商标申请，其优先权日期应认定为原欧盟商标的优先日还是英国实际申请日。申请人主张依据《2019年商标（修正等）（欧盟退出）条例》附表2A第25条，应沿用欧盟商标优先日以证明非恶意。被申请人则认为该条款仅适用于相对理由审查。分析指出，相关立法说明未排除绝对理由情形，且旨在保障权利连续性，故倾向于支持沿用欧盟优先权日期，这对判断申请是否构成恶意至关重要。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业需密切关注脱欧过渡期后的商标权利衔接问题。若基于原有欧盟商标在英国重新申请或延伸保护，务必准确主张优先权日期，以避免因时间差被认定为恶意抢注。建议企业在进行跨国商标布局时，保留完整的优先权证明文件，并在异议程序中积极援引相关法规，确保在先权利的法律效力得到承认。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["争议核心在于确定脱欧后英国商标申请的优先权日期以判定恶意", "申请人主张依据法规附表2A第25条沿用欧盟商标的优先权日期", "立法说明未限制该条款仅适用于相对理由，支持延续优先权效力"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["脱欧后英商标申请应准确主张欧盟优先权日期以防恶意指控", "企业需保留完整优先权证明并积极援引法规维护在先权利"]

items.title: UPC judge: ‘Expectations of you as a woman are the challenge’
items.title_zh: UPC法官：女性职业期待是最大挑战
items.title_en: UPC judge: ‘Expectations of you as a woman are the challenge’
items.summary: In the first of a series of interviews with women UPC judges, Ingeborg Simonsson talks to Sarah Speight about her career highs and lows, including defying expectations of women in the profession and why having a clear goal is key.
items.ai_summary_zh: World IP Review发布对统一专利法院（UPC）女法官Sarah Speight的专访。文章探讨了她在知识产权法律职业生涯中的起伏，重点分析了女性在专业领域面临的性别刻板印象与挑战。Speight强调，克服外界对女性角色的固有期待需要坚定的职业目标与清晰的规划。该系列访谈旨在展示UPC司法团队中女性领导者的视角，揭示行业多样性现状及个人在高压司法环境下的成长路径。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注UPC司法团队的多元化背景，理解其潜在的文化视角。虽然本案非直接裁判，但反映了欧洲IP界对公平与包容的重视。建议中国企业在参与UPC诉讼或招聘时，注重合规与多元文化适应，避免因文化差异产生误解，同时可借此提升品牌形象与国际合规能力。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC女法官分享职业生涯高低点及应对性别期待的经验", "强调清晰职业目标对于克服专业领域性别偏见的关键作用", "通过系列访谈展现UPC司法机构内部的女性领导力与多元文化"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC司法团队多元化背景，预判潜在文化视角差异", "加强跨文化合规管理，避免在欧洲IP实践中因文化差异受损"]

items.title: The Creativity Of Design: Do You Need To Be Original To Have Individual Character
items.title_zh: 设计创意：拥有个体特征是否需要原创性？
items.title_en: The Creativity Of Design: Do You Need To Be Original To Have Individual Character
items.summary: The European Court of Justice has delivered a landmark ruling clarifying what constitutes "individual character" in EU design protection, addressing whether creativity and originality are necessary requirements. This decision resolves longstanding uncertainty about how design rights differ from copyright protection and whether selecting from predefined components can still qualify for intellectual property protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院（CJEU）就欧盟外观设计保护中的“个体特征”作出里程碑式裁决，澄清了创造力与原创性是否为必要条件。该决定解决了长期存在的法律不确定性，明确了外观设计权与版权保护的区别，并确认从预定义组件中进行选择仍可能符合知识产权保护要求。文章指出，新颖性和个体特征是欧盟外观设计有效的两大支柱，其中个体特征取决于知情用户产生的整体印象是否与现有设计不同。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需严格区分外观设计注册标准与版权保护门槛。企业应关注“知情用户”视角下的整体印象评估，而非仅关注设计元素的独创性。后续需监测该裁决在具体侵权诉讼中对“预定义组件”组合设计的适用尺度。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁决澄清欧盟外观设计中“个体特征”的定义，明确其不同于版权的“原创性”", "欧盟外观设计有效性需同时满足新颖性与个体特征，后者基于知情用户的整体印象差异", "裁决解决了时尚趋势对个体特征影响的法律疑虑，确认组件选择可获保护"]

items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
items.title_zh: 基于注册的启用：非技术性特征认定
items.title_en: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
items.summary: This page appears to be a security verification checkpoint that temporarily blocks access while validating the incoming request. The system implements protective measures to distinguish legitimate users from automated traffic or potential security threats.
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO上诉委员会在T 1745/23案中裁定，电子加热吸烟系统需经互联网注册才能启用的特征属于非技术性商业约束，不贡献技术特性。尽管该要求改变了设备运行状态，但其本质仍为行政或业务需求。该案源于异议程序，强调了区分技术实现与商业规则在专利创造性评估中的重要性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需严格区分技术解决方案与商业规则。若发明点仅在于通过软件限制实现商业目的（如注册验证），即便涉及硬件交互，也可能被认定为缺乏技术贡献。申请人在撰写时应避免将纯商业逻辑包装为技术特征，以免在异议或无效程序中因缺乏创造性被驳回。

items.title: Ban On The Use Of Meat Names For Plant-based Alternatives
items.title_zh: 欧盟禁止植物基替代品使用肉类名称：商标与合规影响分析
items.title_en: Ban On The Use Of Meat Names For Plant-based Alternatives
items.summary: The debate over plant-based meat naming and labeling continues to evolve as the alternative protein industry faces regulatory scrutiny and marketing challenges. Understanding the legal landscape surrounding product nomenclature is crucial for manufacturers navigating consumer protection laws and trademark considerations.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟机构达成协议，禁止植物基产品使用特定肉类名称（如鸡肉、牛肉），旨在防止消费者被误导。该禁令依据《欧盟商标条例》第7条关于误导性商标的规定。虽然“汉堡”等通用词仍可使用，但含动物部位词的现有品牌可能面临无法使用的风险。企业需利用过渡期调整包装并注册新商标以建立品牌知名度。
items.ai_insight_zh: 此政策动态凸显了标签法规对商标存续的直接冲击。权利人需关注《欧盟商标条例》中“误导性”标准的执法尺度变化。实务上应监测过渡期具体时长及后续司法判例，评估现有品牌组合在欧盟市场的合规风险，提前规划非误导性且具显著性的新标识注册策略。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟议会、理事会及成员国同意禁止植物基替代品使用具体肉类名称以防误导消费者", "禁令针对特定动物部位词汇，但允许使用“汉堡”或“香肠”等描述性通用词汇", "含禁用肉类名称的现有注册商标可能失去在产品上的使用权，迫使企业更换品牌", "企业需利用数年适应期寻找替代名称并评估其可注册性，以重建品牌资产"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 植物基食品生产商及拥有相关肉制品名称商…", "Practice note/实务观察: 重点评估现有商标是否包含被禁的具体动物部位词汇…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注欧盟官方公报发布的最终文本细节、过渡期…"]

items.ai_summary_zh: 文章发布了欧盟《人工智能法案》(Regulation (EU) 2024/1689)与暂定商定的“AI数字综合法案”修正案的整合阅读版本。该修正案旨在解决实施挑战，引入新禁令、扩大AI办公室执法权等，但未改变法案整体结构。目前尚无官方发布的正式整合文本，此版本供参考，最终效力以官方公报为准。
items.ai_insight_zh: 企业需关注修正案对合规义务的具体调整，特别是新增禁令和执法权变化。由于正式立法程序尚未完成，应持续监控欧盟理事会和议会的最终批准进展及官方整合文本的发布。

items.title: Skykick v Sky – what has changed in practice
items.title_zh: SkyKick诉Sky案：实务中发生了哪些变化
items.title_en: Skykick v Sky – what has changed in practice
items.summary: Following the Supreme Court’s judgment in SkyKick v Sky in December 2024, the UK Intellectual Property Office issued updated guidance for...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年12月英国最高法院在SkyKick案中裁定Sky部分商标注册因恶意无效。随后英国知识产权局（UKIPO）于2025年6月发布更新指南，明确若申请覆盖全部45类或第9类所有商品将自动触发恶意异议。申请人应仅列明真实意图使用的商品服务，避免过于宽泛的规范以规避审查风险。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了更严格的恶意审查标准，UKIPO虽未大幅增加异议数量，但明确了自动触发机制。权利人在撰写申请时需平衡保护范围与合规性，避免因缺乏真实使用意图导致注册无效，实务中应注重商业合理性说明。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO对覆盖全部45类或第9类全项的申请自动触发恶意异议", "申请人需确保商品服务列表反映真实使用意图，避免过度宽泛", "建议细化如“计算机软件”等宽泛术语，并审查类别标题的使用"]

items.title: IP lawyer hopes to ease admin agony with free court bundle software
items.title_zh: 律师推免费法院案卷软件以减轻行政负担
items.title_en: IP lawyer hopes to ease admin agony with free court bundle software
items.summary: Tristan Sherliker says he hopes to solve an access to justice issue by making the automated court bundle tool free to use
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权律师Tristan Sherliker推出免费的自动化法院案卷生成工具，旨在解决司法准入难题并减轻法律从业者的行政压力。该工具通过自动化处理繁琐的文件整理工作，提高诉讼准备效率。此举反映了行业对降低诉讼成本、提升程序效率的关注，虽非立法或判例变动，但对欧洲尤其是英国的IP诉讼实务具有实际影响，有助于优化案件管理流程。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉诉时，可关注此类开源或免费工具以降低外部律师依赖及行政成本。建议法务团队评估此类自动化工具在案件材料整理中的适用性，提升内部合规与诉讼响应效率。同时，留意英国及欧盟地区诉讼程序数字化的趋势，提前布局数字化案件管理系统，以应对日益复杂的跨境知识产权纠纷。

items.title: Fake beauty, real risk: The rise of counterfeit cosmetics
items.title_zh: 虚假美妆，真实风险：假冒化妆品兴起
items.title_en: Fake beauty, real risk: The rise of counterfeit cosmetics
items.summary: Cheap, accessible and just a click away - counterfeit cosmetics are flooding the UK market, but the real cost may be far higher than...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出假冒化妆品正涌入英国市场，通过电商平台和社交媒体销售，不仅侵犯商标权，更构成公共健康风险。依据《1994年英国商标法》，使用相同商标属明确侵权，未注册元素可诉诸假冒诉讼。由于造假者利用短期账户逃避打击，传统执法手段滞后。品牌方需采取AI监控、平台下架及海关备案等敏捷策略，以应对声誉损害并保护品牌价值。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧出口美妆企业应警惕供应链被仿冒风险，提前在英国及欧盟海关备案商标。建议建立数字化监测机制，利用平台投诉程序快速下架侵权链接。同时，加强产品防伪技术，以防假货引发的健康纠纷反噬品牌声誉，确保合规经营。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["假冒化妆品借电商社交渠道泛滥，严重侵害商标识别功能及消费者安全", "英国法下假冒行为构成商标侵权或普通法假冒，且可能面临刑事追责", "传统执法滞后，品牌需结合AI监控、平台协作及海关备案进行主动维权"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需提前在英欧海关备案商标，阻断跨境假冒物流链条", "建立AI驱动的数字监测体系，利用平台规则快速响应并处置侵权"]

items.title: IPEC insights: Duadata v Tian Cha Le – damages secured by reference…
items.title_zh: IPEC案例：Duadata诉Tian Cha Le基于假设许可费的损害赔偿
items.title_en: IPEC insights: Duadata v Tian Cha Le – damages secured by reference…
items.summary: The Intellectual Property Enterprise Court (IPEC) considers the quantification of a damages claim based on fees that would have been payable under a…
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权企业法院（IPEC）审理了Duadata诉Tian Cha Le Limited（TCL）假冒案。Duadata指控前加盟商TCL在特许经营终止后，继续使用其菜单设计、饮品配方及价格进行经营。法院已作出缺席判决并颁发禁令。本案焦点在于损害赔偿量化，Duadata主张以假设的特许经营许可费作为损失计算基础，认为因TCL侵权导致其无法在该区域授权新加盟商。被告辩称相似性短暂且已整改。该案例确立了通过参考假设许可协议费用来量化假冒损失的路径。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英经营的中国餐饮企业应严格区分加盟期与解约后的品牌使用界限。一旦特许经营终止，必须立即停止使用原品牌的菜单、装修及配方，避免构成假冒。在面临索赔时，需注意对方可能采用“假设许可费”模式主张高额赔偿，应积极举证自身整改速度及市场混淆程度的局限性，以争取降低赔偿额度。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPEC认定TCL构成假冒，禁止其使用Duadata的菜单设计及商业标识", "损害赔偿基于假设的特许经营许可费，而非实际利润损失或侵权获利", "权利人需证明若无侵权，本可通过授权类似加盟商获得相应许可收入"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["解约后立即清除原品牌视觉元素及配方，规避假冒法律风险", "警惕权利人利用假设许可费模型主张高额损害赔偿"]

items.title: The Commission's Draft High-Risk AI Guidelines under the EU AI Act: A First Read
items.title_zh: 欧盟委员会《人工智能法案》高风险AI指南草案初读
items.title_en: European Commission Publishes Draft Guidelines on High-Risk AI Classification Under Article 6
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年5月19日发布关于《人工智能法案》第6条高风险AI系统分类的指南草案，征求利益相关方意见至6月23日。该草案虽无法律约束力，但明确了通过附件一（产品安全）和附件三（用例）两条路径判定高风险系统的标准，为监管机构评估提供框架。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission published draft Guidelines on classifying high-risk AI systems under Article 6 of the EU AI Act on 19 May 2026. The consultation, open until 23 June 2026, details two classification routes: Annex I (product safety legislation) and Annex III (specific use cases). Although non-binding, these guidelines indicate how national market surveillance authorities will likely assess compliance. The Commission clarifies that only the Court of Justice of the European Union provides authoritative legal interpretation. This document serves as a primary working framework for providers and deployers to determine if their systems fall within the high-risk regime during the consultation period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该指南草案为企业提供了判断AI系统是否落入高风险监管 regime 的关键操作框架。需密切关注最终版指南对具体用例的界定，以及欧盟法院后续可能的权威解释，以调整合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: These draft guidelines offer the first detailed operational framework for distinguishing high-risk AI systems from those outside the regime. While not legally binding, they represent the Commission’s current interpretive stance, making them essential for immediate compliance planning. Organizations should use this text to audit existing AI portfolios against the specified Annex I and III criteria. Given the lack of binding force, legal strategies must remain flexible to accommodate potential shifts following stakeholder feedback or future CJEU rulings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟委员会发布高风险AI系统分类指南草案，明确附件一产品安全与附件三用例两条独立判定路径", "指南草案开放反馈至2026年6月23日，正式版本无明确通过时间表，最终解释权归欧盟法院", "尽管指南不具约束力，但其代表了委员会及各国市场监管机构对高风险认定的主要工作思路"]

items.title: Subsonic wins against Sony
items.title_zh: Subsonic胜诉索尼：法高院驳回侵权指控及不正当竞争诉求
items.title_en: Subsonic wins against Sony
items.summary: JUVE Patent, the German publication that has become a leading authority in the legal press and specializes in European patent law, reports in an article on Subsonic’s victory in the patent infringement dispute between Subsonic and Sony. Written by Jean-Christophe Guerrini, Raphaël Fleurance .
items.ai_summary_zh: Subsonic与索尼就PlayStation控制器三项过期专利进行长达十年的诉讼。巴黎初审法院曾因索尼未登记专利转让而驳回起诉。法国最高法院随后裁定，登记后权利人可追溯主张转让前后的损害赔偿。案件发回重审后，上诉法院认定索尼具备诉讼资格且专利有效，但索尼未能证明侵权成立，其不正当竞争诉求亦被驳回。此案凸显了程序合规在专利维权中的关键作用。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利诉讼时，需高度重视权利链条的完整性与登记时效。即便实体上未侵权，程序上的主体资格瑕疵也可能导致败诉风险。建议企业建立完善的IP权属管理档案，确保转让、许可等变动及时在各国专利局登记，以规避因程序问题引发的法律纠纷及赔偿风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法高院裁定专利转让登记后，权利人可追溯主张转让前后的侵权损害赔偿", "巴黎上诉法院重审认定索尼具备诉讼主体资格，涉案专利有效，但未构成侵权", "索尼的不正当竞争诉求被驳回，该案展示了程序瑕疵对专利维权的致命影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视专利权属转移的行政登记程序，确保诉讼主体资格无瑕疵", "完善在欧知识产权档案管理，防范因程序违规导致的维权或应诉失败"]

items.title: Concurrence déloyale et compétence juridictionnelle : les demandes reconventionnelles ne font pas la loi !
items.title_zh: 不正当竞争与司法管辖：反诉不具决定性效力
items.title_en: Concurrence déloyale et compétence juridictionnelle : les demandes reconventionnelles ne font pas la loi !
items.summary: Par Me Olivier HUBERT, Avocat Docteur en droit  Propriété intellectuelle; Concurrence déloyale; Numérique; Procédure;  Contrat. Cabinet Olivier HUBERT En matière de vin comme en procédure, le choix du terrain est souvent déterminant. Il est bien établi que, lorsque les faits peuvent relever à la fois de la contrefaçon et de la concurrence déloyale ou parasitaire, […] L’article Concurrence déloyale et compétence juridictionnelle : les demandes reconventionnelles ne font pas la loi ! est apparu en
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文由律师Olivier Hubert撰写，探讨在法国法律实践中，当案件事实同时涉及侵权（如假冒）与不正当竞争或寄生竞争时，法院管辖权的确定问题。文章指出，尽管当事人可能通过提出反诉来试图改变管辖法院，但根据既定判例，反诉本身并不能自动确立管辖权。法院需依据主要诉求的性质及相关法律规定独立判断管辖权，确保程序正义与法律适用的准确性，避免当事人通过策略性反诉操纵审判地。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业面临混合侵权指控时，切勿依赖反诉策略转移管辖。应深入理解法国及欧盟关于不正当竞争与IP侵权的程序差异，提前评估管辖权风险。建议在应诉初期即聘请当地专业律师，针对管辖权异议进行精准抗辩，防止因程序失误增加诉讼成本或陷入不利司法辖区。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["事实重合时，不正当竞争与侵权案件的管辖权需依法独立判定", "当事人提出的反诉请求不能单方面决定或改变法院的法定管辖权", "律师建议审慎选择诉讼策略，避免因程序错误导致管辖权异议风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕利用反诉改变管辖的策略失效，需依主诉求性质预判法院", "加强中法诉讼程序差异研究，防范不正当竞争指控中的管辖陷阱"]

items.title: Nick Dudziak leaves Sisvel to join independent licensing firm Equiviti
items.title_zh: Nick Dudziak离开Sisvel加入独立许可公司Equiviti

This personnel change is interpreted as a signal of increased confidence in independent patent dealmaking activities. The move highlights ongoing shifts within the European intellectual property licensing sector, where professionals are transitioning between established entities and independent platforms.

items.title: Sécuriser les œuvres créées par les salariés en droit d’auteur : panorama des outils à disposition de l’employeur
items.title_zh: 法国法下保障雇员创作作品著作权的雇主工具全景
items.title_en: Sécuriser les œuvres créées par les salariés en droit d’auteur : panorama des outils à disposition de l’employeur
items.summary: Introduction Les contenus créatifs générés par les salariés (textes, supports marketing, visuels, contenus pédagogiques, designs, photographies, scénarios, etc.) constituent des actifs immatériels pour l’entreprise. En droit français, le contrat de travail n’emporte toutefois pas cession automatique des droits d’auteur au profit de l’employeur : les droits sur la création naissent en
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出在法国法律框架下，劳动合同并不自动导致雇员创作的版权转让给雇主。创意内容如文本、设计等虽为企业无形资产，但权利自创作完成时即归属于作者。因此，企业需采取特定法律手段来确保对这些资产的控制权。文章综述了雇主可用的各种工具，旨在帮助企业通过合同条款或其他方式合法有效地获取或许可使用员工在职期间产生的智力成果，以规避潜在的法律风险并保护商业利益。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需注意法国严格的作者权传统，切勿默认雇佣关系包含版权转让。建议在入职合同或专项协议中明确约定职务作品的财产权转让或独家许可条款，并保留创作过程证据。这有助于避免未来因权属不清导致的商业使用障碍及高额赔偿风险，确保营销与设计资产的合规流转。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国法规定劳动合同不自动转让雇员作品的著作权给雇主", "创意内容作为企业无形资产，其权利自创作起归作者所有", "雇主需利用特定法律工具确保对员工创作成果的合法控制"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中企在法经营须通过书面协议明确职务作品权属，不可依赖默认规则", "完善内部IP管理流程，确保所有创意产出均有合法的授权或转让依据"]

items.title: Patent Law & Practice at a Glance – May 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: GJE五月专利法与实践综述：英欧及PCT动态

items.summary: Read our May 2026 edition of patent law and practice which includes updates from the UK, the EPO and the WIPO/PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系GJE发布的2026年5月专利法与实践简报。内容涵盖英国、欧洲专利局（EPO）及世界知识产权组织（WIPO/PCT）的最新法律与实务更新。正文特别指出英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布了2026-2027年度企业计划，明确了其未来两年的战略重点与运营方向。该资讯为从业者提供了跨法域的专利程序与政策变化概览，有助于把握英国脱欧后及欧洲层面的专利审查与管理趋势。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注UKIPO新企业计划对申请策略的影响，特别是费用与服务变更。同时，应跟踪EPO与PCT的程序更新，优化跨国专利布局效率。建议结合简报中的实务指引，调整在英欧市场的专利申请与维护节奏，以应对潜在的政策波动并降低合规成本。

items.title: [Book Review] Art and Copyright Law: An Interdisciplinary Study on Interpictoriality
items.title_zh: 书评：艺术与版权法中的图像互文性跨学科研究
items.title_en: [Book Review] Art and Copyright Law: An Interdisciplinary Study on Interpictoriality
items.summary: This is a review of the book, Art and Copyright Law: An Interdisciplinary Study on Interpictoriality (Routledge, 2025) authored by Giulia Walter (University of Zurich). The book provides for a much need, however, until very recently [Merpel: I see that someone is hovering around Pelham…], under-studied account of the issue of interpictoriality, i.e., reusing existing works or parts
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文是对苏黎世大学Giulia Walter所著《艺术与版权法：图像互文性跨学科研究》的书评。该书深入探讨了长期被忽视的“图像互文性”问题，即对现有作品或其部分的复用。评论指出，随着Pelham案等近期司法判例的出现，该议题在欧洲版权界日益受到关注。本书填补了学术空白，为理解艺术创作与版权保护之间的张力提供了重要的理论框架和跨学科视角。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国数字内容出海需警惕图像复用风险。建议企业建立严格的素材溯源机制，避免未经授权使用受版权保护的视觉元素。同时，关注欧盟及瑞士在图像互文性上的司法倾向，提前评估二次创作的法律合规性，以降低侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["聚焦图像互文性，即复用现有作品的法律与伦理边界", "结合Pelham案等近期判例，分析欧洲版权司法新动态", "提供跨学科视角，填补艺术创作与版权保护研究的空白"]

items.title: Merz v. Viatris on Appeal: Urgency Is Still Everything — But Not Every Signal Is Urgent
items.title_zh: Merz诉Viatris上诉案：紧急性仍是关键但非所有信号均紧急
items.title_en: Merz v. Viatris on Appeal: Urgency Is Still Everything — But Not Every Signal Is Urgent
items.summary: This week, I have the pleasure of speaking in Amsterdam at C5’s 19th Annual Forum on Pharma & Biotech Patent Litigation in Europe, in a panel devoted to running and defending preliminary injunctions at the UPC and beyond. The programme expressly puts urgency, proportionality, timing, knowledge and delay at the centre of the discussion. Merz v. Viatris will inevitably be part of that conversation.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院推翻巴黎地方法院拒绝颁发临时禁令的决定，裁定Viatris在法国停止销售仿制药至SPC到期。法院认定Merz未不合理延迟，强调紧急性需被保留而非仅主张。该案涉及fampridine专利及SPC，是UPC处理原研药与仿制药纠纷中SPC asserted的重要案例，明确了收购知识产权后的知情义务与紧急性判断标准。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案细化了UPC框架下临时禁令“紧急性”的审查标准，特别是针对知识产权受让人的知情义务与行动时限。实务中需密切关注法院如何平衡市场准入时间与维权反应速度，以及SPC有效期届满前的最后阶段诉讼策略对禁令 granted 的影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院授予针对Viatris的临时禁令，禁止其在法国销售相关仿制药直至2026…", "法院逆转一审关于不合理延迟的认定，明确紧急性必须被实质保留，且收购方知情时间点影响紧急…", "案件涉及EP专利及法国SPC，是UPC早期处理原研/仿制纠纷中SPC执行的关键判例，具…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院对“紧急性”要件的解释比一审更精确，强调实质保留而非形式主张", "知识产权收购方的尽职调查与获知侵权风险的时间点，将成为评估是否构成不合理延迟的关键事实", "需持续观察UPC在SPC有效期临近届满时的临时救济倾向，及其对仿制药上市计划的具体约束力"]

items.title: Copyright royalty rates excessive of economic value may be abusive of dominant position, rules CJEU against Czech CMO
items.title_zh: CJEU：忽视入住率的版权费率过高或构成滥用市场支配地位
items.title_en: CJEU: Copyright Royalties Ignoring Occupancy May Abuse Dominant Position
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院裁定捷克集体管理组织OSA向酒店收取固定版权费未考虑房间入住率，可能违反TFEU第102条构成滥用支配地位。法院强调版权许可费应与被许可人实际获得的经济价值成比例，若行政上可行，忽略入住率等实际使用情况的定价属不公平高价。该裁决要求版权收费机制必须反映真实经济价值，对欧盟内版权许可实践具有深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU ruled that a Czech collective management organisation (OSA) may have abused its dominant position by charging hotel operators flat-rate copyright fees without accounting for room occupancy or seasonality. The Court held that under Article 102 TFEU, royalty rates must reflect the actual economic value derived from the use of protected works. By ignoring occupancy levels, OSA’s pricing disconnected fees from the commercial benefit realistically obtained by licensees. This decision clarifies that while installing reception equipment constitutes communication to the public, competition law requires licensing schemes to adequately account for actual usage metrics when determining fair remuneration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营酒店、餐饮及娱乐设施的中国企业应重新审查现有版权许可协议。若支付固定费用且未挂钩实际使用情况，可依据此判例主张费率不公并寻求调整。建议评估历史缴费记录，必要时通过谈判或法律途径争取更公平的计费方式，以降低合规成本并规避反垄断风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the intersection between intellectual property rights and EU competition law, specifically regarding the fairness of licensing terms set by entities holding dominant positions. For rights holders and collective management organisations, it signals that administrative convenience cannot override the requirement to link fees to actual economic value. Licensees, particularly in hospitality and entertainment sectors, may leverage this precedent to challenge rigid pricing models that do not correlate with real-world usage patterns, potentially leading to more nuanced and variable licensing structures across the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定忽略入住率的固定版权费率可能构成滥用市场支配地位", "版权许可费必须与被许可人实际获得的经济价值及作品使用情况成比例", "若行政可行，集体管理组织需采用基于实际使用（如入住率）的计费方法"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules that failing to account for hotel room occupancy in copyright fee calculations may constitute an abuse of dominance under Article 102 TFEU.", "Royalty schemes must reflect the actual economic value and commercial benefit derived by licensees from the use of protected copyrighted material.", "Flat-rate fees ignoring seasonal closures or non-operation periods are deemed disproportionate to the size of the relevant public."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在欧中企应审查版权许可费是否与实际使用情况挂钩以规避反垄断风险", "可依据CJEU判例主张固定费率不公，通过谈判或诉讼争取合理计费方式"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Hospitality and entertainment operators should review existing copyright licenses to assess if flat-rate fees ignore actual usage metrics like occupancy.", "Practice note: Collective management organisations must ensure royalty calculation methods adequately reflect the economic value actually received by licensees.", "What to monitor: Future national competition authority decisions applying this CJEU standard to other sectors using collective copyright licensing models."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules that failing to account for hotel room occupancy in copyright fee calculations may constitute an abuse of dominance under Article 102 TFEU.
Royalty schemes must reflect the actual economic value and commercial benefit derived by licensees from the use of protected copyrighted material.
Flat-rate fees ignoring seasonal closures or non-operation periods are deemed disproportionate to the size of the relevant public.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Hospitality and entertainment operators should review existing copyright licenses to assess if flat-rate fees ignore actual usage metrics like occupancy.
Practice note: Collective management organisations must ensure royalty calculation methods adequately reflect the economic value actually received by licensees.
What to monitor: Future national competition authority decisions applying this CJEU standard to other sectors using collective copyright licensing models.

items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
items.title_en: German Bad Faith Trademark Filings: DPMA Warns of Rising Strategic Abuse Risks
items.summary: Discover how the concept of ‘Bösgläubigkeit’ (bad faith) operates in German law and how to avoid filing or being infringed by bad‑faith filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) has highlighted a significant increase in potentially bad-faith trademark applications since mid-2025. These filings often target signs already used by third parties without formal protection, aiming to exert competitive pressure rather than indicate commercial origin. The DPMA attributes this trend to technological developments, such as AI tools facilitating the identification of unregistered signs, and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms that allow swift blocking of third parties. Under the German Trademark Act (MarkenG), applications filed without genuine intent to use for abusive objectives are excluded from registration or subject to invalidation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the increasing scrutiny of applicant intent in German practice, aligning with broader EU trends. Rights holders must recognize that mere registration is no longer an absolute shield against challenges based on bad faith. Proactive portfolio management is essential to mitigate risks associated with strategic filings designed to disrupt market access or extract undue advantages through enforcement mechanisms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击", "德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效", "恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["DPMA reports a surge in bad-faith filings targeting unregistered signs used by third parties for strategic leverage.", "AI tools and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms facilitate rapid identification and blocking of competitors.", "German law excludes registration or allows invalidation of marks filed without genuine intent to use."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险", "遭遇恶意指控时，应积极收集在先使用证据，依法提起无效宣告以维权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early registration remains critical to secure rights, but businesses must also prepare defenses against bad-faith challenges.", "Practice note: Monitor new filings closely to identify potential bad-faith applications targeting existing unregistered signs.", "What to monitor: Watch for DPMA guidance and Federal Court of Justice case law refining the definition of bad faith in digital contexts."]
core_points_en: DPMA reports a surge in bad-faith filings targeting unregistered signs used by third parties for strategic leverage.
AI tools and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms facilitate rapid identification and blocking of competitors.
German law excludes registration or allows invalidation of marks filed without genuine intent to use.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early registration remains critical to secure rights, but businesses must also prepare defenses against bad-faith challenges.
Practice note: Monitor new filings closely to identify potential bad-faith applications targeting existing unregistered signs.
What to monitor: Watch for DPMA guidance and Federal Court of Justice case law refining the definition of bad faith in digital contexts.

items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
items.title_en: Strategic Planning for Successful IP Mediation Amidst New European ADR Frameworks
items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises, individuals and universities
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
items.ai_summary_en: Mediation offers a confidential, cost-effective alternative to litigation for resolving intellectual property disputes, particularly benefiting SMEs and individuals. The World Intellectual Property Organization reported a 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with significant participation from non-corporate entities. In Europe, the landscape is expanding with the EUIPO Mediation Centre extending its services to all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs as of June 2025. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court will inaugurate its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, providing new forums for alternative dispute resolution across jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of institutional mediation frameworks in Europe provides rights holders with structured avenues to resolve cross-border IP conflicts efficiently. For portfolio managers and legal teams, integrating these mechanisms can facilitate commercially focused settlements that courts may not offer. Understanding the procedural nuances of WIPO, EUIPO, and upcoming UPC facilities allows parties to leverage confidentiality and flexibility, potentially preserving business relationships while addressing infringement or licensing disagreements without the adversarial nature of traditional litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高", "EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心", "调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO recorded a 42% rise in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising 59% of involved parties.", "The EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its scope in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.", "The Unified Patent Court will launch its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, enhancing European ADR options."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险", "建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers confidential, commercially oriented resolutions for IP disputes, reducing reliance on public litigation.", "Practice note: Consider incorporating mandatory mediation clauses in commercial agreements to utilize EUIPO and future UPC dispute resolution services.", "What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre scheduled for June 2026."]
core_points_en: WIPO recorded a 42% rise in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising 59% of involved parties.
The EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its scope in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.
The Unified Patent Court will launch its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, enhancing European ADR options.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers confidential, commercially oriented resolutions for IP disputes, reducing reliance on public litigation.
Practice note: Consider incorporating mandatory mediation clauses in commercial agreements to utilize EUIPO and future UPC dispute resolution services.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre scheduled for June 2026.

items.title: Droit d’auteur : les clauses de cession de droits doivent être rédigées avec précision
items.title_zh: 巴黎法院：著作权转让条款须严格符合法定形式要件
items.title_en: Droit d’auteur : les clauses de cession de droits doivent être rédigées avec précision
items.summary: A Paris court ruled that a furniture designer's copyright was infringed when a paint company reproduced his creations on product packaging and marketing materials. The case centered on whether a contractual clause purporting to transfer exploitation rights met the strict formal requirements imposed by French intellectual property law for valid copyright assignments.
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎司法法院2026年4月1日判决，一涂料公司因在包装及营销材料上再现家具设计师作品构成侵权。被告援引合同中“转让所有权利”的概括性条款抗辩，但法院依据《知识产权法典》L.131-3条，认定该条款未明确区分权利种类、范围、用途、地域及期限，不符合严格的形式要求而无效。法院最终判赔设计师38,000欧元，重申著作权转让必须精准界定各项要素。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显法国法对著作权转让形式要件的严苛立场。企业在签署涉及创意作品的许可或转让协议时，需确保条款精确涵盖权利类型、使用方式、地域及期限等法定要素，避免使用笼统表述导致授权无效及侵权风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定概括性转让“所有权利”的条款因未具体界定范围、用途、地域和期限而无效", "依据《知识产权法典》L.131-3条，著作权转让必须在合同中逐项列明且严格限定各要素", "涂料公司因无法证明获得有效授权，被判定侵犯设计师版权并赔偿38,000欧元"]

items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
items.title_zh: CJEU需裁定：前药是否可独立获得SPC保护？
items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on SPC Eligibility for Pro-Drugs of Previously Approved Actives
items.summary: HGF operates a comprehensive network of intellectual property offices spanning major cities across the United Kingdom, Europe, and Ireland.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院（CJEU）受理新案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得欧盟SPC保护。背景涉及武田的LDX（右旋苯丙胺前药），其虽获新药上市许可，但母体药物DEX早已上市。争议焦点在于前药是否被视为“不同”的活性物质，该裁决将澄清行业规则并解决相关诉讼。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has received a new referral (Case C-794/25) concerning whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient qualifies for Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection independently. The case centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug of dexamphetamine (DEX). Although DEX is a long-established generic, LDX was granted marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to its distinct safety and efficacy profile. The CJEU must determine if such derivatives constitute separate active substances under EU law, aiming to resolve ongoing litigation and provide legal certainty for the pharmaceutical sector regarding SPC eligibility criteria.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案核心在于解释《药品指令》中关于活性物质衍生的定义。若CJEU认定前药可独立获SPC，将显著延长创新药的市场独占期；反之则可能限制基于已知分子结构的改良型创新的专利策略。企业需密切关注判决对SPC申请策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights critical uncertainties in defining 'active substance' for SPC purposes when dealing with molecular derivatives. The outcome will significantly impact portfolio strategies for lifecycle management and second-generation medicines. Rights holders must assess how the court balances regulatory definitions of New Active Substances against the strict requirements for SPC eligibility under Regulation (EC) No 469/2009.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU被要求裁定已获批活性成分的前药在欧盟是否可独立获得SPC保护", "案件背景涉及武田的LDX及其母体药物DEX，争议在于前药是否构成不同的活性物质", "裁决结果旨在为制药行业提供法律确定性，并解决该领域的现有诉讼争议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU referral C-794/25 addresses whether pro-drugs of known actives can secure independent SPC protection in the EU.", "Lisdexamfetamine was authorized as a New Active Substance despite being a metabolic derivative of older drug dexamphetamine.", "The ruling will interpret Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive regarding significant differences in safety or efficacy."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Determines potential for extending market exclusivity via SPCs for improved formulations of legacy drugs.", "Practice note: Evaluate if derivative compounds meet the threshold for differing significantly in safety or efficacy profiles.", "What to monitor: Watch for the CJEU's interpretation of 'different salts, esters, ethers' versus distinct active substances in SPC contexts."]
core_points_en: CJEU referral C-794/25 addresses whether pro-drugs of known actives can secure independent SPC protection in the EU.
Lisdexamfetamine was authorized as a New Active Substance despite being a metabolic derivative of older drug dexamphetamine.
The ruling will interpret Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive regarding significant differences in safety or efficacy.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Determines potential for extending market exclusivity via SPCs for improved formulations of legacy drugs.
Practice note: Evaluate if derivative compounds meet the threshold for differing significantly in safety or efficacy profiles.
What to monitor: Watch for the CJEU's interpretation of 'different salts, esters, ethers' versus distinct active substances in SPC contexts.

items.title: Setback for Align and Carpmaels & Ransford in orthodontic aligners dispute
items.title_zh: Align与律所正畸纠纷受挫
items.title_en: Align and Carpmaels & Ransford Face Setback in European Orthodontic Aligner Patent Dispute
items.summary: Align Technology is owner of EP 4 295 806, relating to a method of designing clear orthodontic aligners used for tooth movement. Specifically, the patent covers methods and aligners that use counter moments and differential moments to control tooth movement more precisely during orthodontic treatment, especially in extraction cases. Align uses the technology in its Invisalign […] The post Setback for Align and Carpmaels & Ransford in orthodontic aligners dispute appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Align Technology拥有的EP 4 295 806号欧洲专利涉及利用反向力矩和差动力矩精确控制牙齿移动的隐形正畸矫治器设计方法。在近期关于该专利的争议中，Align及其代理律所Carpmaels & Ransford遭遇挫折。尽管具体裁判细节未完全披露，但这一 setback 表明其核心技术在欧洲市场的排他性保护面临挑战，可能影响Invisalign相关产品的市场独占地位及后续维权行动的有效性。
items.ai_summary_en: Align Technology, the owner of European patent EP 4 295 806, has encountered a significant setback in a recent legal dispute concerning this intellectual property right. The patent covers methods for designing clear orthodontic aligners that utilize counter moments and differential moments to precisely control tooth movement during treatment, particularly in extraction cases. This technology is central to Align's Invisalign product line. Although specific details of the ruling remain partially undisclosed, the adverse outcome suggests that the exclusivity of Align's core technology in the European market faces substantial challenges. This development may impact the market dominance of Invisalign-related products and affect the effectiveness of future enforcement actions by the rights holder.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国隐形正畸企业应密切关注此案进展，评估EP 4 295 806专利在欧洲的稳定性风险。建议加强FTO分析，规避类似力矩控制技术，或探索替代方案。同时，关注欧洲专利诉讼策略变化，优化海外IP布局以应对潜在竞争压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the stability risks associated with EP 4 295 806 within the European jurisdiction. It is advisable to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses to identify potential infringement risks related to similar moment control technologies. Companies may consider exploring alternative technical solutions to circumvent these specific patent claims. Furthermore, observing shifts in European patent litigation strategies will help optimize overseas intellectual property layouts, allowing firms to better manage competitive pressures and strengthen their defensive IP positions in the orthodontic sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Align拥有EP 4 295 806专利，覆盖隐形正畸矫治器设计方法", "专利核心技术为利用反向力矩和差动力矩精确控制牙齿移动", "Align在相关专利争议中遭遇挫折，维权进程受阻"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Align Technology holds EP 4 295 806, which protects methods for designing clear orthodontic aligners using specific force mechanisms.", "The patented technology employs counter moments and differential moments to achieve precise control over tooth movement during orthodontic procedures.", "Align and its legal representatives faced a procedural or substantive setback in the recent patent dispute, hindering their enforcement efforts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕Align核心专利稳定性风险，加强欧洲市场FTO排查", "优化技术路线，规避力矩控制专利，完善海外IP防御体系"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess stability risks of Align's core patent EP 4 295 806 in Europe and conduct robust freedom-to-operate checks.", "Practice note: Optimize technical routes to avoid infringing on moment control patents and strengthen overseas intellectual property defense systems.", "What to monitor: Track developments in European patent litigation strategies and potential changes in the enforceability of orthodontic aligner patents."]
core_points_en: Align Technology holds EP 4 295 806, which protects methods for designing clear orthodontic aligners using specific force mechanisms.
The patented technology employs counter moments and differential moments to achieve precise control over tooth movement during orthodontic procedures.
Align and its legal representatives faced a procedural or substantive setback in the recent patent dispute, hindering their enforcement efforts.

Practice note: Optimize technical routes to avoid infringing on moment control patents and strengthen overseas intellectual property defense systems.
What to monitor: Track developments in European patent litigation strategies and potential changes in the enforceability of orthodontic aligner patents.

items.title: AI in the patent industry: The risks of AI shadow use
items.title_zh: 专利业AI阴影使用风险：权限限制与合规隐患
items.title_en: AI Shadow Use in European Patent Firms: Risks of Restricted Access and Compliance Gaps
items.summary: In many patent firms, permission to use AI tools is currently restricted to the most senior individuals. Whilst almost every firm seems to have a dedicated “AI task-force” these days to trial new software, this group is usually restricted to partners or senior associates, whilst trainees and more junior associates are denied access to AI entirely. Indeed, many partners freely admit their
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出欧洲专利律所普遍限制初级员工使用AI工具，仅合伙人及资深律师可访问。尽管多数律所设立AI工作组测试软件，但内部权限隔离导致“阴影IT”现象。这种不透明的使用方式增加了客户机密泄露及违反职业保密义务的风险，呼吁建立统一、透明的AI使用政策以保障数据安全。
items.ai_summary_en: European patent firms frequently restrict artificial intelligence tool access to partners and senior associates, excluding trainees and junior staff. Although many firms establish dedicated AI task forces to evaluate new software, this hierarchical permission structure often drives unauthorized usage among excluded employees. This phenomenon, known as 'shadow IT,' creates significant compliance vulnerabilities. The lack of transparent, unified policies increases the risk of inadvertent client confidentiality breaches and violations of professional secrecy obligations. Consequently, there is an urgent need for clear, firm-wide AI governance frameworks. Establishing standardized protocols ensures data security while allowing controlled technological adoption, thereby mitigating legal and ethical risks associated with opaque or unregulated AI utilization within intellectual property practices.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国律所及企业应警惕AI使用的合规边界，避免员工私自使用未授权工具处理敏感案件。建议建立明确的内部AI使用指引，加强数据保密培训，防止因“影子使用”导致的知识产权泄露或违反GDPR及职业伦理，确保技术赋能与风险控制平衡。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize that restricting AI access without providing secure alternatives often drives employees toward unmonitored tools. This behavior undermines data protection efforts and exposes firms to GDPR violations and professional misconduct claims. To balance innovation with risk management, organizations must implement comprehensive internal guidelines. These guidelines should clearly define acceptable use cases for sensitive case data and mandate regular compliance training. By formalizing AI protocols, rights holders can prevent shadow usage scenarios, ensuring that technological empowerment does not compromise intellectual property security or ethical standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利律所普遍限制初级员工使用AI，仅高层享有访问权限", "权限隔离引发“阴影IT”，增加客户机密泄露与合规违规风险", "缺乏统一政策导致隐性使用，需建立透明规范以保障数据安全"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European patent firms commonly restrict AI tool access to senior personnel, excluding junior staff from authorized usage channels.", "Permission isolation fosters shadow IT practices, significantly elevating risks regarding client confidentiality and regulatory compliance.", "The absence of unified policies necessitates transparent governance frameworks to ensure data security and mitigate operational liabilities."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Unauthorized AI usage by staff poses direct threats to client confidentiality and may trigger severe regulatory penalties under GDPR.", "Practice note: Establish clear internal AI usage guidelines prohibiting the processing of sensitive case data via unapproved external platforms.", "What to monitor: Monitor employee adherence to AI policies and conduct regular training to prevent shadow IT incidents and ethical breaches."]
core_points_en: European patent firms commonly restrict AI tool access to senior personnel, excluding junior staff from authorized usage channels.
Permission isolation fosters shadow IT practices, significantly elevating risks regarding client confidentiality and regulatory compliance.
The absence of unified policies necessitates transparent governance frameworks to ensure data security and mitigate operational liabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Unauthorized AI usage by staff poses direct threats to client confidentiality and may trigger severe regulatory penalties under GDPR.
Practice note: Establish clear internal AI usage guidelines prohibiting the processing of sensitive case data via unapproved external platforms.
What to monitor: Monitor employee adherence to AI policies and conduct regular training to prevent shadow IT incidents and ethical breaches.

items.title: Can the name of a fictional character be a Trade Mark? Lessons from Obélix
items.title_zh: 虚构角色名称能否作为商标：奥贝利克斯案启示
items.title_en: EU General Court: Fictional Character Names Can Function as Trademarks
items.summary: The General Court’s judgment in Case T‑24/25 (OBELIX) provides guidance on the treatment of trade marks derived from fictional characters, particularly ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中推翻EUIPO上诉委员会决定，裁定“OBELIX”可受保护。此前EUIPO以声誉证据不足为由驳回无效申请，认为公众仅视其为角色指代。法院指出，关联虚构角色不自动排除商标功能，原评估存在缺陷，需重新审视其作为来源标识的能力及声誉损害风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The EU General Court annulled the EUIPO Board of Appeal’s decision in Case T‑24/25, supporting publisher Les Éditions Albert René. The dispute concerned a Polish individual’s registration of the word mark OBELIX for firearms and explosives. The publisher argued this registration damaged the reputation of its famous comic character. Previous EUIPO bodies held that consumers viewed the term merely as a character reference rather than a trademark source indicator. The General Court ruled the Board’s assessment was flawed, stating that association with a fictional character does not automatically preclude trademark function. The judgment emphasizes the need to comprehensively evaluate reputation and consumer perception, providing significant guidance for protecting trademarks derived from fictional characters.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清了虚构角色名称在商标法下的地位，纠正了“角色名即非商标”的简单推定。权利人需注意，即便角色知名，仍需提供充分证据证明相关公众将其感知为来源标识，而不仅是内容指代。这对动漫、游戏IP衍生品的商标布局与维权举证标准具有参考意义，需关注后续重审对证据要求的细化。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies that fictional character names are not inherently excluded from trademark protection. Rights holders must actively accumulate evidence demonstrating that the public perceives the name as a source identifier, distinct from decorative or descriptive use. Conversely, businesses utilizing elements from well-known intellectual property must exercise caution to avoid infringement risks associated with reputational damage. In opposition or invalidation proceedings, robust proof of acquired distinctiveness and commercial origin perception is critical for successfully defending or challenging such registrations within the European Union market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院认定，标志与虚构角色关联并不自动排除其作为商标指示商业来源的功能", "法院指出EUIPO上诉委员会未充分评估证据，错误地认为公众仅将OBELIX视为角色参考…", "案件发回重审，强调需结合具体商品背景，综合判断角色名称是否具备显著性及声誉保护条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court ruled fictional character names can possess trademark function, overturning the EUIPO finding that they serve only as character references.", "It is essential to prove relevant consumers perceive the sign as an indicator of commercial origin, rather than solely as a description of content.", "The judgment establishes review standards for assessing reputation and likelihood of confusion in invalidation actions involving fictional character marks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["虚构角色名称并非天然缺乏显著性，关键在于证明公众将其视为商业来源标识而非单纯内容描述", "EUIPO在评估声誉侵权时，不能仅因角色知名就推定商标功能缺失，需具体分析消费者认知", "后续需观察重审阶段法院对“角色名作为商标”的证据采信标准，以及对该类IP商业化保护的边…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owners of original characters should strengthen evidence of source identification to prevent findings of mere decorative or descriptive use.", "Practice note: Users of third-party well-known IP elements must proceed cautiously to mitigate invalidation risks triggered by claims of reputational harm.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU applications of these standards regarding consumer perception of fictional character signs in diverse goods categories."]
core_points_en: The court ruled fictional character names can possess trademark function, overturning the EUIPO finding that they serve only as character references.
It is essential to prove relevant consumers perceive the sign as an indicator of commercial origin, rather than solely as a description of content.
The judgment establishes review standards for assessing reputation and likelihood of confusion in invalidation actions involving fictional character marks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owners of original characters should strengthen evidence of source identification to prevent findings of mere decorative or descriptive use.
Practice note: Users of third-party well-known IP elements must proceed cautiously to mitigate invalidation risks triggered by claims of reputational harm.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU applications of these standards regarding consumer perception of fictional character signs in diverse goods categories.

items.title: Senior Expertise Without the Senior Headcount: The Case for a Fractional IP Advisor
items.title_zh: 无需全职高管： fractional IP顾问的价值主张
items.title_en: Senior Expertise Without the Senior Headcount: The Case for a Fractional IP Advisor
items.summary: An experienced external IP advisor can provide senior-level strategic, legal and operational support without adding permanent headcount.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出许多成长型企业面临IP管理碎片化风险，却难以承担全职资深IP专家成本。建议采用“fractional IP顾问”模式，即聘请经验丰富的外部专家提供战略、法律及运营支持。该模式具备可扩展性，能随企业发展阶段灵活调整支持力度，确保IP策略与商业目标一致，避免价值流失并降低合规风险，是中小企业优化IP管理的理想方案。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国中小企业应摒弃仅将IP视为注册事务的观念，积极引入外部资深顾问构建系统化IP战略。通过灵活配置高端智力资源，可在控制成本前提下提升品牌与技术壁垒，有效应对投资尽调及国际扩张中的复杂IP挑战，实现商业价值最大化。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["中小企业常因缺乏专职IP领导导致管理被动且碎片化，易错失保护机会或引发风险", "Fractional IP顾问模式提供资深战略支持，无需增加固定人力成本，具备高度灵活性", "外部顾问需深入理解商业背景，随企业扩张提供从早期指导到尽职调查的全周期服务"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用外部顾问弥补内部IP战略短板，以低成本获取资深专业支持", "建立动态IP管理机制，确保知识产权布局紧密配合企业出海与融资节奏"]

items.title: Burden or boon? Lawyers debate clients’ use of AI
items.title_zh: 律师辩论客户使用AI：负担还是福音？
items.title_en: Lawyers Debate Client AI Use: Enhancing Relationships or Creating Burdens?
items.summary: Attorneys contemplate whether clients using AI for legal guidance is beneficial to attorney-client relationships or more of a nuisance
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨了律师界对于客户在寻求法律指导时使用人工智能（AI）工具的看法。核心争议在于这种趋势是有助于加强律师与客户的关系，还是仅仅成为一种干扰或负担。文章反映了法律科技应用背景下，传统法律服务模式面临的挑战与机遇，以及专业人士对AI辅助决策的伦理、效率及责任归属的深层思考。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the legal profession's divided response to clients increasingly utilizing artificial intelligence tools for preliminary legal guidance. The central debate focuses on whether this technological adoption strengthens attorney-client collaboration by improving efficiency and accessibility, or if it merely introduces friction, complicating service delivery and raising concerns about accuracy. The discussion highlights the broader challenges traditional legal service models face amidst rapid legal tech integration. It reflects a cautious yet adaptive approach within the industry, as practitioners grapple with the ethical implications, operational efficiencies, and shifting liability frameworks associated with AI-assisted decision-making processes in professional legal contexts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营应关注AI法律工具的合规边界，避免数据泄露风险。建议建立内部AI使用指引，明确人机协作责任，确保在利用AI提升效率的同时，维持专业法律意见的准确性与保密性，防范潜在的知识产权与数据合规隐患。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants leveraging AI for legal research must carefully navigate compliance boundaries to mitigate data leakage risks, particularly regarding sensitive cross-border information flows. Establishing robust internal guidelines for AI usage is essential to clearly define responsibilities in human-machine collaborative workflows. This ensures that while operational efficiency is enhanced through automation, the accuracy, confidentiality, and privilege of professional legal opinions are strictly maintained. Proactive governance helps prevent potential intellectual property disputes and ensures adherence to stringent European data protection regulations, balancing innovation with risk management.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["律师界就客户使用AI获取法律建议的影响展开激烈辩论", "焦点在于AI是优化客户关系还是增加服务流程的复杂性", "反映法律行业对AI技术整合的谨慎态度与适应性探索"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The legal community is actively debating the impact of clients using AI tools for obtaining initial legal advice and guidance.", "Key discussions center on whether AI integration optimizes client relationships or unnecessarily increases procedural complexity.", "The sector demonstrates a cautious attitude towards technology adoption while simultaneously exploring necessary adaptations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注AI法律工具合规性，严防敏感数据跨境泄露风险", "建立内部AI使用规范，明确人机协作中的法律责任归属"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor compliance boundaries of AI legal tools to prevent sensitive data leakage and ensure regulatory adherence.", "Practice note: Establish internal AI usage protocols to clarify liability allocation in human-machine collaborative legal tasks.", "What to monitor: Emerging standards for data privacy and intellectual property rights concerning AI-generated legal insights."]
core_points_en: The legal community is actively debating the impact of clients using AI tools for obtaining initial legal advice and guidance.
Key discussions center on whether AI integration optimizes client relationships or unnecessarily increases procedural complexity.
The sector demonstrates a cautious attitude towards technology adoption while simultaneously exploring necessary adaptations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor compliance boundaries of AI legal tools to prevent sensitive data leakage and ensure regulatory adherence.
Practice note: Establish internal AI usage protocols to clarify liability allocation in human-machine collaborative legal tasks.
What to monitor: Emerging standards for data privacy and intellectual property rights concerning AI-generated legal insights.
