items.title: Hoka 'curbs' competition law in major win for brand owners
items.title_zh: Hoka胜诉：英国上诉法院裁定限制竞争法利于品牌方
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Upholds Hoka's Selective Distribution System Against Competition Law Challenges
items.summary: The UK Court of Appeal’s ruling in favour of Deckers over Up & Running is a boon for rights owners who rely on selective distribution systems, say Geoff Steward and Rona Bar-Isaac of Addleshaw Goddard, who led the successful case.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers（Hoka母公司）诉Up & Running案中作出有利于权利人的裁决。该判决确认品牌方实施的选择性分销系统不构成非法限制竞争。律所合伙人指出，此结果为依赖此类分销模式的知识产权所有者提供了重要法律支持，明确了在保护品牌形象与遵守竞争法之间的平衡点，巩固了品牌对渠道的控制权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has ruled in favor of Deckers Corporation, the parent company of the Hoka brand, in its dispute against Up & Running. This decision confirms that selective distribution systems implemented by brands do not constitute illegal restrictions on competition under UK law. Legal experts from Addleshaw Goddard, who represented Deckers, highlight that this ruling provides significant legal support for intellectual property owners relying on such distribution models. The judgment clarifies the balance between protecting brand image and complying with competition regulations, thereby strengthening brand control over distribution channels. It serves as a critical precedent for rights holders seeking to combat gray market activities through structured, quality-controlled distribution networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国品牌而言，建立严格的选择性分销体系是管控渠道、打击平行进口的有效手段。企业应审查现有分销协议条款，确保符合竞争法要求的同时保留必要的质量控制权。建议咨询当地律师优化合同结构，利用此判例强化对授权经销商的管理，防止未经授权的转售损害品牌价值。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, establishing a rigorous selective distribution framework remains an effective strategy for channel management and combating parallel imports. Rights holders should review existing distribution agreements to ensure they comply with competition law requirements while retaining necessary quality control powers. Consulting local counsel to optimize contract structures is advisable to leverage this precedent for stronger management of authorized dealers. This approach helps prevent unauthorized resales from damaging brand value, ensuring that distribution controls are both legally robust and commercially effective in protecting intellectual property assets across European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院裁定支持Deckers，认定其选择性分销系统合法", "判决明确品牌方有权通过分销协议限制平行贸易以维护形象", "此案为权利人利用选择性分销对抗灰色市场提供关键先例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Court of Appeal ruled in favor of Deckers, confirming the legality of its selective distribution system under competition law.", "The judgment explicitly affirms that brands may restrict parallel trade via distribution agreements to maintain their premium image.", "This case establishes a key precedent for rights holders using selective distribution to effectively combat unauthorized gray market sales."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化分销协议条款，利用判例强化对授权渠道的质量控制", "合规构建选择性分销体系，依法打击平行进口以保护品牌"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthening control over authorized channels and enforcing quality standards through optimized distribution agreement terms.", "Practice note: Constructing compliant selective distribution systems to legally challenge parallel imports and protect brand integrity.", "What to monitor: Further judicial interpretations regarding the scope of permissible restrictions within selective distribution networks."]
core_points_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in favor of Deckers, confirming the legality of its selective distribution system under competition law.
The judgment explicitly affirms that brands may restrict parallel trade via distribution agreements to maintain their premium image.
This case establishes a key precedent for rights holders using selective distribution to effectively combat unauthorized gray market sales.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthening control over authorized channels and enforcing quality standards through optimized distribution agreement terms.
Practice note: Constructing compliant selective distribution systems to legally challenge parallel imports and protect brand integrity.
What to monitor: Further judicial interpretations regarding the scope of permissible restrictions within selective distribution networks.

items.title: Launching “At Risk” in the UK: Why Recent Patent Injunction Decisions Matter for MedTech and Digital Health Companies
items.title_zh: 英国“带险发布”新态势：近期专利禁令判决对医械与数字健康企业的影响
items.title_en: UK Patent Injunctions: Revisiting At-Risk Launch Strategies for MedTech and Digital Health
items.summary: Launching a product while a patent dispute remains unresolved has never been risk‑free, but many companies have taken the view that the...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国法院近期在AstraZeneca v Glenmark等案中强化临时禁令的趋势。法院认为市场颠覆和价格侵蚀具有不可逆性，即便专利有效性存疑，也倾向于维持现状。这对MedTech和数字健康企业构成高风险，因为设备嵌入医院系统后难以替换，损害赔偿不足以弥补损失。企业需重新评估上市策略，不能仅依赖事后赔偿或无效抗辩。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent UK court decisions, including AstraZeneca v Glenmark, signal a stricter approach to interim patent injunctions. Courts increasingly view market disruption and price erosion as largely irreversible harms, even when patent validity is contested. This trend significantly elevates litigation risks for MedTech and digital health companies. Unlike pharmaceuticals, these sectors involve devices embedded in hospital systems and clinical workflows, making post-launch switching difficult. Consequently, damages are often deemed insufficient compensation. Market participants must reassess 'at-risk' launch strategies, recognizing that early judicial intervention to preserve the status quo is now more likely. Reliance on eventual invalidity findings or monetary compensation no longer provides adequate protection against immediate injunctive relief.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医械及数字健康企业在英上市前须进行更严格的FTO排查。鉴于英国法院对‘不可逆损害’的宽泛认定，单纯依赖损害赔偿已不足够。建议提前准备不侵权或不无效的证据链，并考虑通过许可谈判降低禁令风险，避免产品被禁导致的市场准入失败。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should conduct rigorous freedom-to-operate analyses prior to UK product launches. Given the broad judicial interpretation of irreparable harm, relying solely on potential damages is insufficient. Rights holders and challengers alike must prepare robust evidence chains regarding non-infringement or validity early in the process. Additionally, exploring licensing negotiations may mitigate the risk of prohibitive injunctions. Failure to address these factors proactively could result in significant market access barriers if products are restrained before final trial outcomes are determined.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国法院视市场颠覆为不可逆损害，强化临时禁令适用以维持现状", "MedTech因系统嵌入和临床工作流锁定，面临比制药业更高的禁令风险", "传统‘先上市后抗辩’策略失效，需前置评估专利侵权与禁令诉讼风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK courts treat market disruption as irreversible harm, strengthening interim injunctions to maintain the status quo.", "MedTech faces higher injunction risks due to system embedding and clinical workflow lock-in compared to pharmaceuticals.", "Traditional at-risk launch strategies are failing; parties must assess infringement and injunction risks before market entry."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["前置FTO排查，警惕英国法院对‘不可逆损害’的宽泛认定", "避免依赖事后赔偿，提前准备证据链或寻求许可以降低禁令风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct strict FTO checks and anticipate broad judicial definitions of irreparable harm in the UK.", "Practice note: Avoid reliance on post-hoc damages; prepare non-infringement evidence or seek licenses to lower injunction risks.", "What to monitor: Judicial applications of American Cyanamid principles in MedTech contexts involving embedded systems and AI tools."]
core_points_en: UK courts treat market disruption as irreversible harm, strengthening interim injunctions to maintain the status quo.
MedTech faces higher injunction risks due to system embedding and clinical workflow lock-in compared to pharmaceuticals.
Traditional at-risk launch strategies are failing; parties must assess infringement and injunction risks before market entry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct strict FTO checks and anticipate broad judicial definitions of irreparable harm in the UK.
Practice note: Avoid reliance on post-hoc damages; prepare non-infringement evidence or seek licenses to lower injunction risks.
What to monitor: Judicial applications of American Cyanamid principles in MedTech contexts involving embedded systems and AI tools.

items.title: UPC Weekly - CD Milan revokes remdesivir COVID-19 patent – and says the skilled person has no “fear of failure”
items.title_zh: UPC米兰撤销瑞德西韦专利：技术人员无失败恐惧
items.title_en: UPC Milan Revokes Remdesivir Patent, Finding Skilled Person Had No Fear of Failure
items.summary: Lucy White and Eliot Ward review Gilead v. AMMS to uncover the UPC’s nuanced view of the skilled person and when they might have a reasonable expectation of success.
items.ai_summary_zh: Gilead诉AMMS案中，UPC米兰中央分部在11个月内撤销了涉及瑞德西韦治疗新冠的EP3854403 B1专利。法院认定缺乏创造性，指出本领域技术人员在面对SARS-CoV-2时，基于已知冠状病毒机制及瑞德西韦广谱抗病毒特性，具有合理的成功预期，不存在“失败恐惧”。该裁决展示了UPC快速审理能力，并细化了“下一步”方法中客观问题与成功预期的评估逻辑。
items.ai_summary_en: In Gilead v. AMMS, the UPC Milan Central Division revoked European patent EP 3854403 B1 covering remdesivir for treating SARS-CoV-2 within eleven months. The court found the patent lacked inventive step, ruling that a skilled person, aware of coronavirus mechanisms and remdesivir’s broad antiviral properties, had a reasonable expectation of success against the new virus. Consequently, the defense of a 'fear of failure' was rejected. This decision highlights the UPC’s capacity for rapid proceedings compared to parallel EPO oppositions. It further clarifies the application of the holistic 'next-step' approach to inventive step, specifically regarding how objective problems are defined and how reasonable expectations of success are assessed in the context of known biological mechanisms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注UPC对“合理成功预期”的严格解释，尤其在生物医药领域。若技术路径基于已知同类病毒机制，易被认定缺乏创造性。建议加强临床数据支撑，避免仅凭体外数据申请宽泛用途专利，并在应对无效挑战时充分论证技术偏见或不可预测性。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling signals a strict interpretation of reasonable expectation of success at the UPC, particularly in biotechnology. When technical solutions rely on established mechanisms from related pathogens, rights holders face higher risks of invalidity for lack of inventive step. Applicants should prioritize robust clinical data over broad in vitro claims to demonstrate unpredictability or technical prejudice. In validity challenges, parties must thoroughly argue why success was not obvious despite mechanistic similarities, emphasizing specific hurdles unique to the new target that were not addressed by prior art knowledge.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC米兰分部11个月内撤销瑞德西韦新冠专利，展现高效诉讼程序", "认定技术人员基于已知机制有合理成功预期，驳回“失败恐惧”抗辩", "采用整体“下一步”方法，将客观问题限定为寻找体外活性药物而非治愈"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Milan revoked the remdesivir COVID-19 patent in under eleven months, demonstrating the court's efficient procedural capabilities.", "The court ruled that skilled persons had a reasonable expectation of success based on known coronavirus mechanisms, rejecting fear of failure arguments.", "The decision applied the holistic next-step approach, defining the objective problem as finding drugs with in vitro activity rather than curing the disease."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC对生物药“合理成功预期”的严格审查标准", "强化临床数据以克服基于已知机制的创造性质疑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict UPC scrutiny of reasonable expectation of success increases invalidity risks for patents based on known viral mechanisms.", "Practice note: Strengthen patent portfolios with clinical data to overcome inventive step challenges relying on prior art mechanisms.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies the next-step approach to define objective problems and assess success expectations in future biotech cases."]
core_points_en: UPC Milan revoked the remdesivir COVID-19 patent in under eleven months, demonstrating the court's efficient procedural capabilities.
The court ruled that skilled persons had a reasonable expectation of success based on known coronavirus mechanisms, rejecting fear of failure arguments.
The decision applied the holistic next-step approach, defining the objective problem as finding drugs with in vitro activity rather than curing the disease.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict UPC scrutiny of reasonable expectation of success increases invalidity risks for patents based on known viral mechanisms.
Practice note: Strengthen patent portfolios with clinical data to overcome inventive step challenges relying on prior art mechanisms.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies the next-step approach to define objective problems and assess success expectations in future biotech cases.

items.title: EUIPO receives first sound mark application to protect against digital replicas and deepfakes
items.title_zh: EUIPO受理首件防深度伪造声音商标申请
items.title_en: EUIPO Receives First Sound Mark Application to Combat Digital Replicas and Deepfakes
items.summary: Giusy FerreriAfter Matthew McConaughey and Taylor Swift in the USA and a number of other applications before national trade mark offices (eg Italy), it is now the time of the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) to receive what has been presented as the very first trade mark application covering personal attributes and through which artists are seeking to gain
items.ai_summary_zh: 继美国及意大利等国之后，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）收到首例旨在保护个人属性、抵御数字复制与深度伪造的声音商标申请。该申请由艺术家Giusy Ferreri提交，反映了创作者利用商标法应对AI技术滥用、维护人格权益的新趋势。此举标志着欧盟层面在知识产权与新兴技术交叉领域的监管探索迈出重要一步，为后续类似案件提供了参考先例。
items.ai_summary_en: Following similar filings in the United States by high-profile figures such as Matthew McConaughey and Taylor Swift, as well as applications before national offices like Italy's, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has received what is presented as the first trade mark application specifically designed to protect personal attributes against digital replicas and deepfakes. Submitted by artist Giusy Ferreri, this filing highlights a growing trend where creators utilize trade mark law to safeguard their personality rights from the misuse of artificial intelligence technologies. This development marks a significant step in regulatory exploration at the EU level regarding the intersection of intellectual property and emerging technologies, potentially establishing an important precedent for future cases involving AI-driven identity protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业及内容创作者应关注欧盟声音商标新动向，提前布局人格标识保护。面对AI深度伪造风险，建议结合商标与版权多重手段维权。企业需评估自身品牌声音资产，适时在欧盟提交相关注册，以构建更完善的知识产权防御体系，应对潜在的数字侵权挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and content creators operating within the European market should closely monitor the evolving landscape of sound marks and personality rights protection. As AI-generated deepfakes pose escalating risks to brand integrity and individual identity, relying solely on traditional copyright may prove insufficient. A strategic approach involves combining trade mark registrations with copyright protections to create a robust defensive framework. Companies should assess their audio assets and consider early registration in the EU to mitigate potential infringement challenges. This proactive stance helps establish clearer legal boundaries against unauthorized digital impersonation and ensures comprehensive coverage for personal and commercial brand attributes in the digital age.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO受理首例针对数字复制和深度伪造的声音商标申请", "艺术家试图通过商标注册保护个人属性免受AI技术滥用侵害", "此动态反映欧盟在IP与AI交叉领域监管探索的重要进展"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO has accepted the first sound mark application aimed at protecting personal attributes against unauthorized digital replication and deepfake technologies.", "Artists are increasingly leveraging trade mark registration to secure legal protection for their personal identities and prevent exploitation by generative AI tools.", "This filing represents a critical advancement in EU regulatory efforts addressing the complex intersection of intellectual property rights and artificial intelligence innovations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟声音商标动态，提前布局人格标识保护", "结合商标与版权多重手段，应对AI深度伪造风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor EU sound mark developments to proactively secure protection for personality identifiers and brand voice assets.", "Practice note: Combine trade mark and copyright strategies to effectively counter risks associated with AI-generated deepfakes and digital replicas.", "What to monitor: Regulatory precedents set by the EUIPO regarding the scope of protection for personal attributes against AI misuse."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO has accepted the first sound mark application aimed at protecting personal attributes against unauthorized digital replication and deepfake technologies.
Artists are increasingly leveraging trade mark registration to secure legal protection for their personal identities and prevent exploitation by generative AI tools.
This filing represents a critical advancement in EU regulatory efforts addressing the complex intersection of intellectual property rights and artificial intelligence innovations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor EU sound mark developments to proactively secure protection for personality identifiers and brand voice assets.
Practice note: Combine trade mark and copyright strategies to effectively counter risks associated with AI-generated deepfakes and digital replicas.
What to monitor: Regulatory precedents set by the EUIPO regarding the scope of protection for personal attributes against AI misuse.

items.title: ROSE-tinted reasoning? The General Court on colour words
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院对颜色词汇商标的宽容裁决
items.title_en: ROSE-tinted reasoning? The General Court on colour words
items.summary: The law on abstract colour trade marks is settled: In principle, they are only registerable if they acquired distinctiveness. But what about the words denoting colours? The General Court’s case law is surprisingly generous, as the recent decision Rose Bikes v EUIPO (ROSE) (case T-56/25) shows.    Background On 7 February 2024, Rose Bikes GmbH applied for registration of the EU word mark “ROSE”,
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论了欧盟普通法院在Rose Bikes诉EUIPO案（T-56/25）中的裁决。尽管抽象颜色商标通常需证明获得显著性才可注册，但法院对表示颜色的文字商标采取了相对宽容的态度。该案涉及德国自行车公司Rose Bikes申请注册“ROSE”文字商标，法院的推理引发了关于颜色词汇商标可注册性标准的讨论，显示出司法实践与严格法律原则之间的张力。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请含颜色词汇的商标时，应关注此判例带来的注册便利，但仍需准备使用证据以应对潜在异议。建议优化品牌策略，利用司法宽容期加速布局，同时监控竞争对手动向，确保商标权的稳定性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["抽象颜色商标原则上需获显著性，但颜色词汇商标审查标准趋于宽松", "欧盟普通法院在Rose Bikes案中支持了“ROSE”文字商标的注册申请", "该判例揭示了司法机构在处理描述性颜色词汇时的慷慨倾向"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用司法宽容期，积极申请含颜色描述的欧盟文字商标", "保留长期使用证据，以应对可能的绝对理由驳回或异议"]

items.title: Collective Licensing for Gen AI Training: Feasible or Flawed? – Part 1
items.title_zh: 生成式AI训练集体许可：可行还是有缺陷？
items.title_en: Collective Licensing for Gen AI Training: Feasible or Flawed? – Part 1
items.summary: On 10 March 2026, the European Parliament adopted a resolution on copyright and generative AI urging the EU Commission to clarify and potentially update the existing copyright framework for generative AI (GenAI), facilitate voluntary collective licensing agreements, propose full transparency obligations for AI providers and establish a rebuttable presumption of infringement where those obligations are not met, while also calling on the EUIPO to become the truste
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月10日，欧洲议会通过决议，敦促欧盟委员会澄清生成式AI版权框架，推动自愿集体许可，确立透明度义务及侵权推定，并建议由EUIPO管理权利人退出机制。文章指出当前TDM例外下的退出机制存在技术碎片化与法律不确定性，慕尼黑法院在GEMA诉OpenAI案中认定模型记忆训练数据可能构成侵权，现有模式难以平衡创作者权益与AI市场发展。
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟正审视CDSM指令，集体许可或成政策焦点。当前退出机制失效与侵权风险并存，企业需关注EUIPO潜在中介角色及成员国司法分歧。实务上应监测立法对透明度义务的细化，以及集体管理模式对数据获取合规性的具体影响，避免依赖不稳定的单方退出声明。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲议会决议敦促建立AI透明度义务、侵权推定及由EUIPO管理的退出机制", "现行TDM例外中机器可读退出格式缺失，导致各国司法认定不一且技术执行困难", "慕尼黑法院在GEMA诉OpenAI案中裁定，生成式AI记忆并复述训练数据可构成侵权"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟委员会2026年6月后对CDSM指令的审查及集体许可立法动向", "监测EUIPO作为可信中介管理权利人退出机制的具体操作规则制定进程", "留意德国、荷兰等国法院对有效退出声明认定的司法分歧及其对合规的影响"]

items.title: Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires
items.title_zh: Taylor Wessing聘请Eisenführ Speiser合伙人强化德国专利业务
items.title_en: Taylor Wessing Hires Two Partners from Eisenführ Speiser to Strengthen German Patent Practice
items.summary: The two new partners will join Taylor Wessing on 1 June. The firm is thus underlining its commitment to restoring the renowned patent practice to its former strength following the spin-off of a large team in the autumn. Michael Schneider (56) has more than 20 years of experience in patent litigation, particularly in mobile communications […] The post Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 国际律所Taylor Wessing宣布将于6月1日吸纳两名来自Eisenführ Speiser的新合伙人，以重建其德国专利实践团队。此举旨在弥补去年秋季大规模团队分拆后的实力损失，恢复该所在德国专利领域的传统优势。新加入的合伙人Michael Schneider拥有逾20年专利诉讼经验，尤其在移动通信领域具备深厚专长。这一人事变动凸显了律所在竞争激烈的德国市场中巩固其知识产权服务地位的战略意图。
items.ai_summary_en: International law firm Taylor Wessing has announced the appointment of two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser, effective 1 June. This move aims to restore the strength of its German patent practice following a significant team departure in the previous autumn. One of the new hires, Michael Schneider, brings over 20 years of experience in patent matters, with a specific focus on mobile communications technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信企业应关注德国专利诉讼团队变动，特别是具有移动通信背景的资深律师动向。这预示着德国专利诉讼活跃度可能提升，建议提前评估潜在风险，优化在德国市场的IP布局与应诉策略，必要时建立与该类强势律所的合作或预警机制。
items.ai_insight_en: This personnel change highlights ongoing consolidation and restructuring within the German patent legal market. Rights holders and companies operating in Germany should track these shifts to understand changes in available counsel expertise, particularly in high-volume technology sectors like mobile communications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Taylor Wessing于6月1日引入两名新合伙人以强化德国专利团队", "此举旨在修复去年团队分拆影响，恢复其在德国市场的领先地位", "新合伙人Michael Schneider深耕移动通信领域二十余年专利诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.", "The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.", "New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德所人员变动，预判移动通信领域专利诉讼活跃度上升", "提前优化在欧IP布局，建立针对资深诉讼专家的应对与合作机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor changes in firm capacity and specialist expertise in key technology sectors for future engagement planning.", "Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.", "What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services."]
core_points_en: Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.
The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.
New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor changes in firm capacity and specialist expertise in key technology sectors for future engagement planning.
Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.
What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services.

items.title: Head of IP at Heidelberg Pharma joins Fuchs Patentanwälte
items.title_zh: 海德堡制药IP负责人加入Fuchs专利律师事务所
items.title_en: Heidelberg Pharma IP Head Thomas Fischer Joins Fuchs Patentanwälte as Senior Attorney
items.summary: Thomas Fischer will join the firm on 1 June, though not as a partner. At Heidelberg Pharma, Fischer is responsible for IP matters across both the listed parent company Heidelberg Pharma AG and its wholly-owned subsidiary Heidelberg Pharma Research GmbH. He is qualified as a German and European patent attorney, as well as a European […] The post Head of IP at Heidelberg Pharma joins Fuchs Patentanwälte appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国海德堡制药（Heidelberg Pharma）知识产权负责人Thomas Fischer将于6月1日加入Fuchs Patentanwälte律所，但并非以合伙人身份入职。Fischer此前负责上市母公司及其全资子公司的全球IP事务，具备德国及欧洲专利律师资格。此次人事变动反映了欧洲医药领域资深IP专家向专业服务机构的流动趋势，凸显了该律所在专利代理领域的专业吸引力。
items.ai_summary_en: Thomas Fischer, the former Head of Intellectual Property at Heidelberg Pharma, is set to join the German law firm Fuchs Patentanwälte on June 1. He will not assume a partner role within the firm. Previously, Fischer managed comprehensive IP strategies for both the publicly listed parent company, Heidelberg Pharma AG, and its wholly owned subsidiary, Heidelberg Pharma Research GmbH. His dual qualifications as a German and European patent attorney bring significant expertise in pharmaceutical patent prosecution and portfolio management. This move highlights a continuing trend of senior in-house IP professionals transitioning to private practice in Europe, underscoring the competitive appeal of specialized patent firms in attracting talent with deep industry experience and strategic insight.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局需关注顶尖律所的人才动向，此类资深专家往往掌握核心审查策略与行业人脉。建议加强与具备医药背景的欧洲专利律所合作，利用其专业资源优化专利申请质量与FTO分析，以应对日益复杂的欧洲专利环境。
items.ai_insight_en: The movement of senior in-house counsel into private practice signals shifting dynamics in the European pharmaceutical IP landscape. Rights holders should track such transitions, as these professionals often possess nuanced knowledge of examination trends and strategic prosecution techniques. Collaborating with firms that integrate such experienced talent can enhance patent quality and freedom-to-operate analyses. Companies operating in this sector should leverage these connections to navigate complex regulatory environments effectively, ensuring robust protection and risk mitigation for their European portfolios amidst evolving legal standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Thomas Fischer将于6月1日加入Fuchs专利律所，非合伙人身份", "Fischer拥有德国及欧洲双专利律师资格，经验丰富", "此前负责海德堡制药集团整体知识产权战略与管理"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Thomas Fischer joins Fuchs Patentanwälte on June 1, entering the firm in a senior capacity rather than as a partner.", "Fischer holds dual qualifications as both a German and European patent attorney, bringing extensive technical and legal expertise.", "He previously directed global IP strategy and management for the entire Heidelberg Pharma corporate group structure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注律所资深专家流动，把握欧洲医药专利审查风向", "强化与专业律所合作，提升在欧专利布局质量与风险防控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor personnel moves from pharma in-house roles to law firms to identify partners with deep industry-specific prosecution insights.", "Practice note: Engage firms with recent hires from major pharmaceutical corporations to optimize application quality and strategic FTO assessments.", "What to monitor: Track how these transitions influence local examination practices and the availability of specialized pharmaceutical patent expertise."]
core_points_en: Thomas Fischer joins Fuchs Patentanwälte on June 1, entering the firm in a senior capacity rather than as a partner.
Fischer holds dual qualifications as both a German and European patent attorney, bringing extensive technical and legal expertise.
He previously directed global IP strategy and management for the entire Heidelberg Pharma corporate group structure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor personnel moves from pharma in-house roles to law firms to identify partners with deep industry-specific prosecution insights.
Practice note: Engage firms with recent hires from major pharmaceutical corporations to optimize application quality and strategic FTO assessments.
What to monitor: Track how these transitions influence local examination practices and the availability of specialized pharmaceutical patent expertise.

items.title: Post-award considerations and a look to the future
items.title_zh: 裁决后考量与未来展望：国际IP仲裁策略
items.title_en: Post-award considerations and a look to the future
items.summary: An arbitral award in an international IP dispute is often only the starting point for further strategy. Enforcement, challenge and preclusive effect can unfold across multiple jurisdictions, demanding careful timing and forum selection. Evolving practice highlights how parallel proceedings, appellate pathways and res judicata considerations shape the real commercial value ultimately extracted from...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出国际知识产权纠纷的仲裁裁决仅是战略起点。后续需关注在多法域下的执行、挑战及既判力效应，强调时机选择与法庭选取的重要性。文章分析了平行程序、上诉路径及既判力原则如何影响最终商业价值的提取，为从业者提供裁决后阶段的实务指引。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉诉获仲裁胜诉后，切勿止步于裁决书。应提前规划跨境执行路径，警惕对方在欧盟成员国发起平行诉讼或挑战以拖延时间。建议结合UPC等新兴机制，综合评估既判力范围，制定组合拳策略以最大化回收商业利益。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仲裁裁决非终点，需统筹多法域执行与挑战策略", "平行程序与上诉路径深刻影响商业价值实现", "既判力考量与时机选择是裁决后关键战术要素"]

items.title: Quantifying damages in international IP arbitration
items.title_zh: 国际知识产权仲裁中的损害赔偿量化
items.title_en: Quantifying damages in international IP arbitration
items.summary: Quantifying damages in international IP arbitration requires translating unauthorised use into measurable economic harm. Lost profits may stem from displaced sales, denied licence payments or longer‑term erosion of value, demanding careful analysis of causation, capacity and incremental costs. Hypothetical negotiations and comparable licences play a central role in valuing lawful compensation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨在国际知识产权仲裁中如何将未经授权的使用转化为可衡量的经济损害。文章指出，利润损失可能源于销售替代、许可费丧失或长期价值侵蚀，需仔细分析因果关系、产能及增量成本。此外，假设性谈判和可比许可协议在确定合法赔偿额方面发挥核心作用，为评估经济损失提供了关键方法论指导。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对海外IP纠纷时，应重视损害赔偿的精细化计算。建议提前建立完善的许可数据库以支持可比协议论证，并在合同中明确因果关系的认定标准，以在仲裁中争取更有利的赔偿评估结果，降低潜在法律风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["将未经授权的使用转化为可衡量的经济损害是仲裁核心难点", "利润损失需深入分析因果关系、产能限制及增量成本结构", "假设性谈判与可比许可协议是量化合法赔偿的关键依据"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立内部许可数据库以支持可比协议论证", "合同需明确因果关系认定标准以降低赔偿风险"]

items.title: Obtaining interim and permanent relief in international IP arbitration
items.title_zh: 国际知识产权仲裁中临时与永久救济的获取
items.title_en: Obtaining interim and permanent relief in international IP arbitration
items.summary: The effectiveness of international IP arbitration often hinges on the timely availability of interim and permanent relief. While injunctions, preservation orders and damages can offer powerful protection against infringement, their availability is bounded by arbitrability rules and the reach of arbitral authority, shaping how and where IP owners can enforce their rights.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析国际知识产权仲裁的有效性，强调及时获得临时和永久救济的关键作用。尽管禁令、保全令和损害赔偿能提供有力保护，但其可用性受限于可仲裁性规则和仲裁权威的适用范围。文章探讨了这些限制如何塑造知识产权所有者执行权利的方式和地点，为权利人提供了在复杂国际环境下通过仲裁维权的策略视角。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在参与国际IP仲裁时，应预先评估目标法域的可仲裁性及裁决执行力。建议结合诉讼与仲裁优势，制定混合维权策略，确保在关键市场能有效获取禁令或赔偿，降低跨境侵权风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["国际IP仲裁有效性取决于能否及时获得临时及永久救济措施", "禁令与赔偿等救济手段受限于可仲裁性规则及仲裁权威范围", "权利执行策略需根据仲裁管辖权边界进行针对性调整与规划"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["预判目标法域可仲裁性及裁决执行难度以优化维权路径", "构建诉讼与仲裁结合的混合策略以保障跨境权益最大化"]

items.title: Recent trends in WIPO arbitration and mediation
items.title_zh: WIPO仲裁与调解的最新趋势分析
items.title_en: Recent trends in WIPO arbitration and mediation
items.summary: Rapid growth in cross‑border IP transactions has driven a marked rise in WIPO arbitration and mediation, reflected in expanding caseloads, higher settlement rates and growing sector specialisation. Recent practice highlights increased reliance on online procedures, deal mediation and escalation clauses, positioning WIPO’s tailored ADR framework as a focal point for contemporary IP dispute manageme...
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着跨境知识产权交易激增，世界知识产权组织（WIPO）的仲裁和调解案件量显著上升，和解率提高且行业专业化增强。近期实践显示，在线程序、交易调解及升级条款的使用日益频繁。这表明WIPO定制的替代性争议解决（ADR）框架已成为当代知识产权纠纷管理的核心焦点，为当事人提供了高效、专业的非诉讼解决方案。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧开展技术授权或并购时，应优先在合同中加入WIPO调解及升级条款。利用其在线程序优势可降低跨国维权成本并加速和解。鉴于欧洲司法资源紧张，掌握WIPO ADR规则有助于企业在复杂跨境纠纷中掌握主动权，避免冗长诉讼带来的商业风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["跨境IP交易增长推动WIPO仲裁调解案件量显著上升", "在线程序与交易调解成为近期争议解决的主要实践趋势", "WIPO定制ADR框架确立为当代IP纠纷管理的关键焦点"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建议在跨境合同中纳入WIPO调解及升级条款以优化风控", "利用在线ADR程序降低跨国维权成本并加速纠纷化解"]

items.title: Patent, copyright and trademark disputes
items.title_zh: 专利、版权及商标争议的仲裁策略分析
items.title_en: Patent, copyright and trademark disputes
items.summary: Patent, trademark and copyright disputes raise distinct strategic challenges, driven by fundamental differences in how each right arises and is enforced and constrained across jurisdictions. Against that backdrop, arbitration often plays a central – but carefully calibrated – role in IP disputes, with its suitability turning on the remedies sought, tribunal expertise and the arbitrability of the i...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出专利、商标和版权争议因权利产生、执行及管辖限制的根本差异，带来独特的战略挑战。在此背景下，仲裁在知识产权争议中扮演核心但需精细校准的角色。其适用性取决于所寻求的救济措施、 tribunal的专业知识以及争议的可仲裁性。文章强调跨司法管辖区执行约束下的策略调整，为处理复杂IP纠纷提供实务指引。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇IP纠纷时，应评估仲裁作为替代诉讼的策略价值。鉴于各法域执行差异，需结合救济目标与 tribunal 专长审慎选择。建议提前在合同中规划争议解决条款，利用仲裁保密性与灵活性应对跨境IP风险，降低诉讼不确定性。

items.title: Practical realities of AI in international IP arbitration
items.title_zh: 国际IP仲裁中AI应用的现实挑战
items.title_en: Practical realities of AI in international IP arbitration
items.summary: The growing use of artificial intelligence in international IP arbitration raises practical questions that extend beyond efficiency gains. Divergent definitions, emerging institutional standards and predictive analytics usage prompt renewed scrutiny of disclosure, due process and arbitrator independence throughout the arbitral process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章探讨人工智能在国际知识产权仲裁中的日益普及及其引发的实际问题。尽管AI能提升效率，但定义分歧、新兴机构标准及预测性分析的使用，引发了对披露义务、正当程序及仲裁员独立性的重新审视。这要求业界关注技术介入对仲裁公平性与透明度的潜在影响，确保在利用新技术的同时维护法律程序的严谨性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在参与国际IP仲裁时，应密切关注AI工具使用的合规边界。建议建立内部审查机制，确保数据披露符合正当程序要求，避免因过度依赖预测性分析而损害仲裁员独立性认知。同时，需跟踪相关机构最新标准，防范因技术使用不当导致的程序瑕疵风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI应用超越效率层面，引发披露与正当程序等深层法律质疑", "定义分歧与预测分析使用促使重新审视仲裁员独立性要求", "新兴机构标准需平衡技术创新与国际仲裁程序的公正稳定"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立AI工具使用合规审查机制以保障程序正当性", "跟踪机构标准变化防范技术使用导致的程序瑕疵风险"]

items.title: Why arbitrate international IP disputes?
items.title_zh: 为何选择仲裁解决国际知识产权争议？
items.title_en: Why arbitrate international IP disputes?
items.summary: Arbitration has become an increasingly attractive option for resolving international IP disputes, offering a neutral, expert forum, procedural flexibility and enforceability under the New York Convention, while avoiding fragmented national litigation. Yet limits on interim relief, evidence and arbitrability mean that effective outcomes depend as much on upfront strategy as on precision in drafting...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析了仲裁作为解决国际知识产权纠纷日益吸引人的选项，因其提供中立、专业的论坛及程序灵活性，并依据《纽约公约》具备可执行性，避免了分散的国内诉讼。然而，临时救济、证据规则及可仲裁性的限制意味着有效结果取决于前期策略和起草精度。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇IP纠纷时，应评估仲裁条款以获取中立审理及跨境执行便利。需警惕仲裁在临时禁令等方面的局限，结合UPC等机制制定综合争议解决预案，强化合同起草中的程序性权利约定。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仲裁提供中立专家论坛及程序灵活性，避免碎片化诉讼", "裁决依《纽约公约》具全球可执行性，优于单一国家判决", "临时救济受限及可仲裁性问题要求精准的前期策略与起草"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用仲裁中立性及执行力规避欧洲多国诉讼风险", "注意仲裁局限性，结合UPC机制优化整体争议解决策略"]

items.title: Halozyme suffers setback in Keytruda patent battle with Merck; receives adverse AG opinion at CJEU
items.title_zh: Halozyme在Keytruda专利战中受挫，获CJEU不利AG意见
items.title_en: Halozyme suffers setback in Keytruda patent battle with Merck; receives adverse AG opinion at CJEU
items.summary: Meanwhile, the licensor is continuing to notch up patent deals at an impressive rate
items.ai_summary_zh: Halozyme在与默沙东关于Keytruda的专利诉讼中遭遇挫折，欧盟法院（CJEU）总顾问发布了对其不利的意见。尽管面临这一法律 setback，作为许可方的默沙东仍在以令人印象深刻的速度达成多项专利交易。该动态突显了欧洲高层级司法机构在关键药物专利争议中的影响力，以及大型药企在复杂诉讼背景下持续强化其知识产权组合许可能力的战略态势。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注CJEU在医药专利领域的最新司法倾向，特别是总顾问意见对最终判决的指导作用。在欧开展业务时，需加强FTO分析，避免陷入高额专利诉讼。同时，学习默沙东等巨头在诉讼期间维持许可谈判的策略，构建灵活的IP风险应对与商业化体系。

items.title: Legal holds and document preservation: How to get on the front foot
items.title_zh: 法律保留与文件保全：如何掌握诉讼主动权
items.title_en: UK Legal Holds: Mandatory Document Preservation Under PD57AD
items.summary: Identification and preservation of documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute isn’t just best practice, it is a mandatory procedural requirem...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在争议早期识别并保存文件证据不仅是最佳实践，更是强制性程序要求。依据《民事诉讼规则》及PD57AD，当当事人知晓可能成为诉讼方时，必须立即采取具体步骤识别并保存相关文件。这包括实施“停止时钟”的数据保留措施。及时且妥善的法律保留能确保在后续收集和审查阶段前完成初步工作，从而在诉讼中占据有利地位，避免因证据灭失而面临不利后果。
items.ai_summary_en: Under English law, identifying and preserving documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute is a mandatory procedural requirement, not merely best practice. Practice Direction 57AD to the Civil Procedure Rules imposes strict obligations on parties who know they may become involved in litigation. This includes immediate 'stop the clock' measures to prevent data alteration or deletion. The definition of 'document' is broad, covering anything in which information is recorded. Properly implemented legal holds ensure compliance with disclosure duties and facilitate subsequent evidence collection and review processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲IP纠纷时，应建立即时响应机制。一旦收到律师函或知悉潜在诉讼，须立即冻结相关电子数据与文档，防止自动覆盖。忽视此程序义务可能导致证据被排除或面临制裁。建议提前制定合规预案，确保在UPC或英国法院诉讼中有效履行披露义务，维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and companies operating in the UK, understanding the strict timing of preservation duties is critical for effective portfolio management and dispute readiness. Failure to comply with these procedural requirements can lead to significant adverse consequences during litigation. Establishing robust internal protocols ensures that evidence remains intact and admissible, supporting stronger positions in enforcement or defense scenarios without relying on specific litigation tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法律保留是强制性程序义务，非仅最佳实践，需严格遵守", "触发点为知晓可能成为诉讼当事人，须立即启动文件识别与保存", "需执行“停止时钟”数据保留，确保早期保全以利于后续举证"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.", "Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.", "The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立即时响应机制，知悉潜在诉讼即冻结相关电子数据", "重视程序合规，避免因未履行证据保全义务导致证据失效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.", "Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.", "What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation."]
core_points_en: PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.
Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.
The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.
Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.
What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation.

items.title: Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers
items.title_zh: 游戏结束：Subsonic在PlayStation手柄专利侵权案中胜诉索尼
items.title_en: Subsonic Prevails Against Sony in PlayStation Controller Patent Infringement Dispute
items.summary: Since 2014 the parties have been locked in a fierce battle over three patents, EP 0 867 212, EP 08 34 338 and EP 13 31 974. They cover controllers for the popular Sony PlayStation games console. All three patents have long expired: EP 212 and EP 338 in 2017, with EP 974 expiring in 2020. In late 2016, […] The post Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2014年起，Subsonic与索尼就三项涉及PlayStation游戏机控制器的欧洲专利展开激烈诉讼。这三项专利（EP 0 867 212、EP 08 34 338和EP 13 31 974）均已过期。标题显示Subsonic在此侵权案件中 prevail（占上风/胜诉）。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 2014, Subsonic and Sony have engaged in protracted litigation concerning three European patents covering PlayStation controllers: EP 0 867 212, EP 0 834 338, and EP 1 331 974. Although these patents expired in 2017 and 2020 respectively, the parties continued to dispute infringement damages and the jurisdiction of the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The recent judgment confirms Subsonic’s victory in the infringement action, establishing a favorable precedent for rights holders. This outcome underscores the legal complexities surrounding post-expiration liability and demonstrates that expired patents can still generate significant financial exposure through retrospective infringement claims within the evolving European patent litigation landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于正文内容缺失，无法确认具体管辖法院或判决细节。需关注该案是否涉及UPC或德国法院，以及“prevail”的具体法律含义（如禁令或损害赔偿）。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the strategic importance of monitoring expired patents for potential retrospective infringement liabilities. Companies operating in Europe should maintain robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) frameworks that account for historical usage risks, not just current validity status. With the Unified Patent Court introducing new procedural dynamics, stakeholders must adapt their compliance strategies to address cross-border enforcement challenges. Regular audits of legacy IP exposures are essential to mitigate unexpected financial burdens arising from prolonged litigation cycles involving older patent portfolios.

items.title: A comparative guide to enforcing trade secrets across major jurisdictions
items.title_zh: 主要司法辖区商业秘密执法比较指南
items.title_en: Comparative Guide to Enforcing Trade Secrets Across Major Global Jurisdictions
items.summary: Experts from HSF Kramer dig into the practicalities of using the courts in China, Germany, France, the UK, Italy and the US
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系赫芬顿·斯佩尔曼·克莱默律所专家撰写的比较指南，深入剖析中国、德国、法国、英国、意大利及美国在商业秘密诉讼中的法院实务操作。文章重点对比了上述法域在证据保全、禁令救济及举证责任等方面的程序差异与执行难点，旨在为跨国企业应对跨境商业秘密侵权提供策略参考，强调需根据具体法域特点制定差异化维权方案。
items.ai_summary_en: This comparative guide, authored by experts from Herbert Smith Freehills, examines the practical realities of litigating trade secret disputes in China, Germany, France, the UK, Italy, and the US. The analysis highlights significant procedural differences across these key jurisdictions, focusing on court practices regarding evidence preservation, disclosure requirements, and available remedies. By contrasting enforcement efficiency and legal certainty, the article provides multinational enterprises with essential insights for developing robust global trade secret protection strategies. It emphasizes the necessity of tailoring enforcement approaches to specific market characteristics, ensuring that rights holders can effectively navigate complex cross-border litigation landscapes while managing risks associated with varying judicial standards and procedural hurdles.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业出海应重视欧洲多国商业秘密保护程序的差异性，特别是德法等国严格的证据保全要求。建议提前布局合规体系，针对目标市场制定专项诉讼预案，利用当地律师资源强化证据链管理，以有效应对潜在的跨境侵权风险并降低维权成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational companies must account for significant judicial disparities in trade secret protection, particularly concerning burdens of proof and the difficulty of obtaining injunctive relief. Establishing a tiered confidentiality system is crucial, alongside pre-planned evidence preservation protocols tailored to each target jurisdiction. In civil law countries like Germany and France, emphasis should be placed on preventive measures and administrative complaints to address high evidentiary thresholds. Conversely, in common law jurisdictions such as the US and UK, strengthening pre-litigation evidence preservation efforts is vital to enhance the likelihood of securing preliminary injunctions, thereby reducing cross-border enforcement costs and improving overall success rates.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["对比中欧美六国法院在商业秘密案件中的诉讼程序与实务差异", "分析各国在临时禁令申请及证据披露规则上的关键区别", "为跨国企业提供针对不同法域的商业秘密维权策略建议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes judicial practice differences and procedural characteristics in trade secret litigation across six major jurisdictions.", "Evaluates the practical effectiveness of key remedies such as evidence disclosure and preliminary injunctions in various courts.", "Provides strategic recommendations for cross-border trade secret enforcement based on specific jurisdictional legal frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德法等国严格证据规则，提前完善内部合规与证据留存", "依据目标国诉讼特点定制维权策略，善用当地法律资源"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt enforcement strategies to local judicial nuances, prioritizing prevention in civil law systems and pre-trial preparation in common law regions.", "Practice note: Implement tiered confidentiality measures and jurisdiction-specific evidence preservation plans to mitigate cross-border litigation risks.", "What to monitor: Track evolving standards for preliminary injunctions and evidence disclosure requirements in key markets like Germany, France, the US, and the UK."]
core_points_en: Analyzes judicial practice differences and procedural characteristics in trade secret litigation across six major jurisdictions.
Evaluates the practical effectiveness of key remedies such as evidence disclosure and preliminary injunctions in various courts.
Provides strategic recommendations for cross-border trade secret enforcement based on specific jurisdictional legal frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt enforcement strategies to local judicial nuances, prioritizing prevention in civil law systems and pre-trial preparation in common law regions.
Practice note: Implement tiered confidentiality measures and jurisdiction-specific evidence preservation plans to mitigate cross-border litigation risks.
What to monitor: Track evolving standards for preliminary injunctions and evidence disclosure requirements in key markets like Germany, France, the US, and the UK.

items.title: TALKUAL Selects Markify Watch To Strengthen Its Brand Protection Strategy
items.title_zh: TALKUAL选用Markify强化品牌保护策略
items.title_en: TALKUAL Adopts Markify Watch for Global Trademark Monitoring and Brand Protection
items.summary: Questel, a global leader in intellectual property (IP) solutions, is pleased to announce that TALKUAL has chosen Markify to watch for new, identical, and confusingly similar trademarks
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球知识产权解决方案领导者Questel宣布，西班牙食品科技公司TALKUAL选择其旗下Markify服务进行商标监测。TALKUAL致力于减少食物浪费，随着业务扩张，将知识产权保护列为战略重点。通过采用Markify Watch，该公司能早期检测潜在冲突，监控相同或混淆性相似的商标，从而 safeguarding 品牌身份。此举凸显了扩展期企业对稳健IP策略的需求。
items.ai_summary_en: Questel, a global leader in intellectual property solutions, has announced that TALKUAL, a Spain-based foodtech company, has selected its Markify service for comprehensive trademark monitoring. TALKUAL is dedicated to reducing food waste by distributing imperfect produce directly from farmers to consumers. As the company expands its market presence, protecting intellectual property has become a strategic priority. By implementing Markify Watch, TALKUAL gains advanced capabilities to detect potential conflicts early, specifically monitoring for identical or confusingly similar trademarks. This move safeguards the company’s brand identity during its growth phase. The collaboration underscores the critical need for robust IP strategies among scaling enterprises, particularly those driving innovation in sustainability and the food technology sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视品牌全生命周期管理，尤其在出海扩张期需部署自动化监测工具。建议借鉴TALKUAL案例，利用专业SaaS平台实时监控近似商标，降低维权成本与法律风险，确保品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies undergoing rapid international expansion, integrating automated trademark monitoring tools is essential to mitigate infringement risks effectively. Leveraging professional SaaS platforms enables real-time surveillance of similar marks, which significantly reduces enforcement costs and legal exposure while ensuring brand asset security. Market participants should view continuous brand lifecycle management not merely as a legal formality but as a core component of commercial strategy. Proactive monitoring allows rights holders to address conflicts before they escalate, preserving brand equity and market position in competitive landscapes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Questel宣布TALKUAL选用Markify进行全球商标监测以强化品牌保护", "TALKUAL作为快速成长的食品科技公司，将IP保护视为扩张期的战略优先事项", "Markify Watch提供高级监测能力，帮助识别风险并早期发现潜在商标冲突"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Questel announces that TALKUAL has selected Markify for global trademark monitoring to strengthen its overall brand protection strategy.", "TALKUAL, a rapidly growing foodtech firm, prioritizes intellectual property protection as a key strategic element during its business expansion.", "Markify Watch provides advanced monitoring capabilities to identify risks and detect potential trademark conflicts at an early stage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出海企业应在扩张期部署自动化商标监测工具以防范侵权风险", "利用专业SaaS平台实时监控可显著降低维权成本并保障品牌安全"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies expanding globally must deploy automated trademark monitoring tools to prevent infringement risks during critical growth phases.", "Practice note: Utilizing professional SaaS platforms for real-time monitoring can significantly lower enforcement costs and safeguard brand security.", "What to monitor: Rights holders should track identical and confusingly similar trademark filings to enable early detection and cost-effective conflict resolution."]
core_points_en: Questel announces that TALKUAL has selected Markify for global trademark monitoring to strengthen its overall brand protection strategy.
TALKUAL, a rapidly growing foodtech firm, prioritizes intellectual property protection as a key strategic element during its business expansion.
Markify Watch provides advanced monitoring capabilities to identify risks and detect potential trademark conflicts at an early stage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies expanding globally must deploy automated trademark monitoring tools to prevent infringement risks during critical growth phases.
Practice note: Utilizing professional SaaS platforms for real-time monitoring can significantly lower enforcement costs and safeguard brand security.
What to monitor: Rights holders should track identical and confusingly similar trademark filings to enable early detection and cost-effective conflict resolution.

items.title_en: Licences de propriété intellectuelle : quand la géopolitique s'invite à la table des négociations

items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出2026年地缘政治成为IP许可核心变量。霍尔木兹海峡危机导致供应链重组，增加替代路线侵权风险。中美贸易摩擦使跨国企业面临合规两难。不可抗力条款需精确涵盖制裁与封锁；许可费估值因成本波动变得不稳定，长期合同需重新审视风险地图。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需审查现有许可协议中的不可抗力定义是否涵盖地缘政治阻断。在长周期合同中，应建立动态的风险评估机制，将实际履行地的政治稳定性纳入权利估值模型，以应对不可预见的合规与物流冲击。

items.title: Ban on the use of meat names for plant-based alternatives
items.title_zh: 欧盟达成禁令：植物基替代品禁用肉类名称
items.title_en: EU Agreement Bans Specific Meat Names for Plant-Based Alternatives
items.summary: The EU recently reached an agreement on the question of whether a meat name could be used for plant-based veg products marketed as a substitute for meat.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟机构达成协议，禁止在植物基替代产品上使用特定肉类名称（如鸡肉、牛肉），旨在防止消费者被误导并依据《欧盟商标条例》第7条避免注册误导性商标。通用词汇如“汉堡”仍可使用。该禁令可能导致含肉类名称的植物基品牌无法继续使用现有注册商标，迫使供应商修改包装及品牌策略以符合新规。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament, Council, and member states have agreed to prohibit the use of specific meat names for plant-based alternatives. This measure aims to prevent consumer confusion by reserving terms like 'chicken' or 'beef' exclusively for animal products. While generic descriptors such as 'burger' remain permissible, brands containing restricted animal references may face usage restrictions under Article 7 of the EU Trademark Regulation, which prohibits misleading trademarks regarding product nature.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧出口植物基食品的中国企业而言，需立即审查现有商标及包装，移除鸡、牛等具体肉类词汇以避免侵权或违规。建议保留burger等通用词，重新设计品牌标识。同时评估现有注册商标的有效性，必要时进行变更或新申请，确保符合欧盟反误导规定，降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This regulatory shift impacts portfolio management for food sector rights holders. Brands using protected marks with specific animal references must assess compliance risks, as legal protection does not automatically override statutory bans on misleading commercial practices. Companies should evaluate whether their current packaging and branding strategies align with the new distinction between specific meat terms and generic descriptors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟禁止植物基产品使用特定动物部位名称，以防误导公众", "依据EUTMR第7条，误导性商标不得注册，现有品牌可能受限", "通用描述词如burger允许使用，但需移除具体肉类指代词汇"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.", "Generic descriptive words like burger or sausage remain allowed for marketing plant-based food substitutes.", "Existing trademarks referencing specific animals may become unusable if deemed misleading under current EU regulations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查在欧植物基产品包装，移除具体肉类名称以合规", "评估现有含肉类词商标风险，及时调整品牌策略应对禁令"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders in the plant-based sector must review existing trademarks and packaging for prohibited specific meat terminology.", "Practice note: Assess potential conflicts between registered trademark rights and the statutory ban on misleading indications under EUTMR Article 7.", "What to monitor: Implementation timelines and enforcement guidance from national authorities regarding the removal of restricted terms from labels."]
core_points_en: Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.
Generic descriptive words like burger or sausage remain allowed for marketing plant-based food substitutes.
Existing trademarks referencing specific animals may become unusable if deemed misleading under current EU regulations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders in the plant-based sector must review existing trademarks and packaging for prohibited specific meat terminology.
Practice note: Assess potential conflicts between registered trademark rights and the statutory ban on misleading indications under EUTMR Article 7.
What to monitor: Implementation timelines and enforcement guidance from national authorities regarding the removal of restricted terms from labels.

items.title: PROTACs reach the market: what the first regulatory approval means for drug discovery and the patent landscape
items.title_zh: PROTAC获批上市：首个监管批准对药物发现与专利格局的影响
items.title_en: First PROTAC Approval: Implications for Targeted Protein Degradation Drug Discovery and Patent Strategy
items.summary: The FDA's approval of vepdegestrant marks a significant milestone for PROTAC therapies, reshaping drug discovery and patent strategies in targeted protein degradation.
items.ai_summary_zh: FDA批准Arvinas的vepdegestrant，标志着PROTAC疗法首次获得监管认可。文章指出小分子药物占比回升，PROTAC通过事件驱动机制降解难治靶点蛋白，具备显著优势。然而，其结构复杂性导致专利权利要求撰写困难，且面临拥挤的专利 landscape。分析强调创新者需重新评估资产保护策略，以应对技术重叠和复杂的专利环境，这对全球药物研发及IP战略具有里程碑意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The FDA’s approval of Arvinas’ vepdegestrant marks a pivotal milestone as the first regulatory clearance for a PROTAC therapy. This validation underscores the clinical viability of targeted protein degradation (TPD) and signals a resurgence in small molecule drug development, which continues to dominate global pharmaceutical revenues despite the growth of biologics. PROTACs utilize an event-driven mechanism to degrade previously undruggable targets, offering distinct advantages over traditional occupancy-based inhibitors. However, the structural complexity of these heterobifunctional molecules presents significant challenges for patent claim drafting. With an increasingly crowded patent landscape and overlapping technologies, innovators must strategically reassess their intellectual property protection approaches to secure robust assets in this evolving therapeutic domain.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国创新药企应密切关注PROTAC专利布局动态，规避密集专利区。鉴于结构复杂性，建议加强FTO分析，重点排查连接子与E3配体组合的自由实施风险。同时，探索差异化设计或外围专利包围策略，以在欧美市场建立稳固的IP护城河，应对潜在侵权诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should prioritize comprehensive freedom-to-operate analyses within the PROTAC sector, specifically focusing on linker structures and E3 ligase combinations to mitigate infringement risks. Given the structural intricacies involved, adopting differentiated molecular designs or establishing peripheral patent thickets can help construct resilient intellectual property barriers for European and US markets. These strategies are essential for navigating potential litigation and securing competitive advantage amidst dense patent clusters and technological convergence in targeted protein degradation therapies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["FDA批准首款PROTAC药物，验证靶向蛋白降解疗法的临床价值与小分子复兴趋势", "PROTAC事件驱动机制优于传统抑制剂，但结构复杂致专利撰写难度显著增加", "专利景观日益拥挤且存在技术重叠，创新者需优化权利要求以强化资产保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["FDA approval validates TPD clinical value and highlights the continued dominance of small molecules in new drug approvals.", "PROTACs offer superior event-driven degradation mechanisms but face heightened difficulties in complex patent claim drafting.", "Crowded patent landscapes and technology overlaps require innovators to optimize claims for stronger asset protection."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加强PROTAC领域自由实施分析，规避连接子与配体组合的密集专利风险", "采取差异化设计或外围专利包围策略，构建针对欧美市场的坚实IP壁垒"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct rigorous FTO analysis on linker and E3 ligase combinations to avoid dense patent zones.", "Practice note: Employ differentiated design or peripheral patent enclosure strategies to build robust IP moats.", "What to monitor: Evolving patent landscapes and technical overlaps in PROTAC structures to anticipate infringement risks."]
core_points_en: FDA approval validates TPD clinical value and highlights the continued dominance of small molecules in new drug approvals.
PROTACs offer superior event-driven degradation mechanisms but face heightened difficulties in complex patent claim drafting.
Crowded patent landscapes and technology overlaps require innovators to optimize claims for stronger asset protection.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct rigorous FTO analysis on linker and E3 ligase combinations to avoid dense patent zones.
Practice note: Employ differentiated design or peripheral patent enclosure strategies to build robust IP moats.
What to monitor: Evolving patent landscapes and technical overlaps in PROTAC structures to anticipate infringement risks.

items.title: IA et droit d’auteur : vers une « taxe sur les données » en Europe ?
items.title_zh: 欧盟拟议AI数据税：Mistral AI提议营收贡献机制
items.title_en: IA et droit d’auteur : vers une « taxe sur les données » en Europe ?
items.summary: Mistral AI propose un mécanisme de contribution financière pour les entreprises d'IA utilisant des contenus protégés, visant à concilier innovation technologique et respect du droit d'auteur.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对欧盟AI企业与版权方的紧张关系，Mistral AI提议建立基于营收比例的财务贡献机制，以换取使用公开受版权保护内容的合法性。该方案旨在弥补现行“选择退出”机制的执行缺陷，为AI公司提供法律确定性，同时保障权利人获得报酬。此举被视为应对美中竞争、平衡技术创新与文化生态的战略尝试，但遭到部分文化行业强烈反对，认为其削弱了版权基础。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI企业在欧运营需密切关注此政策动向。若“数据税”落地，将增加合规成本。建议企业提前评估数据源版权风险，探索授权合作模式，避免依赖灰色地带。同时，应关注欧盟立法进程，利用公共咨询渠道表达立场，争取公平合理的监管环境。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Mistral AI提议按营收比例付费，以合法使用公开版权内容训练模型", "现行版权指令的“选择退出”机制因追踪困难而失效，需新补偿框架", "提案旨在提升欧洲AI竞争力并平衡创新与版权保护，但争议巨大"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟可能实施的AI数据使用费，提前规划版权合规预算", "从被动规避转向主动授权，构建可持续的数据获取合规体系"]

items.title: BMS and Hengrui Pharma sign $15.2bn deal as patent cliff nears
items.title_zh: BMS与恒瑞医药签署152亿美元协议应对专利悬崖
items.title_en: BMS and Hengrui Pharma Sign $15.2 Billion Deal to Navigate Approaching Patent Cliff
items.summary: 190 drugs face loss of exclusivity between 2026 and 2030, with the list including Bristol Myers Squibb’s blood-thinning drug Eliquis and immunotherapy medication Opdivo
items.ai_summary_zh: 新闻指出2026至2030年间将有190种药物面临独占权丧失，包括百时美施贵宝的Eliquis和Opdivo。在此“专利悬崖”背景下，百时美施贵宝与中国恒瑞医药达成价值152亿美元的许可与合作协议。该交易旨在通过引入新资产弥补核心药物专利到期后的收入缺口，反映了大型药企在专利保护期届满前积极寻求外部创新以维持市场地位的普遍策略。
items.ai_summary_en: With approximately 190 drugs facing loss of exclusivity between 2026 and 2030, including major assets like Bristol Myers Squibb’s Eliquis and Opdivo, the industry faces a significant patent cliff. In response, BMS has entered into a licensing and cooperation agreement with China’s Hengrui Pharma valued at $15.2 billion. This strategic partnership aims to introduce new assets to offset revenue gaps caused by expiring patents on core medications. The deal highlights a broader trend where large pharmaceutical companies actively seek external innovation before their own patent protections expire. By securing access to novel therapies through cross-border collaborations, rights holders aim to maintain market position and financial stability amidst increasing competitive pressure from generics and biosimilars during this critical transition period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企正从单纯仿制转向高价值创新输出，此案例证明国际巨头认可中国研发实力。对在欧中国企业而言，应重视IP组合质量而非数量，利用高质量专利进行跨境许可谈判。同时需密切关注欧洲专利期限补偿制度变化，提前规划核心产品上市节奏，以最大化专利剩余价值并规避侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This transaction underscores the growing recognition of high-value innovation capabilities within the Chinese pharmaceutical sector. For market participants operating in Europe, the focus must shift from merely accumulating patent quantity to enhancing IP portfolio quality. High-quality patents are essential for supporting complex cross-border licensing negotiations and establishing credible partnerships. Companies should rigorously evaluate their intellectual property assets to ensure they meet international standards for enforceability and commercial value. Furthermore, robust IP strategies are increasingly vital for attracting investment and facilitating technology transfer agreements in a competitive global landscape where innovation output is closely scrutinized by potential partners.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["种药物将在2026-2030年失去独占权，引发行业专利悬崖担忧", "BMS与恒瑞医药达成152亿美元巨额交易，涵盖新药授权与合作", "交易旨在对冲核心药物专利到期风险，体现药企外部创新战略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["One hundred ninety drugs face loss of exclusivity between 2026 and 2030, triggering widespread industry concerns regarding the impending patent cliff.", "Bristol Myers Squibb and Hengrui Pharma executed a massive $15.2 billion transaction involving new drug licensing and strategic cooperation.", "The agreement serves to hedge against risks from expiring core drug patents, reflecting a strategic shift toward external innovation sourcing."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国创新药获国际认可，应强化高价值专利布局以支撑跨境许可", "关注欧洲专利期限补偿机制，优化产品上市节奏以最大化IP价值"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: International recognition of Chinese innovative drugs necessitates strengthening high-value patent layouts to support cross-border licensing deals.", "Practice note: Prioritize IP portfolio quality over quantity to enhance leverage in international negotiations and partnership discussions.", "What to monitor: European supplementary protection certificate regulations and product launch timing to maximize remaining patent value."]
core_points_en: One hundred ninety drugs face loss of exclusivity between 2026 and 2030, triggering widespread industry concerns regarding the impending patent cliff.
Bristol Myers Squibb and Hengrui Pharma executed a massive $15.2 billion transaction involving new drug licensing and strategic cooperation.
The agreement serves to hedge against risks from expiring core drug patents, reflecting a strategic shift toward external innovation sourcing.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: International recognition of Chinese innovative drugs necessitates strengthening high-value patent layouts to support cross-border licensing deals.
Practice note: Prioritize IP portfolio quality over quantity to enhance leverage in international negotiations and partnership discussions.
What to monitor: European supplementary protection certificate regulations and product launch timing to maximize remaining patent value.

items.title: Streaming Without Copying: The CJEU Redefines Private Use in Stichting de Thuiskopie (C-496/24)
items.title_zh: CJEU重定义私人复制：Stichting de Thuiskopie案中的流媒体副本判定
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Offline Streaming Copies Do Not Qualify for Private Copying Exception
items.summary: Introduction
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月16日，CJEU在Stichting de Thuiskopie案中裁决，订阅制流媒体服务的“离线流媒体副本”不构成《信息社会指令》下的私人复制例外。此类副本受技术措施严格控制，用户无法转移文件，权利持有人保留控制权。因此，硬件制造商无需为此类副本缴纳私人复制税。该判决旨在根据数字使用和平台模式重新校准版权例外范围。
items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the Court of Justice ruled in Stichting de Thuiskopie that offline streaming copies from subscription services do not fall under the private copying exception in Article 5(2)(b) of the InfoSoc Directive. The Court determined that these copies are strictly controlled by technological protection measures, preventing users from transferring files or accessing them outside the designated application. Consequently, rights holders retain control over access and distribution. As a result, hardware manufacturers are not required to pay private copying levies for devices used primarily for such controlled offline streaming. This judgment recalibrates the scope of copyright exceptions in the digital era, emphasizing that technical restrictions preventing free user control exclude content from the levy system applicable to traditional private copying.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国流媒体及硬件出口企业需关注欧盟版权 levy 豁免边界。若产品提供受TPM严格限制的离线功能，可能免于征税，但需确保符合“无自由复制”的技术特征。建议优化DRM策略以契合司法认定，避免被认定为可自由传播的私人复制行为，从而降低合规成本与税务风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the boundary between taxable private copying and licensed digital access. For technology providers and hardware manufacturers operating in the EU, implementing robust Digital Rights Management (DRM) that prevents file transfer and restricts access to specific applications is crucial. Such technical measures can effectively exclude products from private copying levy obligations. Companies should review their DRM strategies to ensure they align with the CJEU’s definition of non-free copying. By maintaining strict technical control over content usage, businesses can mitigate compliance costs and avoid unexpected tax liabilities associated with the private copying exception framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定订阅制流媒体的离线副本不属于私人复制例外范畴", "因技术限制导致用户无法自由控制文件，权利持有人仍保有访问控制权", "硬件制造商无需为这类受控的流媒体离线副本缴纳私人复制税"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Offline streaming copies within subscription services do not qualify as private copying under EU law due to strict technical controls.", "Users cannot transfer or freely access files, meaning rights holders maintain effective control over the content distribution.", "Hardware manufacturers are exempt from paying private copying levies for devices used for these technologically restricted streams."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化DRM技术以符合“非自由复制”特征，争取免除欧盟私人复制税", "密切关注CJEU对数字版权例外的最新解释，调整在欧业务合规策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implementing strict DRM to prevent file transfer may exempt hardware from EU private copying levies.", "Practice note: Align technical protection measures with CJEU standards to demonstrate lack of free user control over content.", "What to monitor: Future CJEU interpretations of digital copyright exceptions and evolving definitions of private use exemptions."]
core_points_en: Offline streaming copies within subscription services do not qualify as private copying under EU law due to strict technical controls.
Users cannot transfer or freely access files, meaning rights holders maintain effective control over the content distribution.
Hardware manufacturers are exempt from paying private copying levies for devices used for these technologically restricted streams.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implementing strict DRM to prevent file transfer may exempt hardware from EU private copying levies.
Practice note: Align technical protection measures with CJEU standards to demonstrate lack of free user control over content.
What to monitor: Future CJEU interpretations of digital copyright exceptions and evolving definitions of private use exemptions.

items.title: Design Registration: The Optimal Way To Protect Your Design
items.title_zh: 外观设计注册：保护设计的最佳途径
items.title_en: Design Registration: The Optimal Way To Protect Your Design
items.summary: Companies invest heavily in visual identity, but without proper protection, that investment remains vulnerable. Design registration offers a practical approach to safeguarding product appearance by focusing on novelty and individual character, making it essential to consider protection early and align filing strategies with target markets.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出企业应重视外观设计注册以保护视觉资产，因其比版权或3D商标更易确权。尽管欧盟存在未注册设计权，但其保护范围和期限有限。作者推荐利用WIPO海牙体系进行国际申请，该体系允许单件申请包含多达100项设计，且无需基础申请。数据显示2025年通过海牙体系的国际申请量增长9.4%，设计数量创历史新高。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注不同IP权利（版权、商标、外观）的累积保护策略差异。鉴于3D商标确权的司法实践难度，企业在构建产品外观保护组合时，应将注册外观设计作为核心手段。同时，海牙体系的使用率上升反映了企业对单一程序多国保护的偏好，建议评估该体系在目标市场的成本与效率优势。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["外观设计注册侧重新颖性和个体特征，相比证明原创性的版权更具操作性", "D商标注册虽可行，但因难以证明显著性，获取和执行权利均极为困难", "WIPO海牙体系支持电子提交且无前置申请要求，是覆盖多领土的高效工具"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]

items.title: Big reputation
items.title_zh: 泰勒·斯威夫特商标战略：声誉背后的布局故事
items.title_en: Taylor Swift’s Trademark Strategy: How Systematic Brand Protection Safeguards Reputation
items.summary: Taylor Swift has got a long list of trade marks, and each one tells a story about strategy, writes Amelia Skelding.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文由CITMA发布，深入剖析流行歌手泰勒·斯威夫特庞大的商标注册组合。文章指出，其每一项商标申请并非孤立行为，而是蕴含特定商业策略的体现。作者Amelia Skelding通过梳理其商标列表，揭示了权利人如何利用商标法保护品牌声誉、防止搭便车行为，并展示在竞争激烈的娱乐市场中，系统性的商标布局对于维护长期品牌价值的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary by Amelia Skelding, published by CITMA, provides a detailed analysis of pop star Taylor Swift’s extensive trademark portfolio. The article emphasizes that each trademark application is not an isolated action but reflects specific commercial strategies. By examining her trademark list, the author reveals how rights holders utilize trademark law to protect brand reputation and prevent free-riding behaviors. The piece highlights the importance of systematic trademark layout in maintaining long-term brand value within the highly competitive entertainment market. It demonstrates how a comprehensive approach to registration serves as a critical tool for safeguarding intellectual property assets against infringement and unauthorized use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应借鉴此类系统性布局思维，不仅关注单一商品注册，更需结合品牌声誉进行防御性布局。在欧洲市场，尤其是英国和欧盟，针对核心品牌及衍生词汇进行全方位商标保护，能有效遏制恶意抢注与侵权，降低维权成本，提升品牌资产的安全性与商业价值。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should adopt a systematic approach to trademark layout, moving beyond single product registrations to include defensive strategies aligned with brand reputation. In European markets, particularly the UK and EU, comprehensive protection for core brands and derivative terms helps curb malicious squatting and infringement. This proactive stance reduces enforcement costs and enhances the security and commercial value of brand assets. Rights holders are advised to integrate reputation management into their IP strategy, ensuring broader coverage against potential threats while maintaining robust legal defenses in competitive sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["泰勒·斯威夫特拥有庞大且策略明确的商标注册组合", "每个商标背后均反映了特定的品牌保护与商业运营逻辑", "系统性商标布局是维护高知名度品牌声誉的关键手段"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Taylor Swift maintains a vast and strategically defined trademark portfolio that reflects careful planning.", "Each individual trademark application embodies specific logic regarding brand protection and commercial operations.", "Systematic trademark layout is essential for preserving the reputation of high-profile brands effectively."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借鉴系统性商标布局思维，强化品牌防御性注册", "结合声誉保护进行全方位布局，遏制恶意抢注侵权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adopt systematic trademark layouts combining defensive registrations with brand reputation protection.", "Practice note: Secure comprehensive coverage for core brands and derivatives in the UK and EU to deter bad-faith filings.", "What to monitor: Track new applications involving brand-related terms to identify and oppose potential infringement early."]
core_points_en: Taylor Swift maintains a vast and strategically defined trademark portfolio that reflects careful planning.
Each individual trademark application embodies specific logic regarding brand protection and commercial operations.
Systematic trademark layout is essential for preserving the reputation of high-profile brands effectively.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adopt systematic trademark layouts combining defensive registrations with brand reputation protection.
Practice note: Secure comprehensive coverage for core brands and derivatives in the UK and EU to deter bad-faith filings.
What to monitor: Track new applications involving brand-related terms to identify and oppose potential infringement early.

items.title: They shoot, but do they score?
items.title_zh: Instagram胜诉：EUIPO维持对“-GRAM”后缀的强保护
items.title_en: EUIPO Upholds Instagram's Strong Rights Over the -GRAM Suffix in Opposition Case
items.summary: Instagram’s grip on the -GRAM suffix proves picture perfect, writes Oli Gray. B 3 219 888, Instagram LLC v Panathinaikos, EUIPO Opposition Division.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论了EUIPO异议部在Instagram LLC诉Panathinaikos案（B 3 219 888）中的决定。尽管“-gram”作为词缀具有描述性，但鉴于Instagram品牌的极高知名度，其对该后缀的控制力被认定为完美。该裁决确认了在后申请包含“-gram”的商标若易导致公众混淆，将面临被异议驳回的风险，强化了知名商标的保护范围。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary analyzes the EUIPO Opposition Division's decision in Instagram LLC v Panathinaikos (Case B 3 219 888). Although the suffix '-gram' possesses descriptive characteristics, Instagram's brand is recognized for its exceptionally high reputation. Consequently, the authority affirmed that the company maintains a firm grip on this suffix. The ruling confirms that later trademark applications incorporating '-gram' face significant risks of opposition and rejection if they are likely to cause public confusion. This decision reinforces the protective scope afforded to well-known trademarks, demonstrating that substantial market recognition can overcome the inherent descriptiveness of specific word elements in establishing likelihood of confusion.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局社交、内容类APP或平台时，应严格规避使用“-gram”后缀，以防侵权风险。即便词源上具描述性，知名品牌通过长期使用获得的第二含义受强力保护。建议进行详尽的欧盟商标检索，避免在命名策略上触碰Instagram的权利边界，确保品牌合规与安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing social media or content platforms should carefully evaluate naming strategies to mitigate infringement risks associated with the '-gram' suffix. Even if a term has etymological descriptive value, well-known brands secure robust protection through acquired distinctiveness and extensive use. Conducting comprehensive EU trademark searches is essential before finalizing brand names. Applicants must ensure their branding does not encroach upon the protected boundaries of famous marks like Instagram, thereby ensuring legal compliance and safeguarding long-term brand security within the European Union.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO异议部裁定Instagram对“-GRAM”后缀拥有强势权利", "品牌高知名度可克服词缀描述性，确立混淆可能性", "企业注册含“-gram”商标需警惕侵犯Instagram在先权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO Opposition Division ruled that Instagram holds strong exclusive rights regarding the use of the '-GRAM' suffix in relevant markets.", "High brand fame effectively overcomes the descriptive nature of the suffix, successfully establishing a likelihood of confusion among consumers.", "Companies registering trademarks containing '-gram' must exercise caution to avoid infringing upon Instagram's established prior rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规避“-gram”后缀以降低欧盟商标注册与侵权风险", "重视知名品牌通过知名度获得的扩展保护范围"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using the '-gram' suffix to reduce risks related to EU trademark registration and potential infringement claims.", "Practice note: Recognize that well-known brands benefit from an expanded scope of protection derived from their significant market reputation.", "What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO decisions involving descriptive suffixes used by major brands to understand evolving standards for confusion."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO Opposition Division ruled that Instagram holds strong exclusive rights regarding the use of the '-GRAM' suffix in relevant markets.
High brand fame effectively overcomes the descriptive nature of the suffix, successfully establishing a likelihood of confusion among consumers.
Companies registering trademarks containing '-gram' must exercise caution to avoid infringing upon Instagram's established prior rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using the '-gram' suffix to reduce risks related to EU trademark registration and potential infringement claims.
Practice note: Recognize that well-known brands benefit from an expanded scope of protection derived from their significant market reputation.
What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO decisions involving descriptive suffixes used by major brands to understand evolving standards for confusion.
