items.title: Find my reputation
items.title_zh: CITMA评析：相似元素能否转移品牌声誉？
items.title_en: UK IPO Review: Can Similar Elements Shift Brand Reputation in Major Disputes?
items.summary: In disputes involving major brands, can marks’ similar elements meaningfully shift consumer perception? Charlene Nelson finds out. O/1195/25, MEGA POP podjetje za oglasevanje na prostem d.o.o v Apple Inc, UK IPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于英国知识产权局（UKIPO）裁决O/1195/25，分析MEGA POP与Apple Inc.的商标争议。文章探讨在涉及大品牌的纠纷中，商标的相似元素是否足以实质性改变消费者的认知与感知。通过案例回顾，作者Charlene Nelson深入剖析了声誉转移的法律标准及证据要求，为理解英国商标法中的混淆可能性与声誉利用提供了专业视角。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the UK Intellectual Property Office decision O/1195/25 in the trademark dispute between MEGA POP and Apple Inc. The article investigates whether similar elements within trademarks can meaningfully alter consumer perception, particularly when major brands are involved. Through a detailed case review, author Charlene Nelson dissects the legal standards and evidentiary requirements for establishing reputation transfer. The piece provides professional insights into the likelihood of confusion and the exploitation of reputation under UK trademark law, offering a clear perspective on how these complex legal concepts are applied in contemporary opposition proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局时需警惕“搭便车”风险，避免使用与大品牌相似的视觉或文字元素。应建立严格的商标检索机制，评估潜在冲突。若遭遇异议，需重点准备证据证明自身品牌独立性，防止因声誉转移理论导致注册失败或侵权认定。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should carefully evaluate the risk of free-riding allegations when selecting brand identifiers. Avoiding visual or textual elements that closely resemble established global brands is crucial to mitigate infringement risks associated with reputation transfer theories. Rights holders must maintain robust evidence chains demonstrating their brand's independent development and distinct market presence. This preparation is essential for effectively responding to oppositions based on likelihood of confusion. Proactive trademark searches and thorough conflict assessments remain vital strategies for securing registration and defending against claims of unfair advantage or dilution in competitive markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["分析UKIPO裁决O/1195/25中MEGA POP诉Apple案的商标争议焦点", "探讨商标相似元素在涉及知名品牌时是否影响消费者感知的法律标准", "评估声誉转移理论在商标异议程序中的适用性及举证责任分配"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes the key trademark dispute focal points in the UK IPO decision O/1195/25 involving MEGA POP and Apple Inc.", "Explores the legal standards determining if similar elements influence consumer perception in cases involving well-known brands.", "Assesses the applicability of reputation transfer theory in trademark oppositions and the allocation of burden of proof."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规避与大品牌相似元素，防范声誉转移导致的侵权风险", "强化独立品牌证据链，应对基于混淆可能性的商标异议"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using elements similar to major brands to prevent infringement risks linked to reputation transfer.", "Practice note: Strengthen evidence of independent brand identity to counter oppositions based on likelihood of confusion.", "What to monitor: Legal developments regarding reputation transfer standards and evidentiary burdens in UK trademark oppositions."]
core_points_en: Analyzes the key trademark dispute focal points in the UK IPO decision O/1195/25 involving MEGA POP and Apple Inc.
Explores the legal standards determining if similar elements influence consumer perception in cases involving well-known brands.
Assesses the applicability of reputation transfer theory in trademark oppositions and the allocation of burden of proof.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using elements similar to major brands to prevent infringement risks linked to reputation transfer.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence of independent brand identity to counter oppositions based on likelihood of confusion.
What to monitor: Legal developments regarding reputation transfer standards and evidentiary burdens in UK trademark oppositions.

items.title: Keeping your word
items.title_zh: 保持信誉：文字商标用于Logo仍视为真实使用
items.title_en: UKIPO: Using Registered Word Marks in Logos Can Constitute Genuine Use
items.summary: Using a registered word mark as part of a logo mark does not preclude a finding of genuine use – as long as the words remain the dominant element, writes Trecina Sookhoo. O/0080/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Limo UK Ltd; O/0028/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v EasyTerra Besloten Vennootschap; O/1148/25, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Avenues Ltd, UK IPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国知识产权局（UKIPO）近期三起案件（O/0080/26等），探讨将注册文字商标作为图形标识一部分使用的法律效力。文章指出，只要文字元素在整体标识中保持主导地位，这种使用方式并不妨碍被认定为“真实使用”。该裁决澄清了商标使用形式的合规性标准，为权利人在品牌视觉升级中保留法律保护提供了依据，强调了维持商标显著特征的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent UK Intellectual Property Office decisions confirm that incorporating a registered word mark into a composite logo does not preclude a finding of genuine use. Provided the word element remains the dominant feature of the overall sign, such usage satisfies the requirement to maintain registration validity. This analysis covers three specific opposition cases involving Kilburn & Strode LLP and various respondents, clarifying the standards for acceptable trademark use forms under current UK practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧市场进行品牌视觉升级时，常将文字与图形结合。需注意保留文字的主导地位，避免因过度设计导致被视为新标识而丧失原有保护。建议定期审查商标使用证据，确保符合“真实使用”标准，以防因连续五年不使用而被撤销注册，保障品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: This development provides clarity for portfolio management regarding brand evolution. Companies updating their visual identity can integrate existing word marks into new logos without necessarily jeopardizing their registration status, provided the core distinctiveness is preserved. Legal teams should review evidence of use to ensure it aligns with these dominance criteria to defend against non-use cancellation actions effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["文字商标融入图形Logo若保持主导元素，可认定构成真实使用", "UKIPO在多起案件中确认非纯粹文字形式的使用符合维持注册要求", "权利人需确保修改后的标识未改变原注册商标的显著特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.", "The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.", "Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌视觉升级应保留文字主导地位以维持注册有效性", "定期审查使用证据以防因未真实使用导致商标被撤销"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.", "Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.", "What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes."]
core_points_en: Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.
The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.
Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.
Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.
What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes.

items.title: The distinguishing mark of man
items.title_zh: 名人之名非版权保护对象：奥威尔遗产诉EUIPO案评析
items.title_en: Copyright Protects Works, Not Names: EUIPO Rejects Orwell Estate Claim
items.summary: No matter how famous an author is, copyright exists to protect their work, not their name, writes Matthew Harris. R 2248/2019-G, The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell v EUIPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析了EUIPO上诉委员会在R 2248/2019-G案中的决定，涉及已故作家乔治·奥威尔的遗产管理人Sonia Brownell Orwell。文章指出，尽管作者享有盛誉，但版权法的宗旨在于保护其创作的作品，而非其姓名或名声。该案件凸显了在欧盟商标注册中，试图通过版权主张来垄断知名人物姓名的法律局限性，强调了知识产权不同领域间的界限。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary by Matthew Harris for CITMA analyzes the EUIPO Board of Appeal decision in case R 2248/2019-G, involving The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell. The core legal principle reaffirmed is that copyright law exists to protect an author's creative works, not their name or personal reputation, regardless of the author's fame. The analysis highlights the limitations of attempting to secure trademark protection for a famous name solely through copyright assertions, emphasizing the distinct boundaries between different intellectual property rights within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局品牌时，应明确版权与商标权的边界。切勿试图利用版权法垄断通用名称或名人姓名以获取商标独占权，此举在欧盟缺乏法律依据。建议企业建立清晰的IP组合策略，分别通过商标和版权保护不同的资产要素，避免因权利基础混淆导致注册失败或无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and applicants operating in Europe, this decision underscores the importance of selecting the correct legal basis for IP protection. While copyright safeguards literary and artistic expressions, it does not extend to exclusive control over a person's name. Applicants seeking to register marks associated with famous figures must rely on appropriate trademark grounds rather than copyright arguments, ensuring compliance with established EU jurisprudence regarding the separation of IP regimes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权法旨在保护作品表达，不延伸至保护作者的姓名或公众声誉", "EUIPO驳回奥威尔遗产方基于版权主张注册其姓名的尝试", "知名人物姓名不能仅凭版权理由获得排他性商标保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright law protects the expression of creative works rather than the names or reputations of authors.", "The EUIPO rejected attempts by the Orwell Estate to register a famous name based on copyright claims.", "Intellectual property rights have distinct scopes, preventing copyright from monopolizing personal names."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["厘清版权与商标保护客体差异，避免权利基础混淆", "在欧注册含名人元素商标需评估版权主张的法律可行性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must distinguish between copyrightable works and non-copyrightable elements like names when building portfolios.", "Practice note: Trademark applications relying on copyright assertions for famous names face significant legal hurdles at the EUIPO.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions clarifying the boundary between copyright protection and trademark registration of personal identifiers."]
core_points_en: Copyright law protects the expression of creative works rather than the names or reputations of authors.
The EUIPO rejected attempts by the Orwell Estate to register a famous name based on copyright claims.
Intellectual property rights have distinct scopes, preventing copyright from monopolizing personal names.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must distinguish between copyrightable works and non-copyrightable elements like names when building portfolios.
Practice note: Trademark applications relying on copyright assertions for famous names face significant legal hurdles at the EUIPO.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions clarifying the boundary between copyright protection and trademark registration of personal identifiers.

items.title: How to file an opposition at the European Patent Office (EPO) | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 如何在欧洲专利局提起专利异议
items.title_en: Procedural Requirements for Filing EPO Oppositions Under Article 100 EPC
items.summary: An opposition is a central way for third parties to challenge a granted European patent.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详解在欧洲专利局（EPO）对已授权专利提起异议的程序。异议是第三方挑战专利有效性的核心途径，须在法定期限内提交书面或电子申请并缴费。异议理由严格限于《欧洲专利公约》第100条规定的缺乏可专利性、公开不充分或超范围等特定法律依据。文章强调需通过官方渠道如在线 filing 2.0 提交，且费用支付需即时完成，为从业者提供实操指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis outlines the procedural framework for filing an opposition against a granted European patent at the European Patent Office (EPO). It specifies that third parties must submit a signed notice of opposition either in writing to designated EPO offices or electronically via Online Filing 2.0. The official opposition fee must be paid simultaneously with the filing, typically through a debit order. Crucially, the challenge is restricted to specific legal grounds defined under Article 100 of the European Patent Convention, such as lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter, preventing broad or arbitrary challenges to patent validity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧获权后应建立监控机制，防范竞争对手利用异议程序拖延或无效专利。若作为异议方，需精准锁定EPC第100条 grounds，避免理由泛化被驳回。建议熟悉EPO电子提交流程，确保程序合规，以低成本高效解决专利争议，维护市场自由实施空间。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and competitors operating within the European jurisdiction, understanding these strict procedural and substantive limits is essential for effective portfolio management. Ensuring compliance with filing channels and payment timing prevents immediate rejection of oppositions. For patentees, recognizing that challenges are confined to Article 100 grounds allows for targeted defense strategies focused on technical and legal sufficiency rather than broader commercial disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议是挑战已授权欧洲专利的核心行政程序", "必须基于EPC第100条特定法律理由如缺乏创造性", "需通过指定渠道提交并同步缴纳官方异议费"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.", "The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.", "Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利监控机制防范对手发起异议挑战", "精准锁定EPC第100条理由确保异议程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.", "Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.", "What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC."]
core_points_en: Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.
The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.
Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.
Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.
What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC.

items.title: Consolidation en UDRP : un levier stratégique pour rationaliser les actions contre le cybersquatting
items.title_zh: UDRP合并审理：打击网络抢注的战略杠杆
items.title_en: Consolidation en UDRP : un levier stratégique pour rationaliser les actions contre le cybersquatting
items.summary: La consolidation en UDRP permet aux titulaires de marques de regrouper plusieurs noms de domaine litigieux dans une seule procédure, réduisant ainsi les coûts et optimisant l'efficacité des actions contre le cybersquatting.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出UDRP程序允许商标持有人将多个争议域名合并至单一投诉中，以降低成本并提高打击网络抢注的效率。该机制适用于.com等通用顶级域及部分国家域名，需满足共同控制和公平效率两大标准。文中引用欧莱雅集团涉及700多个域名的案例，说明即使注册人不同，若存在共同控制亦可合并。这为权利人在数字环境中高效维权提供了关键路径。
items.ai_insight_zh: 对于在欧盟及全球拥有广泛品牌组合的企业，利用UDRP合并程序是应对大规模网络抢注的高性价比策略。权利人应注重收集证明域名注册人之间存在关联或共同控制的证据链，而不仅依赖表面注册信息。鉴于并非所有国家域名都支持此机制，制定维权策略时需预先评估目标域名的政策适用性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UDRP允许将多个争议域名合并至单一投诉，显著降低维权成本并提升效率", "合并审理需满足两个核心标准：域名间存在共同控制，且合并符合公平与效率原则", "即使域名注册人形式不同，若能证明背后存在共同控制，面板仍可批准合并申请"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 适用于面临多域名侵权的品牌方，提供低成…", "Practice note/实务观察: 重点在于构建“共同控制”的证据链，而非仅看名义…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注各国ccTLD对UDRP合并规则的采纳…"]

items.title: Have you reviewed your IP agreements following Brexit?
items.title_zh: 脱欧五年后，您是否审查了知识产权协议？
items.title_en: Have you reviewed your IP agreements following Brexit?
items.summary: It has now been over five years since the UK's departure from the European Union (Brexit) and the end of the transition period. If you...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出脱欧已逾五年，建议企业审查此前签署的IP协议。重点在于评估“欧盟”地域范围定义的模糊性，这可能导致英国被排除在保护范围外。建议通过修正案明确定义或采用特定日期构成来规避未来变动风险。此外，还需考虑人员流动限制、法律监管差异及贸易壁垒对合同条款的影响。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中应关注存量合同中地域条款的解释风险。对于涉及跨境许可或保护的协议，单纯依赖“欧盟”这一动态概念存在法律漏洞。建议建立定期审查机制，针对关键商业条款进行压力测试，确保在政治经济环境变动下权利义务的确定性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["脱欧后若协议仅笼统指代“欧盟”，法院可能解释为不包含英国，引发执行不确定性", "建议修订协议以明确包含英国，或定义基于特定日期的欧盟成员名单以增强稳定性", "需综合评估人员流动、监管分歧及贸易壁垒等因素对现有合同条款适用性的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]

items.title: Belgian private equity-backed flooring company launches IP consulting service
items.title_zh: 比利时私募地板企推出IP咨询服务
items.title_en: Belgian private equity-backed flooring company launches IP consulting service
items.summary: i4F is no stranger to the patent world. The company develops new technologies for floor coverings and is constantly battling with competitors in court. The company fought its competitors Unilin and Välinge, for example, for a long time before the Dutch patent courts. In October 2020, former Simmons & Simmons partner Mattie de Koning joined […] The post Belgian private equity-backed flooring company launches IP consulting service appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 比利时私募支持的地板技术公司i4F宣布推出知识产权咨询服务。该公司长期深耕地板覆盖物新技术研发，并在荷兰专利法院与Unilin、Välinge等竞争对手进行长期诉讼。前Simmons & Simmons合伙人Mattie de Koning于2020年10月加入，此次服务推出标志着其将内部积累的丰富专利实战经验转化为外部商业服务，旨在利用其在复杂专利纠纷中的专业知识开拓新业务领域。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的地板及建材企业需警惕此类由诉讼方转型的IP咨询机构。其具备极强的专利挖掘与诉讼策略能力，可能成为竞争对手的“军师”。建议中国企业加强FTO分析，建立更严密的专利防御体系，并关注核心技术人员流动带来的潜在风险，避免陷入被动诉讼局面。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["i4F依托私募背景及技术研发实力，正式进军IP咨询服务业态", "公司在荷兰法院与主要竞品进行过长期且激烈的专利诉讼对抗", "引入资深法律合伙人，将内部诉讼经验转化为对外专业服务产品"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕对手利用专业IP咨询强化专利围堵策略", "加强出口产品自由实施分析以规避侵权风险"]

items.title: Patentability In AI – Navigating The Legal Landscape
items.title_zh: AI可专利性： navigating 法律格局
items.title_en: AI Patentability in Europe: Navigating Technical Character and Effect Requirements
items.summary: AI and machine learning are reshaping industries, opening new opportunities for innovation. But can your AI invention be patented?
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨AI发明在欧洲的专利可行性。指出AI发明若具备“技术特征”并产生“技术效果”，即可获得专利保护。欧洲专利局（EPO）与美国专利商标局（USPTO）均关注AI是否应用于特定技术目的并带来可衡量的技术优势。文章以自动驾驶和图像处理为例，说明通过正确的技术框架可将创新转化为受保护的资产。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the patent eligibility of artificial intelligence inventions within the European jurisdiction. It establishes that AI innovations can secure patent protection if they satisfy two fundamental criteria: possessing technical character and producing a technical effect. Both the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office evaluate whether the AI application serves a specific technical purpose and delivers measurable technical advantages over existing solutions. The analysis highlights practical examples, such as autonomous vehicle control systems and advanced image processing technologies. These cases demonstrate how proper technical framing is essential for transforming innovative concepts into legally protected assets. By focusing on concrete technical contributions rather than abstract algorithms, applicants can effectively navigate the complex legal landscape surrounding AI patentability in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI企业在欧申请专利时，应避免仅强调算法本身，而需着重阐述其在具体技术领域（如控制、处理）的应用及带来的技术性改进。建议在撰写申请文件时，明确界定技术问题与技术方案，确保符合EPO关于技术贡献的要求，以提高授权率并规避客体排除风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants should avoid focusing exclusively on algorithmic logic when seeking European patent protection. Instead, drafting strategies must emphasize the specific technical application of AI within defined fields, such as control systems or data processing. It is critical to clearly articulate the underlying technical problem and the corresponding technical solution to meet the EPO’s rigorous requirements for technical contribution. This approach enhances the likelihood of grant by demonstrating a tangible improvement in technical performance, thereby mitigating the risk of rejection based on exclusions for non-technical subject matter or abstract ideas.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI发明获专利需满足技术特征与技术效果两大核心门槛", "EPO审查重点在于AI是否服务于特定技术目的及产生实际技术优势", "正确构建技术框架有助于将自动驾驶等AI创新转化为受保护资产"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI inventions require both technical character and a demonstrable technical effect to qualify for patent protection under current standards.", "The EPO strictly assesses whether AI applications serve specific technical purposes and yield actual, measurable technical advantages.", "Properly constructing a technical framework helps convert AI innovations, like autonomous driving, into enforceable intellectual property assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["申请策略应侧重阐述AI在具体场景的技术应用而非纯算法逻辑", "需明确界定技术问题与方案，以满足EPO对技术贡献的严格审查要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus claims on specific technical applications and improvements rather than pure algorithmic logic to ensure eligibility.", "Practice note: Clearly define the technical problem and solution in specifications to satisfy the EPO's strict technical contribution standards.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding the boundary between abstract AI methods and patentable technical implementations in Europe."]
core_points_en: AI inventions require both technical character and a demonstrable technical effect to qualify for patent protection under current standards.
The EPO strictly assesses whether AI applications serve specific technical purposes and yield actual, measurable technical advantages.
Properly constructing a technical framework helps convert AI innovations, like autonomous driving, into enforceable intellectual property assets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus claims on specific technical applications and improvements rather than pure algorithmic logic to ensure eligibility.
Practice note: Clearly define the technical problem and solution in specifications to satisfy the EPO's strict technical contribution standards.
What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding the boundary between abstract AI methods and patentable technical implementations in Europe.

items.title: First-Ever CJEU Case On Generative AI And Copyright
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院首审生成式AI版权案：Google Gemini被诉侵权
items.title_en: CJEU to Rule on Generative AI Copyright: Google Gemini Case Sets Precedent for EU Law
items.summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) will, for the first time, assess how EU copyright law applies to large language models.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院（CJEU）将首次评估欧盟版权法如何适用于大型语言模型。在Like Company诉Google Ireland案（C-250/25）中，Google的Gemini被指控未经许可复制新闻内容。案件核心争议涉及LLM训练合法性、AI输出责任及文本和数据挖掘例外条款。尽管裁决预计2027年才出炉，但其结果将深远影响欧洲AI合规、出版商权利及版权风险格局。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) will, for the first time, assess how EU copyright law applies to large language models. In Like Company v. Google Ireland (C-250/25), Google’s Gemini is accused of reproducing news content without permission, raising key questions about LLM training, AI output liability, and text/data mining exceptions. Although a ruling isn’t expected until 2027, the outcome is likely to have a lasting impact on AI compliance, publisher rights, and copyright risk in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此案将重塑欧洲AI训练数据合规底线。中国出海科技企业需立即审查数据获取来源，确保符合TDM例外或获得授权。建议建立严格的版权尽职调查机制，规避未来高额赔偿风险，并密切关注2027年裁决以调整全球AI战略。
items.ai_insight_en: This case will redefine the compliance baseline for AI training data in Europe. Market participants should immediately review data acquisition sources to ensure alignment with text and data mining exceptions or secure explicit authorization. Establishing robust copyright due diligence mechanisms is critical to mitigate potential high-value infringement liabilities. Stakeholders must closely monitor developments leading to the 2027 judgment to adjust global AI strategies and manage evolving copyright risks effectively across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院首次介入，界定大型语言模型在欧盟版权法下的法律地位", "Google Gemini因未授权复制新闻内容被诉，焦点在于训练数据合法性", "裁决将厘清AI输出责任及文本数据挖掘例外的适用范围与边界"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU will define the legal status of large language models under EU copyright law for the first time.", "Google Gemini faces allegations of unauthorized news content reproduction, focusing on training data legality.", "The ruling will clarify AI output liability and the scope of text and data mining exceptions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审查训练数据来源，确保符合TDM例外或获取明确授权", "建立版权尽职调查机制，规避潜在的高额侵权赔偿风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Review training data sources to ensure compliance with TDM exceptions or obtain explicit authorization.", "Practice note: Implement strict copyright due diligence protocols to mitigate risks of substantial infringement damages.", "What to monitor: Track procedural updates toward the 2027 CJEU ruling to inform global AI strategy adjustments."]
core_points_en: The CJEU will define the legal status of large language models under EU copyright law for the first time.
Google Gemini faces allegations of unauthorized news content reproduction, focusing on training data legality.
The ruling will clarify AI output liability and the scope of text and data mining exceptions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review training data sources to ensure compliance with TDM exceptions or obtain explicit authorization.
Practice note: Implement strict copyright due diligence protocols to mitigate risks of substantial infringement damages.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates toward the 2027 CJEU ruling to inform global AI strategy adjustments.

items.title: AMI fails to secure ban on Martin-Baker’s ejection seats for fighter jets
items.title_zh: AMI未能获得马丁-贝克战斗机弹射座椅禁令
items.title_en: AMI Denied Injunction Against Martin-Baker Ejection Seats in UK High Court
items.summary: With increasing armed conflict around the world and rising military spending, military technology is once again coming into the public spotlight. For example, two of the world’s largest manufacturers of ejection seats are currently battling it out at the UK High Court. These modern ejection seats are used in fighter jets to save pilots’ lives […] The post AMI fails to secure ban on Martin-Baker’s ejection seats for fighter jets appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 在全球武装冲突增加及军费上升背景下，两大弹射座椅制造商AMI与马丁-贝克在英国高等法院展开诉讼。AMI试图通过法律手段禁止马丁-贝克的弹射座椅用于战斗机，以保护其市场地位。然而，AMI未能成功获得该禁令。此案件凸显了军事高科技领域的激烈商业竞争及知识产权在其中的关键作用，反映了英国司法机构在处理此类高价值工业专利纠纷时的立场。
items.ai_summary_en: Amidst rising global armed conflicts and increased military spending, military technology has returned to the public spotlight. Two of the world’s largest ejection seat manufacturers are currently engaged in litigation at the UK High Court. These advanced systems are critical for pilot safety in fighter jets. One manufacturer, AMI, sought a legal injunction to prohibit the use of competitor Martin-Baker’s ejection seats in fighter aircraft, aiming to protect its market position through intellectual property enforcement. However, the court denied the request, meaning AMI failed to secure the ban. This case underscores the intense commercial competition within the high-stakes military technology sector and highlights the pivotal role of patent law. It also reflects the stance of the UK judiciary when adjudicating high-value industrial patent disputes involving essential safety components.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国军工及高科技出口企业应密切关注英国高等法院在专利禁令上的裁量标准。在涉及关键安全部件时，法院可能更倾向于维持供应链稳定而非单纯保护专利权人。建议加强FTO分析，并在遭遇海外诉讼时，积极利用公共利益或供应链连续性作为抗辩理由，以规避禁售风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UK High Court's discretionary standards for granting patent injunctions, particularly concerning critical safety components. The judicial approach suggests a preference for maintaining supply chain stability over strictly enforcing patent rights in such sensitive contexts. Rights holders and defendants alike should prioritize robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses before entering new markets. In the event of overseas litigation, leveraging arguments related to public interest and supply chain continuity may serve as effective defenses against injunctive relief. Understanding this balance between IP protection and broader economic or security implications is vital for strategic risk management in high-tech industries.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AMI在英国高等法院申请禁令被驳回，未能阻止竞争对手产品", "案件聚焦于军用战斗机弹射座椅的专利侵权与市场竞争", "全球军费增长加剧了高端军事技术领域的知识产权博弈"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK High Court dismissed AMI's application for an injunction, failing to halt the competitor's product sales.", "The dispute centers on patent infringement claims and fierce market competition regarding military fighter jet ejection seats.", "Rising global defense budgets have intensified intellectual property battles within the high-end military technology sector."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英法院对关键部件禁令的审慎态度及公共利益考量", "强化出口前FTO分析及利用供应链稳定性进行诉讼抗辩"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Courts may prioritize supply chain stability over patent exclusivity for critical safety components.", "Practice note: Strengthen pre-export FTO analysis and consider public interest arguments in litigation defenses.", "What to monitor: The UK judiciary's cautious approach to injunctions and its consideration of broader public interests."]
core_points_en: The UK High Court dismissed AMI's application for an injunction, failing to halt the competitor's product sales.
The dispute centers on patent infringement claims and fierce market competition regarding military fighter jet ejection seats.
Rising global defense budgets have intensified intellectual property battles within the high-end military technology sector.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Courts may prioritize supply chain stability over patent exclusivity for critical safety components.
Practice note: Strengthen pre-export FTO analysis and consider public interest arguments in litigation defenses.
What to monitor: The UK judiciary's cautious approach to injunctions and its consideration of broader public interests.

items.title: Battery recycling moves centre stage: what the surge in circularity innovation means for industry and IP strategy
items.title_zh: 电池回收成焦点：循环创新激增对行业及IP战略的影响
items.title_en: Battery recycling moves centre stage: what the surge in circularity innovation means for industry and IP strategy
items.summary: The global battery sector is entering a new phase. As electric vehicle and energy‑storage deployment accelerates, attention is rapidly shifting from how batteries are made to what happens when they reach end-of-life. According to the latest analysis from the European Patent Office and the International Energy Agency, inventions relating to battery reuse and recycling have increased more than seven‑fold over the last decade – making battery circularity one of the most dynamic areas of battery inn
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着电动汽车和储能部署加速，全球电池行业重心转向寿命终结处理。欧洲专利局与国际能源署数据显示，过去十年电池回收再利用发明增长超七倍，成为最活跃的创新领域。文章指出这不仅是可持续发展议题，更关乎材料安全、工业竞争力及知识产权战略，建议企业调整IP策略以应对循环经济带来的新挑战。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国电池企业应重视欧洲市场回收环节的专利布局，规避侵权风险。鉴于EPO数据表明该领域创新激增，建议加强循环利用技术的研发与专利申请，构建防御性专利组合。同时，关注欧盟相关政策动向，将IP战略融入供应链闭环管理，以提升在欧洲市场的合规性与竞争力。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["电池回收再利用专利发明近十年增长超七倍，创新活力显著增强", "行业关注点从制造转向寿命终结处理，循环经济成为核心竞争领域", "电池回收涉及材料安全与工业竞争力，需重构知识产权战略布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加强电池回收技术专利布局，构建防御性组合以规避侵权风险", "关注欧盟政策动向，将IP战略融入供应链闭环管理提升竞争力"]

items.title: Contentious COVID-19 patents: UPC revokes remdesivir patent
items.title_zh: UPC撤销中国机构瑞德西韦新冠专利
items.title_en: UPC Milan Revokes Remdesivir COVID-19 Patent for Lack of Inventive Step
items.summary: On 4th May, the UPC revoked the ‘remdesivir’ anti-viral drug unitary patent owned by Chinese research institute AMMS.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）米兰中央分部于2025年5月4日撤销了中国AMMS持有的瑞德西韦治疗新冠的单元专利。吉利德科学公司主张该二用途缺乏创造性且公开不充分。法院虽驳回公开不充分抗辩，但认定鉴于SARS-CoV-2基因组已公开及前期研究提示，该用途对技术人员而言显而易见，故缺乏创造性。此判决凸显UPC对医药二用途专利创造性审查趋严。
items.ai_summary_en: On 4 May 2025, the Unified Patent Court’s Central Division in Milan revoked unitary patent EP 3854403, owned by research institute AMMS, covering the use of remdesivir for treating COVID-19. The Court rejected all limitation proposals submitted by the patentee. Gilead Sciences, the original developer of remdesivir, initiated revocation proceedings immediately upon grant, arguing that the claimed second medical use lacked inventive step and sufficiency of disclosure. While the Court dismissed the objection regarding insufficiency, it found the invention obvious to a skilled person at the priority date. This conclusion relied on prior knowledge of SARS-CoV-2 genomic similarities to other coronaviruses and existing preclinical data suggesting efficacy against related viral families. The decision underscores the UPC’s rigorous approach to assessing inventive step for second medical use claims, particularly where speculative applications of known compounds are involved.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局需警惕‘第二医疗用途’专利风险。UPC严格审查创造性，仅凭已知药物对新病毒的推测性应用难以获权。建议加强临床数据积累，确保申请前无公开暗示，并评估自由实施风险，避免盲目抢注引发无效诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking protection for second medical uses must ensure robust clinical data supports their applications, as speculative claims based solely on structural or mechanistic analogies face significant validity risks under UPC jurisprudence. The decision illustrates that mere identification of a new viral target is insufficient if prior art provides a reasonable expectation of success. Companies should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before filing, avoiding reliance on broad class predictions. Strengthening the technical contribution through specific experimental evidence is critical to overcoming obviousness challenges in contentious proceedings involving established therapeutic compounds.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC以缺乏创造性为由撤销中国机构持有的瑞德西韦新冠治疗专利", "法院认定基于已知病毒相似性及前期研究，该用途属技术人员显而易见选择", "判决显示UPC对第二医疗用途专利的创造性门槛要求显著提高"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Milan Central Division revoked AMMS’s remdesivir patent due to lack of inventive step, rejecting all proposed limitations.", "The Court deemed the treatment obvious based on known viral genome similarities and prior preclinical research expectations.", "This ruling highlights the UPC’s heightened threshold for inventive step in second medical use pharmaceutical patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC严格审查二用途创造性，推测性申请易被无效", "药企应强化临床数据支撑，避免依赖已知关联进行抢注"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Second medical use patents require strong empirical data; speculative applications of known drugs are vulnerable to invalidity.", "Practice note: Assess obviousness risks early by evaluating prior art expectations of success for new indications of known compounds.", "What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the evidentiary standards required to prove inventive step for new therapeutic uses."]
core_points_en: The UPC Milan Central Division revoked AMMS’s remdesivir patent due to lack of inventive step, rejecting all proposed limitations.
The Court deemed the treatment obvious based on known viral genome similarities and prior preclinical research expectations.
This ruling highlights the UPC’s heightened threshold for inventive step in second medical use pharmaceutical patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Second medical use patents require strong empirical data; speculative applications of known drugs are vulnerable to invalidity.
Practice note: Assess obviousness risks early by evaluating prior art expectations of success for new indications of known compounds.
What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the evidentiary standards required to prove inventive step for new therapeutic uses.

items.title: New Court, new approaches: How the UPC is reshaping pharmaceutical patent litigation
items.title_zh: 新法院新路径：UPC如何重塑医药专利诉讼
items.title_en: UPC Reshaping Pharma Litigation: Preliminary Injunctions, Bolar Exemption, and Imminent Infringement Standards
items.summary: UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）自2023年启动以来，已发布逾50项医药案件裁决。文章分析指出，UPC通过Boehringer v Zentiva等案例确立了临时禁令的“紧迫性”三要素标准，明确完成医保定价即构成 imminent infringement。同时确认Bolar豁免及CE标志获取的非侵权性质。这些判例为原研药、仿制药及生物类似药制造商的程序与实体争议设定了关键框架，深刻影响未来欧洲医药专利诉讼策略。
items.ai_summary_en: Since its launch in June 2023, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has issued over fifty rulings in pharmaceutical cases, significantly influencing litigation strategies for originators, generics, and biosimilar manufacturers. Key Court of Appeal decisions have established critical procedural standards for preliminary injunctions, particularly regarding urgency and imminent infringement. The Court clarified that completing medical insurance pricing constitutes imminent infringement, while confirming that activities under the Bolar exemption and obtaining CE marks remain non-infringing. These rulings provide a definitive framework for substantive issues, including inventive step analysis and second medical use claims, setting the tone for future European patent enforcement and defense strategies in the pharmaceutical sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企赴欧需警惕UPC对‘即将发生侵权’的宽泛解释，完成医保定价可能触发禁令。建议提前评估Bolar豁免适用性，在研发与注册阶段合规利用豁免条款。同时，应密切关注UPC关于二医疗用途权利要求的最新判例，优化专利布局与FTO分析，以应对更严格的执法环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must carefully assess the UPC’s broad interpretation of imminent infringement, where finalizing medical insurance pricing may trigger preliminary injunctions. Rights holders should strategically plan market entry timelines to mitigate enforcement risks. Conversely, applicants can leverage the confirmed scope of the Bolar exemption to conduct compliant R&D and regulatory preparations without fear of liability. It is also essential to monitor evolving jurisprudence on second medical use claims, as this area remains dynamic. Integrating these insights into freedom-to-operate analyses and patent portfolio management will help navigate the stricter enforcement environment effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC确立临时禁令紧迫性三要素：准备就绪且仅待行动启动即构成侵权风险", "确认获取上市许可及CE标志属Bolar豁免范围，不构成专利侵权", "UPC判例已构建起涵盖程序标准与实体认定的医药专利诉讼新框架"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC established a three-factor test for urgency, ruling that completing medical insurance pricing triggers imminent infringement risks.", "Activities related to obtaining marketing authorizations and CE marks are confirmed as non-infringing under the Bolar exemption.", "UPC case law now provides a comprehensive framework for both procedural standards and substantive patent validity assessments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕UPC将完成医保定价认定为即将发生侵权，提前规划上市节奏", "充分利用Bolar豁免进行合规研发，并关注二医疗用途权利要求的执法动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Finalizing medical insurance pricing may constitute imminent infringement, requiring careful timing of market entry strategies.", "Practice note: Utilize the Bolar exemption for compliant R&D and regulatory activities, ensuring strict adherence to statutory limits.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC precedents on second medical use claims and their impact on patent validity and enforcement outcomes."]
core_points_en: The UPC established a three-factor test for urgency, ruling that completing medical insurance pricing triggers imminent infringement risks.
Activities related to obtaining marketing authorizations and CE marks are confirmed as non-infringing under the Bolar exemption.
UPC case law now provides a comprehensive framework for both procedural standards and substantive patent validity assessments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Finalizing medical insurance pricing may constitute imminent infringement, requiring careful timing of market entry strategies.
Practice note: Utilize the Bolar exemption for compliant R&D and regulatory activities, ensuring strict adherence to statutory limits.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC precedents on second medical use claims and their impact on patent validity and enforcement outcomes.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: VCL HEVC/VVC licensors asked to vote to change pool administrator to Sisvel
items.title_zh: 独家：VCL HEVC/VVC许可方拟投票改选Sisvel为专利池管理者
items.title_en: EXCLUSIVE: VCL HEVC/VVC licensors asked to vote to change pool administrator to Sisvel
items.summary: Patent owners have also been asked to endorse a suspension of royalty reductions for departing licensors, IAM has learned
items.ai_summary_zh: 据IAM Media独家报道，VCL HEVC/VVC专利池的许可方被要求投票，提议将专利池管理员变更为Sisvel。同时，许可方还需批准一项暂停措施，即停止对退出许可方的特许权使用费减免。这一动态反映了视频编码标准专利池管理权的潜在更迭，可能影响现有许可架构及费率结构，是SEP许可市场的重要风向标。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注Sisvel接管后的许可政策变化，特别是费率调整及合规要求。建议提前评估现有HEVC/VVC产品线的许可风险，加强与新管理方的沟通，确保在专利池转换期间的许可连续性，避免因条款变动引发潜在的侵权诉讼或高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["VCL许可方被要求投票变更专利池管理员至Sisvel", "提案包含暂停对退出许可方的特许权使用费减免", "此举可能重塑HEVC/VVC标准必要专利许可格局"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注Sisvel接管后的许可费率及合规政策变动", "评估产品线许可风险并确保转换期许可连续性"]

items.title: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer): when an indefinite trade mark licence can end | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 无限期商标许可非永久：英国法院裁定可凭合理通知终止
items.title_en: UK Courts Rule Indefinite Trademark Licences Are Terminable on Reasonable Notice
items.summary: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer) – An indefinite trade mark licence doesn’t necessarily last forever and the termination of a long-running commercial arrangement doesn’t necessarily require a long period of notice to be “reasonable”.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Zaha Hadid案中裁定，“无限期”商标许可不等于“永久”，允许被许可方在给予合理通知后终止合同。随后枢密院在Budweiser案中进一步指出，合理通知期的长短取决于商业背景而非仅看合作时长。该系列判决澄清了长期商业安排中默示终止权的存在，强调合同解释需符合商业逻辑，打破了无限期许可即不可解除的传统认知。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent decisions by the UK Court of Appeal and the Privy Council clarify that an 'indefinite' trademark licence is not synonymous with a perpetual one. In the Zaha Hadid case, the Court of Appeal held that such licences carry an implied right to terminate upon reasonable notice, even if the contract expressly grants termination rights to only one party. The Privy Council further guided that determining what constitutes 'reasonable' notice depends heavily on the specific commercial context rather than merely the duration of the relationship. These rulings establish that long-running commercial arrangements involving IP rights are not locked in indefinitely without clear contractual language to the contrary.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署长期或无限期商标许可时，切勿默认其不可撤销。建议在合同中明确约定终止条款、通知期限及违约责任，以规避法律不确定性。若面临纠纷，应重点举证商业背景的合理性，利用判例主张默示终止权，避免陷入被动锁定的商业困境。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and licensees, these judgments underscore the importance of precise drafting in long-term IP agreements. Relying on terms like 'indefinite' creates significant uncertainty regarding exit mechanisms. Parties should explicitly define termination rights, notice periods, and post-termination obligations to avoid disputes over implied contractual terms. This approach ensures clarity for portfolio management and reduces litigation risk when commercial relationships evolve or dissolve.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无限期商标许可不等同于永久许可，隐含双方无意永久绑定", "被许可方可依据默示权利，通过给予合理通知终止许可协议", "合理通知期的判定核心在于具体商业背景，而非单纯的合作年限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.", "The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.", "Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["签约时需明确终止机制，避免依赖模糊的无限期条款", "争议中应侧重论证商业背景以确立合理通知期的合法性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.", "Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.", "What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models."]
core_points_en: An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.
The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.
Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.
Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.
What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Same patent, different claim interpretation, different results in UPC and UK
items.title_zh: UPC周报：同一专利在UPC与英国因权利要求解释不同致结果迥异
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki vs UK Courts: Divergent Claim Interpretation Leads to Opposite Infringement Outcomes
items.summary: Matthew Naylor compares the UPC Helsinki LD approach to claim interpretation with the UK Court of Appeal, with the different courts reaching starkly different views.
items.ai_summary_zh: AIM Sport与Supponor就EP3295663 B1专利侵权在德国、英国及统一专利法院（UPC）展开诉讼。德国慕尼黑高等地区法院二审推翻一审侵权认定；英国上诉法院维持侵权但要求修改专利以确保持续有效；UPC赫尔辛基地方分部则认定专利有效但不侵权。文章重点对比了UPC与英国法院在解释独立方法权利要求12时的不同路径，指出UPC采用更严格的字面解释导致不侵权结论，而英国法院通过等同原则或不同解释框架认定侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: AIM Sport and Supponor (now TGI Sport) are engaged in multi-jurisdictional litigation concerning EP 3295663 B1. While German courts reversed an initial infringement finding, the UK Court of Appeal upheld infringement after requiring patent amendments for validity. Conversely, the UPC Helsinki Local Division ruled the patent valid but not infringed. This analysis contrasts the UPC’s strict literal interpretation of independent method claim 12 with the UK courts’ approach, which relied on different interpretive frameworks or equivalents. The divergent outcomes highlight significant judicial inconsistency regarding claim construction across European jurisdictions, creating uncertainty for rights holders seeking consistent enforcement strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局需警惕UPC与成员国法院在权利要求解释上的差异。UPC倾向于严格字面解释，可能缩小保护范围。建议申请时撰写多套权利要求以备不同法域，并在诉讼中针对性准备解释策略，避免依赖单一法域的胜诉经验。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe must anticipate potential discrepancies between UPC and national court interpretations of patent claims. The UPC’s tendency toward strict literal construction may narrow the effective scope of protection compared to broader national approaches. Consequently, applicants should consider drafting layered claim sets during prosecution to accommodate varying jurisdictional standards. Litigation strategies must be tailored to each forum, avoiding reliance on precedents from single jurisdictions. Proactive alignment of claim language with anticipated judicial scrutiny can mitigate risks associated with inconsistent enforcement outcomes across the unified patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC赫尔辛基分部认定涉案专利有效但未构成侵权，与英国法院判决形成鲜明对比", "争议焦点在于对权利要求12中特定技术特征的解释，UPC采取严格字面解释方法", "德国、英国与UPC对同一专利的效力与侵权认定呈现显著司法分歧，增加维权不确定性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Helsinki Local Division found the patent valid but not infringed, contrasting sharply with the UK Court of Appeal's decision.", "The dispute centers on interpreting specific technical features in claim 12, where the UPC applied a strict literal interpretation.", "Divergent rulings in Germany, the UK, and the UPC demonstrate significant judicial fragmentation affecting patent enforcement certainty."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC严格字面解释可能缩小专利保护范围，企业需调整欧洲维权预期", "建议在专利申请阶段构建多层次权利要求体系，以应对不同法域的审查标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict UPC literal interpretation may reduce patent scope, requiring adjusted enforcement expectations.", "Practice note: Build multi-tiered claim structures during filing to address diverse examination and litigation standards.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law on claim construction differences between the UPC and key member state courts."]
core_points_en: The UPC Helsinki Local Division found the patent valid but not infringed, contrasting sharply with the UK Court of Appeal's decision.
The dispute centers on interpreting specific technical features in claim 12, where the UPC applied a strict literal interpretation.
Divergent rulings in Germany, the UK, and the UPC demonstrate significant judicial fragmentation affecting patent enforcement certainty.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict UPC literal interpretation may reduce patent scope, requiring adjusted enforcement expectations.
Practice note: Build multi-tiered claim structures during filing to address diverse examination and litigation standards.
What to monitor: Evolving case law on claim construction differences between the UPC and key member state courts.

items.title: Inside INTA London and the IP ‘carnival’
items.title_zh: INTA伦敦年会回顾：全球IP界的狂欢盛会
items.title_en: INTA Annual Meeting Returns to London: Highlights from the Global IP Community
items.summary: Against a backdrop of rooftop receptions, hot-topic debates, hectic networking, and cross-city navigation, WIPR brings you highlights and key talking points from the world’s largest IP conference.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权评论（WIPR）回顾了在国际商标协会（INTA）于伦敦举办的年度会议。这是该会议时隔35年重返英国首都，吸引了来自180个国家的逾万名代表参加。尽管面临交通拥堵等挑战，与会者仍积极参与屋顶招待会及社交活动。文章强调了此次会议作为全球最大IP盛会的地位，突出了伦敦作为举办地的吸引力以及业界在商标领域的高热度讨论和网络交流机会。
items.ai_summary_en: The International Trademark Association (INTA) held its annual meeting in London for the first time in over 35 years, drawing more than 10,000 delegates from 180 countries. The five-day event featured intense networking, rooftop receptions, and debates on current trademark issues. Despite logistical challenges including transport disruptions on the Elizabeth line and DLR, attendees engaged actively with the city's historic venues. The conference reinforced London's status as a key hub for global intellectual property professionals, combining high-level industry discourse with significant social interaction across Soho, Mayfair, and the City.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For European and US rights holders, this gathering serves as a critical barometer for global brand protection trends and enforcement priorities. The convergence of such a large international cohort facilitates direct exchange on cross-border compliance and emerging regulatory pressures. While specific policy outcomes are not detailed here, the scale of participation underscores the importance of maintaining active engagement with major international forums to stay aligned with evolving global IP standards and peer practices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INTA年会时隔35年重返伦敦，吸引180国逾万名代表参会", "会议涵盖商标热点辩论与高强度社交，展现行业活力", "尽管交通受阻，伦敦地标与社交活动仍极大提升了参会体验"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INTA's annual meeting returned to London after 35 years, attracting over 10,000 delegates from 180 countries.", "The event combined hot-topic trademark debates with extensive networking at venues across central London.", "Transport disruptions did not dampen attendee enthusiasm for the conference and associated social activities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用顶级平台洞察欧美商标政策风向，优化海外布局", "拓展国际人脉以应对跨境侵权，提升品牌全球合规力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Large-scale international conferences provide vital context for global brand strategy and cross-border compliance alignment.", "Practice note: Engaging with diverse jurisdictions at major events helps identify emerging enforcement trends and regulatory shifts.", "What to monitor: Future INTA announcements and panel discussions may signal changes in global trademark examination or enforcement norms."]

The event combined hot-topic trademark debates with extensive networking at venues across central London.

items.title: Droit d’auteur et IA : vers une présomption de culpabilité ?
items.title_zh: 法国AI版权新规：或引入有罪推定？
items.title_en: France AI Copyright Proposal: Shifting Burden of Proof to LLM Providers
items.summary: Le débat sur l'encadrement juridique de l'intelligence artificielle en France franchit une nouvelle étape avec une proposition sénatoriale visant à instaurer une présomption d'exploitation des œuvres protégées par les fournisseurs de modèles de langage.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国参议员提案拟建立LLM供应商使用受保护作品的推定机制，引发版权与AI监管新 debate。国务委员会认为该措施合宪且符合欧盟法，但需调整。核心在于举证责任倒置，由AI供应商证明未侵权或合规，以平衡权利人与技术方的信息不对称。然而，此举可能与欧盟DAMUN指令的数据挖掘例外冲突，且“疑似证据”定义模糊，存在违宪风险及执行复杂性。
items.ai_summary_en: A French senatorial proposal seeks to establish a presumption that Large Language Model providers exploit protected works, marking a potential shift in copyright regulation for AI. The Conseil d'État recently validated the legislature's competence to enact this measure, deeming it constitutionally and EU-compliant subject to specific adjustments. The core mechanism reverses the burden of proof: if indicators suggest AI usage of protected content, providers must prove they did not use such data or complied with applicable rules. This rebuttable presumption aims to address information asymmetry between rights holders and tech firms, who control training data. However, the proposal raises compatibility concerns with the EU DSM Directive’s text and data mining exceptions, creating legal uncertainty regarding enforcement and definitions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI企业出海欧洲需警惕法国潜在的严格版权合规要求。建议完善训练数据溯源体系，保留合法授权证据，以应对可能的举证责任倒置。同时密切关注欧盟层面立法动态，评估DAMUN指令适用空间，避免因地方法规差异导致合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI models in Europe should anticipate stricter procedural hurdles in France. The proposed reversal of the burden of proof necessitates robust internal compliance frameworks. Companies must implement comprehensive data lineage tracking systems to document training data sources and secure explicit licensing agreements where required. Reliance on broad statutory exceptions may become insufficient if local presumptions of infringement are adopted. Stakeholders should closely monitor the final legislative text and parallel developments at the EU level regarding the Digital Single Market Directive. Proactive preparation is essential to mitigate litigation risks arising from potential conflicts between national procedural innovations and harmonized European copyright standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国提案拟对LLM供应商建立版权侵权推定，实行举证责任倒置", "国务委员会认可立法权限，认为不违宪及欧盟法，但需具体调整", "措施旨在平衡信息不对称，但可能冲击欧盟DAMUN指令数据挖掘例外"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["French senators propose a rebuttable presumption that LLM providers exploit protected works, reversing the standard burden of proof in copyright disputes.", "The Conseil d'État confirmed legislative competence, finding the measure compliant with constitutional and EU law principles, pending necessary technical adjustments.", "The initiative targets information asymmetry but risks conflicting with the EU DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["完善训练数据溯源，保留合法授权证据以应对举证责任倒置", "关注欧盟DAMUN指令适用，规避地方法规差异带来的合规风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish rigorous data provenance systems and retain valid licensing evidence to counter potential presumptions of unauthorized use.", "Practice note: Assess current training datasets against emerging French procedural standards to ensure defensibility under reversed burden of proof regimes.", "What to monitor: Track the interplay between French national proposals and EU-level interpretations of the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions."]
core_points_en: French senators propose a rebuttable presumption that LLM providers exploit protected works, reversing the standard burden of proof in copyright disputes.
The Conseil d'État confirmed legislative competence, finding the measure compliant with constitutional and EU law principles, pending necessary technical adjustments.
The initiative targets information asymmetry but risks conflicting with the EU DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish rigorous data provenance systems and retain valid licensing evidence to counter potential presumptions of unauthorized use.
Practice note: Assess current training datasets against emerging French procedural standards to ensure defensibility under reversed burden of proof regimes.
What to monitor: Track the interplay between French national proposals and EU-level interpretations of the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.

items.title: G 1/25 “Hydroponics”: Enlarged Board hears challenge to EPO description amendment practice
items.title_zh: G 1/25案：扩大上诉委员会审理挑战EPO说明书修改惯例
items.title_en: G 1/25 Hydroponics: Enlarged Board Hears Challenge to EPO Description Amendment Practice
items.summary: Users of the European patent system will be familiar with the EPO’s long-standing practice of requiring the description to be brought into line with amended claims, both before grant and in post-grant proceedings.
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会于5月8日就G 1/25案举行口头听证，核心争议在于《欧洲专利公约》是否强制要求申请人在权利要求修改后调整说明书以保持一致性。该问题源于技术上诉委员会判例分歧及2021年EPO强化删除或标记非保护实施例的政策。案件背景T 697/22中，因说明书与修改后权利要求不一致且程序上无法进一步修正，引发关于第84条支持要求的法律质疑。此举旨在澄清长期存在的法律基础争议及操作不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 8, the EPO’s Enlarged Board of Appeal held oral proceedings in case G 1/25. The central issue is whether the European Patent Convention mandates that applicants adapt the description to align with amended claims. This referral stems from diverging case law among Technical Boards of Appeal and the EPO’s 2021 policy strengthening requirements to delete or prominently mark embodiments no longer covered by claims. In the underlying case T 697/22, the patentee amended claims during opposition appeal. Although these claims met requirements for added matter, sufficiency, novelty, and inventive step, the Board found inconsistencies with the description. Since further procedural amendment was impossible, the Board questioned the legal basis under Article 84 EPC. This referral aims to clarify the long-standing controversy regarding the legal foundation and operational uncertainty of this administrative practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注G 1/25裁决结果。若维持现状，在欧专利申请及异议程序中需投入更多资源进行说明书精细化修改，以符合“ prominently ”标记或删除要求，避免程序风险。建议优化内部撰写规范，提前规划实施例布局，降低后续合规成本与确权不确定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should closely monitor the outcome of G 1/25, as it will define the compliance burden for European patent applications and opposition proceedings. If the current strict practice is upheld, rights holders must allocate more resources to precisely amend descriptions, ensuring non-covered embodiments are deleted or prominently marked to avoid procedural risks. It is advisable to optimize internal drafting standards and strategically plan embodiment layouts early in the prosecution process. These measures can help reduce future compliance costs and minimize uncertainties related to patent validation and enforcement across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会审理EPO是否强制要求修改权利要求后同步调整说明书", "争议源于2021年政策强化及不同技术上诉委员会间的判例法律基础分歧", "焦点在于审查《欧洲专利公约》条款是否为该行政实践提供明确法律依据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Enlarged Board examines if the EPC strictly requires adapting the description after claim amendments are made.", "Controversy arises from the 2021 policy shift and conflicting legal foundations cited by different Technical Boards of Appeal.", "The focus is on determining if specific EPC provisions provide a clear legal basis for this established administrative practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注裁决结果，评估对欧专利申请及异议程序中说明书修改成本的潜在影响", "优化内部撰写规范，提前规划实施例布局，以降低合规成本与确权风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the potential impact on description amendment costs in European patent applications and opposition proceedings.", "Practice note: Optimize internal drafting standards and plan embodiment layouts early to lower compliance costs and validation risks.", "What to monitor: Watch for the final decision in G 1/25 to adjust prosecution strategies regarding description consistency requirements."]
core_points_en: The Enlarged Board examines if the EPC strictly requires adapting the description after claim amendments are made.
Controversy arises from the 2021 policy shift and conflicting legal foundations cited by different Technical Boards of Appeal.
The focus is on determining if specific EPC provisions provide a clear legal basis for this established administrative practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the potential impact on description amendment costs in European patent applications and opposition proceedings.
Practice note: Optimize internal drafting standards and plan embodiment layouts early to lower compliance costs and validation risks.
What to monitor: Watch for the final decision in G 1/25 to adjust prosecution strategies regarding description consistency requirements.

items.title: AI chatbot output as "evidence" at the EPO: emerging trends in case law - GJE
items.title_zh: EPO中AI聊天机器人输出作为证据：新兴判例趋势
items.title_en: AI chatbot output as "evidence" at the EPO: emerging trends in case law - GJE
items.summary: This article looks at emerging themes from recent decisions of the Boards of Appeal to answer this question.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着大型语言模型普及，当事方开始在EPO程序中引用ChatGPT等AI生成内容支持论点。本文分析EPO上诉委员会近期决定，探讨AI生成内容在EPO程序中的证据地位及可接受性。文章指出需明确AI输出作为证据的边界与标准，反映了知识产权实务界对新技术介入法律程序的密切关注与规范需求。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利诉讼中应谨慎使用AI工具辅助论证，避免直接将其作为独立证据提交。建议建立内部审核机制，确保所有基于AI生成的技术或法律观点均有传统文献支撑，以符合EPO对证据真实性和可验证性的严格要求，规避程序风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["LLM普及促使当事方在EPO程序中引用AI生成内容支持其主张", "文章聚焦EPO上诉委员会近期决定，分析AI内容的证据效力", "探讨AI生成内容在EPO程序中被采纳为证据的具体范围与限制"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["慎用AI生成内容作为独立证据，需有传统文献支撑", "建立内部审核机制，确保证据符合EPO真实性要求"]

items.title: Genentech, TD Bank innovation leaders reveal why they back AI defensive patent organisation
items.title_zh: Genentech与TD银行揭秘支持AI专利防御组织缘由
items.title_en: Genentech and TD Bank Back AI Defensive Patent Group to Secure Foundation Model Freedom to Operate
items.summary: As AI becomes critical to pharma and banking, Genentech and TD Bank explain why freedom to operate on foundation models now matters far beyond Silicon Valley
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着AI在制药和银行业的关键作用日益凸显，Genentech和TD银行解释了为何基础模型的自由实施权（FTO）已超越硅谷范畴变得至关重要。两家公司作为创新领导者，公开支持AI防御性专利组织，旨在通过集体行动降低侵权风险并促进技术共享。这一动态反映了非科技巨头在传统行业中对AI知识产权布局的重视，强调在全球范围内构建安全运营环境的必要性。
items.ai_summary_en: As artificial intelligence becomes strategically critical in the pharmaceutical and banking sectors, major industry players Genentech and TD Bank have publicly explained their support for an AI defensive patent organization. These innovation leaders emphasize that freedom to operate regarding foundation models is no longer a concern limited to Silicon Valley technology firms. By joining this collective initiative, companies aim to mitigate infringement risks through shared defensive resources and promote broader technology sharing. This development highlights a significant shift where traditional industry giants are actively prioritizing global AI intellectual property strategies. It underscores the growing necessity for non-tech enterprises to establish secure operational environments worldwide, ensuring they can safely deploy AI technologies without facing disruptive litigation or access barriers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展AI应用时，需警惕传统行业的专利壁垒。建议关注防御性专利池动态，评估基础模型FTO风险，并通过加入相关组织或获取许可来保障市场准入，避免陷入被动诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI applications within European jurisdictions should remain vigilant regarding emerging patent barriers in traditional industries like healthcare and finance. It is advisable to closely monitor developments in defensive patent pools and conduct thorough assessments of freedom to operate risks associated with foundational AI models. Securing market access and avoiding passive litigation exposure may require joining relevant defensive organizations or obtaining necessary licenses. Proactive management of these IP dynamics is essential for maintaining operational continuity and competitive advantage in increasingly regulated AI landscapes across Europe.

items.title: G1/25 hearing: turning point or status quo?
items.title_zh: G1/25听证：转折点还是维持现状？
items.title_en: EPO G1/25 Hearing: Parties Oppose Description Adaptation Requirement
items.summary: G 1/25 addresses a particularly controversial feature of EPO practice: the requirement to adapt the description. The long-anticipated hearing on G 1/25 took place on 8 May 2026, and unfolded in a manner rarely seen before the Enlarged Board of Appeal – the patent proprietor and the opponent presenting somewhat aligned positions, both opposing the preliminary opinion.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会于2026年5月8日举行G1/25听证，聚焦异议程序中权利要求修改后是否必须相应修改说明书以消除不一致。专利权人与异议人罕见地联合反对初步意见，主张EPC无此强制规定。尽管双方提出法律解释及国际协调论据，但扩委会倾向于维持要求修改说明书的现行实践。本案将决定审查与异议阶段说明书适应性的法律义务边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Enlarged Board of Appeal held a hearing on 8 May 2026 regarding G 1/25, addressing the controversial EPO practice requiring description adaptation. Unusually, both the patent proprietor and opponent aligned against the preliminary opinion, arguing that the European Patent Convention does not mandate such adaptation. The proprietor cited the lack of explicit EPC provisions, while the opponent highlighted global harmonization concerns and Article 84 EPC implications. Despite these arguments, the Enlarged Board’s provisional view suggests it may uphold the current requirement to adapt descriptions in both prosecution and opposition proceedings to resolve inconsistencies with amended claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 若EPO维持强制修改说明书的实践，中国申请人在欧维权成本将增加。建议企业在答复审查意见或应对异议时，提前规划说明书修改策略，确保权利要求与描述严格一致，避免因形式缺陷导致权利不稳定或额外费用。同时需关注最终裁决对全球专利实务的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral clarifies whether description adaptation is legally mandatory under the EPC or merely administrative practice. A confirmation of the status quo would solidify the current procedural burden on applicants and proprietors to ensure strict consistency between claims and description, impacting portfolio management strategies across examination and opposition stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G1/25听证探讨异议中权利要求修改后是否需强制适配说明书", "当事人联合反对初步意见，主张EPC未规定此类强制性适应义务", "扩委会倾向维持现行实践，要求审查及异议中消除描述不一致"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.", "The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.", "The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需强化说明书与权利要求的一致性管理以规避合规风险", "应预判程序成本上升并优化在欧专利申请与答辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.", "Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.", "What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation."]
core_points_en: Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.
The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.
The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.
Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.
What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation.

items.title: Dupe culture is booming, but can trademark law keep pace?
items.title_zh: 仿冒文化盛行，商标法能否跟上步伐？
items.title_en: Dupe Culture Goes Mainstream: Can Trademark Law Effectively Curb Imitation Trends?
items.summary: Dupe culture is no longer on the fringes. FIFA’s senior counsel joins Alo’s in-house counsel and other brand protection experts to explore how rights owners can tackle the trend.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文聚焦‘仿冒文化’（Dupe culture）从边缘走向主流的趋势，指出其对品牌权益构成的挑战。文章汇集了国际足联高级法律顾问、Alo内部律师及多位品牌保护专家的观点，深入探讨权利人如何有效应对这一现象。核心议题在于现行商标法律框架是否足以遏制模仿行为，以及企业应采取何种策略来维护品牌价值与市场地位，反映了行业对新型侵权形态的关注。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the shift of 'dupe culture' from a niche phenomenon to a mainstream market force, highlighting the significant challenges it poses for brand protection. It features insights from FIFA’s senior counsel, Alo’s in-house legal team, and other industry experts on how rights holders can strategically address this trend. The discussion centers on whether current trademark frameworks are sufficient to deter widespread imitation and what measures companies should adopt to safeguard their brand value and market position. This reflects growing industry concern regarding new forms of infringement that blur the lines between legitimate competition and unauthorized copying.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国品牌需警惕‘高仿’与‘平替’的法律边界，避免陷入商标侵权诉讼。建议加强品牌独特性设计，利用反不正当竞争法补充保护。同时，应密切关注欧盟执法动态，建立快速响应机制，以应对仿冒文化带来的市场混淆风险，确保合规经营。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must carefully navigate the legal boundaries between acceptable 'dupes' and infringing counterfeits to mitigate litigation risks. Strengthening distinctive brand elements and leveraging unfair competition laws can provide supplementary protection where trademark law may fall short. Companies should also monitor enforcement developments within the European Union closely. Establishing rapid response mechanisms is essential to address market confusion caused by dupe culture effectively. Proactive compliance and strategic brand differentiation remain key to maintaining competitive advantage and protecting intellectual property assets in this evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仿冒文化已非边缘现象，成为品牌保护面临的主流挑战", "多方专家探讨权利人应对仿冒趋势的策略与法律适用性", "质疑现有商标法体系能否有效遏制日益普遍的模仿行为"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Dupe culture has evolved into a mainstream challenge for brand protection strategies globally.", "Experts discuss strategic responses and the applicability of existing laws to curb imitation trends.", "Questions arise regarding the effectiveness of current trademark systems against pervasive copying."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕仿冒文化法律边界，避免陷入商标侵权诉讼风险", "加强品牌独特性设计，利用反不正当竞争法补充保护"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Navigate legal boundaries between dupes and counterfeits to avoid infringement litigation risks.", "Practice note: Enhance brand distinctiveness and utilize unfair competition laws for supplementary protection.", "What to monitor: EU enforcement dynamics and establish rapid response mechanisms for market confusion."]
core_points_en: Dupe culture has evolved into a mainstream challenge for brand protection strategies globally.
Experts discuss strategic responses and the applicability of existing laws to curb imitation trends.
Questions arise regarding the effectiveness of current trademark systems against pervasive copying.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Navigate legal boundaries between dupes and counterfeits to avoid infringement litigation risks.
Practice note: Enhance brand distinctiveness and utilize unfair competition laws for supplementary protection.
What to monitor: EU enforcement dynamics and establish rapid response mechanisms for market confusion.

items.title: Colour as a trademark: Lessons from Mondelēz
items.title_zh: 颜色商标：亿滋国际的经验教训
items.title_en: Colour Trademarks: Strategic Lessons from Mondelēz on Protection and Enforcement
items.summary: At INTA’s Annual Meeting in London, counsel from the parent company of Cadbury and Milka shared why colour marks remain among the most commercially valuable, and legally complex, forms of IP protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 在伦敦举行的INTA年会上，亿滋国际（卡夫亨氏母公司）法律顾问分享了关于颜色商标的见解。尽管颜色商标具有极高的商业价值，但其法律保护路径复杂且充满挑战。会议强调了在欧盟和英国司法管辖区内，维持颜色商标注册的有效性需要严格的证据支持和持续的市场使用证明，以应对潜在的无效宣告风险。
items.ai_summary_en: At the International Trademark Association’s Annual Meeting in London, legal counsel from Mondelēz International, the parent company of Cadbury and Milka, provided critical insights into the strategic management of colour trademarks. While these marks offer immense commercial value by serving as powerful brand identifiers, they present significant legal complexities regarding registration and maintenance. The discussion highlighted that securing and defending colour rights within the European Union and United Kingdom requires rigorous evidentiary support. Rights holders must demonstrate consistent market use to establish acquired distinctiveness. Without such proof, registrations remain vulnerable to invalidation actions based on a lack of inherent distinctiveness or failure to function as a trademark.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业若依赖单一颜色作为品牌标识，需在欧英市场建立严格的使用证据留存机制。鉴于颜色商标易受攻击，建议结合图形或文字商标构建组合保护体系，降低因缺乏显著性而被撤销的风险，确保品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: For brands relying on single colours as primary identifiers, establishing robust mechanisms to document market usage is essential in the EU and UK markets. Given the susceptibility of colour marks to cancellation due to alleged lack of distinctiveness, practitioners should advise clients to integrate colour elements within broader combination marks featuring text or graphics. This layered approach mitigates the risk of total rights loss if the standalone colour registration is challenged. Continuous monitoring of enforcement trends and judicial standards for acquired distinctiveness remains critical for safeguarding brand assets effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["颜色商标虽具高商业价值，但面临复杂的法律确权与维护挑战", "需通过持续使用证据巩固权利，以应对欧盟及英国的无效宣告风险", "企业应制定长期策略，确保证据链完整以维持颜色商标的独占性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Colour trademarks provide high commercial value but face complex legal hurdles for initial registration and ongoing maintenance.", "Maintaining validity in EU and UK jurisdictions demands strict evidence of continuous use to counter potential invalidation risks.", "Companies must implement long-term strategies ensuring complete evidentiary chains to preserve exclusive rights over colour marks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格的使用证据留存机制，应对颜色商标确权挑战", "构建组合保护体系，降低单一颜色商标被撤销的法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implement strict evidence retention protocols to address the high burden of proving acquired distinctiveness for colour marks.", "Practice note: Construct combined protection systems using graphic or word elements to reduce the legal risk of cancelling standalone colour registrations.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards in the EU and UK regarding the threshold for distinctiveness required to maintain colour trademark validity."]
core_points_en: Colour trademarks provide high commercial value but face complex legal hurdles for initial registration and ongoing maintenance.
Maintaining validity in EU and UK jurisdictions demands strict evidence of continuous use to counter potential invalidation risks.
Companies must implement long-term strategies ensuring complete evidentiary chains to preserve exclusive rights over colour marks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implement strict evidence retention protocols to address the high burden of proving acquired distinctiveness for colour marks.
Practice note: Construct combined protection systems using graphic or word elements to reduce the legal risk of cancelling standalone colour registrations.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards in the EU and UK regarding the threshold for distinctiveness required to maintain colour trademark validity.
