items.title: DIAMS Infinity Redefines How Intellectual Property Is Managed In Practice
items.title_zh: Dennemeyer推出DIAMS Infinity：AI重塑IP管理实务
items.title_en: Dennemeyer Launches DIAMS Infinity: AI-Driven Automation Reshapes IP Portfolio Management
items.summary: The Dennemeyer Group introduces DIAMS Infinity, a next-generation IP management software that leverages artificial intelligence to automate repetitive tasks and streamline portfolio management. How does this cloud-based solution address the operational complexity and manual coordination challenges that have long plagued IP professionals managing expanding portfolios?
items.ai_summary_zh: Dennemeyer集团发布新一代IP管理软件DIAMS Infinity，旨在解决企业日益复杂的组合管理挑战。该软件利用人工智能自动化处理重复性任务，如记录更新和期限跟踪，从而减少人为错误并提高效率。通过内置智能助手，系统能实时情境化数据，释放专业人员时间以专注于高价值战略工作。此举标志着IP管理从手动协调向智能化执行转变，帮助企业在不增加运营复杂性的情况下实现更高效、一致的合规与决策。
items.ai_summary_en: The Dennemeyer Group has introduced DIAMS Infinity, a next-generation intellectual property management software designed to address the growing complexity of expanding IP portfolios. By leveraging artificial intelligence, the platform automates repetitive administrative tasks such as record updates and deadline tracking, significantly reducing manual coordination efforts and minimizing human error. The system utilizes built-in intelligent assistants to contextualize data in real time, ensuring information consistency across fragmented databases. This shift from manual oversight to automated execution allows IP professionals to reclaim time previously spent on routine administration. Consequently, teams can focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making. The solution aims to enhance operational efficiency and compliance without adding structural complexity, offering organizations greater control and speed in managing their global intellectual property assets effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应关注此类AI IP管理工具的应用，以提升全球组合管理的效率与准确性。引入自动化工具可降低因人为疏忽导致的期限失误风险，优化运营成本。建议评估现有IP管理系统，考虑集成AI功能以应对日益增长的海外专利维护压力，确保在欧业务的合规性与竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants managing global intellectual property portfolios should evaluate AI-driven management tools to enhance operational efficiency and accuracy. Integrating automation helps mitigate compliance risks stemming from manual errors, particularly regarding critical deadlines and filings in multiple jurisdictions. By reducing administrative burdens, organizations can optimize costs and redirect resources toward strategic business support. Companies operating in Europe should assess their current IP management systems for potential AI integration to handle increasing maintenance pressures. Adopting such technologies ensures consistent compliance and strengthens competitive positioning by allowing legal teams to focus on value-added activities rather than routine coordination tasks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Dennemeyer发布AI驱动的DIAMS Infinity，自动化处理IP管理中的…", "软件通过内置智能助手情境化数据，减少碎片化输入风险，提升决策速度与一致性", "旨在将IP团队从繁琐的日常协调中解放，使其聚焦于高价值的战略性知识产权工作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["DIAMS Infinity automates repetitive IP management tasks like record updates and deadline tracking using integrated artificial intelligence capabilities.", "Built-in smart assistants contextualize data in real time, reducing risks associated with fragmented inputs and improving decision-making speed.", "The platform shifts workflows from manual coordination to automated execution, enabling teams to prioritize high-value strategic intellectual property work."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国企业应评估引入AI IP管理工具，以提升全球组合管理效率并降低合规风险", "利用自动化技术释放人力，使IP团队更专注于支持业务发展的战略性高价值工作"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate AI-driven IP management tools to improve global portfolio efficiency, reduce compliance risks, and optimize operational costs.", "Practice note: Integrate automation to minimize human error in deadline tracking, freeing IP teams to focus on strategic business support and high-value analysis.", "What to monitor: Assess existing IP systems for AI integration capabilities to handle growing overseas patent maintenance pressures and ensure European compliance."]
core_points_en: DIAMS Infinity automates repetitive IP management tasks like record updates and deadline tracking using integrated artificial intelligence capabilities.
Built-in smart assistants contextualize data in real time, reducing risks associated with fragmented inputs and improving decision-making speed.
The platform shifts workflows from manual coordination to automated execution, enabling teams to prioritize high-value strategic intellectual property work.

items.title: IPEC insights: Prosecco PDO Consortium v Prosecco International -…
items.title_zh: IPEC驳回Prosecco国际公司驳回起诉申请
items.title_en: IPEC Dismisses Strike-Out Bid in Prosecco PDO Infringement Case Against UK Spirits Firm
items.summary: The IPEC has dismissed a strike-out bid by a British spirits company accused of misusing the protected Prosecco name, allowing the case to proceed to…
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利Prosecco PDO保护协会在英国IPEC起诉Prosecco International Ltd滥用受保护的“Prosecco”名称。被告申请驳回起诉被拒，案件将进入审判阶段。原告指控被告在不符合PDO规范的饮料、投资合作及公司名称中使用该标识，侵犯欧盟原产地名称保护法规（已纳入英国法）及英国证明商标。此案涉及管辖权、平行诉讼及PDO保护范围等关键法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: The Intellectual Property Enterprise Court (IPEC) has dismissed a strike-out application by Prosecco International Ltd, allowing the Consorzio Di Tutela Della Denominazione Di Origine Controllata Prosecco to proceed with its infringement claim. The Consorzio alleges that the defendant misused the protected 'Prosecco' name on websites and in corporate branding for products and services not complying with the Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) specification. The case involves claims under assimilated EU regulations and the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification mark infringement. This procedural decision addresses jurisdictional issues and parallel proceedings, confirming that the matter will advance to trial.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国酒企出口英国需严格审查品牌命名，避免使用受保护地理标志。即使产品未直接销售，若网站提供投资或合伙机会并关联GI名称，亦可能构成侵权。建议提前进行FTO检索，确保营销材料符合UK assimilated law要求，规避高额诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the continued robust protection of geographical indications in the UK post-Brexit, as EU PDO regulations have been assimilated into domestic law. Rights holders can enforce these protections alongside certification mark rights against entities using GI terms for unrelated commercial activities or non-compliant products. For market participants, this underscores the importance of ensuring that brand names and digital marketing strategies do not infringe upon established PDOs, even when offering services rather than physical goods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPEC驳回被告驳回起诉申请，案件正式进入审判程序", "原告主张被告违反EU PDO法规及英国1994年商标法证明商标条款", "争议焦点包括域名使用、非合规产品标注及公司名称侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The IPEC rejected the defendant's bid to strike out the claim, permitting the litigation concerning alleged misuse of the Prosecco PDO to proceed to trial.", "Claims rely on assimilated EU regulations protecting designations of origin and sections 10(2) and 10(3) of the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification marks.", "Alleged infringements include using PROSECCO on non-compliant drinks, investment opportunities, company names, and specific domain names like prosecco.com."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口英国需警惕受保护地理标志，避免品牌命名侵权", "网络营销关联GI名称即便无实体销售也可能构成侵权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must ensure brand naming and digital content avoid unauthorized use of protected geographical indications to prevent infringement liability.", "Practice note: Enforcement actions may combine assimilated EU PDO regulations with national trade mark laws, including certification mark provisions under the Trade Marks Act 1994.", "What to monitor: The upcoming trial outcome will clarify the scope of PDO protection regarding online activities, investment offers, and corporate naming conventions in the UK."]
core_points_en: The IPEC rejected the defendant's bid to strike out the claim, permitting the litigation concerning alleged misuse of the Prosecco PDO to proceed to trial.
Claims rely on assimilated EU regulations protecting designations of origin and sections 10(2) and 10(3) of the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification marks.
Alleged infringements include using PROSECCO on non-compliant drinks, investment opportunities, company names, and specific domain names like prosecco.com.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must ensure brand naming and digital content avoid unauthorized use of protected geographical indications to prevent infringement liability.
Practice note: Enforcement actions may combine assimilated EU PDO regulations with national trade mark laws, including certification mark provisions under the Trade Marks Act 1994.
What to monitor: The upcoming trial outcome will clarify the scope of PDO protection regarding online activities, investment offers, and corporate naming conventions in the UK.

items.title: IAM’s No 1 Market Maker and his push to simplify video codec licensing
items.title_zh: IAM头号市场缔造者推动简化视频编解码许可
items.title_en: Access Advance CEO Drives Simplification in Video Codec Licensing Markets
items.summary: Long Read: How CEO Peter Moller turned Access Advance into a consequential force in video codec licensing – and why the firm’s moves last year could reshape the market
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文深入报道Access Advance CEO Peter Moller如何将该公司打造为视频编解码许可领域的重要力量。文章分析了其简化许可流程的策略及去年举措对市场的潜在重塑作用。作为行业深度评论，它揭示了专利池运营的新趋势，强调通过高效、透明的许可机制降低交易成本，对全球视频技术产业链产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: This long read examines how Access Advance, under the leadership of CEO Peter Moller, has emerged as a significant player in the video codec licensing landscape. The article analyzes the company's strategic focus on simplifying licensing procedures to reduce transaction costs for industry participants. It highlights how recent market moves by Access Advance could fundamentally reshape the global video technology sector. By promoting efficient and transparent licensing mechanisms, the firm aims to streamline access to essential patents. This analysis provides critical context for understanding emerging trends in patent pool operations and their potential impact on the broader supply chain and intellectual property ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注Access Advance等新兴专利池的动态，其简化许可模式可能改变传统FRAND谈判生态。建议出口企业提前评估相关视频编解码专利风险，建立灵活的许可应对机制，避免因忽视新兴许可主体而面临供应链中断或高额索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolution of emerging patent pools like Access Advance, as their simplified licensing models challenge traditional FRAND negotiation frameworks. Companies involved in video technology supply chains are advised to proactively assess exposure to relevant video codec patents. Establishing flexible licensing response mechanisms is crucial to mitigate risks associated with overlooking new licensing entities. Failure to adapt to these changing dynamics could result in supply chain disruptions or exposure to substantial infringement claims, necessitating a strategic review of current IP compliance and acquisition practices.

items.title: Réseaux sociaux et diffusion contrefaisante : l’image comme support d’atteinte aux droits
items.title_zh: 社交媒体与侵权传播：图像作为权利侵害载体
items.title_en: Paris Court Rules Social Media Image Distribution Constitutes Copyright Infringement
items.summary: Par Maître Solène DAGUIER, DTMV Avocats (CA Paris, 13 févr. 2026, n° 24/18475) La contrefaçon d’une œuvre ne résulte pas seulement de sa reproduction matérielle. Elle peut également être caractérisée par la diffusion de son image. Dans un arrêt du 13 février dernier, la Cour d’appel de Paris en donne une illustration en retenant la […] L’article Réseaux sociaux et diffusion contrefaisante : l’image comme support d’atteinte aux droits est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎上诉法院于2026年2月13日作出判决（案号24/18475），指出作品侵权不仅限于物质复制，通过社交媒体传播作品图像同样可被认定为侵权。该判决强调在数字环境下，图像的广泛扩散本身即构成对权利人专有权利的侵害，无需证明传统的物理复制行为。这一裁决为打击网络环境下的版权侵权行为提供了新的司法依据，强化了图像传播者的法律责任。
items.ai_summary_en: In a significant ruling dated February 13, 2026 (Case No. 24/18475), the Paris Court of Appeal clarified that copyright infringement is not limited to the physical reproduction of a work. The court established that the dissemination of an image via social media platforms can independently constitute infringement. This decision underscores that in the digital environment, the widespread distribution of visual content itself violates the rights holder’s exclusive prerogatives, without necessitating proof of traditional physical copying. By recognizing digital diffusion as a distinct basis for liability, the judgment provides new judicial grounds for combating online copyright violations and reinforces the legal responsibilities of those who share unauthorized images on social networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕在官方或营销账号中未经授权使用他人图片、字体或设计元素。即便未进行实体复制，仅在社交媒体发布含侵权内容的图文，亦面临法国及欧盟境内的诉讼风险。建议建立严格的素材合规审查机制，确保所有发布内容拥有合法授权，避免高额赔偿及品牌声誉受损。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and market participants operating in France and the EU must recognize that unauthorized use of third-party visuals, fonts, or design elements on official or marketing social media accounts carries substantial litigation risk. Liability arises from the act of publication itself, regardless of whether physical copies were made. Entities should implement rigorous compliance mechanisms to verify that all digital content possesses valid authorization prior to release. Proactive management of digital asset licensing is essential to mitigate exposure to high damages claims and protect brand reputation against allegations of intellectual property violation in cross-border operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎上诉法院裁定社交媒体传播作品图像构成版权侵权", "侵权认定不再局限于物质复制，扩散行为本身即可定罪", "判决强化了对数字环境中图像非法传播的法律规制力度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that distributing images on social media constitutes copyright infringement.", "Infringement findings are no longer restricted to material reproduction, as diffusion acts alone suffice for conviction.", "The judgment strengthens legal regulation regarding the illegal dissemination of images within the digital environment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严禁在社媒营销中未经授权使用第三方视觉素材", "建立全流程数字内容版权合规审查与授权管理机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strictly prohibit the unauthorized use of third-party visual materials in social media marketing campaigns to avoid infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Establish a comprehensive end-to-end compliance review and authorization management system for all digital content publishing.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding digital diffusion as a standalone basis for copyright infringement in European jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that distributing images on social media constitutes copyright infringement.
Infringement findings are no longer restricted to material reproduction, as diffusion acts alone suffice for conviction.
The judgment strengthens legal regulation regarding the illegal dissemination of images within the digital environment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strictly prohibit the unauthorized use of third-party visual materials in social media marketing campaigns to avoid infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Establish a comprehensive end-to-end compliance review and authorization management system for all digital content publishing.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding digital diffusion as a standalone basis for copyright infringement in European jurisdictions.

items.title: International networks ‘foundational’ to IP service, despite what data says
items.title_zh: 数据虽冷：国际网络仍是IP服务基石
items.title_en: International Networks Remain Foundational to IP Service Despite Data Contradictions
items.summary: In-house counsel aren’t impressed with law firms’ international networks, but practitioners say they are crucial for business
items.ai_summary_zh: Managing IP报道指出，尽管数据显示企业法务对律所国际网络评价不高，但从业者坚持认为其至关重要。文章揭示了IP服务市场中客户感知与专业服务供给之间的认知错位。在跨境知识产权布局中，律所的全球协作能力被视为提供无缝服务的关键，即便客户当前未充分认可其价值，这种基础设施对于处理复杂的跨国IP事务仍具有不可替代的基础性作用。
items.ai_summary_en: Managing IP reports that while in-house counsel express limited appreciation for law firms' international networks, practitioners maintain these structures are vital for business success. This highlights a significant cognitive disconnect between client perceptions and professional service delivery within the intellectual property market. In cross-border IP strategies, global collaboration capabilities are viewed as essential for providing seamless legal support. Even if clients do not currently fully recognize this value, such infrastructure remains indispensable for managing complex multinational IP matters effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展时，应超越短期成本考量，重视律所全球协同能力。选择具备强大国际网络的律所，能更有效应对多国专利布局、诉讼及合规风险。建议建立长期合作机制，充分利用律所的国际资源，以弥补自身海外法律团队不足，确保IP战略在全球范围内的连贯性与执行力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should prioritize a firm's global coordination capabilities over short-term cost considerations or single-jurisdiction quotes. Selecting partners with robust international networks enables more effective handling of multi-country patent filings, litigation, and compliance risks. Establishing long-term cooperative mechanisms allows companies to leverage external global resources, compensating for limitations in internal overseas legal teams. This approach ensures the consistency and enforceability of IP strategies across different jurisdictions, aligning legal execution with broader corporate objectives.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["企业法务对律所国际网络满意度低，但从业者视其为业务关键", "数据与客户反馈存在背离，凸显IP服务价值认知的复杂性", "国际协作网络是应对跨境知识产权挑战的基础设施"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["In-house counsel report low satisfaction with law firm international networks, yet practitioners view them as critical operational assets.", "Divergence between quantitative data and qualitative feedback underscores the complexity of valuing IP services accurately.", "International collaboration networks serve as essential infrastructure for addressing challenges in cross-border intellectual property management."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视律所全球协同能力，而非仅看单一法域报价", "利用国际网络弥补海外团队短板，保障IP战略连贯"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize global coordination capabilities over single-jurisdiction pricing to ensure comprehensive IP protection.", "Practice note: Leverage international networks to supplement internal team limitations and maintain strategic coherence globally.", "What to monitor: The evolving gap between client perception of network value and the practical necessity of cross-border legal integration."]
core_points_en: In-house counsel report low satisfaction with law firm international networks, yet practitioners view them as critical operational assets.
Divergence between quantitative data and qualitative feedback underscores the complexity of valuing IP services accurately.
International collaboration networks serve as essential infrastructure for addressing challenges in cross-border intellectual property management.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize global coordination capabilities over single-jurisdiction pricing to ensure comprehensive IP protection.
Practice note: Leverage international networks to supplement internal team limitations and maintain strategic coherence globally.
What to monitor: The evolving gap between client perception of network value and the practical necessity of cross-border legal integration.

items.title: UPC highly popular with Chinese and US claimants
items.title_zh: UPC受中美原告青睐，新案件管理系统上线
items.title_en: UPC Gains Favor Among US and Chinese Claimants Following Case Management System Overhaul
items.summary: The team behind the UPC has every right to be proud. Just three years after its launch, the UPC has overcome its biggest criticism from users — the much-maligned CMS has been completely overhauled. “The successful launch of the Court’s new Case Management System marked a major milestone, showcasing the effectiveness of close cooperation between […] The post UPC highly popular with Chinese and US claimants appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）成立三年后，彻底重构了此前备受诟病的案件管理系统（CMS）。新系统的成功上线标志着重大里程碑，展示了紧密合作的有效性。尽管正文未详述数据，但标题指出中国和美国原告对UPC表现出高度热情，表明该法院在国际专利诉讼中的吸引力日益增强，且技术障碍已显著改善。
items.ai_summary_en: Three years after its inception, the Unified Patent Court has successfully addressed its most significant operational criticism by completely overhauling the previously maligned Case Management System. The successful launch of this new platform marks a major milestone for the institution, demonstrating the effectiveness of close cooperation among stakeholders in resolving early technical hurdles. Although detailed statistical data is not provided in the excerpt, the heightened interest from claimants based in China and the United States signals growing confidence in the Court’s infrastructure. This surge in participation indicates that the UPC is increasingly viewed as an attractive venue for international patent litigation, with improved technological reliability reducing procedural friction and enhancing overall judicial efficiency for global rights holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC系统优化降低了程序风险，中美企业活跃度高预示竞争加剧。中国企业应密切关注UPC诉讼策略变化，评估在德国等核心辖区的应诉成本与胜诉率，提前布局专利防御体系，以应对可能增加的跨境专利纠纷挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: The optimization of the UPC’s procedural framework reduces legal uncertainty and lowers barriers to entry for complex patent disputes. As participation from major technology markets increases, rights holders should anticipate intensified competitive dynamics within the Court. Companies operating in Europe must proactively assess their exposure to potential infringement claims, particularly in key jurisdictions like Germany. Evaluating local defense costs, historical success rates, and strategic litigation options is essential. Establishing robust patent defense mechanisms and monitoring shifts in judicial practice will help mitigate risks associated with the rising volume of cross-border patent litigation before the Unified Patent Court.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC成立三年彻底重构备受批评的案件管理系统", "新系统上线标志合作成效，提升法院运作效率", "中美原告对UPC诉讼表现出极高参与热情"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC has fully replaced its criticized Case Management System three years post-launch, resolving major user concerns regarding digital infrastructure.", "The new system's successful deployment highlights effective stakeholder collaboration and significantly improves the Court's operational efficiency.", "High engagement levels from US and Chinese plaintiffs suggest the UPC is becoming a preferred forum for cross-border patent enforcement actions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC程序优化降低诉讼不确定性，需警惕竞争加剧", "建议中企提前布局防御体系，评估跨境应诉策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhanced system reliability lowers procedural risks, making the UPC a more viable venue for high-stakes patent enforcement and defense strategies.", "Practice note: Rights holders should evaluate defense costs and success rates in core jurisdictions like Germany to prepare for increased litigation activity.", "What to monitor: Shifts in UPC litigation strategies and case management trends to anticipate competitive pressures and adjust cross-border IP defenses accordingly."]
core_points_en: The UPC has fully replaced its criticized Case Management System three years post-launch, resolving major user concerns regarding digital infrastructure.
The new system's successful deployment highlights effective stakeholder collaboration and significantly improves the Court's operational efficiency.
High engagement levels from US and Chinese plaintiffs suggest the UPC is becoming a preferred forum for cross-border patent enforcement actions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enhanced system reliability lowers procedural risks, making the UPC a more viable venue for high-stakes patent enforcement and defense strategies.
Practice note: Rights holders should evaluate defense costs and success rates in core jurisdictions like Germany to prepare for increased litigation activity.
What to monitor: Shifts in UPC litigation strategies and case management trends to anticipate competitive pressures and adjust cross-border IP defenses accordingly.

items.title: Green light for the EU-India Trade Agreement: a new landscape of opportunities for intellectual property
items.title_zh: 欧盟-印度自贸协定获政治绿灯：知识产权新机遇格局
items.title_en: EU-India Trade Agreement Receives Political Approval: New Opportunities for Intellectual Property
items.summary: The political green light for the free trade agreement between the European Union and India, reached in January 2026, marks a turning point in the economic relationship between the two blocs. This is one of the most ambitious agreements signed by India to date and one of the most significant strategic agreements for the EU ... Read more La entrada Green light for the EU-India Trade Agreement: a new landscape of opportunities for intellectual property se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年1月，欧盟与印度自由贸易协定获得政治批准，标志着双方经济关系的转折点。这是印度迄今签署的最雄心勃勃的协议之一，也是欧盟重要的战略协定。文章指出该协定将为知识产权保护创造新的机遇 landscape，强调其在双边贸易中的战略地位及对知识产权领域的深远影响，但未详述具体法律条款或执行细节。
items.ai_summary_en: The political approval of the free trade agreement between the European Union and India, reached in January 2026, marks a significant turning point in the economic relationship between these two major blocs. Recognized as one of the most ambitious agreements signed by India to date, it also stands as a strategically vital accord for the European Union. The agreement aims to establish a robust framework for bilateral trade, with a specific focus on optimizing the intellectual property protection environment. This development creates new market opportunities for businesses operating in both regions. While the strategic importance is clear, the current analysis highlights the broader implications for IP landscapes without detailing specific legal clauses or enforcement mechanisms at this stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国对欧出口企业需密切关注协定中IP条款落地，预判印度市场执法趋严。建议提前在印进行专利商标布局，利用新规规避侵权风险，并评估供应链合规成本，以把握中欧印三角贸易中的知识产权红利。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the implementation of intellectual property provisions within the new EU-India trade framework. Anticipating stricter enforcement practices in the Indian market is essential for effective risk management. Companies are advised to proactively secure patent and trademark registrations in India to leverage new regulatory standards and mitigate infringement risks. Furthermore, assessing supply chain compliance costs will be critical. By preparing for these changes, rights holders can better navigate the evolving legal landscape and capitalize on the intellectual property benefits arising from enhanced trade relations between Europe, China, and India.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟与印度自贸协定于2026年1月获政治批准，确立双边经贸新框架", "该协定被视为印度最雄心勃勃及欧盟最具战略意义的贸易协议之一", "协定旨在优化知识产权保护环境，为双方企业带来新的市场机遇"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EU-India Free Trade Agreement received political approval in January 2026, establishing a new framework for bilateral economic relations.", "This pact is regarded as one of India's most ambitious trade deals and a strategically significant agreement for the European Union.", "The agreement aims to optimize intellectual property protection environments, creating new market opportunities for enterprises in both regions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注协定IP条款落地，预判印度执法趋严并提前布局", "评估供应链合规成本，规避侵权风险以把握贸易红利"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor IP clause implementation and anticipate stricter enforcement in India to protect market interests.", "Practice note: Proactively file patents and trademarks in India to utilize new regulations and avoid infringement liabilities.", "What to monitor: Supply chain compliance costs and the evolving triangular trade dynamics involving Europe, China, and India."]
core_points_en: The EU-India Free Trade Agreement received political approval in January 2026, establishing a new framework for bilateral economic relations.
This pact is regarded as one of India's most ambitious trade deals and a strategically significant agreement for the European Union.
The agreement aims to optimize intellectual property protection environments, creating new market opportunities for enterprises in both regions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor IP clause implementation and anticipate stricter enforcement in India to protect market interests.
Practice note: Proactively file patents and trademarks in India to utilize new regulations and avoid infringement liabilities.
What to monitor: Supply chain compliance costs and the evolving triangular trade dynamics involving Europe, China, and India.

items.title: From raw AI output to reliable patent infringement intelligence
items.title_zh: 从原始AI输出到可靠专利侵权情报
items.title_en: Transforming Raw AI Output into Reliable Patent Infringement Intelligence for Enforcement
items.summary: Bikram Singh, founder of ClaimHit, analyses five key questions to distinguish between a preliminary screening that effectively lowers enforcement costs and one that risks producing unreliable AI-generated content
items.ai_summary_zh: ClaimHit创始人Bikram Singh分析了五个关键问题，旨在区分能有效降低执法成本的初步筛选与可能产生不可靠AI生成内容的风险。文章探讨了如何利用AI技术优化专利侵权情报的准确性，避免虚假阳性结果，从而提升专利执行效率并控制相关成本，为权利人提供更具操作性的智能化工具应用指南。
items.ai_summary_en: Bikram Singh, founder of ClaimHit, examines five critical questions designed to differentiate preliminary screening processes that effectively reduce enforcement costs from those risking the generation of unreliable AI content. The analysis focuses on leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance the accuracy of patent infringement intelligence, specifically aiming to minimize false positive results. By addressing these key considerations, rights holders can improve the efficiency of patent enforcement strategies while maintaining strict control over associated legal and operational expenses. This approach provides market participants with a practical framework for applying intelligent tools, ensuring that automated data processing supports robust, defensible legal actions rather than introducing significant risks through unverified algorithmic outputs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权或应对诉讼时，应审慎评估AI工具的误报率，避免依赖未经人工复核的自动化情报。建议建立“AI初筛+专家复核”的双重验证机制，确保侵权比对结论的法律严谨性，防止因数据偏差导致策略失误或额外成本支出。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI for patent enforcement must carefully evaluate tool error rates and avoid relying solely on unverified automated intelligence. Establishing a dual verification mechanism combining initial AI screening with expert human review is essential to ensure legal rigor in infringement comparisons. This approach prevents strategic errors or additional costs arising from data biases or false positives. Rights holders should integrate these safeguards to maintain defensibility in litigation contexts, ensuring that cost-saving automation does not compromise the quality or reliability of the underlying evidence used for enforcement decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["分析五个关键问题以区分有效筛选与不可靠AI内容", "强调降低专利执法成本需确保AI生成情报的可靠性", "探讨利用AI优化侵权情报准确性以提升执行效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyze five key questions to distinguish effective preliminary screening from unreliable AI-generated content risks.", "Emphasize that reducing patent enforcement costs requires ensuring the reliability and accuracy of AI-generated intelligence.", "Explore using AI to optimize infringement intelligence accuracy, thereby enhancing overall enforcement efficiency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审慎评估AI工具误报率，避免依赖未复核的自动化情报", "建立AI初筛与专家复核双重机制，确保法律严谨性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Carefully assess AI tool false positive rates and avoid dependence on unreviewed automated intelligence.", "Practice note: Establish a dual mechanism of AI preliminary screening and expert review to ensure legal rigor.", "What to monitor: Monitor the integration of human oversight in automated workflows to prevent data bias impacts."]
core_points_en: Analyze five key questions to distinguish effective preliminary screening from unreliable AI-generated content risks.
Emphasize that reducing patent enforcement costs requires ensuring the reliability and accuracy of AI-generated intelligence.
Explore using AI to optimize infringement intelligence accuracy, thereby enhancing overall enforcement efficiency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Carefully assess AI tool false positive rates and avoid dependence on unreviewed automated intelligence.
Practice note: Establish a dual mechanism of AI preliminary screening and expert review to ensure legal rigor.
What to monitor: Monitor the integration of human oversight in automated workflows to prevent data bias impacts.

items.title: Where Should Cases Go? Or Where Should Patent Law Be Made?
items.title_zh: 案件去向与法律制定：统一专利法院案件分配的制度反思
items.title_en: UPC Case Distribution: Where Litigation Concentrates and Patent Law Is Formed
items.summary: "Gedanken ohne Inhalt sind leer, Anschauungen ohne Begriffe sind blind." — Immanuel Kant (Thoughts without content are empty, intuitions without concepts are blind.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回应关于统一专利法院（UPC）案件分配过于抽象的批评，指出问题核心在于案件集中是否扭曲了UPC法的形成条件。尽管案件集中于特定分部反映了用户信心与可预测性，但法院并非市场，其产出是法律。若法律实践仅由少数中心通过重复程序塑造，将导致早期教义固化。作者主张需审视这种由用户偏好驱动的自强化均衡，因其可能非中性地决定未来专利法的结构与发展路径。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary addresses concerns that UPC case distribution debates remain too abstract. It argues the core issue is whether concentrating cases in specific local divisions affects how UPC law is formed. While concentration reflects user confidence and predictability, a court produces law rather than merely allocating market demand. If legal practice is shaped by repeated procedures in a limited number of divisions, early doctrines may become entrenched. The self-reinforcing equilibrium driven by user choice requires scrutiny to ensure it does not non-neutrally determine the future structure of European patent law.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注UPC主要分部的司法倾向，因早期判例将深远影响后续诉讼策略。建议避免盲目追随热门法庭，需综合评估各分部的程序效率与文化差异，以规避因‘胜者通吃’效应导致的法律解释单一化风险，优化全球专利布局与应诉策略。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and counsel, understanding the structural impact of case concentration is vital for long-term portfolio strategy. The reliance on predictable divisions offers immediate procedural advantages but carries the risk of shaping a uniform legal interpretation through limited judicial perspectives. Monitoring this dynamic helps anticipate shifts in legal doctrine formation within the new unified system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["案件集中反映用户信心，但需警惕其对UPC法形成条件的潜在扭曲", "法院产出法律而非分配需求，少数中心的重复实践将固化早期教义", "单纯的数据透明或程序调整无法解决由用户偏好驱动的制度结构性问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Case concentration in certain UPC divisions reflects user confidence but raises questions about how law is systematically formed.", "Courts produce legal precedents, meaning concentrated procedural environments can entrench early judicial doctrines.", "User-driven case distribution creates a self-reinforcing equilibrium that structurally influences future patent law development."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC主要分部早期判例，预判未来专利法解释趋势", "综合评估各分部差异，避免单一法庭依赖带来的战略风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Concentrated litigation venues influence the evolution of UPC jurisprudence, affecting long-term enforcement predictability.", "Practice note: Evaluate divisional differences beyond speed to understand how repeated practices may solidify specific legal interpretations.", "What to monitor: Track whether self-reinforcing case distribution leads to the entrenchment of early doctrinal positions in key divisions."]
core_points_en: Case concentration in certain UPC divisions reflects user confidence but raises questions about how law is systematically formed.
Courts produce legal precedents, meaning concentrated procedural environments can entrench early judicial doctrines.
User-driven case distribution creates a self-reinforcing equilibrium that structurally influences future patent law development.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Concentrated litigation venues influence the evolution of UPC jurisprudence, affecting long-term enforcement predictability.
Practice note: Evaluate divisional differences beyond speed to understand how repeated practices may solidify specific legal interpretations.
What to monitor: Track whether self-reinforcing case distribution leads to the entrenchment of early doctrinal positions in key divisions.

items.title: Navigating the complex LNP IP landscape
items.title_zh: Navigating the complex LNP IP landscape
items.title_en: Navigating the Complex and Fast-Moving Intellectual Property Landscape of Lipid Nanoparticles
items.summary: It is difficult to find a field of patent law in pharma and biotech at the moment that is more complicated and fast-moving than the field of lipid nanoparticles (LNPs). IP strategy for LNPs involves sophisticated science, multiple and overlapping technology platforms, trade secret and ownership disputes and global patent cross-litigation. As we move away from historical disputes surrounding the
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出脂质纳米颗粒（LNP）是当前医药生物技术中最复杂且快速变化的专利领域。其知识产权策略涉及尖端科学、多重重叠的技术平台、商业秘密及所有权争议，以及全球范围内的专利交叉诉讼。文章强调随着历史争议的演变，企业需应对日益复杂的IP环境，平衡技术保护与法律风险，以在激烈的全球竞争中维持优势地位。
items.ai_summary_en: Lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) currently represent one of the most complex and rapidly evolving sectors within pharmaceutical and biotechnology patent law. Effective intellectual property strategy in this domain requires navigating sophisticated scientific challenges, managing multiple overlapping technology platforms, and addressing significant trade secret and ownership disputes. Furthermore, market participants face extensive global patent cross-litigation. As historical legal conflicts evolve, companies must adapt to an increasingly intricate IP environment. Success depends on balancing robust technical protection with careful management of legal risks. This dynamic landscape demands strategic agility to maintain competitive advantages amidst intense global competition and shifting regulatory and judicial interpretations surrounding LNP technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海应警惕LNP底层专利壁垒，提前进行FTO分析。鉴于全球交叉诉讼频发，建议构建组合式IP防御体系，重视商业秘密保护与专利布局的协同，避免陷入被动诉讼。同时关注欧盟相关司法动态，灵活调整海外授权与许可策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should proactively conduct freedom-to-operate analyses to identify underlying patent barriers in the LNP space. Given the prevalence of global cross-litigation, establishing a comprehensive defensive IP portfolio is essential. Rights holders must ensure synergy between patent filings and trade secret protections to avoid passive litigation positions. Additionally, monitoring relevant EU judicial developments allows for flexible adjustments to overseas licensing and authorization strategies. This approach helps mitigate infringement risks while securing commercial interests in a highly contested technological field requiring continuous legal vigilance.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["LNP领域为当前医药生物最复杂且快速变化的专利赛道", "IP策略涵盖尖端科学、重叠平台及商业秘密所有权争议", "面临全球专利交叉诉讼，需应对复杂IP环境与法律风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["LNP represents the most complex and fast-moving patent sector in current pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries.", "IP strategies involve advanced science, overlapping platforms, and critical trade secret alongside ownership disputes.", "Global patent cross-litigation necessitates managing complex IP environments and mitigating significant legal risks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕LNP底层专利壁垒，提前开展自由实施分析规避风险", "构建组合式IP防御体系，强化商业秘密与专利布局协同"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct early freedom-to-operate analyses to navigate underlying LNP patent barriers and mitigate infringement risks.", "Practice note: Build a combined IP defense system that synergizes trade secret protection with strategic patent layout.", "What to monitor: Track EU judicial dynamics to flexibly adjust overseas licensing and authorization strategies."]
core_points_en: LNP represents the most complex and fast-moving patent sector in current pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries.
IP strategies involve advanced science, overlapping platforms, and critical trade secret alongside ownership disputes.
Global patent cross-litigation necessitates managing complex IP environments and mitigating significant legal risks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct early freedom-to-operate analyses to navigate underlying LNP patent barriers and mitigate infringement risks.
Practice note: Build a combined IP defense system that synergizes trade secret protection with strategic patent layout.
What to monitor: Track EU judicial dynamics to flexibly adjust overseas licensing and authorization strategies.

items.title: A Venetian surprise: Does the UPCA transitional period really require an opt-out to access national courts?
items.title_zh: UPCA过渡期： accessing国家法院是否必须提交退出申请？
items.title_en: UPCA Transitional Period: Is an Opt-Out Mandatory for National Court Access?
items.summary: This Kat is looking you dead in the eyeto ask: 'Are you absolutely sureyou didn't need to file that opt-out?'The transitional period of the Unified Patent Court Agreement (UPCA) is widely understood to offer a safe harbor of choice. For a period of seven years, patentees holding "classic" European patents have rested on the comfortable assumption that they can freely choose between asserting
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章质疑关于统一专利法院（UPC）过渡期规则的普遍误解。传统观点认为专利权人可自由选择在国家法院或UPC提起诉讼，但作者指出这可能并非绝对安全港。若未正式提交“退出”（opt-out）申请，可能面临管辖权争议风险。该分析旨在提醒专利权人重新审视其经典欧洲专利的诉讼策略，确保符合程序要求以避免法律不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary challenges the widespread assumption that the Unified Patent Court Agreement's transitional period provides an unconditional safe harbor for patent holders. While it is commonly believed that proprietors of classic European patents can freely choose between national courts and the UPC during the seven-year window, this analysis suggests such freedom may not be absolute. The author argues that failing to formally file an opt-out could expose rights holders to significant jurisdictional disputes. Consequently, patentees are urged to critically re-evaluate their litigation strategies for classic European patents. Ensuring strict compliance with procedural requirements is essential to avoid legal uncertainty and potential loss of access to preferred national forums.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应警惕UPC过渡期管辖权陷阱。切勿默认拥有选择权，需全面核查专利组合的退出申请状态。若意图保留在国家法院诉讼的权利，务必确保已合规提交退出申请，避免因程序瑕疵导致被迫进入UPC诉讼，增加维权成本与不确定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should not assume automatic jurisdictional flexibility under the UPCA transitional rules. Relying on ambiguous interpretations of forum selection carries the risk of being compelled into UPC proceedings due to procedural oversights. To preserve the right to litigate in national courts, rights holders must ensure that valid opt-out applications have been correctly filed for all relevant classic European patents. Immediate verification of these filings is critical to mitigate the increased costs and legal uncertainties associated with unintended UPC jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["挑战UPC过渡期专利权人可自由择庭的传统认知假设", "强调未提交正式退出申请可能导致无法进入国家法院诉讼", "警示专利权人需立即审查并确认其经典欧洲专利的程序状态"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Challenges the traditional assumption that patent holders possess unrestricted forum choice during the UPCA transitional period.", "Emphasizes that failing to submit a formal opt-out application may prevent access to national court litigation.", "Warns rights holders to immediately review and confirm the procedural status of their classic European patent portfolios."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即核查所有经典欧洲专利的UPC退出申请提交状态", "避免依赖模糊的择庭假设，以防陷入不利的UPC管辖程序"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify the submission status of UPC opt-out applications for all classic European patents immediately.", "Practice note: Avoid relying on assumed forum selection rights to prevent falling into unfavorable UPC jurisdictional procedures.", "What to monitor: Potential jurisdictional challenges arising from missing or defective opt-out filings during the transitional period."]
core_points_en: Challenges the traditional assumption that patent holders possess unrestricted forum choice during the UPCA transitional period.
Emphasizes that failing to submit a formal opt-out application may prevent access to national court litigation.
Warns rights holders to immediately review and confirm the procedural status of their classic European patent portfolios.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify the submission status of UPC opt-out applications for all classic European patents immediately.
Practice note: Avoid relying on assumed forum selection rights to prevent falling into unfavorable UPC jurisdictional procedures.
What to monitor: Potential jurisdictional challenges arising from missing or defective opt-out filings during the transitional period.

items.title: Generative AI and the EU Copyright Law’s Blind Spots
items.title_zh: 生成式AI与欧盟版权法的盲区
items.title_en: Generative AI and the EU Copyright Law’s Blind Spots: A Legislative Miss
items.summary: Legislatures are notoriously inefficient in legislating for future technologies. These technologies are sometimes developing at the same time as legislatures are debating new laws, resulting in the legislatures missing, by a small margin, opportunities to design the laws for the new technologies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出立法者难以跟上技术发展步伐，以TRIPS协议忽视万维网为例，类比当前欧盟版权立法与生成式AI发展的错位。尽管DSM指令包含文本和数据挖掘（TDM）例外，但立法历史显示，制定者当时并未预见生成式AI的兴起。因此，现行TDM条款并非专为生成式AI设计，这构成了欧盟版权法的潜在盲区，可能影响未来AI训练数据的法律合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: Legislatures often struggle to regulate emerging technologies effectively, as seen with the TRIPS Agreement's failure to address the World Wide Web. Similarly, the EU's 2019 Digital Single Market Directive text and data mining exceptions were not drafted with generative AI in mind. Legislative history confirms that lawmakers did not anticipate generative AI's rapid development when adopting Articles 3 and 4. This disconnect creates potential blind spots in current EU copyright law regarding AI training data compliance.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI企业需警惕欧盟版权法在生成式AI领域的解释风险。由于TDM例外非为AI定制，建议企业在欧开展数据训练前，进行严格的版权合规审查，并密切关注后续司法实践或立法补充，以降低侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This analysis highlights a significant interpretive risk for rights holders and AI developers operating within the EU. Since the existing text and data mining framework was not tailored for generative AI, stakeholders should anticipate potential legal uncertainties regarding training data usage. Monitoring future judicial interpretations and possible legislative updates is crucial for maintaining compliance and managing portfolio risks in this evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["立法滞后导致欧盟版权法未预见生成式AI对TDM例外的需求", "DSM指令TDM条款制定时，生成式AI尚未成为立法者的考量重点", "现行法律框架存在盲区，可能引发生成式AI训练数据的版权争议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU legislators did not consider generative AI when drafting the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.", "Legislative history confirms Articles 3 and 4 were not designed to address generative AI technology needs.", "The parallel development of EU copyright laws and generative AI created a regulatory gap similar to past technological misses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟TDM例外非为AI定制的合规解释风险", "加强数据训练版权审查并关注后续立法动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and AI companies face uncertainty as current TDM exceptions may not adequately cover generative AI training activities.", "Practice note: Stakeholders should review data sourcing practices against the original intent of DSM Directive Articles 3 and 4 to assess exposure.", "What to monitor: Future court decisions interpreting TDM exceptions in the context of generative AI and any subsequent EU legislative amendments."]
core_points_en: EU legislators did not consider generative AI when drafting the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.
Legislative history confirms Articles 3 and 4 were not designed to address generative AI technology needs.
The parallel development of EU copyright laws and generative AI created a regulatory gap similar to past technological misses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and AI companies face uncertainty as current TDM exceptions may not adequately cover generative AI training activities.
Practice note: Stakeholders should review data sourcing practices against the original intent of DSM Directive Articles 3 and 4 to assess exposure.
What to monitor: Future court decisions interpreting TDM exceptions in the context of generative AI and any subsequent EU legislative amendments.

items.title: Siren call: Starbucks’ IP chief on safeguarding a global brand
items.title_zh: 星巴克IP主管谈规模化品牌保护
items.title_en: Starbucks IP Chief Discusses Strategies for Safeguarding a Global Brand at Scale
items.summary: A global network of more than 35,000 stores makes the coffee chain one of the world’s most recognisable brands. Its IP chief Russ Jacobs discusses how it protects its brand at scale.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于World IP Review对星巴克知识产权主管Russ Jacobs的采访。鉴于星巴克拥有超过35,000家门店的全球网络，其品牌辨识度极高。文章探讨了星巴克如何在如此庞大的规模下有效保护其品牌资产，重点在于应对全球范围内的侵权风险、维护品牌一致性以及实施大规模的品牌监控与执法策略，为跨国企业提供了品牌管理的实务视角。
items.ai_summary_en: Based on an interview with Starbucks’ Intellectual Property Chief Russ Jacobs in World IP Review, this article examines how the coffee giant protects its brand assets across a vast global network of over 35,000 stores. Given the company’s high brand recognition, maintaining consistency and preventing dilution are critical challenges. The discussion highlights specific strategies for managing infringement risks worldwide, ensuring uniform brand presentation, and implementing large-scale monitoring and enforcement protocols. These insights offer practical perspectives for multinational corporations on managing complex intellectual property portfolios, addressing cross-border infringement issues, and executing effective brand protection measures in diverse legal jurisdictions while sustaining global brand integrity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应借鉴星巴克经验，建立全球化品牌监控体系。在欧盟等关键市场，需提前布局商标防御性注册，并制定应对大规模侵权的标准化执法流程，以低成本高效维护品牌权益，避免被动诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational rights holders should consider adopting comprehensive global brand monitoring systems to detect and respond to infringements efficiently. In key jurisdictions such as the European Union, proactive defensive trademark registration is essential to secure market positions before potential conflicts arise. Establishing standardized enforcement procedures allows companies to manage large-scale infringement issues cost-effectively, reducing reliance on reactive litigation. This approach ensures that brand equity is maintained through consistent legal protection and rapid response mechanisms, which are vital for sustaining competitive advantage in international markets where counterfeiting and unauthorized use pose significant risks to established brands.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["星巴克依托三万五千家门店构建全球高辨识度品牌网络", "IP主管探讨在超大规模运营中实施品牌保护的具体策略", "强调通过系统化手段应对全球市场中的品牌侵权与挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Starbucks leverages its network of over 35,000 stores to establish a highly recognizable global brand presence.", "The IP chief outlines specific strategies for implementing robust brand protection within ultra-large scale operations.", "Emphasis is placed on systematic approaches to address brand infringement and challenges across global markets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立全球化品牌监控与快速响应机制以应对侵权", "在欧盟等核心市场提前进行防御性商标布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implement global brand monitoring and rapid response mechanisms to effectively address infringement threats.", "Practice note: Conduct proactive defensive trademark registrations in core markets like the EU to secure rights early.", "What to monitor: Track emerging infringement patterns and regulatory changes affecting brand enforcement strategies globally."]
core_points_en: Starbucks leverages its network of over 35,000 stores to establish a highly recognizable global brand presence.
The IP chief outlines specific strategies for implementing robust brand protection within ultra-large scale operations.
Emphasis is placed on systematic approaches to address brand infringement and challenges across global markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implement global brand monitoring and rapid response mechanisms to effectively address infringement threats.
Practice note: Conduct proactive defensive trademark registrations in core markets like the EU to secure rights early.
What to monitor: Track emerging infringement patterns and regulatory changes affecting brand enforcement strategies globally.

items.title: WATCH: Constantin Eikel of Bird & Bird
items.title_zh: Bird & Bird合伙人Constantin Eikel访谈：商标、AI与INTA见闻
items.title_en: Bird & Bird Partner Constantin Eikel Discusses Trademarks, AI, and INTA Insights
items.summary: Constantin Eikel, a partner at Bird & Bird in Germany, chats with Tom Phillips at the London event about trademarks, networking, and the INTA conference experience—including his 'word of the week' and top London tips.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文记录了德国Bird & Bird合伙人Constantin Eikel在伦敦活动中与Tom Phillips的对话。内容涵盖商标领域的最新趋势、人工智能对行业的影响以及专业社交网络的重要性。Eikel分享了参加国际商标协会（INTA）会议的体验，并推荐了“本周热词”及伦敦最佳去处。该视频旨在为从业者提供实务见解与行业交流机会。
items.ai_summary_en: This video features a conversation between Constantin Eikel, a partner at Bird & Bird in Germany, and Tom Phillips during a London event. The discussion explores current trends in trademark law, the transformative impact of artificial intelligence on brand protection, and the critical role of professional networking. Eikel shares his experiences from the International Trademark Association conference, highlighting its value for industry intelligence. He also offers practical advice for legal professionals visiting London, including recommended venues and his selected word of the week. This content provides practitioners with actionable insights into navigating the evolving IP landscape while fostering connections within the European intellectual property community.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注AI技术在商标监测与侵权判定中的应用趋势，提前布局相关合规策略。积极参与INTA等国际会议有助于拓展欧洲人脉，获取前沿情报。建议加强跨国律所合作，利用本地化专家资源应对复杂的欧盟及英国商标环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the integration of AI technologies in trademark monitoring and infringement determination processes. Proactively developing compliance strategies is essential to address emerging technological challenges effectively. Engaging in international conferences like INTA remains a strategic method for expanding professional networks across Europe and accessing cutting-edge industry intelligence. Furthermore, strengthening collaborations with local law firms allows rights holders to leverage specialized expertise. This approach enhances enforcement efficiency and ensures robust navigation of the complex regulatory environments governing trademarks in both the European Union and the United Kingdom.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["探讨商标领域当前热点及人工智能技术对品牌保护带来的新挑战与机遇", "分享参加INTA大会的专业体验，强调行业会议在建立人脉与获取情报中的价值", "结合德国律师视角，提供伦敦地区的专业社交建议及行业流行术语解读"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Examining current trademark hotspots and how AI creates new challenges and opportunities for effective brand protection strategies.", "Sharing professional experiences from the INTA conference to emphasize the vital importance of industry meetings for networking.", "Providing London-based social recommendations and interpreting trending industry terms from the perspective of a German attorney."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注AI对商标实务影响，提前布局合规策略以应对新技术挑战", "借助国际会议拓展欧洲人脉，强化与本地律所合作以提升维权效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor AI impacts on trademark practice and proactively develop compliance strategies to address new technological challenges.", "Practice note: Leverage international conferences to expand European networks and strengthen cooperation with local firms for efficient enforcement.", "What to monitor: Evolving AI applications in brand protection and regulatory shifts in EU and UK trademark enforcement mechanisms."]
core_points_en: Examining current trademark hotspots and how AI creates new challenges and opportunities for effective brand protection strategies.
Sharing professional experiences from the INTA conference to emphasize the vital importance of industry meetings for networking.
Providing London-based social recommendations and interpreting trending industry terms from the perspective of a German attorney.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor AI impacts on trademark practice and proactively develop compliance strategies to address new technological challenges.
Practice note: Leverage international conferences to expand European networks and strengthen cooperation with local firms for efficient enforcement.
What to monitor: Evolving AI applications in brand protection and regulatory shifts in EU and UK trademark enforcement mechanisms.

items.title: WATCH: Toni Nijm, chief product officer at Anaqua
items.title_zh: Anaqua高管谈AI在IP防御中的挑战与关键性
items.title_en: Anaqua Executive Highlights AI's Critical Role and Adoption Challenges in IP Defense
items.summary: Toni Nijm, chief product officer at IP services firm Anaqua, chats with Tom Phillips at INTA 2026 about why AI adoption is challenging but critical for successfully defending IP.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道了知识产权服务公司Anaqua首席产品官Toni Nijm在INTA 2026会议上的发言。Nijm指出，尽管人工智能技术的采用面临诸多挑战，但对于成功进行知识产权防御至关重要。该观点强调了在日益复杂的全球IP环境中，利用AI技术提升防御效率的必要性，反映了行业对技术赋能IP管理的关注趋势。
items.ai_summary_en: This report covers remarks made by Toni Nijm, Chief Product Officer at Anaqua, during the INTA 2026 conference. In a discussion with Tom Phillips, Nijm emphasized that while integrating artificial intelligence into intellectual property workflows presents significant operational hurdles, its adoption is indispensable for effective IP defense strategies. The commentary underscores the necessity of leveraging AI technologies to enhance defensive efficiency within an increasingly complex global IP landscape. This perspective reflects a broader industry trend toward utilizing advanced technological tools to strengthen IP management capabilities, ensuring rights holders can navigate modern enforcement challenges more effectively despite implementation barriers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应重视AI在IP风险预警与防御中的应用。面对欧盟复杂环境，建议引入智能工具监控侵权风险，优化应对策略。虽存在实施门槛，但早期布局有助于提升合规效率，降低潜在诉讼成本，增强国际竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize the integration of AI tools for proactive IP risk monitoring and defense. Given the regulatory complexity of the EU market, deploying intelligent systems to track potential infringement allows for optimized response strategies. Although initial implementation thresholds exist, early adoption of smart defense frameworks enhances compliance efficiency and reduces potential litigation costs. This strategic approach strengthens international competitiveness by enabling faster, data-driven decision-making in IP enforcement matters across diverse jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Anaqua高管强调AI对成功防御知识产权具有关键作用", "指出当前企业在采纳AI技术过程中面临显著挑战", "反映行业对利用技术手段强化IP管理能力的重视"]

items.title: Secrets of success: In-house with Orange’s VP and head of licensing
items.title_zh: Orange高管揭秘：如何通过团队协作实现许可收入高增长
items.title_en: Orange’s VP Reveals How Team Collaboration Drives High Licensing Revenue Growth
items.summary: The telecoms giant’s VP and head of IP licensing, Harold Barrault, tells Sarah Speight about his role in achieving high growth in licensing revenues for the business, starting with getting the IP department to work fully as a team.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文采访了法国电信巨头Orange的副总裁兼IP许可负责人Harold Barrault。文章重点探讨了其领导下的IP部门如何通过强化内部团队协作，成功推动公司许可业务的高增长。Barrault分享了其在优化IP资产管理、提升许可谈判效率及构建高效IP团队方面的管理经验，强调了从单一职能向协同作战转变对实现商业价值最大化的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: This article features an interview with Harold Barrault, Vice President and Head of IP Licensing at the French telecommunications giant Orange. It highlights how his leadership in strengthening internal team collaboration within the IP department successfully drove significant growth in the company's licensing business. Barrault shares his management experience regarding the optimization of IP asset management, improving the efficiency of licensing negotiations, and building a high-performing IP team. The discussion emphasizes the critical role of shifting from isolated functional operations to coordinated, cross-functional teamwork in maximizing commercial value and achieving strategic business objectives for large technology enterprises.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应借鉴Orange经验，打破IP部门孤岛，建立跨部门协同机制。在应对海外许可谈判时，需整合法务、研发与市场资源，提升整体议价能力与响应速度，从而最大化IP资产的商业回报并降低合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants can draw valuable lessons from Orange’s operational model by breaking down silos between IP functions and establishing robust cross-departmental coordination mechanisms. When navigating complex international licensing negotiations, companies should integrate resources from legal, research and development, and marketing teams. This holistic approach enhances overall bargaining power and response speed, thereby maximizing the commercial return on IP assets while simultaneously mitigating compliance risks in global markets.

items.title: Présidentielle 2027 : la guerre des noms de domaine est lancée
items.title_zh: 2027法国大选：域名争夺战已打响
items.title_en: France 2027 Presidential Election: The Strategic Battle for Domain Names Begins
items.summary: Avant les meetings, avant les affiches, avant même les déclarations officielles : tout se joue désormais sur quelques caractères tapés dans une barre d'adresse.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出在2027年法国总统大选前，域名已成为战略资产。投机者利用“先注先得”规则抢注候选人域名，构成网络抢注或打字错误劫持。尽管法国民法典保护姓名权，知识产权法保护商标，但需权利人迅速行动。政治讽刺受欧洲人权公约保护，但若造成官方混淆则违法。建议提前进行品牌防御性注册。
items.ai_summary_en: Ahead of the 2027 French presidential election, domain names have emerged as critical strategic assets. Speculators exploit the first-come, first-served registration principle to preemptively secure domains associated with potential candidates, engaging in cybersquatting or typosquatting. While the French Civil Code protects personal name rights and Intellectual Property Code safeguards trademarks, rights holders must act swiftly to enforce these protections. Political satire remains protected under the European Convention on Human Rights; however, impersonating official sources to create confusion constitutes infringement. Consequently, proactive brand defense through early domain registration is essential to mitigate reputational risks and prevent misinformation campaigns targeting political figures and their communications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营时，应重视域名的品牌防御价值。针对潜在的政治或社会热点，提前注册相关域名以防被恶意抢注或用于虚假宣传。同时，需注意区分合法讽刺与侵权混淆的界限，避免法律风险。建立快速响应机制，监测并处理侵犯商标权或姓名的域名注册行为。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies and public figures operating in Europe, securing digital real estate is a fundamental component of brand protection strategy. Proactive defensive registration of relevant domains prevents opportunistic squatting and misuse for disinformation. It is crucial to distinguish between lawful political commentary and infringing impersonation that causes consumer or public confusion. Establishing robust monitoring mechanisms allows for the timely detection and resolution of unauthorized domain registrations. This approach minimizes reputational damage and ensures control over official communication channels during sensitive periods such as elections or major public events.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["域名成为大选关键战略资产，投机者利用先注先得规则抢注", "网络抢注与打字错误劫持干扰竞选，需依据民法与IP法维权", "政治讽刺受保护，但冒充官方造成混淆即构成侵权", "候选人应提前布局域名注册，防范声誉风险与信息误导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Domain names are vital strategic assets in elections, with speculators using first-come rules to register candidate-related URLs preemptively.", "Cybersquatting and typosquatting disrupt campaigns, requiring rapid legal action based on civil law name rights and trademark protections.", "Political satire is legally protected, but impersonating official entities to cause confusion constitutes trademark or name right infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前防御性注册域名，防止被投机者抢注或用于虚假宣传", "建立监测机制，及时应对侵犯商标权或姓名的域名侵权行为"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure key domains proactively to prevent speculative squatting and protect against false advertising or reputational harm.", "Practice note: Implement continuous monitoring systems to detect and address domain registrations that infringe on trademark or personal name rights.", "What to monitor: Watch for typosquatting variations and unauthorized use of names that may confuse the public or mimic official sources."]
core_points_en: Domain names are vital strategic assets in elections, with speculators using first-come rules to register candidate-related URLs preemptively.
Cybersquatting and typosquatting disrupt campaigns, requiring rapid legal action based on civil law name rights and trademark protections.
Political satire is legally protected, but impersonating official entities to cause confusion constitutes trademark or name right infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure key domains proactively to prevent speculative squatting and protect against false advertising or reputational harm.
Practice note: Implement continuous monitoring systems to detect and address domain registrations that infringe on trademark or personal name rights.
What to monitor: Watch for typosquatting variations and unauthorized use of names that may confuse the public or mimic official sources.

items.title: Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial
items.title_zh: Feed诉The Feed：欧盟在先权利胜诉法国商业成功
items.title_en: Paris Court Annuls 'Feed' Marks: EU Prior Rights Prevail Over Local Commercial Success
items.summary: Par Maître Etienne Bucher, avocat au Barreau de Paris – cabinet Eris Avocat Le 18 février 2026, le Tribunal judiciaire de Paris a prononcé l’annulation de quatre marques françaises comportant le signe « Feed » et a condamné in solidum la société O.K.R (anciennement Feed SA) et son fondateur à verser 179 416,41 € à la société américaine […] L’article Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月18日，巴黎司法法院撤销了O.K.R公司（原Feed SA）持有的四个含“Feed”字样的法国商标。法院认定美国公司的欧盟商标具有在先权利，其效力优于被告在法国市场的商业成功。被告及其创始人被判连带赔偿约17.9万欧元。此案确立了欧盟层面在先商标权在对抗成员国注册商标时的优先地位，强调了权利基础而非市场知名度在确权中的决定性作用。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 18, 2026, the Paris Judicial Court ordered the cancellation of four French trademarks containing the sign 'Feed', held by O.K.R (formerly Feed SA). The court recognized the prior rights of a US company’s European Union trademark, ruling that such supranational priority outweighs any commercial success achieved by the defendant in the French market. Consequently, O.K.R and its founder were held jointly and severally liable to pay approximately €179,416.41 in damages. This decision reinforces the supremacy of EU-wide trademark rights over national registrations within member states. It establishes that mere market reputation or local commercial performance cannot serve as a valid defense against legitimate prior EU rights, emphasizing the critical importance of securing a clean legal basis for brands operating across Europe rather than relying solely on national registration strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局时，必须优先检索EUIPO数据库而非仅关注单一国家注册。即使已在某国建立市场声誉，若侵犯欧盟在先商标权，仍面临撤销及高额赔偿风险。建议采取“欧盟商标为主、成员国为辅”的策略，并在进入新市场前进行全面的FTO尽职调查，避免因权利瑕疵导致品牌资产归零。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must prioritize comprehensive searches within the EUIPO database rather than relying exclusively on single-country registration checks. Even if a brand has established substantial market reputation in a specific member state, it remains vulnerable to cancellation and high damages if it infringes upon an earlier EU trademark. A strategic approach should center on securing EU-wide protection as the primary asset, supplemented by national filings where necessary. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry is essential to mitigate the risk of brand assets being invalidated due to underlying rights defects, ensuring long-term stability and compliance with European IP frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎法院撤销四个含“Feed”的法国商标，确认欧盟在先商标权优先", "判决明确商业成功不能对抗合法的欧盟在先权利，被告需承担法律责任", "O.K.R公司及创始人被判连带赔偿17.9万欧元，凸显跨国品牌合规风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.", "The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.", "O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视欧盟商标在先检索，避免单一国家注册掩盖跨国侵权风险", "商业成功非抗辩理由，需确保品牌权利基础在欧盟层面的合法性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.", "Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.", "What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation."]
core_points_en: Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.
The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.
O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.
Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.
What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Show me your prior art
items.title_zh: UPC周报：展示你的现有技术
items.title_en: UPC Weekly - Show me your prior art
items.summary: 2026 Week 18
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系Mewburn Ellis发布的UPC第18周简报，标题“Show me your prior art”聚焦于统一专利法院诉讼中关于现有技术的披露与举证策略。文章 likely 探讨了在UPC程序中，当事人如何有效提交和论证现有技术以支持无效抗辩或侵权防御。作为欧洲专利诉讼的关键环节，现有技术的及时、规范披露直接影响案件走向。该资讯为从业者提供了关于UPC程序实务的重要观察与分析。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需高度重视现有技术的早期检索与规范化整理。应建立符合UPC程序要求的证据链，确保在法定期限内高效披露。同时，密切关注UPC法官对现有技术认定的倾向性，优化无效抗辩策略，以降低败诉风险并争取有利和解条件。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["聚焦UPC诉讼中现有技术披露的程序要求与实务策略", "分析现有技术证据提交对专利无效抗辩的关键影响", "提供律所视角下应对UPC程序规则的合规与辩护建议"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立符合UPC程序的现有技术证据管理体系", "关注法官认定倾向，优化无效抗辩与诉讼策略"]

items.title: Emotional Perception vs Inventive Step – Challenges Ahead?
items.title_zh: 情感感知案：创造性步骤评估的挑战与COMVIK方法的应用
items.title_en: Emotional Perception: Adopting COMVIK and Navigating Inventive Step Challenges in the UK
items.summary: In Emotional Perception the UK Supreme Court delivered its verdict on how the patent eligibility of computer-implemented inventions should be assessed in the UK. The decision was a tour de force , rejecting the much-maligned Aerotel approach favoured by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), instead putting the European COMVIK (sometimes referred to as the “any hardware”) approach at the heart of UK patent practice. However, the UK Supreme Court decision in Emotional Perception left behind
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在“情感感知”案中裁定，废弃UKIPO长期采用的Aerotel测试法，转而采纳欧洲专利局（EPO）的COMVIK方法作为英国专利实践中评估计算机实施发明适格性的核心标准。然而，该判决未明确COMVIK方法在创造性步骤评估中的具体实施细节。本文分析了这一遗留问题，指出UKIPO在未来执行创造性步骤审查时可能面临的挑战及法律不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court’s ruling in Emotional Perception marks a significant shift in assessing patent eligibility for computer-implemented inventions. The decision rejects the long-standing Aerotel test favored by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), formally adopting the European Patent Office’s COMVIK approach as the core standard for determining subject matter eligibility in UK practice. However, the judgment leaves critical implementation details unresolved, particularly regarding how the COMVIK framework should be applied during inventive step assessments. This analysis examines the resulting legal ambiguities and identifies potential operational challenges the UKIPO may face when integrating this new standard into examination practice, highlighting areas where further guidance is needed to ensure consistent application.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业赴英申请软件专利时，应摒弃旧有Aerotel思路，主动对标EPO COMVIK标准，强调技术贡献而非单纯商业逻辑。鉴于英国法院对创造性步骤评估尚存模糊，建议企业在撰写申请文件时强化硬件交互与技术问题解决过程的描述，以应对潜在审查风险并提升授权稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking patent protection for software-related inventions in the UK must align their drafting strategies with the EPO’s COMVIK standards rather than relying on previous Aerotel-based approaches. Emphasizing technical contributions and concrete hardware interactions is essential to demonstrate inventiveness under the new regime. Given the current ambiguity surrounding inventive step assessments, applicants should provide detailed descriptions of how technical problems are solved through specific system architectures. Monitoring forthcoming UKIPO examination guidelines will be crucial for adapting prosecution strategies and mitigating risks associated with the transitional period in legal interpretation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英最高法院废弃Aerotel测试，正式采纳EPO的COMVIK方法评估软件专利适格性", "判决未解决COMVIK方法在创造性步骤评估中的具体实施路径，留下法律模糊地带", "UKIPO未来在执行新标准时将面临操作挑战，需进一步明确审查指南以统一实践"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test with the EPO's COMVIK approach for assessing software patent eligibility.", "The judgment fails to clarify specific implementation paths for applying COMVIK during inventive step evaluations.", "The UKIPO faces operational challenges and legal uncertainty in executing the new inventive step assessment standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["对标EPO COMVIK标准，强化软件发明的技术贡献与硬件交互描述", "关注UKIPO后续审查指南，优化专利申请策略以应对创造性评估的不确定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Align software patent applications with EPO COMVIK standards, emphasizing technical contributions over pure business logic.", "Practice note: Strengthen descriptions of hardware interactions and technical problem-solving processes to enhance grant stability.", "What to monitor: Watch for updated UKIPO examination guidelines addressing inventive step assessments under the COMVIK framework."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test with the EPO's COMVIK approach for assessing software patent eligibility.
The judgment fails to clarify specific implementation paths for applying COMVIK during inventive step evaluations.
The UKIPO faces operational challenges and legal uncertainty in executing the new inventive step assessment standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Align software patent applications with EPO COMVIK standards, emphasizing technical contributions over pure business logic.
Practice note: Strengthen descriptions of hardware interactions and technical problem-solving processes to enhance grant stability.
What to monitor: Watch for updated UKIPO examination guidelines addressing inventive step assessments under the COMVIK framework.
