items.title: The UPC’s Fujifilm v Kodak decision significantly qualifies its long-arm jurisdiction over the UK
items.title_zh: UPC富士胶片诉柯达案裁决显著限制其对英长臂管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Fujifilm v Kodak Ruling Significantly Limits Long-Arm Jurisdiction Over UK Defendants
items.summary: The January ruling adds strategic limitations to the use of UPC long-reach powers, write lawyers from Taylor Wessing
items.ai_summary_zh: Taylor Wessing律师指出，统一专利法院（UPC）在富士胶片诉柯达案中的一月裁决，为UPC行使针对英国的“长臂管辖”权力增加了战略性限制。该判决明确了UPC在涉及非成员国（如英国）被告时的管辖权边界，限制了其通过长臂管辖原则扩大案件受理范围的能力。这一决定改变了此前市场对UPC广泛管辖权的预期，对涉及英国实体的跨国专利诉讼策略产生深远影响，要求当事人在选择诉讼地时更加谨慎评估管辖权风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Lawyers from Taylor Wessing highlight that the Unified Patent Court’s January decision in Fujifilm v Kodak imposes significant strategic limitations on its long-arm jurisdiction powers. This ruling clarifies the boundaries of UPC jurisdiction when defendants are located in non-member states, such as the United Kingdom. Consequently, the court’s ability to expand case acceptance through long-arm principles is restricted. This development alters previous market expectations regarding the UPC’s broad jurisdictional reach. It has profound implications for cross-border patent litigation strategies involving UK entities, requiring parties to carefully assess jurisdictional risks when selecting forums for legal proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉诉需重新评估UPC管辖风险，尤其当涉及英国关联主体时。建议调整全球诉讼布局，避免过度依赖UPC的广泛管辖权。在制定应对策略时，应结合英国法院与UPC的不同程序特点，精准选择有利法域，以降低法律成本并提升维权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must reassess the certainty of UPC jurisdiction in cases involving UK-related subjects. Global litigation portfolios should be optimized to mitigate risks associated with limited long-arm reach. When formulating enforcement or defense strategies, parties should leverage the distinct procedural characteristics of both UK courts and the UPC. Precise selection of favorable jurisdictions can reduce legal costs and enhance enforcement efficiency. Avoiding over-reliance on the UPC’s previously assumed broad jurisdiction is now essential for effective dispute resolution planning.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC裁决明确限制了对英国被告的长臂管辖权适用范围", "判决为UPC行使长臂管辖权力增加了关键的战略性限制条件", "该案改变了涉及英国实体跨国专利诉讼的管辖权预期与策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC ruling explicitly restricts the scope of long-arm jurisdiction applicable to defendants based in the UK.", "The judgment introduces critical strategic limitations on how the UPC exercises its extended jurisdictional powers.", "This decision reshapes jurisdictional expectations and litigation strategies for multinational cases involving UK entities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新评估UPC对含英国主体案件的管辖确定性", "优化全球诉讼布局以规避管辖权受限风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess jurisdictional certainty under the UPC for cases involving UK-based entities or connections.", "Practice note: Optimize global litigation layouts to avoid risks stemming from restricted long-arm jurisdiction capabilities.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the interface between member state jurisdiction and non-member state defendants."]
core_points_en: The UPC ruling explicitly restricts the scope of long-arm jurisdiction applicable to defendants based in the UK.
The judgment introduces critical strategic limitations on how the UPC exercises its extended jurisdictional powers.
This decision reshapes jurisdictional expectations and litigation strategies for multinational cases involving UK entities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess jurisdictional certainty under the UPC for cases involving UK-based entities or connections.
Practice note: Optimize global litigation layouts to avoid risks stemming from restricted long-arm jurisdiction capabilities.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the interface between member state jurisdiction and non-member state defendants.

items.title: Trademark News: What Flew Under the Radar in April 2026
items.title_zh: 2026年4月欧洲商标动态：CJEU裁决与EUIPO新政
items.title_en: April 2026 Trademark Roundup: CJEU Ruling on Prior Rights and EUIPO DSA Cooperation
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.Argentina eases technology transfer and trademark registration processesOn 31 March 2026, Argentina’s National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) introduced measures to simplify the registration of technology transfer agreements and speed up the trademark registration process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文综述了2026年4月全球商标要闻，重点聚焦欧洲。欧盟法院在C-337/22案中裁定，依据《欧盟商标条例》第8(4)条提起异议时，所依赖的在先权利必须在申请日及EUIPO作出决定日均有效。此外，EUIPO与欧盟委员会签署五年协议，依据《数字服务法》加强打击在线假冒与盗版。其他非欧动态包括阿根廷简化注册、巴西加入马德里电子提交系统及印度Waymo禁令案。
items.ai_summary_en: This monthly briefing highlights key trademark developments from April 2026, with a primary focus on European jurisprudence and policy. The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled in Case C-337/22 that prior rights invoked under Article 8(4) EUTMR must remain valid not only at the filing date but also when the EUIPO issues its decision. Concurrently, the EUIPO and the European Commission signed a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to intensify efforts against online counterfeiting. Global updates include Argentina’s simplification of technology transfer and trademark registration processes, Brazil becoming the first Latin American country to join the WIPO Madrid e-Filing service, and an interim injunction granted by the Delhi High Court in favor of Waymo. These developments reflect ongoing shifts in procedural requirements, digital enforcement frameworks, and international filing efficiencies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧企业需建立全周期权利监控机制，确保证据链覆盖从申请到裁决全过程以应对CJEU新标准。同时应重视DSA合规，利用EUIPO新机制主动维权。建议关注马德里体系电子化趋势，优化国际注册策略以降低管理成本并提升响应速度。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe should implement robust monitoring mechanisms to ensure evidence chains cover the entire lifecycle from application to final decision, aligning with the new CJEU standard for prior rights validity. Compliance with the Digital Services Act is increasingly critical; companies should leverage the enhanced cooperation between the EUIPO and the European Commission to proactively address online infringement. Furthermore, market participants should adapt their international registration strategies to accommodate the growing digitization of the Madrid System, particularly with Brazil’s integration into e-Filing, to optimize management costs and improve response times across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定异议程序中的在先权利需在申请日及决定日均持续存在", "EUIPO与欧盟委员会签约强化DSA框架下在线打假执法力度", "多国推进数字化改革，如巴西接入马德里e-Filing及阿根廷简化流程"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules that prior rights in opposition proceedings must exist continuously from the application filing date through to the final decision date.", "EUIPO and the European Commission sign a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to strengthen actions against online counterfeiting and piracy.", "Brazil joins the Madrid e-Filing service as the first Latin American member, while Argentina introduces measures to simplify registration processes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确保证据链覆盖全程以应对CJEU对在先权利存续时间的新要求", "利用DSA新机制强化在线维权并适应马德里体系电子化趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure continuous proof of prior rights existence from filing to decision to satisfy CJEU opposition standards.", "Practice note: Leverage the new EUIPO-EC Digital Services Act framework to enhance proactive online anti-counterfeiting enforcement.", "What to monitor: Track the expansion of Madrid e-Filing adoption and evolving electronic filing rules in key non-European markets."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules that prior rights in opposition proceedings must exist continuously from the application filing date through to the final decision date.
EUIPO and the European Commission sign a five-year agreement under the Digital Services Act to strengthen actions against online counterfeiting and piracy.
Brazil joins the Madrid e-Filing service as the first Latin American member, while Argentina introduces measures to simplify registration processes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure continuous proof of prior rights existence from filing to decision to satisfy CJEU opposition standards.
Practice note: Leverage the new EUIPO-EC Digital Services Act framework to enhance proactive online anti-counterfeiting enforcement.
What to monitor: Track the expansion of Madrid e-Filing adoption and evolving electronic filing rules in key non-European markets.

items.title: How To Achieve Efficient Trademark Renewals Through Outsourcing
items.title_zh: 通过外包实现高效商标续展的策略分析
items.title_en: Optimizing EU Trademark Renewals: Leveraging Outsourcing for Efficiency and Cost Control
items.summary: If you’re looking to outsource your trademark renewals, you’ll need a provider you can trust to deliver the best possible service at a sensible cost.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨企业如何通过外包优化欧盟及法国的商标续展流程。文章指出，选择可靠的服务提供商需平衡内部干预与外部信任。以Questel为例，其提供单一联系人机制、无纸化网络平台及优化的代理网络，旨在降低行政负担、确保成本效益并提升服务质量。该模式强调通过API集成和严格的质量验证，为企业提供更省心、高效的知识产权管理服务。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines how companies can optimize trademark renewal processes in the European Union and France through strategic outsourcing. It highlights that selecting a reliable service provider requires balancing internal oversight with external trust. Using Questel as a case study, the text details a solution featuring a single point of contact, a paperless web platform, and an optimized agent network. These elements aim to reduce administrative burdens, ensure cost-effectiveness, and enhance service quality. The model emphasizes peace of mind through digital integration and strict quality verification, allowing rights holders to manage renewals efficiently while maintaining control over their intellectual property portfolios without excessive internal resource allocation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视商标续展的合规性与效率，利用专业外包服务规避因程序疏漏导致的权利丧失风险。建议评估服务商的数字化能力与本地代理网络质量，通过API集成实现全球IP资产统一管理，从而在控制成本的同时提升品牌保护的安全性与响应速度。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize compliance and efficiency in trademark renewals by leveraging specialized outsourcing services. This approach helps mitigate risks of rights loss due to procedural oversights. Companies are advised to evaluate providers based on their digital capabilities and the quality of local agent networks. Integrating these services via API facilitates unified global IP asset management. This strategy allows organizations to control costs effectively while enhancing the security and responsiveness of brand protection measures across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["建立单一联系点机制，简化客户与管理方沟通，显著降低行政负担", "采用无纸化平台与API集成，实现全天候监控及数据无缝对接", "持续优化代理网络并通过批量协议降低成本，同时严格验证文件质量"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Establishing a single point of contact simplifies communication between clients and providers, significantly reducing administrative burdens.", "Implementing a paperless platform with API integration enables twenty-four-hour monitoring and seamless data connectivity.", "Continuously optimizing the agent network and utilizing volume agreements lowers costs while strictly verifying document quality."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用专业外包规避程序风险，确保欧盟商标权利稳定延续", "评估服务商数字化能力，通过API集成实现全球IP资产高效管理"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize professional outsourcing to avoid procedural risks and ensure the stable continuation of EU trademark rights.", "Practice note: Evaluate provider digital capabilities and leverage API integration for efficient global IP asset management.", "What to monitor: Assess the balance between internal intervention and provider trust to maintain optimal renewal workflows."]
core_points_en: Establishing a single point of contact simplifies communication between clients and providers, significantly reducing administrative burdens.
Implementing a paperless platform with API integration enables twenty-four-hour monitoring and seamless data connectivity.
Continuously optimizing the agent network and utilizing volume agreements lowers costs while strictly verifying document quality.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize professional outsourcing to avoid procedural risks and ensure the stable continuation of EU trademark rights.
Practice note: Evaluate provider digital capabilities and leverage API integration for efficient global IP asset management.
What to monitor: Assess the balance between internal intervention and provider trust to maintain optimal renewal workflows.

items.title: Managing Intellectual Property Recordals—A Brainteaser For In-House Legal Teams
items.title_zh: 管理IP登记：企业内部法务团队的难题
items.title_en: Managing IP Recordals: Strategic Timing and Documentation for In-House Teams
items.summary: The process of managing an intellectual property (IP) recordal project can be complex for an in-house IP legal team since it requires detailed and accurate information specific to the applicable countries and jurisdictions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在欧盟及成员国管理知识产权（IP）登记项目复杂且具挑战性。内部法务团队需处理特定法域的详细信息、文件准备及费用问题。建议分三步走：首先评估时机，权衡延期至续展以节省成本与即时登记以避免法律风险；其次组织文件，建立系统或委托外部代理以提高效率；最后分配预算与资源，确保项目顺利执行，避免因记录错误引发财务和法律损失。
items.ai_summary_en: Managing intellectual property recordal projects in the European Union and its member states presents significant complexity for in-house legal teams. These initiatives require precise handling of jurisdiction-specific details, meticulous document preparation, and careful fee management. The process involves balancing strategic timing to optimize costs while mitigating legal risks associated with delayed filings. Furthermore, the administrative burden of organizing varied documentation across different regions necessitates robust systems or external support. Effective execution demands accurate budgeting and resource allocation to prevent discrepancies in ownership records. Such errors can lead to substantial financial losses and legal vulnerabilities. This overview highlights the critical steps for planning and implementing successful IP recordals, ensuring compliance and protecting asset value across diverse European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧扩张时，应建立统一的IP登记监控机制，避免仅依赖续展节点而忽视变更登记的时效性。针对多国差异，建议引入专业代理机构或数字化工具管理文档，确保权属清晰，降低因程序瑕疵导致的维权障碍及潜在罚款风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders operating across Europe, relying solely on renewal cycles for IP updates creates exposure to procedural gaps. A unified monitoring mechanism ensures that changes in ownership or licensing are recorded promptly, regardless of renewal dates. Given the divergent documentary requirements among EU member states, leveraging specialized external agencies or digital management tools is advisable. This approach minimizes the risk of procedural defects that could hinder enforcement actions or result in fines. Maintaining clear and current records is fundamental to securing robust legal protection and avoiding unnecessary administrative burdens during critical enforcement periods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IP登记需结合具体法域要求，平衡登记时机以优化成本并规避法律风险", "文档管理繁杂，应建立标准化流程或授权外部代理以提升跨国登记效率", "需精准规划预算与资源，防止因信息滞后导致权利归属错误及经济损失"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["IP recordals must align with specific jurisdictional requirements, balancing timing to optimize costs and avoid legal risks.", "Document management is complex; establishing standardized processes or engaging external agents improves cross-border efficiency.", "Precise budget and resource planning are essential to prevent ownership errors and subsequent financial or legal losses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立统一监控机制，避免仅依赖续展节点，确保变更登记及时准确", "利用外部代理或数字化工具应对多国文档差异，降低程序瑕疵风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish a unified monitoring system to ensure timely and accurate recording of IP changes, rather than depending exclusively on renewal deadlines.", "Practice note: Utilize external specialists or digital solutions to manage varying national documentation standards and reduce procedural error risks.", "What to monitor: Track jurisdiction-specific filing requirements and deadlines to prevent ownership discrepancies that could compromise enforcement capabilities."]
core_points_en: IP recordals must align with specific jurisdictional requirements, balancing timing to optimize costs and avoid legal risks.
Document management is complex; establishing standardized processes or engaging external agents improves cross-border efficiency.
Precise budget and resource planning are essential to prevent ownership errors and subsequent financial or legal losses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish a unified monitoring system to ensure timely and accurate recording of IP changes, rather than depending exclusively on renewal deadlines.
Practice note: Utilize external specialists or digital solutions to manage varying national documentation standards and reduce procedural error risks.
What to monitor: Track jurisdiction-specific filing requirements and deadlines to prevent ownership discrepancies that could compromise enforcement capabilities.

items.title: The UPC And License Agreements
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院与许可协议：关键考量
items.title_en: Unified Patent Court and License Agreements: Key Considerations for Rights Holders
items.summary: 2023 will most likely be the year the Unified Patent Court (UPC) will open its doors. With the imminent start of the new European court system, the underlying legal framework will also become applicable.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着统一专利法院（UPC）即将启动，其法律框架将自动适用于传统欧洲专利。文章指出，需审查现有及未来许可协议以应对新系统风险。重点包括：确定谁有权决定“选择退出”UPC管辖，这通常取决于谁负责维持专利；明确谁有权决定是否申请具有单一效力的欧洲专利；以及界定在新旧系统中谁拥有专利执行权。建议在未来合同中明确这些权利归属，以避免争议并优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_summary_en: With the Unified Patent Court (UPC) set to commence operations, its legal framework will automatically apply to classic European patents unless an opt-out is declared. This shift introduces centralized enforcement benefits but also risks of centralized validity attacks. Consequently, rights holders must review existing and future license agreements to ensure compatibility with the new system. Critical issues include determining who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction, which often depends on maintenance obligations. Additionally, parties must clarify who decides whether to apply for a Unitary Patent and define enforcement rights across both traditional and new systems. Clear contractual provisions are essential to manage these strategic decisions effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应全面审查现有专利许可协议，明确UPC退出权及单一专利申请权的归属。建议在后续谈判中增加相关条款，以规避集中无效风险并掌握诉讼主动权。同时，需评估被许可人的执行权限，确保知识产权战略与新法院体系兼容，保护核心资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should comprehensively audit existing patent license agreements to clarify ownership of UPC opt-out rights and unitary patent application decisions. Future negotiations ought to include explicit clauses addressing these powers to mitigate risks associated with centralized invalidation actions. Furthermore, evaluating licensee enforcement permissions ensures that intellectual property strategies remain compatible with the new court structure. Proactively establishing clear rules helps secure core assets and maintains control over litigation strategies in the evolving European patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["审查许可协议以确定谁有权决定从UPC系统选择退出", "明确谁有权决定是否申请具有单一效力的欧洲专利", "界定在UPC及传统系统中谁拥有专利执行权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Review license agreements to determine who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction.", "Clarify which party has the right to decide whether to apply for a European patent with unitary effect.", "Define enforcement rights within both the UPC system and traditional national court frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审查协议明确UPC退出权及单一专利申请权归属", "增加条款明确执行权限，规避集中无效风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Audit existing licenses to clarify UPC opt-out and unitary patent decision rights.", "Practice note: Include explicit contractual clauses defining enforcement powers to mitigate central invalidation risks.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of licensee enforcement authority under the new UPC framework."]
core_points_en: Review license agreements to determine who holds the authority to decide on opting out of UPC jurisdiction.
Clarify which party has the right to decide whether to apply for a European patent with unitary effect.
Define enforcement rights within both the UPC system and traditional national court frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Audit existing licenses to clarify UPC opt-out and unitary patent decision rights.
Practice note: Include explicit contractual clauses defining enforcement powers to mitigate central invalidation risks.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of licensee enforcement authority under the new UPC framework.

items.title: Small town, big bite: local restaurant shows genuine use doesn’t need…
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院：单一餐厅本地使用可构成欧盟商标真实使用
items.title_en: General Court: Local Restaurant Use With Cross-Border Reach Qualifies As Genuine EU Trade Mark Use
items.summary: A single steakhouse, social media buzz and cross-border visibility: the General Court clarifies how “local” use in one member state can still amount to…
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-297/25案中裁定，德国一家位于小城的牛排馆“OX”虽仅在一国经营，但通过社交媒体、多语言在线广告及跨境客源，其商标影响力超越本地，构成欧盟范围内的真实使用。法院推翻了EUIPO以未使用为由撤销商标的决定，确认单一成员国的使用若具备跨境商业影响，即可维持欧盟商标权，强调服务类商标的使用地与宣传地可分离。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case T-297/25, the General Court ruled that a single steakhouse named “OX” in a small German town constituted genuine use of an EU trade mark. Although operating in only one member state, the business demonstrated cross-border influence through social media engagement, multilingual online advertising, and international clientele. The Court overturned the EUIPO’s initial revocation for non-use, confirming that local physical presence combined with digital visibility satisfies EU-wide requirements. This decision clarifies that genuine use does not require extensive geographical coverage across the Union. Instead, it emphasizes that service providers can maintain rights by proving their trade mark’s impact extends beyond immediate locality via digital channels and cross-border consumer interaction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营无需追求全境实体布局，应重视数字化营销与跨境流量转化。保留好在线广告、多语种用户反馈及国际订单等证据，可有效应对“不使用撤销”风险，以低成本维护欧盟商标权利。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating primarily in one EU member state should prioritize robust documentation of digital marketing efforts and cross-border customer interactions to defend against non-use cancellation actions. Maintaining records of online advertisements, user feedback in multiple languages, and international bookings provides critical evidence of genuine use. This approach allows businesses to sustain EU trade mark protection without establishing physical operations across all member states. By leveraging digital presence to demonstrate broader market influence, companies can effectively mitigate cancellation risks while optimizing resource allocation for brand maintenance.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一成员国内的局部使用若具跨境商业影响，可认定为欧盟商标真实使用", "服务类商标的使用地不仅限于物理经营场所，还包括在线广告及数字足迹覆盖区", "社交媒体粉丝量、多语言评论及跨境客源是证明商标超出本地范围的关键证据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Local use within a single member state may constitute genuine EU trade mark use if it demonstrates significant cross-border commercial impact.", "For service marks, the place of use includes areas covered by online advertising and digital footprints, not just physical service locations.", "Social media metrics, multilingual reviews, and evidence of cross-border customers are key proofs of market reach beyond local boundaries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化数字营销证据留存，以证明商标在欧盟市场的实际影响力", "利用跨境在线曝光弥补单一成员国实体经营的地理局限"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Documenting digital footprint and cross-border engagement helps validate genuine use for localized service businesses.", "Practice note: Preserve evidence of multilingual online ads, social media activity, and international customer data to counter non-use claims.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding the separation between physical service locations and digital trade mark exposure."]
core_points_en: Local use within a single member state may constitute genuine EU trade mark use if it demonstrates significant cross-border commercial impact.
For service marks, the place of use includes areas covered by online advertising and digital footprints, not just physical service locations.
Social media metrics, multilingual reviews, and evidence of cross-border customers are key proofs of market reach beyond local boundaries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Documenting digital footprint and cross-border engagement helps validate genuine use for localized service businesses.
Practice note: Preserve evidence of multilingual online ads, social media activity, and international customer data to counter non-use claims.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding the separation between physical service locations and digital trade mark exposure.

items.title: Wikipedia won’t save you: why professionally drafted evidence is key
items.title_zh: 维基百科非铁证：专业证据在商标异议中的关键作用
items.title_en: UKIPO Rejects Wikipedia Evidence: Why Professional Proof Wins Trademark Oppositions
items.summary: OpenAI, the developer behind the AI chatbot ChatGPT, has recently succeeded in its opposition to the application for the word mark “CallGPT”, filed by…
items.ai_summary_zh: OpenAI成功反对XEROTECH的“CallGPT”商标申请。审查员认定两商标视觉、听觉及概念上存在中高度相似。尽管申请人提供维基百科等链接证明“GPT”含义，但因来源不可靠且缺乏公众认知度证据，未被采纳。相反，OpenAI通过大量用户数据证明CHATGPT获得显著显著性，最终胜诉。此案强调高质量量化证据的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: OpenAI successfully opposed XEROTECH LTD’s “CallGPT” trademark application in the UK, highlighting the critical role of evidence quality. The hearing officer found medium to high visual and aural similarity between “CallGPT” and OpenAI’s “CHATGPT,” noting that “GPT” lacks clear meaning for average consumers. Crucially, the applicant’s reliance on Wikipedia links to define “GPT” was rejected as inherently unreliable. This case underscores that unverified web sources are insufficient to establish descriptive meanings or consumer understanding in UK opposition proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces that UKIPO hearing officers demand rigorous, verifiable evidence over easily accessible but unverified online sources. Rights holders must avoid relying on general internet definitions to argue descriptiveness or lack of distinctiveness. Instead, submissions should include concrete data demonstrating actual consumer perception and market usage to withstand scrutiny during opposition proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["审查员认定CallGPT与CHATGPT在多维度存在中高度相似性", "依赖维基百科等网络来源证明术语含义因可靠性不足被驳回", "基于直接用户参与数据的量化证据有效确立了商标的获得显著性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Hearing officer found medium to high similarity between CallGPT and CHATGPT based on shared initial letter and identical suffix.", "Wikipedia links were rejected as evidence because the source is considered inherently unreliable for establishing consumer knowledge.", "Applicant failed to prove GPT is a widely understood descriptor among average UK customers without robust market data."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["摒弃单一网络来源，构建多维度的专业证据体系", "利用量化市场数据强化商标显著性主张"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Unverified web sources like Wikipedia are insufficient to prove technical terms are widely understood by consumers.", "Practice note: Submit robust, verifiable market data rather than generic internet printouts to establish or rebut distinctiveness claims.", "What to monitor: How UKIPO applies evidentiary standards regarding technical acronyms and consumer awareness in future AI-related disputes."]
core_points_en: Hearing officer found medium to high similarity between CallGPT and CHATGPT based on shared initial letter and identical suffix.
Wikipedia links were rejected as evidence because the source is considered inherently unreliable for establishing consumer knowledge.
Applicant failed to prove GPT is a widely understood descriptor among average UK customers without robust market data.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Unverified web sources like Wikipedia are insufficient to prove technical terms are widely understood by consumers.
Practice note: Submit robust, verifiable market data rather than generic internet printouts to establish or rebut distinctiveness claims.
What to monitor: How UKIPO applies evidentiary standards regarding technical acronyms and consumer awareness in future AI-related disputes.

items.title: Going nowhere with a bathing ape: earlier rights in EUTM oppositions
items.title_zh: CJEU裁定：欧盟商标异议中在先权利须在决定时持续有效
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Earlier Rights Must Remain Valid at EUIPO Decision in EU Trade Mark Oppositions
items.summary: A Bathing Ape (BAPE) is a famous, luxury, Japanese fashion brand. The eponymous BAPE is sometimes stern and sometimes very cute. The cute BAPE is Baby…
items.ai_summary_zh: 日本品牌BAPE因英国未注册权在异议期间因脱欧失效，遭EUIPO驳回。CJEU在C-337/22 P案中裁定，主张在先权利不仅需在争议商标申请日存在，还必须在EUIPO作出最终决定时保持有效且可执行。若权利在程序中灭失，异议即失败。此判决澄清了法律不确定性，恢复了EUIPO的既有做法，强调权利在整个诉讼期间的稳定性至关重要。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) clarified in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22 P) that earlier rights relied upon in EU trade mark oppositions must remain valid and enforceable at the time the EUIPO issues its final decision. The case involved BAPE’s opposition based on UK unregistered rights, which lapsed due to Brexit during proceedings. The CJEU confirmed that existence at the filing date is insufficient; rights must persist throughout the entire procedural lifecycle. This restores the EUIPO’s established approach, rejecting the argument that rights need only exist at the application stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权时，应避免仅依赖易失效的短期或未注册权利。建议优先选择稳定的注册商标作为异议基础，并建立动态监控机制，确保在漫长的异议及上诉期内，在先权利持续有效，防止因权利瑕疵导致维权失败。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly increases the temporal risk for opponents in EU trade mark proceedings. Rights holders must ensure their basis for opposition remains robust against expiration, non-use revocation, or invalidation throughout potentially multi-year proceedings including appeals. Reliance on rights with shorter lifespans or those susceptible to jurisdictional changes requires heightened diligence to prevent fatal procedural failures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定在先权利须在EUIPO最终决定时仍有效且可执行", "异议期间权利若灭失将直接导致异议失败，风险贯穿全程", "权利人需加强组合管理，确保证书续展及维持使用以防失效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights must be valid and enforceable at the time the EUIPO issues its final decision, not just at the contested application's filing date.", "If an earlier right expires, is revoked, or loses effect during opposition proceedings, the opposition fails regardless of its initial validity.", "This ruling resolves legal uncertainty by confirming that the relevant period for assessing earlier rights spans the entire life of the opposition proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先选用稳定注册商标，规避短期或未注册权利的失效风险", "建立异议期权利动态监控机制，确保证书续展及使用合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Opponents face extended risk exposure as earlier rights must survive until the final EUIPO decision, impacting portfolio stability assessments.", "Practice note: Prioritize stable registered rights over short-term or unregistered assets when initiating oppositions to mitigate lapse risks.", "What to monitor: Track renewal deadlines, use requirements, and potential invalidity actions affecting earlier rights throughout the opposition timeline."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights must be valid and enforceable at the time the EUIPO issues its final decision, not just at the contested application's filing date.
If an earlier right expires, is revoked, or loses effect during opposition proceedings, the opposition fails regardless of its initial validity.
This ruling resolves legal uncertainty by confirming that the relevant period for assessing earlier rights spans the entire life of the opposition proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Opponents face extended risk exposure as earlier rights must survive until the final EUIPO decision, impacting portfolio stability assessments.
Practice note: Prioritize stable registered rights over short-term or unregistered assets when initiating oppositions to mitigate lapse risks.
What to monitor: Track renewal deadlines, use requirements, and potential invalidity actions affecting earlier rights throughout the opposition timeline.

items.title: Abus de procédure pour une demande de nullité de la marque BORIS BECKER devant l’EUIPO ?
items.title_zh: EUIPO裁定BORIS BECKER商标无效申请涉嫌程序滥用
items.title_en: EUIPO Board Rules on Alleged Procedural Abuse in BORIS BECKER Trademark Nullity Case
items.summary: Par Julie DESROIS, CPI au Cabinet Chaillot   Abus de procédure pour une demande de nullité de la marque BORIS BECKER devant l’EUIPO ? Décision R 760/2024-2 du 10/02/2026 de la 2ème Chambre de Recours de l’EUIPO  L’abus de procédure se définit comme l’utilisation détournée ou excessive des mécanismes judiciaires dans le but de nuire à […] L’article Abus de procédure pour une demande de nullité de la marque BORIS BECKER devant l’EUIPO ? est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析了EUIPO第二上诉委员会在R 760/2024-2号决定中的观点。该案涉及“BORIS BECKER”商标的无效宣告请求。委员会探讨了程序滥用的定义，即出于损害目的而扭曲或过度使用司法机制。该分析旨在厘清在欧盟商标异议或无效程序中，何种行为可能构成程序滥用，为权利人应对恶意诉讼提供法律依据和实务参考。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the EUIPO Second Board of Appeal’s decision in case R 760/2024-2, concerning a nullity application against the BORIS BECKER trademark. The Board addressed allegations of procedural abuse, defining it as the distorted or excessive use of judicial mechanisms with the specific intent to cause harm. The article clarifies the legal boundaries for identifying such abusive conduct within EU trademark opposition and invalidation proceedings. By establishing clear criteria for what constitutes procedural abuse, the ruling provides essential guidance for rights holders facing bad-faith litigation strategies. It serves as a critical reference for understanding how the EUIPO balances legitimate enforcement actions against tactics designed solely to harass competitors or burden opponents with unnecessary legal costs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧进行品牌布局时，应警惕对方利用无效程序进行恶意骚扰。若遭遇无实质理由的反复攻击，可援引此裁决主张程序滥用以维护权益。建议建立完善的证据链，记录对方的不当诉讼行为，并在答辩中积极提出反制措施，以降低维权成本并遏制恶意竞争。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant against opponents attempting to weaponize invalidation procedures for malicious harassment. When facing repetitive attacks lacking substantive merit, parties may invoke this ruling to claim procedural abuse as a defensive strategy. It is advisable to maintain comprehensive records of opposing counsel's conduct to support such claims. Establishing a robust evidence chain documenting improper litigation behavior allows rights holders to counteract bad-faith tactics effectively. This approach helps mitigate legal expenses and deters unfair competitive practices by demonstrating that the proceedings are being used primarily to cause damage rather than to resolve genuine intellectual property disputes.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO Board of Appeal adjudicated allegations of procedural abuse within the context of a BORIS BECKER trademark nullity action.", "Procedural abuse is defined as the distorted or excessive utilization of judicial mechanisms specifically intended to inflict harm on another party.", "The ruling offers practical guidance for regulating conduct in EU trademark invalidation and opposition procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕对手利用无效程序恶意骚扰，需及时主张程序滥用权利", "完善证据留存，记录对方不当诉讼行为以支持反制主张"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can leverage this decision to counter bad-faith invalidation attempts aimed at harassing competitors without legal merit.", "Practice note: Maintain detailed documentation of opposing parties' conduct to substantiate claims of procedural abuse during proceedings.", "What to monitor: Watch for patterns of repetitive, unsubstantiated filings that may indicate an intent to misuse judicial mechanisms for harm."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO Board of Appeal adjudicated allegations of procedural abuse within the context of a BORIS BECKER trademark nullity action.
Procedural abuse is defined as the distorted or excessive utilization of judicial mechanisms specifically intended to inflict harm on another party.
The ruling offers practical guidance for regulating conduct in EU trademark invalidation and opposition procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can leverage this decision to counter bad-faith invalidation attempts aimed at harassing competitors without legal merit.
Practice note: Maintain detailed documentation of opposing parties' conduct to substantiate claims of procedural abuse during proceedings.
What to monitor: Watch for patterns of repetitive, unsubstantiated filings that may indicate an intent to misuse judicial mechanisms for harm.

items.title: Transforming IP management: the shift from digital tools to intelligent systems
items.title_zh: IP管理变革：从数字工具向智能系统的演进
items.title_en: Transforming IP management: the shift from digital tools to intelligent systems
items.summary: IP portfolio platforms are moving beyond databases and dashboards. See how transparent AI enables smarter, auditable support for professional workflows.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出传统IP管理软件已沦为基线功能，无法应对日益增长的数据量与操作压力。新一代IP管理系统需超越数据库模式，利用透明AI技术实现自然语言交互、自动化流程指导及非结构化数据处理。这种转变将认知负担从用户转移至系统，提升审计透明度与工作流效率，标志着IP管理从被动记录向主动智能辅助的根本性范式转移。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局应关注IP管理工具的智能化升级，利用AI处理多法域复杂数据以提升合规效率。建议优先选择支持自然语言交互且符合欧盟数据透明要求的平台，以降低人工成本并增强决策的可审计性，从而在激烈的国际竞争中保持敏捷响应能力。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["传统IPMS仅作为数据库，认知负担在用户，已无法满足当前复杂需求", "引入透明AI实现自然语言搜索与上下文理解，降低使用门槛并提升效率", "系统具备处理邮件等非结构化数据能力，提供步骤化指引以加速团队上手"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采用支持自然语言交互的AI工具，降低多法域IP管理的认知负荷", "确保系统符合欧盟数据透明要求，提升合规审计能力与决策效率"]

items.title: HOFFMANN EITLE retains #1 position for European patent filings at the EPO
items.title_zh: HOFFMANN EITLE蝉联EPO欧洲专利申请量榜首
items.title_en: HOFFMANN EITLE Retains Top Position for European Patent Filings at the EPO
items.summary: The latest IP Pilot report once again highlights HOFFMANN EITLE as the leading firm for E
items.ai_summary_zh: 根据IP Pilot最新报告，HOFFMANN EITLE在2023至2025年间向欧洲专利局（EPO）提交超过13,000件申请，再次位居首位。该排名证实了该所在欧洲专利 prosecution 领域的持续领导力，反映了其在管理跨行业、跨司法管辖区复杂专利组合方面的规模、技术专长及一致性。此举旨在展示其应对不断演变的市场环境的能力，并感谢客户信任与团队承诺。
items.ai_summary_en: The latest IP Pilot report identifies HOFFMANN EITLE as the leading firm for European patent filings at the European Patent Office (EPO). Between 2023 and 2025, the firm filed more than 13,000 applications, securing the number one position. This ranking underscores the firm's sustained leadership in European patent prosecution, highlighting its capacity to manage complex patent portfolios across various industries and jurisdictions. The firm emphasizes its technical expertise and consistency in supporting innovation throughout the patent lifecycle in an evolving market environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局应关注头部律所的服务能力与稳定性。HOFFMANN EITLE的高吞吐量表明其具备处理大规模组合的经验。建议中资企业评估此类顶级律所在多法域协调及复杂案件处理上的实力，以优化海外专利资产质量，确保在激烈竞争中获得稳健的法律支持。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing substantial European patent portfolios, this ranking signals the operational scale and throughput capabilities of top-tier firms. The volume of filings suggests robust infrastructure for handling high-volume prosecution workloads. In-house counsel and IP teams may consider such metrics when evaluating external counsel for large-scale filing programs or complex multi-jurisdictional strategies. Monitoring these industry benchmarks helps assess market concentration and service provider stability within the European patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HOFFMANN EITLE三年间向EPO提交超1.3万件申请，稳居第一", "报告确认其在欧洲专利审查领域的持续领导地位与技术专长", "体现其管理跨行业复杂专利组合的规模优势与市场适应性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["HOFFMANN EITLE ranked first for EPO filings with over 13,000 applications submitted between 2023 and 2025.", "The IP Pilot report confirms the firm's leadership in European patent prosecution and portfolio management.", "The firm highlights its scale, technical expertise, and ability to handle complex cases across multiple jurisdictions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注头部律所处理大规模专利组合的能力与稳定性", "评估顶级律所在多法域协调及复杂案件中的专业实力"]

core_points_en: HOFFMANN EITLE ranked first for EPO filings with over 13,000 applications submitted between 2023 and 2025.
The IP Pilot report confirms the firm's leadership in European patent prosecution and portfolio management.
The firm highlights its scale, technical expertise, and ability to handle complex cases across multiple jurisdictions.

Practice note: Portfolio managers should assess external counsel's demonstrated throughput and consistency in EPO proceedings.
What to monitor: Industry reports like IP Pilot provide data on market leaders and shifting dynamics in European patent services.

items.title: Mobile Foot Measurement’s IP Battle Highlights Easily Overlooked Step In Startup Journey, Managing IP, 2026 – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 移动足部测量IP战凸显初创企业易忽视环节
items.title_en: Maiwald: Single Process Patent Shapes German Mobile Foot Measurement Market
items.summary: In an article for Managing IP, Dr Christian Meyer of Maiwald, in collaboration with Footprint Technologies, explains how a single process patent has had a
items.ai_summary_zh: Maiwald律所文章分析Footprint Technologies案例，其AI手机测脚技术通过单一方法专利深刻影响德国市场。文章强调早期且周密的IP战略对AI及软件初创企业至关重要，不仅保护技术，更关乎市场准入、竞争定位及长期商业成功。Maiwald协助该企业制定IP战略、进行侵权评估并构建塑造市场的专利组合。
items.ai_summary_en: In an article for Managing IP, Dr. Christian Meyer of Maiwald analyzes the impact of a single process patent on the German market for mobile foot measurement technologies. The piece focuses on Footprint Technologies, an AI-based startup using smartphones for precise foot scanning. Maiwald assisted the company with IP strategy, infringement assessment, and structuring a market-shaping patent portfolio. The analysis underscores how early, well-considered IP strategies are critical for AI and software startups, influencing not just technology protection but also market access, competitive positioning, and long-term commercial viability in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI出海企业应摒弃重研发轻IP观念，在进军欧洲前完成FTO检索。针对算法类创新，需注重方法专利布局而非仅依赖著作权。建议早期引入专业机构进行侵权风险评估，构建防御性专利组合，以避免因单一关键专利缺失导致的市场禁入风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the disproportionate leverage a single, well-drafted process patent can exert in specific European tech niches. For rights holders in AI and computer-implemented inventions, securing method claims early is as critical as protecting the underlying code or hardware. Effective portfolio management must align with commercial goals, ensuring that IP assets actively facilitate market entry rather than merely serving as defensive shields against competitors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一方法专利显著影响德国移动足部测量市场竞争格局", "早期周密IP战略决定AI初创企业的市场准入与商业成功", "专利组合构建需结合侵权评估以确立竞争优势"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A single process patent significantly influenced the competitive landscape of the German mobile foot measurement sector.", "Early IP strategy is vital for AI and software startups to secure market access and ensure long-term commercial success.", "Maiwald supported Footprint Technologies with infringement assessments and the structuring of a strategic patent portfolio."]

items.title: Withers & Rogers bets on Birmingham for new UK office
items.title_zh: Withers & Rogers押注伯明翰开设英国新办公室
items.title_en: Withers & Rogers Opens New Birmingham Office to Strengthen UK Regional Presence
items.summary: IP firm's new base will be located inside the tallest office space in the UK's ‘second city’
items.ai_summary_zh: 知名知识产权律师事务所Withers & Rogers宣布在英国“第二城市”伯明翰开设新办事处。该办公室将位于该市最高的办公空间内，旨在加强其在英国中部地区的法律服务能力。此举反映了顶级IP律所对英国区域市场潜力的重视，以及通过物理扩张来贴近客户、提升服务覆盖面的战略意图，是英国知识产权服务市场持续活跃的一个缩影。
items.ai_summary_en: Prominent intellectual property firm Withers & Rogers has announced the establishment of a new office in Birmingham, widely recognized as the UK's second city. The new base will be situated within the tallest office building in the region, signaling a significant commitment to expanding its legal service capabilities across the Midlands. This strategic move highlights the growing importance that top-tier IP firms place on regional markets beyond London. By physically expanding into key regional hubs, the firm aims to enhance client proximity and broaden its service coverage. This development serves as a notable indicator of the continued vitality and dynamic nature of the United Kingdom's intellectual property services market, reflecting broader trends of geographic diversification among leading legal providers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英布局应关注律所区域化服务趋势，利用伯明翰等枢纽降低沟通成本。选择具备广泛地域覆盖的律所有助于优化全球IP组合管理效率，确保在不同司法辖区获得及时、一致的法律支持，从而提升整体合规与维权效能。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should note the trend of major IP firms decentralizing from London to regional hubs like Birmingham. Establishing or partnering with firms possessing broad geographic coverage can significantly reduce communication barriers and management costs for clients operating across multiple jurisdictions. Selecting legal partners with robust regional networks ensures more timely and consistent support for global IP portfolios. This approach facilitates better alignment between local legal realities and corporate strategy, ultimately enhancing overall compliance efficiency and enforcement effectiveness across diverse European and UK markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Withers & Rogers在伯明翰设立新办公室以拓展英国中部业务", "新址位于英国第二大城市最高办公建筑内，彰显扩张决心", "此举体现顶尖IP律所对区域市场潜力及服务覆盖的重视"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Withers & Rogers establishes a new Birmingham office to expand its operational footprint and strengthen business presence in the UK Midlands region.", "The new location is situated in the tallest office building in Birmingham, demonstrating a strong commitment to regional expansion and market visibility.", "This initiative underscores the increasing focus of leading IP firms on regional market potential and the strategic value of enhanced service coverage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注律所区域扩张，利用枢纽城市降低在英沟通与管理成本", "优选广覆盖律所以优化全球IP组合管理，确保多地法律支持一致性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage regional hubs like Birmingham to lower communication and management costs while accessing specialized local expertise.", "Practice note: Prioritize law firms with extensive geographic coverage to optimize global IP portfolio management and ensure consistent multi-jurisdictional support.", "What to monitor: Track further regional expansions by top-tier IP firms to identify emerging service centers and potential partnership opportunities."]
core_points_en: Withers & Rogers establishes a new Birmingham office to expand its operational footprint and strengthen business presence in the UK Midlands region.
The new location is situated in the tallest office building in Birmingham, demonstrating a strong commitment to regional expansion and market visibility.
This initiative underscores the increasing focus of leading IP firms on regional market potential and the strategic value of enhanced service coverage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage regional hubs like Birmingham to lower communication and management costs while accessing specialized local expertise.
Practice note: Prioritize law firms with extensive geographic coverage to optimize global IP portfolio management and ensure consistent multi-jurisdictional support.

items.title: Patents are getting pricier – and policy is the reason
items.title_zh: 专利成本攀升：政策驱动下的价值重塑
items.title_en: Patent Value Rises as UPC Enforcement Stakes Increase and US Challenges Decline
items.summary: Long Read: As PTAB challenges dry up and the UPC raises enforcement stakes, patents are regaining clout—pushing companies into earlier, costlier deals and reshaping what IP is worth
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着美国PTAB挑战减少及统一专利法院（UPC）提高执法风险，专利影响力回升。这迫使企业更早进行昂贵交易，重塑知识产权估值体系。文章指出政策变化是专利变贵的核心原因，强调在UPC高 stakes环境下，企业需重新评估专利组合策略与许可谈判时机，以应对日益增加的成本与合规压力。
items.ai_summary_en: The intellectual property landscape is undergoing a significant shift driven by policy changes. With the decline of Patent Trial and Appeal Board challenges in the United States, patents are regaining their strategic clout. Simultaneously, the Unified Patent Court has raised enforcement stakes across Europe, increasing the potential costs and risks associated with infringement litigation. This dual dynamic is compelling market participants to engage in earlier and more expensive licensing negotiations to mitigate legal exposure. Consequently, the perceived value of patent portfolios is being reshaped, forcing companies to reassess their IP strategies. Rights holders must now navigate a complex environment where compliance pressures and acquisition costs are rising, requiring a more proactive approach to portfolio management and dispute resolution to maintain competitive advantage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应警惕UPC带来的高额诉讼风险，避免被动应诉。建议优化专利布局，加强FTO分析，并在早期通过许可谈判化解纠纷。同时，需密切关注欧洲IP政策动向，调整全球IP战略以应对成本上升趋势，确保合规与竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should proactively manage the heightened litigation risks introduced by the Unified Patent Court. Integrating freedom-to-operate analyses and initiating licensing discussions at earlier stages can help mitigate potential liabilities. It is crucial to monitor evolving European IP policies that impact maintenance costs and enforcement mechanisms. Companies should dynamically adjust their global IP strategies to align with these cost trends, ensuring robust compliance and sustained competitiveness. A strategic review of patent portfolios is recommended to optimize asset value while minimizing exposure to expensive enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC提升执法风险，促使专利重新获得市场话语权", "政策变动导致专利获取与维护成本显著上升", "企业被迫提前进行高价交易以规避潜在法律风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC enforcement increases litigation risks, restoring greater market influence and leverage to patent holders.", "Policy shifts significantly raise the costs associated with obtaining and maintaining patent rights globally.", "Companies face pressure to secure early, high-value licensing deals to avoid costly legal disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC执法风险，前置FTO分析与许可谈判", "关注政策成本变化，动态调整在欧专利战略布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate assessments and licensing negotiations to reduce UPC litigation exposure.", "Practice note: Monitor policy-driven cost increases and adapt global IP strategies to maintain compliance and competitiveness.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC enforcement trends and European IP policy changes affecting patent valuation and maintenance."]
core_points_en: UPC enforcement increases litigation risks, restoring greater market influence and leverage to patent holders.
Policy shifts significantly raise the costs associated with obtaining and maintaining patent rights globally.
Companies face pressure to secure early, high-value licensing deals to avoid costly legal disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate assessments and licensing negotiations to reduce UPC litigation exposure.
Practice note: Monitor policy-driven cost increases and adapt global IP strategies to maintain compliance and competitiveness.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC enforcement trends and European IP policy changes affecting patent valuation and maintenance.

items.title: Preliminary objections before the UPC: A clear boundary between jurisdiction and substance
items.title_zh: UPC初步异议：管辖权与实体问题的清晰界限
items.title_en: UPC Preliminary Objections: Strict Separation of Jurisdiction and Merits in REEL v Fives
items.summary: Discover what the Rules of Procedure of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) say about raising a preliminary objection and providing a response to such objections.
items.ai_summary_zh: 在REEL International诉Fives ECL专利撤销案中，UPC中央分部依据《程序规则》第19.1条驳回被告提出的初步异议。法院裁定，初步异议仅适用于法院管辖权、分部管辖权及诉讼语言三类情形。原告起诉利益及既判力等涉及实体权利的问题不属于初步异议范畴，需经完整事实审查后在实体阶段解决，防止程序滥用以拖延诉讼。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the Unified Patent Court’s refusal to accept a preliminary objection in the dispute between REEL International and Fives ECL. The defendant sought dismissal by challenging the plaintiff’s interest in bringing proceedings and invoking res judicata from prior German decisions. Relying on Rule 19.1 of the Rules of Procedure, the Court clarified that preliminary objections are strictly limited to three grounds: court jurisdiction, division competence, and language of proceedings. Consequently, issues regarding standing and res judicata were deemed substantive matters requiring full factual and legal examination, not procedural filters for early dismissal. The ruling reinforces the structural distinction between jurisdictional determinations and merits-based defenses, preventing parties from using preliminary motions to bypass comprehensive review of complex factual disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应精准识别程序策略。避免将实体抗辩误作初步异议提出，以免被快速驳回并暴露底牌。建议聚焦管辖权瑕疵进行早期阻击，同时将既判力等复杂实体问题留待全面审理阶段，结合证据充分论证，以优化诉讼资源分配并提升胜算。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the Unified Patent Court must precisely distinguish between procedural jurisdictional challenges and substantive defenses. Attempting to frame merit-based arguments, such as res judicata or lack of standing, as preliminary objections will likely fail under Rule 19.1. Parties should instead prepare robust factual and legal evidence for the main proceedings. Strategic planning must account for this strict separation, ensuring that defenses are integrated into the substantive phase rather than relying on quick procedural dismissals. This approach enhances litigation efficiency and avoids wasted resources on inadmissible preliminary motions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC初步异议仅限管辖权、分部管辖及语言三类，清单封闭", "起诉利益与既判力属实体问题，不得通过初步异议提前裁决", "法院拒绝将实体争议前置审理，坚持管辖与实质分离原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 19.1 restricts preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language of proceedings.", "Standing and res judicata are substantive issues requiring full evidentiary review, not procedural filters.", "The UPC rejects separating these substantive defenses into independent pre-merit hearings or orders."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["区分程序与实体抗辩，避免误用初步异议导致策略失效", "利用管辖权异议争取主动，实体争议留待全面审理阶段举证"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid mischaracterizing substantive defenses as preliminary objections to prevent procedural delays and strategic setbacks.", "Practice note: Prepare comprehensive factual records for standing and res judicata arguments intended for the merits phase.", "What to monitor: Future UPC case law clarifying the boundaries of Rule 19.1 and admissibility of related procedural requests."]
core_points_en: Rule 19.1 restricts preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language of proceedings.
Standing and res judicata are substantive issues requiring full evidentiary review, not procedural filters.
The UPC rejects separating these substantive defenses into independent pre-merit hearings or orders.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid mischaracterizing substantive defenses as preliminary objections to prevent procedural delays and strategic setbacks.
Practice note: Prepare comprehensive factual records for standing and res judicata arguments intended for the merits phase.
What to monitor: Future UPC case law clarifying the boundaries of Rule 19.1 and admissibility of related procedural requests.

items.title: Taylor Swift turns to trademarks over ‘blank space’ in AI law
items.title_zh: 泰勒·斯威夫特借商标法填补AI法律空白
items.title_en: Taylor Swift Uses Trademarks to Fill AI Law Gaps: Implications for Personality Rights
items.summary: As deepfakes blur the line between reality and replica, celebrities are turning to trademark law to protect their personas. Is it time to upgrade personality rights?
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着深度伪造技术模糊现实与复制品的界限，名人正转向商标法以保护其个人形象。本文探讨了在现有法律框架下，通过商标注册来抵御AI滥用个人身份的策略，并进一步追问是否应当升级人格权法律保护体系，以应对人工智能时代带来的新型侵权挑战，为欧洲知识产权实务提供前瞻性视角。
items.ai_summary_en: As deepfake technology increasingly blurs the distinction between authentic reality and digital replicas, high-profile celebrities are turning to trademark law to safeguard their personas. This commentary examines how rights holders utilize existing trademark frameworks to combat AI-driven misuse of personal identity. It further explores whether current legal structures require significant upgrades to personality rights protections. The analysis addresses new infringement challenges emerging in the artificial intelligence era, offering a forward-looking perspective for European intellectual property practitioners navigating the intersection of traditional IP laws and evolving digital threats.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，需警惕AI技术引发的品牌及代言人形象侵权风险。建议提前布局核心标识的商标防御性注册，特别是涵盖数字媒体类别。同时，密切关注欧盟关于人格权与知识产权交叉领域的立法动态，建立针对AI生成内容的监测与维权机制，以规避潜在的法律合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should remain vigilant regarding brand and spokesperson image infringement risks triggered by AI technologies. Proactive defensive trademark registration for core identifiers, particularly within digital media categories, is recommended to mitigate potential liabilities. Stakeholders must closely monitor legislative developments in the EU concerning the intersection of personality rights and intellectual property. Establishing robust monitoring and enforcement mechanisms for AI-generated content is essential to navigate compliance requirements and protect commercial interests effectively in this rapidly evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["深度伪造技术促使名人转向商标法寻求个人形象保护", "现行法律存在空白，引发关于升级人格权保护的讨论", "文章聚焦于商标策略在对抗AI侵权中的实际应用与局限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Deepfake technology is driving celebrities to seek trademark protection as a primary strategy for defending their personal images against unauthorized digital replication.", "Existing legal frameworks contain significant gaps regarding AI misuse, sparking urgent discussions about upgrading and modernizing personality right protections.", "The article focuses on the practical application and inherent limitations of using trademark strategies to counteract AI-related identity infringement risks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前布局数字媒体类别商标以防御AI形象侵权", "关注欧盟人格权立法动态并建立AI内容监测机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure defensive trademark registrations for core brands in digital media classes to prevent AI-driven identity misuse.", "Practice note: Implement systematic monitoring and enforcement protocols for AI-generated content to address emerging infringement vectors.", "What to monitor: Track EU legislative updates regarding the convergence of personality rights and intellectual property regulations."]
core_points_en: Deepfake technology is driving celebrities to seek trademark protection as a primary strategy for defending their personal images against unauthorized digital replication.
Existing legal frameworks contain significant gaps regarding AI misuse, sparking urgent discussions about upgrading and modernizing personality right protections.
The article focuses on the practical application and inherent limitations of using trademark strategies to counteract AI-related identity infringement risks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure defensive trademark registrations for core brands in digital media classes to prevent AI-driven identity misuse.
Practice note: Implement systematic monitoring and enforcement protocols for AI-generated content to address emerging infringement vectors.
What to monitor: Track EU legislative updates regarding the convergence of personality rights and intellectual property regulations.

items.title: In-house interview: Why tackling piracy is ‘like the war on drugs’
items.title_zh: 内部访谈：为何打击盗版如同“禁毒战争”
items.title_en: Ex-Disney Counsel Compares Global Anti-Piracy Efforts to the War on Drugs
items.summary: Ex-Disney counsel Thomas Limouzin-Lamothe tells Sarah Speight how he is combating global piracy in his new role with a major membership body for Hollywood studios, broadcasters and streaming platforms.
items.ai_summary_zh: 前迪士尼法律顾问Thomas Limouzin-Lamothe在接受World IP Review专访时，分享了其在新职位中应对全球盗版的策略。他目前服务于代表好莱坞制片厂、广播公司和流媒体平台的会员机构。Limouzin-Lamothe将打击盗版比作“禁毒战争”，强调这是一个持续且复杂的挑战，需要行业协作与持续努力来遏制非法传播，保护内容创作者的合法权益。
items.ai_summary_en: Former Disney legal counsel Thomas Limouzin-Lamothe discusses his strategic approach to combating global piracy in an interview with World IP Review. Now serving a major membership body representing Hollywood studios, broadcasters, and streaming platforms, he draws parallels between anti-piracy enforcement and the war on drugs. He emphasizes that tackling illegal content distribution is a complex, ongoing challenge requiring sustained industry collaboration. The discussion highlights the necessity for continuous efforts to curb unauthorized dissemination and protect the legitimate rights of content creators across international markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕全球版权执法趋严，尤其是流媒体领域。建议建立完善的数字版权合规体系，主动监控海外侵权风险。同时，关注国际行业联盟的反盗版动态，避免因版权瑕疵导致市场准入受阻或高额索赔，确保业务合规稳健发展。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants distributing digital content globally should anticipate increasingly stringent copyright enforcement, particularly within the streaming sector. Establishing robust digital copyright compliance frameworks and proactively monitoring overseas infringement risks are essential operational steps. Companies must closely track anti-piracy developments led by international industry alliances to avoid market access barriers or significant liability claims arising from copyright deficiencies. Ensuring comprehensive compliance supports sustainable business expansion and mitigates cross-border legal exposure in competitive media markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["前迪士尼律师分享打击全球盗版的新角色职责与战略视角", "类比“禁毒战争”，强调反盗版工作的长期性与复杂性", "依托好莱坞及流媒体平台联盟，强化行业协同治理机制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Former Disney lawyer outlines new responsibilities and strategic perspectives for leading global anti-piracy initiatives within the entertainment sector.", "Anti-piracy efforts are likened to the war on drugs, highlighting the long-term nature and inherent complexity of enforcing copyright laws globally.", "Strengthening collaborative governance mechanisms through alliances involving Hollywood studios and streaming platforms remains critical for effective enforcement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加强流媒体等数字内容的全球版权合规审查与监控", "关注国际行业联盟反盗版动向，规避跨境侵权法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen global copyright compliance reviews and monitoring for digital content, especially in streaming services.", "Practice note: Monitor international industry alliance anti-piracy trends to mitigate cross-border infringement legal risks.", "What to monitor: Shifts in global enforcement priorities and collaborative actions by major content holder coalitions."]
core_points_en: Former Disney lawyer outlines new responsibilities and strategic perspectives for leading global anti-piracy initiatives within the entertainment sector.
Anti-piracy efforts are likened to the war on drugs, highlighting the long-term nature and inherent complexity of enforcing copyright laws globally.
Strengthening collaborative governance mechanisms through alliances involving Hollywood studios and streaming platforms remains critical for effective enforcement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen global copyright compliance reviews and monitoring for digital content, especially in streaming services.
Practice note: Monitor international industry alliance anti-piracy trends to mitigate cross-border infringement legal risks.
What to monitor: Shifts in global enforcement priorities and collaborative actions by major content holder coalitions.

items.title: Duracell cannot cut power to BASF’s secrets complaint
items.title_zh: 杜拉塞尔驳回巴斯夫商业秘密诉讼申请被拒
items.title_en: Duracell Denied Dismissal in BASF Trade Secrets Lawsuit Alleging Third-Party Disclosure
items.summary: The world’s biggest battery maker’s bid to dismiss a lawsuit has fallen flat, and it must now face allegations that it took the chemical company’s secrets to a third party.
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球最大电池制造商杜拉塞尔试图驳回巴斯夫提起的诉讼失败。法院裁定杜拉塞尔必须面对指控，即其将巴斯夫的商业秘密泄露给第三方。该案件核心围绕商业秘密保护与侵权认定，不涉及专利或商标等传统工业产权，但属于广义知识产权及不正当竞争领域的重要司法动态，反映了欧洲市场对商业机密保护的严格立场。
items.ai_summary_en: The world's largest battery manufacturer, Duracell, has failed in its attempt to dismiss a lawsuit brought by chemical giant BASF. The court rejected the motion to dismiss, meaning Duracell must now proceed to defend itself against serious allegations. The core of the dispute involves claims that Duracell improperly disclosed BASF's confidential trade secrets to an unauthorized third party. This case centers exclusively on trade secret protection and infringement determination, rather than traditional industrial property rights such as patents or trademarks. It represents a significant judicial development within the broader scope of intellectual property and unfair competition law. The ruling underscores the European market's stringent stance on protecting non-public technical information and commercial confidentiality, highlighting the severe legal consequences for mishandling sensitive business data.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧经营应强化商业秘密合规体系，严格隔离竞业信息。需警惕供应链合作中的保密义务，避免无意泄露引发高额索赔。建议完善内部数据分级管理，签署严谨NDA，以应对欧洲严格的商业秘密执法环境，降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must strengthen their trade secret compliance frameworks to navigate the region's strict enforcement environment. Establishing rigorous information isolation mechanisms is critical to prevent accidental disclosure during competitive activities. Companies should carefully manage confidentiality obligations within supply chain collaborations to avoid unintentional leaks that could trigger substantial litigation risks. Implementing comprehensive internal data classification systems and executing precise non-disclosure agreements are essential practices. These measures help mitigate legal exposure and ensure alignment with high standards of commercial secrecy protection expected by European courts and regulators.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["杜拉塞尔驳回诉讼的申请被法院正式拒绝，需继续应诉", "指控核心为杜拉塞尔涉嫌将巴斯夫商业秘密透露给第三方", "案件聚焦商业秘密侵权，凸显欧洲对非公开技术信息的法律保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court formally denied Duracell's application to dismiss the lawsuit, requiring the company to continue defending the case.", "The central allegation accuses Duracell of disclosing BASF's protected trade secrets to an external third party without authorization.", "The case focuses on trade secret infringement, emphasizing robust legal protection for non-public technical information in Europe."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化在欧商业秘密合规，建立严格的信息隔离机制", "完善供应链保密协议，防范因泄密引发的巨额诉讼风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen trade secret compliance in Europe by establishing strict information isolation mechanisms to protect sensitive data.", "Practice note: Enhance supply chain confidentiality agreements and internal data management to prevent costly litigation from accidental disclosures.", "What to monitor: Strict enforcement trends regarding trade secret infringement and the legal standards for proving unauthorized third-party disclosure."]
core_points_en: The court formally denied Duracell's application to dismiss the lawsuit, requiring the company to continue defending the case.
The central allegation accuses Duracell of disclosing BASF's protected trade secrets to an external third party without authorization.
The case focuses on trade secret infringement, emphasizing robust legal protection for non-public technical information in Europe.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen trade secret compliance in Europe by establishing strict information isolation mechanisms to protect sensitive data.
Practice note: Enhance supply chain confidentiality agreements and internal data management to prevent costly litigation from accidental disclosures.
What to monitor: Strict enforcement trends regarding trade secret infringement and the legal standards for proving unauthorized third-party disclosure.

items.title: Everyday IP: How Intellectual Property Powers The World Of Sports
items.title_zh: 日常IP：知识产权如何赋能体育世界
items.title_en: Everyday IP: How Intellectual Property Powers the Global Sports Industry
items.summary: From the roar of the crowd to the precision of a last-second goal, sport captivates billions worldwide. But behind every athletic achievement lies a complex web of innovation, branding, and technology—all powered by Intellectual Property. Discover how patents, trademarks, and copyrights shape the games we love, from basketball's humble beginnings to today's data-driven training systems.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文从世界知识产权日视角出发，分析知识产权在体育产业中的核心作用。文章指出，从篮球起源到现代数据驱动训练，技术创新依赖专利保护；同时，球队标识与品牌形象通过商标和设计权确立身份。随着2026年冬奥会及世界杯临近，IP成为连接体育竞技、商业品牌与全球传播的关键纽带，强调创新与品牌保护对体育全球化的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the critical role of intellectual property within the sports sector, framed by World Intellectual Property Day. It highlights how technological innovations, ranging from early basketball equipment to modern data-driven training systems, rely on patent protection to enhance competitive performance. Simultaneously, team logos and visual identities are secured through trademarks and design rights to establish distinct brand presence. With major global events like the 2026 Winter Olympics and FIFA World Cup approaching, IP serves as the essential infrastructure connecting athletic competition, commercial branding, and worldwide broadcasting. The analysis underscores that robust innovation and brand protection are fundamental to the successful globalization of sports enterprises.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国体育科技企业出海需重视底层技术专利布局，避免侵权风险。同时，应强化品牌商标的国际注册策略，利用设计权保护产品外观。建议企业建立IP合规体系，将IP管理融入产品研发与市场推广全流程，以应对欧洲严格的知识产权保护环境，提升品牌溢价能力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize comprehensive patent strategies for underlying sports technologies to mitigate infringement risks in this strict regulatory environment. Strengthening international trademark registration and leveraging design rights are crucial for building a robust brand moat. Companies are advised to integrate IP compliance into their product development and marketing workflows. This holistic approach ensures alignment with European legal standards while enhancing brand premium capabilities and safeguarding commercial interests against unauthorized use or counterfeiting.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["体育装备与技术革新（如智能足球）依赖专利保护以提升竞技水平", "球队Logo、色彩等视觉元素通过商标与设计权构建品牌身份", "大型赛事商业化运作中，IP是保障转播权与品牌价值的基础设施"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Sports equipment and technical innovations, such as smart footballs, depend on patent protection to elevate competitive standards.", "Team logos, colors, and visual elements utilize trademarks and design rights to construct a unique and recognizable brand identity.", "In large-scale event commercialization, intellectual property forms the foundational infrastructure for securing broadcasting rights and preserving brand value."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加强体育科技硬件专利布局，规避欧洲市场侵权风险", "完善国际商标注册与设计权保护，构建品牌护城河"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure patent coverage for sports tech hardware to avoid infringement liabilities in the European market.", "Practice note: Implement rigorous international trademark registration and design right protections to build a strong brand defense.", "What to monitor: Evolving European enforcement trends regarding IP compliance in sports equipment and digital broadcasting sectors."]
core_points_en: Sports equipment and technical innovations, such as smart footballs, depend on patent protection to elevate competitive standards.
Team logos, colors, and visual elements utilize trademarks and design rights to construct a unique and recognizable brand identity.
In large-scale event commercialization, intellectual property forms the foundational infrastructure for securing broadcasting rights and preserving brand value.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure patent coverage for sports tech hardware to avoid infringement liabilities in the European market.
Practice note: Implement rigorous international trademark registration and design right protections to build a strong brand defense.
What to monitor: Evolving European enforcement trends regarding IP compliance in sports equipment and digital broadcasting sectors.

items.title: Saint Gobain and Splanemann defend smart glass patent at EPO
items.title_zh: 圣戈班与Splanemann在EPO捍卫智能玻璃专利
items.title_en: Saint-Gobain and Splanemann Defend Smart Glass Patent Validity at the EPO
items.summary: The market for innovations in speciality glass for the automotive industry is fiercely competitive. There have been several recent patent disputes in this industry, such as Fuyao Glass vs Saint Gobain and ACG concerning head-up displays and Sekisui Chemicals and Kuraray concerning special films for car windscreens. The current case revolves around Saint Gobain’s EP 3 774 417, […] The post Saint Gobain and Splanemann defend smart glass patent at EPO appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 汽车行业特种玻璃创新市场竞争激烈，近期发生多起专利纠纷。本案聚焦于圣戈班的欧洲专利EP 3 774 417，该公司联合Splanemann在欧洲专利局（EPO）进行辩护。此前行业已出现福耀玻璃与圣戈班关于抬头显示、以及Sekisui与Kuraray关于挡风玻璃膜的争议。此次EPO程序旨在维护该智能玻璃技术的专利权有效性，反映头部企业在关键汽车部件领域的知识产权博弈持续升级。
items.ai_summary_en: The automotive specialty glass sector is experiencing intense competition, marked by recent high-profile patent disputes involving major industry players. Notable conflicts include Fuyao Glass versus Saint-Gobain and ACG regarding head-up display technologies, as well as Sekisui Chemicals against Kuraray concerning special films for windshields. The current proceedings focus on European patent EP 3 774 417, held by Saint-Gobain. In this ongoing case, Saint-Gobain has joined forces with Splanemann to defend the patent’s validity before the European Patent Office (EPO). This legal action aims to uphold the intellectual property rights protecting smart glass technology. The dispute underscores the escalating strategic importance of IP protection in critical automotive components, reflecting a broader trend where leading companies actively litigate to secure their technological advantages and market positions in this rapidly evolving field.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及供应链应密切监控圣戈班等巨头在欧洲的专利布局，特别是智能玻璃与HUD相关技术。建议提前进行FTO分析，规避侵权风险，并考虑通过交叉许可或无效宣告程序应对潜在诉讼，确保出口业务安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the automotive supply chain should closely monitor the patent strategies of dominant holders like Saint-Gobain in Europe, particularly regarding smart glass and head-up display innovations. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses prior to product launch is essential to mitigate infringement risks. Companies should also evaluate potential responses to enforcement actions, such as pursuing cross-licensing agreements or initiating invalidation proceedings. Proactive IP management ensures business continuity and protects export operations from disruptive litigation threats in key European markets.
