items.title: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reduce Trademark Costs For SMEs
items.title_zh: EUIPO 2026 中小企业基金：降低商标申请成本
items.title_en: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reimbursement for Trademark Registration Costs
items.summary: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) SME Fund 2026 enables eligible small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to reclaim part of the cost of trademark registration...
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 将于 2026 年 2 月启动中小企业基金，符合条件的企业可报销部分国家及欧盟商标注册费。该举措旨在应对日益激烈的品牌竞争，鼓励中小企业尽早布局知识产权。文章对比了马耳他国内商标与欧盟商标的覆盖范围，强调早期申请对规避冲突及获取全域保护的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has launched the SME Fund 2026, enabling eligible small and medium-sized enterprises to reclaim a portion of trademark registration fees. Starting 2 February 2026, the initiative covers costs for national applications, including those in Malta, as well as European Union Trademarks (EUTM). With record application volumes in 2025, early filing is critical to secure exclusive rights and mitigate opposition risks. The fund supports businesses with fewer than 250 employees and turnover under €50 million, facilitating cost-effective brand protection across the EU single market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国赴欧中小企业应密切关注该基金申请窗口，利用财政补贴降低欧盟商标布局成本。建议结合市场规划，优先选择欧盟商标以实现广域保护，避免因费用顾虑延迟注册而遭遇抢注或侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: This reimbursement scheme offers a tangible financial incentive for rights holders to formalize their IP portfolios ahead of peak filing periods. For companies operating in Malta or targeting the broader EU market, leveraging the EUTM system via the fund maximizes territorial coverage while minimizing upfront expenditure. Strategic timing remains essential; securing registration before market entry strengthens enforcement positions and protects brand goodwill against increasing competitive pressures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["eligible 中小企业可获商标申请费用部分报销，涵盖欧盟及成员国层面", "欧盟商标单次申请即覆盖 27 国，相比单一国家注册更具扩张优势", "提前布局商标能有效降低异议风险，确立品牌独占权并防止侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Eligible SMEs can apply for partial reimbursement of trademark fees starting 2 February 2026.", "An EU trademark provides coverage across all 27 Member States, unlike national filings.", "Early registration reduces conflict risks and secures exclusive rights against competitors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EUIPO 基金补贴，显著降低中国品牌进入欧盟市场的知识产权门槛", "权衡单一国与欧盟商标差异，制定符合扩张战略的高性价比注册方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Eligible SMEs can reduce upfront IP costs by utilizing the EUIPO reimbursement scheme for national and EU-wide filings.", "Practice note: Prioritize EUTM applications to secure simultaneous protection across all 27 Member States efficiently.", "What to monitor: Application windows and eligibility criteria to ensure timely submission before potential fee increases or quota limits."]
core_points_en: Eligible SMEs can apply for partial reimbursement of trademark fees starting 2 February 2026.
An EU trademark provides coverage across all 27 Member States, unlike national filings.
Early registration reduces conflict risks and secures exclusive rights against competitors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Eligible SMEs can reduce upfront IP costs by utilizing the EUIPO reimbursement scheme for national and EU-wide filings.
Practice note: Prioritize EUTM applications to secure simultaneous protection across all 27 Member States efficiently.
What to monitor: Application windows and eligibility criteria to ensure timely submission before potential fee increases or quota limits.

items.title: Copyright: Free Creative Choices That Reflect Personality
items.title_zh: CJEU判决：实用物品版权需体现作者个性的自由创造选择
items.title_en: CJEU MIO & Konektra: Copyright for Utilitarian Objects Requires Personality-Reflecting Creative Choices
items.summary: Finally, the CJEU published its long-awaited judgment in the joint cases MIO & Konektra. A utilitarian object is protected by copyright if it expresses free and creative choices and reflects the author's personality.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在MIO & Konektra案中裁定，实用物品获版权保护须体现作者的自由创造性选择并反映其个性。仅由技术或功能驱动的选择、平庸或非功能性选择均不具创造性，但普通元素组合可能构成作品。侵权判定不再看整体印象，而是看是否可识别地再现了符合门槛的创造性元素。文章以荷兰Secrid案为例，指出单纯设计选择若无个性体现则无版权，建议企业及时注册欧盟外观设计以强化保护。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has issued its long-awaited judgment in the joined cases MIO and Konektra, clarifying copyright protection standards for utilitarian objects. The Court ruled that such objects qualify for copyright only if they embody free and creative choices reflecting the author's personality. Decisions driven solely by technical or functional constraints, as well as banal or ordinary non-functional choices, do not meet this threshold. However, a combination of individually commonplace elements may still constitute an original work. The CJEU further established that the intent to create art or museum exhibition status is irrelevant. Crucially, the infringement test has shifted from assessing overall impression to determining whether recognizable creative elements meeting the work threshold have been reproduced. This analysis highlights the Secrid cardholder case, where Dutch courts denied copyright due to a lack of personality-reflected creativity, underscoring the strategic importance of timely EU design registration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业对欧销售家居、电子产品时，需注意版权保护门槛提高。单纯的功能性设计难以获得版权，建议提前布局欧盟外观设计注册。在应对侵权诉讼时，应重点抗辩对方设计缺乏反映个性的创造性选择，避免依赖模糊的整体印象比对，以降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants selling home goods or electronics in Europe face heightened copyright thresholds following the CJEU's MIO and Konektra rulings. Purely functional designs are unlikely to secure copyright protection, creating potential gaps in legal coverage. Rights holders should prioritize early EU design registration to ensure stable protection independent of subjective creativity assessments. In litigation, defendants can effectively challenge claims by demonstrating that alleged infringing features lack personality-reflected creative choices. Reliance on vague overall impression comparisons is no longer sufficient for establishing infringement. Practitioners must adapt strategies to focus on specific creative elements and consider design rights as a primary enforcement tool alongside copyright claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["实用物品版权保护需满足自由创造性选择及反映作者个性的双重标准", "纯功能性或平庸选择非创造性，但普通元素组合仍可能具备独创性", "侵权测试转向可识别再现创造性元素，整体印象匹配不再是唯一标准", "鉴于版权门槛提高，企业应优先通过欧盟外观设计注册获取稳定保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Utilitarian objects require free, creative choices reflecting the author's personality to qualify for copyright protection under EU law.", "Purely functional or banal choices lack creativity, yet combinations of commonplace elements may still form an original work.", "Infringement assessment now focuses on recognizable reproduction of creative elements rather than matching the overall impression."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["对欧出口产品应提前注册欧盟外观，规避版权高门槛带来的保护真空", "侵权抗辩需聚焦对方设计缺乏反映个性的创造性，摒弃整体印象比对"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure EU design registration before market entry to mitigate risks from elevated copyright thresholds for utilitarian objects.", "Practice note: In infringement defenses, argue that challenged designs lack personality-reflected creative choices rather than relying on overall impression comparisons.", "What to monitor: National court applications of the new 'recognizable reproduction' standard for creative elements in design disputes."]
core_points_en: Utilitarian objects require free, creative choices reflecting the author's personality to qualify for copyright protection under EU law.
Purely functional or banal choices lack creativity, yet combinations of commonplace elements may still form an original work.
Infringement assessment now focuses on recognizable reproduction of creative elements rather than matching the overall impression.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure EU design registration before market entry to mitigate risks from elevated copyright thresholds for utilitarian objects.
Practice note: In infringement defenses, argue that challenged designs lack personality-reflected creative choices rather than relying on overall impression comparisons.
What to monitor: National court applications of the new 'recognizable reproduction' standard for creative elements in design disputes.

items.title: Indications géographiques : quand les noms des boissons alcoolisées deviennent un enjeu commercial mondial
items.title_zh: 地理标志：酒精饮料名称成全球商业博弈焦点
items.title_en: Geographical Indications: Alcoholic Beverage Names as Global Commercial Battlegrounds
items.summary: Scotch, Tequila, Cognac ou Prosecco : derrière ces noms se cache un puissant levier juridique, l’indication géographique.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨地理标志（GI）在全球酒类市场中的法律与商业价值，重点分析欧盟与澳大利亚历经多年谈判达成的自由贸易协定。该协定解决了关于
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the legal and commercial significance of geographical indications (GIs) within the global wine and spirits market. It focuses on the resolution of long-standing trade tensions through the EU-Australia Free Trade Agreement, finalized in March 2026. A central point of contention was the status of 'Prosecco,' a name with dual identity for European producers and Australian manufacturers. The analysis highlights how GIs serve as critical intellectual property rights that certify origin and quality, offering competitive advantages to regional producers while ensuring authenticity for consumers. However, this protection often clashes with naming conventions in New World countries, where terms are frequently viewed as generic. The agreement illustrates the complex balance between protecting traditional European appellations and accommodating established international market practices.
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and market participants, the finalization of the EU-Australia agreement signals a shift towards resolving GI conflicts through comprehensive trade deals rather than prolonged litigation. Companies must assess how such bilateral agreements impact their ability to use descriptive terms in key export markets. The precedent set regarding Prosecco suggests that even well-established names may face restrictions if they conflict with protected GIs. Stakeholders should review their branding strategies against evolving international IP standards to mitigate risks of infringement or forced rebranding in jurisdictions prioritizing geographical authenticity over generic usage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["本文探讨地理标志（GI）在全球酒类市场中的法律与商业价值，重点分析欧盟与澳大利亚历经多…", "该协定解决了关于"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Geographical indications provide exclusive rights certifying product origin, offering significant competitive advantages and premium pricing potential for regional producers.", "Trade negotiations frequently encounter friction between the EU's strict GI protections and New World countries' preference for flexible, generic naming conventions.", "The EU-Australia Free Trade Agreement resolves the Prosecco dispute, demonstrating a negotiated approach to balancing GI exclusivity with existing market realities."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess exposure to GI restrictions in export markets, particularly for brands using terms potentially protected under new trade agreements.", "Practice note: Monitor bilateral trade negotiations for clauses that may redefine the status of semi-generic or geographic terms used in branding.", "What to monitor: Enforcement actions and judicial interpretations following the EU-Australia agreement, especially regarding transitional periods for disputed names."]
core_points_en: Geographical indications provide exclusive rights certifying product origin, offering significant competitive advantages and premium pricing potential for regional producers.
Trade negotiations frequently encounter friction between the EU's strict GI protections and New World countries' preference for flexible, generic naming conventions.
The EU-Australia Free Trade Agreement resolves the Prosecco dispute, demonstrating a negotiated approach to balancing GI exclusivity with existing market realities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess exposure to GI restrictions in export markets, particularly for brands using terms potentially protected under new trade agreements.
Practice note: Monitor bilateral trade negotiations for clauses that may redefine the status of semi-generic or geographic terms used in branding.
What to monitor: Enforcement actions and judicial interpretations following the EU-Australia agreement, especially regarding transitional periods for disputed names.

items.title: Should You Transfer IPR At The Same Time Or Gradually?
items.title_zh: 知识产权转移应一次性完成还是逐步进行？
items.title_en: Should You Transfer IPR At The Same Time Or Gradually?
items.summary: When undergoing a business change that requires the transfer of intellectual property rights, a key question to consider is whether such changes should be implemented all at once or gradually. Although there are many perceived advantages to each approach, the best approach is often to complete the transfers simultaneously.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析企业在业务变更时转移知识产权的策略，对比了逐步转移与一次性整体转移的优劣。尽管逐步转移看似节省预算且任务轻松，但实际会导致费用累积、权利归属记录错误，进而阻碍执法、许可及海关备案。文章结论建议，为避免法律风险和管理冗余，最佳实践是作为专项项目一次性完成所有相关权利的转移记录。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧并购或重组时，应避免因节省短期预算而分步转移 IP。权属登记滞后将严重影响海关扣货维权及侵权诉讼资格。建议设立专项预算，借助专业机构一次性清理并更新欧盟及各成员国权利记录，确保资产安全。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["逐步转移虽看似灵活，但长期成本高于一次性整体转移项目", "权利归属记录错误将直接导致执法困难及许可协议失效风险", "推荐将知识产权转移作为专项，同步完成以消除合规隐患"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["分步转移易致权属记录混乱，增加后续维权与海关备案难度", "建议中国企业设立专项预算，一次性完成在欧 IP 资产过户登记"]

items.title: PGA Intellectual Property Selects Questel’s Orbit Intelligence To Enhance Patent Intelligence Capabilities
items.title_zh: PGA 知识产权选用 Questel 平台增强专利情报能力
items.title_en: PGA Intellectual Property Adopts Questel Orbit Intelligence to Strengthen Patent Analytics
items.summary: Questel, a leading provider of intellectual property (IP) and innovation management solutions, today announced that PGA Intellectual Property has selected its Orbit Intelligence platform to support and enhance its patent intelligence activities.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国咨询公司 PGA Intellectual Property 选定 Questel 的 Orbit Intelligence 平台，以提升其专利情报活动能力。该举措旨在利用先进的全球专利搜索、分析及技术监控功能，帮助客户在研发与商业决策中转化复杂专利数据为可操作洞察，应对日益依赖专利数据的创新环境。
items.ai_summary_en: French consulting firm PGA Intellectual Property has selected Questel’s Orbit Intelligence platform to enhance its patent intelligence capabilities. This strategic move aims to leverage advanced global patent search, analytics, and technology monitoring functions. The adoption supports the transformation of complex patent data into actionable insights for clients navigating an innovation environment increasingly dependent on patent information. By integrating these tools, the firm seeks to better guide research and development efforts and business decisions. The collaboration highlights the growing importance of sophisticated digital solutions in managing intellectual property strategies effectively within the European market and beyond.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局需重视专业情报工具应用，建议借鉴此类合作模式，利用数字化平台提升专利预警与导航能力，以支持海外研发决策及风险防控，避免盲目投入。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating internationally, particularly those expanding into Europe, leveraging specialized intelligence platforms is critical for effective patent portfolio management. Adopting similar digital tools can significantly enhance early warning systems and navigation capabilities, thereby supporting overseas R&D decisions and risk mitigation. Reliance on data-driven analytics allows rights holders to accurately assess competitive dynamics and avoid inefficient resource allocation. This approach ensures that strategic investments are informed by comprehensive market intelligence rather than intuition, reducing exposure to potential infringement risks and optimizing global IP positioning.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PGA 公司引入 Orbit 平台强化专利战略分析能力", "借助大数据工具监测技术趋势与竞争格局", "提升将复杂专利数据转化为商业洞察的效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PGA Intellectual Property integrates Orbit Intelligence to strengthen its capacity for comprehensive patent strategy analysis and consulting services.", "The platform enables robust monitoring of technology trends and competitive landscapes through advanced big data analytical tools and visualization features.", "Adoption improves efficiency in converting complex patent datasets into clear commercial insights, supporting strategic decision-making for clients."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用专业情报工具优化海外专利布局策略", "依托数据分析提升技术竞争态势感知能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize professional intelligence tools to optimize overseas patent layout strategies and ensure alignment with local market dynamics.", "Practice note: Leverage data analytics to enhance situational awareness regarding technology competition and emerging innovation trends.", "What to monitor: Track the integration of AI-driven analytics in IP workflows to maintain competitive advantage in patent intelligence gathering."]
core_points_en: PGA Intellectual Property integrates Orbit Intelligence to strengthen its capacity for comprehensive patent strategy analysis and consulting services.
The platform enables robust monitoring of technology trends and competitive landscapes through advanced big data analytical tools and visualization features.
Adoption improves efficiency in converting complex patent datasets into clear commercial insights, supporting strategic decision-making for clients.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize professional intelligence tools to optimize overseas patent layout strategies and ensure alignment with local market dynamics.
Practice note: Leverage data analytics to enhance situational awareness regarding technology competition and emerging innovation trends.
What to monitor: Track the integration of AI-driven analytics in IP workflows to maintain competitive advantage in patent intelligence gathering.

items.title: [Guest post] Oh my influencer! Italian rules on influencer marketing
items.title_zh: 嘉宾投稿：哦我的网红！意大利网红营销规则解析
items.title_en: Italian Influencer Marketing Rules: Regulatory Developments and Compliance Obligations
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Maria Cristina Michelini (Baglioni Hotels & Resorts) detailing developments in the Italian regulatory system for influencers. Here’s what she writes:Oh my influencer! Italian rules on influencer marketingby Maria Cristina MicheliniKat-influencers are also a thingOnce upon a time, there were (only)
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文由 Baglioni 酒店集团专家撰稿，详细阐述意大利针对网红营销的监管体系发展。文章回顾了从仅有传统广告到如今网红经济爆发的背景，重点分析了意大利现行法律框架下对网红披露义务、商业合作标识及违规处罚的具体规定，旨在厘清数字营销中的合规边界。
items.ai_summary_en: This guest contribution details the evolution of Italy's regulatory framework governing influencer marketing activities. It traces the shift from traditional advertising models to the current digital landscape, highlighting the establishment of specific rules for online content creators. The analysis focuses on mandatory disclosure obligations, requiring clear identification of commercial partnerships within promotional posts. It further examines the legal consequences for non-compliance, including administrative penalties and reputational risks. The piece aims to clarify compliance boundaries for market participants operating in Italy, ensuring that digital marketing practices align with local consumer protection standards and advertising laws.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营的中国品牌及出海企业需警惕意大利严格的网红合规要求。建议在开展当地营销时，严格审查合作网红的披露标识，避免因第三方违规导致品牌连带受罚，确保符合欧盟及意大利本地广告法标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Italy should integrate rigorous influencer compliance checks into their local marketing strategies. Brands must ensure that third-party creators adhere to strict disclosure requirements to avoid joint liability for regulatory violations. Aligning campaigns with both EU directives and Italian national advertising laws is essential for risk mitigation. Proactive monitoring of partner content helps prevent reputational damage and financial penalties associated with undeclared commercial communications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["意大利建立专门监管体系以规范网红营销商业行为", "明确网红在推广内容中必须进行清晰的商业披露", "违规未标识广告行为将面临具体的行政处罚与法律风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Italy has established a dedicated regulatory system to govern commercial behaviors within the influencer marketing sector.", "Influencers must provide clear and conspicuous disclosures regarding commercial relationships in all promotional content.", "Failure to properly label advertisements exposes participants to specific administrative penalties and legal liabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国出海品牌需将网红合规审查纳入意大利市场准入流程", "防范因合作方未披露商业关系引发的连带法律与声誉风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands must incorporate influencer compliance verification into their Italian market entry procedures.", "Practice note: Implement strict review protocols for partner disclosures to mitigate joint legal and reputational risks.", "What to monitor: Changes in Italian enforcement actions regarding undeclared commercial relationships in digital media."]
core_points_en: Italy has established a dedicated regulatory system to govern commercial behaviors within the influencer marketing sector.
Influencers must provide clear and conspicuous disclosures regarding commercial relationships in all promotional content.
Failure to properly label advertisements exposes participants to specific administrative penalties and legal liabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must incorporate influencer compliance verification into their Italian market entry procedures.
Practice note: Implement strict review protocols for partner disclosures to mitigate joint legal and reputational risks.
What to monitor: Changes in Italian enforcement actions regarding undeclared commercial relationships in digital media.

items.title: OpenAI hit with a trade secret suit seeking billions over “foundational” technology
items.title_zh: OpenAI 因基础技术遭数十亿商业秘密诉讼
items.title_en: OpenAI Faces Billion-Dollar Trade Secret Suit Over Foundational AI Technology
items.summary: The unusual case alleges unauthorised use of innovations that underpin LLM functioning – and adds to a growing body of plantiffs using IP rights to seek a slice of the lucrative AI pie
items.ai_summary_zh: OpenAI 面临一起罕见的商业秘密诉讼，原告指控其未经授权使用了支撑大语言模型运行的基础创新技术。该案反映了当前利用知识产权权利争夺人工智能巨额利润的趋势日益明显，凸显了 AI 领域核心技术保护与侵权认定的复杂性。
items.ai_summary_en: OpenAI is the defendant in a rare trade secret lawsuit alleging unauthorized use of innovations that underpin the functioning of large language models. The complaint seeks billions in damages, marking a significant escalation in intellectual property disputes within the artificial intelligence sector. This case reflects a growing trend where plaintiffs leverage IP rights to claim a share of the lucrative AI market profits. It highlights the increasing complexity surrounding the protection of core technologies and the determination of infringement in this rapidly evolving field. The litigation underscores the high stakes involved in foundational AI development and the potential for substantial financial liability when proprietary methods are allegedly misappropriated during model training or deployment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 企业出海需警惕基础算法与训练数据的商业秘密合规风险。建议加强技术来源审查，建立严格的保密隔离机制，避免卷入类似高额索赔诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing or deploying generative AI systems face heightened exposure to trade secret claims regarding foundational algorithms and training data. Rights holders should conduct rigorous due diligence on technology sources to verify provenance and avoid inadvertent infringement. Establishing strict confidentiality protocols and data isolation mechanisms is critical to mitigate risks associated with high-value litigation. Companies must ensure clear documentation of independent development paths. Monitoring emerging legal precedents in AI-related trade secret enforcement will be essential for navigating compliance obligations and managing potential liability in this competitive landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["OpenAI 被诉非法使用支撑大模型的基础技术创新", "案件性质为寻求巨额赔偿的商业秘密侵权纠纷", "反映利用 IP 权利分割人工智能市场利益的趋势"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["OpenAI is accused of illegally using foundational technical innovations that support the operation of large language models.", "The case involves a trade secret infringement dispute seeking billions in compensation for alleged unauthorized technology use.", "This litigation reflects a broader trend of using intellectual property rights to divide benefits within the artificial intelligence market."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化技术来源尽职调查以规避商业秘密侵权风险", "建立数据与算法隔离机制应对潜在高额索赔诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: AI developers and deployers face significant trade secret risks regarding foundational algorithms and training data sourcing.", "Practice note: Strengthen technology source due diligence and establish strict data isolation mechanisms to mitigate infringement liabilities.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards for proving trade secret misappropriation in complex AI model development contexts."]
core_points_en: OpenAI is accused of illegally using foundational technical innovations that support the operation of large language models.
The case involves a trade secret infringement dispute seeking billions in compensation for alleged unauthorized technology use.
This litigation reflects a broader trend of using intellectual property rights to divide benefits within the artificial intelligence market.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AI developers and deployers face significant trade secret risks regarding foundational algorithms and training data sourcing.
Practice note: Strengthen technology source due diligence and establish strict data isolation mechanisms to mitigate infringement liabilities.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards for proving trade secret misappropriation in complex AI model development contexts.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Costs: reversed / agreed / guaranteed
items.title_zh: UPC周报：诉讼费用的撤销、同意与担保
items.title_en: UPC Weekly: Judicial Reversal of Costs, Party Agreements, and Security for Costs Orders
items.summary: 2026 Week 15
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析统一专利法院（UPC）近期关于诉讼费用的三项关键裁决。内容涵盖法院对费用分摊的重新评估、当事人就费用达成的一致意见以及要求提供费用担保的情形。这些案例明确了UPC在管理诉讼成本方面的司法倾向，强调了程序合规性及财务准备在跨境专利诉讼中的重要性，为当事人预估诉讼风险提供了具体参考依据。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines three pivotal recent rulings by the Unified Patent Court concerning litigation costs. The discussion covers the Court’s reassessment and adjustment of cost allocations, the judicial recognition of party agreements regarding fee sharing, and instances where security for costs was mandated. These cases clarify the UPC’s judicial tendencies in managing litigation expenses, emphasizing the critical importance of procedural compliance and financial preparedness in cross-border patent disputes. The insights provide concrete reference points for parties to accurately estimate litigation risks and navigate the financial complexities inherent in UPC proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需高度重视费用担保机制，提前规划资金流动性。建议建立精细化的诉讼预算模型，密切关注法官对费用分摊的自由裁量权，通过积极协商降低潜在成本。同时，应强化程序合规意识，避免因程序瑕疵导致不必要的费用负担。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must prioritize the security for costs mechanism, ensuring adequate liquidity to address potential sudden demands. Establishing a detailed budget model is essential for effective financial planning. Furthermore, monitoring judicial discretion regarding cost apportionment allows for strategic negotiation to mitigate overall expenses. Strengthening awareness of procedural compliance is equally vital to avoid unnecessary financial burdens resulting from procedural defects. Proactive management of these elements helps rights holders control risk exposure and optimize resource allocation during complex cross-border patent enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC法院对特定案件诉讼费用进行重新评估并作出撤销或调整决定", "确认当事人可就诉讼费用分担达成协议，法院予以尊重并记录在案", "明确在特定情形下法院有权要求原告提供诉讼费用担保以防范风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC reassessed specific case costs, issuing decisions to reverse or adjust previous cost allocation rulings.", "The Court confirmed that parties may agree on cost sharing, respecting these settlements and recording them officially.", "The Court clarified its authority to require plaintiffs to provide security for costs in specific risk-mitigation scenarios."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC费用担保机制，提前规划诉讼资金流动性以应对突发要求", "建立精细化预算模型，利用协商机制降低跨境专利诉讼的整体成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize security for costs mechanisms and plan liquidity early to handle unexpected financial demands.", "Practice note: Develop precise budget models and leverage negotiation to reduce total cross-border patent litigation costs.", "What to monitor: Track judicial discretion on cost apportionment and ensure strict procedural compliance to avoid penalties."]
core_points_en: The UPC reassessed specific case costs, issuing decisions to reverse or adjust previous cost allocation rulings.
The Court confirmed that parties may agree on cost sharing, respecting these settlements and recording them officially.
The Court clarified its authority to require plaintiffs to provide security for costs in specific risk-mitigation scenarios.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize security for costs mechanisms and plan liquidity early to handle unexpected financial demands.
Practice note: Develop precise budget models and leverage negotiation to reduce total cross-border patent litigation costs.
What to monitor: Track judicial discretion on cost apportionment and ensure strict procedural compliance to avoid penalties.

items.title: Brandsmiths acquires Sipara, moves into UK’s top 10 TM filing firms
items.title_zh: Brandsmiths 收购 Sipara 跻身英国商标申请前十强
items.title_en: Brandsmiths Acquires Sipara, Entering UK's Top Ten Trademark Filing Firms
items.summary: While Sipara will continue operating under its existing name and leadership for now, both firms plan to present a united front at the INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知名商标代理机构 Brandsmiths 宣布完成对同行 Sipara 的收购。此次并购使 Brandsmiths 的业务规模显著扩大，成功进入英国商标申请量排名前十的律所行列。尽管被收购方 Sipara 短期内将保留原有名称及管理团队独立运营，但双方计划在今年的伦敦 INTA 年会上以统一形象对外展示，标志着英国商标服务市场的进一步整合。
items.ai_summary_en: UK-based trademark agency Brandsmiths has announced the completion of its acquisition of peer firm Sipara. This strategic merger significantly expands Brandsmiths' operational scale, successfully propelling it into the top ten law firms in the United Kingdom for trademark filing volume. Although Sipara will continue to operate independently under its existing name and leadership team in the short term to ensure business continuity, both entities plan to present a unified front at the upcoming International Trademark Association Annual Meeting in London. This development marks a notable step toward further consolidation within the UK trademark service market, highlighting the growing trend of integration among leading intellectual property service providers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 英国商标服务市场集中度提升，头部效应加剧。在欧中企应关注大型律所整合后的服务流程变化，评估其全球协同能力。建议利用市场竞争格局，重新审视现有代理合作模式，确保品牌获得更高效的战略支持。
items.ai_insight_en: The concentration of the UK trademark service market is increasing, with head effects becoming more pronounced. Rights holders should monitor changes in service processes following large-scale firm integrations and assess the global coordination capabilities of these consolidated entities. It is advisable to leverage the evolving competitive landscape to re-examine existing agency cooperation models. Ensuring that brands receive efficient strategic support requires careful evaluation of how agent changes impact maintenance strategies and overall service efficiency in this consolidating market environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Brandsmiths 通过收购 Sipara 成功跻身英国商标申请量前十强机构", "Sipara 短期内维持原名与领导层不变，保持业务运营的连续性", "双方定于伦敦 INTA 年会首次以统一品牌形象亮相国际舞台"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Brandsmiths successfully enters the top ten UK trademark filing institutions through the strategic acquisition of Sipara.", "Sipara maintains its original name and leadership structure in the short term to ensure continuous business operations.", "Both firms are scheduled to debut their unified brand image on the international stage at the INTA Annual Meeting in London."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国商标代理市场整合加速，头部机构服务能力进一步增强", "在欧中企需评估代理商变更对品牌维护策略及服务效率的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor service process changes after major firm integrations to assess global coordination capabilities.", "Practice note: Re-examine existing agency cooperation models to ensure brands receive efficient strategic support.", "What to monitor: Evaluate how agent changes impact brand maintenance strategies and service efficiency."]
core_points_en: Brandsmiths successfully enters the top ten UK trademark filing institutions through the strategic acquisition of Sipara.
Sipara maintains its original name and leadership structure in the short term to ensure continuous business operations.
Both firms are scheduled to debut their unified brand image on the international stage at the INTA Annual Meeting in London.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor service process changes after major firm integrations to assess global coordination capabilities.
Practice note: Re-examine existing agency cooperation models to ensure brands receive efficient strategic support.
What to monitor: Evaluate how agent changes impact brand maintenance strategies and service efficiency.

items.title: Biogen, Vertex among latest licensees in fast-growing drug delivery patent licensing space

items.title_en: Biogen, Vertex among latest licensees in fast-growing drug delivery patent licensing space
items.summary: Halozyme and Alteogen continue deal-making spree despite shadow cast by ongoing Keytruda battles
items.ai_summary_zh: IAM Media报道指出，尽管Keytruda专利诉讼带来阴影，Halozyme与Alteogen仍持续进行药物递送技术许可交易。Biogen和Vertex成为最新被许可方，显示该领域专利授权市场活跃。文章分析了主要参与者的商业策略及当前专利纠纷对行业许可活动的影响，强调了核心专利资产在医药合作中的关键价值。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应密切关注欧美药物递送平台专利布局，避免侵权风险。在出海合作中，需提前进行FTO分析，评估核心专利壁垒。建议通过交叉许可或早期谈判获取合法使用权，以规避高额诉讼成本，保障产品在欧洲市场的顺利准入与商业化进程。

items.title: Samsung, Nokia sign licence deals with Longhorn IP entities
items.title_zh: 三星诺基亚分别与Longhorn旗下实体签署专利许可协议
items.title_en: Samsung, Nokia sign licence deals with Longhorn IP entities
items.summary: Samsung licensed Hannibal IP’s 5G patents, while Nokia licensed Nyckel UI Technologies’ IoT patents
items.ai_summary_zh: IAM Media报道，科技巨头三星与诺基亚分别与其专利许可对手达成和解。三星获得了Hannibal IP持有的5G标准必要专利许可，而诺基亚则就Nyckel UI Technologies拥有的物联网（IoT）专利达成了授权协议。这两项交易均涉及Longhorn IP旗下的实体，标志着双方在相关技术领域的知识产权争议得到解决，反映了全球通信与物联网领域专利许可市场的活跃态势及头部企业对合规运营的重视。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应密切关注此类NPE许可动向，尤其是5G与IoT领域。建议提前进行FTO分析，建立专利预警机制，并参考行业头部企业的许可费率与条款，制定灵活的谈判策略，以规避高额诉讼成本与市场禁入风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三星获得Hannibal IP持有的5G专利许可，解决潜在侵权风险", "诺基亚就Nyckel UI Technologies的IoT专利达成授权协议", "两笔交易均涉及Longhorn IP旗下实体，体现NPE运营策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注5G与IoT领域NPE许可趋势，提前布局FTO分析", "参考头部企业条款，制定灵活谈判策略以规避诉讼风险"]

items.title: European patent office fee increases: What applicants must know
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局费用上调：申请人须知
items.title_en: European Patent Fee Shifts: EPO Modest Rise Contrasts with Sharp UK Hike
items.summary: Europe’s latest patent fee increases are modest at the EPO but include a rare steep hike in the UK, forcing applicants to reassess filing routes, portfolio value, and long-term cost strategies, say James Bell and Yelena Morozova.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲最新专利费用调整中，欧洲专利局（EPO）涨幅温和，但英国出现罕见大幅上涨。这一差异迫使申请人重新评估申请路径、组合价值及长期成本策略。文章指出，面对英国费用的显著增加，企业需审慎规划在欧洲的专利布局，以优化资源配置并应对成本压力。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent adjustments to European patent fees present a divergent landscape for rights holders. The European Patent Office has implemented modest increases, keeping overall costs relatively predictable and manageable for applicants. In stark contrast, the United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office has introduced a rare and significant fee hike. This substantial increase in UK charges significantly elevates the financial burden for maintaining or validating patents within that jurisdiction. Consequently, market participants are compelled to reassess their strategic filing routes, evaluate the commercial value of existing portfolios, and refine long-term cost management strategies. The disparity between the two offices necessitates a careful review of resource allocation to ensure that patent protection remains economically viable across different European territories.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局需警惕英国费用激增风险，建议重新核算进入英国的专利性价比。对于非核心市场，可考虑调整申请策略或依托欧洲专利局程序优化成本，避免盲目维持高成本国家的低价值专利。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should critically reassess the cost-benefit ratio of maintaining patent protection in the UK given the sharp fee increases. For markets where commercial returns do not justify the elevated costs, adjusting validation strategies or relying on centralized European Patent Office procedures may offer more efficient resource utilization. Companies should avoid retaining low-value patents in high-cost jurisdictions solely out of habit. Instead, a data-driven approach to portfolio pruning and strategic routing is essential to mitigate financial pressure while preserving key intellectual property assets across Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利局费用小幅上调，整体成本可控", "英国专利费用罕见大幅上涨，显著增加当地申请负担", "申请人需重估申请路径与长期组合成本策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The European Patent Office implements modest fee increases, ensuring that overall procedural costs remain largely controlled and predictable for applicants.", "The UK Intellectual Property Office enacts a rare, steep fee hike, substantially increasing the financial burden for local patent applications and validations.", "Applicants must urgently re-evaluate filing routes and long-term portfolio strategies to address the growing cost disparity between European jurisdictions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重估英国专利布局性价比，规避非必要成本支出", "利用欧专局程序优势，优化跨国专利申请路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Re-evaluate the cost-effectiveness of UK patent holdings to avoid unnecessary expenditure in non-core markets.", "Practice note: Leverage European Patent Office procedures to optimize cross-border application paths and reduce reliance on costly national validations.", "What to monitor: Track further regulatory changes in national IP offices that may impact the total cost of ownership for European patent portfolios."]
core_points_en: The European Patent Office implements modest fee increases, ensuring that overall procedural costs remain largely controlled and predictable for applicants.
The UK Intellectual Property Office enacts a rare, steep fee hike, substantially increasing the financial burden for local patent applications and validations.
Applicants must urgently re-evaluate filing routes and long-term portfolio strategies to address the growing cost disparity between European jurisdictions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Re-evaluate the cost-effectiveness of UK patent holdings to avoid unnecessary expenditure in non-core markets.
Practice note: Leverage European Patent Office procedures to optimize cross-border application paths and reduce reliance on costly national validations.
What to monitor: Track further regulatory changes in national IP offices that may impact the total cost of ownership for European patent portfolios.

items.title: Patents In Construction. A Technology Dispute Starts Much Earlier Than With The Filing Of A Lawsuit
items.title_zh: 建筑领域专利：技术争端远早于诉讼提起
items.title_en: Construction Patents: Technology Disputes Begin Long Before Litigation
items.summary: In the construction industry, success is nowadays less often determined only by the lowest price or deadline. Technology has become a new field of competitive struggle, as it dictates the conditions on construction sites. Practice shows that it is the patents that increasingly often determine who can build, sell, and make money from innovations, and who will be forced to pay high compensation for violating someone else's rights
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出建筑业竞争已从价格转向技术，专利成为决定市场准入与估值的关键。随着绿色建材与智能建造创新增多，相关专利申请持续高位。波兰等地建筑专利纠纷日益复杂，涵盖屋顶、幕墙及设备等领域，企业需提前布局以规避高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Competition in the construction industry has shifted from price and deadlines to technology, making patents a critical determinant of market access and corporate valuation. As innovations in green building materials and smart construction solutions increase, patent applications in this sector remain at high levels. In jurisdictions such as Poland, patent disputes are becoming increasingly complex, covering areas like roofing, facade structures, and specialized equipment. Rights holders must proactively manage their intellectual property strategies to secure market advantages and avoid significant financial risks associated with infringement claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应重视建筑施工技术的专利排查与布局，尤其关注绿色建材与智能系统。建议在项目早期进行 FTO 分析，避免因侵权面临高额赔偿或禁令，同时利用专利提升在欧洲市场的议价能力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize comprehensive patent clearance and strategic layout for construction technologies, particularly focusing on green materials and intelligent systems. Conducting freedom-to-operate analyses during the early project phases is essential to mitigate the risk of costly infringement damages or injunctions. Furthermore, leveraging robust patent portfolios can significantly enhance bargaining power within the European market, transforming intellectual property into a tangible asset for business growth and competitive defense against rivals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["技术专利成建筑业新竞争核心，直接决定市场垄断权与企业估值", "欧盟建筑类专利申请持续高位，绿色技术与智能方案增长显著", "专利纠纷范围扩大，从材料到施工设备均成争议高发区"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Technology patents have become the core of construction competition, directly determining market monopoly rights and enterprise valuations.", "European construction patent applications remain high, with significant growth observed in green technologies and smart building solutions.", "The scope of patent disputes is expanding, with materials and construction equipment emerging as high-risk areas for legal conflicts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出海建企需在项目前期开展专利自由实施（FTO）尽职调查", "加大绿色与智能建造技术研发，构建欧洲本地化专利护城河"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must conduct freedom-to-operate due diligence early in projects to prevent infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Increase investment in R&D for green and smart construction to build localized European patent barriers.", "What to monitor: Rising complexity in disputes involving specific construction materials and automated assembly equipment."]
core_points_en: Technology patents have become the core of construction competition, directly determining market monopoly rights and enterprise valuations.
European construction patent applications remain high, with significant growth observed in green technologies and smart building solutions.
The scope of patent disputes is expanding, with materials and construction equipment emerging as high-risk areas for legal conflicts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must conduct freedom-to-operate due diligence early in projects to prevent infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Increase investment in R&D for green and smart construction to build localized European patent barriers.
What to monitor: Rising complexity in disputes involving specific construction materials and automated assembly equipment.

items.title: George Orwell: Is fame an obstacle to trademark protection?
items.title_zh: 乔治·奥威尔：名声是否阻碍商标保护？
items.title_en: George Orwell Trademark: Does Fame Obstacle Protection in the EU?
items.summary: Discover why the George Orwell trademark was partially rejected in the EU, and the challenges of registering famous names as trademarks.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局及上诉大委员会历经八年审理，最终维持对“乔治·奥威尔”商标的部分驳回决定。裁决认为，鉴于该作者的极高知名度，相关公众会将该名称视为商品内容主题而非来源标识，导致其在文化类商品上缺乏显著性并具有描述性。此案确立了名人姓名在特定语境下可能无法发挥商标功能的裁判规则。
items.ai_summary_en: After eight years of proceedings, the EUIPO Grand Board of Appeal upheld the partial refusal of the GEORGE ORWELL trademark application. The decision confirms that due to the author’s exceptional fame, relevant consumers perceive the name as a reference to the content of cultural goods rather than as an indicator of commercial origin. Consequently, the sign lacks distinctiveness and is deemed descriptive for audiovisual media, printed matter, and entertainment services. This ruling highlights the significant challenges rights holders face when attempting to register famous personal names as trademarks, particularly within the cultural sector where such names are intrinsically linked to the subject matter of the products or services offered.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请含名人姓名商标时需警惕显著性风险，尤其涉及文创产品。建议避免直接使用高知名度人物姓名，或通过长期证据证明其已获得第二含义，以防因被视为描述性标志而被驳回。
items.ai_insight_en: This case establishes a critical precedent regarding the registrability of famous personal names in the European Union. Rights holders seeking to protect celebrity or historical figure names must anticipate heightened scrutiny regarding distinctiveness, especially for cultural goods. Direct registration may fail if the public associates the name primarily with the person’s work or persona. To overcome these barriers, applicants should consider alternative branding strategies or invest in gathering robust evidence of acquired distinctiveness through long-term use. Demonstrating that the name functions as a unique source identifier despite its fame is essential for successful protection in contested classes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["极高知名度导致消费者将姓名视为内容主题而非商业来源标识", "名人姓名在文化娱乐类商品上易被认定为具有描述性而缺乏显著性", "欧盟上诉大委员会重申商标描述性需依据相关公众感知进行评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["High public recognition causes consumers to view famous names as content themes rather than commercial source identifiers.", "Famous personal names in cultural and entertainment sectors are often found descriptive and lacking inherent distinctiveness.", "The Grand Board reaffirms that trademark descriptiveness must be assessed strictly based on the perception of the relevant public."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规避直接使用高知名度人物姓名申请文创类商标以防被拒", "需准备充分证据证明姓名已获第二含义以克服显著性障碍"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid direct registration of highly recognizable names for cultural products to prevent refusal based on descriptiveness.", "Practice note: Prepare comprehensive evidence of acquired distinctiveness via secondary meaning to overcome inherent lack of distinctiveness.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions applying this fame-based descriptiveness standard to other notable public figures."]
core_points_en: High public recognition causes consumers to view famous names as content themes rather than commercial source identifiers.
Famous personal names in cultural and entertainment sectors are often found descriptive and lacking inherent distinctiveness.
The Grand Board reaffirms that trademark descriptiveness must be assessed strictly based on the perception of the relevant public.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid direct registration of highly recognizable names for cultural products to prevent refusal based on descriptiveness.
Practice note: Prepare comprehensive evidence of acquired distinctiveness via secondary meaning to overcome inherent lack of distinctiveness.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions applying this fame-based descriptiveness standard to other notable public figures.

items.title: Popstar Katy Perry loses to Katie Perry, fashion designer, in trademark dispute
items.title_zh: 凯蒂·佩里商标案：澳洲高院限制名人声誉跨类保护
items.title_en: Australian High Court Limits Cross-Class Protection: Fame in Music Does Not Automatically Extend to Clothing
items.summary: The trademark dispute between Katy Perry popstar and Katie Perry fashion designer has taken an interesting turn. Read our article to find out more.
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国歌手凯蒂·佩里诉澳洲设计师凯蒂·佩里商标侵权案，澳洲高院终审判决歌手败诉。法院认定音乐领域声誉不自动延伸至服装类，若无实际使用证据，不能仅凭行业惯例主张跨类混淆。文章借此对比欧盟商标法中关于享有声誉商标的跨类保护规则及适用界限。
items.ai_summary_en: The Australian High Court has ruled in favor of fashion designer Katie Perry, determining that pop star Katy Perry’s reputation in the music industry does not automatically extend to clothing. The Court held that without evidence of actual use in the apparel sector, general industry practices regarding merchandise are insufficient to establish a risk of confusion across different classes. This decision highlights strict boundaries for cross-class protection based on reputation. The analysis contrasts this outcome with EU trademark law, examining the rigorous conditions required for well-known marks to secure protection beyond their registered goods or services, emphasizing that mere fame is not enough to prevent third-party registrations in unrelated sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局时应注意，仅有知名度而无实际使用难以获跨类保护。建议提前在核心及关联类别注册并保留使用证据，避免依赖声誉扩张被驳回。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking broad protection in Europe must recognize that high visibility alone does not guarantee cross-class rights. To successfully oppose later applications or enforce against infringement in unrelated categories, substantial evidence of actual use and established reputation in those specific sectors is critical. Relying solely on general fame carries significant legal risk. Companies should proactively secure registrations in core and adjacent product categories to create a robust defensive portfolio. Maintaining comprehensive records of commercial use ensures that claims of extended reputation are supported by factual market presence rather than theoretical consumer association.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["澳洲高院裁定音乐名人声誉不自动覆盖服装商品类别", "缺乏实际使用证据时，行业惯例不足以证明跨类混淆风险", "文章对比欧盟法律，探讨声誉商标跨类保护的严格适用条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Australian High Court ruled that a celebrity's reputation in music does not automatically extend to clothing products without specific proof.", "General industry practices regarding merchandise sales are insufficient to prove cross-class confusion absent evidence of actual market use.", "The article compares this ruling with EU standards, highlighting the strict application limits for protecting trademarks with a reputation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["知名度不等于跨类权，实际使用证据是维权关键基石", "对欧出口企业应完善多类别注册，防范他人抢注风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: High brand awareness does not equate to automatic cross-class protection; actual use evidence is essential for enforcement.", "Practice note: Secure registrations in core and related categories early to mitigate risks of third-party squatting or conflicting marks.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in the EU regarding the threshold of proof required to demonstrate reputation extending beyond registered goods."]
core_points_en: The Australian High Court ruled that a celebrity's reputation in music does not automatically extend to clothing products without specific proof.
General industry practices regarding merchandise sales are insufficient to prove cross-class confusion absent evidence of actual market use.
The article compares this ruling with EU standards, highlighting the strict application limits for protecting trademarks with a reputation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: High brand awareness does not equate to automatic cross-class protection; actual use evidence is essential for enforcement.
Practice note: Secure registrations in core and related categories early to mitigate risks of third-party squatting or conflicting marks.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in the EU regarding the threshold of proof required to demonstrate reputation extending beyond registered goods.

items.title: Fordham 33 (Report 3):  Top 5 Takeaways: The Global Divide in Design Protection
items.title_zh: 福特汉姆会议：外观设计保护的全球分歧五大要点
items.title_en: Fordham 33 Report: Top Five Takeaways on the Global Divide in Design Protection
items.summary:
A perfectly designed panel on design protectionThe IPKat is back in
Fordham, with the Spring skies and the chirping birds gracing the streets of
the Upper West Side. As a proud and long-standing partner with the Fordham IP Conference, now in its 33rd year, the IPKat is thrilled to partner with the students
of Fordham for reports from this years’ conference.  This report comes from Seth B.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文源自第 33 届福特汉姆知识产权会议，聚焦外观设计保护领域的全球性分歧。文章总结了面板讨论的五大核心观点，对比了欧盟与其他主要法域在外观设计制度上的差异。内容涵盖注册程序、保护范围及执法力度的不同，旨在为从业者提供跨国布局的策略参考，凸显协调全球规则的必要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This report from the 33rd Fordham Intellectual Property Conference examines the significant global divergences in design protection regimes. It synthesizes five key insights from a dedicated panel discussion, contrasting the European Union’s approach with other major jurisdictions. The analysis highlights critical differences in registration procedures, the scope of legal protection, and enforcement mechanisms. By mapping these disparities, the article provides strategic guidance for practitioners managing cross-border portfolios. It underscores the urgent need for greater international harmonization to improve efficiency and predictability in design rights management across different legal systems.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局外观设计时，需警惕全球规则差异带来的风险。建议深入研读欧盟独特制度，避免直接套用国内或美国经验。同时应利用会议洞察，优化跨国申请策略，提升在欧维权效率与合规水平。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must recognize that design laws vary significantly across borders, preventing the application of a uniform global strategy. Rights holders should avoid assuming that domestic or US-based practices are directly transferable to the EU context. Instead, stakeholders should tailor their approaches to align with specific local requirements, particularly regarding registration formalities and infringement standards. Leveraging insights from leading IP conferences can help optimize multinational filing strategies. This targeted approach enhances compliance, reduces procedural risks, and improves the effectiveness of enforcement actions within the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["全球外观设计保护制度存在显著的地域性差异与分歧", "欧盟与其他法域在注册程序和保护范围上各有侧重", "会议强调了加强国际协调以提升保护效率的重要性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Significant regional disparities exist in global design protection frameworks, creating complex challenges for international rights holders.", "The EU and other major jurisdictions differ substantially in their registration processes and the specific scope of design protection offered.", "Enhancing international coordination is essential to streamline procedures and improve the overall efficiency of global design protection."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需针对欧盟规则定制策略，避免全球通用化误区", "利用国际会议洞察，优化跨国申请与维权布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid applying generic global strategies; customize design protection approaches to meet specific EU regulatory requirements.", "Practice note: Leverage conference insights to refine multinational filing workflows and ensure robust compliance with local design laws.", "What to monitor: Track ongoing efforts toward international harmonization of design rules to anticipate changes in cross-border enforcement."]
core_points_en: Significant regional disparities exist in global design protection frameworks, creating complex challenges for international rights holders.
The EU and other major jurisdictions differ substantially in their registration processes and the specific scope of design protection offered.
Enhancing international coordination is essential to streamline procedures and improve the overall efficiency of global design protection.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid applying generic global strategies; customize design protection approaches to meet specific EU regulatory requirements.
Practice note: Leverage conference insights to refine multinational filing workflows and ensure robust compliance with local design laws.
What to monitor: Track ongoing efforts toward international harmonization of design rules to anticipate changes in cross-border enforcement.

items.title: Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno
items.title_zh: 微软在芬兰专利费用争议中胜诉但无实质收益
items.title_en: Microsoft Prevails in Cost Dispute with Suinno but Secures No Substantive Relief
items.summary: Finnish company Suinno is the owner of EP 2 671 173 covering a “method and means for browsing by walking”. It relates to people browsing the internet by walking to discover search results as they come into close proximity. The Helsinki-based company accuses Microsoft of infringing the patent and sued the US tech giant at […] The post Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰公司 Suinno 拥有涉及“步行浏览”方法的欧洲专利 EP 2 671 173，指控微软侵权并在赫尔辛基提起诉讼。最新进展显示，虽然微软在与 Suinno 的费用争端中获胜，但并未获得实质性赔偿或利益。该案反映了欧洲专利诉讼中程序性胜利与实体获益之间的差异，凸显了维权成本风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Suinno, a Finnish entity holding European patent EP 2 671 173 for a 'walking browsing' method, initiated infringement proceedings against Microsoft in Helsinki. The patent covers technologies enabling users to discover internet search results while walking. Although Microsoft successfully defended its position in the subsequent cost dispute regarding legal fees, it did not receive any substantive compensation or financial benefit from the ruling. This outcome underscores a critical distinction in European patent litigation: procedural victories on ancillary matters like costs do not automatically translate into commercial gains or damages. The case illustrates how rights holders and defendants may incur significant expenses without achieving meaningful economic resolution, highlighting the complex risk profile of enforcing or defending intellectual property rights in cross-border jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利诉讼时，需警惕高昂的程序成本。即便在费用裁决上获胜，若无法证明实质侵权或获取赔偿，仍可能面临“惨胜”局面。建议提前评估诉讼性价比，制定包含和解在内的多元策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in European patent disputes must carefully evaluate the cost-benefit ratio of litigation strategies. A favorable ruling on procedural issues, such as legal costs, does not guarantee overall financial success if substantive infringement claims remain unproven or if no damages are awarded. Companies should anticipate scenarios where legal wins result in net losses due to high operational and legal expenditures. It is advisable to develop diversified dispute resolution plans that include settlement options alongside traditional litigation. Proactive management of procedural cost risks is essential to avoid situations where defending or enforcing rights leads to pyrrhic victories rather than sustainable business outcomes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Suinno 持欧洲专利指控微软步行浏览技术侵权", "微软在费用争议案中胜诉但未获实际经济赔偿", "案件揭示专利诉讼中程序胜利不等于商业获益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Suinno holds European patent EP 2 671 173 covering walking-based browsing technology and accused Microsoft of infringement.", "Microsoft won the specific cost dispute against Suinno but failed to secure any actual monetary compensation or damages.", "The case demonstrates that procedural victories in patent litigation do not necessarily equate to tangible commercial benefits."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["诉讼胜诉未必带来经济回报，需权衡成本收益", "应重视欧洲专利诉讼中的程序费用风险管理"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigation success does not always yield financial returns; parties must weigh total costs against potential recovery.", "Practice note: Effective risk management requires assessing procedural cost exposure early in European patent enforcement actions.", "What to monitor: Track judicial trends in awarding costs versus substantive damages to refine future litigation budgeting."]
core_points_en: Suinno holds European patent EP 2 671 173 covering walking-based browsing technology and accused Microsoft of infringement.
Microsoft won the specific cost dispute against Suinno but failed to secure any actual monetary compensation or damages.
The case demonstrates that procedural victories in patent litigation do not necessarily equate to tangible commercial benefits.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigation success does not always yield financial returns; parties must weigh total costs against potential recovery.
Practice note: Effective risk management requires assessing procedural cost exposure early in European patent enforcement actions.
What to monitor: Track judicial trends in awarding costs versus substantive damages to refine future litigation budgeting.

items.title: Distribution issues: Italian Competition Authority fines Morellato for resale price maintenance and online marketplace restrictions
items.title_zh: 意竞争局因转售限价及平台禁令罚没莫雷拉托
items.title_en: Italian Competition Authority Fines Morellato for Resale Price Maintenance and Discriminatory Online Marketplace Bans
items.summary: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) announced that it had imposed a fine of just under €26 million on Morellato, a leading Italian...
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利竞争管理局对钟表商莫雷拉托处以近二千六百万欧元罚款，认定其实施转售价格维持及歧视性禁止经销商使用电商平台。尽管欧盟法院允许奢侈品基于品牌形象限制平台销售，但本案因品牌方自身利用该平台而构成歧视，违反比例原则。
items.ai_summary_en: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) imposed a fine of nearly €26 million on Morellato for antitrust violations. The authority found that the watch and jewellery manufacturer engaged in resale price maintenance by preventing distributors from freely setting prices. Additionally, Morellato prohibited authorised distributors from selling on online marketplaces like Amazon and eBay while simultaneously using these platforms for its own sales. The ICA ruled this ban discriminatory and disproportionate, rejecting arguments based on brand image preservation under the CJEU’s Coty judgment. This decision underscores strict enforcement against hardcore restrictions and non-uniform application of selective distribution rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应审查分销协议，避免设定最低转售价。若限制经销商入驻电商平台，须确保自身未在同一平台销售，以防被认定为歧视性限制而招致反垄断重罚。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights significant compliance risks for brands operating selective distribution systems in Europe. While the CJEU’s Coty judgment permits luxury goods suppliers to restrict third-party marketplace use to protect brand image, such restrictions must be proportionate and non-discriminatory. Rights holders must ensure their internal sales practices align with distributor obligations. Discrepancies between supplier behavior and contractual restrictions can lead to severe penalties and invalidate justifications based on brand protection or anti-counterfeiting measures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["固定或最低转售价格属核心限制，系近年欧盟及各成员国执法重点", "禁止使用电商平台需统一非歧视，品牌方自用则不得禁止经销商使用", "该案警示选择性分销体系中网络销售限制的合规边界与比例原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Resale price maintenance remains a hardcore competition restriction and a key enforcement priority for national authorities and the European Commission.", "Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while utilizing the same platforms for direct sales.", "The ICA rejected proportionality defenses, citing Commission Guidelines that deem such restrictions inappropriate when the supplier self-preferences the channel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严禁在协议中约定固定或最低转售价格，规避核心限制风险", "实施平台禁令须保持中立，品牌方自营与经销商政策必须一致"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands must audit selective distribution agreements to ensure online sales restrictions do not conflict with the supplier's own commercial activities on the same…", "Practice note: Avoid fixed or minimum resale prices entirely, as these constitute hardcore restrictions with high enforcement risk across EU jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Monitor national competition authority decisions regarding the proportionality of online marketplace bans, particularly where suppliers engage in direct-to-consum…"]
core_points_en: Resale price maintenance remains a hardcore competition restriction and a key enforcement priority for national authorities and the European Commission.
Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while utilizing the same platforms for direct sales.
The ICA rejected proportionality defenses, citing Commission Guidelines that deem such restrictions inappropriate when the supplier self-preferences the channel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must audit selective distribution agreements to ensure online sales restrictions do not conflict with the supplier's own commercial activities on the same…
Practice note: Avoid fixed or minimum resale prices entirely, as these constitute hardcore restrictions with high enforcement risk across EU jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Monitor national competition authority decisions regarding the proportionality of online marketplace bans, particularly where suppliers engage in direct-to-consum…

items.title: Malikie targets Hisense in licensing push; BlackBerry’s patent income dips
items.title_zh: Malikie 瞄准海信推进许可；黑莓专利收入下滑
items.title_en: Malikie Targets Hisense in Global Enforcement Push as BlackBerry Joins Sisvel Pool
items.summary: BlackBerry reports $22.2 million in licensing revenue and joins new Sisvel pool, while Malikie puts its former patents to work in global enforcement effort
items.ai_summary_zh: 黑莓公司报告季度专利许可收入为 2220 万美元，呈现下降趋势，并宣布加入新的 Sisvel 专利池以优化资产运营。与此同时，前属黑莓的专利组合由 Malikie 公司接管，并在全球范围内启动执法行动，明确将中国海信集团列为目标，显示出非执业实体在欧洲及全球市场加强专利变现与诉讼施压的最新动态。
items.ai_summary_en: BlackBerry reported quarterly patent licensing revenue of $22.2 million, reflecting a downward trend in its intellectual property monetization results. To optimize asset operations and streamline licensing processes, the company has announced its participation in a new Sisvel patent pool. Concurrently, Malikie, which has acquired former BlackBerry patents, has initiated a global enforcement campaign. This effort explicitly identifies Chinese electronics manufacturer Hisense Group as a primary target for licensing negotiations or litigation. These developments highlight the increasing activity of non-practicing entities in leveraging European and global markets to enforce patent rights and apply pressure through potential legal actions, signaling a shift in how legacy patent portfolios are being managed and monetized by specialized entities.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕 NPE 利用欧洲统一专利法院（UPC）机制发起批量诉讼。建议海信等被点名企业提前进行自由实施分析（FTO），评估无效宣告可能性，并制定包含和解谈判在内的综合应对策略，避免在市场关键期遭受禁令风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding non-practicing entities utilizing the Unified Patent Court mechanism to initiate concentrated litigation campaigns across Europe. Companies identified as targets, such as Hisense, should proactively conduct freedom-to-operate analyses and assess the viability of invalidation proceedings. Developing comprehensive response strategies that include settlement negotiations is crucial to mitigate the risk of injunctions during critical market periods. This approach ensures robust defense capabilities against aggressive IP enforcement tactics while maintaining operational continuity and minimizing potential financial exposure from prolonged legal disputes.

items.title: Awaiting the CJEU’s judgment in IKEA (C-298/23): trade mark rights vs freedom of expression
items.title_zh: 等待欧盟法院宜家案判决：商标权与言论自由之博弈
items.title_en: Awaiting CJEU Judgment in IKEA Case: Balancing Trademark Rights and Freedom of Expression
items.summary: One of the most anticipated rulings of the CJEU in trade mark law this year is the one in the IKEA case (C-298/23), which concerns the widely discussed conflict between trade mark rights and freedom of expression. See on this point also the previous article in this blog by Elena Izyumenko (here).
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧盟法院待决的宜家案（C-298/23），该案源于比利时法院提请，核心在于协调商标权与言论自由。总法务官意见提出“正当理由”可作为保护言论自由的机制，但强调需以商业使用为前提。若仅用于政治批评而非商品服务关联，则不构成商标侵权，无需限制言论自由。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the pending CJEU judgment in case C-298/23 (IKEA), referred by a Belgian court, which addresses the tension between trademark rights and freedom of expression. Advocate General Szpunar’s opinion suggests that 'due cause' can protect free speech, provided the use is commercial. However, if the sign is used solely for political criticism without linking to specific goods or services, no trademark conflict arises. The AG emphasizes that trademark use requires a connection to identifiable goods or services. Consequently, non-commercial political commentary referencing a brand does not constitute trademark infringement, ensuring freedom of expression remains unrestricted in such contexts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用他人商标进行评论或营销时，需严格区分商业与非商业用途。若涉及政治或社会批评，应确保不与自身商品服务直接关联，以利用言论自由抗辩规避侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must strictly distinguish between commercial exploitation and non-commercial commentary when using third-party trademarks. Utilizing a well-known mark for political or social criticism may avoid infringement liability if the usage is not directly linked to the user's own goods or services. Rights holders should monitor whether contested uses qualify as genuine trademark use in commerce. Conversely, critics and activists should ensure their expressions remain distinct from commercial branding activities to successfully invoke freedom of expression defenses against potential trademark claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总法务官主张正当理由可平衡商标权与言论自由冲突", "非商业性使用或仅关联权利人商品时不产生权利冲突", "知识产权与言论自由同为基本权利，需在侵权要件中平衡"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Advocate General proposes 'due cause' as a mechanism to balance trademark protection with freedom of expression considerations.", "No conflict exists if the sign is used exclusively for the proprietor's goods or purely for non-commercial political critique.", "Intellectual property and free speech are fundamental rights requiring balance within general trademark infringement conditions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["区分商业与非商业用途是规避商标侵权的关键防线", "利用言论自由抗辩需确保符号使用不指向自身商品服务"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Distinguishing commercial from non-commercial use is critical to avoiding trademark infringement risks.", "Practice note: Ensure symbolic use for criticism does not directly reference the user's own goods or services.", "What to monitor: How courts define 'use in the course of trade' regarding political or social commentary involving brands."]
core_points_en: Advocate General proposes 'due cause' as a mechanism to balance trademark protection with freedom of expression considerations.
No conflict exists if the sign is used exclusively for the proprietor's goods or purely for non-commercial political critique.
Intellectual property and free speech are fundamental rights requiring balance within general trademark infringement conditions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Distinguishing commercial from non-commercial use is critical to avoiding trademark infringement risks.
Practice note: Ensure symbolic use for criticism does not directly reference the user's own goods or services.
What to monitor: How courts define 'use in the course of trade' regarding political or social commentary involving brands.

items.title: Dépôt de marque en Europe : les clés d'une stratégie réussie pour les entreprises américaines en expansion
items.title_zh: 欧洲商标布局：美企扩张的成功策略指南
items.title_en: Dépôt de marque en Europe : les clés d'une stratégie réussie pour les entreprises américaines en expansion
items.summary: Lorsqu'une entreprise envisage une expansion à l'international, la propriété intellectuelle (PI) constitue un enjeu stratégique majeur. Megan Baker propose aux titulaires de marques américains des conseils...
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对美企欧洲扩张需求，文章解析后脱欧时代商标保护策略。重点阐述 EUIPO 统辖欧盟成员国、UKIPO 管辖英国及瑞士等国的独立注册体系差异。指出既有欧盟商标已自动克隆为英国权利，新申请需单独指定。强调知识产权是特许经营基石，企业应结合目标市场灵活选择欧盟或单一国家注册路径以确权。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应借鉴此策略，厘清欧盟与英国分立的商标版图。在布局时避免盲目依赖单一欧盟申请，需根据业务实质评估是否追加英国或瑞士等国别注册，构建严密的欧洲商标防护网，防范因地域覆盖缺失导致的品牌侵权风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["脱欧后英国脱离 EUIPO 体系，需通过 UKIPO 单独获取商标保护", "欧盟商标覆盖成员国，非欧盟国如瑞士须走国家注册或国际指定程序", "知识产权资产是跨国特许经营战略核心，需提前规划确权路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["精准区分欧盟与英国商标管辖权，避免因地域遗漏导致权利真空", "将商标布局纳入特许经营整体战略，利用 IP 资产夯实海外扩张根基"]

items.title: Bilan 2025 de la Douane : ce que les titulaires de droits de propriété intellectuelle doivent retenir
items.title_zh: 2025 法国海关年报：知识产权权利人需知要点
items.title_en: French Customs 2025 Report: Key Takeaways for Intellectual Property Rights Holders
items.summary: Le bilan 2025 de la Douane française confirme que la contrefaçon est un phénomène massif, transversal et en constante adaptation. Avec plus de 20 millions d'articles contrefaisants retirés du marché en 2025...
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国海关 2025 年报显示查获超 2000 万件假货，三年累计逾 6000 万件。法国干预申请量居欧盟第二，仅次于德国。侵权呈现碎片化特征，89% 小包裹源自中国，Shein 等平台成主要渠道。玩具与体育用品占查获量六成五，且常伴随健康安全风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The French Customs 2025 report confirms that counterfeiting remains a massive, evolving phenomenon. Over 20 million infringing articles were removed from the market in 2025, bringing the three-year total to more than 60 million seizures. These figures reflect both intense enforcement controls and the sheer volume of illicit flows. France ranks second in the EU for customs intervention applications, highlighting its strategic role as a transit hub. The landscape has shifted towards fragmented logistics, with e-commerce and express freight becoming primary channels. Small packages under 150 euros accounted for the majority of interceptions, with nearly 90 percent originating from China. Major platforms like Shein, Temu, and AliExpress dominate these shipments. Counterfeit goods increasingly pose health and safety risks, particularly in toys and sports equipment, necessitating vigilant monitoring by rights holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应高度警惕跨境电商小包渠道的合规风险，避免成为重点监控对象。建议提前在法备案知识产权，主动配合海关排查，并严控供应链质量以防因安全问题的连带法律责任。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should prioritize compliance within cross-border e-commerce small package channels to avoid becoming targets of intensified customs scrutiny. Proactive registration of intellectual property with French customs authorities is essential to facilitate early interception of infringing goods. Companies must also enforce strict supply chain quality controls to mitigate liability for safety-related issues. By leveraging customs recording mechanisms, businesses can actively protect their rights and reduce the probability of cargo detention. This approach ensures better alignment with evolving enforcement trends and minimizes operational disruptions caused by regulatory actions against suspected counterfeit shipments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国海关干预申请量居欧盟第二，凸显其战略枢纽地位", "电商与小包裹成造假主渠道，近九成涉华货物被拦截", "热门玩具如 Labubu 仿冒泛滥，产品安全隐患日益突出"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["France ranks second in the EU for customs intervention applications, underscoring its strategic importance as a key transit hub for international trade.", "E-commerce and small package shipments are now the primary channels for counterfeit goods, with nearly ninety percent of intercepted parcels originating from China.", "Popular items like Labubu dolls face widespread imitation, while seized products increasingly present significant health and safety risks to consumers."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规避电商小包高风险通道，强化出口合规审查", "利用海关备案机制主动维权，降低货物扣留概率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Mitigate risks in high-volume e-commerce small package channels through rigorous export compliance reviews.", "Practice note: Utilize customs IP recording mechanisms to proactively enforce rights and lower the likelihood of goods detention.", "What to monitor: Track the rise of health and safety risks associated with counterfeit toys and sports equipment in express freight."]
core_points_en: France ranks second in the EU for customs intervention applications, underscoring its strategic importance as a key transit hub for international trade.
E-commerce and small package shipments are now the primary channels for counterfeit goods, with nearly ninety percent of intercepted parcels originating from China.
Popular items like Labubu dolls face widespread imitation, while seized products increasingly present significant health and safety risks to consumers.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Mitigate risks in high-volume e-commerce small package channels through rigorous export compliance reviews.
Practice note: Utilize customs IP recording mechanisms to proactively enforce rights and lower the likelihood of goods detention.
What to monitor: Track the rise of health and safety risks associated with counterfeit toys and sports equipment in express freight.

Legal professionals report that they are actively educating clients on the nuances of this mechanism, aiming to demonstrate how it can be utilized most effectively in complex disputes. This shift reflects a broader trend where specialized procedural tools are gaining prominence, requiring both counsel and rights holders to stay informed about evolving litigation tactics and defensive strategies in European trademark enforcement contexts.

As firms adjust their strategies, rights holders should ensure their legal teams are proficient in leveraging these tools defensively or offensively. Understanding the practical implications of Schedule A allows companies to anticipate opposing counsel's moves and prepare robust responses. Continuous monitoring of case law developments involving this mechanism is essential for maintaining a competitive edge in trademark enforcement and dispute resolution across European jurisdictions.
