items.title: New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court
items.title_zh: 新专利权人 Silimed 在慕尼黑法院获针对 Polytech 的临时禁令
items.title_en: Silimed Secures Preliminary Injunction Against Polytech in Munich Breast Implant Patent Dispute
items.summary: Silimed has achieved its first success in the infringement dispute over breast implant technology. After the Brazilian manufacturer secured the rights to a patent for the manufacture of breast implants in a complex entitlement action, Silimed is now taking measures against its German competitor and the former owner of the patent, Polytech. At the centre […] The post New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴西制造商 Silimed 在复杂的权属诉讼中成功获得乳房植入物制造专利后，在德国慕尼黑法院对前专利权人及竞争对手 Polytech 提起侵权诉讼并取得初步胜利。法院颁发了临时禁令（PI），标志着双方在植入物技术领域的纠纷进入实质执行阶段，凸显了德国作为专利诉讼地的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: Brazilian manufacturer Silimed has achieved a significant early victory in its patent infringement dispute concerning breast implant technology. Following a complex entitlement action where Silimed successfully secured ownership rights to the relevant manufacturing patent, the company initiated enforcement measures against its German competitor and former patent holder, Polytech. The Munich Regional Court has granted a preliminary injunction, effectively halting Polytech’s alleged infringing activities. This development marks a transition of the conflict into the substantive enforcement phase. The case underscores the strategic importance of Germany as a key jurisdiction for patent litigation, particularly in the medical device sector, where rapid judicial remedies can decisively influence market dynamics and competitive positioning between rival manufacturers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧并购或技术引进时，须前置排查专利权属链条风险。面对德国高频诉讼环境，应建立快速响应机制，利用临时禁令程序保护市场或应对突发指控，避免陷入被动。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies engaged in cross-border transactions or technology acquisitions, thorough due diligence regarding historical patent ownership chains is essential to mitigate latent risks. In jurisdictions like Germany, where preliminary injunctions are frequently utilized, market participants must establish robust rapid response mechanisms. Understanding local procedural nuances allows firms to either protect their market position aggressively or defend against sudden allegations effectively. Proactive preparation ensures that rights holders can navigate high-stakes IP conflicts without being caught off guard, maintaining operational stability during critical enforcement phases.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Silimed 通过权属诉讼确立专利所有权并发起反击", "慕尼黑法院颁发临时禁令制止 Polytech 涉嫌侵权行为", "案件反映医疗器械领域专利权属与侵权纠纷的复杂性"]

items.title: CMA ends vet services probe – key findings and what to look out for next
items.title_zh: 英国 CMA 结束兽医服务调查：关键发现与后续展望
items.title_en: CMA Concludes Veterinary Services Probe: Mandatory Ownership Disclosure and Enhanced RCVS Oversight
items.summary: The UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) recently concluded its 2-year investigation into the veterinary services industry. We take a look at th...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争与市场管理局（CMA） concludes 为期两年的兽医服务行业调查，发布综合救济方案。措施包括强制披露集团所有权信息以增强消费者比价能力，并赋予皇家兽医学院（RCVS）新的监控职能及修订行为准则权限，相关成本将由行业分摊。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has finalized its two-year investigation into the veterinary services sector, introducing a comprehensive remedies package aimed at boosting market competition. Key measures include mandatory clear identification of single-entity control over groups of veterinary practices on signage and online platforms to empower pet owners with better information for comparing prices and service quality. The CMA has assigned the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons (RCVS) a new monitoring role to assess compliance with these remedies and facilitate the sharing of practice data for comparison tools. Additionally, the RCVS is recommended to update its Codes and Guidance to align with CMA requirements and enhance its 'Find a Pet' service. These expanded regulatory activities will be funded through a levy imposed on veterinary businesses, signaling a shift toward greater transparency and accountability in the industry.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英开展动物医疗或相关服务的中国企业需关注所有权披露新规，确保合规标识。同时应留意 RCVS 新职权下的行业标准变化，提前调整运营策略以适应更透明的市场竞争环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating veterinary practices in the UK must prioritize strict adherence to new ownership disclosure mandates to ensure full regulatory compliance. Beyond immediate labeling requirements, stakeholders should anticipate broader shifts in industry standards as the RCVS integrates CMA remedies into its professional codes. This evolution towards transparent pricing and service metrics will likely intensify competitive pressures. Companies should proactively review their operational strategies and branding to align with these enhanced transparency expectations, ensuring they are well-positioned within a more informed and competitive consumer landscape.

items.title: Action en nullité des droits de propriété industrielle : le caractère rétroactif de l’imprescriptibilité affirmé
items.title_zh: 法最高院确认：工业产权无效诉讼之不受时效限制具溯及力
items.title_en: French Supreme Court Affirms Retroactive Effect of Timeless Nullity Actions for Industrial Property Rights
items.summary: (Cour de cassation, civile, Chambre commerciale, 28 janvier 2026, 24-14.760, Publié au bulletin) Par Maître Charlotte GAUVIN, Avocate au barreau de Paris * La loi n° 2019-486 du 22 mai 2019 relative à la croissance et la transformation des entreprises (ci-après la « loi Pacte ») a instauré le principe d’imprescriptibilité des actions en nullité […] L’article Action en nullité des droits de propriété industrielle : le caractère rétroactif de l’imprescriptibilité affirmé est apparu en premier sur
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国最高法院商事庭于 2026 年 1 月作出判决，明确 2019 年《帕克特法案》确立的工业产权无效诉讼不受时效限制原则具有溯及既往的效力。这意味着即便在法案生效前已逾旧法诉讼时效的案件，权利人或利害关系人仍可提起无效宣告请求，彻底消除了时间壁垒对权利稳定性的潜在威胁。
items.ai_summary_en: The Commercial Chamber of the French Cour de cassation issued a landmark ruling on January 28, 2026, confirming that the principle of imprescriptibility for industrial property nullity actions, established by the 2019 PACTE Law, applies retroactively. This decision clarifies that the statutory limitation periods under previous legislation no longer bar challenges to patents and trademarks, even if those deadlines expired before the new law entered into force. Consequently, rights holders and interested parties may initiate nullity proceedings against existing industrial property titles without temporal restrictions. This interpretation fundamentally alters the legal landscape by removing time-based barriers to invalidity claims, thereby exposing previously secured rights to renewed scrutiny and potential cancellation regardless of their age or prior procedural history.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在法拥有或布局知识产权的中国企业需警惕存量专利商标被随时挑战的风险。建议立即开展法国 IP 资产合规性自查，评估核心权利稳定性，并针对潜在无效风险制定防御策略，避免陷入被动诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts the stability of intellectual property portfolios in France. Rights holders must recognize that previously secure assets are now vulnerable to indefinite challenges. It is critical to conduct immediate audits of patent and trademark holdings to assess potential weaknesses. Companies should proactively evaluate the strength of their core rights and develop robust defense strategies against potential invalidity claims. Maintaining comprehensive documentation and monitoring competitor activities will be essential to mitigate litigation risks and ensure long-term portfolio security in this new regulatory environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国最高院裁定无效诉讼不受时效限制原则具溯及力", "年帕克特法案新规可适用于法案生效前的旧案", "工业产权权利稳定性面临长期且无时间界限的挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The French Supreme Court ruled that the imprescriptibility of nullity actions under the 2019 PACTE Law applies retroactively to all cases.", "Nullity challenges can now be filed against industrial property rights even if the statute of limitations had already expired under prior laws.", "Industrial property rights face ongoing validity risks as there are no longer any time limits for initiating nullity proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在法存量知识产权面临无时限被提无效的高风险", "亟需开展权利稳定性自查并构建长效防御机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Existing French IP assets face heightened risk of indefinite nullity challenges, requiring urgent stability assessments.", "Practice note: Conduct immediate compliance audits of patent and trademark portfolios to identify vulnerabilities and prepare defense mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretations regarding retroactive application and emerging trends in post-grant invalidity proceedings."]
core_points_en: The French Supreme Court ruled that the imprescriptibility of nullity actions under the 2019 PACTE Law applies retroactively to all cases.
Nullity challenges can now be filed against industrial property rights even if the statute of limitations had already expired under prior laws.
Industrial property rights face ongoing validity risks as there are no longer any time limits for initiating nullity proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Existing French IP assets face heightened risk of indefinite nullity challenges, requiring urgent stability assessments.
Practice note: Conduct immediate compliance audits of patent and trademark portfolios to identify vulnerabilities and prepare defense mechanisms.
What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretations regarding retroactive application and emerging trends in post-grant invalidity proceedings.

items.title: UK government confirms new subscription contracts rules for Spring 2027
items.title_zh: 英国政府确认订阅合同新规将于 2027 年春季生效
items.title_en: UK Government Confirms New Subscription Contract Rules Effective Spring 2027
items.summary: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its long-awaited response on the new subscription contracts regime. The announcement outlines plans for sec...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布回应，计划依据《数字市场、竞争与消费者法案》制定二级立法，预计 2027 年春季生效。新规旨在解决每年造成 16 亿英镑损失的 unwanted 订阅问题，通过增强透明度和退出机制保护消费者。竞争与市场管理局（CMA）强调将利用现有职权执法，违规企业最高面临全球年营业额 10% 的罚款。
items.ai_summary_en: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its response regarding new regulations for subscription contracts under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCCA). These rules are scheduled to take effect in Spring 2027, aiming to reduce unwanted subscriptions by enhancing consumer transparency and exit mechanisms. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) retains enforcement powers during the interim period, with potential fines reaching 10% of global turnover. Businesses are advised to review practices early to mitigate enforcement risks before formal commencement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英及面向英国市场的中国企业需立即审查订阅条款，确保取消流程便捷透明。建议提前对标 CMA 执法案例进行合规自查，避免因新规实施或过渡期执法行动遭受高额罚款及声誉损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and service providers operating in the UK market should proactively align their subscription management systems with the anticipated DMCCA requirements. Early compliance assessments can mitigate the risk of CMA enforcement actions during the transition period. Monitoring forthcoming government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions is essential for maintaining robust consumer law compliance strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新规预计 2027 年春季生效，旨在消除强制订阅陷阱", "CMA 将加强执法，违规罚款可达全球年营业额一成", "政府将发布指南协助企业落实合理的退出及混合合同实践"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Secondary legislation under the DMCCA will introduce stricter transparency and cancellation requirements for subscription services by Spring 2027.", "The CMA remains active in enforcing existing consumer protection laws, including investigations into unfair cancellation fees and mixed contracts.", "Non-compliance may result in significant penalties, prompting businesses to assess current subscription terms against upcoming regulatory standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查订阅取消流程，确保符合即将出台的透明度要求", "警惕 CMA 过渡期执法风险，开展合规自查以规避高额罚款"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies offering subscription services must prepare for enhanced transparency obligations and streamlined cancellation processes mandated by the DMCCA.", "Practice note: Conduct internal audits of current subscription terms and cancellation flows to identify gaps against expected regulatory standards and CMA enforcement priorities.", "What to monitor: Await detailed government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions to ensure precise alignment with final implementation rules."]
core_points_en: Secondary legislation under the DMCCA will introduce stricter transparency and cancellation requirements for subscription services by Spring 2027.
The CMA remains active in enforcing existing consumer protection laws, including investigations into unfair cancellation fees and mixed contracts.
Non-compliance may result in significant penalties, prompting businesses to assess current subscription terms against upcoming regulatory standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies offering subscription services must prepare for enhanced transparency obligations and streamlined cancellation processes mandated by the DMCCA.
Practice note: Conduct internal audits of current subscription terms and cancellation flows to identify gaps against expected regulatory standards and CMA enforcement priorities.
What to monitor: Await detailed government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions to ensure precise alignment with final implementation rules.

items.title: Comment bénéficier du nouveau barème de remboursement des taxes de l’OMPI en matière de procédure UDRP pour obtenir les données WHOIS à moindre coût ?
items.title_zh: 如何利用 WIPO 新退费标准低成本获取 UDRP 程序中的 WHOIS 数据
items.title_en: Leveraging WIPO’s New UDRP Fee Reimbursement to Access WHOIS Data Cost-Effectively
items.summary: Introduction L’entrée en vigueur du RGPD a restreint l’accès aux données WHOIS, rendant plus complexe l’identification des titulaires de noms de domaine litigieux. Dans ce contexte, la procédure UDRP tend à être mobilisée comme un mécanisme indirect d’accès à ces informations d’identification. La révision du barème des honoraires de l’OMPI
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出 GDPR 实施限制了 WHOIS 数据访问，增加了识别域名持有人的难度。在此背景下，UDRP 程序常被用作间接获取身份信息的机制。文中重点分析了 WIPO 修订后的费用退还标准，指导申请人如何通过该新机制在启动 UDRP 程序时降低获取关键数据的经济成本。
items.ai_summary_en: The implementation of the GDPR has significantly restricted public access to WHOIS data, complicating the identification of domain name registrants involved in disputes. In this environment, the Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy (UDRP) procedure is increasingly utilized as an indirect mechanism to obtain essential identification information. This analysis examines the revised fee schedule introduced by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), specifically focusing on new reimbursement standards. It provides practical guidance for applicants on how to utilize these updated rules to reduce the financial burden associated with initiating UDRP proceedings. The article highlights that rights holders can now lower the economic threshold for accessing critical registrant data, transforming the procedural cost structure into a more manageable investigative tool for enforcing intellectual property rights against anonymous domain registrations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇域名侵权且无法获取对方信息时，可评估利用 UDRP 新费率策略。建议提前核算成本收益，将此作为突破数据封锁、推进维权的战术选项，而非仅视为最终裁决程序。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders facing domain infringement in Europe, particularly where registrant data is obscured by privacy regulations, should evaluate the strategic use of UDRP under the new WIPO fee structures. Rather than viewing UDRP solely as a final adjudication mechanism, it can serve as a tactical instrument to uncover infringer identities at a reduced net cost. Stakeholders are advised to conduct preliminary cost-benefit analyses, factoring in potential fee reimbursements. This approach allows for optimized budget allocation when pursuing anonymous registrations, ensuring that the investment in dispute resolution yields both evidentiary value and potential substantive relief.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["GDPR 限制导致通过常规渠道获取域名持有人身份信息变得复杂困难", "UDRP 程序正逐渐演变为权利人间接获取被投诉人身份信息的重要手段", "利用 WIPO 最新费用退还规则可有效降低为获取数据而启动程序的经济门槛"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["GDPR restrictions have made obtaining domain holder identity information through standard channels complex and difficult.", "UDRP proceedings are evolving into a key strategic method for rights holders to indirectly acquire respondent identity details.", "Utilizing WIPO's latest fee reimbursement rules effectively lowers the financial barrier to initiating data-access procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用程序规则变化，将 UDRP 转化为低成本获取侵权主体信息的调查工具", "针对匿名注册域名，需重新评估维权预算，利用退费机制优化投入产出比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use UDRP as a low-cost investigative tool to identify anonymous infringers when standard WHOIS access is blocked.", "Practice note: Reassess enforcement budgets for anonymous domains, leveraging fee reimbursement mechanisms to improve return on investment.", "What to monitor: Track WIPO administrative practices regarding fee refunds to ensure accurate calculation of net procedural costs."]
core_points_en: GDPR restrictions have made obtaining domain holder identity information through standard channels complex and difficult.
UDRP proceedings are evolving into a key strategic method for rights holders to indirectly acquire respondent identity details.
Utilizing WIPO's latest fee reimbursement rules effectively lowers the financial barrier to initiating data-access procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use UDRP as a low-cost investigative tool to identify anonymous infringers when standard WHOIS access is blocked.
Practice note: Reassess enforcement budgets for anonymous domains, leveraging fee reimbursement mechanisms to improve return on investment.
What to monitor: Track WIPO administrative practices regarding fee refunds to ensure accurate calculation of net procedural costs.

items.title: UPC update: Glucose monitoring PI upheld | CoA offers transcription clarity
items.title_zh: UPC 更新：血糖监测禁令维持，上诉庭明确转录规则
items.title_en: UPC Update: Glucose Monitoring Preliminary Injunction Upheld and Court of Appeal Clarifies Transcription Rules
items.summary: In major recent developments, Dyson snagged another win against Hong Kong-based competitor Dreame and a new AI-powered UPC platform was launched
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道统一专利法院（UPC）两项重要进展：一是维持针对血糖监测技术的临时禁令，彰显 UPC 在医药专利保护上的强硬立场；二是上诉庭就庭审转录程序提供澄清，提升司法透明度。此外，文中提及戴森胜诉及 AI 平台上线等动态，反映 UPC 生态快速演进。
items.ai_summary_en: This report highlights two significant developments at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). First, the court upheld a preliminary injunction concerning glucose monitoring technology, signaling a robust stance on enforcing pharmaceutical and medical device patents. Second, the UPC Court of Appeal provided essential clarification regarding courtroom transcription procedures, thereby enhancing procedural transparency and legal certainty for litigants. Additionally, the article notes Dyson’s recent victory against competitor Dreame and the launch of a new AI-powered platform designed to streamline case management. These events collectively illustrate the rapid evolution of the UPC ecosystem, demonstrating its growing influence in European patent litigation and its commitment to improving operational efficiency and judicial clarity for all market participants involved in cross-border IP disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械与科技企业应警惕 UPC 禁令风险，提前布局欧洲专利组合。建议密切关注 UPC 程序规则变化，利用新工具优化诉讼策略，并考虑在关键市场申请临时禁令以反制竞争。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in the medical technology sector should closely monitor the UPC’s increasing willingness to grant preliminary injunctions, as this trend impacts freedom-to-operate strategies and competitive dynamics in Europe. The clarification of transcription rules offers greater procedural certainty, allowing legal teams to better anticipate case management timelines and prepare more effectively for hearings. Furthermore, the introduction of AI tools presents an opportunity to optimize litigation workflows and conduct more efficient prior art searches. Companies should integrate these procedural updates into their broader European IP strategy, ensuring that patent portfolios are robust enough to withstand scrutiny while leveraging new technological resources to manage costs and improve response times in potential infringement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 维持血糖监测技术临时禁令，强化专利执行力", "上诉庭明确庭审转录规则，提升程序清晰度与可预测性", "新 AI 驱动平台上线，助力 UPC 案件管理与信息检索效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC upheld a preliminary injunction for glucose monitoring technology, reinforcing strong enforcement capabilities for medical device patents across participating member state…", "The Court of Appeal clarified rules for courtroom transcriptions, significantly improving procedural predictability and transparency for parties engaged in complex patent litigati…", "A new AI-driven platform was launched to enhance case management and information retrieval efficiency, reflecting the UPC's ongoing efforts to modernize its digital infrastructure…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 UPC 禁令趋势，强化高风险产品专利自由实施分析", "善用新 AI 平台与程序规则，提升在欧洲的诉讼应对能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor UPC preliminary injunction trends to assess risks for high-value medical devices and adjust freedom-to-operate analyses accordingly.", "Practice note: Leverage clarified transcription rules and new AI platforms to enhance litigation preparedness, streamline case management, and improve strategic decision-making.", "What to monitor: Track further procedural clarifications from the Court of Appeal and the adoption rates of AI tools to refine European patent enforcement and defense strategies."]
core_points_en: The UPC upheld a preliminary injunction for glucose monitoring technology, reinforcing strong enforcement capabilities for medical device patents across participating member state…
The Court of Appeal clarified rules for courtroom transcriptions, significantly improving procedural predictability and transparency for parties engaged in complex patent litigati…
A new AI-driven platform was launched to enhance case management and information retrieval efficiency, reflecting the UPC's ongoing efforts to modernize its digital infrastructure…
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UPC preliminary injunction trends to assess risks for high-value medical devices and adjust freedom-to-operate analyses accordingly.
Practice note: Leverage clarified transcription rules and new AI platforms to enhance litigation preparedness, streamline case management, and improve strategic decision-making.
What to monitor: Track further procedural clarifications from the Court of Appeal and the adoption rates of AI tools to refine European patent enforcement and defense strategies.

items.title: [GuestPost] Keeping enemies closer: C&J Clark v TBC and trade mark coexistence agreements
items.title_zh: 客席文章：化敌为友——C&J Clark 案与商标共存协议
items.title_en: C&J Clark v Trek: UK Court Clarifies Legal Role of Trademark Coexistence Agreements
items.summary: The IPKat is grateful to Katfriend Thomas Hood (Gatehouse Chambers) for the following contribution on the recent UK decision in C & J Clark International ltd v Trek Bicycle Corporation & Anor [2026] EWHC 659 (Ch). Over to Tom:In the world of trade marks, businesses can often end up using similar marks in different sectors and risk stepping on each other’s toes. A
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国高等法院近期作出的 C&J Clark 诉 Trek 自行车公司一案。案件聚焦于不同行业企业使用相似商标时的冲突风险，重点探讨了商标共存协议在解决此类纠纷中的法律效力与实际应用，为处理跨类商标冲突提供了司法实践指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the recent High Court judgment in C & J Clark International Ltd v Trek Bicycle Corporation & Anor [2026] EWHC 659 (Ch). The case addresses the inherent risks when businesses operating in distinct sectors utilize similar trademarks, potentially leading to commercial conflict. The discussion centers on the legal validity and practical application of trademark coexistence agreements as mechanisms to resolve such disputes. By scrutinizing how courts interpret these contracts, the article provides essential judicial guidance for managing cross-class trademark conflicts. It highlights the strategic importance of formalized agreements in defining rights boundaries, offering a clear framework for market participants navigating complex intellectual property landscapes where brand similarity intersects with diverse commercial activities.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展时，若遇近似商标障碍，可考虑签署共存协议。需确保协议条款清晰界定商品类别与地域，避免因约定不明导致后续无效或侵权诉讼，降低出海合规成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants encountering similar trademark obstacles during European expansion should consider negotiating coexistence agreements. These instruments can effectively lower entry barriers by resolving potential conflicts without prolonged litigation. However, parties must ensure that agreement terms clearly specify relevant goods categories and geographic territories. Ambiguous or poorly drafted clauses may lead to subsequent invalidity claims or infringement suits, thereby increasing compliance costs. Clear contractual definitions are essential to prevent future legal uncertainties. Rights holders should prioritize precision in drafting to secure enforceable protections and maintain stable market positions across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["不同行业使用相似商标易引发侵权风险与商业冲突", "法院审查商标共存协议以界定双方权利边界与使用范围", "共存协议是化解跨类商标纠纷及避免诉讼的重要工具"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Using similar trademarks across different industries frequently triggers infringement risks and significant commercial conflicts.", "Courts examine coexistence agreements to precisely define the boundaries of rights and permissible scopes of use.", "Coexistence agreements serve as vital tools for resolving cross-class disputes and avoiding costly litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用共存协议化解跨类商标冲突，降低进入欧洲市场门槛", "协议条款须明确具体，防止因模糊约定引发后续法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Coexistence agreements can resolve cross-class conflicts and lower entry barriers to the European market.", "Practice note: Agreement terms must be explicit regarding product classes and territories to prevent legal risks from vague wording.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of coexistence agreement scope to ensure enforceability and avoid future infringement disputes."]
core_points_en: Using similar trademarks across different industries frequently triggers infringement risks and significant commercial conflicts.
Courts examine coexistence agreements to precisely define the boundaries of rights and permissible scopes of use.
Coexistence agreements serve as vital tools for resolving cross-class disputes and avoiding costly litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Coexistence agreements can resolve cross-class conflicts and lower entry barriers to the European market.
Practice note: Agreement terms must be explicit regarding product classes and territories to prevent legal risks from vague wording.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of coexistence agreement scope to ensure enforceability and avoid future infringement disputes.

items.title: Ocado brings police to trade fair booth in warehouse tech row
items.title_zh: Ocado 召警察至展台执行仓储技术专利禁令
items.title_en: Ocado Deploys Police at Trade Fair to Enforce Patent Injunction Against Competitor
items.summary: The grocery tech company caused a stir when police were called to enforce a patent injunction on a competitor’s stand, turning a legal dispute into a dramatic public showdown.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国杂货科技公司 Ocado 在贸易展会上引发轰动，其呼叫警方介入以执行针对竞争对手展位的专利禁令。该事件将原本的法律纠纷升级为公开的戏剧性对峙，凸显了专利持有人在欧洲利用临时禁令及执法力量在商业展会现场进行即时维权的激进策略与实际操作流程。
items.ai_summary_en: Grocery technology firm Ocado generated significant attention by involving law enforcement to execute a patent injunction at a competitor’s exhibition booth. This action transformed a standard legal dispute into a highly visible public confrontation, demonstrating the aggressive tactics rights holders may employ in Europe. The incident highlights the practical application of preliminary injunctions and the immediate involvement of enforcement authorities during commercial trade shows. It underscores how intellectual property conflicts can escalate rapidly from courtroom proceedings to on-site operational disruptions, affecting exhibitors’ ability to display products and conduct business during critical industry events.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国参展企业需警惕对手利用临时禁令在展会突袭。建议提前开展 FT 分析，制定应急预案，避免展位被查封或展品被扣押，确保在欧洲市场的合规运营。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exhibiting in Europe should anticipate the risk of sudden patent enforcement actions, including police-assisted injunctions at trade fairs. Proactive freedom-to-operate analysis is essential before launching products or attending major industry events. Companies must develop robust contingency plans to address potential site seizures or product confiscations. Ensuring compliance with local IP regulations and preparing rapid response protocols can mitigate operational disruptions and protect brand reputation during high-profile commercial showcases.

items.title: AI patent drafting: A blessing and a curse
items.title_zh: AI 专利撰写：机遇与风险并存
items.title_en: AI Patent Drafting: Balancing Efficiency Gains Against Reliability Risks in Protection
items.summary: The integration of AI into patent drafting is undeniably transformative, but overreliance on these tools can put meaningful protection for inventions at risk, says Ana Neves of Inventa.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出人工智能正深刻改变专利撰写流程，显著提升研究与申请效率。然而，过度依赖算法可能导致信息可靠性下降，削弱发明的实质保护。专家警告，若缺乏合格专利律师的审核，自动化工具可能引发法律风险，需在效率与质量间寻求平衡。
items.ai_summary_en: The integration of artificial intelligence into patent drafting represents a significant transformation for the intellectual property sector. While AI-driven tools substantially enhance the efficiency and speed of research, application preparation, and prosecution, they introduce critical challenges regarding reliability. As algorithms replace qualified patent attorneys in processing information, overreliance on these automated systems risks compromising the substantive protection of inventions. Experts warn that without rigorous human oversight, the accuracy of generated content may suffer, potentially leading to legal vulnerabilities. Consequently, rights holders must carefully balance the operational benefits of automation with the necessity of maintaining high-quality, legally sound patent documents to ensure effective enforcement and avoid invalidity risks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，应审慎使用 AI 辅助工具，避免完全自动化。建议保留资深欧洲专利律师对关键案件进行人工复核，确保权利要求书的严谨性，防止因算法幻觉导致保护范围缺失或无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI for European patent applications should adopt a hybrid approach, leveraging automation for routine tasks while retaining senior European patent attorneys for strategic oversight. It is crucial to prevent complete automation of critical case elements to maintain claim rigor. Human review acts as a necessary safeguard against algorithmic hallucinations or logical gaps that could narrow protection scopes or trigger invalidity proceedings. This balanced strategy ensures that efficiency gains do not come at the expense of legal certainty, preserving the enforceability and commercial value of intellectual property assets across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI 工具大幅提升专利检索与撰写的效率和速度", "过度依赖算法处理信息可能降低专利文件的可靠性", "合格专利律师的审核是确保发明获得有效保护的关键"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI tools significantly accelerate patent search and drafting processes, offering substantial efficiency gains for firms and inventors alike.", "Excessive reliance on algorithmic processing may reduce the reliability of patent documents, undermining the quality of legal protection.", "Review by qualified patent attorneys remains essential to ensure inventions receive robust and meaningful legal safeguards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 AI 提效但须由专业律师把控核心法律逻辑", "警惕算法生成内容的准确性，防范潜在无效风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage AI for procedural efficiency but mandate professional attorney control over core legal logic and claim construction.", "Practice note: Scrutinize algorithm-generated content for accuracy to mitigate potential invalidity risks arising from technical or legal errors.", "What to monitor: Emerging case law regarding AI-assisted drafting errors and evolving EPO guidelines on the use of automated tools in prosecution."]
core_points_en: AI tools significantly accelerate patent search and drafting processes, offering substantial efficiency gains for firms and inventors alike.
Excessive reliance on algorithmic processing may reduce the reliability of patent documents, undermining the quality of legal protection.
Review by qualified patent attorneys remains essential to ensure inventions receive robust and meaningful legal safeguards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage AI for procedural efficiency but mandate professional attorney control over core legal logic and claim construction.
Practice note: Scrutinize algorithm-generated content for accuracy to mitigate potential invalidity risks arising from technical or legal errors.
What to monitor: Emerging case law regarding AI-assisted drafting errors and evolving EPO guidelines on the use of automated tools in prosecution.

items.title: The impact of EU food labelling on IP Rights
items.title_zh: 欧盟食品标签规则对知识产权权利的影响分析
items.title_en: EU Food Labelling Rules Reshape Trademark Strategy and Brand Protection
items.summary: EU food labelling rules affect trademarks and patents. Learn how terms like “milk” or “organic” impact IP protection and brand strategy
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨欧盟严格的食品标签法规（如限制植物基产品使用“牛奶”等术语）如何重塑企业的品牌与知识产权策略。文章指出，尽管欧盟知识产权局不单纯因术语受管制而驳回商标，但会结合消费者认知判定其是否构成误导。法院判例已确认修饰词无法豁免禁令，企业需在营销创意与法律合规间寻找平衡，避免因描述性词汇导致商标注册受阻或面临挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines how strict EU food labelling regulations, such as restrictions on plant-based products using terms like 'milk', reshape corporate brand and intellectual property strategies. Although the European Union Intellectual Property Office does not refuse trademark applications solely because a term is regulated, it assesses consumer perception to determine if the mark is misleading. Court precedents confirm that qualifiers do not exempt prohibited dairy terms from legal bans. Consequently, businesses must balance marketing creativity with regulatory compliance to avoid registration refusals or challenges arising from descriptive vocabulary that conflicts with established agricultural protections.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国食品及替代蛋白企业在欧布局时，须警惕通用描述词的商标风险。建议在品牌创设阶段进行合规预审，避免直接使用受保护术语，转而开发具有显著性的独创标识，以防因标签违规导致商标驳回或市场准入障碍。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering the EU food sector should anticipate heightened scrutiny regarding descriptive terms in trademark applications. Rights holders are advised to conduct preliminary compliance reviews during brand creation, avoiding direct use of protected agricultural terminology. Instead, developing distinctive, original identifiers helps mitigate risks of refusal due to misleading nature or lack of distinctiveness. Aligning brand strategy with regulatory requirements prevents market access barriers and ensures long-term IP stability against potential invalidation actions based on non-compliant labeling practices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法律严格保留特定食品术语，植物基产品使用此类词汇易被认定为误导", "商标注册审查中，监管机构将结合法规背景评估消费者对术语的实际认知", "企业命名策略需超越营销直觉，确保符合不断演变的食品标签监管框架"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU law strictly reserves specific food terminology, making plant-based product usage likely misleading.", "Trademark examiners evaluate actual consumer understanding of terms within the broader regulatory context.", "Naming strategies must transcend marketing intuition to ensure compliance with evolving food label frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业应规避在商标中使用欧盟保留的食品专用术语以防驳回", "品牌策略需从营销导向转向合规导向，提前评估标签法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using EU-reserved food-specific terms in trademarks to prevent application refusals.", "Practice note: Shift brand strategy from purely marketing-driven to compliance-oriented by assessing label risks early.", "What to monitor: Evolving legislative agreements on plant-based meat terminology and EUIPO examination guidelines."]
core_points_en: EU law strictly reserves specific food terminology, making plant-based product usage likely misleading.
Trademark examiners evaluate actual consumer understanding of terms within the broader regulatory context.
Naming strategies must transcend marketing intuition to ensure compliance with evolving food label frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using EU-reserved food-specific terms in trademarks to prevent application refusals.
Practice note: Shift brand strategy from purely marketing-driven to compliance-oriented by assessing label risks early.
What to monitor: Evolving legislative agreements on plant-based meat terminology and EUIPO examination guidelines.

items.title: The power of GI protection: The battle over names in the global drinks market
items.title_zh: 地理标志保护之力：全球酒类市场的名称之争
items.title_en: Geographical Indication Power: Navigating Name Disputes in the Global Drinks Market
items.summary: Discover what a geographical indication is, what GI protection offers, and they key arguments for and against this system, using the Prosecco case study.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨地理标志（GI）作为质量与原产地标志的法律价值及其引发的贸易摩擦。以普罗塞克（Prosecco）为例，分析欧盟与澳大利亚在自贸协定中关于该名称是“产地”还是“葡萄品种”的长期博弈。文章指出，尽管存在法律冲突，双方最终达成妥协，体现了 GI 在全球化经济中平衡传统 heritage 与现实贸易的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the legal value of geographical indications (GIs) as markers of quality and origin, highlighting their role in triggering international trade frictions. Using the Prosecco case study, it analyzes the prolonged dispute between the European Union and Australia regarding whether the name constitutes a protected place of origin or a generic grape variety. The analysis demonstrates that despite significant legal conflicts, both parties reached a pragmatic compromise within their free trade agreement. This outcome illustrates the critical function of GI frameworks in balancing traditional heritage preservation with the practical realities of globalized commerce and market access.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国酒企出口欧洲需严格排查地理标志侵权风险，避免滥用受保护名称。同时应借鉴欧盟经验，推动中国本土特色产品申请地理标志国际互认，提升品牌溢价与法律保护力度。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting alcoholic beverages to Europe must conduct rigorous due diligence to identify potential geographical indication infringements, avoiding the unauthorized use of protected terms to prevent customs seizures or litigation. Rights holders should also consider leveraging bilateral trade agreements to secure international mutual recognition for local specialty products. Establishing robust GI portfolios enhances brand premium positioning and strengthens legal protection against imitation in key export markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["地理标志赋予产品排他性优势，是欧盟捍卫传统的核心工具", "普罗塞克案凸显了地名与品种名在法律认定上的复杂冲突", "欧盟与澳大利亚通过自贸协定达成了务实的名称使用妥协方案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["GI protection grants exclusive competitive advantages, serving as a core instrument for the EU to defend traditional production methods.", "The Prosecco dispute highlights complex legal conflicts between designated place names and generic grape variety classifications.", "The EU and Australia achieved a pragmatic naming compromise through their free trade agreement negotiations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业须规避欧盟受保护地理标志，防止海关扣押或诉讼", "建议加速中国特产地理标志海外布局，利用双边协定争取互认"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters must strictly avoid infringing on EU-protected geographical indications to mitigate risks of customs detention or legal action.", "Practice note: Accelerate overseas GI registration strategies for local specialties to leverage bilateral agreements for mutual recognition.", "What to monitor: Developments in international trade negotiations affecting the scope and enforcement of geographical indication protections."]
core_points_en: GI protection grants exclusive competitive advantages, serving as a core instrument for the EU to defend traditional production methods.
The Prosecco dispute highlights complex legal conflicts between designated place names and generic grape variety classifications.
The EU and Australia achieved a pragmatic naming compromise through their free trade agreement negotiations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters must strictly avoid infringing on EU-protected geographical indications to mitigate risks of customs detention or legal action.
Practice note: Accelerate overseas GI registration strategies for local specialties to leverage bilateral agreements for mutual recognition.
What to monitor: Developments in international trade negotiations affecting the scope and enforcement of geographical indication protections.

items.title: Worldwatch “Patent Litigation” – Apr–Jun 2026 (Corporate Disputes Magazine) – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 2026 年二季度全球专利诉讼观察：聚焦 UPC 与德国
items.title_en: Global Patent Litigation Review: UPC Strategy, FRAND Trends, and AI Analytics
items.summary: The Apr–Jun 2026 issue of Corporate Disputes Magazine features the Worldwatch section on “Patent Litigation,” with Maiwald partner and UPC representative Dr.
items.ai_summary_zh: Maiwald 合伙人 Dr. Derk Vos 作为德国专家参与《公司纠纷杂志》专题，综述中、美、英、荷、德专利诉讼动态。内容涵盖侵权与无效程序互动、管辖权、FRAND 争议、统一专利法院（UPC）策略影响、AI 检索应用及诉讼成本分析，提供跨法域比较视角。
items.ai_summary_en: Maiwald partner Dr. Derk Vos contributes as the German panel expert to Corporate Disputes Magazine’s Worldwatch section on patent litigation for April–June 2026. This comparative analysis surveys key dynamics across China, the United States, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands, and Germany. The discussion addresses critical interactions between infringement and nullity actions, jurisdictional complexities, and evolving FRAND disputes. It examines strategic implications of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) alongside national court practices, particularly focusing on cost drivers and timeline pressures. Additionally, the article evaluates the growing integration of AI-enabled prior art search tools in validity attacks and offers practical guidance on forum selection, settlement strategies, and aligning litigation approaches with broader business objectives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业需重视 UPC 新机制下的诉讼风险，特别是德国“禁令缺口”带来的压力。建议结合 AI 工具优化现有技术抗辩，并针对 SEP 纠纷提前布局全球和解或诉讼策略，以匹配商业目标。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should carefully assess litigation risks arising from the UPC’s new mechanisms, particularly the pressure created by the injunction gap in Germany. Integrating AI-driven tools can enhance prior art retrieval capabilities, thereby strengthening defenses against validity challenges. Furthermore, rights holders are advised to develop coordinated global strategies for SEP disputes, balancing potential litigation with settlement opportunities. Aligning these legal tactics with commercial goals ensures robust protection while mitigating enforcement risks across diverse European and international forums.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["解析德国分立制下的禁令缺口及 UPC 战略影响", "探讨 SEP/FRAND 争议在多法域的演变与应对策略", "评估 AI 技术在现有技术检索与有效性攻击中的应用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes Germany’s bifurcation system and the resulting injunction gap, assessing strategic implications under the new UPC framework.", "Explores the evolving landscape of SEP/FRAND disputes across multiple jurisdictions and outlines effective response strategies.", "Evaluates the application of AI technologies in prior art searches and their impact on strengthening validity attack arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 AI 增强现有技术检索以应对有效性挑战", "统筹 UPC 与各国法院策略以规避禁令执行风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage AI-enhanced prior art searches to effectively counter validity challenges in high-stakes proceedings.", "Practice note: Coordinate strategies between the UPC and national courts to mitigate risks associated with injunction enforcement.", "What to monitor: Evolving SEP/FRAND jurisprudence and procedural shifts within the UPC that may impact global dispute resolution."]
core_points_en: Analyzes Germany’s bifurcation system and the resulting injunction gap, assessing strategic implications under the new UPC framework.
Explores the evolving landscape of SEP/FRAND disputes across multiple jurisdictions and outlines effective response strategies.
Evaluates the application of AI technologies in prior art searches and their impact on strengthening validity attack arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage AI-enhanced prior art searches to effectively counter validity challenges in high-stakes proceedings.
Practice note: Coordinate strategies between the UPC and national courts to mitigate risks associated with injunction enforcement.
What to monitor: Evolving SEP/FRAND jurisprudence and procedural shifts within the UPC that may impact global dispute resolution.

items.title: Anthropic, IBM, Meta, Microsoft launch patent licensing group in response to AI legal risk
items.title_zh: Anthropic 等巨头组建专利许可集团应对 AI 法律风险
items.title_en: Tech Giants Launch Shared AI License Foundation to Mitigate Generative AI Patent Risks
items.summary: Genentech, Block and Figma are also on the board of the Shared AI License Foundation, offering licences to 33,000 AI foundation model patents
items.ai_summary_zh: Anthropic、IBM、Meta 及微软等科技巨头联合 Genentech 等企业发起成立“共享 AI 许可基金会”。该组织旨在应对生成式 AI 领域的法律不确定性，计划向市场提供涵盖 33,000 项 AI 基础模型专利的许可服务。此举标志着行业试图通过集体许可机制化解潜在的专利侵权诉讼风险，重塑全球及欧洲 AI 知识产权格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Anthropic, IBM, Meta, and Microsoft have jointly established the Shared AI License Foundation, joined by board members Genentech, Block, and Figma. This initiative aims to address legal uncertainties within the generative AI sector by offering licenses for a portfolio of approximately 33,000 AI foundation model patents. By creating a collective licensing mechanism, the group seeks to reduce the threat of patent infringement litigation and provide greater clarity for market participants. This development represents a significant shift in how intellectual property rights are managed in the AI industry, potentially reshaping the global and European IP landscape by standardizing access to critical foundational technologies through pooled licensing arrangements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 企业及出口商需警惕该联盟可能形成的专利壁垒。建议提前评估产品是否落入其 3.3 万件专利范围，主动寻求交叉许可或加入相关池化机制，避免在欧洲市场面临高额诉讼风险与禁令威胁。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should assess whether their generative AI products or services fall within the scope of the 33,000 patents covered by the Shared AI License Foundation. Proactively evaluating potential exposure allows companies to consider joining the pool or negotiating cross-licenses, thereby reducing the risk of costly litigation and injunctive relief. As this collective mechanism matures, it may establish de facto standards for AI IP compliance. Rights holders and developers must monitor the foundation's licensing terms closely to ensure strategic alignment with their own IP portfolios and commercial objectives in the evolving European regulatory environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["多家科技巨头联合成立共享 AI 许可基金会以应对法律风险", "该基金将提供针对三万三千项 AI 基础模型专利的许可服务", "行业试图通过集体许可模式化解生成式 AI 领域的侵权隐患"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Major technology firms including Anthropic, IBM, Meta, and Microsoft formed the Shared AI License Foundation to address legal risks.", "The foundation offers licensing access to a substantial portfolio covering thirty-three thousand AI foundation model patents.", "Industry participants aim to mitigate generative AI infringement liabilities through this new collective licensing framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估自身技术是否落入该联盟三万三千件专利覆盖范围", "主动探索交叉许可策略以规避欧洲市场潜在诉讼风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess if proprietary AI technologies infringe upon the foundation's extensive patent portfolio to manage liability.", "Practice note: Explore cross-licensing opportunities or membership in the pool to secure freedom to operate in European markets.", "What to monitor: Track the foundation's licensing terms and expansion to anticipate shifts in AI IP enforcement strategies."]
core_points_en: Major technology firms including Anthropic, IBM, Meta, and Microsoft formed the Shared AI License Foundation to address legal risks.
The foundation offers licensing access to a substantial portfolio covering thirty-three thousand AI foundation model patents.
Industry participants aim to mitigate generative AI infringement liabilities through this new collective licensing framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess if proprietary AI technologies infringe upon the foundation's extensive patent portfolio to manage liability.
Practice note: Explore cross-licensing opportunities or membership in the pool to secure freedom to operate in European markets.
What to monitor: Track the foundation's licensing terms and expansion to anticipate shifts in AI IP enforcement strategies.

items.title: Is ChatGPT allowed under EU copyright law to generate a 'Game of Thrones' sequel?
items.title_zh: 欧盟版权法允许 ChatGPT 生成《权力的游戏》续集吗？
items.title_en: EU Copyright Risks: Can ChatGPT Legally Generate Game of Thrones Sequels?
items.summary: Sadly, there won't be a ninth season of "Game of Thrones" anytime soon. However, in the US, ChatGPT has written a sequel to one of the underlying books, and a US court is hearing the case on grounds of copyright infringement. How would a European court assess such a case?
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章对比美国 ChatGPT 生成《冰与火之歌》续集涉诉案与欧洲法律环境。指出在欧盟及德国法下，小说续集需获版权人许可，引用德国联邦法院“拉拉的女儿”案确立原则。虽慕尼黑法院曾判 OpenAI 侵权，但本案聚焦叙事性衍生作品，强调未经许可生成续集在欧构成侵权风险。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis contrasts a pending US lawsuit involving ChatGPT-generated sequels to George R. R. Martin’s works with the European legal framework. While US courts consider fair use defenses, EU copyright law strictly requires explicit authorization for creating derivative works, such as novel sequels. The discussion references established German Federal Court precedents confirming that unauthorized continuation of fictional narratives constitutes infringement. Although recent Munich Regional Court rulings found OpenAI liable for reproducing song lyrics, this case specifically addresses narrative derivatives. Consequently, generating storylines featuring protected characters and plots without permission poses significant infringement risks under EU law, highlighting substantial divergence from US judicial approaches regarding AI-generated content.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧利用 AI 进行内容创作时，须警惕衍生作品侵权风险。避免直接使用受保角色或情节生成续集，应建立合规审查机制，确保训练数据及输出内容不侵犯欧盟既有版权，防范高额赔偿诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing generative AI for creative content must rigorously assess compliance with EU copyright standards before deploying derivative works. Reliance on US-style fair use arguments is ineffective within European jurisdictions, where moral and economic rights of authors are strongly protected. Companies should implement robust internal review mechanisms to ensure that AI outputs do not infringe upon protected characters, plot structures, or expressive elements. Establishing clear licensing protocols for training data and generated content is essential to mitigate exposure to high-value litigation and injunctions across member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟版权法规定虚构作品续集须获权利人明确授权许可", "德国联邦法院既往判例确认未经授权续写构成版权侵权", "美欧法律差异显著，美国公平使用抗辩在欧盟难以直接适用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU copyright law mandates explicit rights holder authorization for creating fictional work sequels and derivative narratives.", "German Federal Court precedents confirm that unauthorized continuation of existing stories constitutes direct copyright infringement.", "US fair use defenses are generally inapplicable in Europe, creating distinct liability risks for AI-generated narrative content."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 AI 创作衍生内容前务必评估欧盟版权许可合规性", "切勿依赖美国公平使用原则作为在欧侵权抗辩的主要依据"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess EU copyright compliance before using AI to create sequels or derivative narratives based on protected IP.", "Practice note: Do not rely on US fair use principles as a primary defense against infringement claims in European courts.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding AI-generated narrative derivatives and potential legislative updates on generative AI liability."]
core_points_en: EU copyright law mandates explicit rights holder authorization for creating fictional work sequels and derivative narratives.
German Federal Court precedents confirm that unauthorized continuation of existing stories constitutes direct copyright infringement.
US fair use defenses are generally inapplicable in Europe, creating distinct liability risks for AI-generated narrative content.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess EU copyright compliance before using AI to create sequels or derivative narratives based on protected IP.
Practice note: Do not rely on US fair use principles as a primary defense against infringement claims in European courts.
What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding AI-generated narrative derivatives and potential legislative updates on generative AI liability.

items.title: Court of Appeal specifies requirements for public access to the register
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭明确公众查阅登记册的具体要求
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Public Access Requirements for Register and Case Files
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭近期澄清了程序规则第 262.1(b) 条下公众申请查阅书面诉状和证据的具体要求。法院主要裁定：向上诉庭提出的查阅申请可涵盖一审记录；申请措辞必须具体，禁止使用笼统引用加“特别是”的模糊表述。此举平衡了诉讼透明度与当事人保密利益，为平行案件的信息获取确立了明确标准。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has clarified specific requirements for public access requests under Rule 262.1(b) of the Rules of Procedure regarding written pleadings and evidence. The Court ruled that applications filed with the Court of Appeal may validly extend to records held by the Court of First Instance, ensuring comprehensive access across judicial levels. Furthermore, the Court emphasized that request wording must be precise; generic references combined with vague qualifiers such as 'in particular' are insufficient. This decision balances the UPCA’s transparency objectives with the need to protect party confidentiality under Article 45. By establishing these clear standards, the ruling provides legal certainty for litigants seeking information from parallel proceedings while safeguarding sensitive commercial interests against overly broad disclosure demands.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利诉讼时，可利用此规则精准调取竞争对手在平行案件中的诉状与证据，优化抗辩策略。同时需警惕自身案件信息被对手通过具体化申请获取，应提前评估保密必要性并准备反驳理由。
items.ai_insight_en: This clarification significantly impacts litigation strategy for parties involved in parallel UPC proceedings. Rights holders can now more effectively leverage Rule 262.1(b) to obtain specific written pleadings and evidence from competitor cases, provided they draft precise, reasoned requests rather than relying on broad generalizations. Conversely, litigants must proactively assess the confidentiality risks associated with their own filings. It is advisable to prepare robust arguments for confidential treatment early in the process, citing Article 45 grounds, to prevent opponents from accessing sensitive strategic information through narrowly tailored public access applications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭确认查阅申请效力可延伸至一审法院的案件记录", "申请必须具体指明文件，禁止使用笼统概括性措辞", "明确权衡公共利益与当事人保密需求的司法审查标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applications for register access filed with the Court of Appeal may validly encompass records from the Court of First Instance.", "Requests must precisely identify specific documents, prohibiting generic descriptions or vague qualifiers like 'in particular'.", "Judicial review requires balancing public interest in transparency against party confidentiality needs under Article 45 UPCA."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新规精准获取平行案件证据以辅助国内企业抗辩", "防范对手利用具体化申请获取我方敏感诉讼文书"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties can strategically access parallel case files to strengthen defense positions by submitting precise, well-reasoned access requests.", "Practice note: Draft access requests with specific document identifiers and avoid vague language to ensure compliance with Court of Appeal standards.", "What to monitor: Monitor opponent access requests closely and prepare timely confidentiality objections based on Article 45 UPCA criteria."]
core_points_en: Applications for register access filed with the Court of Appeal may validly encompass records from the Court of First Instance.
Requests must precisely identify specific documents, prohibiting generic descriptions or vague qualifiers like 'in particular'.
Judicial review requires balancing public interest in transparency against party confidentiality needs under Article 45 UPCA.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties can strategically access parallel case files to strengthen defense positions by submitting precise, well-reasoned access requests.
Practice note: Draft access requests with specific document identifiers and avoid vague language to ensure compliance with Court of Appeal standards.
What to monitor: Monitor opponent access requests closely and prepare timely confidentiality objections based on Article 45 UPCA criteria.

items.title: Illustration très concrète de la mauvaise foi lors du dépôt de marque
items.title_zh: 商标注册中恶意行为的具體实例：TOGG 商标被撤销
items.title_en: Paris Court Annuls TOGG Trademark Registration Due to Applicant Bad Faith
items.summary: Par Maître Jessica SANDOWSKI, Avocate (CA de Paris, Pôle 5 – Ch. 2, 19 décembre 2025, n°24/10600) Le 19 décembre 2025, la cour d’appel de Paris, infirmant une décision de l’INPI, a annulé la marque française TOGG, déposée par un particulier, en raison de la mauvaise foi de celui-ci lors du dépôt. Elle a, pour […] L’article Illustration très concrète de la mauvaise foi lors du dépôt de marque est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎上诉法院于 2025 年 12 月 19 日作出判决，推翻法国国家工业产权局（INPI）此前决定，裁定撤销由个人注册的
items.ai_summary_en: On December 19, 2025, the Paris Court of Appeal issued a significant ruling in case number 24/10600, overturning a prior decision by the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI). The court ordered the cancellation of the French trademark TOGG, which had been registered by an individual applicant. The judicial body determined that the registration was filed in bad faith, constituting a clear violation of trademark law principles. This decision highlights the judiciary's active role in scrutinizing the intent behind trademark applications. By reversing the INPI's initial acceptance, the court reinforced the legal standards required for valid trademark acquisition. The ruling serves as a concrete illustration of how bad faith is assessed and penalized during the registration process, ensuring that the trademark system is not abused by applicants acting with improper motives or deceptive intentions against established market players.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the critical importance of demonstrating good faith during trademark filings in France. Rights holders should be aware that national courts actively review the subjective intent of applicants, particularly when marks coincide with well-known foreign brands. For brand owners, this precedent strengthens the ability to challenge squatters or opportunistic registrants who lack genuine commercial intent. Legal teams must gather robust evidence of the applicant's knowledge and lack of legitimate interest to succeed in bad faith claims. Monitoring new filings for potential conflicts with existing global portfolios remains essential, as early opposition based on bad faith can prevent costly enforcement actions later in the lifecycle of the mark.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎上诉法院于 2025 年 12 月 19 日作出判决，推翻法国国家工业产权局（IN…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Paris Court of Appeal overturned an INPI decision on December 19, 2025, cancelling the TOGG trademark.", "The court found the individual applicant acted in bad faith at the time of filing the trademark application.", "This ruling reinforces strict judicial scrutiny of applicant intent and prevents abuse of the registration system."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthens grounds for challenging opportunistic registrations by proving applicant bad faith and lack of genuine intent.", "Practice note: Gather comprehensive evidence of the applicant's knowledge of prior rights and absence of legitimate commercial purpose.", "What to monitor: New trademark filings that mirror established international brands, especially those filed by individuals or shell entities."]
core_points_en: The Paris Court of Appeal overturned an INPI decision on December 19, 2025, cancelling the TOGG trademark.
The court found the individual applicant acted in bad faith at the time of filing the trademark application.
This ruling reinforces strict judicial scrutiny of applicant intent and prevents abuse of the registration system.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthens grounds for challenging opportunistic registrations by proving applicant bad faith and lack of genuine intent.
Practice note: Gather comprehensive evidence of the applicant's knowledge of prior rights and absence of legitimate commercial purpose.
What to monitor: New trademark filings that mirror established international brands, especially those filed by individuals or shell entities.

items.title: Lundbeck and Biogaran fight in Milan and Paris over drug for alcohol dependence
items.title_zh: 灵北与生物加兰在米兰巴黎争夺酒精依赖药物专利
items.title_en: Lundbeck and Biogaran Litigate Nalmefene Patents in Milan and Paris
items.summary: Nalmefene is an opioid antagonist used in the EU to reduce alcohol dependence. The active ingredient works by antagonising those receptors in the brain responsible for producing the body’s feel-good chemical, dopamine. This prevents the alcohol-induced release of this chemical, resulting in reduced desire to continue drinking. The drug is sold by Lundbeck under the […] The post Lundbeck and Biogaran fight in Milan and Paris over drug for alcohol dependence appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 丹麦灵北公司与法国生物加兰公司就酒精依赖药物纳美芬的活性成分专利，分别在意大利米兰和法国巴黎发起诉讼对抗。该药物通过拮抗大脑受体减少饮酒欲望。案件聚焦于原研药企与仿制药企在欧洲主要法域的专利有效性及侵权争议，反映了医药领域激烈的市场博弈。
items.ai_summary_en: Danish pharmaceutical company Lundbeck and French generic manufacturer Biogaran are engaged in parallel patent litigation concerning nalmefene, an opioid antagonist used to treat alcohol dependence. The disputes, filed in Milan and Paris respectively, center on the validity and infringement of patents protecting the drug's active ingredient. Nalmefene functions by blocking brain receptors that release dopamine, thereby reducing the urge to drink. These proceedings highlight the intense market competition between originator and generic companies within key European jurisdictions. The cases serve as a significant example of how rights holders enforce intellectual property protections across different national legal systems, reflecting the strategic importance of securing market exclusivity for pharmaceutical products in the EU.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海需警惕欧洲多国平行诉讼风险，提前开展 FTO 分析。建议关注法意等国司法实践差异，制定针对性应诉或无效策略，避免因地域裁判不一导致市场准入受阻。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering the European pharmaceutical sector must anticipate the risks associated with multi-jurisdictional parallel litigation. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before launch is essential to mitigate potential infringement claims. Companies should also prepare for divergent judicial practices in key markets like France and Italy, developing tailored defense or invalidation strategies. Establishing rapid response mechanisms and maintaining accessible legal resources can help navigate these complex disputes effectively, ensuring smoother market access and minimizing the impact of inconsistent regional rulings on commercial operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["灵北与生物加兰针对纳美芬药物在法意两国展开专利诉讼", "争议核心在于阿片受体拮抗剂活性成分的专利保护范围", "跨国平行诉讼凸显欧洲医药专利维权的地域性策略特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Lundbeck and Biogaran are conducting parallel patent litigation regarding nalmefene in both France and Italy.", "The core dispute involves the scope of patent protection for the opioid antagonist active ingredient used in alcohol dependence treatment.", "These cross-border proceedings illustrate the regional strategic characteristics of pharmaceutical patent enforcement in Europe."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口欧洲前应针对重点国别进行深度专利自由实施分析", "需建立应对多国平行诉讼的快速响应机制与法律资源库"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct deep freedom-to-operate analyses for target countries prior to European market entry to identify infringement risks.", "Practice note: Develop tailored litigation strategies accounting for specific judicial differences in France, Italy, and other key jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Establish rapid response mechanisms and legal resource pools to manage multi-country parallel litigation efficiently."]
core_points_en: Lundbeck and Biogaran are conducting parallel patent litigation regarding nalmefene in both France and Italy.
The core dispute involves the scope of patent protection for the opioid antagonist active ingredient used in alcohol dependence treatment.
These cross-border proceedings illustrate the regional strategic characteristics of pharmaceutical patent enforcement in Europe.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct deep freedom-to-operate analyses for target countries prior to European market entry to identify infringement risks.
Practice note: Develop tailored litigation strategies accounting for specific judicial differences in France, Italy, and other key jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Establish rapid response mechanisms and legal resource pools to manage multi-country parallel litigation efficiently.

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal: can you amend a patent in preliminary injunction proceedings?
items.title_zh: UPC周报：初步禁令程序中能否修改专利？
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Patent Amendment Feasibility in Preliminary Injunction Proceedings
items.summary: 2026 Week 14
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在初步禁令程序中的关键程序问题：专利权人是否可在此阶段修改专利。文章指出，尽管UPC规则允许修改，但在紧急的禁令程序中，法院需平衡诉讼效率与被告防御权。若修改导致争议焦点变更或拖延审理，法院可能拒绝。该分析为当事人在UPC诉讼中制定策略提供了重要参考，强调了程序时机的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a key procedural issue addressed by the UPC Court of Appeal regarding whether patent holders may amend their patents during preliminary injunction proceedings. Although the Unified Patent Court Rules permit such amendments, courts must carefully balance the need for litigation efficiency against the defendant's right to a fair defense. The article highlights that if proposed amendments alter the subject matter of the dispute or cause significant delays, the court is likely to reject them to maintain procedural speed. This guidance offers critical strategic insights for parties involved in UPC litigation, emphasizing the importance of timing and procedural conduct when seeking or opposing amendments in urgent interim relief contexts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC初步禁令时，应预判对方可能通过修改专利规避无效抗辩。建议提前准备多套不侵权及无效证据组合，并密切关注程序节点，及时提出程序异议以保护自身权益，避免因对方突袭修改而陷入被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing UPC preliminary injunctions should anticipate potential strategies where opponents seek patent amendments to circumvent invalidity defenses. It is advisable to prepare diverse combinations of non-infringement and invalidity evidence in advance. Stakeholders must closely monitor procedural milestones and promptly raise objections to protect their interests. By proactively addressing these risks, rights holders and defendants can avoid being placed in a passive position due to unexpected amendments, ensuring robust protection of their legal rights throughout the expedited proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭探讨初步禁令程序中专利修改的可行性与限制条件", "法院需在快速裁决需求与被告充分辩护权利之间寻求程序平衡", "不当修改可能导致程序拖延，从而被法院驳回以维持诉讼效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal evaluates the feasibility and specific limitations of amending patents within preliminary injunction proceedings.", "Courts must strike a procedural balance between the urgent need for rapid rulings and the defendant's right to adequate legal defense.", "Improper amendments causing procedural delays may be rejected by the court to preserve overall litigation efficiency and fairness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["预判对手利用修改专利规避无效抗辩的策略风险", "提前准备多元证据并及时提出程序异议以保护权益"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate opponent strategies using patent amendments to bypass invalidity challenges in urgent proceedings.", "Practice note: Prepare multiple evidence sets for non-infringement and invalidity, and file timely procedural objections.", "What to monitor: Critical procedural nodes and any attempts at surprise amendments that could shift the dispute's focus."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal evaluates the feasibility and specific limitations of amending patents within preliminary injunction proceedings.
Courts must strike a procedural balance between the urgent need for rapid rulings and the defendant's right to adequate legal defense.
Improper amendments causing procedural delays may be rejected by the court to preserve overall litigation efficiency and fairness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate opponent strategies using patent amendments to bypass invalidity challenges in urgent proceedings.
Practice note: Prepare multiple evidence sets for non-infringement and invalidity, and file timely procedural objections.
What to monitor: Critical procedural nodes and any attempts at surprise amendments that could shift the dispute's focus.

items.title: 'Stop playing games': INTA bemoans low sign-ups from London firms
items.title_zh: INTA 谴责伦敦律所参会注册率低并质疑其伦理
items.title_en: INTA CEO Criticizes Low Registration Rates Among London Firms and Questions Ethics of Non-Registered Attendance
items.summary: INTA’s CEO says London-based firms have registered fewer delegates compared to past meetings in San Diego and Atlanta, and questions the 'ethics' of trying to participate without registering
items.ai_summary_zh: 国际商标协会（INTA）首席执行官指出，与伦敦以往在圣地亚哥和亚特兰大举办的会议相比，此次伦敦本地律所的代表注册人数显著偏低。CEO 公开批评部分从业者试图在不注册的情况下参与活动，质疑此类行为的职业伦理，呼吁停止投机取巧，维护行业会议的正规性与公平性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Chief Executive Officer of the International Trademark Association (INTA) has highlighted a significant decline in delegate registrations from London-based law firms for the recent annual meeting. This trend contrasts sharply with previous gatherings held in San Diego and Atlanta, where local participation was notably higher. The CEO publicly criticized industry practitioners who attempt to attend sessions and network without completing official registration procedures. Such behavior is described as ethically questionable and detrimental to the integrity of professional associations. The leadership emphasized that bypassing registration fees undermines the financial sustainability and fairness of these events. INTA urges all market participants to adhere strictly to established rules, ensuring that attendance is formalized through proper channels to maintain the professional standards expected within the global intellectual property community.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧参与国际 IP 活动时需严守合规底线，避免因小失大损害声誉。建议提前规划会议预算，通过正规渠道注册参会，展现专业形象，同时关注国际组织对职业伦理的监管趋严态势。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies and legal teams operating in Europe, maintaining strict compliance with conference registration protocols is essential to preserving professional reputation. Attempting to circumvent registration fees or participate informally may trigger scrutiny regarding ethical standards and corporate governance. Organizations should prioritize early budget planning and utilize official registration channels to demonstrate commitment to industry norms. As international bodies increasingly monitor participation ethics, adhering to these guidelines mitigates reputational risks. Ensuring transparent engagement supports long-term credibility within the global IP ecosystem and aligns with evolving expectations for professional conduct among rights holders and service providers.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INTA 高管批评伦敦律所参会注册率远低于往届水平", "官方质疑未注册即参会的行为违背职业道德规范", "呼吁业界遵守规则，杜绝规避会议注册费用的现象"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INTA executives noted significantly lower registration rates among London-based firms compared to previous meetings in US cities like San Diego and Atlanta.", "Official statements questioned the professional ethics of practitioners attempting to participate in association activities without completing mandatory registration.", "The organization called for strict adherence to rules to prevent fee avoidance and uphold the fairness and regularity of international IP conferences."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["参与国际 IP 会议务必正规注册，规避伦理风险", "关注国际组织对职业操守的审查，维护企业合规形象"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all team members attending international IP events complete formal registration to avoid ethical breaches and protect corporate reputation.", "Practice note: Allocate sufficient budget for conference fees well in advance and enforce internal policies requiring official sign-ups for all external industry engagements.", "What to monitor: Watch for tightening oversight by international IP organizations regarding professional ethics and non-compliant attendance practices at major industry forums."]
core_points_en: INTA executives noted significantly lower registration rates among London-based firms compared to previous meetings in US cities like San Diego and Atlanta.
Official statements questioned the professional ethics of practitioners attempting to participate in association activities without completing mandatory registration.
The organization called for strict adherence to rules to prevent fee avoidance and uphold the fairness and regularity of international IP conferences.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all team members attending international IP events complete formal registration to avoid ethical breaches and protect corporate reputation.
Practice note: Allocate sufficient budget for conference fees well in advance and enforce internal policies requiring official sign-ups for all external industry engagements.
What to monitor: Watch for tightening oversight by international IP organizations regarding professional ethics and non-compliant attendance practices at major industry forums.

items.title: [Book Review] Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide
items.title_zh: 《斯通论欧盟外观设计法：实务指南》第三版书评
items.title_en: Review: Stone on EU Design Law Third Edition Offers Key Updates
items.summary: This is a review of the third edition of Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide (Oxford University Press, 2025), authored by David Stone.  More than twenty years after the European Community Design system came into force, one might have expected the dust to have settled on EU design law. The reality is slightly different, to say the least. The EU’s significant 2024
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评述大卫·斯通所著《欧盟外观设计法：实务指南》第三版。尽管欧盟外观设计制度已实施二十余年，但法律实践仍动态变化。该书深入解析 2024 年以来的重大法律发展，为从业者提供应对复杂确权与侵权纠纷的最新指引，反映欧盟外观设计法并未尘埃落定。
items.ai_summary_en: This article reviews the third edition of David Stone’s 'Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide', published by Oxford University Press in 2025. Although the European Community Design system has been operational for over twenty years, legal practice remains dynamic rather than settled. The book provides an in-depth analysis of significant legal developments since 2024, offering practitioners essential guidance for navigating complex registration and infringement disputes. It highlights that EU design law continues to evolve, requiring up-to-date resources to address current challenges in rights acquisition and enforcement effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局外观设计需关注 2024 年新动向，避免因法规迭代导致权利瑕疵。建议利用该指南优化申请策略，提升应对 EUIPO 审查及跨境侵权诉讼的合规能力与胜诉概率。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor the regulatory shifts introduced in 2024 to prevent potential defects in their design rights due to iterative legislative changes. Utilizing authoritative guides like Stone’s can help optimize application strategies, ensuring better compliance with EUIPO examination standards. This approach enhances the ability to handle cross-border infringement litigation effectively, thereby improving overall compliance capabilities and increasing the probability of successful outcomes in legal disputes.
