core_points_en: Cup&Cino holds three key European patents covering milk foam production methods and modular components.
The litigation focused on alleged infringement of specific manufacturing techniques and structural unit designs.
German courts ruled in favor of the patent owner, ordering the cessation of infringing activities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters of small appliances must prioritize pre-market FTO clearance for fluid handling tech.
Practice note: Retaining specialized local counsel is vital for navigating German patent litigation effectively.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in interpreting method versus apparatus claims for electromechanical devices.

items.title: Commercial disputes in the life sciences sector: the complexity of assessing loss
items.title_zh: 生命科学商业纠纷：损失评估的复杂性
items.title_en: Life Sciences Commercial Disputes: Navigating the Complexity of Assessing Loss
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. With thanks to Shreya Gupta and Mohammed Khalil of Oxera, and Jemima Stra...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文源自 Bristows 年度生物技评论，探讨生命科学领域商业纠纷中损失评估的难点。文章指出，该行业特有的研发合作、许可及供应链协议易引发争议，且因产品周期长、监管复杂，导致损害赔偿分析极具挑战性，需结合具体交易结构进行精细化考量。
items.ai_summary_en: This article, part of Bristows' Biotech Review of the Year, examines the difficulties in assessing loss within life sciences commercial disputes. It highlights that the sector's unique characteristics, including long product cycles and complex regulatory environments, make damages analysis particularly challenging. The text identifies key areas where disputes frequently arise, such as intellectual property-rich transactions like R&D collaborations and licensing deals, as well as intricate supply chain agreements covering manufacturing, distribution, and logistics. Due to these complexities, calculating compensation requires a nuanced approach tailored to specific transaction structures. The analysis underscores the need for precise contractual frameworks to address potential breaches effectively, ensuring that parties can navigate the intricate landscape of liability and loss quantification inherent in the highly regulated and innovation-driven life sciences industry.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧开展授权或供应链合作时，应在合同中明确损失计算机制。建议提前预设赔偿模型，以应对潜在的商业纠纷与复杂的损害评估挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: In cross-border life sciences transactions, parties should proactively define clear mechanisms for calculating losses within their contracts. Pre-agreed damage models can significantly reduce uncertainty during dispute resolution. Given the industry's complexity, standard valuation methods may prove insufficient. Rights holders must consider how specific sector attributes, such as regulatory hurdles and long market entry times, impact breach consequences. Establishing robust analytical frameworks early helps manage risks associated with complex damage assessments. This approach ensures that potential commercial disputes are handled with greater predictability, protecting commercial interests against the backdrop of intricate operational and legal challenges inherent in the global life sciences market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["生命科学纠纷多源于研发合作与许可等富含 IP 的交易环节", "供应链协议如制造、分销及物流合同亦是争议高发地带", "行业独特性使得损害赔偿的计算与分析过程异常复杂困难"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Disputes frequently originate from IP-intensive transactions, including research collaborations, co-development agreements, and licensing deals.", "Supply chain contracts, encompassing manufacturing, distribution, and logistics services, represent another significant area prone to commercial conflict.", "The sector's distinct features, such as lengthy development timelines and strict regulations, complicate the calculation and analysis of damages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在跨境交易中预先约定损害赔偿计算逻辑以降低不确定性", "关注生命科学特殊属性对违约损失认定带来的复杂影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pre-define damage calculation logic in cross-border contracts to minimize uncertainty during potential disputes.", "Practice note: Account for the unique impact of life sciences attributes on breach determination and loss assessment processes.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial or arbitral approaches to quantifying loss in complex, regulated industry contexts."]
core_points_en: Disputes frequently originate from IP-intensive transactions, including research collaborations, co-development agreements, and licensing deals.
Supply chain contracts, encompassing manufacturing, distribution, and logistics services, represent another significant area prone to commercial conflict.
The sector's distinct features, such as lengthy development timelines and strict regulations, complicate the calculation and analysis of damages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pre-define damage calculation logic in cross-border contracts to minimize uncertainty during potential disputes.
Practice note: Account for the unique impact of life sciences attributes on breach determination and loss assessment processes.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial or arbitral approaches to quantifying loss in complex, regulated industry contexts.

items.title: 2026: A year of clarity for the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation?
items.title_zh: 2026 年：SPC 制造豁免条例能否迎来清晰元年？
items.title_en: 2026: A Year of Clarity for the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation?
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Between 2024 and 2025, there were multiple cases across Europe addressing...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出 2024 至 2025 年间，欧洲多国法院对补充保护证书（SPC）制造豁免条例的适用范围出现解释分歧。该条例旨在允许欧盟仿制药企在特定条件下为出口或期满入市进行生产。预计 2026 至 2027 年，随着新判决出炉及欧盟委员会咨询推进，相关法律不确定性有望得到澄清，英国脱欧后亦保留了类似豁免机制。
items.ai_summary_en: Between 2024 and 2025, courts across Europe issued divergent interpretations regarding the scope of the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) Manufacturing Waiver Regulation. This regulation permits generic and biosimilar manufacturers to produce within the EU for export to third countries or for stockpiling prior to SPC expiry, aiming to level the competitive playing field. However, inconsistent judicial approaches have created legal uncertainty. Looking ahead to 2026 and 2027, further case law and an anticipated European Commission consultation are expected to clarify the regulation’s boundaries. Meanwhile, the United Kingdom has retained a similar waiver mechanism post-Brexit, allowing manufacturing for export outside both the UK and EU. Stakeholders must navigate these evolving standards to ensure compliance while leveraging manufacturing opportunities during the protection period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企利用欧洲产能进行全球供应链布局时，需密切关注各法域对豁免条件的差异化解读。建议提前评估目标市场的合规风险，利用政策澄清期优化通知流程与标签管理，避免因侵权诉讼导致出口受阻或市场准入延迟。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing European manufacturing capacity for global supply chains should closely monitor differing national interpretations of waiver conditions. It is prudent to assess compliance risks in target jurisdictions early, particularly regarding notification timelines and labeling obligations. Leveraging the current period of policy clarification allows rights holders and manufacturers to optimize internal due diligence processes. Proactive management of these requirements helps mitigate the risk of infringement litigation, which could otherwise disrupt export operations or delay market entry upon SPC expiry.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲各国法院对 SPC 制造豁免条例的适用范围存在显著司法分歧", "欧盟委员会咨询与新判决预期将在 2026 年后明确条例具体边界", "英国脱欧后通过国内立法保留并调整了针对第三国出口的豁免规则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European courts have issued divergent interpretations regarding the specific scope and application of the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation.", "Further judgments and a European Commission consultation in 2026 are expected to clarify the regulatory boundaries and reduce uncertainty.", "The UK retained and adjusted its own manufacturing waiver rules post-Brexit, permitting exports to countries outside both the UK and EU."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需针对不同成员国司法实践制定差异化的合规生产与出口策略", "利用政策澄清窗口期完善内部尽职调查，规避潜在侵权法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Develop differentiated compliance strategies for production and export based on varying judicial practices across EU member states.", "Practice note: Use the current policy clarification window to strengthen internal supply chain due diligence and avoid potential infringement liabilities.", "What to monitor: Track upcoming European Commission consultations and new court decisions defining the precise limits of the manufacturing waiver."]
core_points_en: European courts have issued divergent interpretations regarding the specific scope and application of the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation.
Further judgments and a European Commission consultation in 2026 are expected to clarify the regulatory boundaries and reduce uncertainty.
The UK retained and adjusted its own manufacturing waiver rules post-Brexit, permitting exports to countries outside both the UK and EU.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Develop differentiated compliance strategies for production and export based on varying judicial practices across EU member states.
Practice note: Use the current policy clarification window to strengthen internal supply chain due diligence and avoid potential infringement liabilities.
What to monitor: Track upcoming European Commission consultations and new court decisions defining the precise limits of the manufacturing waiver.

items.title: Has biotech litigation entered a new era?
items.title_zh: 生物制药诉讼是否迈入新时代？
items.title_en: Biotech Litigation Enters New Era: Biosimilar Competition and UPC Dynamics in Europe
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. 2025 was an important year for biotech patent litigation, in Europe and f...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025 年欧洲生物制药诉讼因仿制药竞争加剧而呈现新态势，案件混合了国内、跨境及统一专利法院（UPC）纠纷。文章以阿柏西普（Eylea）为例，指出在补充保护证书到期后，诉讼焦点转向制剂专利。英国法院重点审理了基于等同原则的侵权认定，评估变体配方是否以实质相同方式实现相同结果。
items.ai_summary_en: 2025 marked a pivotal shift in European biotech patent litigation, characterized by increased volume and a mix of national, cross-border, and Unified Patent Court disputes. This evolution is driven by intensified biosimilar competition, as manufacturers from lower-cost jurisdictions enter the market. Using aflibercept (Eylea) as a primary case study, the analysis highlights how litigation focus shifted to formulation patents after the supplementary protection certificate expired. Key proceedings in the English courts centered on whether biosimilar variants infringed remaining patents under the doctrine of equivalents. The assessment required determining if these alternative formulations achieved substantially the same result in substantially the same way as the original product, signaling a complex new landscape for market participants navigating post-SPC exclusivity periods.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国生物药企出海需警惕欧洲制剂专利壁垒，尤其关注等同原则在英国的适用标准。建议提前开展 FTO 分析，针对变体配方制定差异化规避策略，并密切关注 UPC 判例对跨境诉讼的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering the European biosimilar sector must carefully navigate formulation patent barriers, particularly in jurisdictions like the UK where the doctrine of equivalents plays a significant role in infringement determinations. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in development is essential to identify risks associated with variant formulations. Companies should develop differentiated design-around strategies to mitigate potential equivalence claims. Furthermore, given the increasing importance of the Unified Patent Court, stakeholders must monitor its evolving case law to effectively manage cross-border litigation risks and align their enforcement or defense strategies across multiple European territories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["生物制药诉讼量增且性质转变，UPC 成为重要战场", "阿柏西普制剂专利成焦点，等同原则适用受关注", "低成本国家入局加剧生物类似药市场竞争格局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Biotech litigation volume and nature are transforming, with the Unified Patent Court emerging as a critical forum for resolving complex cross-border disputes.", "Formulation patents have become central to litigation strategies following SPC expirations, particularly regarding biosimilar variants that differ from originator products.", "The application of the doctrine of equivalents in UK courts is crucial for assessing infringement when biosimilar formulations are not identical to the patented reference product."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视制剂专利等同侵权风险，优化配方规避设计", "跟踪 UPC 最新判例，统筹欧洲跨境诉讼应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess infringement risks for biosimilar variants under the doctrine of equivalents, especially in UK proceedings involving formulation patents.", "Practice note: Optimize formulation design to create clear distinctions from originator products, reducing exposure to equivalence-based infringement claims.", "What to monitor: Track Unified Patent Court decisions to understand their impact on cross-border litigation strategies and harmonization of patent enforcement."]
core_points_en: Biotech litigation volume and nature are transforming, with the Unified Patent Court emerging as a critical forum for resolving complex cross-border disputes.
Formulation patents have become central to litigation strategies following SPC expirations, particularly regarding biosimilar variants that differ from originator products.
The application of the doctrine of equivalents in UK courts is crucial for assessing infringement when biosimilar formulations are not identical to the patented reference product.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess infringement risks for biosimilar variants under the doctrine of equivalents, especially in UK proceedings involving formulation patents.
Practice note: Optimize formulation design to create clear distinctions from originator products, reducing exposure to equivalence-based infringement claims.
What to monitor: Track Unified Patent Court decisions to understand their impact on cross-border litigation strategies and harmonization of patent enforcement.

items.title: From factory to bedside: decentralised medicines manufacturing in the UK
items.title_zh: 从工厂到床边：英国推行去中心化药品制造新规
items.title_en: UK MHRA Introduces Decentralised Medicines Manufacturing Framework to Enhance Patient Access
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. The stated aim of the UK’s regulator for medicines, the MHRA, is to make ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国 MHRA 为推动医疗创新，于 2025 年 7 月生效新立法，重构药品制造与交付模式。该框架通过修订药品及临床试验法规，打破传统集中式生产限制，允许去中心化制造，旨在解决复杂个性化疗法的生产障碍，提升患者获取创新药物的可及性。
items.ai_summary_en: To promote healthcare innovation, the UK’s medicines regulator, the MHRA, implemented new legislation in July 2025 that fundamentally restructures how medicines are manufactured and delivered. This new decentralised manufacturing framework amends existing laws governing medicines and clinical trials to overcome barriers inherent in traditional centralised production models. By shifting manufacturing closer to patients, the regulations aim to facilitate access to complex, personalised therapies that were previously hindered by rigid regulatory requirements. The amendments, which entered into force on 23 July 2025 following a six-month implementation period, provide necessary regulatory flexibility. This shift allows for the safe deployment of innovative treatments, ensuring that the UK remains a leading jurisdiction for healthcare innovations while significantly improving patient access to advanced therapeutic options.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应关注英国监管灵活性带来的市场机遇，利用去中心化模式加速个性化疗法落地。同时需评估新法规对知识产权布局及数据合规的影响，调整在英生产与合作策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the strategic opportunities presented by the UK's enhanced regulatory flexibility for decentralised manufacturing. This framework offers a viable pathway to accelerate the market entry and commercialisation of personalised therapies across Europe. Rights holders must carefully assess how this shift impacts intellectual property strategies, particularly regarding manufacturing rights and data compliance obligations. Adapting production and collaboration strategies to leverage these new provisions can provide a competitive advantage. Companies operating in the UK sector need to review their operational models to ensure alignment with the new legal standards while maximising the benefits of proximity-based manufacturing for innovative treatments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国新立法确立去中心化药品制造框架，贴近患者需求", "修订法规旨在消除阻碍个性化疗法获取的监管壁垒", "新规则于 2025 年 7 月 23 日正式生效，实施期六个月"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New UK legislation establishes a decentralised medicines manufacturing framework designed to align production processes closely with specific patient needs.", "Regulatory amendments eliminate existing barriers within the current framework, thereby facilitating improved access to complex and personalised medical therapies.", "The updated rules officially took effect on 23 July 2025, following a designated six-month implementation period for industry adaptation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用英国新政优化个性化疗法在欧洲的市场准入路径", "重新评估在英制造环节的知识产权风险与合规策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the new UK regulatory framework to optimise market access pathways for personalised therapies within the European landscape.", "Practice note: Reassess intellectual property risks and compliance strategies specifically related to decentralised manufacturing operations in the UK.", "What to monitor: Ongoing guidance from the MHRA regarding the practical application of decentralised manufacturing licenses and data protection requirements."]
core_points_en: New UK legislation establishes a decentralised medicines manufacturing framework designed to align production processes closely with specific patient needs.
Regulatory amendments eliminate existing barriers within the current framework, thereby facilitating improved access to complex and personalised medical therapies.
The updated rules officially took effect on 23 July 2025, following a designated six-month implementation period for industry adaptation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the new UK regulatory framework to optimise market access pathways for personalised therapies within the European landscape.
Practice note: Reassess intellectual property risks and compliance strategies specifically related to decentralised manufacturing operations in the UK.
What to monitor: Ongoing guidance from the MHRA regarding the practical application of decentralised manufacturing licenses and data protection requirements.

items.title: Trial by name, trial by nature: how lack of data protection harmonisation is hindering European clinical trials
items.title_zh: 名实之困：数据保护缺乏协调阻碍欧洲临床试验
items.title_en: Fragmented Data Protection Rules Undermine European Clinical Trials and Innovation
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Europe’s ability to leverage its world-leading academic institutions, hea...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，尽管欧洲拥有顶尖学术与医疗资源，但欧洲经济区内各国对数据保护法律的解释与执行存在显著差异。这种缺乏协调的现状导致跨国临床试验运营成本增加、流程复杂化，致使 2013 至 2023 年间欧洲全球临床试验份额减半，严重阻碍了患者获取新疗法及医疗创新进程。
items.ai_summary_en: Despite Europe’s strong academic and healthcare infrastructure, significant divergences in how data protection laws are interpreted and enforced across the European Economic Area create substantial operational hurdles. This regulatory fragmentation increases costs and complicates processes for multinational clinical trials. Consequently, the EEA’s share of global clinical trials halved between 2013 and 2023, while worldwide numbers rose significantly. This decline severely hinders patient access to new therapies and slows medical innovation. The lack of harmonization forces sponsors to navigate varying national compliance requirements, ethics committee standards, and data governance practices. These inconsistencies not only burden research sponsors but also reduce the competitiveness of the European R&D ecosystem, ultimately limiting early treatment opportunities for patients within the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧开展生物医药研发的中国企业需警惕各国数据合规差异。建议建立国别化数据治理策略，提前评估伦理委员会要求，避免因合规碎片化导致项目延误或成本激增，确保临床数据跨境流转合法高效。
items.ai_insight_en: Sponsors conducting biomedical research in Europe must carefully manage the risks associated with fragmented data compliance landscapes. Establishing localized data governance strategies is essential to address specific national requirements and ethical committee expectations. Proactive assessment of these differences helps prevent project delays and unexpected cost increases caused by regulatory misalignment. Ensuring the lawful and efficient cross-border transfer of clinical data remains critical for maintaining trial momentum. Stakeholders should prioritize agility in their compliance frameworks to adapt to varying national interpretations of data protection laws, thereby safeguarding the continuity and efficiency of multinational clinical operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟内部数据保护执法不一削弱临床研究竞争力", "监管非协调性致欧洲临床试验参与人数大幅下滑", "多国运营需应对不同合规流程增加企业负担"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Inconsistent enforcement of data protection rules across the EEA weakens the competitiveness of European clinical research initiatives.", "Regulatory fragmentation rather than overall regulatory volume drives a sharp decline in clinical trial participation across Europe.", "Operating in multiple jurisdictions requires navigating distinct compliance processes, significantly increasing administrative burdens for sponsors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需针对成员国差异制定本地化数据合规方案", "关注监管协调动态以优化跨国临床试验布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Develop tailored local data compliance strategies to address specific Member State variations effectively.", "Practice note: Monitor regulatory coordination dynamics to optimize the strategic layout of multinational clinical trials.", "What to monitor: Track evolving national interpretations of data protection rules impacting cross-border clinical data flows."]
core_points_en: Inconsistent enforcement of data protection rules across the EEA weakens the competitiveness of European clinical research initiatives.
Regulatory fragmentation rather than overall regulatory volume drives a sharp decline in clinical trial participation across Europe.
Operating in multiple jurisdictions requires navigating distinct compliance processes, significantly increasing administrative burdens for sponsors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Develop tailored local data compliance strategies to address specific Member State variations effectively.
Practice note: Monitor regulatory coordination dynamics to optimize the strategic layout of multinational clinical trials.
What to monitor: Track evolving national interpretations of data protection rules impacting cross-border clinical data flows.

items.title: Proposal for a European Biotech Act: will the Commission’s proposal unlock the potential of the health biotech industry in the EU?
items.title_zh: 欧委会拟推生物技术法案以解锁健康生物产业潜力
items.title_en: EU Commission Proposes Biotech Act to Strengthen Health Sector Competitiveness and Autonomy
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. December of 2025 was an exciting month for the life sciences sector in te...
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对欧盟在临床开发、监管及制造领域的结构性障碍，欧委会发布《欧洲生物技术法案》提案。该法案聚焦健康生物技术全生命周期，旨在通过设立“战略项目”框架、优化资金获取及简化流程，解决初创企业外流问题，提升欧盟相对于美中地区的竞争力与自主性。
items.ai_summary_en: Addressing structural barriers in clinical development, regulation, and manufacturing, the European Commission has published its Proposal for a European Biotech Act. This legislation focuses on the entire lifecycle of health biotechnology products and services. It aims to resolve issues causing start-ups to relocate outside the EU by establishing a strategic project framework, optimizing access to funding, and simplifying administrative processes. The initiative seeks to enhance the EU’s competitiveness and strategic autonomy relative to the US and China. By targeting health biotech applications relevant to human, animal, and plant health, the proposal intends to unlock the sector's potential while maintaining high safety standards for the population and environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国生物医药企业应关注欧盟新规带来的市场准入机遇与合规挑战。建议提前布局符合“战略项目”标准的合作，利用简化的审批路径加速产品进入欧盟市场，同时警惕潜在的供应链本地化要求对出口策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving regulatory landscape regarding the proposed European Biotech Act. The introduction of strategic project frameworks offers potential pathways for accelerated market access and improved funding opportunities. However, companies must also assess compliance requirements, particularly concerning supply chain localization mandates that could impact export strategies and operational models within the EU. Proactive engagement with these new standards is essential for navigating future regulatory expectations and securing competitive advantages in the European health biotech market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧委会提案旨在消除阻碍欧盟生物技术发展的结构性壁垒", "法案覆盖研发至上市全流程，特别关注健康生物技术领域", "引入战略项目认定机制，强化工业生物制造能力与价值链"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Commission proposal aims to eliminate structural barriers hindering the development of the EU biotechnology sector.", "The Act covers the full lifecycle from research to market placement, with specific focus on health biotechnology applications.", "It introduces a strategic project designation mechanism to strengthen industrial biomanufacturing capabilities and value chains."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注战略项目认定标准，争取纳入欧盟支持体系加速准入", "评估新规对供应链本地化要求，调整在欧投资与合规策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor strategic project criteria to leverage EU support systems for accelerated market entry.", "Practice note: Evaluate new regulations for supply chain localization impacts to adjust investment and compliance strategies.", "What to monitor: Developments in simplified approval pathways and their interaction with existing pharmaceutical frameworks."]
core_points_en: The Commission proposal aims to eliminate structural barriers hindering the development of the EU biotechnology sector.
The Act covers the full lifecycle from research to market placement, with specific focus on health biotechnology applications.
It introduces a strategic project designation mechanism to strengthen industrial biomanufacturing capabilities and value chains.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor strategic project criteria to leverage EU support systems for accelerated market entry.
Practice note: Evaluate new regulations for supply chain localization impacts to adjust investment and compliance strategies.
What to monitor: Developments in simplified approval pathways and their interaction with existing pharmaceutical frameworks.

items.title: Competition authorities in Europe target ‘killer acquisitions’ – what biotech companies need to know
items.title_zh: 欧洲竞管机构靶向“扼杀式收购”：生物科技企业须知
items.title_en: EU Competition Authorities Target Killer Acquisitions in Life Sciences Sector
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Competition authorities are increasingly targeting so-called 'killer acqu...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟竞争当局正加强对生命科学领域“扼杀式收购”的审查，即大企业并购初创公司以消除潜在竞争。尽管此类交易常因目标营收低而低于申报门槛，但监管机构现结合合并控制与滥用支配地位规则进行干预。违规交易可能被叫停、撤销或强制剥离，企业还面临高达全球营收 10% 的罚款风险。
items.ai_summary_en: European competition authorities are intensifying scrutiny of 'killer acquisitions' within the life sciences sector, where dominant firms acquire innovative start-ups to neutralize future competitive threats. Although many such transactions historically fell below merger control thresholds due to the target's low or non-existent turnover, regulators now actively apply both merger control and abuse of dominance rules to intervene. This shift reflects a broader strategy to preserve innovation-driven competition. Violations can lead to severe consequences, including the blocking of deals, unwinding of completed transactions, or mandatory divestment of assets. Furthermore, findings of abuse of a dominant position may result in substantial financial penalties, reaching up to 10% of a company’s global annual turnover, significantly impacting business operations and strategic planning.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国生物科技企业赴欧并购或寻求退出时，需警惕即便无营收也可能触发欧盟反垄断审查。建议提前评估交易对创新竞争的潜在影响，避免被视为消除未来威胁的“扼杀式收购”，并预留应对漫长调查及高额罚款的合规预算。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in M&A activities within the European life sciences sector must recognize that low-revenue targets do not guarantee immunity from antitrust review. Even pre-revenue acquisitions can trigger extensive investigations if they are perceived as eliminating potential innovation competition. Companies should proactively assess the competitive impact of their transactions, particularly regarding future market dynamics. It is prudent to allocate sufficient resources for compliance assessments and potential regulatory defense strategies. Early engagement with competition law experts can help mitigate risks associated with prolonged inquiries and significant financial penalties, ensuring that innovation-driven growth strategies remain compliant with evolving EU enforcement priorities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["竞争当局利用合并控制及滥用支配地位规则审查低营收目标并购案", "违规“扼杀式收购”面临被阻断、撤销交易或资产剥离的严厉后果", "认定滥用市场支配地位可能导致企业承担最高全球营收 10% 的罚款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Regulators utilize merger control and abuse of dominance frameworks to review acquisitions involving targets with minimal or no revenue generation.", "Identified killer acquisitions face strict enforcement measures, including transaction prohibition, legal unwinding, or compulsory asset divestiture orders.", "Abuse of dominance determinations expose companies to severe financial liabilities, including fines amounting to ten percent of global turnover."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["低营收初创企业并购亦可能触发欧盟反垄断审查，需提前预判风险", "交易若被认定阻碍创新竞争，将面临叫停、剥离及巨额罚款的多重打击"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Low-revenue start-up acquisitions may still trigger rigorous EU antitrust scrutiny if they threaten future innovation competition.", "Practice note: Conduct early competitive impact assessments to identify and mitigate risks of being classified as a prohibited killer acquisition.", "What to monitor: Evolving enforcement trends regarding abuse of dominance claims and potential fines up to 10% of global turnover."]
core_points_en: Regulators utilize merger control and abuse of dominance frameworks to review acquisitions involving targets with minimal or no revenue generation.
Identified killer acquisitions face strict enforcement measures, including transaction prohibition, legal unwinding, or compulsory asset divestiture orders.
Abuse of dominance determinations expose companies to severe financial liabilities, including fines amounting to ten percent of global turnover.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Low-revenue start-up acquisitions may still trigger rigorous EU antitrust scrutiny if they threaten future innovation competition.
Practice note: Conduct early competitive impact assessments to identify and mitigate risks of being classified as a prohibited killer acquisition.
What to monitor: Evolving enforcement trends regarding abuse of dominance claims and potential fines up to 10% of global turnover.

items.title: ECIJA hires 12-lawyer TM team from Hoyng Rokh Monegier
items.title_zh: ECIJA 从 Hoyng Rokh Monegier 吸纳十二人商标团队
items.title_en: ECIJA Recruits Twelve-Lawyer Trademark Team from Rival Firm Hoyng Rokh Monegier
items.summary: Arrival of Laura Alonso, alongside a team of 11, will bring ‘significant value’ to ECIJA clients, says CEO
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲知识产权律所 ECIJA 宣布从竞争对手 Hoyng Rokh Monegier 引进由 Laura Alonso 领衔的十二人商标律师团队。ECIJA 首席执行官表示，此次招聘将显著提升其为客户提供商标组合管理及战略服务的能力，体现了欧洲商标法律服务市场的整合趋势及对该领域专业人才需求的持续增长。
items.ai_summary_en: European intellectual property firm ECIJA has announced the recruitment of a twelve-lawyer trademark team led by Laura Alonso from competitor Hoyng Rokh Monegier. The move is designed to significantly enhance the firm's capacity to deliver comprehensive trademark portfolio management and strategic advisory services to its clients. This high-profile hiring reflects the ongoing consolidation within the European trademark legal services market, driven by sustained demand for specialized expertise. By integrating this experienced group, ECIJA aims to strengthen its service depth and competitive positioning, illustrating how major firms are actively reshaping their capabilities to meet evolving client needs in a dynamic professional landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应关注律所团队变动带来的服务连续性影响。建议利用市场竞争契机，重新评估并优化欧洲商标代理合作伙伴，确保品牌保护策略获得更专业支持。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess how significant team movements at key law firms may impact service continuity and case management stability. Rights holders are advised to leverage current market consolidation trends to re-evaluate their European trademark agency partnerships. Optimizing partner selection ensures that brand protection strategies receive robust, specialized support. Monitoring these structural shifts allows companies to align their legal resources with firms demonstrating enhanced capacity and strategic focus in trademark prosecution and enforcement matters across Europe.

items.title: One counterclaim, one fee – but not for a later-filed copy
items.title_zh: 一项反诉一份费：后续单独提交副本需另缴费
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim: Separate Court Fees Required for Later-Filed Counterclaims by Individual Defendants
items.summary: Irdeto B.V. v SZ DJI Technology Co., Ltd. and others, Local Division Mannheim, 27 February 2026, CFI_344/2025, CFI_735/2025, PR-UPC-CFI-0000639/2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院曼海姆地方法庭在 Irdeto 诉大疆案中裁定，若多名被告共同提交一份无效反诉，仅需缴纳一份费用；但若被告因送达时间不同而后续单独提交内容相同的反诉，则视为独立程序行为，必须另行缴费。该裁决澄清了程序规则 370.7 的适用边界。
items.ai_summary_en: In Irdeto B.V. v SZ DJI Technology Co., Ltd. and others, the UPC Local Division in Mannheim clarified the application of Rule 370.7 RoP regarding court fees for revocation counterclaims involving multiple defendants served at different times. The Court held that while several defendants jointly filing a single counterclaim incur only one fee, a defendant filing a separate counterclaim later constitutes an independent procedural act. Consequently, even if the content is identical to earlier filings, the later submission requires a distinct court fee. This ruling emphasizes that Rule 370.7 applies to joint actions but does not exempt subsequent individual filings from fee obligations, ensuring strict adherence to procedural timelines and payment requirements under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，若涉及多国被告或送达时间不一，切勿假设已付费用可覆盖后续动作。务必监控每位被告的程序节点，及时为单独提交的反诉预算并缴纳费用，避免因欠费导致丧失无效抗辩权利。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights critical cost implications for multi-defendant litigation strategies before the UPC. Parties must recognize that fee exemptions for joint filings do not extend to subsequent individual submissions, regardless of content similarity. Legal teams should proactively budget for potential separate fees when service of process occurs at staggered intervals. Ignoring these distinct procedural milestones can lead to unexpected costs or severe procedural sanctions, including default judgments. Effective coordination among co-defendants is essential to determine whether joint or separate filings are strategically and financially preferable.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["多被告联合提交反诉仅需缴纳一份固定及价值基础费用", "后续单独提交的反诉即便内容相同，亦被视为独立案件需缴费", "未按期补缴费用可能导致法院依据规则 355 作出缺席判决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Multiple defendants jointly submitting one counterclaim pay only one fixed and value-based court fee.", "A later-filed separate counterclaim by an individual defendant is treated as an independent case requiring a new fee.", "Failure to pay the required fee on time may result in a default decision under Rule 355 of the Rules of Procedure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["送达时间差异会导致反诉费用增加，需提前规划预算", "单独提交反诉未缴费将面临缺席判决风险，须严格监控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Staggered service dates trigger separate fee liabilities for individual counterclaims, impacting litigation budgets.", "Practice note: Verify service timelines early to distinguish between joint filings (single fee) and subsequent individual actions (separate fees).", "What to monitor: Strict compliance with fee payment deadlines to avoid default decisions under Rule 355 RoP for late filers."]
core_points_en: Multiple defendants jointly submitting one counterclaim pay only one fixed and value-based court fee.
A later-filed separate counterclaim by an individual defendant is treated as an independent case requiring a new fee.
Failure to pay the required fee on time may result in a default decision under Rule 355 of the Rules of Procedure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Staggered service dates trigger separate fee liabilities for individual counterclaims, impacting litigation budgets.
Practice note: Verify service timelines early to distinguish between joint filings (single fee) and subsequent individual actions (separate fees).
What to monitor: Strict compliance with fee payment deadlines to avoid default decisions under Rule 355 RoP for late filers.

items.title: Patents in the age of digital twins
items.title_zh: 数字孪生时代的专利挑战与应对策略
items.title_en: Patenting Digital Twins in Healthcare: Navigating Technical Contribution and Sufficiency Hurdles
items.summary: For those new to the term, a “digital twin” is a virtual replica of a physical object. The potential use in healthcare is enormous. For...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨医疗与工程领域数字孪生技术的专利保护难题。指出在英国及欧洲，此类发明需证明“技术贡献”以避开抽象算法排除条款；同时面临充分性与可信度质疑，若说明书未详述模型初始化或数据更新机制易被无效；此外还涉及复杂的侵权认定问题，建议申请人寻求专业指导完善权利要求。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines patent protection challenges for digital twin technologies in healthcare and engineering sectors. In the UK and Europe, such inventions must demonstrate a technical contribution to avoid exclusion as abstract algorithms. Applicants face significant risks regarding sufficiency and plausibility; if specifications fail to detail model initialization or data update mechanisms, patents are vulnerable to invalidity attacks. Furthermore, complex infringement determination issues arise. The article advises applicants to seek professional guidance to refine claims, ensuring they are anchored in specific technical applications rather than pure software descriptions, thereby securing robust monopoly rights in this rapidly growing market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局数字孪生专利时，应避免纯算法描述，强调具体医疗或工业应用场景的技术效果。撰写时需确保实施例详尽，以防因公开不充分被无效，并提前评估侵权取证难度。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, patent strategies for digital twins should prioritize specific medical or industrial application scenarios over pure algorithmic descriptions. Drafting must ensure detailed embodiments to prevent invalidation due to insufficient disclosure. Additionally, applicants should proactively assess the difficulty of enforcing infringement claims. By binding applications to specific hardware or clinical operations, rights holders can mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter and build a stronger defense against potential validity challenges during opposition or litigation proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["数字孪生专利需体现校准设备等具体技术效果以避免被视为抽象方法", "说明书必须详尽披露模型参数与数据更新逻辑，否则易受充分性质疑", "竞争对手可能基于当前技术水平，挑战专利预测功能的科学可信度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Digital twin patents must demonstrate concrete technical effects, such as device calibration, to avoid rejection as abstract mathematical methods.", "Specifications must thoroughly disclose model parameters and data update logic to withstand challenges regarding sufficiency of disclosure.", "Competitors may challenge the scientific plausibility of predictive functions based on current technical standards and available evidence."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专利申请须绑定具体硬件或临床操作，规避抽象客体驳回风险", "完善技术披露细节，构建抵御充分性与可信度挑战的坚实防线"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anchor patent applications to specific hardware or clinical operations to mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter.", "Practice note: Enhance technical disclosure details to construct a robust defense against sufficiency and plausibility challenges.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the technical contribution requirements for computer-implemented medical simulations."]
core_points_en: Digital twin patents must demonstrate concrete technical effects, such as device calibration, to avoid rejection as abstract mathematical methods.
Specifications must thoroughly disclose model parameters and data update logic to withstand challenges regarding sufficiency of disclosure.
Competitors may challenge the scientific plausibility of predictive functions based on current technical standards and available evidence.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anchor patent applications to specific hardware or clinical operations to mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter.
Practice note: Enhance technical disclosure details to construct a robust defense against sufficiency and plausibility challenges.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the technical contribution requirements for computer-implemented medical simulations.

items.title: What happens if someone opposes your trade mark? | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 他人异议您的商标时该如何应对？
items.title_en: Navigating UK Trade Mark Oppositions: Understanding TM7a Notices and Strategic Responses
items.summary: As a UK trade mark attorney, one of the most common and often most stressful situations my clients face is when their application for a trade mark is opposed, or they receive a Notice of Threatened Opposition (a Form TM7a).
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章解析英国商标申请遭遇异议或收到潜在异议通知（TM7a 表格）的情形。指出 TM7a 仅是第三方意图评估权利的预警，非正式异议启动。该机制可延长公告期，为双方提供商业解决窗口。强调收到通知无需恐慌，专业介入常可避免争端升级。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the implications of receiving a Notice of Threatened Opposition (Form TM7a) during the UK trade mark application process. It clarifies that a TM7a serves as an early warning signal indicating a third party’s intent to assess rights, rather than initiating formal legal proceedings. The notice extends the publication period, providing a critical window for commercial resolution. Rights holders are advised not to panic upon receipt, as professional intervention often facilitates quick, cost-effective settlements without escalating into full disputes. The article outlines the distinction between UKIPO refusals and third-party oppositions, emphasizing strategic communication and risk assessment to protect brand interests efficiently during this procedural stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英申请商标时，若遇 TM7a 通知切勿忽视或盲目应诉。应视其为谈判信号，及时聘请当地律师评估风险，利用延长期与对方沟通，争取以共存协议等低成本方式化解潜在冲突，避免程序空转。
items.ai_insight_en: For market participants navigating the UK Intellectual Property Office landscape, a TM7a should be interpreted as a negotiation signal rather than an immediate threat. Ignoring the notice or reacting defensively may lead to procedural inefficiencies. Instead, applicants should engage local counsel promptly to assess infringement risks and explore amicable solutions. Utilizing the extended publication period allows for direct communication with the opposing party, potentially resulting in coexistence agreements or other low-cost settlements. This proactive approach minimizes legal exposure and avoids the resource drain associated with prolonged opposition proceedings, ensuring brand protection strategies remain commercially viable and efficient.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["TM7a 是潜在异议通知，旨在延长审查期而非启动正式法律对抗", "收到通知不代表必然发生争议，多为权利人意在争取评估时间", "通过专业建议，多数此类情况可在早期以商业方式快速解决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A Form TM7a is a Notice of Threatened Opposition designed to extend the publication period, not to initiate formal legal confrontation.", "Receiving a TM7a does not guarantee a dispute will occur; it typically indicates the rights holder requires time to evaluate their position.", "Professional guidance enables rapid, commercial resolutions in many cases, preventing unnecessary escalation into costly legal battles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["视 TM7a 为预警信号，及时寻求专业评估而非被动等待", "利用公告延长期主动沟通，争取以商业和解替代昂贵诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat TM7a notices as opportunities for early dialogue and risk assessment rather than immediate litigation triggers.", "Practice note: Engage qualified UK trade mark attorneys immediately to evaluate merits and negotiate potential coexistence agreements.", "What to monitor: Track the extended publication timeline closely to ensure timely responses and capitalize on settlement windows."]
core_points_en: A Form TM7a is a Notice of Threatened Opposition designed to extend the publication period, not to initiate formal legal confrontation.
Receiving a TM7a does not guarantee a dispute will occur; it typically indicates the rights holder requires time to evaluate their position.
Professional guidance enables rapid, commercial resolutions in many cases, preventing unnecessary escalation into costly legal battles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat TM7a notices as opportunities for early dialogue and risk assessment rather than immediate litigation triggers.
Practice note: Engage qualified UK trade mark attorneys immediately to evaluate merits and negotiate potential coexistence agreements.
What to monitor: Track the extended publication timeline closely to ensure timely responses and capitalize on settlement windows.

items.title: Salts v Pelican: UK Patent Infringement Appeal Fails but Novelty and Costs Arguments Succeed
items.title_zh: Salts 诉 Pelican：英国专利侵权上诉败诉但新颖性抗辩成功
items.title_en: Salts v Pelican: UK Appeal Court Upholds Non-Infringement but Restores Patent Novelty
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院驳回 Salts 关于造口袋专利侵权的上诉，维持一审不侵权认定，包括正常解释与等同原则下均不成立。但法院撤销了一审关于权利要求 8 缺乏新颖性的裁定，并支持了案件移交前的费用主张。Arnold 法官重新界定了发明概念，强调焊接部位需特定形状以均匀分布废物。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal dismissed Salts Healthcare’s appeal regarding patent infringement, affirming that Pelican Healthcare’s ostomy bag did not infringe under normal construction or the doctrine of equivalents. However, the court successfully overturned the High Court’s ruling that claim 8 lacked novelty over prior art. Additionally, the appeal succeeded regarding pre-transfer costs incurred before the case moved from the IPEC to the Patents Court. Arnold LJ clarified that the weld portion must extend downwardly towards the bottom, rejecting Salts’ broader interpretation. The decision reaffirms strict claim construction while validating the patent’s validity against specific prior art challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口英国时，需警惕专利等同原则的灵活适用风险。即便产品结构微调，若功能效果实质相同仍可能侵权。建议企业在研发阶段进行严格的 FTO 分析，并关注英国法院对发明概念的重新界定逻辑，优化规避设计策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the critical importance of precise claim construction in UK patent disputes, particularly concerning geometric features like weld orientations. While the doctrine of equivalents remains a potential avenue for rights holders, courts require clear alignment with the inventive concept. Conversely, the restoration of novelty demonstrates that prior art challenges can succeed even when infringement claims fail. Market participants should ensure robust freedom-to-operate analyses that account for both literal infringement risks and potential validity attacks during litigation strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院维持不侵权判决，认定被诉产品未落入专利保护范围", "成功推翻一审新颖性无效裁定，确认权利要求 8 具备新颖性", "明确等同原则适用标准，重新界定造口袋专利的核心发明概念"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Court of Appeal upheld non-infringement findings for both normal interpretation and equivalence arguments.", "High Court decision on lack of novelty for claim 8 was overturned, restoring the patent's validity.", "Appeal succeeded regarding IPEC pre-transfer costs, impacting litigation expense allocations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业应重视英国专利等同原则，避免功能性实质相似导致的侵权", "利用新颖性抗辩空间，在诉讼中积极挑战对方专利的有效性基础"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must align product designs strictly with claim language to avoid non-infringement rulings.", "Practice note: Defend against novelty attacks by distinguishing technical contributions from prior art disclosures effectively.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in defining the 'inventive concept' for applying the doctrine of equivalents in mechanical patents."]
core_points_en: Court of Appeal upheld non-infringement findings for both normal interpretation and equivalence arguments.
High Court decision on lack of novelty for claim 8 was overturned, restoring the patent's validity.
Appeal succeeded regarding IPEC pre-transfer costs, impacting litigation expense allocations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must align product designs strictly with claim language to avoid non-infringement rulings.
Practice note: Defend against novelty attacks by distinguishing technical contributions from prior art disclosures effectively.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in defining the 'inventive concept' for applying the doctrine of equivalents in mechanical patents.

items.title_zh: 2026 变局：西班牙 AI 创新者须知中国专利令第 84 号修订
items.title_en: 2026 Policy Shift: How China’s Revised Patent Decree 84 Impacts Spanish AI Innovators
items.summary: Based on the official China National Intellectual Property Administration (hereinafter referred to as “CNIPA”) data from 2019 to 2024, while major European industrial powers like Germany, France, Italy, and Sweden have seen their application volumes stagnate or decline over the last five years, Spain’s volume rose steadily from 421(2019) to 494 (2024) applications[1] with a ... Read more La entrada The 2026 shift: what spanish AI innovators need to know about China’s revised Patent Decree 84 se
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章基于 CNIPA 数据指出，德法意瑞等国在华专利申请停滞或下滑，而西班牙逆势增长。针对中国修订后的专利令第 84 号，分析其将于 2026 年带来的政策转向，旨在指导西班牙 AI 领域创新者调整在华知识产权布局策略以应对新规。
items.ai_summary_en: Official data from the China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) covering 2019 to 2024 reveals a distinct divergence in European patent filing trends. While application volumes from major industrial powers such as Germany, France, Italy, and Sweden have stagnated or declined, Spain has demonstrated consistent growth, rising from 421 applications in 2019 to 494 in 2024. This analysis focuses on the strategic implications of China’s revised Patent Decree 84, which is scheduled to take effect in 2026. The article provides essential guidance for Spanish innovators in the artificial intelligence sector, helping them navigate the evolving regulatory landscape. It outlines necessary adjustments to intellectual property strategies in China, ensuring that rights holders can effectively leverage new provisions to strengthen their market position and protect technological advancements amidst changing policy dynamics.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注西班牙等新兴欧洲力量在华专利动态，预判其利用新规加强保护的意图。建议提前评估竞争态势，优化自身在欧及双向技术转移中的专利防御与许可策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the increasing patent activity from emerging European forces like Spain, anticipating their intent to utilize new regulations for stronger protection. Rights holders are advised to proactively assess competitive landscapes and optimize patent defense and licensing strategies regarding technology transfers between Europe and China. Dynamic monitoring of bilateral policy interactions is crucial to mitigate potential infringement risks and manage licensing complexities effectively in this evolving legal framework.

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal imposes clear limit on long arm jurisdiction
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院明确限制长臂管辖权范围
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Imposes Clear Limits on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Cross-Border Cases
items.summary: 2026 Week 12
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院近期作出重要裁决，对“长臂管辖权”施加明确限制。该判决澄清了法院在跨境专利侵权案件中的管辖边界，防止管辖权过度扩张。此举旨在平衡权利人保护与被告正当程序权利，为未来涉及多国被告的专利诉讼确立更清晰的程序规则，减少法律不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued a significant ruling that strictly limits the scope of long-arm jurisdiction. This decision clarifies the boundaries of judicial authority in cross-border patent infringement disputes, preventing excessive expansion of jurisdictional reach. By establishing clearer procedural rules for cases involving multiple defendants across different jurisdictions, the court aims to balance effective protection of intellectual property rights with the due process rights of defendants. This judgment reduces legal uncertainty and provides essential guidance on how jurisdiction is determined when alleged infringements occur in multiple member states, ensuring that courts do not overstep their mandate in complex international litigation scenarios.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼时，可利用此判例抗辩不当管辖。建议密切关注 UPC 程序规则变化，优化欧洲专利布局与应诉策略，避免因地域管辖扩大而陷入被动局面。
items.ai_insight_en: This precedent offers a robust basis for challenging improper jurisdictional claims in UPC proceedings, particularly for entities facing multi-state infringement allegations. Rights holders and defendants should carefully assess whether the court has proper authority before engaging in full litigation, potentially reducing defense costs and strategic exposure. Market participants must stay alert to evolving procedural interpretations regarding venue and joinder of defendants. Optimizing European patent enforcement and defense strategies now requires a nuanced understanding of these jurisdictional limits to avoid being drawn into unfavorable forums or facing fragmented enforcement actions across the unitary patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院裁定限制长臂管辖权的适用范围", "明确跨境专利侵权案件中管辖权的判定标准", "防止管辖权滥用以保障被告正当程序权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal restricts the application of long-arm jurisdiction to prevent overreach in multi-jurisdictional disputes.", "Clear standards are established for determining jurisdiction in cross-border patent infringement cases involving multiple parties.", "The ruling safeguards defendant due process rights by preventing abusive or expansive assertions of judicial authority."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新判例抗辩不当管辖，降低诉讼风险", "关注 UPC 规则动态，优化欧洲专利应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use this precedent to challenge improper jurisdiction and mitigate litigation risks in multi-country disputes.", "Practice note: Monitor UPC procedural developments to refine European patent enforcement and defense strategies.", "What to monitor: Shifts in jurisdictional interpretation affecting venue selection and defendant joinder in future cases."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal restricts the application of long-arm jurisdiction to prevent overreach in multi-jurisdictional disputes.
Clear standards are established for determining jurisdiction in cross-border patent infringement cases involving multiple parties.
The ruling safeguards defendant due process rights by preventing abusive or expansive assertions of judicial authority.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use this precedent to challenge improper jurisdiction and mitigate litigation risks in multi-country disputes.
Practice note: Monitor UPC procedural developments to refine European patent enforcement and defense strategies.
What to monitor: Shifts in jurisdictional interpretation affecting venue selection and defendant joinder in future cases.

items.title: Inclusion, wellbeing and employee confidence gaps: lessons from the 2026 IP Salary Survey
items.title_zh: 包容性与薪酬透明：2026 年 IP 薪资调查启示
items.title_en: Inclusion, Wellbeing and Employee Confidence Gaps: Lessons from the 2026 IP Salary Survey
items.summary: Many organisations continue to face shortages of experienced talent and growing scrutiny over compensation transparency across different roles and regions
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于 2026 年知识产权薪资调查，指出欧洲及全球组织正面临资深 IP 人才短缺的持续挑战。同时，不同角色与区域间的薪酬透明度受到日益严格的审查。文章探讨了包容性、员工福祉及信心差距对行业的影响，强调企业需优化人才策略以应对市场 scrutiny。
items.ai_summary_en: Based on the 2026 Intellectual Property Salary Survey, this analysis highlights that organizations across Europe and globally continue to confront significant shortages of experienced IP professionals. Concurrently, there is intensifying external scrutiny regarding compensation transparency across various roles and geographic regions. The article examines how factors such as workplace inclusion, employee wellbeing, and prevailing confidence gaps are reshaping industry dynamics. It emphasizes that market participants must strategically optimize their talent management approaches to address these mounting pressures effectively. By addressing these structural challenges, companies can better navigate the complex landscape of modern intellectual property workforce management while maintaining operational stability and competitive advantage in attracting top-tier legal and technical expertise.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应关注当地薪酬合规趋势，提升薪资透明度以吸引人才。建议优化多元包容机制，增强团队稳定性，避免因人才流失影响知识产权布局与维权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations operating within European jurisdictions should closely monitor evolving local compensation compliance trends to ensure alignment with regulatory expectations. Enhancing salary transparency serves as a strategic mechanism to attract high-caliber talent while mitigating reputational risks associated with opaque pay practices. Furthermore, establishing robust diversity and inclusion frameworks contributes significantly to team stability. This approach helps prevent disruptive personnel turnover that could otherwise compromise the efficiency of intellectual property portfolio management and enforcement activities. Maintaining a stable, motivated workforce is essential for safeguarding overseas IP assets and ensuring consistent strategic execution in competitive global markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲 IP 行业持续面临资深专业人才短缺的严峻挑战", "各区域与岗位间的薪酬透明度正遭受更严格的外部审查", "员工福祉与包容性文化成为填补信心差距的关键因素"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The European IP sector continues to face severe shortages of experienced professional talent, creating persistent recruitment challenges for organizations seeking specialized expe…", "Compensation transparency across different regions and job roles is undergoing stricter external examination, requiring greater openness from employers regarding pay structures.", "Employee wellbeing and inclusive corporate culture have emerged as critical factors in bridging confidence gaps and retaining valuable intellectual property staff."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提升薪酬透明度以符合欧洲监管趋势并吸引高端人才", "构建包容性文化以稳定团队，保障海外 IP 资产安全"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhance salary transparency to align with European regulatory trends and effectively attract senior intellectual property talent.", "Practice note: Build inclusive organizational cultures to stabilize teams, thereby securing overseas intellectual property assets and operational continuity.", "What to monitor: Evolving compensation disclosure requirements and employee wellbeing metrics impacting retention rates in key European markets."]
core_points_en: The European IP sector continues to face severe shortages of experienced professional talent, creating persistent recruitment challenges for organizations seeking specialized expe…
Compensation transparency across different regions and job roles is undergoing stricter external examination, requiring greater openness from employers regarding pay structures.

items.title: Does copyright subsist in AI-generated content?
items.title_zh: AI 生成内容是否享有版权？英国法律现状与风险分析
items.title_en: Copyright in AI-Generated Content: UK Legal Status and Risk Analysis
items.summary: Brands & advertising
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国现行法律下 AI 生成内容的版权归属问题。目前英国通过《版权、外观设计和专利法》为无人类作者的计算机生成作品提供 50 年保护，但“原创性”需体现人类智力创造的要求存在法律张力。尽管法院可能倾向于保护，但立法不明及全球多数国家不保护的现状，给企业依赖 AI 进行创意生产带来合规风险。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the copyright status of AI-generated content under current UK law. The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 currently provides fifty years of protection for computer-generated works lacking a human author. However, significant legal tension exists because the requirement for originality traditionally demands human intellectual creation. While courts may lean toward protecting such outputs, legislative ambiguities persist. Furthermore, since most global jurisdictions do not offer copyright protection for AI-generated works, businesses face compliance risks when relying on AI for creative production. Companies must carefully evaluate their dependence on AI tools, considering both the potential for limited domestic protection and the lack of international recognition for purely machine-generated assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企利用 AI 生成商标或广告素材时，需注意英国与其他欧洲国家保护差异。建议保留人类创作痕迹以争取标准版权保护，并避免过度依赖纯 AI 生成内容以防权利落空。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI to generate trademarks or advertising materials in the UK should note the divergence from other European jurisdictions where protection is often denied. To secure longer standard copyright terms, rights holders should maintain evidence of human creative intervention to satisfy originality requirements. Over-reliance on purely AI-generated content poses a risk of rights failure, particularly in cross-border operations. Strategic IP management requires balancing efficiency gains from generative AI with robust documentation of human input to ensure enforceable rights across key markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国目前对无人类作者的计算机生成作品提供五十年版权保护", "原创性测试要求人类作者身份，与计算机生成作品定义存在冲突", "未来政府可能限制此类保护，企业需谨慎评估 AI 创意依赖度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK law currently grants fifty-year copyright protection to computer-generated works that have no identified human author.", "The originality test requiring human authorship creates legal conflict with the statutory definition of computer-generated works.", "Future government restrictions on protection are possible, requiring businesses to cautiously assess reliance on AI for creativity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["保留人类创作介入证据，以满足原创性测试争取更长保护期", "关注英国立法动态，评估纯 AI 生成内容在跨国业务中的权利风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Preserve evidence of human creative involvement to meet originality tests and secure longer standard copyright protection periods.", "Practice note: Avoid excessive reliance on purely AI-generated content to prevent rights gaps, especially given varying international protections.", "What to monitor: Track UK legislative developments regarding computer-generated works and assess cross-border IP risks for AI-driven campaigns."]
core_points_en: UK law currently grants fifty-year copyright protection to computer-generated works that have no identified human author.
The originality test requiring human authorship creates legal conflict with the statutory definition of computer-generated works.
Future government restrictions on protection are possible, requiring businesses to cautiously assess reliance on AI for creativity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Preserve evidence of human creative involvement to meet originality tests and secure longer standard copyright protection periods.
Practice note: Avoid excessive reliance on purely AI-generated content to prevent rights gaps, especially given varying international protections.
What to monitor: Track UK legislative developments regarding computer-generated works and assess cross-border IP risks for AI-driven campaigns.

items.title: A reminder of the use or lose it approach to trade marks: easyHire
items.title_zh: easyHire 案警示：商标注册后不使用将导致维权失败
items.title_en: easyHire Case: UK Court Rejects Infringement Claim Due to Lack of Genuine Use
items.summary: Brands & advertising
items.ai_summary_zh: easyGroup 因无法证明其 easyHire 商标在英国过去五年内的真实使用，败诉于针对 EASIHIRE 的侵权指控。被告虽未反诉撤销商标，但成功援引英国商标法第 11A 条“非使用抗辩”。法院认定若易集团能证明使用则构成侵权，但因缺乏证据导致主张失效，而商标注册本身依然有效。
items.ai_summary_en: easyGroup failed in its trade mark infringement action against Jaybank Leisure for using the sign EASIHIRE, primarily because it could not demonstrate genuine use of its registered easyHire mark in the UK during the relevant five-year period. Although the court indicated that the defendant’s use would likely constitute infringement had genuine use been proven, the claim was dismissed under Section 11A of the Trade Marks Act 1994. This provision allows a defendant to raise a non-use defence, barring infringement claims if the asserted registration has not been used in the five years preceding the action. Notably, the defendant did not file a counterclaim for revocation on grounds of non-use. Consequently, while the infringement claim failed, the easyHire registration remains valid and enforceable for future actions where use can be substantiated.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧持有商标需建立定期使用证据归档机制，避免“僵尸商标”导致维权落空。面对侵权诉讼时，应评估对方使用状况，灵活选择直接抗辩或反诉撤销策略，以最大化法律利益。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the critical importance of maintaining robust evidence trails for trade mark use, particularly for registrations older than five years. Rights holders risk losing enforcement capabilities if they cannot substantiate genuine commercial use in the relevant jurisdiction. Conversely, defendants facing infringement allegations should rigorously assess the plaintiff's usage history, considering the strategic advantages of a Section 11A defence versus a full revocation counterclaim. Legal teams must integrate regular use audits into their IP management protocols to prevent registrations from becoming unenforceable due to evidentiary gaps, ensuring that intellectual property assets remain viable tools for market protection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["商标注册满五年后，权利人须承担真实使用的举证责任方可维权", "被告可利用罕见的‘非使用抗辩’阻断侵权索赔，无需主动提起撤销反诉", "视觉细微差异（如字体、i/y 拼写）不影响商标近似性判定，核心在于概念一致性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rights holders must prove genuine use of their marks within the five years prior to litigation to successfully enforce registration rights.", "Defendants may effectively block infringement claims by invoking the Section 11A non-use defence without needing to initiate formal revocation proceedings.", "Minor visual differences such as font variations or spelling changes do not negate conceptual similarity when assessing potential trade mark infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立商标使用证据链，防止因无法举证真实使用而丧失侵权索赔权", "诉讼中应精准研判对手弱点，灵活运用非使用抗辩或反诉撤销程序"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish systematic documentation processes for trade mark use to ensure enforceability and avoid loss of rights due to non-use.", "Practice note: Evaluate opponent's usage evidence early in disputes; consider Section 11A defences as a cost-effective alternative to revocation counterclaims.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'genuine use' requirements and the strategic implications of choosing between non-use defences and revocation actions."]
core_points_en: Rights holders must prove genuine use of their marks within the five years prior to litigation to successfully enforce registration rights.
Defendants may effectively block infringement claims by invoking the Section 11A non-use defence without needing to initiate formal revocation proceedings.
Minor visual differences such as font variations or spelling changes do not negate conceptual similarity when assessing potential trade mark infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish systematic documentation processes for trade mark use to ensure enforceability and avoid loss of rights due to non-use.
Practice note: Evaluate opponent's usage evidence early in disputes; consider Section 11A defences as a cost-effective alternative to revocation counterclaims.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'genuine use' requirements and the strategic implications of choosing between non-use defences and revocation actions.

items.title: More Lessons on Validity before the UPC – Part I
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院有效性判例新启示（第一部分）
items.title_en: UPC Validity Insights: Interpreting Medical Use Claims in Amgen v. Sanofi
items.summary: It has been a while since I last commented on the case law of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) on validity. To be precise, it was on 22 July 2024 in a blog titled “First Lessons on Validity before the UPC”.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于统一专利法院（UPC）近期判决，特别是慕尼黑中央分部在 Amgen 诉 Sanofi 案中的裁定，深入分析医药用途权利要求的解释规则。文章确认 UPC 将“产品 X 用于治疗 Y
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent Unified Patent Court (UPC) case law on patent validity, building upon earlier commentary from July 2024. It focuses specifically on the Munich Central Division’s decision in Amgen v. Sanofi regarding the interpretation of medical use claims. The court treats 'product X for use in treating Y' claims as purpose-limited product claims, requiring the product to be objectively suitable and therapeutically effective for the specified indication. This approach aligns with established European Patent Office practice and Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000, confirming that technical effects function as limiting features in such claims.
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should note that the UPC consistently applies a technical rather than purely philological approach to claim construction. For pharmaceutical patents, this means medical use claims are strictly evaluated based on their therapeutic efficacy and specific indications. Practitioners must ensure that patent descriptions clearly define these technical purposes to support validity arguments. Monitoring future Court of Appeal decisions will be crucial to confirm whether this strict alignment with EPO standards remains consistent across all divisions and appeal stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["本文基于统一专利法院（UPC）近期判决，特别是慕尼黑中央分部在 Amgen 诉 San…", "文章确认 UPC 将“产品 X 用于治疗 Y"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC interprets medical use claims as purpose-limited product claims requiring objective therapeutic suitability.", "Technical effects are treated as functional features, aligning with long-standing EPO and German legal practice.", "This interpretation ensures conformity with Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000 regarding novelty of known substances."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders must ensure medical use claims clearly define therapeutic efficacy to withstand UPC validity challenges.", "Practice note: Drafting strategies should align with EPO standards, treating technical effects as functional limitations in use claims.", "What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal rulings to confirm if the Munich Division's strict adherence to EPC Article 54 persists."]
core_points_en: The UPC interprets medical use claims as purpose-limited product claims requiring objective therapeutic suitability.
Technical effects are treated as functional features, aligning with long-standing EPO and German legal practice.
This interpretation ensures conformity with Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000 regarding novelty of known substances.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders must ensure medical use claims clearly define therapeutic efficacy to withstand UPC validity challenges.
Practice note: Drafting strategies should align with EPO standards, treating technical effects as functional limitations in use claims.
What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal rulings to confirm if the Munich Division's strict adherence to EPC Article 54 persists.

items.title: AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovation Group Ltd – The UK Court of Appeal rules in the second instalment of the Aga cooker sAGA
items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院裁决 AGA 炉具改装案版权侵权争议
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Rules on Copyright and Trademark Issues in AGA Cooker Retrofit Case
items.summary: In this post we review the recent Court of Appeal decision in AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovations Group Ltd & Anor [2025] EWCA Civ 1622 concerning claims of trade mark and copyright infringement related to retrofitted AGA range cookers.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析英国上诉法院关于 AGA 诉 UKIG 改装炉具案的判决。案件核心在于改装行为是否构成商标侵权及控制面板设计是否受版权保护。法院重点审查了《版权设计与专利法》第 51 条抗辩的适用性，并探讨了其与欧盟 Cofemel 案确立标准的兼容性，界定了工业产品版权保护边界。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis reviews the UK Court of Appeal’s decision in AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovation Group Ltd [2025] EWCA Civ 1622, concerning trademark and copyright infringement claims related to retrofitted AGA range cookers. The case examines whether aftermarket modifications exceed the boundaries of trademark exhaustion under the Trade Marks Act 1994. A primary focus is the application of Section 51 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, which excludes copyright protection for industrial designs. The court assessed the compatibility of this provision, and the UK’s closed-list system, with assimilated EU law following the CJEU’s Cofemel judgment. The ruling clarifies the intersection of design and copyright protections for functional products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国家电出口企业需注意，对欧洲经典产品的改装若涉及控制面板等设计复制，极易引发版权诉讼。即便主张功能性抗辩，在英国脱欧后法律环境下仍面临不确定性。建议企业在开展售后改装业务前，进行严格的知识产权合规评估，避免触碰设计权与版权的重叠保护区。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaged in aftermarket modification of branded goods must carefully assess intellectual property risks, particularly regarding copied components like control panels. Post-Brexit, the interaction between UK statutory defenses and retained EU case law creates legal uncertainty. Rights holders and third-party modifiers should conduct thorough compliance evaluations before launching retrofit services. Understanding the precise boundaries where design rights overlap with copyright is essential to mitigate litigation exposure in the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院审理改装炉具营销是否逾越商标权用尽原则的合法边界", "焦点在于功能性工业设计能否依据英国法排除版权保护的延伸", "判决需平衡英国封闭列表体系与欧盟 Cofemel 案确立的统一标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court examined whether retrofitting and marketing classic AGA cookers exceeded permissible limits under trademark exhaustion principles.", "The decision analyzed whether functional industrial designs, such as control panels, are excluded from extended copyright protection under UK law.", "The ruling addressed the tension between the UK's closed-list copyright system and the broader standards established by the CJEU in Cofemel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["改装欧洲品牌产品时，需警惕控制面板等部件的版权侵权风险", "英国法下功能性抗辩适用范围不明，出口企业应提前规避设计雷区"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Aftermarket modifiers face significant copyright infringement risks when replicating protected design elements of original equipment.", "Practice note: Legal teams should evaluate the applicability of Section 51 CDPA defenses against potential claims arising from functional product adaptations.", "What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretation of the compatibility between UK design exclusions and EU-derived copyright standards post-Brexit."]
core_points_en: The court examined whether retrofitting and marketing classic AGA cookers exceeded permissible limits under trademark exhaustion principles.
The decision analyzed whether functional industrial designs, such as control panels, are excluded from extended copyright protection under UK law.
The ruling addressed the tension between the UK's closed-list copyright system and the broader standards established by the CJEU in Cofemel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Aftermarket modifiers face significant copyright infringement risks when replicating protected design elements of original equipment.
Practice note: Legal teams should evaluate the applicability of Section 51 CDPA defenses against potential claims arising from functional product adaptations.
What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretation of the compatibility between UK design exclusions and EU-derived copyright standards post-Brexit.

items.title: Why Mars is using behavioural psychology to help tackle lookalikes
items.title_zh: 玛氏利用行为心理学助力打击仿冒产品
items.title_en: Mars Leverages Behavioral Psychology to Enhance Trade Dress Enforcement Against Lookalikes
items.summary: From consumer behaviour to trade dress enforcement, brand owners are refining their approach to lookalike products, according to Mars’ IP counsel.
items.ai_summary_zh: 玛氏公司知识产权顾问指出，品牌所有者正从消费者行为学角度优化对仿冒产品的应对策略。通过结合行为心理学分析与商业外观执法，企业能更精准识别并遏制模仿者，提升在欧洲市场的商标保护效率与维权成功率。
items.ai_summary_en: According to Mars’ intellectual property counsel, brand owners are increasingly refining their strategies to combat lookalike products by integrating consumer behavior insights with traditional trade dress enforcement. This evolving approach moves beyond purely legal arguments, incorporating behavioral psychology to better understand how consumers perceive and interact with packaging similarities. By analyzing cognitive responses to visual cues, rights holders can more accurately identify instances of confusion and strengthen their enforcement actions. This multidisciplinary method aims to improve the efficiency of trademark protection within the European market, offering a more robust framework for distinguishing genuine brands from imitators. The strategy highlights a shift towards evidence-based enforcement that aligns legal remedies with actual consumer perception dynamics.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应借鉴跨国巨头策略，将心理学洞察融入商标监测与维权体系。在欧遭遇仿冒时，除法律手段外，可举证消费者混淆心理机制，增强执法说服力，提升维权实效。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe should consider integrating psychological insights into their trademark monitoring and enforcement frameworks. When addressing lookalike products, supplementing standard legal arguments with evidence on consumer confusion mechanisms can significantly strengthen case persuasiveness. Demonstrating how specific visual elements trigger cognitive associations helps establish likelihood of confusion more effectively than generic claims. This approach allows for a more nuanced presentation of evidence, potentially improving outcomes in administrative and judicial proceedings. Companies should evaluate whether their current enforcement strategies adequately account for consumer perception data alongside traditional legal metrics.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借鉴行为心理学优化欧洲商标侵权证据链构建", "结合消费认知分析提升对仿冒品的执法打击力"]

Brand owners are developing refined trade dress enforcement strategies specifically targeting lookalike product designs.
