items.title: Why data centres are the next big thing in patent pool licensing
items.title_zh: 为何数据中心将成为专利池许可的下一个焦点
items.title_en: Data Centres Emerge as Key Focus for Next Generation Patent Pool Licensing Initiatives
items.summary: A major licensing company plans to launch a series of data centre patent pools, aiming to avoid the next big computer memory bottleneck while driving the growth of AI, reports Sarah Speight.
items.ai_summary_zh: 一家主要许可公司计划推出一系列数据中心专利池，旨在避免计算机内存瓶颈并推动人工智能增长。该举措反映了欧洲及全球在 AI 基础设施领域的专利聚合趋势，通过集体许可模式降低交易成本，解决潜在的技术实施障碍，为数据中心相关技术的广泛应用提供法律保障。
items.ai_summary_en: A leading licensing entity intends to establish a series of patent pools specifically targeting data centre technologies. This strategic move aims to prevent critical computer memory bottlenecks that could hinder the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure. By aggregating patents through collective licensing models, the initiative seeks to reduce transaction costs and resolve potential technical implementation barriers. This development reflects a broader trend in Europe and globally towards patent aggregation within the AI sector. The pools are designed to provide legal certainty and facilitate the widespread adoption of essential data centre technologies, ensuring smoother market entry and operational scalability for stakeholders involved in next-generation computing environments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局 AI 与数据中心业务时，需警惕专利池带来的许可费叠加风险。建议提前开展自由实施分析，评估入池必要性，或通过交叉许可谈判降低合规成本，避免陷入被动局面。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI and data centre solutions in Europe should assess exposure to cumulative royalty obligations arising from new patent pools. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in the product lifecycle is critical to identify necessary licenses and evaluate the cost-benefit of joining versus negotiating separately. Engaging in cross-licensing discussions can help mitigate compliance expenses and reduce litigation risks. Stakeholders must remain agile in responding to evolving licensing terms to maintain competitive positioning while ensuring full adherence to emerging collective rights management frameworks governing essential data technologies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["主要许可公司拟建立数据中心专项专利池以整合资源", "旨在突破计算机内存瓶颈并加速人工智能技术落地", "专利池模式将成为解决复杂技术许可问题的关键路径"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Major licensing firms plan to create specialized data centre patent pools to consolidate fragmented intellectual property resources efficiently.", "The primary objective is to overcome computer memory constraints and accelerate the practical deployment of advanced artificial intelligence technologies.", "Patent pool mechanisms are becoming essential pathways for resolving complex licensing challenges inherent in modern data centre infrastructure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧洲专利池动态以防许可费用不可控上升", "主动参与标准制定或谈判以争取有利许可条件"]

items.title: Hashtags et marques : pas de contrefaçon sans indication d’origine
items.title_zh: 标签与商标：无来源指示不构成商标侵权
items.title_en: Paris Court Rules Hashtag Use Without Origin Indication Does Not Constitute Trademark Infringement
items.summary: En juin 2023, la société Printemps (ci-après « Printemps ») bien connue du grand public, avait lancé sur son site internet et ses réseaux sociaux, une opération commerciale de « Live Shopping » destinée à promouvoir ses produits.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国百货公司 Printemps 在直播购物中使用了罗兰加洛斯网球赛片段及
items.ai_summary_en: In June 2023, the French department store Printemps launched a 'Live Shopping' campaign on its website and social media to promote products. The promotional broadcast included video clips from previous Roland-Garros tennis tournaments and used the hashtag '#RG' on Instagram. The French Tennis Federation (FFT), organizer of the tournament, sued Printemps for unauthorized exploitation, parasitism, and trademark infringement regarding its semi-figurative mark. While the Paris Judicial Tribunal confirmed violations of the FFT's exclusive exploitation rights and parasitism, it rejected the trademark infringement claim. The court reasoned that the hashtag use did not function as a source indicator, thereby failing to meet the legal criteria for trademark counterfeiting despite the commercial context.
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the boundary between unfair competition and trademark infringement in digital marketing contexts. Rights holders must demonstrate that a hashtag functions as a distinctive sign indicating commercial origin to succeed in infringement claims. Mere association with a famous event or brand via hashtags may support parasitism arguments but does not automatically equate to trademark counterfeiting. Market participants should carefully assess whether their social media tags serve descriptive or promotional purposes versus acting as source identifiers. This distinction is critical when enforcing IP rights against competitors leveraging trending topics or event-related terminology for visibility without explicit licensing agreements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国百货公司 Printemps 在直播购物中使用了罗兰加洛斯网球赛片段及"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Printemps used Roland-Garros video clips and the #RG hashtag in a Live Shopping campaign without FFT authorization.", "The Paris Judicial Tribunal confirmed parasitism and violation of exclusive exploitation rights by Printemps.", "The court ruled that using the hashtag did not constitute trademark infringement as it lacked origin indication."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establishes that hashtag usage alone may not infringe trademarks if it does not indicate commercial origin.", "Practice note: Enforcement strategies should differentiate between parasitism claims and strict trademark infringement requirements.", "What to monitor: How courts interpret 'origin indication' in evolving social media marketing practices across jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: Printemps used Roland-Garros video clips and the #RG hashtag in a Live Shopping campaign without FFT authorization.
The Paris Judicial Tribunal confirmed parasitism and violation of exclusive exploitation rights by Printemps.
The court ruled that using the hashtag did not constitute trademark infringement as it lacked origin indication.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establishes that hashtag usage alone may not infringe trademarks if it does not indicate commercial origin.
Practice note: Enforcement strategies should differentiate between parasitism claims and strict trademark infringement requirements.
What to monitor: How courts interpret 'origin indication' in evolving social media marketing practices across jurisdictions.

items.title: Four Reasons To Choose Our End-to-end Trademark Service
items.title_zh: 选择端到端商标服务的四大理由
items.title_en: Strategic Benefits of End-to-End Trademark Management for Corporate Portfolios
items.summary: Our team developed this offering to tackle some of the key problems faced by corporates when working with external counsel to manage their IP. In particular, it provides a flexible, comprehensive service for your IP assets, taking the stress of managing your brands and related IP portfolios away and giving you peace of mind that you’re in safe hands.
items.ai_summary_zh: Novagraaf 集团针对企业外包知识产权管理的痛点，推出端到端商标服务。该服务主打灵活性，允许企业按需组合续展、维权等模块；强调预算透明，采用预协议固定费用制以避免隐性成本；并依托全球网络提供丰富经验，旨在成为企业外部知识产权职能的有效延伸，降低品牌管理压力。
items.ai_summary_en: Novagraaf Group has introduced an end-to-end trademark service designed to address common challenges corporations face when outsourcing intellectual property management. This flexible solution allows rights holders to customize their engagement, selecting specific modules such as renewals, maintenance, searches, prosecution, and enforcement. The service emphasizes budget transparency through pre-agreed fixed rates, eliminating unexpected costs often associated with external counsel. By integrating seamlessly with existing legal networks or leveraging Novagraaf’s global provider ecosystem, the offering acts as an effective extension of in-house teams. It aims to reduce administrative burdens and provide peace of mind, ensuring efficient brand portfolio management across multiple jurisdictions while maintaining high standards of care and support throughout the lifecycle of IP assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展时，常面临跨国商标管理成本高、响应慢的难题。此类端到端服务模式提示企业，可通过外包非核心事务并锁定固定成本，提升管理效率。建议评估自身需求，考虑引入类似集成服务以优化欧洲知识产权资产布局。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies managing complex international portfolios, adopting integrated trademark services can significantly enhance operational efficiency. By outsourcing non-core administrative tasks under a fixed-cost model, in-house counsel can better control budgets and mitigate the risks of unpredictable legal fees in diverse jurisdictions. This approach allows legal teams to focus resources on strategic brand protection and core business objectives. Evaluating such end-to-end solutions is particularly relevant for organizations seeking to streamline workflows, improve response times, and optimize the overall management of European intellectual property assets without compromising on quality or oversight.

items.title: [Blog] Hashtag et contrefaçon : une précision importante du Tribunal judiciaire de Paris
items.title_zh: 巴黎法院厘清标签使用与商标侵权界限
items.title_en: Paris Court Clarifies Hashtag Use: No Trademark Infringement Without Origin Function
items.summary: Dans une décision du 12 février 2026, le Tribunal judiciaire de Paris apporte une clarification sur l’usage des hashtags en matière de droit des marques.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 2 月 12 日，巴黎司法法院就 Printemps 公司涉罗兰加洛斯赛事案作出裁决。法院认定被告虽构成寄生竞争及侵害赛事开发权，但使用#RG 标签不构成商标侵权。裁判核心在于该标签仅具检索功能，缺乏指示商品来源的商标本质功能，未作为商标使用。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 12, 2026, the Judicial Court of Paris issued a significant ruling regarding hashtag usage in trademark law. The court addressed claims against Printemps concerning its association with the Roland-Garros tournament. While the retailer was found liable for parasitism and infringement of the tournament's exploitation rights, the judges dismissed the claim of trademark infringement related to the use of the hashtag #RG. The decisive factor was the absence of an origin-indicating function. The court determined that the hashtag served merely as a search and indexing tool rather than functioning as a trademark. This distinction highlights that not all uses of protected terms in social media tags constitute trademark infringement. The decision provides critical guidance for digital communication strategies and brand protection on social platforms, emphasizing the need to differentiate between technical metadata usage and commercial trademark application.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧营销需区分标签检索功能与商标使用界限。虽单纯标签可能不侵权，但仍需警惕寄生竞争风险。建议优化社媒策略，避免搭便车嫌疑，确保合规运营。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the boundary between technical hashtag usage and trademark infringement for rights holders operating in Europe. Companies should recognize that using a competitor's mark or event name as a hashtag may not constitute trademark infringement if it serves solely as a search index without indicating origin. However, this does not grant immunity from other legal risks. Market participants must remain vigilant against claims of parasitism or unfair competition, which can still arise from leveraging another entity's reputation. Digital marketing strategies should be optimized to avoid any appearance of free-riding on established brands or events, ensuring compliance with broader intellectual property and competition laws beyond strict trademark definitions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定标签仅为检索工具而非商标性使用", "缺乏来源指示功能是排除商标侵权的关键理由", "被告仍因寄生竞争及侵害赛事权利被判承担责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court ruled that hashtags function primarily as search tools, meaning their use does not automatically qualify as trademark usage under the law.", "Excluding trademark infringement liability relied heavily on the finding that the hashtag lacked the essential function of indicating commercial origin.", "Despite avoiding trademark infringement, the defendant remained liable for parasitic competition and unauthorized exploitation of event rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确标签检索属性可降低商标侵权法律风险", "需防范非商标类不正当竞争及寄生行为指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Distinguishing between hashtag retrieval functions and trademark use helps mitigate specific trademark infringement risks in EU social media campaigns.", "Practice note: Rights holders should monitor non-trademark liabilities, such as parasitism allegations, even when trademark infringement claims are weak.", "What to monitor: Courts continue to refine the definition of 'use as a trademark' in digital contexts, requiring careful review of social media tagging practices."]
core_points_en: The court ruled that hashtags function primarily as search tools, meaning their use does not automatically qualify as trademark usage under the law.
Excluding trademark infringement liability relied heavily on the finding that the hashtag lacked the essential function of indicating commercial origin.
Despite avoiding trademark infringement, the defendant remained liable for parasitic competition and unauthorized exploitation of event rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Distinguishing between hashtag retrieval functions and trademark use helps mitigate specific trademark infringement risks in EU social media campaigns.
Practice note: Rights holders should monitor non-trademark liabilities, such as parasitism allegations, even when trademark infringement claims are weak.
What to monitor: Courts continue to refine the definition of 'use as a trademark' in digital contexts, requiring careful review of social media tagging practices.

items.title: AI and copyright in the UK - the government decides to wait and see
items.title_zh: 英国政府决定暂缓调整 AI 训练版权例外规则
items.title_en: UK Government Pauses AI Copyright Exception Plans, Adopts Wait-and-See Approach
items.summary: Earlier this week, the UK government published its much-anticipated report on the interaction between copyright law and AI. It has been prepared followi...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布报告回应关于版权法与人工智能互动的咨询，收到逾万份反馈。鉴于各方观点分歧及证据不足，政府决定暂不推行允许 AI 训练的广泛版权例外（含退出机制），而是采取观望态度，等待技术与法律环境进一步明朗。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has published its response to the consultation on copyright and artificial intelligence, receiving over 11,500 submissions from diverse stakeholders including creators, developers, and academics. Contrary to expectations of imminent legislative reform, the report indicates a decision to pause and observe developments in both technology and law globally. Notably, the government has abandoned its previous preference for a broad copyright exception with an opt-out mechanism for AI model training. This reversal stems from strong opposing views during the consultation, identified gaps in evidence, and the rapidly evolving international context. The current stance prioritizes monitoring market and legal evolutions before committing to specific statutory changes, leaving the existing copyright framework largely intact for now while acknowledging the need for future adaptation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英运营的中国 AI 企业需警惕侵权风险，不可依赖即将出台的例外条款。建议立即开展版权合规审计，建立数据授权机制，并密切关注后续立法动向以调整战略。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in the UK, the suspension of legislative reforms means that existing strict copyright protections remain fully enforceable. Rights holders can continue to rely on current laws to protect their works against unauthorized use in AI training datasets. Market participants should not anticipate immediate statutory exemptions or safe harbors for AI development activities. Instead, organizations must proactively establish robust data governance frameworks, ensuring all training data is sourced through proper licensing or authorized channels. Legal teams should prioritize comprehensive copyright compliance audits to mitigate infringement risks, as reliance on potential future exceptions is no longer a viable strategic option in the current regulatory environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["政府放弃推行带退出机制的广泛版权例外方案", "决策主因是咨询反馈强烈且行业证据尚不充分", "英国将采取观望策略以应对快速演变的 AI 局势"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The government has withdrawn its proposal for a broad copyright exception with opt-out for AI training due to significant stakeholder opposition.", "Decision-making is driven by insufficient evidence regarding AI impacts and the need to assess the rapidly changing global regulatory landscape.", "The UK will adopt a wait-and-see strategy, delaying legislative changes to allow technology and international legal standards to mature further."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["暂缓立法意味着当前严格版权保护仍具约束力", "企业应主动构建合规数据获取渠道而非等待豁免"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Existing copyright laws remain strictly binding; no new statutory exceptions for AI training are currently available or imminent.", "Practice note: Organizations must proactively secure data licenses and conduct compliance audits rather than relying on anticipated legislative waivers.", "What to monitor: Future government consultations and international regulatory developments that may eventually shape the UK's AI copyright framework."]
core_points_en: The government has withdrawn its proposal for a broad copyright exception with opt-out for AI training due to significant stakeholder opposition.
Decision-making is driven by insufficient evidence regarding AI impacts and the need to assess the rapidly changing global regulatory landscape.
The UK will adopt a wait-and-see strategy, delaying legislative changes to allow technology and international legal standards to mature further.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Existing copyright laws remain strictly binding; no new statutory exceptions for AI training are currently available or imminent.
Practice note: Organizations must proactively secure data licenses and conduct compliance audits rather than relying on anticipated legislative waivers.
What to monitor: Future government consultations and international regulatory developments that may eventually shape the UK's AI copyright framework.

items.title: Changes announced to the UK FDI mandatory notification regime
items.title_zh: 英国宣布调整外商直接投资强制申报制度
items.title_en: UK Government Announces Changes to Mandatory FDI Notification Regime Under NSIA
items.summary: The UK government announces changes to the mandatory notification regime under the National Security and Investment Act. What are the implications for l...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府基于国家安全与投资法案，宣布调整外商直接投资强制申报机制。新规旨在平衡投资促进与安全审查：一方面缩小人工智能等领域监管范围，豁免仅使用现成工具的企业；另一方面将半导体、关键矿产等纳入强制审查，并提高能源行业门槛。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has confirmed amendments to the mandatory notification regime under the National Security and Investment Act 2021, following a twelve-week consultation. These changes aim to balance investment promotion with national security scrutiny by refining sectoral definitions. The reforms narrow the scope for certain technologies, specifically excluding off-the-shelf artificial intelligence tools from mandatory review, thereby streamlining processes for non-developer entities. Conversely, the regime expands to include critical sectors such as semiconductors, critical minerals, and water, while introducing stricter capacity thresholds in energy. New de minimis exemptions for communications and intragroup reorganizations further adjust the compliance landscape. These modifications will take effect later this year, requiring market participants to reassess transaction structures and notification obligations to ensure alignment with the updated regulatory framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英并购或投资高科技资产时，需重新评估申报义务。虽部分 AI 应用企业获松绑，但涉足半导体及能源领域将面临更严审查。建议提前规划交易架构，利用新豁免条款降低合规成本与时间风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in transactions involving UK assets must reassess their notification obligations under the updated National Security and Investment Act framework. While the exclusion of off-the-shelf artificial intelligence applications reduces compliance burdens for many technology users, the inclusion of semiconductors, critical minerals, and water sectors significantly broadens the scope of mandatory reviews. Energy sector participants face heightened scrutiny due to new cumulative capacity thresholds. Companies should proactively evaluate whether their target assets fall within these expanded categories or qualify for new exemptions. Early assessment of transaction structures is essential to mitigate delays and ensure timely completion, particularly for deals involving sensitive technologies or infrastructure assets subject to the revised mandatory notification requirements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["重定义人工智能范畴，豁免仅使用现成工具的非研发型企业", "新增半导体、关键矿产及水务 sector 为强制申报重点领域", "通信行业引入最低限额豁免，集团内部重组亦获免除审查"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Redefining artificial intelligence to exclude off-the-shelf tools, exempting companies that do not primarily develop AI technologies from mandatory notifications.", "Expanding the mandatory regime to include new sectors such as semiconductors, critical minerals, and water, increasing national security oversight.", "Introducing de minimis exemptions for the communications sector and confirming exemptions for intragroup reorganizations without ownership changes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["涉半导体与能源投资的中企需警惕审查范围扩大带来的合规阻力", "纯应用现成 AI 工具的企业可利用新定义豁免，加速并购交易进程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Transactions involving semiconductors, critical minerals, and energy assets face expanded mandatory review scopes and stricter thresholds.", "Practice note: Leverage new exemptions for off-the-shelf AI users and intragroup reorganizations to streamline deal timelines and reduce compliance costs.", "What to monitor: Implementation dates for the amended regulations and guidance on applying new aggregate capacity thresholds in the energy sector."]
core_points_en: Redefining artificial intelligence to exclude off-the-shelf tools, exempting companies that do not primarily develop AI technologies from mandatory notifications.
Expanding the mandatory regime to include new sectors such as semiconductors, critical minerals, and water, increasing national security oversight.
Introducing de minimis exemptions for the communications sector and confirming exemptions for intragroup reorganizations without ownership changes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Transactions involving semiconductors, critical minerals, and energy assets face expanded mandatory review scopes and stricter thresholds.
Practice note: Leverage new exemptions for off-the-shelf AI users and intragroup reorganizations to streamline deal timelines and reduce compliance costs.
What to monitor: Implementation dates for the amended regulations and guidance on applying new aggregate capacity thresholds in the energy sector.

items.title: Patent Law & Practice at a Glance – April 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: GJE：2026年4月专利法律与实务概览
items.title_en: GJE Patent Law Update: UKIPO One IPO Launch, EPO and PCT Practice Changes
items.summary: Read our April 2026 edition of patent law and practice which includes updates from the UK, the EPO and the WIPO/PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系GJE发布的2026年4月专利法律与实务简报。核心动态为英国知识产权局（UKIPO）于4月1日正式公开推出“One IPO”新服务，并发布了相关用户指南。此外，简报还涵盖了欧洲专利局（EPO）及世界知识产权组织PCT体系的最新实务更新。该资讯旨在为企业和内部IP团队提供欧洲层面的专业知识产权洞察，助力投资决策与业务保障。
items.ai_summary_en: This April 2026 edition of GJE’s Patent Law & Practice at a Glance provides essential updates for intellectual property professionals across Europe. The primary development is the public launch of the UK Intellectual Property Office’s new One IPO service on 1 April 2026, accompanied by comprehensive user guidance to assist applicants in navigating the updated procedural landscape. Additionally, this briefing covers significant recent developments and practice changes within the European Patent Office and the World Intellectual Property Organization’s Patent Cooperation Treaty system. These insights are designed to support in-house IP teams and legal counsel in maintaining compliance, optimizing application strategies, and securing robust protection for their portfolios amidst evolving administrative frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注UKIPO“One IPO”系统的具体操作要求，及时调整英国专利申请策略。鉴于简报同时涉及EPO和PCT动态，建议同步审查在欧洲及国际阶段的程序合规性，确保在新服务环境下维持申请效率，避免因流程不熟悉导致的权利丧失或延误。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should closely examine the specific operational requirements of the UKIPO’s One IPO system to adjust their United Kingdom filing strategies accordingly. Given the concurrent updates from the European Patent Office and the PCT framework, it is advisable to simultaneously review procedural compliance during European and international phases. This holistic approach ensures continued application efficiency under the new service environment, mitigating risks of rights loss or delays caused by unfamiliarity with updated workflows. Maintaining alignment across jurisdictions is crucial for effective global portfolio management.

items.title: No luck for Broadcom as Netflix and Quinn Emanuel succeed in another nullity claim
items.title_zh: Netflix 再胜诉：博通视频编码专利被宣告无效
items.title_en: Broadcom Faces Setback as Netflix Secures Nullity of Video Coding Patent EP 2 723 094
items.summary: Netflix has been battling Broadcom, previously known as Avago, over multiple video coding patents since 2018. In total, the extensive campaign comprises eight patents, of which seven have now finally been revoked. The current case centres on EP 2 723 094. The patent owned by Avago covers a “set top box architecture with application based […] The post No luck for Broadcom as Netflix and Quinn Emanuel succeed in another nullity claim appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2018 年起，Netflix 与博通（原 Avago）就多项视频编码专利展开激烈诉讼。此次案件聚焦欧洲专利 EP 2 723 094，涉及机顶盒架构技术。继此前七项专利被撤销后，本次诉讼中 Netflix 再次胜诉，该专利亦被宣告无效。这标志着博通在该系列专利战中遭遇重大挫折，凸显了统一专利法院在专利无效裁决中的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 2018, Netflix has engaged in extensive litigation against Broadcom (formerly Avago) concerning multiple video coding patents. This long-running campaign involves eight patents, seven of which have now been successfully revoked. The latest development centers on European patent EP 2 723 094, which covers set top box architecture with application-based features. Following previous successes, Netflix, represented by Quinn Emanuel, achieved another victory with the declaration of invalidity for this specific patent. This outcome marks a significant setback for Broadcom in its ongoing patent enforcement efforts and underscores the critical role of the Unified Patent Court in determining patent validity within Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局视频编码等标准必要专利时，需警惕高频率无效挑战。建议提前进行严格的专利稳定性评估，并关注 UPC 判例趋势，制定灵活的诉讼与许可策略，避免重蹈博通覆辙。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders asserting standard-essential or video coding patents in Europe should anticipate rigorous validity challenges before the Unified Patent Court. High-frequency invalidation actions demonstrate that patent portfolios require robust pre-filing stability assessments to withstand scrutiny. Market participants must closely monitor UPC case law trends to refine their litigation and licensing approaches. Proactive evaluation of patent strength and adaptive strategic planning are essential to mitigate the risk of widespread revocations and ensure effective intellectual property protection across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Netflix 累计使博通八项视频编码专利中七项被撤销", "本案核心为 EP 2 723 094 号欧洲专利的无效宣告请求", "博通在长期专利诉讼中持续失利，专利稳定性受挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Netflix has successfully secured the revocation of seven out of eight video coding patents asserted against it by Broadcom since 2018.", "The current legal dispute focuses on the nullity claim regarding European patent EP 2 723 094, covering set top box architecture technologies.", "Broadcom continues to face challenges in maintaining patent stability during prolonged litigation, highlighting risks in enforcement strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化专利事前稳定性筛查，规避无效风险", "密切关注 UPC 裁判动态，优化海外维权策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct thorough pre-litigation stability screenings for video coding patents to minimize invalidation risks in Europe.", "Practice note: Monitor Unified Patent Court decisions closely to adapt overseas enforcement and licensing strategies effectively.", "What to monitor: Trends in UPC nullity rulings affecting standard-essential and multimedia technology patents."]
core_points_en: Netflix has successfully secured the revocation of seven out of eight video coding patents asserted against it by Broadcom since 2018.
The current legal dispute focuses on the nullity claim regarding European patent EP 2 723 094, covering set top box architecture technologies.
Broadcom continues to face challenges in maintaining patent stability during prolonged litigation, highlighting risks in enforcement strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct thorough pre-litigation stability screenings for video coding patents to minimize invalidation risks in Europe.
Practice note: Monitor Unified Patent Court decisions closely to adapt overseas enforcement and licensing strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Trends in UPC nullity rulings affecting standard-essential and multimedia technology patents.

items.title: After ownership change: Silimed and Polytech poised to fight it out at UPC
items.title_zh: 权属变更后：Silimed 与 Polytech 拟在 UPC 展开对决
items.title_en: Silimed and Polytech Set for UPC Clash Following German Court Ownership Ruling
items.summary: Just over a year ago, the Frankfurt Higher Regional Court granted Silimed the property rights to EP 2 581 193. The patent protects a process for manufacturing implants. Since 2017 the Brazilian manufacturer had fought with competitor Polytech over the ownership of the technology. After the rights were officially transferred, Silimed recently launched an infringement […] The post After ownership change: Silimed and Polytech poised to fight it out at UPC appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法兰克福高等地方法院一年前裁定巴西 Silimed 公司拥有 EP2581193 号植入物制造专利权属，终结了其与竞争对手 Polytech 自 2017 年的纠纷。权属正式转移后，Silimed 随即在统一专利法院（UPC）提起侵权诉讼。此案标志着双方战场从德国国内法院转向泛欧司法体系，凸显 UPC 在处理跨国专利争端中的新角色。
items.ai_summary_en: Following a decisive ruling by the Frankfurt Higher Regional Court, Brazilian manufacturer Silimed secured full ownership of European patent EP 2 581 193, which covers a specific process for manufacturing medical implants. This judgment effectively concluded a protracted legal dispute with competitor Polytech that had persisted since 2017 regarding the technology's rightful owner. With the property rights officially transferred to Silimed, the company has promptly initiated infringement proceedings against Polytech at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). This strategic shift moves the conflict from national German courts to the new pan-European judicial framework. The case highlights the UPC's growing role in resolving complex, cross-border patent disputes and demonstrates how rights holders are leveraging the new court system to enforce their patents across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利权属或侵权纠纷时，需高度关注 UPC 的新管辖权。建议提前评估欧洲专利组合风险，熟悉 UPC 程序规则，避免因属地诉讼策略滞后而陷入被动，必要时利用统一法院机制维权。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UPC's evolving jurisdiction over patent ownership and infringement matters. As the new court becomes a primary venue for cross-border disputes, companies must adapt their European litigation strategies accordingly. It is critical to conduct thorough due diligence on patent title chains before initiating or defending against actions. Rights holders should assess potential risks within their European patent portfolios early, ensuring they are prepared for the procedural nuances of the UPC. Proactive planning helps avoid being disadvantaged by fragmented national strategies, allowing for more effective enforcement or defense across member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国法院裁定 Silimed 获得涉案欧洲专利完整所有权", "权属确立后，Silimed 立即在统一专利法院发起侵权诉讼", "案件体现欧洲专利争端解决机制向 UPC 集中化的趋势"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视 UPC 管辖权，调整欧洲专利诉讼与应对策略", "强化权属尽职调查，防范跨国专利纠纷连锁反应"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt European patent litigation strategies to account for the UPC's expanding jurisdiction and centralized enforcement capabilities.", "Practice note: Strengthen patent ownership due diligence processes to mitigate risks associated with cross-border title disputes and subsequent infringement claims.", "What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments regarding patent ownership transfers and their impact on parallel infringement proceedings."]

Silimed immediately filed an infringement action at the Unified Patent Court following the official transfer of patent rights.
The dispute illustrates a broader trend of centralizing European patent enforcement and dispute resolution within the UPC system.

Practice note: Strengthen patent ownership due diligence processes to mitigate risks associated with cross-border title disputes and subsequent infringement claims.
What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments regarding patent ownership transfers and their impact on parallel infringement proceedings.

items.title: UK kicks the AI copyright can down the road: 5 takeaways for business
items.title_zh: 英国推迟 AI 版权改革：给企业的五点启示
items.title_en: UK Pauses AI Copyright Reform: Five Strategic Takeaways for Business Amid Policy Uncertainty
items.summary: The country hit pause on AI and copyright reform. But who benefits from indecision and what underpriced issue is “arguably the most significant”?
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府决定暂时搁置人工智能与版权相关的法律改革计划，维持现状。这一决策引发了关于谁从不作为中受益的讨论，并指出某些被低估的议题可能最具深远影响。文章分析了该暂停决定对商业环境的五点关键启示，强调企业在不确定性中需重新评估风险策略。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has officially suspended its plans to reform copyright laws regarding artificial intelligence, choosing to maintain the current legal framework. This decision to pause legislative progress has sparked debate over which market participants benefit from regulatory indecision and highlighted specific underpriced issues that may prove most significant in the long term. The article outlines five key takeaways for businesses operating in this environment, emphasizing the need to reassess risk strategies amidst ongoing uncertainty. By delaying statutory changes, the state creates a temporary status quo, yet leaves critical questions unanswered. Stakeholders must now navigate a landscape where the absence of clear rules is itself a strategic factor, requiring careful evaluation of existing exemptions and potential future liabilities associated with AI training data usage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应警惕英国政策滞后带来的合规模糊期，避免盲目依赖现有豁免条款。建议密切关注后续立法动向，提前布局数据授权机制，防范未来法律回溯风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding the compliance ambiguities created by the delay in UK policy updates. Reliance on existing copyright exceptions for AI training carries inherent risks if future legislation introduces retroactive obligations or stricter standards. It is prudent to utilize this interim period to conduct thorough audits of data sourcing practices and licensing agreements. Establishing robust internal monitoring mechanisms will enable rapid adaptation to any sudden shifts in the legislative landscape. Proactively preparing flexible data authorization frameworks can mitigate exposure to future legal challenges, ensuring business continuity regardless of how the regulatory environment eventually evolves.

items.title: Local division Mannheim flirts with setting FRAND rate
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部倾向裁定 FRAND 费率
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Division Signals Openness to Determining FRAND Rates in ZTE v Samsung
items.summary: Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to making a FRAND-rate determination.
items.ai_summary_zh: 继慕尼黑地方法院后，统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院在 ZTE 诉三星案中展现新动向。主审法官托赫特曼提出 UPC 框架下裁定 FRAND 费率的程序构想，打破德国法院长期回避此议题的传统。因三星提出反诉，法庭需对此作出裁决，预示欧洲标准必要专利诉讼格局生变。
items.ai_summary_en: Following the Munich Regional Court, the Unified Patent Court’s Mannheim Local Division is shifting away from the traditional German judicial reluctance to determine Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) royalty rates. In the ongoing ZTE v Samsung proceedings, Presiding Judge Peter Tochtermann outlined a procedural framework for such determinations within the UPC system. This development arises because Samsung filed a FRAND counterclaim, compelling the court to address the issue substantively rather than avoiding it. The hearing attracted significant attention from legal and economic experts across Europe and Asia. This move suggests a potential transformation in European standard-essential patent litigation, challenging the historical dominance of UK courts in setting global FRAND terms and establishing new precedents for continental European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更直接的费率裁判风险与机遇。需重新评估 UPC 诉讼策略，准备详尽的经济证据以应对费率裁定。建议密切关注曼海姆及巴黎分院动态，利用新机制争取有利许可条件或防御高额索赔。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in standard-essential patent disputes should anticipate increased judicial willingness in Germany to set binding FRAND rates. Rights holders and implementers must adapt their Unified Patent Court litigation strategies by preparing robust economic evidence and valuation models to support or challenge proposed royalty figures. The interplay between the Mannheim and Paris divisions may offer strategic opportunities for forum selection. Parties should closely monitor how these new procedural frameworks develop, as they could significantly influence licensing negotiations and the overall landscape of SEP enforcement in Europe, potentially reducing reliance on non-European jurisdictions for rate-setting decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["曼海姆分院打破德国法院不裁定 FRAND 费率的传统", "法官在 ZTE 诉三星案中提出 UPC 费率裁定程序框架", "三星的反诉迫使法庭必须对费率问题做出实质性回应"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Mannheim Local Division breaks with the long-standing tradition of German courts avoiding substantive FRAND rate determinations.", "Presiding Judge Tochtermann presented a specific procedural framework for UPC FRAND rate settings during the ZTE v Samsung hearing.", "Samsung's filed counterclaim legally obligates the division to provide a substantive response regarding the appropriate licensing rates."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业需调整 UPC 诉讼策略，强化费率抗辩与经济证据准备", "关注曼海姆与巴黎分院联动，灵活选择管辖地争取主动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants face heightened risks and opportunities regarding direct judicial determination of FRAND rates in Germany.", "Practice note: Strengthen economic evidence and adjust litigation strategies to effectively engage with UPC rate-setting mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Procedural developments in Mannheim and Paris divisions to optimize jurisdictional choices and licensing outcomes."]

Presiding Judge Tochtermann presented a specific procedural framework for UPC FRAND rate settings during the ZTE v Samsung hearing.

What to monitor: Procedural developments in Mannheim and Paris divisions to optimize jurisdictional choices and licensing outcomes.

items.title: A strong trademark stands out - Arnold & Siedsma [EN]
items.title_zh: 强商标脱颖而出：显著性是品牌保护核心
items.title_en: Distinctiveness as Core Brand Protection: How Strong Trademarks Stand Out in the Market
items.summary: YUN is a strong trademark because it is distinctive and closely connected to the skincare products it represents. The name is memorable, easy to recognize,
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文以护肤品牌
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the skincare brand YUN to illustrate how distinctiveness serves as the foundation for effective trademark protection. The article highlights that YUN is a strong mark because it is memorable, easily recognizable, and clearly differentiates the brand from competitors in the crowded skincare sector. Unlike descriptive terms, unique names facilitate quicker consumer association with specific product quality and corporate reputation. This recognition builds consumer trust and becomes a valuable long-term asset. Furthermore, distinctive marks are generally easier to register and enforce against infringers, preventing market confusion. The piece underscores that securing legal protection for unique identifiers supports sustained brand growth and strengthens market position, making distinctiveness a critical strategic consideration for rights holders seeking robust intellectual property coverage.
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders, prioritizing distinctiveness during brand creation significantly reduces registration risks and enforcement costs. Companies should avoid descriptive or generic terms that lack inherent distinctiveness, as these face higher refusal rates at offices like the EUIPO and BOIP. Instead, adopting arbitrary or fanciful names strengthens legal standing and facilitates broader protection against infringement. This approach not only secures immediate registration but also builds a defensible portfolio capable of supporting global expansion. Legal teams must conduct thorough clearance searches to ensure chosen marks do not conflict with existing rights while maintaining sufficient uniqueness to stand out in the marketplace.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["本文以护肤品牌"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Distinctive trademarks like YUN enhance brand identity by creating memorable associations with product quality and company reputation.", "Unique, non-descriptive marks are easier to register and enforce, providing stronger legal barriers against confusingly similar competitor names.", "Strong trademark protection supports long-term commercial growth by securing valuable brand assets and maintaining clear market differentiation."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritizing inherent distinctiveness during brand development minimizes registration refusals and strengthens enforcement capabilities against infringers.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches focusing on phonetic and conceptual similarities to ensure marks are sufficiently unique for registration.", "What to monitor: Track opposition trends at the EUIPO and BOIP regarding descriptive versus distinctive marks to refine filing strategies and risk assessments."]
core_points_en: Distinctive trademarks like YUN enhance brand identity by creating memorable associations with product quality and company reputation.
Unique, non-descriptive marks are easier to register and enforce, providing stronger legal barriers against confusingly similar competitor names.
Strong trademark protection supports long-term commercial growth by securing valuable brand assets and maintaining clear market differentiation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritizing inherent distinctiveness during brand development minimizes registration refusals and strengthens enforcement capabilities against infringers.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches focusing on phonetic and conceptual similarities to ensure marks are sufficiently unique for registration.
What to monitor: Track opposition trends at the EUIPO and BOIP regarding descriptive versus distinctive marks to refine filing strategies and risk assessments.

items.title: Clinical Trial Inventions In The Squeeze: The New Transparency Rules For Clinical Trials In The EU And The Assessment Of Patentability By The EPO And The UPC, Epi Information, 1/2026 – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 欧盟临床试验新规挤压下发明的可专利性评估
items.title_en: Clinical Trial Inventions Under Pressure: EU Transparency Rules and Patentability Assessments by the EPO and UPC
items.summary: The article examines how the EU’s new transparency framework for clinical trials affects patentability assessments for inventions arising in the clinical
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨欧盟临床试验新透明度框架如何影响相关发明的可专利性评估。作者从监管与专利法双视角，分析试验注册等披露何时构成现有技术，并阐释其与欧洲专利局判例及统一专利法院首批裁决的互动。文章进一步提供实务指引，建议协调监管时间表与申请策略，以稳固技术效果与权利要求范围。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines how the European Union’s new transparency framework for clinical trials impacts the patentability of inventions developed during the clinical phase. From both regulatory and patent law perspectives, the authors analyze where and when disclosures, such as trial registrations and associated documents, may constitute prior art. The analysis explores the interaction between these disclosure obligations and established case law from the European Patent Office (EPO), alongside recent decisions from the Unified Patent Court (UPC). Based on these findings, the piece offers practical guidance for aligning regulatory timelines and publication duties with filing strategies. This approach aims to ensure that technical effects are robustly supported and claim scopes remain secure, helping rights holders navigate the complex intersection of regulatory compliance and intellectual property protection in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧开展临床试验时，须警惕注册信息提前公开导致专利失效。建议重新审视欧洲布局节奏，确保在强制披露前完成关键申请，并利用统一专利法院新判例优化维权策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants conducting clinical trials in the EU must carefully manage the timing of regulatory disclosures to prevent premature public availability of data from destroying patent novelty. It is critical to review European portfolio strategies, ensuring that key patent applications are filed before mandatory transparency requirements trigger public access to trial information. Additionally, rights holders should leverage recent Unified Patent Court decisions to refine enforcement strategies. By synchronizing regulatory compliance with intellectual property planning, companies can mitigate risks associated with accidental prior art creation while maintaining robust protection for their innovations across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新规下试验披露可能构成现有技术并影响新颖性", "需结合欧洲专利局判例与统一专利法院最新裁决", "应协调监管公开义务与专利申请策略以规避风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trial disclosures under new EU transparency rules may constitute prior art, potentially jeopardizing the novelty of related patent applications.", "Assessments must integrate established EPO case law with emerging jurisprudence from the Unified Patent Court regarding early disclosures.", "Applicants should coordinate regulatory publication schedules with patent filing strategies to safeguard technical effects and claim scope."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕临床试验注册数据提前公开破坏新颖性", "调整欧洲专利申请时机以匹配监管披露节点"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure critical patent applications are filed before mandatory clinical trial registrations become publicly accessible to preserve novelty.", "Practice note: Align European patent filing timelines with regulatory disclosure nodes to avoid inadvertent self-collision with prior art.", "What to monitor: Track evolving Unified Patent Court rulings on the impact of regulatory transparency on patent validity and enforcement."]

Assessments must integrate established EPO case law with emerging jurisprudence from the Unified Patent Court regarding early disclosures.
Applicants should coordinate regulatory publication schedules with patent filing strategies to safeguard technical effects and claim scope.

What to monitor: Track evolving Unified Patent Court rulings on the impact of regulatory transparency on patent validity and enforcement.

items.title: Businesses can handle UKIPO fee hike: lawyers
items.title_zh: 律师称企业可应对英国知识产权局费用上调
items.title_en: Lawyers Assert UKIPO Remains Competitive Despite Planned Fee Increases
items.summary: UKIPO will remain a competitive option as long as efficient service continues
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对英国知识产权局（UKIPO）计划上调官费一事，法律界人士指出，只要该机构能持续提供高效服务，其作为知识产权申请地的竞争力将得以维持。业界普遍认为，尽管成本增加，但英国市场的重要性及 UKIPO 的服务效率足以让企业消化此次费用调整，不会导致申请量大幅流失。
items.ai_summary_en: Legal professionals indicate that the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) will retain its status as a competitive jurisdiction for intellectual property filings, provided it maintains high standards of service efficiency. Although the office plans to implement new fee increases, industry consensus suggests that the strategic importance of the UK market and the perceived value of UKIPO’s operational speed are sufficient for businesses to absorb these additional costs. Consequently, significant migration of application volumes to alternative jurisdictions is not anticipated. Rights holders are expected to view the fee adjustment as a manageable operational expense rather than a deterrent, ensuring continued engagement with the UK system while prioritizing service quality over marginal cost differences in their global IP strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英布局 IP 的中国企业需重新核算维护成本，但不必因费用微调而动摇战略。建议重点关注 UKIPO 审查时效变化，确保在高成本环境下依然获得高质量的确权服务，优化预算分配。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating within the UK should conduct a thorough review of their portfolio maintenance budgets to account for the upcoming fee adjustments. While the cost increase warrants financial recalibration, it does not necessitate a fundamental shift in broader intellectual property strategy. Organizations should prioritize monitoring changes in examination timelines and procedural efficiency at the UKIPO. Ensuring that higher fees correspond with consistent or improved service quality is essential for securing robust rights confirmation. This approach allows companies to optimize budget allocation effectively while maintaining strong legal protection in this key European market.

items.title: Proportionality considerations in proceedings before the UPC
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院诉讼中的比例原则考量分析
items.title_en: UPC Proportionality Analysis: Injunctions as Standard Remedies with Limited Discretion
items.summary: UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析 UPC 在禁令救济中是否适用比例原则。尽管《统一专利法院协议》规定法院“可”授予禁令，但上诉院在 Belkin 诉 Philips 案中明确，纠正措施是侵权的常规后果而非自由裁量结果。仅在侵权轻微或被控方愿整改等特定情形下，才可能因不成比例而拒绝。举证责任主要由被控侵权人承担，需证明缺乏比例性及第三方利益受损。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines whether the Unified Patent Court applies proportionality principles when granting injunctions. Although the UPC Agreement states courts may grant injunctions, the Court of Appeal clarified in Belkin v. Philips that corrective measures are standard consequences of infringement rather than discretionary outcomes. Denial is exceptional, reserved for minor infringements or where infringers actively remedy violations. The burden of proof lies primarily with alleged infringers to demonstrate disproportionality and potential harm to third-party interests. This interpretation aligns with the Enforcement Directive, ensuring high intellectual property protection by making corrective measures the norm.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼时，不能仅依赖比例原则抗辩来规避禁令。应提前评估产品侵权风险，一旦涉诉需积极举证证明整改意愿及能力，并准备应对高昂的举证责任，避免默认承担不利后果。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation in Europe should not rely solely on proportionality defenses to avoid injunctions. Early assessment of product infringement risks is critical. Once involved in proceedings, parties must proactively provide evidence of their willingness and ability to modify products to non-infringing states. Given the high evidentiary threshold, companies must prepare robust documentation to counter claims of disproportionality. Passive strategies are likely to fail, as courts prioritize effective IP enforcement over defendant convenience unless significant third-party harms are proven.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["纠正措施通常为侵权必然结果，法院无广泛自由裁量权拒绝授予", "仅当侵权轻微或被控方主动整改时，才可能认定措施不成比例", "被控侵权人负有证明措施不成比例及愿意消除侵权性质的举证责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Corrective measures are typically mandatory consequences of patent infringement, limiting judicial discretion to deny them broadly.", "Proportionality defenses succeed only in specific scenarios involving minor infringements or active remediation efforts by defendants.", "Alleged infringers bear the heavy burden of proving disproportionate impact and demonstrating willingness to eliminate infringing features."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["比例原则抗辩门槛高，企业需主动举证整改意愿而非被动等待", "纠正措施为常态，出口企业应建立快速响应机制以消除侵权状态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders benefit from predictable enforcement norms, while defendants face higher hurdles to block corrective measures.", "Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms to assess modification feasibility and document remediation capabilities immediately upon notice.", "What to monitor: Track emerging case law defining 'minor infringement' thresholds and acceptable timelines for converting infringing embodiments."]
core_points_en: Corrective measures are typically mandatory consequences of patent infringement, limiting judicial discretion to deny them broadly.
Proportionality defenses succeed only in specific scenarios involving minor infringements or active remediation efforts by defendants.
Alleged infringers bear the heavy burden of proving disproportionate impact and demonstrating willingness to eliminate infringing features.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders benefit from predictable enforcement norms, while defendants face higher hurdles to block corrective measures.
Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms to assess modification feasibility and document remediation capabilities immediately upon notice.
What to monitor: Track emerging case law defining 'minor infringement' thresholds and acceptable timelines for converting infringing embodiments.

items.title: Tech in-house counsel rub shoulders at licensing event in Rome
items.title_zh: 科技企业法务齐聚罗马参加专利许可活动
items.title_en: Tech In-House Counsel Gather in Rome for Licensing Event Highlighting Patent Pool Value
items.summary: Explaining to your teenage kids what the patent pools do is tricky, says Via’s president, but high-tech rights holders have fully bought in to the message, finds Sarah Speight.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道了在罗马举行的技术许可活动，汇聚了众多企业内部法务人员。Via 公司总裁指出，向公众解释专利池运作虽具挑战，但高科技领域的权利人已充分认可其价值。该活动反映了欧洲技术市场在专利授权与组合管理方面的活跃动态，凸显了行业对标准化许可模式的接纳。
items.ai_summary_en: This report covers a recent technology licensing event held in Rome, which brought together numerous corporate legal counsel and industry professionals. Via’s president noted that while explaining the complex mechanics of patent pools to the general public remains challenging, high-tech rights holders have fully embraced their strategic value. The gathering underscores the vibrant dynamics within the European technology market regarding patent licensing and portfolio management. It highlights a growing industry consensus on the effectiveness of standardized licensing models, reflecting increased engagement from key market participants in navigating intellectual property frameworks and fostering collaborative approaches to technology commercialization across borders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展时需关注专利池动态，积极参与许可谈判。建议加强内部法务对专利组合的管理能力，利用此类平台建立合作网络，规避潜在侵权风险并提升授权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor developments within major patent pools, as these structures increasingly define licensing norms and risk profiles. Active participation in licensing negotiations is essential to mitigate potential infringement liabilities and secure favorable terms. Furthermore, strengthening internal legal teams' capabilities in managing diverse patent portfolios will enhance operational efficiency. Leveraging industry platforms for networking allows companies to build strategic alliances, facilitating smoother access to essential technologies and improving overall authorization processes in a competitive landscape.

items.title: Copyright & AI in the UK: The Debate Rolls On
items.title_zh: 英国版权与人工智能：争议持续，立法暂缓
items.title_en: UK Copyright and AI Report: Legislative Action Deferred Pending Further Evidence
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布版权与人工智能报告，虽回应了行业咨询，但未提出具体立法提案。政府决定推迟关键决策，需进一步收集证据，维持现状。报告提及将参考欧盟模式，但暂无明确时间表，各方需关注后续判例及国际动态。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Government has published its long-awaited Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence, fulfilling obligations under the Data (Use and Access) Act. Following a high-engagement consultation period from late 2024 to early 2025, the report addresses intense lobbying from both creative industries and the AI sector. However, it offers no concrete legislative proposals or immediate changes to UK copyright law. Key decisions have been deferred as the government seeks further evidence before considering statutory interventions. While the document outlines a general direction regarding potential future frameworks, including references to international models like the EU’s approach, it provides no specific timelines for updates. Consequently, the current legal status quo remains intact, leaving significant uncertainty for stakeholders awaiting regulatory clarity on data usage and remuneration mechanisms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英中企应维持现有版权合规体系，密切关注 Getty v Stability 等判例。鉴于立法不确定性，建议提前布局数据授权机制，并评估欧盟规则差异对跨境业务的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: In the absence of imminent statutory reform, market participants should maintain robust existing copyright compliance frameworks. Reliance on contractual licensing and data authorization mechanisms becomes critical to mitigate risks during this legislative window. Stakeholders are advised to track significant judicial outcomes, such as Getty Images v Stability AI, which will likely shape interim interpretations of fair use and text-and-data mining exceptions. Furthermore, given the report’s reference to international models, companies should assess how diverging approaches between the UK and EU regimes may impact cross-border data training strategies and operational compliance requirements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府发布报告但未承诺立即修改版权法以规范 AI", "关键议题被搁置，政府计划收集更多证据后再行立法", "权利人与开发者需持续关注判例法及国际政策演变"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Government released its Copyright and AI Report but declined to propose immediate legislative amendments to regulate artificial intelligence activities.", "Critical policy questions were deferred, with authorities opting to gather additional evidence before pursuing any formal statutory changes.", "Rights holders and AI developers must closely monitor evolving case law and international regulatory developments during this legislative pause."]

core_points_en: The UK Government released its Copyright and AI Report but declined to propose immediate legislative amendments to regulate artificial intelligence activities.
Critical policy questions were deferred, with authorities opting to gather additional evidence before pursuing any formal statutory changes.
Rights holders and AI developers must closely monitor evolving case law and international regulatory developments during this legislative pause.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Maintain current copyright compliance systems and rely on contractual data licenses to manage risk during the legislative gap.
Practice note: Evaluate differences between UK and EU rules to optimize cross-border data training strategies and ensure regulatory alignment.
What to monitor: Track key judicial precedents like Getty Images v Stability AI and ongoing international policy shifts affecting AI data usage.

items.title: Enforcement Era Of The Data Act Begins: What It Means For Dispute Resolution
items.title_zh: 欧盟《数据法案》执法时代开启：对争议解决的影响
items.title_en: Enforcement Era Of The Data Act Begins: What It Means For Dispute Resolution
items.summary: On 12 September 2025, the EU Data Act (Regulation (EU) 2023/2854) became applicable across the EU. Finland’s complementary enforcement legislation, the Act on the Supervision of Data Management and Data Sharing (laki datan hallinnan ja jakamisen valvonnasta 1148/2025), entered into force on 1 January 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟《数据法案》于2025年9月12日生效，芬兰配套执法立法于2026年1月1日生效。该法案规定连接产品数据需免费、结构化地提供给用户及第三方，并允许在涉及商业秘密时暂停共享。芬兰交通和通信局（Traficom）为监管机构，已处理首起跨境投诉，涉及瑞典数据持有者拒绝向芬兰公司提供数据。
items.ai_insight_zh: 企业需关注《数据法案》下数据共享义务与商业秘密保护的平衡机制。鉴于Traficom已启动执法程序，涉及连接产品的制造商及数据持有者应审查其数据访问协议及保密措施，以应对潜在的跨境合规风险及监管调查。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟《数据法案》确立连接产品数据访问规则，要求数据以免费、结构化格式提供", "若数据共享涉及商业秘密且未达成保密措施，数据持有者可暂停共享并通知主管机构", "芬兰Traficom作为主要监管当局，已处理首例涉及瑞典数据持有者的跨境数据共享投诉"]

items.title: EUIPO’s 2024 Examination Guidelines – two years on, NFTs, digital assets and geographical indications
items.title_zh: EUIPO 2024 审查指南：数字资产、NFT 及地理标志两年回顾
items.title_en: EUIPO 2024 Guidelines Review: Digital Evidence Standards, NFTs, and Geographical Indications
items.summary: The past year has seen fewer substantive updates in EU trade mark practice, with the 2025 revisions primarily focused on design law. For...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回顾 EUIPO 自 2024 年 3 月生效的商标审查指南，重点分析数字证据提交新规。指南明确维基百科等可编辑网站截图不能单独作为使用证据，需结合社交媒体元数据、日期及地域佐证。同时澄清了基于公共政策拒绝注册的语境化判断标准，并引用最新上诉案例予以印证。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis reviews the practical impact of the EUIPO’s 2024 Examination Guidelines, which entered into force in March 2024. While recent revisions prioritize design law, the trademark guidelines remain crucial for procedural clarity and digital asset classification. A key focus is the stricter standards for submitting digital evidence. The guidelines explicitly state that printouts from editable platforms like Wikipedia are insufficient as standalone proof of genuine use. Instead, rights holders must provide robust corroboration, such as social media screenshots with verified publication dates, URLs, and analytics data. Recent Appeal Board decisions reinforce these requirements, rejecting unreliable web extracts. Additionally, the text clarifies how public policy refusals should be assessed contextually based on public perception at the time of filing, offering critical guidance for navigating modern trademark registration challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权或应诉时，须升级证据收集策略，避免依赖单一网络截图。应系统保存带有时间戳和地域指标的社交媒体数据，并关注公共道德条款的动态解释，以防注册受阻。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in the EU must upgrade their evidence collection strategies to meet the EUIPO’s heightened standards for digital proof. Relying solely on simple web screenshots is no longer sufficient for defending against non-use cancellations or supporting applications. Practitioners should implement systematic protocols to capture comprehensive digital evidence chains, ensuring all social media submissions include verifiable timestamps, geographic indicators, and analytical data. Furthermore, applicants must closely monitor evolving case law regarding public policy grounds for refusal. Understanding the dynamic interpretation of moral and public order clauses is essential to anticipate compliance risks and prevent unexpected registration obstacles in the current legal landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["可编辑网站如维基百科的打印件不可单独作为商标真实使用证据", "社交媒体证据需附带截图、发布日期、URL 及分析数据以增强可信度", "公共政策驳回需结合申请时相关公众的认知进行语境化评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Editable website printouts, such as Wikipedia extracts, cannot serve as standalone probative evidence of genuine use.", "Social media evidence requires corroborating metadata, including screenshots, publication dates, URLs, and file analytics.", "Public policy refusals require a contextual assessment based on the relevant public's perception at the time of application."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["摒弃单一网页截图，构建包含元数据的完整数字证据链", "密切跟踪公共政策条款判例，预判商标注册的合规风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must abandon reliance on single web screenshots and build complete digital evidence chains with metadata.", "Practice note: Systematically preserve social media data with timestamps and geographic indicators to ensure admissibility.", "What to monitor: Track evolving case law on public policy clauses to preemptively assess trademark registration compliance risks."]
core_points_en: Editable website printouts, such as Wikipedia extracts, cannot serve as standalone probative evidence of genuine use.
Social media evidence requires corroborating metadata, including screenshots, publication dates, URLs, and file analytics.
Public policy refusals require a contextual assessment based on the relevant public's perception at the time of application.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must abandon reliance on single web screenshots and build complete digital evidence chains with metadata.
Practice note: Systematically preserve social media data with timestamps and geographic indicators to ensure admissibility.
What to monitor: Track evolving case law on public policy clauses to preemptively assess trademark registration compliance risks.

items.title: ‘No preferred option’: UK govt backtracks on AI copyright regime
items.title_zh: 英政府撤回 AI 版权方案：暂无首选选项
items.title_en: UK Government Retreats on AI Copyright Framework, Declaring No Preferred Regulatory Option
items.summary: A future opt-out has not been ruled out, but practitioners warn that the UK could fall behind in the AI race
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府在人工智能版权制度设计上出现倒退，明确表示目前“没有首选方案”。尽管未来仍不排除引入某种形式的选择退出机制，但政策不确定性增加。业界警告称，若缺乏清晰规则，英国可能在全球人工智能竞争中落后，影响其作为创新中心的地位及相关法律确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has effectively stepped back from establishing a definitive copyright regime for artificial intelligence, explicitly stating that no preferred regulatory option currently exists. While authorities have not entirely ruled out the potential introduction of an opt-out mechanism for rights holders in the future, the immediate policy landscape remains characterized by significant uncertainty. This lack of clarity has prompted warnings from legal practitioners and industry stakeholders. They caution that without clear, predictable rules governing data usage and training, the UK risks falling behind in the global AI race. Such a delay could undermine the nation's status as a leading innovation hub and create detrimental legal ambiguity for technology developers and content creators operating within the jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕英国版权政策真空期带来的合规风险，避免盲目依赖数据训练豁免。建议密切关注后续立法动向，提前评估内容资产授权链条，必要时调整在英 AI 研发部署策略以规避潜在侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should anticipate a period of regulatory vacuum regarding AI copyright compliance in the UK. Reliance on broad data training exemptions carries inherent infringement risks given the shifting political stance. Rights holders and technology firms must proactively assess their content licensing chains and internal IP reserves to mitigate exposure. It is advisable to maintain flexible operational strategies that can adapt quickly to emerging legislative developments. Continuous monitoring of UKIPO signals and parliamentary debates is essential to navigate this uncertain environment and protect commercial interests against potential future liability claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府否认当前有偏好的 AI 版权监管方案", "未来仍可能保留某种形式的数据训练选择退出权", "从业者担忧政策滞后致英国丧失 AI 竞争优势"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK government denies having a preferred regulatory approach for AI-related copyright issues at this time.", "A future opt-out right for data training purposes remains a possibility but is not currently guaranteed.", "Practitioners warn that policy delays may cause the UK to lose its competitive advantage in the AI sector."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国政策窗口期，动态调整数据合规策略", "强化自有版权储备，降低对外部数据训练依赖"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the UK policy window closely and dynamically adjust data compliance strategies to address regulatory gaps.", "Practice note: Strengthen proprietary copyright reserves to reduce dependency on external data sources for AI training.", "What to monitor: Legislative updates regarding potential opt-out mechanisms and shifts in the UK's AI copyright stance."]
core_points_en: The UK government denies having a preferred regulatory approach for AI-related copyright issues at this time.
A future opt-out right for data training purposes remains a possibility but is not currently guaranteed.
Practitioners warn that policy delays may cause the UK to lose its competitive advantage in the AI sector.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the UK policy window closely and dynamically adjust data compliance strategies to address regulatory gaps.
Practice note: Strengthen proprietary copyright reserves to reduce dependency on external data sources for AI training.
What to monitor: Legislative updates regarding potential opt-out mechanisms and shifts in the UK's AI copyright stance.

items.title: Reel and Hogan Lovells deflect damages suit in dispute with Fives ELC
items.title_zh: Reel 与 Hogan Lovells 成功驳回 Fives ELC 索赔诉请
items.title_en: Reel and Hogan Lovells Successfully Deflect Damages Claim in French Patent Dispute with Fives ELC
items.summary: French mechanical engineering company Fives ECL and competitor Reel are fighting over EP 17 40 740 B1. It protects a compact service module for installations for the electrolytic production of aluminium. The patent expired in March 2025. The parties, who operate in the market for specialised cranes used in aluminium smelters as part of the […] The post Reel and Hogan Lovells deflect damages suit in dispute with Fives ELC appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国机械工程公司 Fives ELC 与竞争对手 Reel 就欧洲专利 EP 17 40 740 B1 发生纠纷，该专利保护铝电解生产装置的紧凑服务模块，已于 2025 年 3 月到期。双方主要在铝冶炼专用起重机市场竞争。最新进展显示，Reel 及其代理律所 Hogan Lovells 成功抵御了 Fives ELC 提出的损害赔偿诉讼，避免了不利判决。
items.ai_summary_en: French mechanical engineering firm Fives ELC and its competitor Reel are engaged in litigation concerning European patent EP 17 40 740 B1. This patent, which protects a compact service module for installations used in the electrolytic production of aluminium, expired in March 2025. Both companies compete in the specialized market for cranes utilized within aluminium smelters. Recent developments indicate that Reel, represented by Hogan Lovells, has successfully defended against a damages suit filed by Fives ELC. The defense effectively neutralized the claim for compensation, preventing an unfavorable judgment against Reel. This outcome highlights the strategic importance of robust legal representation in navigating post-expiry patent disputes and protecting market position in highly specialized industrial sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉足重型机械或冶金设备时，需警惕专利到期前后的诉讼风险。即便专利临近过期，权利人仍可能发起损害赔偿之诉。建议提前进行自由实施分析（FTO），并储备应对策略，避免陷入高额赔偿纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders often initiate damages claims even as patents approach expiration or shortly after lapse, creating significant litigation risks for competitors. Companies operating in heavy machinery or metallurgical equipment sectors must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before market entry. Proactive preparation is essential to mitigate exposure to high-value compensation claims. Effective defense strategies should leverage procedural rules and substantive arguments regarding patent validity and infringement scope. Market participants should anticipate aggressive enforcement tactics during the twilight period of patent protection and maintain readiness to contest unjustified allegations promptly.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["涉案专利为铝电解生产装置技术，已于 2025 年 3 月正式到期失效", "Reel 方在法国市场的损害赔偿诉讼中成功防御，驳回对方诉求", "纠纷聚焦于铝冶炼专用起重机细分领域的市场竞争与专利边界"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The disputed patent covers technology for aluminium electrolysis modules and formally expired in March 2025.", "Reel successfully defended against Fives ELC's damages lawsuit in France, avoiding liability for compensation.", "The dispute centers on competitive boundaries and patent scope within the specialized aluminium smelter crane market."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专利到期前后仍是诉讼高发期，需强化出口前侵权风险排查", "面对海外索赔应积极应诉，利用程序规则有效抵御不当指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers in heavy industry face heightened litigation risk during patent expiry transitions.", "Practice note: Conduct pre-market freedom-to-operate checks and prepare robust defenses for potential damages suits.", "What to monitor: Enforcement activities by rights holders targeting post-expiry commercial exploitation of technology."]
core_points_en: The disputed patent covers technology for aluminium electrolysis modules and formally expired in March 2025.
Reel successfully defended against Fives ELC's damages lawsuit in France, avoiding liability for compensation.
The dispute centers on competitive boundaries and patent scope within the specialized aluminium smelter crane market.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers in heavy industry face heightened litigation risk during patent expiry transitions.
Practice note: Conduct pre-market freedom-to-operate checks and prepare robust defenses for potential damages suits.
What to monitor: Enforcement activities by rights holders targeting post-expiry commercial exploitation of technology.

items.title: Delayed performance and failed renegotiations: key lessons from the case of UK Global HealthCare Limited v Secretary of State for Health and Social Care
items.title_zh: 英国医疗手套案：履约延迟与谈判失败的法律启示
items.title_en: UK Global HealthCare Case: Legal Risks of Delayed Performance and Failed Renegotiations
items.summary: The judgement of Mr Justice Constable in UK Global HealthCare Limited v Secretary of State for Health and Social Care [2026] EWHC 561 offers a helpful i...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国高等法院 UK Global HealthCare 诉卫生与社会保健部一案。法院驳回被告要求退还 1535 万英镑预付款的即决判决申请，认为原告有合理前景证明被告通过扩大合同范围及非法拒收阻碍了履约。案件凸显了在重新协商条款失败时，单方变更需求可能导致弃权或禁反言风险，为复杂商业合同纠纷提供重要判例参考。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the High Court judgment in UK Global HealthCare Limited v Secretary of State for Health and Social Care. The Court dismissed the Defendant’s application for summary judgment seeking a £15.35 million refund following the Claimant’s failure to deliver medical gloves during the pandemic. The decision highlights that the Claimant had a realistic prospect of proving the Defendant prevented performance by expanding the contract scope and unlawfully rejecting goods. Furthermore, representations made during renegotiation attempts may have waived strict delivery deadlines. This case illustrates how unilateral changes and failed negotiations create significant legal uncertainty, offering critical precedents for resolving complex commercial disputes involving delayed performance and contractual variations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署紧急供应合同时，需警惕对方单方面提高标准或变更范围的行为。若遇此类情况，应书面固定证据主张“履约阻碍”，并利用禁反言原则抗辩违约指控，避免盲目承担巨额退款责任。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties entering urgent supply agreements must carefully manage requests for scope changes or standard adjustments. Unilateral demands outside the original contract terms can create defenses based on prevention of performance. Rights holders should document all communications regarding potential variations to establish evidence of waiver or estoppel. This approach helps mitigate risks of substantial refund claims when deliveries are delayed due to counterparties’ changing requirements. Clear records of negotiation positions are essential to defend against allegations of default in high-stakes commercial contracts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定被告试图扩大合同范围可能构成对履约的非法阻碍", "被告在变更谈判中的陈述可能导致其丧失原定交付期限的权利", "即便未实际交付，原告仍可能因被告毁约行为获得损害赔偿"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court found the Defendant’s attempt to expand the contract scope potentially constituted an unlawful prevention of performance.", "Representations made during variation negotiations may lead to waiver of rights or estoppel regarding original delivery deadlines.", "Even without actual delivery, the Claimant might recover damages if the Defendant’s conduct amounted to a repudiatory breach."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["遭遇单方变更需求时，务必书面记录并主张其构成履约阻碍", "利用谈判中的承诺构建禁反言抗辩，防范对方事后追究违约责任"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Document any unilateral changes to scope or standards immediately to support prevention of performance arguments.", "Practice note: Use written records of negotiation commitments to build estoppel defenses against strict deadline enforcement.", "What to monitor: Watch for judicial trends in waiving time-is-of-the-essence clauses during active renegotiation periods."]
core_points_en: The Court found the Defendant’s attempt to expand the contract scope potentially constituted an unlawful prevention of performance.
Representations made during variation negotiations may lead to waiver of rights or estoppel regarding original delivery deadlines.
Even without actual delivery, the Claimant might recover damages if the Defendant’s conduct amounted to a repudiatory breach.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Document any unilateral changes to scope or standards immediately to support prevention of performance arguments.
Practice note: Use written records of negotiation commitments to build estoppel defenses against strict deadline enforcement.
What to monitor: Watch for judicial trends in waiving time-is-of-the-essence clauses during active renegotiation periods.
