items.title: Huawei launches another major battle over streaming against Disney
items.title_zh: 华为在统一专利法院对迪士尼发起流媒体专利新诉讼
items.title_en: Huawei Shifts to Offensive Strategy, Launching Major UPC Patent Litigation Against Disney Over Streaming
items.summary: Since the launch of the UPC, Huawei has increasingly changed its role from defendant to claimant in major patent disputes. With several new lawsuits against Disney over HEVC technology, the Chinese tech group once again shows its determination to defend its own patents in Europe. Huawei filed a lawsuit against twelve companies of the Walt […] The post Huawei launches another major battle over streaming against Disney appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自统一专利法院（UPC）成立以来，华为角色从被告转向原告。近期，华为就 HEVC 技术向迪士尼旗下十二家公司提起多项新诉讼。此举彰显中国科技巨头利用 UPC 机制在欧洲积极维权、捍卫自身专利组合的战略决心，标志着其在欧知识产权策略的重大转变。
items.ai_summary_en: Since the Unified Patent Court (UPC) began operations, Huawei has strategically transitioned from a defendant to an active claimant in significant European patent disputes. The technology group recently initiated multiple lawsuits against twelve entities within the Walt Disney Company group, alleging infringement of its High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) patents related to streaming technologies. This legal action underscores Huawei’s determined effort to enforce its intellectual property rights across Europe using the new centralized judicial framework. By targeting major media and entertainment conglomerates, Huawei demonstrates a clear shift in its European IP strategy, moving away from passive defense toward proactive enforcement. This development highlights the increasing willingness of major tech firms to leverage the UPC’s jurisdiction to protect their standard-essential patent portfolios and secure licensing revenues in the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应善用 UPC 统一管辖优势，变被动为主动。建议提前布局高价值专利，建立常态化监控与诉讼应对机制，将知识产权转化为欧洲市场的核心竞争壁垒与谈判筹码。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize the UPC as a powerful tool for centralizing enforcement actions, allowing rights holders to address infringement across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously. Companies operating in Europe should consider transitioning from purely defensive postures to proactive portfolio management, leveraging unified rulings to establish industry standards and secure licensing agreements. It is advisable to conduct regular audits of high-value patent assets and establish robust monitoring systems to identify potential infringers early. By integrating litigation into broader business strategies, firms can transform intellectual property into a competitive advantage and negotiation leverage within the European digital media and technology sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为利用 UPC 机制转变角色，从被动应诉转为主动发起专利诉讼", "案件聚焦 HEVC 流媒体技术，被告涵盖迪士尼旗下十二家关联公司", "此案体现中国企业依托欧洲统一司法体系强化海外专利保护的意图"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Huawei has strategically shifted from a defensive posture to actively initiating patent litigation as a claimant before the newly established Unified Patent Court.", "The lawsuit targets twelve Disney subsidiaries for alleged infringement of HEVC streaming technology patents, marking a significant enforcement action.", "This case illustrates how major technology companies are utilizing the UPC's centralized jurisdiction to strengthen overseas patent protection strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用 UPC 统一裁判机制，降低多国诉讼成本，提升维权效率", "从防御转向进攻，通过主动诉讼确立行业地位并获取许可收益"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the UPC's unified jurisdiction to reduce multi-country litigation costs and enhance enforcement efficiency for SEP portfolios.", "Practice note: Transition from defensive responses to proactive litigation strategies to establish market position and generate licensing revenue.", "What to monitor: Track the procedural developments in this high-profile dispute to assess the UPC's approach to damages and injunctions in streaming cases."]
core_points_en: Huawei has strategically shifted from a defensive posture to actively initiating patent litigation as a claimant before the newly established Unified Patent Court.
The lawsuit targets twelve Disney subsidiaries for alleged infringement of HEVC streaming technology patents, marking a significant enforcement action.
This case illustrates how major technology companies are utilizing the UPC's centralized jurisdiction to strengthen overseas patent protection strategies.

Practice note: Transition from defensive responses to proactive litigation strategies to establish market position and generate licensing revenue.
What to monitor: Track the procedural developments in this high-profile dispute to assess the UPC's approach to damages and injunctions in streaming cases.

items.title: Amgen vs Sanofi – A chronology of the patent battle over Repatha and Praluent
items.title_zh: 安进对阵赛诺菲：Repatha 与 Praluent 专利战编年史
items.title_en: Amgen vs Sanofi – A chronology of the patent battle over Repatha and Praluent
items.summary: JUVE Patent followed the dispute between Amgen on the one side and Sanofi and Regeneron on the other over cholesterol-lowering drugs for many years. At the heart of the case are the two drugs Repatha and Praluent. While Amgen markets Repatha with the active ingredient evolocumab, Sanofi markets Praluent with the active ingredient alirocumab. Both are […] The post Amgen vs Sanofi – A chronology of the patent battle over Repatha and Praluent appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文梳理了安进与赛诺菲、再生元之间关于降胆固醇药物 Repatha 和 Praluent 的长期专利纠纷。案件核心涉及两种 PCSK9 抑制剂活性成分的专利有效性及侵权认定。该争端横跨多国司法辖区，并已成为检验欧洲统一专利法院（UPC）处理复杂生物医药专利能力的标志性案例，对行业竞争格局影响深远。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海需警惕类似 PCSK9 靶点专利雷区，提前开展 FTO 分析。建议密切关注 UPC 判例走向，利用其统一裁决机制降低多国应诉成本，制定灵活的欧洲专利布局与许可策略。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["安进与赛诺菲就两款降脂药展开多年跨国专利攻防", "案件焦点在于 evolocumab 与 alirocumab 成分专利效力", "该诉讼是观察 UPC 审理高价值医药专利案的关键窗口"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出海药企应针对热门靶点提前进行自由实施尽职调查", "利用 UPC 统一诉讼机制可有效降低欧洲多国维权成本"]

items.title: All in a name: Why ‘George Orwell’ cannot be registered as a trademark
items.title_zh: 全在名字里：为何“乔治·奥威尔”无法注册为商标
items.title_en: George Orwell Name Denied Trademark Status: Distinguishing Cultural Reference from Commercial Origin
items.summary: Following the EUIPO’s confirmation that the famous author’s name cannot be trademarked for certain goods and services, Paula Vener of Inventa examines how this decision was reached.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局确认著名作家“乔治·奥威尔”之名不得在特定商品服务上注册。文章分析指出，尽管权利人希望防止未授权商品，但欧盟商标法核心在于标识来源。若公众将该名称视为对作品内容或主题的描述性文化参考，而非商业来源标识，则依据相关法律规定必须拒绝注册。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has confirmed that the name of the renowned author George Orwell cannot be registered as a trademark for specific goods and services. This decision highlights the tension between commercial interests in licensing names and preventing unauthorized merchandise, and the fundamental function of EU trademark law. The law protects signs that identify commercial origin but refuses registration when a sign is perceived primarily as descriptive information regarding content or subject matter. In Case R2248/2019‑G, the EUIPO determined that 'GEORGE ORWELL' functions mainly as a cultural reference describing the product's theme rather than indicating its source. Consequently, applications by the Orwell estate for various classes were rejected, reinforcing that public perception of a name as informational rather than brand-related leads to refusal under EU regulations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请含名人或文化符号商标时，须警惕描述性驳回风险。建议提前评估名称在目标市场的认知属性，避免将公共文化资源误作私有品牌，必要时调整策略或补充显著性证据以应对审查。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking to register trademarks containing famous names or cultural symbols in Europe must carefully assess the risk of descriptive refusals. It is critical to evaluate how target consumers perceive such signs: whether they view them as indicators of commercial origin or merely as references to cultural content. Applicants should avoid treating public domain resources as proprietary brands without sufficient evidence of acquired distinctiveness. Proactive strategies may include adjusting filing scopes or submitting robust evidence demonstrating that the sign functions as a badge of origin in the minds of consumers, thereby mitigating potential examination objections based on descriptiveness.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["名人姓名注册需区分商业来源标识与文化内容描述功能", "若名称被理解为商品信息而非品牌，欧盟法律将拒绝注册", "奥威尔遗产方申请在多类商品注册遭拒引发法律界限讨论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trademark registration requires distinguishing between signs acting as commercial origin identifiers and those serving as descriptive cultural references.", "EU law mandates refusal if the relevant public perceives the name primarily as information about product content or subject matter rather than a brand.", "The rejection of the Orwell estate's application clarifies legal boundaries regarding the use of famous names across multiple goods and service classes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估文化符号在欧认知属性，规避描述性驳回风险", "勿将公共资源误作私有品牌，需提前布局显著性证据"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess consumer perception of cultural symbols in target markets to mitigate descriptive refusal risks during EU trademark applications.", "Practice note: Avoid treating public cultural resources as private brands; prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness to counter descriptiveness objections.", "What to monitor: EUIPO case law trends regarding the threshold for distinguishing cultural references from valid commercial origin indicators in trademark disputes."]
core_points_en: Trademark registration requires distinguishing between signs acting as commercial origin identifiers and those serving as descriptive cultural references.
EU law mandates refusal if the relevant public perceives the name primarily as information about product content or subject matter rather than a brand.
The rejection of the Orwell estate's application clarifies legal boundaries regarding the use of famous names across multiple goods and service classes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess consumer perception of cultural symbols in target markets to mitigate descriptive refusal risks during EU trademark applications.
Practice note: Avoid treating public cultural resources as private brands; prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness to counter descriptiveness objections.
What to monitor: EUIPO case law trends regarding the threshold for distinguishing cultural references from valid commercial origin indicators in trademark disputes.

items.title: BMW’s Recent Trade Mark Successes on BMU/BMV: Implications for IP Protection in the Battery and Vehicle Sectors
items.title_zh: 宝马 BMU/BMV 商标胜诉对电池汽车行业的启示
items.title_en: BMW’s BMU and BMV Trademark Victices: Strategic Implications for Battery and Vehicle IP Protection
items.summary: BMW’s trade mark victories over BMU and BMV underscore the importance of reputational strength and market convergence in the EV and battery sectors. Read more for IP strategy insights.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析宝马在英欧两地成功异议 BMU 与 BMV 商标的案例。英国知识产权局认定直接混淆及不当得利；欧盟知识产权局及上诉委员会基于宝马声誉及车辆与电池技术关联性，裁定构成联系。裁决强调短字母标志首字母权重及消费者不完美记忆原则，确立跨类保护逻辑。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines BMW’s successful oppositions against the BMU and BMV marks in the UK and EU, highlighting critical trends in intellectual property protection for the electric vehicle and battery sectors. The UKIPO found direct confusion and unfair advantage regarding BMU, while the EUIPO and Board of Appeal established a link for BMV based on BMW’s strong reputation and the functional interrelationship between vehicles and battery monitoring technology. These decisions underscore that short three-letter marks are heavily judged by their initial letters, where consumers’ imperfect recollection often leads to confusion. The rulings confirm that high-reputation brands can secure cross-class protection against functionally related goods, even when the final letters differ, provided the common prefix creates a dominant overall impression.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国新能源企业出海需警惕与知名品牌近似的短字母标识风险。即便商品分类不同，若存在功能关联且对方声誉极高，仍可能因“搭便车”被驳回。建议申请前进行深度近似检索，避免使用常见前缀组合。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants in the EV and battery industries must carefully assess the risks associated with short-letter identifiers that resemble established automotive brands. Even if goods fall under different Nice Classification classes, a functional relationship combined with a rival’s exceptional reputation can lead to refusals based on unfair advantage or dilution. Applicants should conduct comprehensive similarity searches focusing on common prefixes before filing. Avoiding combinations that mimic the distinctive initial sequences of famous marks reduces litigation exposure. This approach ensures robust IP strategies that account for the expanded scope of protection granted to renowned marks in technologically converging sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["短字母标志中首字母序列相似性足以导致混淆或建立联系", "高知名度商标可跨越商品类别，覆盖功能关联的电池监测设备", "消费者不完美记忆原则强化了对知名品牌的宽泛保护范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Similarity in the initial letter sequence of short three-letter signs is sufficient to establish confusion or a mental link between marks.", "High-reputation trademarks can extend protection across classes to cover functionally related goods like battery monitors.", "The principle of imperfect recollection strengthens broad protection for well-known brands, influencing consumer perception significantly."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免在电池及车辆周边产品使用与大牌近似的短字母组合", "跨类注册需评估功能关联性及对方品牌声誉带来的扩张风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess infringement risks for short-letter marks in EV and battery sectors, considering functional links to automotive brands.", "Practice note: Conduct deep similarity searches avoiding common prefixes of famous marks to prevent refusal based on unfair advantage.", "What to monitor: Track how tribunals apply imperfect recollection principles to short signs in cross-class oppositions involving tech convergence."]
core_points_en: Similarity in the initial letter sequence of short three-letter signs is sufficient to establish confusion or a mental link between marks.
High-reputation trademarks can extend protection across classes to cover functionally related goods like battery monitors.
The principle of imperfect recollection strengthens broad protection for well-known brands, influencing consumer perception significantly.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess infringement risks for short-letter marks in EV and battery sectors, considering functional links to automotive brands.
Practice note: Conduct deep similarity searches avoiding common prefixes of famous marks to prevent refusal based on unfair advantage.
What to monitor: Track how tribunals apply imperfect recollection principles to short signs in cross-class oppositions involving tech convergence.

items.title: How Far Can the UPC Reach? The Evolving Borders of Patent Enforcement in Europe - what will the CJEU say?
items.title_zh: UPC 管辖权边界演变：CJEU 将如何裁决其长臂效力？
items.title_en: UPC Jurisdiction Boundaries: How Will the CJEU Rule on Its Long-Arm Reach?
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）一审倾向于“长臂”管辖，受理涉及非成员国（如英国、西班牙）的侵权案。2026 年 3 月，UPC 上诉法院在 Dyson v Dreame 案中向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请初步裁决，核心争议在于 UPC 能否对非成员国被告发布临时禁令，以及授权代表是否构成中间人责任。此举将界定 UPC 跨境执法的最终边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of First Instance has increasingly adopted a 'long-arm' approach, accepting cases involving patent infringement in non-member states such as the UK and Spain. In March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal referred pivotal questions to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) in the Dyson v Dreame case. The core legal issue concerns whether the UPC possesses the authority to grant provisional measures, including injunctions, against defendants located in non-UPC and non-EU countries. Additionally, the referral examines whether authorized representatives constitute intermediaries liable for infringement in these non-member jurisdictions. This preliminary ruling will definitively establish the cross-border enforcement limits of the UPC, determining if single lawsuits can effectively impact patent rights across Europe’s broader geographic landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临 UPC 管辖范围扩大的风险，即便目标市场含非成员国也可能被卷入诉讼。建议重新评估欧洲专利布局与应诉策略，密切关注 CJEU 最终裁决，提前规划针对跨境临时禁令的合规与抗辩方案。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants face heightened exposure to expanded UPC jurisdiction, where litigation may extend beyond member states to affect business operations in non-member countries. Rights holders should reassess their European patent portfolios and litigation readiness, anticipating that single proceedings could have pan-European consequences. It is critical to monitor the upcoming CJEU decision closely, as its interpretation of jurisdictional boundaries will shape future enforcement strategies. Companies must proactively develop contingency plans for cross-border provisional measures, ensuring robust defense mechanisms are in place to address potential injunctions affecting both EU and non-EU markets simultaneously.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 一审实践已倾向接受涉及非成员国专利侵权的长臂管辖权", "上诉法院提请 CJEU 裁决能否对非成员国被告采取临时禁令措施", "案件焦点涵盖授权代表在非 UPC 国家（如西班牙）的中间人责任认定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Court of First Instance practice favors long-arm jurisdiction over patent infringement disputes involving non-member states like the UK and Spain.", "UPC Court of Appeal seeks CJEU guidance on granting provisional measures against defendants situated outside UPC member territories.", "Case focuses on determining intermediary liability for authorized representatives operating within non-UPC countries such as Spain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 UPC 长臂管辖，非成员国业务亦可能受单一诉讼波及", "需针对 CJEU 裁决预判，提前制定跨境临时禁令的应急抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-member state business activities remain vulnerable to UPC long-arm jurisdiction via single consolidated litigation actions.", "Practice note: Develop preemptive defense strategies for cross-border provisional measures ahead of the anticipated CJEU ruling.", "What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment clarifying the extent of UPC authority over non-EU defendants and intermediary liabilities."]
core_points_en: UPC Court of First Instance practice favors long-arm jurisdiction over patent infringement disputes involving non-member states like the UK and Spain.
UPC Court of Appeal seeks CJEU guidance on granting provisional measures against defendants situated outside UPC member territories.
Case focuses on determining intermediary liability for authorized representatives operating within non-UPC countries such as Spain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-member state business activities remain vulnerable to UPC long-arm jurisdiction via single consolidated litigation actions.
Practice note: Develop preemptive defense strategies for cross-border provisional measures ahead of the anticipated CJEU ruling.
What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment clarifying the extent of UPC authority over non-EU defendants and intermediary liabilities.

items.title: First German long-arm proceedings before the Regional Court of Munich I on US patents concluded early with a waiver by Onesta. BMW, represented by BARDEHLE PAGENBERG, no longer faces the risk of claims.
items.title_zh: 宝马在德国美专利长臂诉讼中胜诉，Onesta 撤诉并放弃索赔
items.title_en: Munich Court Ends First US Patent Long-Arm Suit as Onesta Waives Claims Against BMW
items.summary: BARDEHLE PAGENBERG is proud to announce another success for Bayerische Motoren Werke Aktiengesellschaft (BMW) in its legal battle against Onesta IP, LLC (Onesta).
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院终结首例针对美国专利的德国长臂诉讼。NPE 实体 Onesta 在获美国反诉禁令后，撤回对宝马的侵权起诉并不可撤销地放弃所有索赔，承担全部费用。该案测试了欧盟法院 BSH 案判决在非 EPC 专利上的适用性及全球管辖权冲突。
items.ai_summary_en: The Regional Court of Munich I has concluded the first German proceedings asserting US patents, marking a significant development in cross-border IP litigation. Non-practicing entity Onesta IP, LLC withdrew all infringement actions against Bayerische Motoren Werke AG (BMW) and irrevocably waived any substantive claims arising from the patents-in-suit. Consequently, Onesta bears the full costs of the proceedings. This outcome followed an anti-suit injunction issued by the US District Court for the Western District of Texas, which prohibited Onesta from continuing the Munich litigation based on exclusive US jurisdiction. The case tested the applicability of the CJEU’s BSH v Electrolux ruling to non-EPC patents and highlighted complex global jurisdictional conflicts. With the withdrawal, BMW is relieved of further liability risks in this specific dispute, while the strategic use of US procedural tools effectively countered the attempt to leverage European courts for US patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应警惕 NPE 利用欧盟法院新判例在欧洲发起跨国专利狙击。需善用美国反诉禁令等工具对抗不当长臂管辖，并在德美两地同步布局防御性诉讼策略以规避风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding non-practicing entities leveraging recent EU case law to initiate cross-border patent enforcement campaigns in Europe. The successful deployment of US anti-suit injunctions demonstrates their utility in countering perceived improper long-arm jurisdiction attempts. Rights holders are advised to consider synchronized defensive litigation strategies across both US and German jurisdictions to mitigate exposure. Monitoring the interplay between national courts and US federal rulings will be critical for managing global patent disputes. Effective coordination between local counsel and US legal teams can provide robust protection against fragmented or conflicting judicial outcomes in multi-jurisdictional IP conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国法院首次受理基于美国专利的侵权诉讼引发国际关注", "美国德州法院颁发反诉禁令禁止被告继续德国诉讼程序", "原告最终撤诉并放弃索赔，确认德国长臂管辖策略受阻"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["First German court acceptance of US patent infringement suits drew international attention to potential long-arm jurisdiction expansions.", "US District Court for Western District of Texas issued anti-suit injunction prohibiting continuation of Munich proceedings on US patents.", "Plaintiff withdrew claims and waived rights, confirming that German long-arm jurisdiction strategies faced significant legal obstacles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕 NPE 利用欧盟判例在欧洲发起跨国专利狙击", "善用美欧反诉禁令工具对抗不当长臂管辖策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor NPE strategies exploiting EU jurisdictional precedents for cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.", "Practice note: Utilize US anti-suit injunctions and parallel defensive filings to counter improper long-arm jurisdiction tactics.", "What to monitor: Evolving interactions between EU national courts and US federal rulings in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes."]
core_points_en: First German court acceptance of US patent infringement suits drew international attention to potential long-arm jurisdiction expansions.
US District Court for Western District of Texas issued anti-suit injunction prohibiting continuation of Munich proceedings on US patents.
Plaintiff withdrew claims and waived rights, confirming that German long-arm jurisdiction strategies faced significant legal obstacles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor NPE strategies exploiting EU jurisdictional precedents for cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.
Practice note: Utilize US anti-suit injunctions and parallel defensive filings to counter improper long-arm jurisdiction tactics.
What to monitor: Evolving interactions between EU national courts and US federal rulings in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes.

items.title: [Blog] Le marché des noms de domaine premium reste dynamique
items.title_zh: 优质域名市场在 2026 年初保持强劲活力
items.title_en: Premium Domain Market Remains Robust in Early 2026 with High-Value Transactions
items.summary: Le marché des noms de domaine premium affiche une belle santé en ce début d'année 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年初欧洲优质域名市场表现强劲，两笔重大交易印证其吸引力：Midnight.com 以 115 万美元成交，C4.com 售价 26.5 万美元。文章指出，简短、易记及描述性域名被视为数字经济中的战略投资资产，凸显了企业在数字品牌布局中对高价值域名的持续需求与认可。
items.ai_summary_en: The European premium domain name market demonstrated strong vitality at the beginning of 2026. Two significant transactions highlight the enduring appeal of these digital assets: Midnight.com was acquired for 1.15 million dollars, while C4.com was negotiated at 265,000 dollars in early January. These substantial figures underscore the lasting value the market assigns to short, memorable, evocative, or descriptive domain names. Such domains are increasingly regarded as strategic investments within the digital economy. The activity reflects sustained demand and recognition from companies regarding high-value domains in their digital brand layouts. This trend indicates that organizations continue to prioritize securing premium digital real estate to enhance brand visibility and consumer trust in competitive online markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视高端域名储备，将其纳入海外品牌战略。面对高昂的二级市场价格，建议提前布局或考虑替代方案，避免因域名缺失阻碍欧洲市场拓展。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies should integrate premium domain acquisition into their global brand strategy planning core. Given the high costs associated with secondary market purchases, organizations are advised to establish early defensive registration mechanisms. This approach helps mitigate risks related to domain speculation and ensures availability of key identifiers. Proactive portfolio management prevents potential obstacles to market expansion caused by missing or unavailable domains. Rights holders must balance immediate branding needs with long-term asset protection strategies to maintain competitive advantage in the digital landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["优质域名交易活跃，单笔金额高达百万美元级别", "简短易记域名被确认为数字经济战略投资资产", "市场健康度反映企业对数字品牌资产的重视程度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Active premium domain trading features single transaction values reaching multi-million dollar levels.", "Short and memorable domains are confirmed as strategic investment assets within the digital economy.", "Market health indicators reflect corporate emphasis on securing valuable digital brand assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["将优质域名纳入企业出海品牌战略规划核心", "警惕域名投机风险，建立早期防御性注册机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Integrate premium domain reserves into overseas brand strategy planning core.", "Practice note: Establish early defensive registration mechanisms to mitigate domain speculation risks.", "What to monitor: Secondary market pricing trends and availability of short, descriptive domain names."]

Short and memorable domains are confirmed as strategic investment assets within the digital economy.
Market health indicators reflect corporate emphasis on securing valuable digital brand assets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Integrate premium domain reserves into overseas brand strategy planning core.
Practice note: Establish early defensive registration mechanisms to mitigate domain speculation risks.
What to monitor: Secondary market pricing trends and availability of short, descriptive domain names.

items.title: Counterfeits In The Automotive Sector And Software Piracy In Poland: How Brand Protection Works In Practice
items.title_zh: 波兰汽车假冒与软件盗版：品牌保护实务解析
items.title_en: Poland Automotive Counterfeits and Software Piracy: Practical Brand Protection Strategies
items.summary: The automotive market has long been among those most exposed to counterfeits, from spare parts to diagnostic devices. The issue rarely ends with a mere loss of sales.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析波兰汽车领域假冒零件及诊断设备软件盗版现状。指出该行为不仅导致销售损失，更引发安全风险。依据波兰海关数据，2023-2024 年查获大量假冒货物。文章详述波兰法律下，商标假冒适用工业产权法刑事处罚，软件盗版适用著作权法，且常构成加重情节。强调执法需应对证据易灭失挑战，揭示品牌保护在实务中的关键步骤。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the prevalence of counterfeit automotive parts and diagnostic software piracy in Poland. It highlights that these infringements cause not only revenue loss but also significant safety risks due to faulty components and incorrect diagnostic readings. Data from Poland’s National Revenue Administration reveals substantial seizures of counterfeit goods valued at hundreds of millions of PLN in 2023 and 2024, illustrating the scale of illicit trade. The article details the Polish legal framework, where trademark counterfeiting is penalized under Industrial Property Law and software piracy under Copyright Law. Aggravated circumstances often apply, leading to stricter criminal liability. Effective enforcement requires navigating challenges related to evidence preservation, emphasizing critical steps for rights holders to protect their brands through customs recordals and criminal proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国汽配及软件企业出口波兰时，须警惕供应链中混入假冒或盗版组件风险。建议建立严格合规审查机制，避免连带刑事责任。同时应主动利用波兰海关备案与刑事打击机制，维护自身品牌权益，防范本地化侵权冲击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in or exporting to Poland must implement rigorous supply chain compliance checks to prevent the inclusion of counterfeit or pirated components, thereby avoiding potential joint criminal liability. Rights holders should actively utilize Poland’s customs recordal system and cooperate with law enforcement to initiate criminal actions against infringers. This proactive approach is essential for mitigating local infringement impacts and safeguarding brand integrity. Given the high volume of seizures, leveraging administrative and criminal enforcement mechanisms provides a robust strategy for effective brand protection in the automotive sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["汽车假冒件与盗版软件捆绑销售，构成严重安全与法律风险", "波兰海关近年查获数亿欧元假冒货物，显示侵权规模巨大", "商标假冒与软件盗版在波兰分别面临工业产权法与著作权法刑责"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Bundled sales of counterfeit auto parts and pirated diagnostic software create severe safety hazards and legal liabilities for market participants.", "Polish Customs recorded detections of counterfeit goods worth hundreds of millions of PLN recently, demonstrating the massive scale of infringement.", "Trademark counterfeiting faces criminal penalties under Industrial Property Law, while software piracy is prosecuted under Copyright Law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需严查供应链，防止假冒组件引发刑事连带责任", "善用波兰海关与刑事执法机制，主动打击本地侵权保护品牌"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must enforce strict supply chain audits to prevent counterfeit components from causing joint criminal liability.", "Practice note: Leverage Polish customs recordals and criminal enforcement mechanisms to actively combat local trademark and copyright infringement.", "What to monitor: Track seizure data and enforcement trends to adapt brand protection strategies against bundled hardware-software piracy."]
core_points_en: Bundled sales of counterfeit auto parts and pirated diagnostic software create severe safety hazards and legal liabilities for market participants.
Polish Customs recorded detections of counterfeit goods worth hundreds of millions of PLN recently, demonstrating the massive scale of infringement.
Trademark counterfeiting faces criminal penalties under Industrial Property Law, while software piracy is prosecuted under Copyright Law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must enforce strict supply chain audits to prevent counterfeit components from causing joint criminal liability.
Practice note: Leverage Polish customs recordals and criminal enforcement mechanisms to actively combat local trademark and copyright infringement.
What to monitor: Track seizure data and enforcement trends to adapt brand protection strategies against bundled hardware-software piracy.

items.title: [Blog] L'IP Box en Europe, une stratégie fiscale d'optimisation et de valorisation des actifs de PI
items.title_zh: 欧洲 IP Box：优化与增值知识产权资产的税务策略
items.title_en: European IP Box Regimes: OECD Nexus Compliance and Tax Optimization Strategies
items.summary: L'IP Box s'impose comme un levier fiscal central pour la valorisation des actifs de Propriété Intellectuelle (PI) en Europe, dans un cadre désormais harmonisé par l'approche...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧洲 IP Box 制度如何在 OECD“关联法”框架下成为知识产权资产估值的核心税务工具。法国实行 10% 优惠税率，要求研发支出可追溯；卢森堡提供高达 80% 豁免，有效税率约 5%。欧盟多国如爱尔兰、比利时等均采纳类似机制，旨在通过税收优惠绑定本土研发活动，既支持创新又限制激进避税。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines how European Patent Box regimes function as central fiscal tools for valuing intellectual property assets under the harmonized OECD 'nexus' approach. France offers a reduced 10% tax rate on net IP income from patents and software, requiring precise R&D expenditure tracking aligned with OECD standards. Luxembourg provides a highly attractive regime with an 80% exemption on net income, resulting in an effective tax rate of approximately 5%. Several other EU member states, including Ireland, Belgium, and the Netherlands, have adopted comparable mechanisms with effective rates ranging from 2.5% to 10%. These systems aim to link tax benefits directly to substantive domestic R&D activities, thereby supporting local innovation while strictly limiting aggressive tax optimization strategies through mandatory nexus ratio calculations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应评估现有 IP 架构是否符合关联法要求，利用法、卢等国差异优化税负。建议建立研发费用专项台账，确保享受优惠同时规避合规风险，提升无形资产回报率。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating within the European Union should evaluate whether their current intellectual property architectures satisfy strict OECD nexus requirements. Leveraging jurisdictional differences between France, Luxembourg, and other member states can optimize overall tax burdens while maintaining compliance. Companies are advised to establish dedicated ledgers for R&D expenses to ensure accurate calculation of nexus ratios. This approach secures eligibility for preferential tax treatments and mitigates regulatory risks, ultimately enhancing the return on investment for intangible assets across cross-border operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["OECD 关联法原则已 harmonize 欧洲各国 IP Box 制度，强调研发实质…", "法国 IP Box 对专利等净收入征收 10% 税率，需配合研发支出精细追踪", "卢森堡等国税负更低，吸引国际高价值 IP 持有结构，但均受关联比例约束"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["OECD nexus principles have harmonized European IP Box regimes, emphasizing that tax benefits must correlate with substantial, verifiable R&D activities.", "France applies a 10% reduced tax rate on patent and software income, mandating rigorous traceability of qualifying R&D expenditures.", "Luxembourg and other jurisdictions offer lower effective rates but strictly enforce nexus ratios, attracting international high-value IP holding structures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查研发支出归集逻辑，确保满足 OECD 关联比例以享税收优惠", "对比成员国税率差异，合理规划高价值专利持有地以降低整体税负"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify R&D cost allocation logic to meet OECD nexus ratios and secure available tax incentives.", "Practice note: Compare member state tax rates to strategically plan the holding location of high-value patents and reduce global tax liability.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of nexus calculations and compliance obligations across key European IP jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: OECD nexus principles have harmonized European IP Box regimes, emphasizing that tax benefits must correlate with substantial, verifiable R&D activities.
France applies a 10% reduced tax rate on patent and software income, mandating rigorous traceability of qualifying R&D expenditures.
Luxembourg and other jurisdictions offer lower effective rates but strictly enforce nexus ratios, attracting international high-value IP holding structures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify R&D cost allocation logic to meet OECD nexus ratios and secure available tax incentives.
Practice note: Compare member state tax rates to strategically plan the holding location of high-value patents and reduce global tax liability.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of nexus calculations and compliance obligations across key European IP jurisdictions.

items.title: Copyright And Champagne Labels: French Court Confirms Rejection Of The Accessory Theory And Tacit Assignment
items.title_zh: 法法院确认拒绝版权附属理论与默示转让抗辩
items.title_en: French Court Rejects Accessory Theory and Tacit Assignment in Champagne Label Copyright Dispute
items.summary: In its 20 November 2025 ruling, France's Douai Court of Appeal reinforced the strict interpretation of the author's exclusive right to exploit their work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国杜埃上诉法院裁定，香槟生产商未经授权在标签及数字媒体使用艺术家作品构成侵权。法院驳回“附属理论”抗辩，认定作品具有商业推广功能而非 incidental；同时强调艺术家多年未异议不构成经济权利的默示转让，重申了作者专有权利的严格解释原则。
items.ai_summary_en: In a ruling dated 20 November 2025, the Douai Court of Appeal upheld a copyright infringement conviction against a Champagne producer for unauthorized reproduction of an artist’s sculpture on labels, packaging, and digital media. The court firmly rejected the 'accessory theory' defense, determining that the artwork was central to the product's visual identity and commercial differentiation rather than incidental. Furthermore, the court dismissed the argument that the artist’s years of silence constituted a tacit assignment of economic rights. This decision reinforces the strict interpretation of authors' exclusive exploitation rights under French law, clarifying that long-term tolerance of use does not imply consent or transfer of rights, thereby strengthening legal protections for creators against commercial misuse.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用艺术作品时，切勿误判沉默为许可或依赖附属理论免责。务必签署书面权利转让协议，明确界定使用范围与期限，避免因商业性使用被认定为侵权而面临高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing artistic works for branding, packaging, or marketing must secure explicit written licenses rather than relying on implied permissions or the accessory theory. Silence or lack of immediate enforcement by rights holders does not equate to legal consent or rights transfer. Companies should conduct thorough due diligence to ensure all commercial uses are contractually covered, defining scope and duration clearly. Failure to formalize these agreements exposes businesses to significant infringement liabilities and damages, as courts increasingly prioritize the author's exclusive economic interests over perceived industry practices or passive acquiescence.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定作品用于商业区分策略，不适用附属理论抗辩", "艺术家长期容忍使用行为，不被视为经济权利的默示转让", "判决强化了法国司法对作者专有开发权的严格保护立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The accessory theory defense failed because the artwork served a key commercial differentiation strategy rather than appearing incidentally.", "The artist's prolonged failure to object to the usage did not establish a tacit assignment of economic rights to the Champagne house.", "The judgment reaffirms the strict judicial protection of authors' exclusive rights to exploit their works commercially in France."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["商业宣传中使用的艺术作品难获附属理论豁免保护", "权利获取必须书面化，长期默许不等于法律上的默示转让"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands must avoid assuming that passive acceptance by artists grants implicit licenses for commercial exploitation of their works.", "Practice note: Secure comprehensive written assignment or licensing agreements covering all intended media and commercial applications upfront.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends limiting the accessory theory exception when artworks contribute directly to product value and brand identity."]
core_points_en: The accessory theory defense failed because the artwork served a key commercial differentiation strategy rather than appearing incidentally.
The artist's prolonged failure to object to the usage did not establish a tacit assignment of economic rights to the Champagne house.
The judgment reaffirms the strict judicial protection of authors' exclusive rights to exploit their works commercially in France.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must avoid assuming that passive acceptance by artists grants implicit licenses for commercial exploitation of their works.
Practice note: Secure comprehensive written assignment or licensing agreements covering all intended media and commercial applications upfront.
What to monitor: Judicial trends limiting the accessory theory exception when artworks contribute directly to product value and brand identity.

items.title: A German court rules that AI-generated designs do not benefit from copyright protection | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 德国法院裁定 AI 生成设计因缺乏人类创作而不受版权保护
items.title_en: Munich Court Rules AI-Generated Logos Lack Human Creativity for Copyright Protection
items.summary: In order that a design or other work can be protected by copyright in the EU and UK, it must be original in the sense of involving a human author’s making of creative choices such that their personality finds its expression in the work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院近期裁定，三幅由 AI 生成的标志因缺乏足够的人类创造性投入而不具备原创性，无法获得版权保护。法院依据欧盟法院判例指出，作品需体现作者个性方可受保。尽管原告使用了提示词迭代，但法院认为 AI 的技术贡献超过了人类的创意选择，故不构成版权作品。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich District Court recently ruled that three AI-generated logos lacked sufficient human creative input to qualify for copyright protection. Relying on CJEU case law, the court emphasized that protected works must reflect the author's personality. Although the plaintiff used iterative prompts, the judges determined that the AI's technical contribution outweighed the human's creative choices. Consequently, the designs were deemed not original enough to constitute copyright works. This decision clarifies that merely providing instructions to an AI system does not automatically grant copyright status if the human element remains subordinate to the algorithm's output.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用 AI 辅助设计时，须保留充分的人类创意干预证据。建议建立详细的创作过程文档，确保人类对最终成果拥有决定性控制，以符合欧盟版权保护的原创性门槛，避免权利落空。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders utilizing AI for design creation in Europe should maintain comprehensive documentation of their creative process. It is crucial to evidence decisive human control over the final output to satisfy the requirement that the work reflects the author's personality. Merely relying on automated generation risks leaving creations unprotected. Companies should ensure that human modifications and specific creative decisions are distinct and significant, thereby establishing a clear chain of authorship that aligns with current judicial interpretations of originality.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI 生成内容需有人类实质性创意投入才具原创性", "单纯提供提示词不足以证明人类对作品的个性表达", "法院判定 AI 技术贡献过大导致人类创意占比不足"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI-generated content requires substantial human creative intervention to meet the originality threshold under EU copyright law.", "Providing prompts alone is insufficient to demonstrate that the final work expresses the human author's unique personality.", "The court found the AI's technical contribution exceeded human creative control, preventing the logos from qualifying as protected works."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["保留人类深度干预创作的完整证据链至关重要", "单纯依赖 AI 生成难以满足欧盟版权原创性要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Documenting deep human intervention in the creative process is essential to secure copyright eligibility for AI-assisted designs.", "Practice note: Ensure human contributions are substantive and distinct from the AI's automated output to meet the personality expression standard.", "What to monitor: Future CJEU guidance or national court decisions regarding the threshold of human control required for AI-generated works."]
core_points_en: AI-generated content requires substantial human creative intervention to meet the originality threshold under EU copyright law.
Providing prompts alone is insufficient to demonstrate that the final work expresses the human author's unique personality.
The court found the AI's technical contribution exceeded human creative control, preventing the logos from qualifying as protected works.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Documenting deep human intervention in the creative process is essential to secure copyright eligibility for AI-assisted designs.
Practice note: Ensure human contributions are substantive and distinct from the AI's automated output to meet the personality expression standard.
What to monitor: Future CJEU guidance or national court decisions regarding the threshold of human control required for AI-generated works.

items.title: Amgen and Sanofi end epic battle over Praluent and Repatha
items.title_zh: 安进与赛诺菲终结 Praluent 与 Repatha 专利史诗之战
items.title_en: Amgen and Sanofi Settle Long-Running PCSK9 Inhibitor Patent Dispute in Europe
items.summary: The dispute between Amgen on the one side and Sanofi and Regeneron on the other is probably one of the longest and most intense patent disputes in recent history. Both sides offer cholesterol-lowering drugs. Amgen sells its PCSK9 inhibitor Repatha with the active ingredient evolocumab. Sanofi and Regeneron market their drug under the brand name […] The post Amgen and Sanofi end epic battle over Praluent and Repatha appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 安进与赛诺菲及再生元之间关于降胆固醇药物 Repatha 和 Praluent 的长期专利纠纷宣告结束。该案被视为近年最激烈的专利战之一，双方最终达成和解。此动态发生在统一专利法院（UPC）运作背景下，标志着欧洲生物医药领域重大不确定性消除，对后续类似案件具有风向标意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The extensive patent litigation between Amgen, and Sanofi alongside Regeneron, concerning their respective cholesterol-lowering drugs Repatha and Praluent has concluded with a settlement. This dispute is recognized as one of the most intense and prolonged patent battles in recent history within the pharmaceutical sector. The resolution eliminates significant legal uncertainty for these key biologic medicines in the European market. Occurring against the backdrop of the newly operational Unified Patent Court (UPC), this outcome provides critical clarity for stakeholders. It serves as a notable reference point for future complex multinational patent disputes under the UPC framework, signaling how high-stakes biopharmaceutical conflicts may be resolved through negotiation rather than extended judicial proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海应警惕高价值靶点专利雷区，提前开展 FTO 分析。在 UPC 新机制下，单一诉讼可能波及多国，建议企业建立欧洲专利风险预警机制，并评估通过许可或和解快速解决争议的商业价值。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders entering the European market, particularly in high-value therapeutic areas like biologics, conducting thorough Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses prior to launch is essential to mitigate infringement risks. The Unified Patent Court's structure means that a single action can impact multiple jurisdictions simultaneously, increasing the strategic importance of early risk assessment. Companies should establish robust European patent watch mechanisms to detect potential threats early. Furthermore, evaluating the commercial viability of licensing or settlement options remains crucial, as resolving disputes quickly can preserve market stability and avoid the substantial costs associated with protracted multinational litigation under the new court regime.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["安进与赛诺菲终结长达多年的 PCSK9 抑制剂专利争端", "双方和解消除了欧洲市场关键生物药的法律不确定性", "此案为 UPC 管辖下的复杂跨国专利诉讼提供重要参考"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Amgen and Sanofi have ended their multi-year patent conflict regarding PCSK9 inhibitor technologies through a mutual settlement agreement.", "The settlement resolves major legal uncertainties surrounding essential biologic treatments in the European pharmaceutical market.", "This case offers vital precedential value for managing complex cross-border patent litigation under the new Unified Patent Court system."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["高价值生物药出海需前置 FTO 分析以规避侵权风险", "利用 UPC 机制特点，灵活制定诉讼或和解应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: High-value biologic entrants must prioritize pre-launch FTO analysis to navigate dense patent landscapes effectively.", "Practice note: Leverage UPC characteristics to formulate flexible strategies balancing aggressive litigation with pragmatic settlement opportunities.", "What to monitor: Develop proactive European patent risk warning systems to identify and address potential cross-border infringement claims early."]
core_points_en: Amgen and Sanofi have ended their multi-year patent conflict regarding PCSK9 inhibitor technologies through a mutual settlement agreement.
The settlement resolves major legal uncertainties surrounding essential biologic treatments in the European pharmaceutical market.
This case offers vital precedential value for managing complex cross-border patent litigation under the new Unified Patent Court system.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: High-value biologic entrants must prioritize pre-launch FTO analysis to navigate dense patent landscapes effectively.
Practice note: Leverage UPC characteristics to formulate flexible strategies balancing aggressive litigation with pragmatic settlement opportunities.
What to monitor: Develop proactive European patent risk warning systems to identify and address potential cross-border infringement claims early.

items.title: Generics suspect GSK of playing “divisional game” after EPO revocation
items.title_zh: 仿制药企疑 GSK 在 EPO 撤销后玩弄分案游戏
items.title_en: Generics Accuse GSK of Divisional Strategy After EPO Revokes Vilanterol Dosing Patent
items.summary: At the centre of the current dispute is GlaxoSmithKline’s dosing patent EP 2 400 950. It protects the active ingredient vilanterol in a dosage of 25µg and 50µg daily for the treatment of respiratory diseases such as asthma and COPD. In combination with other active ingredients, vilanterol is included in several of GSK’s best-selling products […] The post Generics suspect GSK of playing “divisional game” after EPO revocation appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 葛兰素史克（GSK）治疗哮喘和慢阻肺的维兰特罗剂量专利 EP 2 400 950 遭 EPO 撤销。该专利保护每日 25µg 和 50µg 剂量。随后，仿制药企指责 GSK 利用分案申请策略（divisional game）试图延续垄断地位。此争议凸显了原研药企在核心专利失效后，通过程序性手段维持市场排他性的复杂博弈。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has revoked GlaxoSmithKline’s dosing patent EP 2 400 950, which protected specific daily doses of vilanterol for treating asthma and COPD. Following this decision, generic manufacturers have accused the originator of engaging in a strategic divisional application campaign to maintain market exclusivity. The dispute centers on allegations that the pharmaceutical giant is utilizing procedural mechanisms to extend protection beyond the intended scope after the core patent was invalidated. This situation highlights the complex interplay between patent validity challenges and subsequent divisional filings by originators seeking to preserve commercial advantages against generic entry in the European respiratory medicine market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出口欧洲需警惕原研药企的分案策略陷阱，密切监控 EPO 审查动态。在发起无效挑战或准备上市时，应全面排查目标药物的分案家族，评估潜在诉讼风险，避免陷入长期法律拉锯战。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should conduct comprehensive freedom-to-operate analyses that explicitly map out entire divisional patent families associated with target drugs in Europe. Relying solely on the status of granted patents may overlook pending divisional applications designed to create new barriers to entry. Companies preparing for launch or invalidity actions must assess the risk of prolonged litigation stemming from these sequential filings. Proactive monitoring of EPO examination trends regarding divisional practices is essential to anticipate potential enforcement moves and develop robust defensive strategies against extended exclusivity claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["GSK 维兰特罗剂量专利被 EPO 正式撤销，核心保护范围失效", "仿制药企指控 GSK 利用分案申请策略不当延长专利保护期", "争议焦点在于原研药企是否滥用程序规则阻碍仿制药上市"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO formally revoked GSK's key vilanterol dosing patent, effectively nullifying its core protective scope for specific daily dosage regimens.", "Generic competitors allege that GSK is employing divisional application strategies to improperly extend patent protection periods after revocation.", "The central controversy involves whether originators abuse procedural rules to delay generic market entry following the loss of primary patent rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口前须深度检索目标药物在欧洲的分案专利申请家族", "关注 EPO 对分案策略的审查趋势，提前制定无效应对预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct deep searches of divisional patent families for target drugs before entering the European market to identify hidden risks.", "Practice note: Monitor EPO examination trends on divisional strategies and prepare invalidity countermeasures early in the lifecycle.", "What to monitor: Watch for new divisional filings by originators after main patent revocations that could signal attempts to block generic entry."]
core_points_en: The EPO formally revoked GSK's key vilanterol dosing patent, effectively nullifying its core protective scope for specific daily dosage regimens.
Generic competitors allege that GSK is employing divisional application strategies to improperly extend patent protection periods after revocation.
The central controversy involves whether originators abuse procedural rules to delay generic market entry following the loss of primary patent rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct deep searches of divisional patent families for target drugs before entering the European market to identify hidden risks.
Practice note: Monitor EPO examination trends on divisional strategies and prepare invalidity countermeasures early in the lifecycle.
What to monitor: Watch for new divisional filings by originators after main patent revocations that could signal attempts to block generic entry.

items.title: UPC update: CJEU set for UPC role | All along the Washtower
items.title_zh: UPC 更新：欧洲法院确立统一专利法院角色
items.title_en: UPC Update: CJEU Defines Role in Unified Patent Court System Amidst Juicer Dispute and Judicial Changes
items.summary: The latest in a dispute over juicing machines, and a shakeup in judicial compositions were also among the top developments
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道统一专利法院（UPC）最新进展，核心聚焦欧洲法院（CJEU）在 UPC 体系中的职能定位。此外，文章还涵盖了榨汁机专利纠纷的最新诉讼动态，以及 UPC 内部法官组成的重大调整。这些事件共同反映了 UPC 运行初期的司法实践挑战与制度完善过程，对理解欧洲专利诉讼格局至关重要。
items.ai_summary_en: This article reports on the latest developments concerning the Unified Patent Court (UPC), with a primary focus on clarifying the judicial review role of the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) within the UPC framework. Additionally, it covers recent litigation dynamics in a high-profile patent dispute involving juicing machines, which serves as a key test case for infringement standards. The piece also details significant adjustments to the judicial composition within the UPC. These concurrent events highlight the practical challenges and institutional refinement processes occurring during the UPC's early operational phase, offering critical insights for understanding the evolving landscape of European patent litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注 CJEU 对 UPC 的判例指引，这将决定专利无效与侵权的最终标准。建议企业重新评估在欧专利布局风险，针对法官组成变化调整诉讼策略，并提前做好应对跨境专利纠纷的准备。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor CJEU precedents guiding UPC decisions, as these will ultimately define standards for patent validity and infringement. It is advisable to reassess European patent portfolio risks in light of these evolving judicial interpretations. Furthermore, stakeholders should adapt their litigation strategies to account for shifts in judicial composition, ensuring preparedness for potential cross-border disputes. Understanding these procedural and substantive developments is essential for effective risk management and strategic planning in the new European patent enforcement environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲法院明确其在统一专利法院体系中的司法审查角色", "榨汁机专利纠纷案件推动 UPC 侵权判定规则的具体化", "UPC 法庭法官组成发生变动，可能影响未来案件审理走向"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU has clarified its specific judicial review function within the broader Unified Patent Court legal architecture.", "Ongoing litigation regarding juicing machine patents is helping to concretize UPC infringement determination rules.", "Changes in the UPC bench composition may significantly influence the direction and outcomes of future case adjudications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["跟踪 CJEU 判例以预判 UPC 裁判尺度，规避侵权风险", "关注法官人员变动，针对性优化诉讼团队与应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor CJEU case law to anticipate UPC adjudication standards and mitigate infringement exposure.", "Practice note: Track judicial personnel changes to optimize litigation teams and tailor defense or enforcement strategies.", "What to monitor: Evolving intersection between CJEU guidance and UPC first-instance rulings on validity and infringement."]
core_points_en: The CJEU has clarified its specific judicial review function within the broader Unified Patent Court legal architecture.
Ongoing litigation regarding juicing machine patents is helping to concretize UPC infringement determination rules.
Changes in the UPC bench composition may significantly influence the direction and outcomes of future case adjudications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor CJEU case law to anticipate UPC adjudication standards and mitigate infringement exposure.
Practice note: Track judicial personnel changes to optimize litigation teams and tailor defense or enforcement strategies.
What to monitor: Evolving intersection between CJEU guidance and UPC first-instance rulings on validity and infringement.

items.title: Halo investment for impact businesses – reframing and securing investment - GJE
items.title_zh: Halo 投资：重塑影响力企业融资与 IP 安全
items.title_en: Halo Investment for Impact Businesses: Reframing IP Strategy to Secure Capital Amid Shifting Market Trends
items.summary: In this article, we explore why investors are turning to Halo companies and how strong IP strategies offer the long-term security they seek.
items.ai_summary_zh: GJE 文章指出欧洲投资风向转变，硬件与气候科技遇冷，AI 及软件受捧。文章强调无论赛道如何切换，强有力的知识产权组合（特别是专利与商标）仍是投资者寻求长期安全感的基石。律所建议“影响力”企业需通过重构 IP 策略来适应新资本偏好，确保持续融资能力。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis by GJE examines the evolving European investment landscape, noting a distinct shift away from climate tech and hardware-focused impact businesses toward software and artificial intelligence ventures. Despite these changing capital preferences, the article emphasizes that robust intellectual property strategies remain fundamental to securing long-term investor confidence. Strong IP portfolios, particularly patents and trademarks, serve as critical assets that provide the security investors seek regardless of sector trends. The firm advises impact-oriented companies to reframe their IP approaches to align with current market dynamics. By strategically positioning IP assets, businesses can enhance their appeal to investors and ensure continued access to funding, demonstrating that effective IP management is essential for navigating volatility and maintaining competitive advantage in the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应警惕欧洲投资风向波动，避免单一依赖政策热点。无论从事硬科技还是 AI，均须构建高质量欧洲 IP 组合，将其作为融资谈判中的核心增信资产，以抵御市场情绪变化带来的融资风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should recognize that investment sentiment fluctuates between hardware, climate tech, and AI sectors. Relying solely on transient policy hotspots creates vulnerability. Companies must construct high-quality European IP portfolios independent of short-term trends. These portfolios should be explicitly leveraged as core credit-enhancing assets during financing negotiations. This approach mitigates risks associated with shifting market emotions and ensures that IP remains a stable foundation for valuation and investor confidence, regardless of the prevailing technological fashion or economic cycle.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲投资热点从气候科技转向 AI 软件，硬件融资难度依旧", "稳健的 IP 策略是跨越行业周期、获取投资者信任的关键", "影响力企业需重新定位 IP 资产以匹配当前资本市场偏好"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European investment focus has shifted from climate tech and hardware sectors to software and AI, making hardware financing increasingly difficult.", "Robust intellectual property strategies are essential for building investor trust and providing security across different industry cycles.", "Impact businesses must realign their IP asset positioning to match current capital market preferences and secure ongoing funding."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["构建跨周期 IP 组合，不随短期投资热点盲目调整战略方向", "将欧洲 IP 资产显性化，作为对接当地资本的核心增信工具"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Build cross-cycle IP portfolios that do not blindly adjust strategic direction based on short-term investment hotspots.", "Practice note: Make European IP assets explicit and visible to serve as core credit-enhancing tools when engaging with local capital providers.", "What to monitor: Track shifts in European investor preferences between hardware, sustainability, and AI to adapt IP positioning accordingly."]
core_points_en: European investment focus has shifted from climate tech and hardware sectors to software and AI, making hardware financing increasingly difficult.
Robust intellectual property strategies are essential for building investor trust and providing security across different industry cycles.
Impact businesses must realign their IP asset positioning to match current capital market preferences and secure ongoing funding.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Build cross-cycle IP portfolios that do not blindly adjust strategic direction based on short-term investment hotspots.
Practice note: Make European IP assets explicit and visible to serve as core credit-enhancing tools when engaging with local capital providers.
What to monitor: Track shifts in European investor preferences between hardware, sustainability, and AI to adapt IP positioning accordingly.

items.title: Mark Peters publishes article on AI and IP | Grünecker
items.title_zh: Mark Peters发表关于欧盟AI与知识产权的文章
items.title_en: Grünecker Expert Analyzes EU AI Act, Data Act, and Copyright Implications for Innovation
items.summary: Mark Peters publishes a contribution on AI, data and IP in the EU in the new book Winning with IP, released on 26 March 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: Grünecker合伙人Mark Peters在《Winning with IP》新书中撰文，深入分析欧盟人工智能、数据与知识产权的监管框架。文章聚焦《AI法案》和《数据法案》，探讨AI系统及其训练数据涉及的版权挑战与机遇。旨在帮助科技企业通过战略性IP管理应对新兴技术合规要求，提升竞争力与企业价值。
items.ai_summary_en: Mark Peters of Grünecker contributes to the sixth edition of Winning with IP: Managing Intellectual Property Today, released on March 26, 2026. His article, Innovating with AI and Data in the EU, examines the evolving regulatory landscape governing artificial intelligence, data, and copyright within the European Union. The analysis focuses on recent developments concerning the AI Act and the Data Act, alongside complex copyright issues surrounding AI systems and their training data. The contribution evaluates both challenges and opportunities these regulations present for innovative companies. It emphasizes the critical role of a strategic intellectual property perspective in navigating emerging technologies, ensuring that rights holders can effectively protect innovation, drive growth, and build long-term enterprise value while maintaining compliance with new European legal frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需密切关注欧盟AI与数据法规的版权合规要求，特别是训练数据的合法性审查。建议建立专项IP合规体系，平衡技术创新与法律风险，利用战略性IP布局保障在欧洲市场的长期竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI solutions in Europe must closely monitor new data and copyright regulations to mitigate compliance risks. Rights holders should proactively audit the legality of training data sources to avoid potential infringement claims or market access barriers. Aligning intellectual property strategies with the requirements of the AI Act is essential for sustaining innovation and securing competitive advantage. Failure to address these regulatory obligations may result in significant legal exposure and operational disruptions. Therefore, integrating robust IP management practices with regulatory compliance efforts is crucial for navigating the complex European digital environment effectively and safely.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["分析欧盟AI与数据监管框架对IP战略的影响", "聚焦AI法案及数据法案下的版权合规挑战", "探讨训练数据版权风险与企业创新保护策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The article analyzes current regulatory dynamics under the EU AI Act and Data Act affecting technology sectors.", "It examines specific copyright legal controversies regarding the use of data for training artificial intelligence systems.", "Companies are urged to adopt strategic IP perspectives to manage compliance challenges associated with emerging technologies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视欧盟AI训练数据版权合规审查", "构建适应新规的战略IP管理体系"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify copyright origins of AI training datasets to mitigate EU regulatory compliance risks.", "Practice note: Adjust intellectual property strategies to align with new EU regulations, ensuring continuous innovation.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of the AI Act and Data Act impacting data usage and copyright enforcement."]
core_points_en: The article analyzes current regulatory dynamics under the EU AI Act and Data Act affecting technology sectors.
It examines specific copyright legal controversies regarding the use of data for training artificial intelligence systems.
Companies are urged to adopt strategic IP perspectives to manage compliance challenges associated with emerging technologies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify copyright origins of AI training datasets to mitigate EU regulatory compliance risks.
Practice note: Adjust intellectual property strategies to align with new EU regulations, ensuring continuous innovation.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of the AI Act and Data Act impacting data usage and copyright enforcement.

items.title: Europe Recalibrates: AI Copyright And GDPR Scope Under Pressure
items.title_zh: 欧洲重构：AI 版权与 GDPR 适用范围面临压力
items.title_en: EU Recalibrates AI Copyright and GDPR Scope as Council Rejects Personal Data Redefinition
items.summary: March 2026 – Two parallel developments suggest that Europe is drawing clearer boundaries around both AI ownership and data protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 3 月，欧盟在 AI 所有权与数据保护上划定新界。理事会泄露草案删除了“数字一揽子计划”中关于重新定义个人数据的核心提议，拒绝引入相对性原则。同时德国法院判决显示对创新名义下重构法律基础持谨慎态度。EDPB 批评原提案增加不确定性，主张维持现有定义并完善去标识化指南。
items.ai_summary_en: March 2026 marks a pivotal moment for European intellectual property and data protection frameworks. Two parallel developments indicate that Europe is establishing clearer boundaries regarding AI ownership and data privacy. A leaked Council compromise draft concerning the Digital Omnibus completely removes the proposed redefinition of 'personal data' under the GDPR, rejecting the introduction of a relativity principle for pseudonymised information. Simultaneously, recent German court judgments demonstrate a cautious approach toward redefining legal fundamentals in the name of innovation or administrative simplification. The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has criticized the original proposal for increasing legal uncertainty, advocating instead for maintaining existing definitions while refining guidance on pseudonymisation. These shifts signal a regulatory environment that prioritizes strict adherence to current standards over simplified compliance mechanisms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧处理数据时，不能依赖拟议的宽松解释，需严格遵循现行 GDPR 对个人数据的宽泛定义。建议重新评估去标识化数据的合规策略，避免因地方法院或欧盟最终维持严标准而引发处罚风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should not rely on anticipated relaxations in data protection rules, as the proposed reforms have been effectively stalled. Compliance strategies must continue to align with the broad definition of personal data under the current GDPR regime. Organizations should reassess their handling of pseudonymised datasets to mitigate risks associated with potential penalties from local courts or EU authorities maintaining strict standards. It is critical to avoid betting on unpassed legislative changes. Instead, entities should prepare for rigorous enforcement by evaluating AI training data acquisition methods against existing high-compliance benchmarks, ensuring robust governance structures are in place regardless of future administrative simplifications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["理事会草案完全删除了重新定义个人数据的争议性条款", "监管机构反对通过实施法案决定去标识化数据是否属个人数据", "欧洲正以更谨慎态度界定 AI 版权归属与数据保护边界"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Council draft entirely deletes controversial clauses aiming to redefine personal data within the GDPR framework.", "Regulators oppose using implementing acts to determine whether pseudonymised data qualifies as personal data.", "Europe adopts a cautious stance in defining AI copyright ownership and tightening data protection boundaries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["勿赌注于未通过的改革，应按最严标准执行数据合规", "关注德国判例风向，调整 AI 训练数据获取与使用策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Do not assume relaxed interpretations; maintain strict compliance with the current broad GDPR definition of personal data.", "Practice note: Reassess pseudonymisation strategies and AI data sourcing to withstand strict judicial scrutiny and avoid penalty risks.", "What to monitor: Track German case law trends and adjust AI training data acquisition policies to align with conservative regulatory signals."]
core_points_en: The Council draft entirely deletes controversial clauses aiming to redefine personal data within the GDPR framework.
Regulators oppose using implementing acts to determine whether pseudonymised data qualifies as personal data.
Europe adopts a cautious stance in defining AI copyright ownership and tightening data protection boundaries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Do not assume relaxed interpretations; maintain strict compliance with the current broad GDPR definition of personal data.
Practice note: Reassess pseudonymisation strategies and AI data sourcing to withstand strict judicial scrutiny and avoid penalty risks.
What to monitor: Track German case law trends and adjust AI training data acquisition policies to align with conservative regulatory signals.

items.title: The UPC’s first CJEU referral – a case of constitutional significance or a jurisdictional nightmare?
items.title_zh: UPC 首案提请欧盟法院裁决：宪制意义还是管辖噩梦？
items.title_en: UPC’s First CJEU Referral: Constitutional Significance or Jurisdictional Nightmare?
items.summary: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep referred four questions concerning the extent of the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction to the Court of Justice of the European Union – making it the UPC’s first ever referral to the CJEU.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 3 月 6 日，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在戴森诉追觅案中，首次就长臂管辖权范围向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请先决裁决。案件核心争议在于非欧盟制造商能否因其在成员国的授权代表而被纳入管辖，以及针对非成员国领土销售的管辖权依据。上诉庭中止部分审理，等待欧盟法院对相关法律条文的澄清。
items.ai_summary_en: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal issued its first-ever referral to the Court of Justice of the European Union in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep. The appeal concerns four questions regarding the extent of the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction under Regulation 1215/2012 and Directive 2004/48. The background involves provisional measures sought by Dyson against multiple entities, including a non-EU manufacturer and its EU-based Authorised Representative. The Court of Appeal stayed proceedings concerning actions against the non-EU entity for sales in Spain and against the Authorised Representative. These stays address unresolved EU law questions on whether an EU-based intermediary can serve as an anchor defendant to join non-UPC member state companies, and how jurisdiction applies to territories outside the UPC framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕在欧设立授权代表可能引发的连带诉讼风险。建议重新评估合规架构，明确代表职责边界，避免成为管辖连接点。同时应关注欧盟法院最终裁决，以便调整在欧洲市场的知识产权防御策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights significant implications for rights holders and market participants operating within the EU. Establishing an Authorised Representative for regulatory compliance may inadvertently create a jurisdictional anchor, exposing non-EU manufacturers to litigation across UPC member states. Companies should carefully evaluate their compliance structures and contractual arrangements with representatives to mitigate the risk of being drawn into broad cross-border patent disputes. It is crucial to monitor the CJEU’s eventual ruling, as the interpretation of 'anchor defendant' status will define the strategic landscape for enforcing and defending patents in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 历史上首次向欧盟法院提起先决裁决请求", "争议焦点为授权代表是否构成连接点以确立管辖权", "涉及非欧盟成员国领土销售行为的跨境管辖界限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal made its historic first preliminary reference to the CJEU regarding cross-border patent enforcement.", "The core dispute examines whether an EU-based Authorised Representative constitutes a valid jurisdictional hook for non-EU manufacturers.", "The referral clarifies the boundaries of UPC jurisdiction concerning sales activities in non-UPC member states like Spain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在欧授权代表可能成为长臂管辖的连接锚点", "需防范因代表关系被卷入跨国专利侵权诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: An EU Authorised Representative may serve as a jurisdictional anchor, potentially exposing non-EU manufacturers to UPC-wide litigation.", "Practice note: Review compliance architectures and representative agreements to clearly define roles and limit exposure to patent infringement claims.", "What to monitor: The CJEU’s final decision on whether intermediaries can establish jurisdiction over non-UPC member state entities."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal made its historic first preliminary reference to the CJEU regarding cross-border patent enforcement.
The core dispute examines whether an EU-based Authorised Representative constitutes a valid jurisdictional hook for non-EU manufacturers.
The referral clarifies the boundaries of UPC jurisdiction concerning sales activities in non-UPC member states like Spain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: An EU Authorised Representative may serve as a jurisdictional anchor, potentially exposing non-EU manufacturers to UPC-wide litigation.
Practice note: Review compliance architectures and representative agreements to clearly define roles and limit exposure to patent infringement claims.
What to monitor: The CJEU’s final decision on whether intermediaries can establish jurisdiction over non-UPC member state entities.

items.title: Guest Post - From Belgian Speculoos biscuits to one of the world’s most popular brands
items.title_zh: 从比利时 Speculoos 到全球品牌：Lotus 的商标战略
items.title_en: From Generic Speculoos to Global Brand: Lotus Bakeries' Strategic Trademark Journey
items.summary:
The IPKat is grateful to Natalia Basałaj (Hansberry Tomkiel, Poland) for the following Guest Post. "The story of Lotus Bakeries demonstrates that
global product success is often driven not only by taste, but above all by a
well-designed strategy.

The decision to move away from the generic name
“Speculoos” – referring to traditional Belgian biscuits with a distinctive
caramel-cinnamon
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文通过分析 Lotus Bakeries 案例，阐述其如何将通用名称
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the trademark strategy of Lotus Bakeries, illustrating how global product success relies heavily on strategic brand building rather than solely on taste. The case highlights the critical decision to abandon the generic term 'Speculoos,' which describes traditional Belgian caramel-cinnamon biscuits, in favor of a distinctive brand identity. By shifting away from descriptive language, the company established unique brand recognition and secured stronger legal protection. The article provides a perspective from Polish legal counsel on how navigating the Benelux trademark system facilitated this transition. It underscores the importance of early planning and distinctiveness in trademark portfolios to achieve international market penetration and long-term commercial viability for food and beverage products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出海应避免使用通用描述词，需提前规划具有显著性的商标组合，利用比荷卢及欧盟体系强化品牌护城河。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants expanding into Europe should avoid relying on generic or descriptive terms that lack inherent distinctiveness. Instead, rights holders must proactively plan trademark portfolios with strong distinctive character to ensure higher registration success rates and enforceability. Utilizing the unified mechanisms of the Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) can optimize efficiency in European trademark layouts. Companies should monitor how local generic terms are treated across EU jurisdictions to prevent brand dilution. This approach helps build a defensible brand moat, ensuring that intellectual property assets effectively support global expansion and competitive positioning in crowded consumer markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["放弃通用名称 Speculoos 以构建独特品牌识别度", "全球化成功依赖精心设计的商标布局而非仅靠产品口味", "波兰律师视角解析比荷卢地区商标策略对全球的启示"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Abandoning the generic name 'Speculoos' was essential for building unique brand recognition and securing robust trademark protection.", "Global commercial success depends significantly on a carefully designed trademark layout and strategic IP management rather than just product quality.", "A Polish legal perspective analyzes how leveraging the unified Benelux trademark system offers valuable insights for broader European strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["规避通用名称风险，提升商标显著性与注册成功率", "借助比荷卢统一市场机制优化欧洲商标布局效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid generic descriptors to enhance trademark distinctiveness and improve registration success rates in key European markets.", "Practice note: Leverage the unified Benelux trademark system to streamline filing procedures and optimize the efficiency of European IP portfolios.", "What to monitor: Track the treatment of descriptive terms in national and EU trademark offices to mitigate risks of brand dilution and opposition."]
core_points_en: Abandoning the generic name 'Speculoos' was essential for building unique brand recognition and securing robust trademark protection.
Global commercial success depends significantly on a carefully designed trademark layout and strategic IP management rather than just product quality.
A Polish legal perspective analyzes how leveraging the unified Benelux trademark system offers valuable insights for broader European strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid generic descriptors to enhance trademark distinctiveness and improve registration success rates in key European markets.
Practice note: Leverage the unified Benelux trademark system to streamline filing procedures and optimize the efficiency of European IP portfolios.
What to monitor: Track the treatment of descriptive terms in national and EU trademark offices to mitigate risks of brand dilution and opposition.
