items.title: Exclusive: IP lawyers' AI tool aims to overcome ‘black box’ woes
items.title_zh: 独家：IP 律师推 AI 工具旨在破解“黑箱”难题
items.title_en: UK Lawyers Launch Attain IP AI Tool to Resolve Black Box Transparency Issues
items.summary: Attain IP, developed by two UK patent lawyers, will meet ‘forensic’ needs of patent attorneys by showing a verifiable reasoning chain, according to its co-founders
items.ai_summary_zh: 由两名英国专利律师开发的 Attain IP 工具，旨在解决法律 AI 的“黑箱”痛点。该工具通过展示可验证的推理链条，满足专利代理人对“法医级”准确度的需求，确保 AI 辅助决策过程透明、可追溯，提升专利实务中的信任度与合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: Attain IP, a new artificial intelligence platform developed by two UK patent attorneys, addresses the critical transparency challenges associated with legal AI applications. By providing a verifiable reasoning chain for its outputs, the tool aims to satisfy the forensic accuracy requirements of patent practitioners. This approach ensures that AI-assisted decision-making processes remain transparent and fully traceable throughout the workflow. The solution is designed to enhance trust and regulatory compliance within patent practice, offering a robust alternative to opaque algorithmic systems. It specifically targets the need for accountability in professional legal services, allowing users to validate the logical steps taken by the AI. This development highlights a growing market demand for explainable AI solutions that can meet the rigorous standards of intellectual property prosecution and litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用 AI 辅助专利业务时，应优先选择具备可解释性的工具以规避合规风险。建议国内 IP 服务商借鉴此模式，开发透明化 AI 产品，以适应欧洲市场对算法问责的严格要求。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI for patent operations in Europe should prioritize tools offering clear interpretability to mitigate potential compliance risks related to algorithmic opacity. Service providers developing AI solutions for this jurisdiction must ensure their products align with strict European accountability standards regarding automated decision-making. Failure to provide transparent reasoning may hinder adoption among risk-averse legal professionals who require defensible audit trails. Consequently, integrating explainability features is becoming a competitive necessity rather than an optional enhancement. Organizations should evaluate whether their current AI vendors can demonstrate sufficient procedural transparency to withstand scrutiny from both clients and regulatory bodies focused on ethical AI deployment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国律师推出 Attain IP 工具，专注解决 AI 黑箱问题", "提供可验证推理链，满足专利代理人的法医级需求", "强调透明度与可追溯性，旨在提升法律 AI 的实务信任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Attain IP, created by UK patent lawyers, directly tackles the black box problem inherent in many current legal AI tools.", "The platform provides a verifiable reasoning chain to meet the high forensic accuracy standards required by patent attorneys.", "Emphasis on transparency and traceability aims to significantly increase trust and compliance in using legal AI technologies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["赴欧企业应选用可解释 AI 工具，规避算法黑箱合规风险", "国内服务商需研发透明化 AI 产品，适配欧洲问责标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Select interpretable AI tools to avoid compliance risks associated with opaque algorithmic decision-making in patent matters.", "Practice note: Develop or procure transparent AI products that align with stringent European standards for algorithmic accountability and auditability.", "What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding explainability and the evidentiary weight of AI-generated legal analysis in European courts."]
core_points_en: Attain IP, created by UK patent lawyers, directly tackles the black box problem inherent in many current legal AI tools.
The platform provides a verifiable reasoning chain to meet the high forensic accuracy standards required by patent attorneys.
Emphasis on transparency and traceability aims to significantly increase trust and compliance in using legal AI technologies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Select interpretable AI tools to avoid compliance risks associated with opaque algorithmic decision-making in patent matters.
Practice note: Develop or procure transparent AI products that align with stringent European standards for algorithmic accountability and auditability.
What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding explainability and the evidentiary weight of AI-generated legal analysis in European courts.

items.title: New EU industrial design law triggers a surge in registrations and marks a turning point
items.title_zh: 欧盟新外观设计法引发注册激增并标志转折点
items.title_en: New EU industrial design law triggers a surge in registrations and marks a turning point
items.summary: The recent reform of the European Union’s Industrial Designs Law is having a tangible impact on the market. Since its partial entry into force in May 2025, the data shows a clear trend: companies are increasingly turning to the European system to protect their designs, which is now perceived as a tool that is better ... Read more La entrada New EU industrial design law triggers a surge in registrations and marks a turning point se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟外观设计法近期改革已于 2025 年 5 月部分生效，对市场产生实质影响。数据显示企业正加速利用欧洲体系保护设计，视其为更优工具。此次法律修订显著提升了制度吸引力，导致注册量 surge，标志着欧盟外观设计保护体系进入新发展阶段。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口及制造企业应抓住欧盟新规红利期，加速布局外观设计以巩固市场地位。建议重新评估现有产品线的保护策略，利用简化程序降低合规成本，防范因竞争加剧导致的侵权风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟外观设计法改革于 2025 年 5 月部分生效并显效", "新规提升体系吸引力促使企业注册量显著激增", "市场趋势表明该制度已被视为更优保护工具"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借势新规红利加速对欧外观设计申请布局", "优化存量产品设计保护策略以应对竞争"]

items.title: European patent descriptions: To adapt or not to adapt?
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利说明书：权利要求变更时是否必须适应性修改？
items.title_en: EPO Patent Descriptions: Is Adaptation Mandatory When Claims Change?
items.summary: Discover when adapting European patent descriptions is legally required when claims are amended and the necessary practice to be followed by the EPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO 近年要求专利说明书须与权利要求一致，主要依据 EPC 第 84 条。但上诉委员会判例出现分歧：部分判决支持必须修改以消除矛盾，另一部分则认为缺乏法律依据。鉴于异议程序中清晰度非无效理由，T697/22 号案件已将此法律争议提交扩大上诉委员会裁决，以明确是否必须修改及具体法律条款依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office increasingly requires patent descriptions to align with amended claims, primarily citing Article 84 EPC regarding clarity and support. However, Boards of Appeal decisions reveal a significant legal divergence. One line of case law mandates description amendments to resolve inconsistencies, while another argues Article 84 lacks the legal basis for such requirements, particularly critical in opposition proceedings where clarity is not a valid ground for revocation. To resolve this conflict, the Board in T697/22 has referred three questions to the Enlarged Board of Appeal. The referral seeks clarification on whether description adaptation is legally necessary when claim amendments create inconsistencies during opposition or appeal, and which specific EPC provisions govern this requirement if an affirmative answer is given.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对异议或上诉时，需关注说明书修改策略。若扩大上诉委员会确认无需修改，企业可据此抗辩 EPO 的修改要求，避免不必要的范围限缩，维持专利保护力度。
items.ai_insight_en: This pending referral creates strategic uncertainty for rights holders managing European patents. If the Enlarged Board confirms that description adaptation is not mandatory, applicants can resist examiner requests to amend descriptions, thereby avoiding unnecessary limitations to the patent's scope. Conversely, a ruling requiring adaptation would standardize current examination practices. Parties involved in opposition or appeal proceedings should carefully evaluate the risks of leaving descriptions inconsistent with claims versus the potential loss of protection breadth from voluntary amendments. Maintaining broader descriptive support may preserve flexibility for future claim interpretations or divisional filings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 依据 EPC 第 84 条要求说明书与权利要求保持一致性", "上诉委员会判例分裂：一方支持强制修改，另一方认为无法定依据", "扩大上诉委员会将裁决异议程序中是否必须适应性修改说明书"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO examiners rely on Article 84 EPC to demand description adaptations ensuring consistency with amended claims.", "Boards of Appeal disagree: some mandate changes to fix contradictions, others rule no legal basis exists for forcing them.", "Enlarged Board of Appeal will decide if description adaptation is compulsory during opposition proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["密切关注扩大上诉委员会裁决，预判 EPO 审查标准变化趋势", "在异议程序中审慎评估说明书修改必要性，防止保护范围不当缩小"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess whether resisting description amendments preserves broader protection scope against examiner objections.", "Practice note: Evaluate the strategic risk of inconsistency in opposition versus the cost of limiting claim scope via amendment.", "What to monitor: Track the Enlarged Board of Appeal decision to anticipate shifts in EPO examination standards."]
core_points_en: EPO examiners rely on Article 84 EPC to demand description adaptations ensuring consistency with amended claims.
Boards of Appeal disagree: some mandate changes to fix contradictions, others rule no legal basis exists for forcing them.
Enlarged Board of Appeal will decide if description adaptation is compulsory during opposition proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess whether resisting description amendments preserves broader protection scope against examiner objections.
Practice note: Evaluate the strategic risk of inconsistency in opposition versus the cost of limiting claim scope via amendment.
What to monitor: Track the Enlarged Board of Appeal decision to anticipate shifts in EPO examination standards.

items.title: When AI chooses: Trademarks and brand control in the agentic age
items.title_zh: AI 自主抉择时代：商标与品牌控制权的博弈
items.title_en: Agentic AI and Trademark Rights: Navigating Brand Control Challenges in the EU
items.summary: As Amazon ramps up its dispute with Perplexity’s AI shopping agent, WIPR takes a closer look at the rise of agentic AI and what it means for brand owners’ rights.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着亚马逊与 Perplexity AI 购物代理争端升级，本文深入探讨代理型 AI 崛起对商标权利人带来的挑战。文章分析当 AI 自主做出购买决策时，传统商标侵权认定及品牌控制机制面临的法律空白，指出欧盟及全球需重新审视现有规则以适应智能代理时代的品牌保护需求。
items.ai_summary_en: As Amazon intensifies its legal dispute with Perplexity’s AI shopping agent, this analysis examines the implications of agentic AI for trademark rights. The article highlights how autonomous purchasing decisions by AI agents blur traditional boundaries of trademark use and infringement, creating significant legal uncertainties. It underscores the urgent need for the European Union and global jurisdictions to reassess existing regulatory frameworks. Current rules are ill-equipped to address brand protection in an era dominated by intelligent agents that mediate consumer interactions without direct human intervention, necessitating a comprehensive update to safeguard intellectual property interests effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应警惕 AI 代理导致的品牌混淆风险，提前布局监测机制。建议关注欧盟关于 AI 与知识产权的政策动向，在商标注册及维权策略中纳入针对算法推荐的防御性条款，防止品牌在无人干预的交易中被滥用或稀释。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should proactively assess risks associated with brand confusion caused by AI agents operating in automated transaction environments. Establishing real-time monitoring systems for AI-generated search results is critical for early detection of potential infringements. Furthermore, trademark strategies must evolve to include defensive clauses addressing algorithmic recommendations. Companies should strengthen arguments regarding likelihood of confusion in contexts driven by machine learning, ensuring robust protection against unauthorized brand exploitation or dilution in the agentic economy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["代理型 AI 自主购物行为模糊了传统商标使用与侵权的界限", "亚马逊与 Perplexity 争端凸显平台与 AI 服务商的责任归属难题", "现有欧盟商标框架亟需更新以应对算法主导的品牌接触场景"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Autonomous AI shopping behaviors obscure traditional distinctions between legitimate trademark use and potential infringement scenarios.", "The Amazon versus Perplexity conflict highlights complex liability attribution challenges between platforms and AI service providers.", "The current EU trademark framework requires urgent updates to address algorithm-driven brand interaction contexts effectively."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立针对 AI 搜索结果的实时品牌监测与预警响应体系", "在商标布局中强化对算法推荐场景下的混淆可能性论证"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implement real-time brand monitoring and alert response systems specifically designed for AI search results.", "Practice note: Strengthen likelihood of confusion arguments within trademark portfolios to cover algorithm-driven recommendation scenarios.", "What to monitor: Policy developments regarding AI and intellectual property within the European Union regulatory landscape."]
core_points_en: Autonomous AI shopping behaviors obscure traditional distinctions between legitimate trademark use and potential infringement scenarios.
The Amazon versus Perplexity conflict highlights complex liability attribution challenges between platforms and AI service providers.
The current EU trademark framework requires urgent updates to address algorithm-driven brand interaction contexts effectively.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implement real-time brand monitoring and alert response systems specifically designed for AI search results.
Practice note: Strengthen likelihood of confusion arguments within trademark portfolios to cover algorithm-driven recommendation scenarios.
What to monitor: Policy developments regarding AI and intellectual property within the European Union regulatory landscape.

items.title: EPO revokes crucial patent for driver assistance systems
items.title_zh: 欧专局撤销福耀玻璃驾驶辅助系统关键专利
items.title_en: EPO Revokes Fuyao Glass HUD Patent, Clearing Path for Competitors in EU Market
items.summary: Chinese glass manufacturer Fuyao Glass owns EP 3 187 917 B1, which covers such an HUD. This is a driver assistance system with which important vehicle data and traffic-relevant information can be projected directly onto the driver’s field of vision on the windscreen, thus enhancing driver safety and improving navigation efficiency. As part of the […] The post EPO revokes crucial patent for driver assistance systems appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 中国福耀玻璃拥有的欧洲专利 EP3187917B1 被欧洲专利局撤销。该专利涵盖将车辆数据投影至挡风玻璃的抬头显示（HUD）技术。此次裁决源于异议程序，圣戈班等竞争对手成功挑战其有效性。专利失效后，相关企业在欧盟市场的技术实施障碍消除，竞争格局发生重大变化。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has revoked EP 3 187 917 B1, a key patent held by Chinese manufacturer Fuyao Glass covering head-up display technology. This system projects vital vehicle data and traffic information onto the windscreen to enhance driver safety and navigation efficiency. The revocation resulted from opposition proceedings where competitors, including Saint-Gobain, successfully challenged the patent's validity. The opponents demonstrated that the invention lacked the necessary requirements for patentability. Consequently, the patent is no longer enforceable. This decision removes significant technical implementation barriers for market participants operating within the European Union. The landscape for automotive display technologies has shifted substantially, allowing rivals to implement similar HUD solutions without fear of infringement claims based on this specific intellectual property right.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应警惕海外专利维持风险，核心技术在欧布局需经得住异议挑战。建议加强事前检索与稳定性评估，建立应对国际巨头专利阻击的长效机制，避免单一专利依赖。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the critical importance of rigorous pre-grant stability assessments for patents intended for the European market. Rights holders must conduct comprehensive prior art searches to ensure their inventions can withstand post-grant opposition challenges from well-resourced international competitors. Relying on a single patent for core technology creates significant vulnerability. Companies should establish robust mechanisms for monitoring competitor filings and responding rapidly to opposition threats. Strengthening the defensive quality of patent portfolios through careful drafting and thorough examination responses is essential to maintain exclusive rights and secure long-term market position against aggressive challengers.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利局正式撤销福耀玻璃持有的 HUD 技术核心专利", "竞争对手通过异议程序成功证明该专利缺乏授权要件", "专利失效为圣戈班等企业扫清了欧盟市场准入障碍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO officially revoked Fuyao Glass's core patent covering head-up display projection technology.", "Competitors successfully proved the patent lacked essential authorization requirements during opposition proceedings.", "The patent's invalidation cleared market access obstacles for Saint-Gobain and other firms in the EU."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化欧洲专利授权前的稳定性评估与检索分析", "建立应对国际竞争对手专利异议的快速响应机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct thorough stability assessments and prior art searches before finalizing European patent strategies.", "Practice note: Establish rapid response protocols to address oppositions filed by major international competitors effectively.", "What to monitor: Track opposition outcomes and validity challenges affecting core technology patents in the automotive sector."]
core_points_en: The EPO officially revoked Fuyao Glass's core patent covering head-up display projection technology.
Competitors successfully proved the patent lacked essential authorization requirements during opposition proceedings.
The patent's invalidation cleared market access obstacles for Saint-Gobain and other firms in the EU.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct thorough stability assessments and prior art searches before finalizing European patent strategies.
Practice note: Establish rapid response protocols to address oppositions filed by major international competitors effectively.
What to monitor: Track opposition outcomes and validity challenges affecting core technology patents in the automotive sector.

items.title: The emerging utility patent strategies of global fashion leaders
items.title_zh: 全球时尚领袖新兴实用新型专利策略解析
items.title_en: Global Fashion Leaders Shift to Utility Patents for Competitive Advantage
items.summary: Technology convergence in the fashion industry is fuelling a dramatic increase in patenting and litigation, highlighting the industry's focus on intellectual property to maintain competitive advantage
items.ai_summary_zh: 时尚行业技术融合正推动专利申请与诉讼急剧增加。全球时尚领导者通过布局实用新型专利等知识产权，以维持其在智能化、功能化产品领域的竞争优势。文章分析了该行业从传统外观设计保护向核心技术专利保护的策略转变，凸显知识产权在商业博弈中的核心地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The convergence of technology and fashion is driving a significant surge in patent filings and litigation across the industry. Global fashion leaders are increasingly leveraging utility patents to secure competitive advantages in smart and functional product segments. This strategic pivot marks a departure from traditional reliance on design rights, emphasizing core technical innovations instead. The trend underscores the critical role of intellectual property in modern commercial competition, as companies seek to build robust legal barriers against rivals. By integrating advanced technologies into apparel and accessories, these market participants are redefining IP strategies to protect functional features, reflecting a broader shift towards comprehensive patent protection in the fashion sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国时尚及科技企业应警惕欧洲竞争对手的专利布局升级，提前开展自由实施分析。建议加大功能性技术创新的专利申请力度，利用欧洲统一专利法院机制应对潜在诉讼，避免陷入被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving patent landscape in Europe, where fashion companies are shifting focus from aesthetic designs to core technical protections. Rights holders must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses to mitigate risks associated with this strategic transition. Strengthening high-value patent portfolios is crucial for navigating potential cross-border disputes. Additionally, understanding the implications of the Unified Patent Court mechanism can provide effective avenues for enforcement or defense. Proactive IP management ensures that companies remain resilient against aggressive litigation tactics while safeguarding their technological innovations in an increasingly competitive environment.

items.title: Dyson ./. Dreame – First referral of the UPC concerning its own long-arm jurisdiction | Grünecker
items.title_zh: 戴森诉追觅案：UPC 首提长臂管辖权先决裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Refers First Long-Arm Jurisdiction Questions to CJEU in Dyson v. Dreame
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal refers questions on long-arm jurisdiction to the CJEU in Dyson v. Dreame.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在戴森诉追觅案中，首次就其对非欧盟被告的“长臂管辖权”范围向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请先决裁决。该案背景涉及布鲁塞尔条例 Ia 及既往判例对跨境专利执法的限制。此举旨在厘清 UPC 能否管辖域外被告或裁决非参与国境内的欧洲专利侵权，将对未来跨国专利诉讼格局产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal has referred questions concerning its long-arm jurisdiction to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) in the case of Dyson v. Dreame. This marks the first such referral by the UPC regarding its authority over non-EU defendants. The legal context involves the Brussels I Recast Regulation and established CJEU case law, which historically limited cross-border patent enforcement by emphasizing the territorial nature of patent rights. The referral seeks to clarify whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over defendants located outside the EU or adjudicate infringement acts occurring in non-participating Member States. This development is significant for the future landscape of multinational patent litigation, as it addresses critical uncertainties regarding the reach of the new unified court system beyond its immediate territorial boundaries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕 UPC 扩张管辖至域外主体的风险，尤其在涉及多国销售时。建议提前评估产品在欧洲的布局，针对潜在长臂管辖制定抗辩策略，并密切关注 CJEU 最终裁决对跨境应诉成本与责任认定的具体影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should be aware of the potential expansion of UPC jurisdiction to extraterritorial entities, particularly when products are sold across multiple jurisdictions. It is advisable to assess product layouts in Europe proactively and develop defense strategies addressing potential long-arm jurisdiction claims. Stakeholders must closely monitor the final CJEU ruling, as it will significantly impact cross-border litigation costs and liability determinations for non-EU defendants involved in European patent disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉庭就长臂管辖权范围首次向 CJEU 提请先决裁决", "案件核心在于界定 UPC 对非欧盟被告及域外侵权行为的管辖边界", "此前 CJEU 判例强调专利权地域性，限制了多国合并诉讼的可能"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal makes its first referral to the CJEU regarding the scope of long-arm jurisdiction over non-EU defendants.", "The core legal issue defines the jurisdictional boundaries of the UPC concerning extraterritorial defendants and infringing acts outside participating states.", "Previous CJEU precedents emphasized patent territoriality, thereby restricting the possibility of consolidated multi-state infringement actions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 UPC 管辖权扩张风险，提前布局跨境专利合规与抗辩", "密切追踪 CJEU 裁决，评估对非欧盟被告的连带责任影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess risks of expanded UPC jurisdiction over extraterritorial subjects, especially in multi-country sales scenarios.", "Practice note: Proactively evaluate European product layouts and formulate defense strategies against potential long-arm jurisdiction claims.", "What to monitor: Track the final CJEU decision to evaluate its specific impact on joint liability and cross-border litigation costs for non-EU defendants."]

items.title: Via Licensing Alliance expands Chinese cohort with addition of Honor
items.title_zh: Via 许可联盟吸纳荣耀加入音频编解码专利池
items.title_en: Via Licensing Alliance Adds Honor to Audio Codec Patent Pool Amid Asian Expansion
items.summary: Via Licensing Alliance expands Chinese cohort with addition of Honor
items.ai_summary_zh: Via 许可联盟宣布中国智能手机巨头荣耀加入其音频编解码专利池。此前已有两家亚洲科技巨头签署 Via 的 Qi 无线充电项目。该动态显示国际专利池运营方正加速整合中国头部终端厂商，旨在通过集体许可模式解决标准必要专利授权问题，优化全球知识产权生态布局。
items.ai_summary_en: Via Licensing Alliance has officially welcomed Honor, the prominent Chinese smartphone manufacturer, into its audio codec patent pool. This development follows closely after two other major Asian technology firms joined Via’s Qi Wireless power consortium. The move highlights a strategic acceleration by international patent pool operators to integrate leading Chinese terminal device manufacturers into their licensing frameworks. By adopting collective licensing models, these pools aim to streamline standard-essential patent (SEP) authorization processes and resolve complex licensing challenges more efficiently. This trend reflects a broader effort to optimize the global intellectual property ecosystem, ensuring smoother market access and reduced friction for rights holders and implementers alike across key jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应正视国际专利池合规压力，主动评估入池利弊。建议提前开展 FTO 分析，权衡单独谈判与集体许可成本，避免在欧洲市场遭遇禁令风险或高额索赔，构建稳健的海外 IP 防御体系。
items.ai_insight_en: The inclusion of major Chinese device makers in international patent pools signals increasing reliance on collective licensing mechanisms to manage SEP obligations. For market participants operating in Europe and globally, this shift underscores the importance of evaluating whether individual negotiations or pool memberships offer greater cost efficiency and legal certainty. Companies should proactively assess their freedom-to-operate positions and weigh the risks of standalone litigation against the benefits of standardized royalty rates. Integrating into established pools may mitigate exposure to injunctions and high-value damages claims, particularly in jurisdictions with robust enforcement regimes like the Unified Patent Court.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["主动评估入池策略以规避欧洲潜在侵权诉讼", "加强海外专利组合建设提升交叉谈判筹码"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate participation in patent pools to mitigate infringement litigation risks and secure predictable licensing costs in European markets.", "Practice note: Strengthen overseas patent portfolios to enhance leverage in cross-licensing negotiations and improve bargaining positions with pool administrators.", "What to monitor: Track further expansions of major patent pools into Asian markets and observe shifts in SEP licensing strategies among global tech giants."]

items.title: When everything becomes a program: a technical analysis of the UK Supreme Court’s judgment in Emotional Perception
items.title_zh: 万物皆程序：英最高法院情感感知案技术分析与专利变革
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Overrules Aerotel: AI Neural Networks as Programs and the New Hardware Threshold
items.summary: As previously reported in this blog, on 11 February 2026, the UK Supreme Court handed down its ruling in Emotional Perception AI Ltd v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks [2026] UKSC 3 which reversed the longstanding Aerotel test for assessment of patentable subject matter, causing a seismic shift in the patent communit
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 2 月，英国最高法院在情感感知案中作出裁决，推翻了沿用近二十年的 Aerotel 测试，转而采纳欧洲专利局 G1/19 决定的逻辑。法院认定人工神经网络属于计算机程序，确立了“任何硬件”即构成发明的低门槛标准，将非技术特征审查后移至创造性步骤，引发英国乃至欧洲 AI 专利格局地震。
items.ai_summary_en: In February 2026, the UK Supreme Court issued its landmark ruling in Emotional Perception AI Ltd v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks. The judgment overturns the longstanding Aerotel test, aligning UK practice with the European Patent Office’s reasoning in G1/19. Crucially, the Court determined that artificial neural networks constitute programs for computers, thereby establishing a low threshold where any hardware involvement qualifies an invention under Article 52(2). This shifts the filtering of non-technical features to the inventive step assessment. The decision fundamentally alters the patentability framework for computer-implemented inventions and AI-related technologies in the UK, creating significant implications for rights holders navigating the intersection of software and hardware claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英申请 AI 专利将迎来窗口期，需重新评估被拒案件。建议立即依据新标准调整权利要求布局，强调硬件结合与技术效果，利用英国新规作为进入欧洲市场的战略跳板，同时警惕审查重心后移带来的不确定性风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling creates a strategic window for applicants with previously refused AI-related patent applications in the UK. Rights holders should reassess these cases under the new framework, potentially reviving claims by emphasizing technical implementations. However, practitioners must anticipate that the substantive examination burden has shifted toward proving inventive step. Consequently, drafting strategies should prioritize robust descriptions of hardware integration and specific technical effects to withstand scrutiny during this later stage. While the lower entry barrier facilitates initial acceptance, the heightened focus on technical contribution requires stronger evidentiary support for innovation claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["最高法院推翻 Aerotel 测试，全面对齐欧洲专利局 G1/19 审查标准", "裁定人工神经网络属于计算机程序，明确其可专利性主体资格", "确立“任何硬件”门槛，将非技术性过滤推迟至创造性判断阶段"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Supreme Court overruled the Aerotel four-step test, fully aligning UK subject matter eligibility with EPO G1/19 standards.", "Artificial neural networks are legally classified as computer programs, confirming their eligibility as patentable subject matter.", "An 'any hardware' threshold is established, deferring the exclusion of non-technical features to the inventive step assessment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用英国新规重启受阻 AI 专利申请，优化权利要求硬件结合描述", "关注审查重心后移风险，强化技术方案创造性证据以应对实质审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reevaluate previously rejected AI patent applications in the UK, leveraging the new 'any hardware' standard to revive claims.", "Practice note: Optimize claim drafting to emphasize hardware integration and concrete technical effects, anticipating stricter inventive step scrutiny.", "What to monitor: Monitor UKIPO examination guidelines regarding the assessment of technical contributions and inventive step for AI inventions."]
core_points_en: The Supreme Court overruled the Aerotel four-step test, fully aligning UK subject matter eligibility with EPO G1/19 standards.
Artificial neural networks are legally classified as computer programs, confirming their eligibility as patentable subject matter.
An 'any hardware' threshold is established, deferring the exclusion of non-technical features to the inventive step assessment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reevaluate previously rejected AI patent applications in the UK, leveraging the new 'any hardware' standard to revive claims.
Practice note: Optimize claim drafting to emphasize hardware integration and concrete technical effects, anticipating stricter inventive step scrutiny.
What to monitor: Monitor UKIPO examination guidelines regarding the assessment of technical contributions and inventive step for AI inventions.

items.title: The UPC’s Long Arm and the Limits of Its Reach
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院的长臂管辖及其效力边界
items.title_en: UPC Jurisdiction Limits: Domicile for Infringement, Registration Site for Validity
items.summary: For the past year, European patent lawyers have been discussing the “long arm” of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) as if jurisdiction were simply a question of geographical reach. In cross-border litigation, expansion has become the dominant theme. Yet in law, extension alone proves little. What ultimately matters is not how far a court may assert jurisdiction, but what it may validly decide and how far its decisions will be effective.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析统一专利法院（UPC）管辖权争议，指出重点非地理范围而是裁判有效性。引用 CJEU 在 BSH 诉 Electrolux 案中的判决，明确被告住所地法院可审理侵权，但专利有效性专属注册地法院管辖。UPC 作为多国共同法院，其管辖权受此原则约束，无效抗辩不自动剥夺侵权诉讼管辖权，但仅限当事人间效力。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines jurisdictional boundaries within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) framework, emphasizing that judicial effectiveness outweighs mere geographical reach. It highlights the CJEU’s judgment in BSH Hausgeräte GmbH v Electrolux AB, which clarifies that while courts at the defendant’s domicile retain jurisdiction over infringement claims under Regulation 1215/2012, exclusive jurisdiction over patent validity remains with the Member State of registration. Consequently, an invalidity defense does not automatically strip the seised court of authority to hear infringement matters. However, any validity assessment by non-registration courts is limited to incidental, inter partes effects, ensuring UPC rulings respect territorial and subject-matter constraints inherent in European patent law.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉时，可利用住所地管辖规则争取有利 forum，但需警惕异地专利被附带挑战无效的风险。建议提前布局欧洲专利组合，评估各国注册状态，制定分国别诉讼策略以规避管辖陷阱。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and defendants operating in Europe must strategically navigate the distinction between infringement and validity jurisdictions. While plaintiffs may leverage defendant domicile rules to secure favorable forums for infringement claims, they face the risk of incidental validity challenges affecting specific national parts of a bundle patent. Conversely, defendants can utilize these rules to manage litigation exposure but must prepare for fragmented validity proceedings. Proactive portfolio management and tailored litigation strategies are essential to mitigate risks associated with cross-border enforcement and potential invalidity defenses across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU 确认被告住所地法院对跨国专利侵权拥有管辖权", "专利有效性争议专属注册地成员国法院，他国仅能附带审查", "UPC 管辖权遵循布鲁塞尔条例，裁决效力受地域与事项限制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU confirmed that defendant domicile courts hold jurisdiction over cross-border patent infringement actions under Brussels I bis Regulation.", "Exclusive jurisdiction regarding patent validity and registration remains strictly reserved for the courts of the Member State where the patent is registered.", "Non-registration courts may examine validity only incidentally with inter partes effect, limiting the broader impact of their decisions on foreign registers."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用被告住所地规则选择有利法院，掌握诉讼主动权", "防范异地专利在侵权诉讼中被附带挑战无效的连锁风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage defendant domicile rules to select favorable forums for infringement litigation while anticipating validity challenges.", "Practice note: Assess national registration statuses early to develop segmented litigation strategies that address both infringement and validity risks.", "What to monitor: Track how UPC divisions apply incidental validity assessments and the subsequent enforceability of such decisions across borders."]
core_points_en: The CJEU confirmed that defendant domicile courts hold jurisdiction over cross-border patent infringement actions under Brussels I bis Regulation.
Exclusive jurisdiction regarding patent validity and registration remains strictly reserved for the courts of the Member State where the patent is registered.
Non-registration courts may examine validity only incidentally with inter partes effect, limiting the broader impact of their decisions on foreign registers.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage defendant domicile rules to select favorable forums for infringement litigation while anticipating validity challenges.
Practice note: Assess national registration statuses early to develop segmented litigation strategies that address both infringement and validity risks.
What to monitor: Track how UPC divisions apply incidental validity assessments and the subsequent enforceability of such decisions across borders.

items.title: Chinese prosthesis manufacturer prevails against Ivoclar with Graf von Westphalen
items.title_zh: 中国假牙制造商借 Graf von Westphalen 律所胜诉 Ivoclar
items.title_en: Huliang Bio-Tech Prevails Against Ivoclar Vivadent in German Dental Blank Patent Dispute
items.summary: Nowadays, dental prostheses are no longer elaborately modelled by dental technicians, but milled in one piece from special blanks. These usually consist of several layers of colour, for example to realistically highlight the gums and teeth. The dispute between Ivoclar Vivadent and its Chinese competitor Huliang Bio-Tech revolves around such flesh- and tooth-coloured blanks. Ivoclar […] The post Chinese prosthesis manufacturer prevails against Ivoclar with Graf von Westphalen appeared first on JU
items.ai_summary_zh: 牙科修复体正从手工模型转向多层色毛坯铣削制造。列支敦士登巨头 Ivoclar Vivadent 与中国竞争者 Huliang Bio-Tech 就此类肉色与牙色毛坯发生专利纠纷。在 Graf von Westphalen 律所代理下，中国制造商成功抵御 Ivoclar 的指控并 prevailing，凸显了欧洲专利诉讼中技术细节与法律策略的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: The dental prosthesis industry has shifted from manual modeling to milling one-piece blanks featuring multi-layered color gradients for realistic gum and tooth aesthetics. A significant patent dispute arose between Liechtenstein-based market leader Ivoclar Vivadent and Chinese competitor Huliang Bio-Tech concerning these flesh- and tooth-colored blanks. Represented by the prominent German firm Graf von Westphalen, Huliang successfully defended against Ivoclar’s infringement allegations. The outcome underscores the critical importance of precise technical analysis and robust legal strategy in European patent litigation. This case highlights how detailed examination of manufacturing processes and product specifications can determine the validity of patent claims in complex medical device sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出海需重视欧洲专利布局与侵权风险应对。本案表明，借助当地顶级律所专业力量，可有效抵御跨国巨头诉讼。建议企业加强核心技术自由实施分析，建立常态化海外维权机制。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the strategic necessity for companies operating in the European medical device sector to maintain rigorous patent freedom-to-operate analyses before market entry. Effective defense against multinational litigants often depends on engaging specialized local counsel capable of dissecting complex technical features and challenging patent validity with precision. Rights holders should establish proactive monitoring mechanisms for potential infringements while simultaneously preparing robust defensive strategies. Understanding the nuances of German patent enforcement and leveraging expert local legal support are essential for mitigating litigation risks and protecting commercial interests in cross-border disputes involving advanced manufacturing technologies.

items.title: Longevity Law in Germany: Intellectual Property and Regulatory Pathways for the Emerging Healthspan Industry | Grünecker
items.title_zh: 德国长寿法：健康寿命产业的知识产权与监管路径
items.title_en: Longevity Law in Germany: IP Protection and Regulatory Pathways for the Healthspan Industry
items.summary: Longevity law in Germany: how IP protection, EU regulation and data governance shape the development and commercialization of healthspan technologies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析德国及欧盟长寿产业面临的法律挑战，指出该领域融合生物技术、AI 与数字健康，需跨越专利保护、药品法规及数据治理等多重框架。文章强调，正确的专利策略与监管分类是创新技术成功商业化的关键，并定义了涵盖 IP 与合规的跨学科“长寿法”概念。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the legal challenges facing the longevity sector in Germany and the European Union. It highlights how this emerging field integrates biotechnology, artificial intelligence, and digital health, requiring navigation across multiple regulatory frameworks including patent protection, pharmaceutical regulations, and data governance. The article emphasizes that effective patent strategies and accurate regulatory classification are critical determinants for the successful commercialization of innovations. Furthermore, it defines the concept of 'longevity law' as an interdisciplinary framework addressing the complex intersection of intellectual property rights and compliance requirements within the healthspan economy.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局长寿科技时，应提前规划专利与监管双重策略。鉴于技术跨界特性，需精准界定产品属性以匹配合规路径，避免因分类错误导致市场准入受阻或 IP 保护落空。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in the healthspan sector, a siloed approach to legal strategy is insufficient due to the hybrid nature of these technologies. Rights holders must proactively align intellectual property filings with anticipated regulatory pathways to ensure market access. Misclassification of products can lead to significant delays or barriers to entry, while inadequate IP coverage may leave core innovations unprotected. Therefore, stakeholders should adopt a holistic strategy that simultaneously addresses patentability, regulatory compliance, and data privacy obligations from the early stages of product development.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["长寿技术需统筹专利保护、药械法规及数据治理框架", "专利策略与监管分类决定创新能否在欧洲成功上市", "行业催生跨学科“长寿法”，解决 IP 与商业化复杂问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Longevity technologies must integrate patent protection with pharmaceutical device regulations and comprehensive data governance frameworks.", "Strategic patent planning and precise regulatory classification determine whether innovations can successfully reach the European market.", "The industry drives the development of interdisciplinary longevity law to resolve complex intellectual property and commercialization challenges."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["针对跨界技术，需制定融合专利与合规的综合战略", "准确进行监管分类是预防欧洲市场准入风险的关键"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must develop integrated strategies combining patent protection and regulatory compliance to navigate the complex longevity sector effectively.", "Practice note: Accurate regulatory classification is essential to prevent market access risks and ensure robust intellectual property protection for cross-sector technologies.", "What to monitor: Evolving definitions of medical devices versus digital health tools in EU jurisdictions to adapt filing and compliance strategies accordingly."]
core_points_en: Longevity technologies must integrate patent protection with pharmaceutical device regulations and comprehensive data governance frameworks.
Strategic patent planning and precise regulatory classification determine whether innovations can successfully reach the European market.
The industry drives the development of interdisciplinary longevity law to resolve complex intellectual property and commercialization challenges.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must develop integrated strategies combining patent protection and regulatory compliance to navigate the complex longevity sector effectively.
Practice note: Accurate regulatory classification is essential to prevent market access risks and ensure robust intellectual property protection for cross-sector technologies.
What to monitor: Evolving definitions of medical devices versus digital health tools in EU jurisdictions to adapt filing and compliance strategies accordingly.

items.title: ‘Big Five’ publishers sue shadow library over alleged AI developer links
items.title_zh: 五大出版商起诉影子图书馆涉 AI 开发链接
items.title_en: Major Publishers Sue Shadow Library Over Alleged AI Training Data Links
items.summary: ‘Big Five’ publishers sue shadow library over alleged AI developer links
items.ai_summary_zh: 英语世界五大出版商联合其他机构，对某影子图书馆档案库提起大规模版权侵权诉讼。原告指控该档案库不仅提供盗版内容，还与人工智能开发者存在关联，涉嫌为 AI 模型训练非法提供数据源。此案反映了传统出版业对生成式 AI 数据合规性的强烈关注及法律反击。
items.ai_summary_en: The five largest English-language publishing houses, alongside other industry stakeholders, have initiated legal proceedings against a prominent shadow library archive. The complaint alleges massive-scale copyright infringement, asserting that the platform not only distributes pirated literary works but also maintains connections with artificial intelligence developers. Plaintiffs claim the archive serves as an unauthorized data source for training generative AI models without proper licensing or consent. This litigation underscores the traditional publishing sector's intensified focus on data compliance within the generative AI landscape. It represents a significant legal countermeasure aimed at regulating how copyrighted content is accessed and utilized for machine learning purposes, signaling a broader industry effort to protect intellectual property rights against perceived unauthorized exploitation by technology firms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业及 AI 公司需警惕欧洲版权诉讼风险，确保训练数据来源合法。建议建立严格的数据合规审查机制，避免使用未授权影子图书馆资源，以防卷入跨国知识产权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and technology companies operating in Europe should closely monitor this litigation, as it may establish critical precedents regarding the legality of using shadow libraries for AI training data. The outcome could significantly influence future copyright enforcement strategies and data sourcing compliance requirements. Market participants must ensure robust due diligence mechanisms are in place to verify the provenance of datasets used in generative AI systems. Reliance on unlicensed archives poses substantial legal risks, including potential liability for mass infringement. Proactive assessment of data supply chains is essential to mitigate exposure to cross-border intellectual property disputes and align with evolving regulatory expectations in the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["五大出版商发起诉讼，指控影子图书馆大规模侵犯版权", "案件焦点在于档案库是否向 AI 开发者非法提供训练数据", "凸显出版界利用法律手段遏制 AI 无序获取版权内容的趋势"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Leading publishers filed suit alleging the shadow library facilitates large-scale copyright infringement through its extensive digital archive.", "The case centers on claims that the platform illegally supplies copyrighted texts to AI developers for model training purposes.", "This action highlights the publishing industry's strategic use of litigation to curb unauthorized access to protected content for AI development."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严控训练数据来源，规避使用未授权影子图书馆资源", "建立数据合规审查机制，应对欧洲日益严厉的版权执法"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies utilizing web-scraped data for AI training must assess litigation risks associated with unlicensed sources like shadow libraries.", "Practice note: Implement strict data compliance reviews to verify authorization status of training datasets and avoid infringing materials.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of fair use and text-and-data mining exceptions in the context of generative AI development."]
core_points_en: Leading publishers filed suit alleging the shadow library facilitates large-scale copyright infringement through its extensive digital archive.
The case centers on claims that the platform illegally supplies copyrighted texts to AI developers for model training purposes.
This action highlights the publishing industry's strategic use of litigation to curb unauthorized access to protected content for AI development.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies utilizing web-scraped data for AI training must assess litigation risks associated with unlicensed sources like shadow libraries.
Practice note: Implement strict data compliance reviews to verify authorization status of training datasets and avoid infringing materials.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of fair use and text-and-data mining exceptions in the context of generative AI development.

items.title: UPC Weekly - Asking the neighbours for some directions – UPC Court of Appeal makes first referral to CJEU
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖权首次向欧盟法院提请裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson v. Dreame
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal makes the UPC’s first referral to the CJEU on the topic of long-arm jurisdiction.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在戴森诉追觅案中，首次就长臂管辖权问题向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请初步裁决。核心争议在于 UPC 是否对非欧盟被告在非 UPC 成员国（如西班牙）的侵权行为拥有管辖权。法院将案件拆分为管辖权（暂停审理等待裁定）与实体侵权（已作终局判决）两部分处理，此举将确立欧洲专利跨境执法的关键先例。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued its first referral to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) regarding long-arm jurisdiction. The central legal question concerns whether the UPC possesses jurisdiction over a non-EU based defendant for alleged patent infringement occurring in a non-UPC EU member state, specifically Spain. In the Dyson v. Dreame litigation, the Court of Appeal strategically bifurcated the proceedings. It stayed the appeal concerning jurisdictional defenses pending the CJEU’s preliminary ruling. Conversely, it issued a final decision on substantive issues, including claim interpretation and infringement findings, applicable to defendants and territories where no jurisdictional defense was raised. This split approach allows immediate enforcement against certain parties while seeking authoritative guidance on cross-border jurisdictional limits, establishing a critical precedent for future European patent litigation involving international supply chains.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕 UPC 长臂管辖风险，即便主体在非欧盟或非 UPC 国家，仍可能因锚定被告被卷入诉讼。建议重新评估欧洲销售架构与合规代表设置，提前制定应对跨境禁令的策略，避免产品在整个欧盟市场受阻。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights significant jurisdictional risks for rights holders and market participants operating across global supply chains. Even entities located outside the EU or non-UPC states may face UPC proceedings if linked to an anchor defendant within the court’s reach. Companies should proactively assess their European sales structures, particularly the role of authorized representatives and service providers mandated by EU product safety regulations. Strategic planning for potential cross-border preliminary injunctions is essential to mitigate the risk of widespread market disruption. Monitoring the CJEU’s eventual ruling will be crucial for understanding the outer limits of UPC jurisdiction and refining compliance strategies for pan-European intellectual property enforcement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院首次向 CJEU 提请关于长臂管辖权的法律解释请求", "核心争议聚焦非欧盟被告在非 UPC 成员国侵权时的管辖权界定", "案件被拆分处理：管辖权问题暂停，实体侵权部分已出终局判决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal submits its first request for a preliminary ruling to the CJEU concerning the scope of long-arm jurisdiction under the UPC Agreement.", "The core dispute addresses whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over non-EU defendants for infringement acts occurring within non-UPC EU member states like Spain.", "The Court bifurcated the case, staying jurisdictional questions for the CJEU while issuing a final substantive infringement decision for other defendants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非欧盟主体亦可能受 UPC 长臂管辖，需排查欧洲销售链条风险", "应优化欧盟授权代表布局，防范因单一节点被诉导致全域禁售"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-EU entities may fall under UPC jurisdiction via anchor defendants, necessitating a review of European sales architectures and representative appointments.", "Practice note: Litigants should prepare for bifurcated proceedings where substantive infringement decisions proceed independently of pending jurisdictional referrals.", "What to monitor: The CJEU’s forthcoming interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction will define the boundaries of cross-border patent enforcement in Europe."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal submits its first request for a preliminary ruling to the CJEU concerning the scope of long-arm jurisdiction under the UPC Agreement.
The core dispute addresses whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over non-EU defendants for infringement acts occurring within non-UPC EU member states like Spain.
The Court bifurcated the case, staying jurisdictional questions for the CJEU while issuing a final substantive infringement decision for other defendants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-EU entities may fall under UPC jurisdiction via anchor defendants, necessitating a review of European sales architectures and representative appointments.
Practice note: Litigants should prepare for bifurcated proceedings where substantive infringement decisions proceed independently of pending jurisdictional referrals.
What to monitor: The CJEU’s forthcoming interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction will define the boundaries of cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.

items.title: Mickey Mouse In The Public Domain. An Opportunity Or A Trap For Business?
items.title_zh: 米奇老鼠进入公有领域：商机还是陷阱？
items.title_en: Mickey Mouse Enters Public Domain: Strategic Opportunity or Legal Trap for Rights Holders
items.summary: The market for toys and licensed products is one of the most dynamic segments of the creative economy. On 1 January every year, more works from pop culture and other areas enter the public domain, creating huge commercial opportunities for entrepreneurs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出米奇老鼠（1928 年版）版权到期进入公有领域，但现代形象仍受保护。欧盟知识产权局驳回了
items.ai_summary_en: The expiration of copyright for the 1928 version of Mickey Mouse in Steamboat Willie has sparked significant commercial interest. However, rights holders must recognize that only this specific early depiction is now in the public domain. The modern, evolved appearance of the character remains fully protected under Disney’s extensive intellectual property portfolio. Consequently, using contemporary versions of Mickey Mouse without authorization constitutes infringement. This distinction creates a complex legal landscape where businesses might mistakenly believe they have unrestricted freedom to exploit the character. Recent actions by the European Union Intellectual Property Office highlight these risks, demonstrating that trademark applications attempting to capitalize on the 'free' status of the character may face rejection if they risk misleading consumers regarding origin or affiliation with the original rights holder.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧开发 IP 衍生品时，须严格区分公有领域素材与现存商标权。避免直接使用知名角色名称或近似标识，以防因误导消费者被异议，造成品牌声誉受损及经济损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exploiting public domain works must rigorously distinguish between expired copyrights and surviving trademark protections. Direct use of famous character names or visually similar logos can trigger opposition based on likelihood of confusion, even if the underlying artwork is free. Companies should conduct thorough clearance searches before launching products derived from historical pop culture figures. Failing to assess these overlapping rights may result in costly litigation, brand damage, and financial losses. Strategic IP planning requires verifying that commercial use does not imply endorsement by the original rights holder, ensuring compliance with both copyright limitations and trademark laws across key jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅特定版本米奇版权到期，现代形象仍受迪士尼权利保护", "欧盟知识产权局驳回相关商标注册，认定易误导消费者", "企业利用公有领域素材需警惕混淆现有商标权的法律风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Only the 1928 Steamboat Willie version of Mickey Mouse entered the public domain; modern iterations remain strictly protected.", "The EUIPO rejected a trademark application for 'MICKEY IS FREE!' on clothing, citing risks of consumer confusion and deception.", "Businesses utilizing public domain materials must carefully navigate existing trademark rights to avoid severe legal and reputational consequences."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["精准界定公有领域范围，避免误用仍受保护的现代形象", "申请商标前评估混淆风险，规避对知名品牌的搭便车行为"]

core_points_en: Only the 1928 Steamboat Willie version of Mickey Mouse entered the public domain; modern iterations remain strictly protected.
The EUIPO rejected a trademark application for 'MICKEY IS FREE!' on clothing, citing risks of consumer confusion and deception.
Businesses utilizing public domain materials must carefully navigate existing trademark rights to avoid severe legal and reputational consequences.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must verify that public domain status applies only to specific historical versions, not modern character designs still covered by active trademark…
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive trademark clearance searches before filing applications involving public domain characters to mitigate risks of refusal based on consumer conf…

items.title: Successful Hearing for GEMA Against Suno
items.title_zh: GEMA 诉 Suno 案听证会获初步成功
items.title_en: Munich Court Hears GEMA Copyright Case Against AI Music Generator Suno
items.summary: Default Description
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院审理 GEMA 指控 Suno 利用六首受版权保护作品训练 AI 模型并生成可识别副本的案件。双方确认数据源自 YouTube 用于训练。法院倾向于参照 GEMA 诉 OpenAI 案逻辑，认定若训练导致近乎 identical 复制即构成侵权。Suno 质疑管辖权及作品相似性，案件仍在审理中。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court held a hearing in GEMA’s copyright infringement lawsuit against Suno, focusing on the use of six protected musical compositions for training an AI music generator. Both parties confirmed that the defendant accessed these works via YouTube for model training. GEMA alleges that the AI produces outputs where original songs are recognizable, constituting unauthorized reproduction and public performance. The court appears inclined to follow precedents set in similar cases, such as GEMA versus OpenAI, suggesting that if training results in near-identical copies or recognizable derivatives, it may constitute copyright infringement. Suno challenges international jurisdiction and the similarity of outputs. The case remains pending, highlighting significant legal risks for AI developers using copyrighted data.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 音乐企业需警惕欧盟严格版权执法，避免使用未授权作品训练模型。建议建立合规数据清洗机制，评估欧洲诉讼风险，防止因生成内容相似性面临禁令与赔偿。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing generative AI models must rigorously assess the copyright status of training datasets, particularly within the European Union. The evolving judicial landscape in Germany indicates strict enforcement against unlicensed use of creative works. Companies should implement robust data compliance mechanisms to mitigate litigation risks. Failure to secure proper licenses for training materials may result in injunctions and substantial damages. Proactive legal review of data sourcing strategies is essential to ensure business continuity and avoid disruptive legal proceedings in key European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑法院审理 GEMA 诉 Suno 案，聚焦 AI 训练数据版权侵权问题", "法院倾向认定若 AI 输出可识别原曲，则训练过程构成版权复制侵权", "被告质疑国际管辖权及输出相似性，原告援引德国集体管理法案维权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich Regional Court examines copyright infringement claims regarding AI training data usage by Suno.", "Court suggests that recognizable AI outputs derived from training may constitute unauthorized copyright reproduction.", "Defendant challenges international jurisdiction and output similarity, while plaintiff relies on German collective management laws."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["赴欧 AI 企业应审查训练数据来源，规避未授权音乐作品引发的侵权风险", "需关注德国司法实践，预判生成内容相似性判定标准对业务模式的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: AI developers must verify licensing for all training data to prevent copyright infringement claims in Europe.", "Practice note: Establish comprehensive data cleansing protocols and legal assessments for input sources used in model training.", "What to monitor: German judicial interpretations of similarity standards and their impact on AI-generated content liability."]
core_points_en: Munich Regional Court examines copyright infringement claims regarding AI training data usage by Suno.
Court suggests that recognizable AI outputs derived from training may constitute unauthorized copyright reproduction.
Defendant challenges international jurisdiction and output similarity, while plaintiff relies on German collective management laws.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AI developers must verify licensing for all training data to prevent copyright infringement claims in Europe.
Practice note: Establish comprehensive data cleansing protocols and legal assessments for input sources used in model training.
What to monitor: German judicial interpretations of similarity standards and their impact on AI-generated content liability.

items.title: Beware the trade secret “striptease”
items.title_zh: 警惕商业秘密的“脱衣舞”式披露
items.title_en: Avoiding the Trade Secret Striptease: Managing Disclosure Risks in Collaborations
items.summary: Saturday Opinion: Companies often reveal more than they should during collaborations and should be on their guard for disclosure risks
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出企业在欧洲开展合作时，常因过度披露信息而面临商业秘密泄露风险。作者比喻此为“脱衣舞”，警示公司需在协作初期建立严格的信息防火墙，明确保密范围，防止核心技术在无保护状态下被合作伙伴获取，从而丧失法律救济基础。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary highlights a critical vulnerability for companies engaging in European collaborations: the tendency to disclose excessive sensitive information prematurely. The author characterizes this phenomenon as a trade secret striptease, where rights holders inadvertently strip away legal protections by revealing core technologies without adequate safeguards. Such over-disclosure during early partnership stages can fatally undermine the legal basis for trade secret protection, rendering subsequent enforcement actions ineffective. To mitigate these risks, organizations must establish rigorous information firewalls at the outset of any engagement. This includes clearly defining the scope of confidential information and implementing strict access controls. By preventing unprotected exposure of technical parameters to partners, companies preserve their ability to seek legal remedies and maintain the competitive advantage inherent in their proprietary knowledge.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧合作时应摒弃“先信任后签约”习惯，务必在接触前签署严密 NDA 并限定披露颗粒度。建议建立内部合规审查流程，避免技术人员在非受控环境下过度展示核心参数，确保存证完整以备维权。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should abandon informal trust-based approaches in favor of structured compliance protocols before initiating technical exchanges. It is imperative to execute comprehensive non-disclosure agreements that specify exact disclosure granularities rather than relying on broad framework intentions. Internal compliance reviews must be instituted to prevent engineers from demonstrating core parameters in uncontrolled environments. Ensuring robust evidence preservation mechanisms allows rights holders to effectively enforce their intellectual property claims if breaches occur, thereby maintaining the integrity of their trade secret portfolios throughout the collaboration lifecycle.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["企业合作中常出现超出必要的敏感信息披露行为", "缺乏前置保密措施将导致商业秘密法律保护失效", "需建立严格的信息分级与访问控制机制以防范风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Collaborative partnerships frequently involve unnecessary disclosure of sensitive data beyond what is strictly required for the project's success.", "Failure to implement preliminary confidentiality measures can result in the complete loss of legal protection for valuable trade secrets.", "Establishing strict information classification systems and access controls is essential to prevent unauthorized exposure of core assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["合作前必须签署涵盖具体范围的保密协议而非框架意向", "建立内部信息分级制度，严禁未经审批的核心数据外流"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must secure specific NDAs covering precise data scopes prior to any substantive technical discussions or demonstrations.", "Practice note: Implement internal information grading systems and strictly prohibit the external release of core data without formal approval.", "What to monitor: Monitor partner interactions for signs of premature information sharing and ensure all disclosures are documented for potential litigation."]
core_points_en: Collaborative partnerships frequently involve unnecessary disclosure of sensitive data beyond what is strictly required for the project's success.
Failure to implement preliminary confidentiality measures can result in the complete loss of legal protection for valuable trade secrets.
Establishing strict information classification systems and access controls is essential to prevent unauthorized exposure of core assets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must secure specific NDAs covering precise data scopes prior to any substantive technical discussions or demonstrations.
Practice note: Implement internal information grading systems and strictly prohibit the external release of core data without formal approval.
What to monitor: Monitor partner interactions for signs of premature information sharing and ensure all disclosures are documented for potential litigation.

items.title: The first CJEU referral from the UPC
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖问题首次向 CJEU 提请初步裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson Case
items.summary: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 在戴森诉追觅及 Eurep 案中，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院于 3 月 6 日发布命令，暂停涉及西班牙领土及非欧盟制造商授权代表的上诉程序。法院正式请求欧盟法院（CJEU）就长臂管辖权及锚定被告问题作出四项初步裁决。此举标志着 UPC 首次启动向 CJEU 的转介程序，旨在厘清跨境专利侵权诉讼中的关键管辖权界限。
items.ai_summary_en: In the Dyson v Dreame & Eurep litigation, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued an order on 6 March staying appeal proceedings concerning Spain and Eurep, the authorized representative for non-EU manufacturers. The court formally requested preliminary rulings from the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) on four specific questions regarding long-arm jurisdiction and the use of anchor defendants. This marks the first instance of the UPC initiating a referral to the CJEU. The move aims to clarify critical jurisdictional boundaries in cross-border patent infringement disputes, specifically addressing whether courts can assert jurisdiction over entities lacking a direct presence within the EU member states involved.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需高度关注此案，因其直接决定非欧盟企业在欧被诉风险。若 CJEU 确认宽泛管辖权，中国企业即便无欧盟实体也可能面临诉讼。建议提前评估欧洲业务架构，制定应对长臂管辖的合规与诉讼策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This development carries significant implications for rights holders and market participants operating globally. A broad interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction by the CJEU could expose non-EU manufacturers to direct litigation risks within the UPC framework, even without physical establishments in Europe. Companies should proactively assess their European business structures and supply chain arrangements. Developing robust compliance protocols and litigation defense strategies is essential to mitigate potential exposure to cross-border patent enforcement actions under this evolving jurisdictional landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院在戴森案中首次暂停部分程序并转介 CJEU", "核心争议聚焦于针对非欧盟实体的长臂管辖权适用范围", "CJEU 的初步裁决将确立 UPC 体系下锚定被告的法律标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal paused partial proceedings in the Dyson case and initiated its first-ever referral to the CJEU for guidance.", "The central legal dispute focuses on determining the scope and applicability of long-arm jurisdiction against non-EU entities.", "The upcoming CJEU ruling will establish definitive legal standards for using anchor defendants within the UPC system."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非欧盟制造企业面临被直接纳入 UPC 长臂管辖的潜在风险", "亟需根据裁决预期调整欧洲市场准入架构与抗辩预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-EU manufacturing entities face increased risk of being subject to UPC long-arm jurisdiction directly.", "Practice note: Urgently adjust European market entry architectures and prepare defense scenarios based on anticipated rulings.", "What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final decision on anchor defendant criteria and its impact on future cross-border cases."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal paused partial proceedings in the Dyson case and initiated its first-ever referral to the CJEU for guidance.
The central legal dispute focuses on determining the scope and applicability of long-arm jurisdiction against non-EU entities.
The upcoming CJEU ruling will establish definitive legal standards for using anchor defendants within the UPC system.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-EU manufacturing entities face increased risk of being subject to UPC long-arm jurisdiction directly.
Practice note: Urgently adjust European market entry architectures and prepare defense scenarios based on anticipated rulings.
What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final decision on anchor defendant criteria and its impact on future cross-border cases.

items.title: The future of the patent profession: Are we looking into an AI abyss?
items.title_zh: 专利职业未来：我们正凝视 AI 深渊吗？
items.title_en: The Future of the Patent Profession: Navigating the AI Divide and Business Model Shifts
items.summary: AI presents a huge dilemma for patent attorneys. There is no doubt that AI will have a dramatic impact on the profession and the business model that many firms have relied on for decades. Faced with this challenge, there is a growing divide in the profession between the firms and attorneys who are actively adopting AI, and those that remain stubbornly AI-sceptic. Amongst the AI adopters,
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出人工智能给专利代理人带来巨大困境，将深刻改变行业及传统商业模式。目前业内出现明显分化：一部分律所积极拥抱 AI 技术，另一部分则坚持怀疑态度。这种分歧预示着专利服务领域即将发生结构性变革，从业者需直面技术冲击。
items.ai_summary_en: Artificial intelligence presents a significant dilemma for patent attorneys, poised to dramatically reshape the profession and disrupt traditional business models relied upon for decades. The industry is experiencing a growing divide between firms actively adopting AI technologies and those maintaining a skeptical stance. This polarization signals an impending structural transformation within patent services. As AI capabilities expand, the sector faces critical questions regarding efficiency, cost structures, and the evolving role of human expertise. The divergence in adoption rates suggests that the future landscape will be defined by how quickly practitioners integrate these tools. This commentary highlights the urgent need for the profession to confront technological impacts, as the gap between adopters and skeptics widens, potentially altering competitive dynamics and service delivery standards across the European intellectual property ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应关注律所技术路线差异，优先选择具备 AI 增效能力的合作伙伴以降低成本。同时，企业 IP 部门需评估自身流程，利用 AI 工具提升专利布局效率，避免在技术迭代中落后于采用新模式的竞争对手。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the diverging technological strategies of legal service providers. Selecting partners with demonstrated AI integration capabilities may enhance service efficiency and optimize cost structures for complex patent portfolios. Internally, corporate IP departments are advised to evaluate current workflows and consider implementing AI-driven tools to streamline patent prosecution and management processes. Proactive adaptation ensures that organizations maintain competitive parity against rivals leveraging new operational models. Failure to assess and integrate these technological advancements risks operational inefficiencies and potential delays in securing intellectual property rights, particularly in fast-moving technical sectors where speed and precision are paramount for effective market positioning.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI 技术将对专利行业商业模式产生戏剧性影响", "专利代理群体内部形成拥抱派与怀疑派的分野", "传统律所面临是否采纳 AI 技术的战略抉择"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI technology is expected to exert a dramatic influence on existing patent industry business models and operational frameworks.", "A clear division has emerged within the patent attorney community between proactive AI adopters and persistent skeptics.", "Traditional law firms face a critical strategic decision regarding whether to integrate AI technologies into their practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优选具备 AI 应用能力的欧洲律所以提升服务效能", "企业内部需主动引入 AI 工具优化专利管理流程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize engaging with European law firms that demonstrate robust AI application capabilities to improve overall service efficacy and responsiveness.", "Practice note: Corporate IP teams should actively introduce AI tools to optimize internal patent management workflows and reduce administrative burdens.", "What to monitor: Track the widening performance gap between AI-adopting and AI-skeptical firms to inform future vendor selection and budget allocation strategies."]
core_points_en: AI technology is expected to exert a dramatic influence on existing patent industry business models and operational frameworks.
A clear division has emerged within the patent attorney community between proactive AI adopters and persistent skeptics.
Traditional law firms face a critical strategic decision regarding whether to integrate AI technologies into their practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize engaging with European law firms that demonstrate robust AI application capabilities to improve overall service efficacy and responsiveness.
Practice note: Corporate IP teams should actively introduce AI tools to optimize internal patent management workflows and reduce administrative burdens.
What to monitor: Track the widening performance gap between AI-adopting and AI-skeptical firms to inform future vendor selection and budget allocation strategies.

items.title: Sisvel and Quectel go public with IoT patent licensing collaboration
items.title_zh: Sisvel 与 Quectel 公开物联网专利许可合作
items.title_en: Quectel and Sisvel Launch Collaboration to Streamline IoT Patent Licensing Access
items.summary: World’s largest module maker to offer “streamlined” access to pool licence to its IoT customers
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球最大物联网模组制造商 Quectel 与专利池运营方 Sisvel 宣布达成合作，旨在为其客户提供更简化的专利池许可接入路径。此举标志着产业链上游制造巨头开始主动整合标准必要专利许可资源，试图通过商业协作降低下游客户的合规成本与诉讼风险，重塑物联网领域的专利授权生态。
items.ai_summary_en: Quectel, recognized as the world’s largest Internet of Things module manufacturer, has announced a strategic collaboration with Sisvel, a prominent patent pool administrator. This partnership aims to provide Quectel’s IoT customers with streamlined and simplified access to essential patent licenses managed by Sisvel. The initiative marks a significant shift in the supply chain, where upstream manufacturing giants are actively integrating standard-essential patent licensing resources. By facilitating this commercial cooperation, the parties seek to reduce compliance costs and litigation risks for downstream clients. This development is poised to reshape the patent licensing ecosystem within the IoT sector, offering a more efficient pathway for hardware manufacturers and device makers to secure necessary intellectual property rights without navigating complex individual negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国物联网模组及终端企业应关注此“制造 + 许可”新模式，评估其对现有 FRAND 谈判策略的影响。建议提前梳理自身产品涉及的专利池范围，利用头部厂商的集采优势降低许可成本，同时警惕潜在的捆绑许可风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor this emerging 'manufacturing plus licensing' model, as it may significantly influence existing FRAND negotiation strategies. Companies ought to evaluate how such integrated channels affect their current licensing frameworks and cost structures. It is advisable to proactively map product portfolios against relevant patent pools to leverage the collective bargaining power offered by leading manufacturers. However, stakeholders must also remain vigilant regarding potential legal risks associated with bundled licensing arrangements. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for maintaining compliance while optimizing intellectual property expenditure in the evolving IoT landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Quectel 作为全球最大模组厂，推动简化专利池许可接入流程", "Sisvel 与其合作旨在优化物联网客户获取标准必要专利许可的效率", "该合作反映了硬件制造商在专利许可链条中角色的主动转变"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Quectel drives simplification of patent pool license access processes for its extensive global customer base.", "Sisvel collaborates to optimize efficiency for IoT clients seeking standard-essential patent licenses through unified channels.", "The agreement reflects a proactive role shift for hardware manufacturers within the broader intellectual property licensing value chain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注头部厂商主导的许可新模式对传统谈判格局的冲击", "利用规模化许可渠道降低成本，同时防范捆绑许可法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the impact of manufacturer-led licensing models on traditional FRAND negotiation dynamics and market positioning.", "Practice note: Leverage scalable licensing channels provided by major hardware vendors to reduce overall permissioning costs effectively.", "What to monitor: Watch for potential legal challenges or regulatory scrutiny concerning bundled licensing practices and anti-competitive concerns."]
core_points_en: Quectel drives simplification of patent pool license access processes for its extensive global customer base.
Sisvel collaborates to optimize efficiency for IoT clients seeking standard-essential patent licenses through unified channels.
The agreement reflects a proactive role shift for hardware manufacturers within the broader intellectual property licensing value chain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the impact of manufacturer-led licensing models on traditional FRAND negotiation dynamics and market positioning.
Practice note: Leverage scalable licensing channels provided by major hardware vendors to reduce overall permissioning costs effectively.
What to monitor: Watch for potential legal challenges or regulatory scrutiny concerning bundled licensing practices and anti-competitive concerns.
