items.title: Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks
items.title_zh: Thom Browne 诉阿迪达斯案：非传统商标面临的挑战
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Sieckmann Criteria for Non-Traditional Marks in Thom Browne v Adidas
items.summary: Kyrana Hulstein and Haydn Walker, ‘Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks (March 2026) Int...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在 Thom Browne 诉阿迪达斯等案中，重新审视了《1994 年商标法》关于“标志”的定义。法院重点厘清了图形表示与文字描述在非传统商标（如颜色、位置标记）中的相互作用，并明确了 Sieckmann 标准（清晰、精确、持久等）在实践中的具体适用，裁定涉案三条纹商标需满足更严格的注册可确定性要求。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal recently addressed the definition of a 'sign' under Section 1 of the Trade Marks Act 1994 in two significant decisions: Thom Browne v Adidas and Babek v Iceland. The judgments clarified the interplay between pictorial representations and written descriptions, particularly for colour and position marks. The court reaffirmed that the amended statutory requirements codify the Sieckmann criteria, mandating that marks be clear, precise, and self-contained to define the scope of protection. In Thom Browne v Adidas, the court examined whether Adidas’s three-stripe registrations met these strict standards for registrability and distinctive character. These rulings provide critical guidance on how non-traditional trademarks must be represented to ensure legal certainty and enforceability within the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请颜色或位置等非传统商标时，须确保图示与文字描述高度一致且精确，避免因定义模糊被驳回。面对大牌诉讼，应善用 Sieckmann 标准挑战对方商标有效性，同时提升自身商标布局的严谨性以规避侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking registration for non-traditional trademarks, such as colour or position marks, must ensure high consistency and precision between graphical representations and textual descriptions to avoid rejection based on ambiguity. When facing enforcement actions by major brands, parties should strategically leverage the Sieckmann criteria to challenge the validity of opposing registrations that lack clear definition. Furthermore, applicants should enhance the rigor of their own trademark portfolios to mitigate infringement risks. Ensuring that mark representations are durable, intelligible, and easily accessible is essential for maintaining robust legal standing and avoiding costly invalidity proceedings in European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院明确非传统商标必须通过图形和文字清晰界定保护范围", "重申 Sieckmann 标准是判断商标能否注册的核心法律依据", "阿迪达斯部分条纹商标因缺乏显著性或定义不清面临无效风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Non-traditional marks require precise graphical and textual definitions to clearly delineate the scope of protection granted to proprietors.", "The Sieckmann criteria remain the central legal standard for assessing whether a trademark representation is sufficiently clear and objective for registration.", "Adidas faced invalidity risks for certain stripe marks due to potential lack of distinctiveness or ambiguous definition under current UK law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非传统商标申请需严格符合清晰精确标准以防无效", "利用判例标准可有效应对国际大牌的商标维权攻势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure graphical and textual elements of non-traditional marks are precisely aligned to meet strict registrability standards.", "Practice note: Use Sieckmann criteria to challenge vague opponent registrations while strengthening own portfolio definitions against infringement claims.", "What to monitor: Judicial application of clarity and precision requirements for position and colour marks in subsequent UK and EU case law."]
core_points_en: Non-traditional marks require precise graphical and textual definitions to clearly delineate the scope of protection granted to proprietors.
The Sieckmann criteria remain the central legal standard for assessing whether a trademark representation is sufficiently clear and objective for registration.
Adidas faced invalidity risks for certain stripe marks due to potential lack of distinctiveness or ambiguous definition under current UK law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure graphical and textual elements of non-traditional marks are precisely aligned to meet strict registrability standards.
Practice note: Use Sieckmann criteria to challenge vague opponent registrations while strengthening own portfolio definitions against infringement claims.
What to monitor: Judicial application of clarity and precision requirements for position and colour marks in subsequent UK and EU case law.

items.title: KPN takes Oppo dispute to India, expands UPC campaign
items.title_zh: KPN 将 Oppo 纠纷诉至印度并扩大 UPC 诉讼战役
items.title_en: KPN Expands Global SEP Campaign Against Oppo with First WebRTC Case in India
items.summary: A WebRTC case – the first in India – is the latest action in a protracted spat between the Dutch telecoms company and India’s second-largest smartphone vendor
items.ai_summary_zh: 荷兰电信巨头 KPN 与印度手机厂商 Oppo 的长期专利纠纷升级。继在欧洲统一专利法院（UPC）提起诉讼后，KPN 首次在印度发起基于 WebRTC 技术的专利诉讼。此举标志着该争端已从欧洲本土蔓延至亚洲关键市场，显示出权利人多法域协同施压的策略，旨在通过全球诉讼网络迫使对手接受许可条件。
items.ai_summary_en: The long-standing patent dispute between Dutch telecom operator KPN and Indian smartphone manufacturer Oppo has escalated significantly. Following initial litigation at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) in Europe, KPN has now initiated its first patent infringement action in India based on WebRTC technology. This strategic move extends the conflict from European jurisdictions into key Asian markets, demonstrating a coordinated multi-jurisdictional enforcement strategy. By leveraging a global network of lawsuits, the rights holder aims to increase pressure on the implementer to accept licensing terms. The case highlights the growing trend of SEP holders utilizing parallel proceedings across different regions to strengthen their negotiating position regarding standard essential patents and global royalty rates.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the increasing complexity of managing SEP disputes across multiple legal systems. Rights holders are actively employing parallel litigation strategies to maximize leverage during negotiations. For market participants, particularly those operating globally, this signals the necessity of preparing for simultaneous enforcement actions in diverse jurisdictions. Companies must evaluate their exposure not just in traditional strongholds like Europe but also in emerging markets where procedural rules may differ. Effective IP management now requires a holistic view of global litigation risks and the potential for cross-border injunctions or damages claims that can impact overall business operations and supply chain stability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["KPN 首次在印度提起 WebRTC 专利诉讼，开辟新战场", "案件是 KPN 在 UPC 诉讼行动的延伸，体现全球联动策略", "纠纷焦点集中在标准必要专利许可及全球费率裁定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["KPN initiates its first WebRTC-based patent lawsuit in India, establishing a new front in the ongoing dispute with Oppo.", "The Indian filing serves as an extension of KPN's broader litigation campaign at the Unified Patent Court, reflecting a coordinated global strategy.", "The central focus remains on securing favorable standard essential patent licensing terms and determining appropriate global royalty rates."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国企业在欧面临"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Multinational implementers face heightened risk of coordinated global SEP enforcement requiring integrated defense strategies.", "Practice note: Monitor parallel filings in non-traditional jurisdictions to anticipate shifts in negotiation leverage and litigation tactics.", "What to monitor: UPC decisions and Indian court rulings on WebRTC standards to assess impacts on global FRAND rate determinations."]
core_points_en: KPN initiates its first WebRTC-based patent lawsuit in India, establishing a new front in the ongoing dispute with Oppo.
The Indian filing serves as an extension of KPN's broader litigation campaign at the Unified Patent Court, reflecting a coordinated global strategy.
The central focus remains on securing favorable standard essential patent licensing terms and determining appropriate global royalty rates.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Multinational implementers face heightened risk of coordinated global SEP enforcement requiring integrated defense strategies.
Practice note: Monitor parallel filings in non-traditional jurisdictions to anticipate shifts in negotiation leverage and litigation tactics.
What to monitor: UPC decisions and Indian court rulings on WebRTC standards to assess impacts on global FRAND rate determinations.

items.title: [Guest Post] Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape
items.title_zh: 博洛尼亚法院解析后 Cofemel 时代的设计与版权保护
items.title_en: Bologna Court Clarifies Design, Shape Marks, and Copyright Overlap Post-Cofemel
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest post by Katfriend Luigi Manna (attorney at Martini Manna & Partners), commenting on a decision from the Bologna IP Court. Here is what Luigi writes:  Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape by Luigi Manna A recent ruling of the IP Court
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析意大利博洛尼亚知识产权法院最新判决，探讨在后 Cofemel 判例背景下，蛇形产品的外观设计、立体商标与版权保护的界限。律师 Luigi Manna 指出，法院严格审视了艺术价值标准，厘清了不同权利类型对同一形状的叠加保护逻辑，为欧盟成员国处理类似案件提供了重要参考。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a recent ruling by the Bologna IP Court regarding the protection of snake-shaped products. The decision addresses the complex interplay between registered designs, three-dimensional trade marks, and copyright in the post-Cofemel legal landscape. Attorney Luigi Manna highlights how the court strictly applied artistic value standards to determine eligibility for copyright protection. The judgment clarifies the boundaries for cumulative protection of the same shape under different intellectual property regimes. This case offers significant guidance for rights holders and legal practitioners across EU member states navigating the evolving jurisprudence on when functional shapes qualify as works of art deserving of copyright protection alongside other IP rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口造型独特产品时，需警惕多重 IP 风险。建议提前评估形状的艺术高度，避免盲目主张版权；同时应针对意大利等关键市场制定差异化的外观与商标布局策略，以防侵权诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders exporting products with distinctive shapes to Europe should carefully assess whether their designs meet the threshold for artistic value to avoid unintended copyright exposure. It is prudent to evaluate the potential for cumulative IP protection early in the product development cycle. Companies operating in key markets like Italy should develop differentiated strategies for design registration and trade mark applications. This approach helps mitigate infringement risks and ensures robust protection without relying solely on copyright claims that may be difficult to enforce under current CJEU precedents.

items.title: NovaCloud strikes Oracle licence as Google, Amazon and Meta defend suits
items.title_zh: NovaCloud 获甲骨文许可并起诉谷歌等云巨头
items.title_en: NovaCloud Secures Oracle License While Suing Six Cloud Giants Over Ericsson Patents
items.summary: Powered by patents from Ericsson, NovaCloud reached a private licence deal with Oracle, but has sued six other cloud computing leaders
items.ai_summary_zh: 依托爱立信专利组合的实体 NovaCloud 已与甲骨文达成私下专利许可协议，终结了双方潜在纠纷。与此同时，该实体继续对谷歌、亚马逊、Meta 等六家云计算领军企业提起专利侵权诉讼。此案反映了标准必要专利（SEP）在云服务领域的商业化趋势及欧洲作为主要诉讼地的地位。
items.ai_summary_en: NovaCloud, an entity leveraging a patent portfolio licensed from Ericsson, has successfully concluded a private licensing agreement with Oracle. This settlement resolves potential disputes between the two parties regarding alleged intellectual property infringements. Concurrently, NovaCloud has initiated patent infringement litigation against six other major leaders in the cloud computing sector, including prominent technology firms such as Google, Amazon, and Meta. This dual strategy highlights a growing trend where standard essential patents (SEPs), traditionally associated with telecommunications hardware, are increasingly being enforced within the cloud services domain. The case underscores Europe’s continued significance as a primary jurisdiction for high-stakes SEP litigation, reflecting the expanding commercial application of communication technologies in modern cloud infrastructure and the resulting legal complexities for market participants operating across these intersecting technical fields.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国云服务企业出海需警惕通信 SEP 延伸至云服务的许可需求。建议提前排查爱立信等巨头专利包覆盖范围，评估潜在侵权风险，并制定 FRAND 谈判预案或无效应对策略，避免在欧洲市场遭遇类似诉讼阻击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants offering cloud services should anticipate increasing enforcement actions involving standard essential patents originally designed for telecommunications. As SEP holders expand their licensing strategies to cover cloud infrastructure, companies must proactively assess whether their services infringe upon these extended patent rights. Establishing robust internal mechanisms for SEP risk screening is critical, particularly for entities operating in or targeting the European market. Rights holders should prepare comprehensive FRAND negotiation frameworks and evaluate potential invalidity arguments early. Proactive engagement with patent portfolios from key licensors like Ericsson can mitigate litigation exposure and facilitate smoother commercial operations in this evolving regulatory landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["NovaCloud 基于爱立信专利与甲骨文达成私下许可和解", "该实体同时对谷歌、亚马逊等六家云厂商发起专利诉讼", "凸显通信专利向云计算领域延伸许可的商业策略与法律风险"]

items.title: Global top 100 report measures technological influence and quality in portfolio management
items.title_zh: 全球百强报告衡量技术影响力与组合管理质量
items.title_en: IAM Report Highlights Shift to Quality in Global Patent Portfolio Management
items.summary: As many organisations place greater emphasis on quality over scale, the 2026 Innovation Momentum report identifies organisations that have outperformed expectations relative to their own starting position two years earlier and highlights sustained representation among established innovators, as well as measurable shifts across industries and regions.
items.ai_summary_zh: IAM 媒体发布 2026 年创新动能报告，聚焦组织在专利组合管理中从规模向质量的转变。报告识别出相较于两年前起点表现超预期的机构，强调成熟创新者的持续代表性，并揭示跨行业与区域的可量化 shifts，为评估技术影响力提供基准。
items.ai_summary_en: IAM Media has released the 2026 Innovation Momentum report, which examines the strategic pivot among organizations from prioritizing patent portfolio scale to emphasizing quality. The analysis identifies entities that have significantly outperformed their own baseline metrics established two years prior. It underscores the sustained presence of established innovators while detailing measurable shifts in technological influence across various industries and geographic regions. By providing a robust benchmark for assessing technical impact, the report offers critical insights into how rights holders are redefining success in portfolio management through higher value standards rather than mere volume accumulation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应摒弃单纯追求专利数量的策略，转而关注高价值专利培育。建议对标报告中欧洲优秀企业的质量指标，优化海外布局，以应对欧洲市场对技术实质影响力的更高要求。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should reconsider strategies focused solely on patent volume, shifting instead toward cultivating high-value intellectual property assets. Aligning with European benchmarks for quality indicators can help optimize overseas portfolios, addressing the EU market's heightened requirements for substantive technological influence. Rights holders are advised to utilize the report’s data as a baseline to evaluate their competitive positioning regarding technical impact within Europe. This approach ensures that portfolio development reflects genuine innovation capacity rather than administrative bulk, supporting stronger enforcement and licensing potential in mature jurisdictions.

items.title: Comment of the European Copyright Society on the request for preliminary ruling in Case C-250/25 (Like Company)
items.title_zh: 欧洲版权协会就 Like Company 案初步裁决请求发表评论
items.title_en: European Copyright Society Comments on Preliminary Ruling Request in Like Company Case
items.summary: The European Copyright Society published its Opinion in Like Company. The European Copyright Society (ECS) was founded in 2012 with the aim of creating a platform for critical and independent scholarly thinking on European Copyright Law and policy.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲版权协会（ECS）就 Like Company 诉谷歌案发表评论，该案被视为界定 AI 训练范围的关键案件。ECS 指出提呈事实模糊，混淆了聊天机器人与大语言模型概念，且未清晰区分新闻出版者权与作者权。协会敦促欧盟法院谨慎审理，避免因技术定性不明而阻碍 AI 社会发展或导致过度许可经济。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Copyright Society (ECS) has published its Opinion regarding the request for a preliminary ruling in Like Company v Google (Case C-250/25). This case is viewed as a potential landmark decision, offering the Court of Justice of the European Union an opportunity to define the scope and conditions for permitted artificial intelligence training and to establish an infringement test for AI outputs. The ECS urges the Grand Chamber to exercise caution, noting that the reference contains factual ambiguities regarding the technology involved. Specifically, the submission conflates concepts such as chatbots, large language models, and search engines. Furthermore, the ECS highlights that the reference fails to consistently identify the subject matter, incorrectly mixing issues related to the press publishers’ right under Article 15 of the CDSMD with authorial works and rights under the InfoSoc Directive.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧部署 AI 产品需警惕版权合规风险，特别是新闻内容使用。建议密切关注本案进展，评估数据训练合法性，避免陷入潜在许可纠纷，并提前规划内容授权策略以应对可能的严格司法解读。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI products in Europe should closely monitor this proceeding, as it addresses critical compliance risks regarding the use of news content. The case may significantly influence the legal characterization of AI training data usage and the scope of permitted activities. Rights holders and technology providers are advised to assess the legality of their current data training practices and evaluate potential exposure to infringement claims. Proactively planning content authorization strategies and conducting thorough audits of source materials can help mitigate risks associated with potential strict judicial interpretations. Maintaining awareness of evolving standards for AI output liability and training data permissions remains essential for sustainable operations within the EU digital single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["ECS 指出案件事实描述模糊，混淆了多种 AI 技术概念", "提呈未能一致识别标的，混淆了新闻出版权与作者权利", "敦促法院谨慎裁决，防止因定义不清阻碍 AI 技术应用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The ECS notes that the reference presents factually murky descriptions, conflating distinct AI technologies like chatbots, large language models, and search engines.", "The submission fails to consistently identify the legal subject matter, confusing press publishers' rights under the CDSMD with traditional authorial copyright protections.", "The Society urges the Court to exercise caution to prevent unclear technical definitions from hindering AI development or creating excessive licensing burdens."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院对 AI 训练数据使用的最终法律定性", "提前排查新闻内容使用风险，规划合规授权路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final legal characterization of AI training data usage and its impact on copyright compliance obligations.", "Practice note: Conduct early risk assessments regarding the use of news content in AI systems and plan compliant authorization pathways.", "What to monitor: Developments in the distinction between press publishers' rights and authorial works within the context of AI-generated outputs."]
core_points_en: The ECS notes that the reference presents factually murky descriptions, conflating distinct AI technologies like chatbots, large language models, and search engines.
The submission fails to consistently identify the legal subject matter, confusing press publishers' rights under the CDSMD with traditional authorial copyright protections.
The Society urges the Court to exercise caution to prevent unclear technical definitions from hindering AI development or creating excessive licensing burdens.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final legal characterization of AI training data usage and its impact on copyright compliance obligations.
Practice note: Conduct early risk assessments regarding the use of news content in AI systems and plan compliant authorization pathways.
What to monitor: Developments in the distinction between press publishers' rights and authorial works within the context of AI-generated outputs.

items.title: Regeneron settles several aflibercept disputes following European injunctions
items.title_zh: 再生元在欧洲获禁令后达成多项阿柏西普纠纷和解
items.title_en: Regeneron Secures Multiple Aflibercept Settlements After UPC Injunction Victories
items.summary: Biologic innovator had secured a wave of agreements in wake of recent injunction victories
items.ai_summary_zh: 生物制药 innovator 再生元（Regeneron）在近期于欧洲获得针对阿柏西普（aflibercept）产品的禁令胜利后，迅速与多家竞争对手达成一系列和解协议。该动态表明统一专利法院（UPC）签发的禁令具有强大的市场威慑力，迫使被告方回到谈判桌，从而快速解决跨国专利争端，确立了权利人在欧洲市场的排他性优势。
items.ai_summary_en: Biologic innovator Regeneron has rapidly concluded a series of settlement agreements with multiple competitors following recent successful injunction actions in Europe concerning its aflibercept products. This development underscores the significant market deterrent effect of injunctions issued by the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The swift judicial relief compelled defendants to return to negotiations, facilitating the rapid resolution of cross-border patent disputes. Consequently, these outcomes have reinforced the rights holder's exclusive market advantages in Europe, demonstrating how effective injunctive relief can accelerate commercial resolutions and solidify market position for originator companies against generic or biosimilar entrants.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海需高度警惕 UPC 禁令的即时杀伤力，其可迫使企业迅速和解。建议提前开展 FTO 分析，评估欧洲专利风险，并制定包含诉讼与商业谈判的综合应对预案，避免陷入被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize the immediate operational impact of UPC injunctions, which can force rapid settlements and restrict market access. It is critical to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and assess European patent risks well before product launch. Companies should develop comprehensive strategies that integrate litigation defense with proactive commercial negotiation capabilities. Relying solely on procedural delays is increasingly ineffective given the UPC's capacity for swift enforcement. A dual-track approach combining robust legal defense with flexible business solutions is essential to mitigate the risk of sudden market exclusion and manage potential infringement liabilities effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["再生元凭借欧洲禁令胜利，迫使多方达成阿柏西普专利和解", "UPC 禁令展现出高效威慑力，加速了跨国专利纠纷的商业解决", "生物药企利用欧洲统一司法机制，成功巩固核心产品市场独占权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Regeneron leveraged European injunction victories to compel multiple parties into settling aflibercept patent disputes.", "UPC injunctions demonstrated high deterrence efficiency, accelerating the commercial resolution of cross-border patent conflicts.", "Biopharmaceutical firms successfully utilized the unified European judicial mechanism to consolidate market exclusivity for core products."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 禁令威慑巨大，中国药企须前置专利风险评估与 FTO 分析", "面对欧洲诉讼，应构建“诉讼 + 谈判”双轨策略以规避市场禁入"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UPC injunctions possess immediate enforceability, compelling swift settlements and potentially blocking market entry for infringing products.", "Practice note: Conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare integrated litigation and negotiation strategies to avoid passive positions.", "What to monitor: The evolving jurisprudence of the Unified Patent Court regarding the speed and scope of preliminary injunctions in biopharmaceutical cases."]
core_points_en: Regeneron leveraged European injunction victories to compel multiple parties into settling aflibercept patent disputes.
UPC injunctions demonstrated high deterrence efficiency, accelerating the commercial resolution of cross-border patent conflicts.
Biopharmaceutical firms successfully utilized the unified European judicial mechanism to consolidate market exclusivity for core products.

Practice note: Conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare integrated litigation and negotiation strategies to avoid passive positions.

items.title: [Guest Post] UK Assessment of Computer Implemented Inventions moves closer to EPO Practice - but by how far? (Emotional perception [2026] UKSC 3)
items.title_zh: 英国计算机实施发明评估向欧专局实践靠拢的程度分析
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Aligns Computer Implemented Invention Test with EPO Practice
items.summary: Now the dust has now settled on the high profile UK Supreme Court decision in Emotional Perception AI Limited (Appellant) v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks (Respondent) [2026] UKSC 3. Katfriend Greg Corcoran provides in-depth analysis: Over to Greg:"In Emotional Perception, the UK Supreme Court (SC) found a new test should apply in the UK for determining the
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文深入分析英国最高法院在 Emotional Perception 案中的判决，该判决确立了英国判断计算机实施发明的新测试标准。文章探讨此新标准如何使英国专利实践进一步向欧洲专利局（EPO）的审查惯例靠拢，并评估两者间剩余的具体差异，对人工智能领域专利布局具有关键指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the UK Supreme Court’s landmark decision in Emotional Perception [2026] UKSC 3, which establishes a new legal test for assessing the patentability of computer-implemented inventions. The article explores how this ruling significantly shifts UK patent examination practice closer to the established standards of the European Patent Office (EPO). It critically evaluates the remaining specific differences between the two jurisdictions’ approaches. The discussion highlights the strategic implications for patent applicants, particularly those operating in the artificial intelligence sector. By clarifying the legal boundaries for AI technologies, the piece provides essential guidance for navigating the evolving landscape of intellectual property protection in the UK and Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英申请 AI 专利时，应参照欧专局标准优化权利要求撰写，利用英欧标准趋同降低跨国布局成本。同时需密切关注新测试法的具体适用细节，避免因审查标准微调导致授权风险，及时调整欧洲整体专利策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage the growing alignment between UK and EPO standards to optimize cross-border patent strategies, potentially reducing costs associated with divergent drafting requirements. Applicants must carefully adapt claim structures to satisfy the new UK test, ensuring consistency with EPO practices to mitigate authorization risks. Continuous monitoring of how the UK Intellectual Property Office applies this new framework is essential. Practitioners should remain agile, adjusting European portfolio strategies as subtle shifts in examination criteria emerge during the initial implementation phase of this precedent.

items.title: Brexit "cloned" trademarks in 2026: the end of a transitional period that could affect your protection in the UK or the EU
items.title_zh: 2026 年脱欧克隆商标过渡期终结：英欧使用要求彻底分离
items.title_en: Brexit Cloned Trademarks: End of Transitional Period and New UK-EU Use Requirements
items.summary: From 1 January 2026, UK trademarks require UK use and EU marks require EU use. Learn how Brexit impacts cloned marks and revocation risks ⚖️
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国脱欧后生成的克隆商标享有五年过渡期，允许利用欧盟使用证据维持英国商标。该过渡期于 2025 年底届满。自 2026 年 1 月 1 日起，英国克隆商标必须依赖英国境内的真实使用来维持效力，欧盟使用不再被认可；反之亦然。未在规定领土内积极使用的商标将面临因连续五年未使用而被撤销的风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The five-year transitional period for Brexit 'cloned' trademarks expired on 31 December 2025. During this window, rights holders could rely on EU use to maintain cloned UK marks and vice versa. From 1 January 2026, this mutual recognition has ceased. Cloned UK trademarks now strictly require genuine use within the United Kingdom to remain valid, while EU trademarks require use within the European Union. Use in one territory no longer supports protection in the other. Rights holders who have not actively used their marks in the specific jurisdiction face significant legal risks. Specifically, marks lacking genuine use for five continuous years are vulnerable to cancellation actions based on non-use. Companies must ensure their trademark portfolios comply with these separated territorial use requirements to avoid losing valuable intellectual property rights in either market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧持有克隆商标者，需立即核查过去五年的使用证据是否局限于单一区域。若仅在一地销售，应尽快在另一地建立真实商业使用或考虑重新申请，以防权利因非使用被撤销，确保持续保护。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders holding cloned trademarks should immediately audit their usage records to determine if commercial activity was limited to a single territory over the past five years. If genuine use exists only in the EU or only in the UK, the corresponding cloned mark in the other jurisdiction is at risk of revocation. To mitigate this, companies should establish real commercial use in the deficient market promptly. Alternatively, filing new applications may be necessary to secure fresh protection periods. Proactive portfolio management is essential to prevent unintended loss of rights following the end of the transitional arrangements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["脱欧克隆商标五年过渡期已于 2025 年 12 月 31 日正式届满", "年起英欧商标使用证据互认终止，须各自满足属地使用要求", "缺乏对应领土真实使用的商标将面临因非使用被撤销的法律风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The five-year transitional period allowing cross-territory use evidence for cloned trademarks ended on 31 December 2025.", "From 1 January 2026, UK cloned marks require UK use and EU marks require EU use; cross-recognition is terminated.", "Trademarks lacking genuine use in their respective territories face cancellation risks due to non-use provisions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即排查仅在一地使用的克隆商标，评估被撤销风险", "需在 2026 年前在缺失市场建立真实使用或采取补救措施"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Audit cloned marks to identify those with use restricted to only one of the two jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Initiate genuine commercial use in the deficient territory or consider new filings to secure protection.", "What to monitor: Potential non-use cancellation actions targeting cloned marks lacking local market presence."]
core_points_en: The five-year transitional period allowing cross-territory use evidence for cloned trademarks ended on 31 December 2025.
From 1 January 2026, UK cloned marks require UK use and EU marks require EU use; cross-recognition is terminated.
Trademarks lacking genuine use in their respective territories face cancellation risks due to non-use provisions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Audit cloned marks to identify those with use restricted to only one of the two jurisdictions.
Practice note: Initiate genuine commercial use in the deficient territory or consider new filings to secure protection.
What to monitor: Potential non-use cancellation actions targeting cloned marks lacking local market presence.

items.title: Quectel sued in UPC amid wave of patent suits against IoT module makers
items.title_zh: 移远通信在 UPC 遭诉，IoT 模块商迎专利诉讼潮
items.title_en: Quectel Sued in UPC as Philips and TurboCode Launch Patent Wave Against IoT Makers
items.summary: Philips has extended the long-running dispute to Europe, while TurboCode has launched slew of cases against Internet-of-Things companies
items.ai_summary_zh: 飞利浦将长期专利纠纷扩展至欧洲统一专利法院（UPC），起诉移远通信；同时 TurboCode 也对多家物联网公司发起大规模诉讼。此举标志着针对 IoT 模块制造商的专利维权浪潮正式席卷欧洲，UPC 成为新的核心战场。
items.ai_summary_en: Philips has extended its long-standing patent dispute into Europe by filing suit against Quectel at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). Concurrently, TurboCode has initiated a significant volume of infringement cases targeting various Internet-of-Things companies. These actions collectively signal that a concentrated wave of patent enforcement against IoT module manufacturers has formally reached Europe. The UPC is rapidly emerging as a central battleground for these disputes, reflecting a strategic shift by rights holders to leverage the new court’s jurisdiction. This development underscores the increasing intensity of IP litigation in the European market, where multiple claimants are simultaneously pursuing claims against key industry players, thereby altering the competitive and legal landscape for hardware suppliers operating within the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业应高度警惕 UPC 的跨境禁令风险，立即排查出口产品专利隐患。建议提前构建防御性专利组合，并制定针对 UPC 程序的快速响应与和解策略，避免市场准入受阻。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must urgently assess freedom-to-operate risks for products sold in Europe, given the heightened threat of cross-border injunctions at the UPC. Companies should establish specialized response mechanisms tailored to UPC procedural characteristics, including dedicated budget reserves for rapid legal engagement. Developing defensive patent portfolios and formulating swift settlement strategies are critical to mitigating potential market access disruptions. Proactive preparation allows firms to navigate the complex litigation environment effectively, ensuring business continuity while managing the financial and operational impacts of multi-jurisdictional patent enforcement actions in the unified European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["飞利浦利用 UPC 机制将专利争端从其他法域延伸至欧洲", "TurboCode 同步对多家物联网企业发起批量专利侵权诉讼", "欧洲 IoT 模块制造商正面临前所未有的集中化专利诉讼风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Philips utilizes the UPC mechanism to extend cross-border patent disputes from other jurisdictions directly into the European legal framework.", "TurboCode synchronously launches batch patent infringement lawsuits against multiple IoT enterprises, intensifying regional litigation pressure.", "European IoT module manufacturers face unprecedented risks of centralized patent litigation, with the UPC serving as the primary venue."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需紧急评估产品在欧销售的专利自由实施（FTO）风险", "应建立针对 UPC 诉讼特点的专项应对机制与预算储备"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Urgent assessment of product FTO risks in Europe is required due to the elevated risk of UPC cross-border injunctions.", "Practice note: Establish specialized response mechanisms and budget reserves tailored to UPC litigation characteristics for rapid action.", "What to monitor: Monitor the strategic use of UPC procedures by rights holders like Philips and TurboCode to anticipate further enforcement waves."]
core_points_en: Philips utilizes the UPC mechanism to extend cross-border patent disputes from other jurisdictions directly into the European legal framework.
TurboCode synchronously launches batch patent infringement lawsuits against multiple IoT enterprises, intensifying regional litigation pressure.
European IoT module manufacturers face unprecedented risks of centralized patent litigation, with the UPC serving as the primary venue.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Urgent assessment of product FTO risks in Europe is required due to the elevated risk of UPC cross-border injunctions.
Practice note: Establish specialized response mechanisms and budget reserves tailored to UPC litigation characteristics for rapid action.
What to monitor: Monitor the strategic use of UPC procedures by rights holders like Philips and TurboCode to anticipate further enforcement waves.

items.title: One and done for the UPC Court of Appeal
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭：一次裁决即终局
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Adopts One-and-Done Approach to Enhance Judicial Efficiency
items.summary: 2026 Week 9
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭的最新动态，指出其在特定程序中可能采取“一次裁决即终局”的立场。该动向表明上诉庭倾向于提高司法效率，减少程序拖延，对专利侵权及无效案件的最终确定性产生深远影响，标志着 UPC  jurisprudence 的进一步成熟。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent developments within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal, highlighting a potential shift toward a 'one and done' procedural stance. This approach indicates that the appellate body intends to finalize specific matters in a single ruling, thereby avoiding repetitive procedural steps. The trend reflects a strong judicial preference for efficiency and the reduction of delays in patent litigation. By limiting opportunities for prolonged appeals, the Court aims to provide greater finality and certainty in both infringement and invalidity disputes. This development marks a significant step in the maturation of UPC jurisprudence, signaling to market participants that the appellate process will prioritize swift resolution over extended procedural maneuvering, fundamentally altering the landscape of European patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更高效的专利诉讼环境，需提升应诉准备质量，避免因程序机会减少而陷入被动。建议提前布局证据链，评估上诉风险，利用确定性规则优化维权或防御策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must adapt to an increasingly efficient patent litigation environment under the UPC. With reduced procedural opportunities, the quality of initial evidence and defense preparation becomes paramount. Parties should proactively strengthen their evidentiary records at the first instance to avoid being placed in a passive position due to limited appeal options. It is advisable to reassess appeal strategies by carefully evaluating risks and leveraging the finality of judgments. Optimizing enforcement or defense tactics through early strategic planning ensures that rights holders can effectively navigate the streamlined appellate process while maintaining robust legal positions throughout the dispute lifecycle.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉庭确立高效裁决原则，避免程序反复", "单一裁决机制将加速欧洲专利纠纷的最终解决", "律所解读该趋势对诉讼双方策略制定的关键影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal establishes efficient adjudication principles to prevent repetitive procedural delays.", "A single-ruling mechanism accelerates the final resolution of complex European patent disputes significantly.", "Law firms interpret this trend as having critical implications for litigation strategy formulation by both parties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["适应 UPC 高效裁决趋势，强化一审证据质量", "重新评估上诉策略，把握终局性裁判特点"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt to the UPC's efficient adjudication trend by strengthening the quality of first-instance evidence.", "Practice note: Reassess appeal strategies to account for the finality and speed of single-ruling mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Procedural rulings from the Court of Appeal that define the scope of the one-and-done principle."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal establishes efficient adjudication principles to prevent repetitive procedural delays.
A single-ruling mechanism accelerates the final resolution of complex European patent disputes significantly.
Law firms interpret this trend as having critical implications for litigation strategy formulation by both parties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to the UPC's efficient adjudication trend by strengthening the quality of first-instance evidence.
Practice note: Reassess appeal strategies to account for the finality and speed of single-ruling mechanisms.
What to monitor: Procedural rulings from the Court of Appeal that define the scope of the one-and-done principle.

items.title: The UK Competition and Markets Authority announces its first ever fine under new consumer enforcement powers
items.title_zh: 英国 CMA 依据新消费者执法权开出首张罚单
items.title_en: UK CMA Imposes First Fine Under New Consumer Powers for Ignoring Information Notice
items.summary: Key takeaways The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) announced on 13 February 2026 that it has fined Euro Car Parks nearly half a million pounds...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争与市场管理局（CMA）对 Euro Car Parks 处以 47.3 万英镑罚款，因其无视信息通知且无正当理由。这是 CMA 依据《数字市场、竞争和消费者法》行使新权力的首例处罚，金额占上限 75%。涉事企业申请禁令失败并已提起上诉，凸显监管机构强化执法力度与程序合规要求。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has imposed a £473,000 fine on Euro Car Parks for failing to respond to an information notice without reasonable excuse. This marks the first penalty issued under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act, reflecting the regulator’s enhanced enforcement capabilities. The fine represents approximately 75% of the maximum allowable amount, underscoring the severity of the non-compliance. Despite multiple delivery attempts via mail and email, the company did not engage until notified of the impending penalty. The High Court recently refused an injunction to prevent the CMA from naming the firm. The company has appealed the decision, highlighting the critical importance of procedural compliance and timely engagement with regulatory authorities to avoid significant financial and reputational consequences.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英中企需高度重视监管函件响应机制，避免因程序疏忽引发高额罚款。面对 CMA 等机构增强的执法权，应建立快速合规响应流程，并在收到处罚意向时及时寻求法律救济，防止声誉受损及经济损失扩大。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the UK must prioritize robust mechanisms for handling official regulatory correspondence. Failure to engage with bodies like the CMA can lead to substantial penalties, even before formal investigations commence. Companies should establish dedicated protocols for managing statutory obligations and verify the authenticity of communications promptly. Upon receiving notices of intended penalties, immediate legal assessment is crucial to mitigate risks. Proactive compliance and swift engagement with regulators are essential to prevent escalation, protect corporate reputation, and minimize potential financial exposure under the strengthened consumer protection framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CMA 开出首笔罚款，认定企业无理由忽视官方信息通知", "罚款金额达 47.3 万英镑，反映违规行为的严重性与威慑力", "企业试图阻止具名失败并提起上诉，案件进入司法审查阶段"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CMA issued its first fine under new powers, ruling that ignoring official notices lacks reasonable excuse.", "The £473,000 penalty reflects severe non-compliance and serves as a strong deterrent against future violations.", "The company failed to block public naming and has appealed the fine, initiating judicial review proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立官方函件专人专管机制，杜绝因失联导致的合规风险", "遭遇处罚意向时应迅速评估，利用司法程序争取缓冲或撤销"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish dedicated management for official regulatory correspondence to prevent compliance gaps.", "Practice note: Assess penalty intentions rapidly and utilize judicial procedures to seek relief or suspension.", "What to monitor: CMA's increasing use of information requests and enforcement actions under new consumer laws."]
core_points_en: CMA issued its first fine under new powers, ruling that ignoring official notices lacks reasonable excuse.
The £473,000 penalty reflects severe non-compliance and serves as a strong deterrent against future violations.
The company failed to block public naming and has appealed the fine, initiating judicial review proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish dedicated management for official regulatory correspondence to prevent compliance gaps.
Practice note: Assess penalty intentions rapidly and utilize judicial procedures to seek relief or suspension.
What to monitor: CMA's increasing use of information requests and enforcement actions under new consumer laws.

items.title: Trade Marks & Copyright 2026
items.title_zh: 2026 年葡萄牙商标与版权法律指南
items.title_en: Portugal Trademark and Copyright Overview: Legal Framework, International Treaties, and Protection Strategies for 2026
items.summary: Copyright is statutory and governed mainly by the Portuguese Copyright and Related Rights Code (Código do Direito de Autor e dos Direitos Conexos), enacted by Decree-Law No 63/85 of 14 March, as amended (most recently by Law No 47/2023).
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详述葡萄牙商标与版权法律体系。版权依《著作权法典》自动产生，无需注册；商标受《工业产权法典》管辖，涵盖声音、气味等非传统标志。葡萄牙作为欧盟成员及多项国际公约缔约国，直接适用欧盟法规及马德里体系，为外国权利人提供明确保护路径。
items.ai_summary_en: This guide outlines the Portuguese intellectual property landscape, detailing how copyright arises automatically under the Copyright and Related Rights Code without registration requirements. It explains that trademark protection is governed by the Industrial Property Code, which permits the registration of non-traditional signs such as sounds and smells. As an EU member state and signatory to major international conventions including the Berne Convention and the Madrid System, Portugal ensures direct applicability of EU regulations. This framework provides clear legal pathways and robust protection for foreign rights holders operating within the jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业进入葡萄牙市场应重视非传统商标注册布局，利用自动版权保护降低确权成本。同时需关注欧盟法规直接适用性，善用马德里体系优化海外商标管理，防范侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering Portugal should leverage the automatic nature of copyright protection to secure immediate legal status for creative assets without incurring registration costs. Simultaneously, businesses must strategically utilize the Madrid System alongside applicable EU regulations to optimize cross-border trademark portfolios. Given the direct applicability of EU laws, rights holders should align their compliance strategies with broader European standards to effectively mitigate infringement risks and enhance enforcement capabilities across the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["葡萄牙版权保护自动生效，无需履行任何登记手续即可获法律保护", "商标法允许注册声音、气味等非传统标志，并承认未注册驰名商标权益", "作为欧盟成员国，葡萄牙直接适用欧盟条例及马德里国际商标注册体系"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright protection in Portugal is automatic upon creation of original works, requiring no formal registration or administrative procedures.", "The Industrial Property Code allows registration of non-traditional trademarks like sounds and smells, while recognizing unregistered well-known marks.", "EU regulations and the Madrid System apply directly in Portugal, offering streamlined international trademark management and enforcement mechanisms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用版权自动保护机制，快速确立文创产品在葡法律地位", "结合马德里体系与欧盟规则，优化商标跨国布局与维权策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage automatic copyright protection to rapidly establish legal standing for cultural and creative products in Portugal without upfront registration expenses.", "Practice note: Integrate Madrid System filings with EU trademark strategies to streamline multinational portfolio management and reduce administrative burdens.", "What to monitor: Track developments in EU regulations directly applicable in Portugal to ensure ongoing compliance and proactive enforcement against potential infringements."]
core_points_en: Copyright protection in Portugal is automatic upon creation of original works, requiring no formal registration or administrative procedures.
The Industrial Property Code allows registration of non-traditional trademarks like sounds and smells, while recognizing unregistered well-known marks.
EU regulations and the Madrid System apply directly in Portugal, offering streamlined international trademark management and enforcement mechanisms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage automatic copyright protection to rapidly establish legal standing for cultural and creative products in Portugal without upfront registration expenses.
Practice note: Integrate Madrid System filings with EU trademark strategies to streamline multinational portfolio management and reduce administrative burdens.
What to monitor: Track developments in EU regulations directly applicable in Portugal to ensure ongoing compliance and proactive enforcement against potential infringements.

items.title: Hurrah! CA overrules High Court in Zaha Hadid case
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院推翻高院判决：Zaha Hadid商标案确立合理通知终止权
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Overrules High Court in Zaha Hadid Trademark Case
items.summary: IP Draughts has previously commented on the High Court decision of Zaha Hadid Ltd v the Zaha Hadid Foundation. See https://ipdraughts.wordpress.com/2024/12/30/when-can-you-terminate-on-reasonable-notice/ In that article, he was troubled by a couple of cases on trade mark licensing, including the Zaha Hadid case, which appeared to up-end his understanding of the law on a right to terminate…
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评述了Zaha Hadid Ltd诉Zaha Hadid Foundation案的上诉判决。此前高院判决引发对商标许可中“合理通知终止权”的担忧。上诉法院Sir Colin Birss法官推翻原判，认定若协议未明确为永久期限，则视为不定期限，适用Martin Baker案先例，允许以合理通知终止合同。此判决澄清了法律不确定性，被视为兼顾常识与法理的优雅解决方案。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary reviews the Court of Appeal judgment in Zaha Hadid Ltd v The Zaha Hadid Foundation, which overturns the earlier High Court decision. The initial ruling had raised concerns regarding the right to terminate trademark licenses on reasonable notice. Sir Colin Birss C, with agreement from colleagues, reversed the lower court’s finding. He determined that where an agreement does not explicitly specify a perpetual duration, it is construed as being of indefinite duration. Consequently, established case law, specifically Martin Baker v Canadian Flight Equipment, applies, permitting termination upon reasonable notice. This approach resolves prior legal ambiguities by distinguishing between perpetual and indefinite terms without relying on artificial distinctions regarding implied terms. The judgment is viewed as a pragmatic resolution that aligns legal interpretation with commercial common sense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署商标或IP许可时，务必明确约定合同期限及终止条件。若未约定固定期限，对方可能依据此判例主张随时以合理通知终止合作。建议完善合同条款，避免使用模糊表述，以降低被单方解约的法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties negotiating trademark or intellectual property licenses in Europe should explicitly define contract durations and termination conditions. If a fixed term is not specified, counterparties may rely on this precedent to argue for termination on reasonable notice. Drafting agreements with clear, unambiguous language regarding duration helps mitigate the risk of unilateral termination. Rights holders should ensure contracts either specify a fixed period or expressly exclude the default application of reasonable notice termination rights to maintain commercial stability and predictability in licensing relationships.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院推翻高院判决，确认不定期限商标许可可依合理通知终止", "区分永久与不定期限，后者适用既有判例法，无需强行解释默示条款", "判决消除了IP许可终止权的法律不确定性，回归合同解释的常规路径"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, confirming that trademark licenses of indefinite duration may be terminated on reasonable notice.", "The judgment distinguishes between perpetual and indefinite durations, applying existing precedent rather than forcing interpretations via implied terms.", "This decision eliminates legal uncertainty surrounding IP license termination rights, restoring standard contractual interpretation principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确约定许可期限，避免被认定为可随时终止的不定期合同", "细化终止条款，排除合理通知终止权的默认适用，保障商业稳定"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Explicitly define license durations to prevent agreements from being classified as terminable at will under indefinite duration precedents.", "Practice note: Draft precise termination clauses to exclude the default application of reasonable notice rights, ensuring greater commercial certainty.", "What to monitor: Monitor how courts interpret 'perpetual' versus 'indefinite' labels in future IP licensing disputes across UK jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, confirming that trademark licenses of indefinite duration may be terminated on reasonable notice.
The judgment distinguishes between perpetual and indefinite durations, applying existing precedent rather than forcing interpretations via implied terms.
This decision eliminates legal uncertainty surrounding IP license termination rights, restoring standard contractual interpretation principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Explicitly define license durations to prevent agreements from being classified as terminable at will under indefinite duration precedents.
Practice note: Draft precise termination clauses to exclude the default application of reasonable notice rights, ensuring greater commercial certainty.
What to monitor: Monitor how courts interpret 'perpetual' versus 'indefinite' labels in future IP licensing disputes across UK jurisdictions.

items.title: AI, trade secrets and corporate risk: insights from Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML and Vay
items.title_zh: AI、商业秘密与企业风险：雀巢等巨头洞察
items.title_en: AI, Trade Secrets and Corporate Risk: Insights from Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML and Vay
items.summary: Speakers from the broad range of corporates at IAM’s Trade Secret Strategy Europe candidly discussed their evolving approaches to AI use in the workplace
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于 IAM 欧洲商业秘密战略会议，汇集雀巢、万事达、ASML 及 Vay 等企业代表观点。内容聚焦跨国公司在欧盟语境下，如何平衡人工智能在工作场所的应用与商业秘密保护。各方探讨了 evolving 的内部合规策略，旨在应对生成式 AI 带来的数据泄露风险及新型知识产权挑战，为企业构建 AI 时代的保密体系提供实践参考。
items.ai_summary_en: Based on proceedings from IAM’s Trade Secret Strategy Europe conference, this commentary synthesizes perspectives from corporate representatives at Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML, and Vay. The discussion centers on how multinational enterprises navigate the intersection of artificial intelligence deployment in the workplace and robust trade secret protection within the European regulatory context. Participants examined evolving internal compliance strategies designed to address data leakage risks associated with generative AI and emerging intellectual property challenges. The analysis provides practical references for organizations seeking to construct effective confidentiality frameworks suitable for the AI era, highlighting the critical need to balance technological innovation with stringent data security measures.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕员工滥用公共 AI 模型导致核心技术外泄。建议立即审查内部数据分级制度，明确 AI 使用红线，并借鉴欧企经验建立专项培训机制，将商业秘密保护嵌入 AI 工作流，规避合规与侵权双重风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations should proactively review internal data classification systems to prevent accidental exposure of core technical information through public AI models. Establishing clear usage boundaries is essential to mitigate compliance and infringement risks. Companies are advised to integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows, drawing on established European compliance practices. This approach ensures that trade secret protection is embedded within daily operational processes rather than treated as an afterthought. By adopting such structured governance, rights holders can better safeguard proprietary assets while leveraging AI efficiencies, ensuring alignment with both corporate security goals and evolving regulatory expectations in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["跨国企业正重新评估 AI 工具使用中的商业秘密泄露风险", "行业领袖分享内部合规策略以应对生成式 AI 带来的新挑战", "欧盟语境下数据保护与技术创新的平衡成为企业关注焦点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Multinational corporations are reassessing trade secret leakage risks inherent in the widespread adoption of AI tools across global operations.", "Industry leaders shared internal compliance strategies aimed at mitigating new challenges posed by generative AI technologies in sensitive environments.", "Balancing strict EU data protection requirements with rapid technological innovation remains a primary focus for enterprise risk management."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立 AI 使用红线，防止核心数据通过公共模型意外泄露", "借鉴欧企合规经验，将保密义务嵌入人工智能工作流程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish clear AI usage boundaries to prevent core data leakage via public models.", "Practice note: Integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows based on European compliance standards.", "What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding data protection and AI-generated content ownership."]
core_points_en: Multinational corporations are reassessing trade secret leakage risks inherent in the widespread adoption of AI tools across global operations.
Industry leaders shared internal compliance strategies aimed at mitigating new challenges posed by generative AI technologies in sensitive environments.
Balancing strict EU data protection requirements with rapid technological innovation remains a primary focus for enterprise risk management.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish clear AI usage boundaries to prevent core data leakage via public models.
Practice note: Integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows based on European compliance standards.
What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding data protection and AI-generated content ownership.

items.title: Future Trends in Intellectual Property Law | Grünecker
items.title_zh: 知识产权法未来趋势：AI影响与UPC诉讼新动态
items.title_en: AI Impact and Strategic Shifts in European IP Law: Insights from Grünecker Partner
items.summary: Prof. Dr. Maximilian Kinkeldey discusses the future of IP law, the impact of ai on intellectual property practice, and strategic developments in international law firms.
items.ai_summary_zh: Grünecker合伙人Kinkeldey教授接受INTA采访，分析AI重塑IP法律工作流、咨询模式及律所战略结构的影响，强调在质量保障与创新间取得平衡。同时指出地缘政治与监管复杂性增加。文末提及代理Horl公司在统一专利法院（UPC）进行的首审侵权程序，反映实务中跨国律所应对技术变革与复杂司法环境的双重挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Grünecker partner Prof. Dr. Maximilian Kinkeldey analyzes the transformative impact of artificial intelligence on intellectual property practice in an interview with the International Trademark Association. The discussion highlights how AI is reshaping legal workflows, research processes, and strategic advisory models within international law firms. Simultaneously, geopolitical shifts and evolving regulatory landscapes are increasing the complexity of trademark protection and compliance. The analysis emphasizes that successful adaptation requires balancing technological innovation with rigorous quality assurance and client focus. Furthermore, the piece notes the rapid evolution of the European Unitary Patent system, underscoring the need for practitioners to develop new competencies. These developments demand clear strategic positioning and long-term orientation from law firm leadership to navigate the changing professional environment effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应重视AI工具在IP检索与风险预警中的应用，提升合规效率。面对UPC等新司法机制，需提前布局跨境诉讼策略，关注地缘政治带来的监管不确定性，加强与具备数字化能力的欧洲律所合作，以应对日益复杂的知识产权保护需求。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should carefully evaluate the application boundaries of AI tools in European IP portfolios to prevent automation errors that could lead to trademark invalidation or procedural failures. It is critical to adhere strictly to EU data protection standards and professional conduct rules when deploying digital solutions. Additionally, rights holders must anticipate stricter regulatory scrutiny driven by geopolitical dynamics. Establishing dynamic compliance mechanisms and engaging specialized legal counsel can help navigate these complexities, particularly regarding the evolving Unitary Patent system and cross-border enforcement strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI深刻改变IP法律工作流、研究流程及客户对专业服务的期望", "国际律所需在质量保证、客户聚焦与创新力之间寻求微妙平衡", "地缘政治与监管差异要求律所具备清晰的战略定位与长期视野", "UPC首审侵权案件显示欧洲司法实践正经历结构性调整与挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Artificial intelligence is fundamentally altering intellectual property workflows, research methods, and the nature of strategic legal advisory services.", "Geopolitical tensions and divergent regulatory frameworks significantly increase the complexity and compliance challenges associated with international trademark protection.", "Law firm leadership must evolve to balance technological integration with quality assurance, ensuring sustained client focus amidst rapid industry changes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用AI工具优化IP尽职调查与风险监控，提升合规响应速度", "适应UPC等新型司法体系，构建灵活高效的跨境维权与防御策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure AI-driven IP processes comply with EU data regulations and professional standards to mitigate risks of automated legal errors.", "Practice note: Develop proactive compliance strategies to address increased regulatory complexity and geopolitical impacts on trademark enforcement.", "What to monitor: Track developments in the Unitary Patent system and evolving expectations for legal expertise in technology-integrated practices."]
core_points_en: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally altering intellectual property workflows, research methods, and the nature of strategic legal advisory services.
Geopolitical tensions and divergent regulatory frameworks significantly increase the complexity and compliance challenges associated with international trademark protection.
Law firm leadership must evolve to balance technological integration with quality assurance, ensuring sustained client focus amidst rapid industry changes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure AI-driven IP processes comply with EU data regulations and professional standards to mitigate risks of automated legal errors.
Practice note: Develop proactive compliance strategies to address increased regulatory complexity and geopolitical impacts on trademark enforcement.
What to monitor: Track developments in the Unitary Patent system and evolving expectations for legal expertise in technology-integrated practices.

items.title: [Guest Post] Ceci n’est pas du Gruyère: Swiss origin claims and how to deal with them
items.title_zh: 非格吕耶尔奶酪：瑞士原产地主张及应对策略
items.title_en: Swiss Origin Claims in the EU: Legal Framework and Compliance Strategies for Market Participants
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host this guest contribution from Katfriend Federica Pezza, who explores the legal framework governing Swiss origin claims in Switzerland and the EU. Ceci n’est pas du Gruyère: Swiss origin claims and how to deal with them by Federica Pezza Switzerland means luxury watches, chocolate and cows, fondue and raclette, Heidi and green mountains. Above all, it
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨瑞士与欧盟管辖下原产地声明的法律框架。文章以格吕耶尔奶酪为例，分析瑞士原产地标识在欧盟市场的法律效力及冲突处理机制。重点阐述跨境贸易中如何合规使用瑞士原产地 claims，避免侵权风险，为相关企业提供法律实务指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This guest contribution by Federica Pezza examines the complex legal framework governing Swiss origin claims within both Switzerland and the European Union. Using the high-profile Gruyère cheese dispute as a primary case study, the article analyzes the validity of Swiss geographical indications in the EU market and the mechanisms for resolving conflicts between domestic Swiss laws and EU regulations. It provides a detailed overview of how rights holders can navigate these overlapping jurisdictions. The analysis focuses on ensuring compliance when using Swiss origin labels in cross-border trade, aiming to mitigate infringement risks. This resource offers practical legal guidance for companies operating at the intersection of Swiss and EU intellectual property regimes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕瑞士严格的原产地保护壁垒，尤其在食品与奢侈品领域。建议提前审查产品标识合规性，避免因不当使用“瑞士制造”或特定地名引发诉讼，必要时寻求专业法律意见以规避市场准入风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting goods to Europe, particularly in food and luxury sectors, must remain vigilant regarding strict Swiss origin protection barriers. Improper use of 'Swiss Made' labels or specific geographical names can trigger significant litigation and market access issues. Companies should implement rigorous pre-market compliance reviews for product labeling to ensure alignment with both EU and Swiss regulatory requirements. Establishing robust internal checks helps prevent infringement disputes arising from geographical indication protections. Seeking specialized legal counsel is advisable to navigate these complex cross-border IP landscapes effectively and safeguard commercial interests against potential enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["解析瑞士原产地声明在欧盟法律体系下的认定标准与效力", "探讨地理标志保护中瑞士国内法与欧盟规则的协调与冲突", "提供企业在面对原产地主张时的合规应对与争议解决策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes recognition standards and legal validity of Swiss origin declarations under the broader EU legal system.", "Explores coordination challenges and conflicts between Swiss domestic protection rules and EU geographical indication frameworks.", "Provides strategic compliance measures and dispute resolution approaches for entities facing origin-related claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严控产品原产地标识，防范因瑞士地理标志保护引发的侵权纠纷", "建立合规审查机制，确保对欧出口产品符合欧盟及瑞士双重规则"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to Swiss origin labeling is critical to avoid infringement disputes driven by strong geographical indication protections.", "Practice note: Establish comprehensive compliance review mechanisms to ensure export products meet dual regulatory standards in the EU and Switzerland.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations of origin claims and potential conflicts between Swiss national laws and EU IP regulations."]
core_points_en: Analyzes recognition standards and legal validity of Swiss origin declarations under the broader EU legal system.
Explores coordination challenges and conflicts between Swiss domestic protection rules and EU geographical indication frameworks.
Provides strategic compliance measures and dispute resolution approaches for entities facing origin-related claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to Swiss origin labeling is critical to avoid infringement disputes driven by strong geographical indication protections.
Practice note: Establish comprehensive compliance review mechanisms to ensure export products meet dual regulatory standards in the EU and Switzerland.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations of origin claims and potential conflicts between Swiss national laws and EU IP regulations.

items.title: Alright, Alright, Alright: Could McConaughey's Sound Trademark Silence AI?
items.title_zh: 声音商标能否遏制 AI：麦康纳案引发的欧盟法律思考
items.title_en: Sound Trademarks as AI Defense: Legal Gaps in EU Portrait and Copyright Regimes
items.summary: Over the past few months, actor Matthew McConaughey has obtained a series of trademark registrations from the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文以美国演员注册声音商标为例，探讨其对抗 AI 深度伪造的潜力。重点分析指出，欧盟现行肖像权仅保护外貌、著作权难覆盖短促声音，导致法律防护不足。丹麦与荷兰正探索立法赋予个人对自身声音的权利，凸显欧洲在应对 AI 侵权上的制度缺口与改革趋势。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the strategic potential of sound trademarks to combat AI-driven voice deepfakes, using a prominent US actor’s recent registrations as a primary case study. It highlights significant limitations within the current European Union legal framework, where portrait rights strictly protect visual appearance rather than auditory characteristics, and copyright law often fails to cover short, non-original vocal phrases. Consequently, existing statutes offer insufficient protection against unauthorized AI voice synthesis. The article further notes that Denmark and the Netherlands are actively exploring legislative reforms to establish specific personal rights over one’s own voice. These developments underscore a growing institutional gap in Europe regarding AI-related personality rights infringement and signal an emerging trend toward statutory reform to address these technological challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用名人声音或开发语音 AI 时，需警惕新型声音商标壁垒。建议提前开展声音元素合规排查，关注荷丹等国立法动态，避免因人格特征商业化引发侵权纠纷，并考虑布局自有声音品牌。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing voice AI technologies or utilizing celebrity voices in commercial campaigns must navigate evolving IP landscapes. The expansion of sound trademark protection in Western jurisdictions creates new compliance barriers for entities relying on voice synthesis. Companies should conduct thorough audits of audio elements used in marketing to mitigate infringement risks associated with personality rights. Furthermore, monitoring legislative developments in Nordic countries is critical, as proposed laws may soon grant individuals explicit control over their vocal identity. Proactive strategies should include securing independent voice branding and ensuring robust contractual safeguards when engaging with voice data to avoid liability for unauthorized commercial exploitation of human characteristics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["美国声音商标注册为对抗 AI 语音仿制提供了新的商标法路径参考", "欧盟现行肖像权与著作权体系难以有效规制非复制型的 AI 声音合成", "丹麦和荷兰正推动立法，拟将人身特征纳入知识产权保护范畴"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["US sound trademark registrations provide a novel legal reference for combating unauthorized AI voice imitation and deepfake technologies.", "Current EU portrait rights and copyright frameworks struggle to effectively regulate non-replicative AI voice synthesis activities.", "Denmark and the Netherlands are advancing legislation to incorporate personal voice characteristics into broader intellectual property protections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧美声音商标扩张，规避 AI 语音合成侵权风险", "关注北欧立法动向，提前规划人格特征商业化合规策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the expansion of sound trademarks in Europe and the US to mitigate AI voice synthesis infringement risks.", "Practice note: Conduct early compliance checks on voice elements and consider securing proprietary sound brands to avoid personality right disputes.", "What to monitor: Legislative trends in Denmark and the Netherlands regarding personal rights over voice and other human characteristics."]
core_points_en: US sound trademark registrations provide a novel legal reference for combating unauthorized AI voice imitation and deepfake technologies.
Current EU portrait rights and copyright frameworks struggle to effectively regulate non-replicative AI voice synthesis activities.
Denmark and the Netherlands are advancing legislation to incorporate personal voice characteristics into broader intellectual property protections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the expansion of sound trademarks in Europe and the US to mitigate AI voice synthesis infringement risks.
Practice note: Conduct early compliance checks on voice elements and consider securing proprietary sound brands to avoid personality right disputes.
What to monitor: Legislative trends in Denmark and the Netherlands regarding personal rights over voice and other human characteristics.

items.title: Using A Designer's Name After Departure May Create A Deceptive Trademark
items.title_zh: 设计师离职后继续使用其姓名商标或构成欺骗
items.title_en: CJEU: Post-Departure Use of Designer Name Trademark May Be Deceptive
items.summary: Does European law preclude the cancellation of a trademark bearing a creator's name when used by the assignee in such a way as to effectively create the impression that the creator is still involved...
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国最高法院提请 CJEU 澄清：受让方在设计师离职后继续使用其姓名商标，若造成设计师仍参与设计的假象，是否导致商标具有欺骗性。CJEU 于 2025 年 12 月裁定，欧盟法律不排除此类情形下商标因使用方式而变为欺骗性并被撤销的可能，修正了此前判例的模糊地带。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has clarified that EU law does not preclude the cancellation of a trademark bearing a creator's name when used by an assignee in a manner that creates the false impression the designer remains involved. This ruling addresses a preliminary question from the French Court of Cassation regarding a dispute between a fashion designer and a company that acquired his business. The CJEU confirmed that while a designer's departure alone does not automatically render a trademark deceptive or invalid, the specific manner of use is critical. If the assignee’s marketing suggests ongoing creative participation by the departed designer, the mark may be deemed deceptive and subject to cancellation. This decision refines previous case law, emphasizing that consumer perception regarding the commercial origin and personal involvement of the creator determines the validity of such trademarks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在收购欧洲品牌或使用创始人姓名商标时，须审慎评估设计师离职后的使用边界，避免因营销暗示引发商标无效风险。建议重新审视授权协议中的署名条款与实际使用场景。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders acquiring businesses with founder or designer names must carefully assess the boundaries of using those names after the individual's departure. Marketing strategies should avoid implying ongoing creative involvement if the relationship has ended, as this may lead to findings of deception and subsequent trademark invalidity. It is advisable to review assignment agreements for specific clauses governing post-termination use and disclosure obligations. Clear communication regarding the current status of design leadership helps mitigate legal risks. Companies should ensure that promotional materials accurately reflect the actual contributors to product development to maintain trademark validity and avoid consumer confusion regarding the brand's origins.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU 确认设计师离职本身不自动导致姓名商标无效", "若使用方式误导公众认为设计师仍参与，可认定具欺骗性", "本案源于法国纠纷，被告曾因侵犯设计师版权被定罪"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A designer's departure from a company does not automatically invalidate a trademark bearing their name under EU law.", "Trademarks may be cancelled if post-departure use misleads the public into believing the designer still participates in product creation.", "The case originated from a French dispute where the defendant was previously convicted of copyright infringement against the designer."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["收购含人名商标需约定离职后使用限制与披露义务", "宣传材料应避免暗示已离职设计师仍参与产品研发"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Acquirers of brands with personal names must define strict limits on post-departure usage to prevent deceptive impressions.", "Practice note: Draft assignment agreements with explicit clauses restricting marketing implications of continued designer involvement.", "What to monitor: Monitor promotional campaigns for any subtle suggestions that a departed creator remains active in product design."]
core_points_en: A designer's departure from a company does not automatically invalidate a trademark bearing their name under EU law.
Trademarks may be cancelled if post-departure use misleads the public into believing the designer still participates in product creation.
The case originated from a French dispute where the defendant was previously convicted of copyright infringement against the designer.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Acquirers of brands with personal names must define strict limits on post-departure usage to prevent deceptive impressions.
Practice note: Draft assignment agreements with explicit clauses restricting marketing implications of continued designer involvement.
What to monitor: Monitor promotional campaigns for any subtle suggestions that a departed creator remains active in product design.

items.title: [Guest post] Retromark Volume XVII: the last six months in trade marks
items.title_zh: 回顾商标第十七卷：过去半年的重要商标案件综述
items.title_en: Retromark Volume XVII: Key Trademark Developments in EU and UK Courts
items.summary: Darren Meale of Simmons & Simmons presents the seventeenth volume of his rundown of notable trade mark cases over the past six months.Retromark Volume XVII: the last six months in trade marksby Darren MealeIt’s been nearly nine years since a much younger me (pictured in Volume 1) started Retromark. Fast forward from 2017 to 2026 and the cases keep coming thick and fast, with the Court of
items.ai_summary_zh: Simmons & Simmons 律师 Darren Meale 发布第十七期商标案例回顾，梳理 2026 年前六个月欧盟及英国法院的重要商标判决。文章延续九年传统，密集分析近期频发的诉讼动态，涵盖确权、侵权及程序性争议，为从业者提供关键判例趋势解读与实务指引。
items.ai_summary_en: Darren Meale of Simmons & Simmons presents the seventeenth edition of Retromark, a comprehensive review of significant trademark cases from the first half of 2026. Continuing a nine-year tradition, this analysis examines the surge in litigation across European Union and United Kingdom jurisdictions. The article dissects recent judicial decisions covering substantive issues such as registration validity, infringement determinations, and procedural disputes. By synthesizing these developments, the piece offers practitioners critical insights into evolving case law trends. It serves as an essential resource for understanding how courts are interpreting current legal standards amidst increasing caseloads, providing necessary context for strategic decision-making in complex trademark matters.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应密切关注此类判例综述，把握欧盟商标审查与诉讼最新风向。建议企业提前排查品牌合规风险，优化布局策略，并借鉴判例经验应对潜在侵权纠纷或异议程序，提升维权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Monitoring these consolidated case reviews allows rights holders to anticipate shifts in EU trademark examination standards and identify potential litigation risks early. By studying established strategies from mature case law, companies can enhance their defensive capabilities and enforcement efficiency within the European market. Proactive assessment of brand compliance risks, informed by recent precedents, enables optimized portfolio management. This approach supports effective responses to potential infringement disputes or opposition proceedings, ensuring that legal strategies remain aligned with current judicial interpretations and procedural expectations.
