items.title: Trade Mark Law & Practice at a Glance – February 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 2026 年 2 月商标法与实务概览：AI 使用监管更新
items.title_en: UK IP Regulators Issue Interim Guidance on AI Use in Trademark Practice
items.summary: Read our February 2026 trade mark law and practice update, including updates from the UK, EU China, Canada and US
items.ai_summary_zh: GJE 发布 2026 年 2 月商标法实务更新，重点阐述英国专利商标代理人监管局（IPReg）联合相关专业机构发布的 AI 使用指导。文件确认行业内使用 AI 不被禁止，但强调必须严格遵守核心监管框架第一章原则及行为准则，标志着在受监管前提下对 AI 应用的接受度提升。
items.ai_summary_en: GJE’s February 2026 trademark law and practice update highlights significant regulatory developments regarding artificial intelligence in the legal profession. The UK’s regulatory body for patent and trademark attorneys, IPReg, has collaborated with professional bodies CIPA and CITMA to publish interim guidance on AI usage. This joint statement confirms that employing AI tools within the profession is not prohibited. However, it strictly emphasizes that all AI applications must adhere to the overarching principles outlined in Chapter 1 of the Core Regulatory Framework and the Rules of Conduct. This development signals a growing acceptance of AI technologies in attorney workflows, provided their use remains regulated and compliant with established ethical and professional standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧委托代理或自建 IP 团队时，需关注英国对 AI 辅助作业的合规边界。建议审查代理机构的 AI 使用政策，确保其符合监管要求，避免因工具滥用导致程序瑕疵或责任风险，同时可利用合规 AI 提升确权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies managing trademark portfolios in the UK, understanding the compliance boundaries of AI-assisted legal work is increasingly critical. Rights holders should verify that their appointed UK attorneys maintain robust policies governing AI usage to prevent procedural defects or liability risks arising from tool misuse. Simultaneously, leveraging compliant AI solutions can enhance the efficiency of trademark registration and maintenance processes. Monitoring these regulatory expectations ensures that technological adoption supports rather than compromises the integrity of intellectual property proceedings and client confidentiality obligations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPReg 联合 CIPA 与 CITMA 发布 AI 使用临时指导意见", "确认代理人行业使用 AI 工具并非被禁止的行为", "强调 AI 应用须严格遵循核心监管原则与行为准则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["IPReg, CIPA, and CITMA have jointly issued interim guidance regulating the use of AI tools by trademark attorneys.", "The use of AI in the legal profession is explicitly permitted, provided it complies with existing regulatory frameworks.", "Attorneys must strictly follow the Core Regulatory Framework principles and Rules of Conduct when utilizing AI."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查欧洲代理机构 AI 使用合规性以规避程序风险", "利用受监管 AI 工具提升商标确权与维护效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify that UK legal representatives have compliant AI policies to mitigate procedural and liability risks.", "Practice note: Leverage regulated AI tools to improve the speed and accuracy of trademark prosecution and maintenance tasks.", "What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of the Core Regulatory Framework concerning AI integration in professional legal services."]
core_points_en: IPReg, CIPA, and CITMA have jointly issued interim guidance regulating the use of AI tools by trademark attorneys.
The use of AI in the legal profession is explicitly permitted, provided it complies with existing regulatory frameworks.
Attorneys must strictly follow the Core Regulatory Framework principles and Rules of Conduct when utilizing AI.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify that UK legal representatives have compliant AI policies to mitigate procedural and liability risks.
Practice note: Leverage regulated AI tools to improve the speed and accuracy of trademark prosecution and maintenance tasks.
What to monitor: Evolving interpretations of the Core Regulatory Framework concerning AI integration in professional legal services.

items.title: Battery Report 2025: Charging Ahead - Patent Trends Powering Battery Innovation
items.title_zh: 2025 电池报告：驱动创新的专利趋势
items.title_en: Battery Report 2025: Patent Trends Driving Innovation in the Battery Sector
items.summary: The Volta Foundation have recently released the 2025 edition of their annual Battery Report . These open-access reports are well-renowned in the battery world and provide a comprehensive analysis of developments in battery research, industry and regulations. We at Mewburn Ellis were delighted to participate in this year’s report, as Senior Associate Chloe Flower co-authored and contributed to the policy section. This is the fourth article in our 2025 Report series. Following on from our article
items.ai_summary_zh: Volta 基金会发布 2025 年度电池报告，Mewburn Ellis 律师参与政策部分撰写。本文作为系列第四篇，聚焦电池技术领域专利申请趋势，分析主要活跃辖区、领军企业及关键技术方向，并承接前文关于该领域知识产权诉讼的讨论，提供全面的市场与法律动态洞察。
items.ai_summary_en: The Volta Foundation has released the 2025 edition of its annual Battery Report, a widely recognized open-access resource offering comprehensive analysis of battery research, industry developments, and regulatory frameworks. Mewburn Ellis contributed to this year’s publication, with Senior Associate Chloe Flower co-authoring the policy section. As the fourth installment in the firm's 2025 Report series, this article builds upon previous discussions regarding intellectual property litigation within the battery sector. It specifically examines current trends in patent applications filed across this technical field. The analysis identifies which territories are experiencing the highest levels of patent activity, highlights the companies driving these innovations, and pinpoints the specific technologies generating significant momentum. This provides stakeholders with a detailed overview of the evolving landscape in battery technology patents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国电池企业需关注欧洲专利布局热点与政策动向，提前规避侵权风险。建议利用此类行业报告优化海外申请策略，加强核心技术在欧洲的专利保护，并密切关注相关诉讼案例以应对潜在纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should leverage industry reports like the Battery Report to identify European patent layout hotspots and optimize filing strategies accordingly. Understanding regional policy shifts and litigation dynamics is essential for proactive risk management. Companies should strengthen patent protection for core technologies in Europe while closely monitoring relevant case law. This approach helps mitigate potential infringement risks and ensures robust intellectual property positioning in a competitive global market.

items.title: When Performance Sport Meets Music Copyright: A Licensing Problem Hiding in Plain Sight
items.title_zh: 竞技体育遇上音乐版权：潜藏的许可困境
items.title_en: Performance Sports and Music Copyright: The Hidden Licensing Challenge in International Competitions
items.summary: Olympic figure skater Tomàs-Llorenç Guarino Sabaté recently had his planned routine - set to music from Universal movie ‘Despicable Me’ - thrown into turmoil, putting an international spotlight on an issue that performance sports have been grappling with for years: how athletes lawfully use musical copyright works when competing on the world stage.
items.ai_summary_zh: 奥运花滑选手因计划使用《神偷奶爸》电影音乐参赛而陷入困境，引发国际关注。该事件凸显了表演类体育项目长期面临的法律难题：运动员如何在世界舞台竞赛中合法使用受版权保护的音乐作品。文章指出这并非孤立个案，而是行业普遍存在的许可盲区。
items.ai_summary_en: The recent disruption faced by Olympic figure skater Tomàs-Llorenç Guarino Sabaté, whose routine set to music from the Universal film Despicable Me was thrown into turmoil, has placed international focus on a persistent legal issue. This incident highlights the long-standing challenge performance sports face regarding the lawful use of copyrighted musical works during global competitions. It is not an isolated case but rather reflects a widespread licensing blind spot within the industry. Athletes and organizers frequently struggle with unclear mechanisms for securing proper rights, creating significant legal risks when competing on the world stage. This analysis underscores the urgent need for clearer frameworks to ensure compliance with copyright laws in high-profile sporting events.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国文体企业及运动员出海参赛时，需高度重视背景音乐版权合规。建议在赛前建立严格的音乐权利清查与许可获取机制，避免因侵权纠纷影响国际赛事表现及品牌声誉。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies and athletes participating in international events, background music copyright compliance is critical. Rights holders must prioritize strict pre-event clearance procedures to avoid infringement disputes that could impact competitive performance and brand reputation. Establishing a specialized intellectual property compliance review mechanism for cultural and sports activities is essential. Proactive management of music rights ensures smooth participation in global markets and mitigates the risk of costly legal challenges or public relations issues arising from unauthorized use of protected works.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["花滑选手因未获音乐授权导致参赛计划受阻", "表演类体育项目长期面临音乐版权许可难题", "国际赛事中运动员合法使用音乐机制尚不完善"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A figure skater's competition plans were disrupted due to failure to secure necessary music licensing rights beforehand.", "Performance sports sectors have long struggled with complex and often unclear music copyright licensing requirements.", "Current mechanisms for athletes to legally use music in international tournaments remain incomplete and problematic."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出海参赛务必提前落实音乐作品版权许可", "建立文体活动知识产权合规审查专项机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Athletes and event organizers must secure music copyright licenses well before international competitions to prevent disruptions.", "Practice note: Implement a dedicated intellectual property compliance review process for all cultural and sports-related activities.", "What to monitor: Evolving licensing frameworks and enforcement trends regarding music usage in global sporting events."]
core_points_en: A figure skater's competition plans were disrupted due to failure to secure necessary music licensing rights beforehand.
Performance sports sectors have long struggled with complex and often unclear music copyright licensing requirements.
Current mechanisms for athletes to legally use music in international tournaments remain incomplete and problematic.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Athletes and event organizers must secure music copyright licenses well before international competitions to prevent disruptions.
Practice note: Implement a dedicated intellectual property compliance review process for all cultural and sports-related activities.
What to monitor: Evolving licensing frameworks and enforcement trends regarding music usage in global sporting events.

items.title: Design protection for object-inspired products
items.title_zh: 物体灵感产品的外观设计保护界限解析
items.title_en: Design Protection Boundaries for Object-Inspired Products: Lessons from Dutch Court Ruling
items.summary: When is an object-inspired product legally protected? A clear explanation of design rights and the limits of design protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文基于荷兰法院关于灭火器造型水瓶的判决，分析物体灵感产品的法律保护边界。法院指出，仅模仿常见物体形态且具备市场通用特征（如圆柱形、红色）的设计，无法产生独特整体印象，故不受外观设计权保护。唯有具体的图形与文字元素可能获保，但若细节不同亦不构成侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the legal boundaries of design protection for products inspired by familiar objects, based on a recent Dutch court decision involving a fire extinguisher-shaped water bottle. The court rejected claims relying on design rights, copyright, and slavish imitation, emphasizing that mere resemblance to an existing object is insufficient for protection. The ruling highlights that common market features, such as cylindrical shapes and standard colors like bright red, do not create a distinct overall impression necessary for design rights. Consequently, only specific graphic and textual elements could potentially qualify for protection, but differences in wording and layout meant no infringement occurred. This case illustrates that abstract ideas alone rarely secure design protection; instead, concrete design choices regarding details, proportions, and element combinations are critical for establishing distinctiveness and enforceable rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧开发创意产品时，切勿误以为“形似物体”即获保护。应聚焦于独特的比例、细节处理及元素组合，并在早期进行自由实施分析，避免因缺乏显著性而无法维权或 inadvertent 侵权。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing object-inspired products should recognize that visual similarity to familiar items does not automatically grant intellectual property rights. Legal protection depends on concrete design execution, including unique proportions, finishing, and element combinations that create a distinct overall impression. Rights holders must conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments to identify potential overlaps with existing designs. This proactive approach helps mitigate risks of inadvertent infringement and ensures that new products possess sufficient distinctiveness to support future enforcement actions against competitors offering similar concepts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院强调整体印象评估，通用特征与常见色彩难获外观设计保护", "单纯创意概念不足以为据，保护源于具体设计细节与组合方式", "图形文字元素虽原则上可保，但需实质相似方构成侵权风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts assess the overall impression, finding that generic shapes and common colors fail to establish distinctive design protection.", "Abstract concepts or inspiration from existing objects are insufficient; protection requires specific, unique design details.", "Graphic and textual elements may be protectable, but infringement requires substantial similarity in these specific components."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免依赖通用形态，需通过独特细节构建显著整体印象以获保护", "上市前务必开展设计自由度评估，预防因概念雷同导致的法律纠纷"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on generic forms; focus on unique details to build a protectable overall impression.", "Practice note: Conduct design freedom-to-operate assessments before launch to prevent disputes arising from conceptual similarities.", "What to monitor: Monitor competitor products for subtle differences in graphic and textual elements that may affect infringement claims."]
core_points_en: Courts assess the overall impression, finding that generic shapes and common colors fail to establish distinctive design protection.
Abstract concepts or inspiration from existing objects are insufficient; protection requires specific, unique design details.
Graphic and textual elements may be protectable, but infringement requires substantial similarity in these specific components.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on generic forms; focus on unique details to build a protectable overall impression.
Practice note: Conduct design freedom-to-operate assessments before launch to prevent disputes arising from conceptual similarities.
What to monitor: Monitor competitor products for subtle differences in graphic and textual elements that may affect infringement claims.

items.title: Colour trademarks: A rare but sought-after form of protection in the EU
items.title_zh: 欧盟颜色商标：罕见但备受追捧的保护形式
items.title_en: EU Colour Trademarks: High Bar for Registration and Distinctiveness Requirements
items.summary: Discover how to protect colour trademarks in the EU, including how to satisfy distinctive requirements.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章以 EUIPO 驳回德国 Weischer 公司颜色商标申请为例，阐述欧盟颜色商标注册的高门槛。指出单一颜色因缺乏显著性且易造成垄断，难以获准注册；而红牛等成功案例依赖于长期使用建立的声誉。强调颜色商标虽具营销价值，但法律保护范围有限，需证明其能独立指示商品来源。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the stringent requirements for registering colour trademarks in the European Union, using the EUIPO’s rejection of Weischer GmbH’s application as a primary case study. The Office determined that the single colour lacked distinctiveness because it was customary in the trade and not easily remembered by consumers as an origin indicator. The article contrasts this with Red Bull’s successful enforcement, which relied on extensive use establishing acquired distinctiveness. It highlights that while colour is vital for marketing, legal protection is narrow. Applicants must prove the colour independently identifies commercial origin, often requiring substantial evidence of long-term use or combining colours with other elements to overcome inherent non-distinctiveness.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请颜色商标需谨慎，避免依赖单一普通色。应注重积累使用证据以证明“后天显著性”，或采用“颜色 + 图形/文字”组合策略。同时需评估垄断颜色对竞争对手的潜在限制，规避被驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders seeking to protect brand identity through colour, relying solely on a single, common shade carries a high risk of refusal due to lack of distinctiveness and potential monopolization concerns. To enhance registrability, applicants should prioritize accumulating robust evidence of long-term use to demonstrate acquired distinctiveness among the relevant public. Alternatively, adopting a combined strategy that integrates specific colours with figurative or verbal elements can lower legal risks and broaden protection scope. This approach balances marketing objectives with the strict legal standards required to secure and enforce colour-based intellectual property rights effectively within the EU market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 驳回申请因颜色普通且公众记忆不深，缺乏显著性", "欧盟判例确立颜色商标审查标准与其他商标一致但更难证明", "仅当颜色经使用获得显著性或组合使用时才可能成功注册"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO rejected Weischer’s colour mark because the shade was customary in the metal products sector and lacked inherent distinctiveness.", "EU case law applies standard distinctiveness criteria to colour marks, yet proving origin function remains significantly harder than for verbal signs.", "Successful registration typically requires proof of acquired distinctiveness through extensive use or strategic combination with other trademark elements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单一颜色注册难，需积累长期使用证据证明显著性", "建议采用组合商标策略，降低被驳回的法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Single colour marks face high refusal rates; strong evidence of acquired distinctiveness is critical for success.", "Practice note: Consider combining colours with verbal or figurative elements to mitigate distinctiveness challenges and broaden protection.", "What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on non-traditional marks and CJEU rulings regarding acquired distinctiveness thresholds for colour combinations."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO rejected Weischer’s colour mark because the shade was customary in the metal products sector and lacked inherent distinctiveness.
EU case law applies standard distinctiveness criteria to colour marks, yet proving origin function remains significantly harder than for verbal signs.
Successful registration typically requires proof of acquired distinctiveness through extensive use or strategic combination with other trademark elements.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Single colour marks face high refusal rates; strong evidence of acquired distinctiveness is critical for success.
Practice note: Consider combining colours with verbal or figurative elements to mitigate distinctiveness challenges and broaden protection.
What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on non-traditional marks and CJEU rulings regarding acquired distinctiveness thresholds for colour combinations.

items.title: Victory for client Rematec before the UPC Court of Appeal | Grünecker
items.title_zh: Rematec在UPC上诉法院胜诉：专利有效性维持并认定侵权
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Reverses Invalidity Ruling, Finds Infringement for Rematec Patent
items.summary: Grünecker turned the tide and succeeded before the UPC Court of Appeal in proceedings for our client Rematec GmbH & Co KG,
items.ai_summary_zh: Grünecker代理客户Rematec在统一专利法院（UPC）上诉程序中逆转局势。上诉法院撤销了一审法院全面撤销欧洲专利EP2548648B1的决定，驳回反诉并认定专利侵权成立。法院在解释权利要求以评估可专利性时，显著引用了EPO扩大上诉委员会G1/24号决定，不仅考虑专利说明书，还考量了说明书中引用的现有技术文件内容。
items.ai_summary_en: Grünecker successfully represented client Rematec GmbH & Co KG before the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal. The appellate court set aside the Court of First Instance’s decision, which had previously revoked European patent EP2548648B1 in its entirety. Instead, the Court of Appeal rejected the counterclaim and ruled that the patent was infringed. Notably, in interpreting claims to assess patentability, the Court referenced the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal decision G1/24. This approach involved considering not only the patent description but also the content of prior art documents cited within that description. This judgment highlights evolving judicial practices regarding claim interpretation and the application of prior art within the UPC system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需关注UPC对权利要求解释的扩张趋势，特别是结合说明书引用文献的做法。建议加强专利撰写质量，确保说明书逻辑严密；在应对UPC诉讼时，应深入研判EPO最新判例对实体法解释的影响，提前布局无效抗辩与侵权规避策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment signals a stricter judicial approach by the UPC regarding the use of background art citations in patent specifications. Rights holders should be aware that citing specific prior art in the description may influence the assessment of inventive step and claim scope under recent precedents like G1/24. Applicants are advised to carefully draft European patent applications, particularly the background section, to avoid unintended admissions that could weaken patentability arguments. Monitoring future UPC case law on this issue is essential for adjusting European patent portfolios and litigation strategies effectively.

items.title: Trademark revocation: How to enforce INPI and EUIPO cost decisions
items.title_zh: 商标撤销：如何执行 INPI 与 EUIPO 的费用裁决
items.title_en: Enforcing EUIPO and INPI Cost Decisions in Trademark Revocation and Invalidity Proceedings
items.summary: Discover how to enforce cost provisions in EUIPO and INPI trademark revocation and invalidity decisions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析在 EUIPO 及法国 INPI 商标撤销与无效程序中，胜诉方如何强制执行败诉方承担费用的裁决。文章指出两机构程序差异：EUIPO 涵盖异议等全流程，而 INPI 仅针对撤销与无效。强调执行需满足裁决金额明确、已生效且无上诉等条件，并警示执行成本可能高于获赔金额的风险。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the enforcement mechanisms for cost provisions within trademark revocation and invalidity decisions issued by the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI). While both offices may order the losing party to reimburse the winning party’s costs, their procedural scopes differ significantly. The EUIPO issues cost decisions across opposition, invalidity, and revocation proceedings. In contrast, the INPI rules on costs only in revocation and invalidity actions upon request, excluding opposition cases. Successful enforcement requires a final decision with a specified cost amount and certification that no appeal has been filed. Rights holders must carefully evaluate whether the potential recovery justifies the additional legal expenses involved in enforcement, as these costs can sometimes exceed the awarded sum.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权获胜后，切勿忽视费用执行环节。应提前评估执行成本与收益，确保证据链完整（如无上诉证明）。针对法国与欧盟不同程序，需制定差异化追偿策略，避免空有胜诉判决却无法实际回款。
items.ai_insight_en: Securing a favorable decision does not guarantee automatic reimbursement of legal costs. Rights holders should proactively assess the financial viability of enforcement before initiating collection efforts. It is critical to obtain necessary documentation, such as certificates of no appeal, immediately after decisions become final. Given the varying procedures between the EUIPO and INPI, tailored strategies are required for each jurisdiction. Parties should conduct a cost-benefit analysis to ensure that the expense of enforcing the decision does not outweigh the recoverable amount, preventing situations where successful litigation results in net financial loss due to high enforcement expenditures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 与 INPI 在费用裁决适用范围上存在显著程序差异", "强制执行前提包括裁决金额确定及取得无上诉证明文件", "需权衡执行成本，避免因追偿费用过高导致得不偿失"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Procedural differences exist between EUIPO, which covers all inter partes proceedings, and INPI, which limits cost rulings to revocation and invalidity actions.", "Enforcement prerequisites include a final decision specifying the exact cost amount and obtaining a certificate confirming no appeal was filed.", "Rights holders must weigh enforcement costs against potential recovery, as legal fees for execution may exceed the awarded compensation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["胜诉后须及时获取无上诉证明，作为启动强制执行的必要文件", "需精细核算执行成本，防止出现追回金额低于执行支出的情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the cost-effectiveness of enforcement actions to ensure recovered amounts justify the additional legal investment required for collection.", "Practice note: Secure certificates of no appeal promptly after final decisions to satisfy mandatory prerequisites for initiating enforcement procedures.", "What to monitor: Distinguish between EUIPO and INPI procedural rules regarding cost awards, particularly noting INPI's exclusion of opposition proceedings from cost rulings."]
core_points_en: Procedural differences exist between EUIPO, which covers all inter partes proceedings, and INPI, which limits cost rulings to revocation and invalidity actions.
Enforcement prerequisites include a final decision specifying the exact cost amount and obtaining a certificate confirming no appeal was filed.
Rights holders must weigh enforcement costs against potential recovery, as legal fees for execution may exceed the awarded compensation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the cost-effectiveness of enforcement actions to ensure recovered amounts justify the additional legal investment required for collection.
Practice note: Secure certificates of no appeal promptly after final decisions to satisfy mandatory prerequisites for initiating enforcement procedures.
What to monitor: Distinguish between EUIPO and INPI procedural rules regarding cost awards, particularly noting INPI's exclusion of opposition proceedings from cost rulings.

items.title: New dawn or damp squib? Mediation and arbitration at the UPC
items.title_zh: 新曙光还是雷声大雨点小？统一专利法院下的调解与仲裁
items.title_en: UPC’s New Mediation Centre: A Strategic Alternative for High-Value Patent Disputes
items.summary: Will the opening of the UPC's Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre lead to more alternative dispute resolution in European patent cases?
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）旗下的专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）将于今年在卢布尔雅那正式开幕。尽管 UPC 已受理近千家案件，但 PMAC 直至 2026 年 3 月才预计发布最终规则。鉴于目前 WIPO 等机构专利类替代性争议解决（ADR）案件占比极低，PMAC 旨在为高价值、高技术复杂度的专利纠纷提供全新且灵活的解决论坛，有望显著提升欧洲专利 ADR 的使用率。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is set to formally open in Ljubljana this year, offering a dedicated forum for alternative dispute resolution. Although the UPC has handled nearly 1,000 cases since 2023, the PMAC’s operational framework is still developing, with final arbitration and mediation rules expected in March 2026 following public consultations. Currently, patent-related ADR cases represent only a small fraction of disputes at major institutions like WIPO. The PMAC aims to address this gap by providing a flexible, specialized venue for complex, high-value patent conflicts. Its establishment in Ljubljana and Lisbon seeks to enhance the appeal of non-litigious solutions, potentially shifting market preferences toward more efficient and confidential resolution mechanisms for European patent holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼时，应关注 PMAC 提供的非诉选项。相比传统诉讼，调解仲裁更具保密性与成本优势，适合处理复杂技术纠纷。建议提前研究即将出台的规则，评估将其纳入全球争议解决策略的可行性，以降低维权或应诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the PMAC as a viable component of global dispute resolution strategies, particularly for sensitive or technically intricate patent matters. Compared to traditional litigation, mediation and arbitration offer enhanced confidentiality and potential cost efficiencies, which are critical for protecting competitive advantages. As the regulatory framework matures in 2026, rights holders and legal teams ought to assess how these mechanisms can mitigate enforcement risks and streamline resolutions. Integrating ADR options early may provide strategic leverage in negotiations and reduce the burden of prolonged court proceedings across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC 设卢布尔雅那和里斯本两处席位，程序地点具有高度灵活性", "仲裁与调解规则草案已完成咨询，最终版预计于 2026 年 3 月公布", "现有国际机构专利类 ADR 占比仅 3%，PMAC 拟填补高价值专利纠纷空白"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The PMAC will operate from seats in Ljubljana and Lisbon, offering significant procedural flexibility regarding the location of hearings and proceedings.", "Draft arbitration and mediation rules have undergone public consultation, with final versions anticipated for publication in March 2026.", "Existing international ADR bodies handle very few patent cases; the PMAC aims to fill this void for high-value, technically complex disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 2026 年规则落地，评估将 PMAC 纳入企业全球争议解决策略", "利用调解仲裁保密高效特点，应对高价值专利技术纠纷以降低风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate PMAC's potential to resolve complex patent disputes efficiently while maintaining strict confidentiality compared to public litigation.", "Practice note: Prepare internal protocols to incorporate mediation and arbitration clauses into licensing and settlement agreements ahead of rule finalization.", "What to monitor: Track the release of final PMAC rules in March 2026 and the appointment of mediators to assess procedural readiness and expertise."]
core_points_en: The PMAC will operate from seats in Ljubljana and Lisbon, offering significant procedural flexibility regarding the location of hearings and proceedings.
Draft arbitration and mediation rules have undergone public consultation, with final versions anticipated for publication in March 2026.
Existing international ADR bodies handle very few patent cases; the PMAC aims to fill this void for high-value, technically complex disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate PMAC's potential to resolve complex patent disputes efficiently while maintaining strict confidentiality compared to public litigation.
Practice note: Prepare internal protocols to incorporate mediation and arbitration clauses into licensing and settlement agreements ahead of rule finalization.
What to monitor: Track the release of final PMAC rules in March 2026 and the appointment of mediators to assess procedural readiness and expertise.

items.title: Trademark protection and marketing trends: The case of 'Quite Luxury’
items.title_zh: 商标保护与营销趋势：'Quite Luxury'案解析
items.title_en: Trademark Protection and Marketing Trends: The EUIPO 'Quite Luxury' Decision
items.summary: Commercial concepts catch the imagination, but can they be registered as a trademark? Discover how to approach marketing trends in brand-naming strategies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国 Fashion Holding 公司申请的
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) rejected an EU trademark application for the sign 'Quite Luxury' on January 5, 2026. Filed by Fashion Holding Düsseldorf GmbH for fashion and luxury goods, the application was refused due to a lack of distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) of the EUTMR. The Office determined that the relevant public would perceive the phrase as a mere promotional expression reflecting the 'quiet luxury' marketing trend, rather than as an indicator of commercial origin. This decision highlights the significant legal challenges in securing trademark protection for terms deeply embedded in global marketing narratives, even when presented with specific typographical elements.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling underscores the difficulty of monopolizing widely recognized marketing concepts through trademark law. Rights holders must ensure their brand names function primarily as origin indicators, not merely as descriptive slogans or sectoral codes. When adopting trendy phrases, applicants should consider adding highly distinctive visual or verbal elements to secure registrability. Monitoring EUIPO examination practices regarding descriptive terms remains essential for developing robust brand naming strategies that withstand absolute grounds for refusal in the EU market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国 Fashion Holding 公司申请的"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO refused registration of 'Quite Luxury' because it lacks distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) of the EUTMR.", "Examiners viewed the sign as a promotional marketing concept rather than a source identifier for fashion and luxury products.", "Specific typography alone was insufficient to overcome the inherent descriptiveness of the popular industry term."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on popular marketing trends for brand names to prevent refusal based on lack of distinctiveness.", "Practice note: Enhance descriptive terms with unique graphical or verbal components to establish acquired distinctiveness or inherent character.", "What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on absolute grounds for refusal involving descriptive phrases and emerging industry terminology."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO refused registration of 'Quite Luxury' because it lacks distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) of the EUTMR.
Examiners viewed the sign as a promotional marketing concept rather than a source identifier for fashion and luxury products.
Specific typography alone was insufficient to overcome the inherent descriptiveness of the popular industry term.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on popular marketing trends for brand names to prevent refusal based on lack of distinctiveness.
Practice note: Enhance descriptive terms with unique graphical or verbal components to establish acquired distinctiveness or inherent character.
What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on absolute grounds for refusal involving descriptive phrases and emerging industry terminology.

items.title: Adjusting the hurdles: UK Supreme Court backs European test for software inventions | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 英国最高法院支持欧洲测试法以调整软件发明门槛
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Adopts EPO Hardware Test for Software Inventions, Replacing Aerotel
items.summary: On 11 February, the UK Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling in Emotional Perception AI Limited v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks¹ that reshapes UK practice on the assessment of computer-implemented inventions, aligning it with the European Patent Office’s long-standing approach.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在 Emotional Perception AI 案中作出里程碑裁决，摒弃原有的 Aerotel 测试，转而采纳欧洲专利局的“任何硬件”方法来评估计算机实施发明。该判决解决了人工神经网络等混合发明的可专利性争议，将英国实践与欧洲标准对齐，简化了资格审查流程，并可能提升英国作为 AI 和软件专利友好辖区的声誉。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling in Emotional Perception AI Limited v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks. This decision fundamentally reshapes the assessment of computer-implemented inventions by abandoning the long-standing Aerotel test. Instead, the Court adopted the European Patent Office’s established “any hardware” approach. This alignment harmonizes UK practice with European standards, specifically addressing the patentability of mixed inventions such as artificial neural networks. The ruling introduces a clearer intermediate step to determine which features genuinely contribute to technical character. By simplifying eligibility assessments, the decision enhances legal certainty and potentially strengthens the UK’s reputation as a favorable jurisdiction for software and artificial intelligence patents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英申请 AI 及软件专利时，应依据新标准重新评估技术方案的技术特征描述。建议利用英欧标准趋同的机遇，优化在欧洲的专利布局策略，降低因法域差异导致的合规成本与驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This harmonization between UK and European patent practices offers significant strategic opportunities for rights holders managing cross-border portfolios. Applicants should reassess their technical disclosures to emphasize specific hardware interactions that drive technical effects, ensuring robust support under the new framework. Leveraging the convergence of standards allows for more streamlined prosecution strategies across both jurisdictions, potentially lowering compliance costs and reducing rejection risks associated with divergent legal tests. Focusing on concrete technical contributions rather than abstract algorithms will be critical for securing protection for AI-driven innovations in this evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["最高法院确立采用欧专局“任何硬件”方法取代旧有 Aerotel 测试标准", "明确人工神经网络等混合技术特征发明的技术贡献判定中间步骤", "裁决统一英欧软件专利审查实践，增强英国对 AI 创新的吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Supreme Court replaces the traditional Aerotel test with the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach for assessing patent eligibility.", "The ruling clarifies the determination of technical contribution for mixed inventions, including those involving artificial neural networks and hardware combinations.", "This decision aligns UK software patent examination practices with European standards, reducing divergence and enhancing attractiveness for AI innovation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用英欧标准趋同机遇，优化在欧人工智能领域专利布局策略", "重新梳理技术交底书，重点突出硬件结合带来的具体技术贡献"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards to streamline patent prosecution strategies and reduce cross-jurisdictional compliance costs.", "Practice note: Revise technical disclosures to explicitly highlight hardware integration and specific technical contributions arising from AI implementations.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent UKIPO examination guidelines and case law developments applying the new “any hardware” test to diverse software categories."]
core_points_en: The Supreme Court replaces the traditional Aerotel test with the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach for assessing patent eligibility.
The ruling clarifies the determination of technical contribution for mixed inventions, including those involving artificial neural networks and hardware combinations.
This decision aligns UK software patent examination practices with European standards, reducing divergence and enhancing attractiveness for AI innovation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards to streamline patent prosecution strategies and reduce cross-jurisdictional compliance costs.
Practice note: Revise technical disclosures to explicitly highlight hardware integration and specific technical contributions arising from AI implementations.
What to monitor: Track subsequent UKIPO examination guidelines and case law developments applying the new “any hardware” test to diverse software categories.

items.title: Patent Law & Practice at a Glance – February 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 2026 年 2 月专利法实务概览：AI 专利性及多国更新
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court AI Patentability Ruling and Global Practice Updates
items.summary: Read our February 2026 edition of patent law and practice which includes updates from the UK, the EPO and the WIPO/PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文综述 2026 年 2 月专利法律实务，核心聚焦英国最高法院对“情感感知”案中人工神经网络专利性的里程碑判决。同时涵盖欧洲专利局审查实践更新及 WIPO/PCT 体系最新动向，为跨国专利布局提供关键法律指引与实务分析。
items.ai_summary_en: This February 2026 update provides a comprehensive overview of recent developments in patent law and practice across key jurisdictions. The primary focus is the landmark judgment by the UK Supreme Court regarding the patentability of artificial neural networks in the Emotional Perception case. This decision establishes critical standards for assessing technical character in AI inventions within the United Kingdom. Additionally, the analysis covers significant updates to examination practices at the European Patent Office concerning computer-implemented inventions. It also details recent procedural changes and operational insights within the WIPO/PCT international application system. These combined insights offer essential guidance for multinational patent strategies, helping rights holders navigate evolving legal landscapes and optimize their global intellectual property portfolios amidst shifting regulatory frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注英国 AI 专利判例变化，调整神经网络算法在欧洲的布局策略。建议结合 EPO 与 UKIPO 审查差异，优化权利要求撰写，利用 PCT 途径统筹中欧美申请，规避因客体适格性问题导致的驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving judicial interpretation of AI patentability in the UK following the new Supreme Court precedent. Rights holders are advised to align their European patent strategies with the distinct examination standards of both the EPO and the UKIPO. Optimizing claim drafting to address specific jurisdictional requirements regarding technical contribution can mitigate risks of rejection based on subject matter eligibility. Utilizing the PCT pathway allows for coordinated management of applications across China, Europe, and the US, ensuring consistent protection while adapting to local legal nuances and avoiding potential pitfalls associated with abstract algorithmic claims.

items.title: Working worse is no defence: a recent UPC order reinforces feature-based claim scope (UPC_CFI_630/2025)
items.title_zh: 功能实现不佳非抗辩理由：UPC裁定强化基于特征的权利要求范围
items.title_en: Inferior Functionality No Defense: UPC Reinforces Feature-Based Claim Scope in Preliminary Injunctions
items.summary: Court docket: LD Düsseldorf
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在Occultech诉Lepu Medical案中发布临时禁令裁定。法院明确，若被控产品具备权利要求的所有结构特征，即使未完全实现预期技术优势，仍落入保护范围。裁定重申说明书和附图用于理解技术语境，且临时禁令程序中被告承担专利无效举证责任，需详细论证现有技术译文准确性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled in preliminary injunction proceedings that a device claim’s scope covers embodiments possessing all claimed structural features, even if they do not fully realize the technical advantages described in the patent specification. The Court emphasized that claim construction must be interpreted in light of the description and drawings, rather than isolating claim features. Furthermore, the decision clarifies procedural burdens: applicants must promptly respond to contested facts with sufficient certainty for provisional measures, as summary proceedings allow limited fact-finding. Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed reasoning for any disputed prior art translations; failure to do so may result in the Court adopting the applicant’s translation version.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定明确了UPC临时禁令中“结构特征优先”的解释原则及被告的严格举证义务。实务中，被告仅主张技术效果差异难以规避侵权，必须针对无效证据提供详尽的译文比对与法律论证，否则法院将采信原告译文并可能颁发禁令。
items.ai_insight_en: In UPC preliminary injunction proceedings, relying solely on arguments regarding inferior product functionality is unlikely to succeed against a patentee asserting structural infringement. Rights holders should focus litigation strategies on demonstrating the presence of all claimed structural features, supported by the specification. Conversely, alleged infringers challenging validity must prepare high-quality translations of prior art documents accompanied by rigorous legal and technical arguments. Insufficient substantiation of translation disputes or invalidity grounds carries significant risk of unfavorable presumptions, given the limited scope for further evidence gathering in expedited procedures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["装置权利要求范围涵盖具备所有结构特征的实施例，即便其未完全实现预期功能或技术优势", "临时禁令程序中，被告承担专利无效举证责任；若争议现有技术译文，被告须详述己方译文正确性", "权利要求解释需结合说明书和附图理解技术语境，该原则同时适用于侵权与有效性评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.", "Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.", "Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC临时禁令程序对被告举证要求极高，需预先准备详尽的现有技术译文对比分析", "权利要求解释回归结构特征本位，单纯强调被控产品技术劣势或功能缺失不再是有效抗辩", "需持续关注UPC上诉法院是否维持此类在简易程序中严格限制事实查明的裁判倾向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases."]
core_points_en: Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.
Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.
Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases.

items.title: Key Decisions of the UPC Court of Appeal 2023-2025: Volume Two - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院2023-2025关键裁决第二卷
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Key Decisions Volume Two: Procedural Rulings 2023-2025
items.summary: Since the UPC’s opening in June 2023, the Court has developed a growing body of case law across key areas. Our first volume examined substantive Court of Appeal decisions on infringement and revocation, applications for preliminary injunctions, and two notable appellate rulings on the scope of the UPC’s jurisdiction. Volume 1 is available via our […]
items.ai_summary_zh: Carpmaels & Ransford发布报告，总结统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院自2023年6月成立至2025年底的关键程序性裁决。内容涵盖证据保全、中止效力、保密性、费用、代理人资格、语言变更及诉讼中止等议题。该报告旨在阐明法院对《程序规则》的解释与适用，为当事人提供应对UPC新兴实践的程序指引，是继第一卷实体法分析后的重要补充。
items.ai_summary_en: Carpmaels & Ransford has released the second volume of its analysis on key decisions from the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal, covering the period from the court's inception in June 2023 through the end of 2025. This report focuses specifically on significant procedural rulings, complementing the first volume which addressed substantive issues such as infringement and revocation. The analysis highlights influential appellate decisions regarding evidence preservation, suspensive effect, confidentiality obligations, cost allocation, representative qualifications, language changes, and stays of proceedings. By clarifying how the Court interprets and applies critical provisions within the Rules of Procedure, this resource provides essential guidance for navigating the UPC’s evolving practice. It serves as a vital reference for understanding the procedural framework that governs litigation before the new European patent court system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注UPC程序规则动态，特别是证据保全与费用分担机制。建议提前规划跨国诉讼策略，利用专业团队协调EPO与UPC平行程序，以优化成本并规避程序风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor developments in UPC procedural rules, particularly concerning evidence preservation protocols and cost-sharing mechanisms. Early strategic planning for cross-border disputes is essential to mitigate procedural risks. Firms with integrated capabilities can effectively coordinate parallel proceedings before the EPO and the UPC, optimizing resource allocation. This approach helps manage litigation costs while ensuring consistent legal strategies across jurisdictions. Understanding these procedural nuances allows companies to navigate the new system with greater confidence and efficiency.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["汇总UPC上诉法院2023-2025年关于证据保全、保密性等关键程序问题的裁决", "澄清法院对《程序规则》的解释，明确诉讼中止、语言变更等操作流程", "作为实务指南，帮助当事人适应UPC不断演变的司法实践与程序要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Summarizes pivotal UPC Court of Appeal procedural rulings from 2023 to 2025, focusing on evidence preservation and confidentiality standards.", "Clarifies judicial interpretation of the Rules of Procedure regarding stays of proceedings and procedural language change mechanisms.", "Provides practical guidance for market participants adapting to the UPC’s developing case law and operational requirements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC程序裁决，优化证据保全与诉讼成本控制策略", "协调EPO与UPC平行程序，提升跨国知识产权维权效率"]

Clarifies judicial interpretation of the Rules of Procedure regarding stays of proceedings and procedural language change mechanisms.

items.title: Différents angles de vue : JUB et Haute Cour de Justice anglaise
items.title_zh: 视角差异：UPC与英国高等法院在ACD诉MI案中的不同裁决
items.title_en: Divergent Perspectives: UPC and English High Court Rulings in ACD v. Molecular Instruments
items.summary: Des approches et des conclusions différentes dans l’affaire Advanced Cell Diagnostics vs. Molecular Instruments  1.1 Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) a assigné Molecular Instruments (MI) pour contrefaçon d’un de ses brevets européens aussi bien devant la Juridiction Unifiée du Brevet (JUB – Chambre Locale de La Haye) que devant la Haute Cour de Justice (suite) L’article Différents angles de vue : JUB et Haute Cour de Justice anglaise est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析了Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) 诉 Molecular Instruments (MI) 侵犯欧洲专利一案。原告同时在统一专利法院（UPC）海牙地方分院和英国高等法院提起诉讼。文章指出，尽管基于同一专利和事实，两个司法管辖区的法院在处理方法、法律适用及最终结论上存在显著差异。这突显了在欧洲进行平行诉讼时，不同法庭可能带来的不可预测性和策略复杂性，强调了理解各法院程序特点的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) versus Molecular Instruments (MI) case concerning alleged infringement of a European patent. The plaintiff initiated parallel proceedings simultaneously before the Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in The Hague and the English High Court. Although both courts adjudicated the same patent and factual matrix, their approaches diverged significantly. The article highlights substantial differences in procedural handling, legal application, and final conclusions reached by each jurisdiction. This case underscores the inherent unpredictability and strategic complexity associated with conducting parallel litigation across Europe. It emphasizes the critical importance for rights holders and defendants to thoroughly understand the distinct procedural characteristics and judicial tendencies of each court when formulating cross-border enforcement or defense strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，需警惕UPC与传统国家法院（如英国）的程序差异。建议制定诉讼策略时，综合评估各法域的举证责任、赔偿标准及禁令倾向。利用平行诉讼的时间差和裁判差异，可争取更有利的和解条件或市场空间，避免单一依赖某国司法体系。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants navigating European patent disputes must carefully assess procedural disparities between the UPC and national courts like the English High Court. Strategic planning should integrate evaluations of differing burdens of proof, compensation standards, and injunction tendencies across jurisdictions. Leveraging timing gaps and divergent rulings from parallel proceedings can create opportunities to secure more favorable settlement terms or preserve market position. Reliance on a single judicial system may limit strategic options; therefore, a coordinated multi-forum approach is often necessary to optimize litigation results and manage risk effectively in complex cross-border IP conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["ACD就同一欧洲专利分别在UPC海牙分院和英国高院起诉MI侵权", "两法院对相同案件采取不同审理路径并得出相异结论", "案例凸显欧洲平行诉讼中司法管辖权选择对结果的关键影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["ACD filed parallel infringement actions against MI regarding the same European patent in both the UPC Hague Local Division and the English High Court.", "Despite identical facts and patents, the two courts adopted different procedural paths and reached materially different legal conclusions.", "The case illustrates how jurisdiction selection critically influences outcomes in European parallel litigation scenarios."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估UPC与国家法院程序差异以优化欧洲诉讼策略", "利用平行诉讼裁判差异争取有利和解或市场空间"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assessing procedural differences between the UPC and national courts helps optimize overall European litigation strategy.", "Practice note: Utilizing divergent rulings in parallel proceedings can help secure advantageous settlements or protect market space.", "What to monitor: Track evolving judicial tendencies regarding evidence, damages, and injunctive relief across key European forums."]
core_points_en: ACD filed parallel infringement actions against MI regarding the same European patent in both the UPC Hague Local Division and the English High Court.
Despite identical facts and patents, the two courts adopted different procedural paths and reached materially different legal conclusions.
The case illustrates how jurisdiction selection critically influences outcomes in European parallel litigation scenarios.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assessing procedural differences between the UPC and national courts helps optimize overall European litigation strategy.
Practice note: Utilizing divergent rulings in parallel proceedings can help secure advantageous settlements or protect market space.
What to monitor: Track evolving judicial tendencies regarding evidence, damages, and injunctive relief across key European forums.

items.title: A new sense of self: Denmark's copyright amendment against deepfakes
items.title_zh: 丹麦拟修版权法：禁止未经许可的深度伪造
items.title_en: Denmark Proposes Copyright Act Amendments to Regulate Unauthorized Deepfakes
items.summary: The explosion in the use of generative artificial intelligence has heightened concern about deepfakes. How can Intellectual Property (IP) legislation address the risks?
items.ai_summary_zh: 生成式 AI 引发的深度伪造风险加剧，丹麦政府于 2025 年 6 月提出版权法修正案。该法案新增条款，禁止未经表演者或自然人同意，向公众提供其逼真的数字模仿形象（含外貌与声音）。保护期延续至死后五十年，旨在应对虚假信息对民主及个人权益的严重威胁。
items.ai_summary_en: Amid growing concerns regarding generative AI and deepfake risks, the Danish government proposed amendments to the Copyright Act in June 2025. These legislative changes introduce new safeguards against unauthorized digital imitations of individuals and artists' performances. The bill explicitly prohibits making realistic digital replicas of a person’s appearance or voice available to the public without their consent. This protection extends for fifty years after death, aiming to safeguard personal rights and performers' interests. With cross-party support, the reforms are expected to enter into force later this year. The initiative addresses the severe threats posed by misinformation and non-consensual synthetic media, seeking to prevent potential democratic disruptions and protect individual dignity against the rapid proliferation of convincing AI-generated content.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕丹麦新规，使用 AI 生成涉及真人形象内容时务必获取明确授权。出口企业应审查营销素材，避免因深度伪造技术侵犯当地人格权与表演者权而面临法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing generative AI tools must ensure strict compliance with Denmark's evolving copyright framework. When creating content involving realistic digital representations of real people, obtaining explicit prior authorization is essential to avoid infringement claims. Companies should proactively review marketing materials and AI workflows to mitigate legal risks associated with personality rights and performers' rights violations. As this legislation sets a precedent in Europe, monitoring similar regulatory developments in other jurisdictions is advisable to maintain global compliance standards and protect brand reputation from potential liabilities arising from unauthorized deepfake usage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["丹麦拟增版权法新条，禁未授权逼真数字模仿", "保护范围涵盖表演者及自然人的外貌与声音特征", "权利保护期为生前加死后五十年，获跨党派支持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New provisions prohibit unauthorized public distribution of realistic digital imitations of natural persons and performers.", "Protected scope covers both visual likeness and vocal characteristics, ensuring comprehensive rights against deepfake misuse.", "Protection term lasts for the individual's lifetime plus fifty years post-mortem, with strong cross-party legislative backing."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["涉欧业务使用 AI 生成真人形象须获明确授权", "审查营销素材合规性，防范深度伪造侵权风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Obtain explicit authorization before using AI to generate realistic digital images or voices of identifiable individuals.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough compliance reviews of marketing assets to prevent infringement of personality and performers' rights.", "What to monitor: Track the final enactment timeline and potential expansion of similar anti-deepfake regulations across other European markets."]
core_points_en: New provisions prohibit unauthorized public distribution of realistic digital imitations of natural persons and performers.
Protected scope covers both visual likeness and vocal characteristics, ensuring comprehensive rights against deepfake misuse.
Protection term lasts for the individual's lifetime plus fifty years post-mortem, with strong cross-party legislative backing.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Obtain explicit authorization before using AI to generate realistic digital images or voices of identifiable individuals.
Practice note: Conduct thorough compliance reviews of marketing assets to prevent infringement of personality and performers' rights.
What to monitor: Track the final enactment timeline and potential expansion of similar anti-deepfake regulations across other European markets.

items.title: Client-attorney privilege in patent matters: Scope, limitations and best practices
items.title_zh: 专利事务中客户 - 律师特权的范围、限制及最佳实践
items.title_en: Client-Attorney Privilege in Patent Matters: Scope, Limitations and Best Practices
items.summary: Understanding the legal and professional concept of client-attorney privilege is vital for navigating the discovery process during international patent disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着国际专利纠纷增多，欧洲企业常面临英美法系国家的证据开示程序。文章辨析了保密性、职业秘密与法律特权的区别，指出仅特权可豁免文件披露。重点探讨了欧洲专利律师与客户沟通在跨境诉讼中享有特权的局限性与认定条件，为企业应对发现程序提供指导。
items.ai_summary_en: As international patent disputes increase, rights holders frequently face discovery proceedings in common law jurisdictions like the United States. This analysis clarifies critical distinctions between confidentiality, professional secrecy, and legal privilege. While contractual confidentiality limits information dissemination, it cannot override court orders for document production. Similarly, professional secrecy imposes ethical duties on practitioners but does not automatically shield communications from disclosure in foreign litigation. Only established legal privilege offers procedural protection against mandatory discovery. The article examines the specific limitations and recognition conditions for privilege regarding communications between clients and European patent attorneys, providing essential guidance for navigating cross-border evidence disclosure risks effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇跨国诉讼时，需警惕内部文件被强制开示风险。建议提前区分沟通性质，利用欧盟境内律师特权规则构建防火墙，避免敏感信息在发现程序中泄露，必要时寻求当地法律顾问介入以确立特权保护。
items.ai_insight_en: In cross-border patent disputes, particularly those involving US-style discovery, parties must proactively manage privilege risks rather than relying solely on general confidentiality measures. Communications should ideally be routed through qualified EU lawyers to maximize the likelihood of privilege protection under local laws. Companies should clearly distinguish between technical discussions and legal advice to ensure sensitive information remains protected. Engaging local counsel early is advisable to establish robust privilege frameworks. This strategic approach helps prevent inadvertent waiver of protections and minimizes exposure to mandatory disclosure of confidential internal materials during international enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["保密承诺无法对抗法院强制披露命令，仅法律特权有效", "职业秘密是伦理义务，不必然保障跨境诉讼中的文件豁免权", "特权认定取决于管辖地规则，欧美对专利律师特权界定存在差异"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Contractual confidentiality commitments cannot successfully resist judicial orders compelling the production of internal documents during litigation.", "Professional secrecy constitutes an ethical obligation for practitioners but does not guarantee document protection in cross-border legal proceedings.", "Privilege recognition depends strictly on applicable jurisdictional rules, with significant differences existing between European and US approaches to patent attorney communicatio…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["厘清保密、职业秘密与特权差异，避免错误依赖合同保密条款", "跨境诉讼中应优先通过欧盟执业律师沟通，以争取特权保护覆盖"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Distinguish clearly between confidentiality, professional secrecy, and legal privilege to avoid erroneous reliance on contractual clauses that fail in court.", "Practice note: Prioritize communications via EU-qualified lawyers in cross-border disputes to secure broader privilege coverage and protect sensitive internal documents.", "What to monitor: Evolving jurisdictional standards regarding patent attorney privilege, particularly how US courts treat communications involving non-lawyer European IP profession…"]
core_points_en: Contractual confidentiality commitments cannot successfully resist judicial orders compelling the production of internal documents during litigation.
Professional secrecy constitutes an ethical obligation for practitioners but does not guarantee document protection in cross-border legal proceedings.
Privilege recognition depends strictly on applicable jurisdictional rules, with significant differences existing between European and US approaches to patent attorney communicatio…

Practice note: Prioritize communications via EU-qualified lawyers in cross-border disputes to secure broader privilege coverage and protect sensitive internal documents.
What to monitor: Evolving jurisdictional standards regarding patent attorney privilege, particularly how US courts treat communications involving non-lawyer European IP profession…

items.title: Can I appeal an EPO opposition decision? | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 能否对欧洲专利局异议决定提起上诉？
items.title_en: Can I appeal an EPO opposition decision? | Potter Clarkson
items.summary: A decision of the EPO Opposition Division (OD) can be appealed, but only by a party who took part in the opposition proceedings and is adversely affected by the decision.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析欧洲专利局（EPO）异议决定的上诉机制。依据《欧洲专利公约》第 107 条，仅受决定不利影响的参与方（如专利权人、异议人或有效介入人）可提起上诉。上诉人须在决定通知后两个月内提交通知书并缴费，四个月内提交理由书。上诉委员会将进行独立司法审查，结果包括维持、撤销原判或发回重审。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利异议败诉时，应严格监控两个月上诉期限，避免因程序瑕疵丧失救济权。建议提前评估“不利影响”要件，精准准备上诉理由，利用独立审查机制争取逆转局势或发回重审机会。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅受决定不利影响的程序参与方具备上诉资格", "须在两个月内提交通知书缴费，四月内提交理由", "上诉委员会可维持、撤销决定或将案件发回重审"]

items.title: EPO opposition vs appeal | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局异议程序与上诉程序的区别解析
items.title_en: EPO Opposition Versus Appeal: Distinguishing Administrative Review From Judicial Scrutiny
items.summary: An EPO opposition and an EPO appeal are distinct procedures, each with its own character, timetable, evidential rules, and decision-making body.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详述欧洲专利局（EPO）异议与上诉程序的本质区别。异议是一审行政重审，由异议部全面审查事实并接纳新证据；上诉则是司法复核，由上诉委员会严格审查原审是否犯错，对提交新证据限制极严。两者在时限、证据规则及决策机构上截然不同。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis clarifies the fundamental distinctions between European Patent Office opposition and appeal procedures. Opposition functions as a first-instance administrative re-examination, where the Opposition Division conducts comprehensive fact-finding and admits new evidence. Conversely, appeals serve as judicial reviews focused on identifying errors in the initial decision, governed by strict admissibility rules that severely limit new submissions. The two processes differ significantly regarding timelines, evidentiary standards, and decision-making bodies. Understanding these procedural differences is essential for rights holders and opponents to navigate the EPO system effectively, ensuring appropriate strategies are employed at each stage of patent validity challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权需精准把握程序窗口：异议期是补充关键证据的最佳时机；一旦进入上诉，策略应转为攻击原审程序或法律适用错误，切忌盲目提交新证据被驳回。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, distinguishing between these stages is critical for effective IP strategy. The nine-month opposition period offers the optimal window for submitting key evidence and amending claims to address validity concerns comprehensively. Once proceedings move to appeal, the focus must shift from introducing new technical arguments to challenging legal interpretations or procedural flaws in the prior decision. Relying on late-filed evidence during appeals often leads to rejection due to stringent admissibility criteria. Rights holders should therefore prioritize robust preparation during the opposition phase rather than expecting to rectify gaps later.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议属行政重审，允许广泛举证与权利要求修改", "上诉为司法复核，聚焦原审错误且严控新证据准入", "两程序时限不同，上诉需在决定通知后两月内提起"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Opposition is an administrative first-instance process allowing broad fact-finding and admission of new evidence during the nine-month window.", "Appeals constitute judicial review focusing on legal or procedural errors in the Opposition Division's decision, with strict limits on new facts.", "Procedural timelines differ markedly, requiring appeals to be filed within two months of notification, contrasting with the longer opposition period."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用异议九个月窗口期充分举证，避免遗留隐患", "上诉阶段应聚焦法律纠错，严禁依赖突击提交新证"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the nine-month opposition window to submit comprehensive evidence and amendments, minimizing risks before final decisions.", "Practice note: During appeals, concentrate arguments on legal errors or procedural violations rather than attempting to introduce new factual evidence.", "What to monitor: Strict adherence to appeal filing deadlines, specifically the two-month notice period following notification of the contested decision."]
core_points_en: Opposition is an administrative first-instance process allowing broad fact-finding and admission of new evidence during the nine-month window.
Appeals constitute judicial review focusing on legal or procedural errors in the Opposition Division's decision, with strict limits on new facts.
Procedural timelines differ markedly, requiring appeals to be filed within two months of notification, contrasting with the longer opposition period.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the nine-month opposition window to submit comprehensive evidence and amendments, minimizing risks before final decisions.
Practice note: During appeals, concentrate arguments on legal errors or procedural violations rather than attempting to introduce new factual evidence.
What to monitor: Strict adherence to appeal filing deadlines, specifically the two-month notice period following notification of the contested decision.

items.title: Supreme Court decision: a game-changer for software inventions in the UK - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 英最高法院裁决：软件发明可专利性迎来重大变革
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Adopts EPO Hardware Standard, Lowering Bar for Software Patent Eligibility
items.summary: So it is finally here, the decision from the UK Supreme Court in the long-running saga starring Emotional Perception AI and the Comptroller-General of Patents. The stage was set to address the fundamentals of the UK’s approach for assessing statutory exclusions to patentability, including for software and AI. Has the decision rightly earned its place in the spotlight? With the last lines delivered, here are the must‑know consequences for innovators.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在 Emotional Perception AI 案中作出里程碑裁决，废除沿用二十年的 Aerotel 测试，转而采纳欧洲专利局“任何硬件”标准。只要权利要求包含硬件特征即不属排除范畴，显著降低门槛。同时引入中间步骤评估技术贡献，但保留本国创造性判断方法，交由 UKIPO 细化执行。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has issued a landmark ruling in the Emotional Perception AI case, fundamentally altering the landscape for software and AI patentability. The Court abolished the twenty-year-old Aerotel test, replacing it with the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach established in decision G 1/19. Consequently, claims containing at least one hardware feature are no longer excluded from patentability under Section 1(2) of the Patents Act 1977, significantly lowering the eligibility threshold. While adopting the EPO’s method for assessing technical contributions via an intermediate step, the Court retained the UK’s traditional Pozzoli framework for inventive step. This hybrid approach requires rights holders to navigate new procedural requirements while awaiting further implementation guidelines from the UK Intellectual Property Office.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 与软件企业在英获权难度大幅降低，应重新评估被驳申请。需关注 UKIPO 后续细则，调整权利要求撰写策略，利用新规则布局高价值专利组合。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision substantially reduces barriers for securing patent protection for software and AI innovations in the UK. Rights holders should review previously refused applications to identify candidates for revival or appeal under the new eligibility criteria. Drafting strategies must now prioritize explicit hardware features to satisfy the 'any hardware' requirement while ensuring claims demonstrate clear technical contributions beyond mere statutory exclusions. Practitioners should closely monitor upcoming UKIPO practice notices to refine claim structures and leverage these changes for building robust, high-value patent portfolios in the UK market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["废除 Aerotel 测试，全面接轨欧专局“任何硬件”可专利性标准", "确立中间审查步骤，评估排除特征对整体技术方案的实际贡献", "维持 Pozzoli 创造性判断法，具体实施细节待 UKIPO 进一步明确"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Abolition of the Aerotel test aligns UK software patent eligibility with the EPO's 'any hardware' standard.", "Introduction of an intermediate assessment step to evaluate how excluded features contribute technically to the invention.", "Retention of the domestic Pozzoli approach for inventive step, pending further clarification from the UKIPO."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借新规重启在英国被拒的软件及 AI 专利申请，挖掘潜在资产", "优化权利要求架构，确保包含硬件特征并突显整体技术贡献"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Re-evaluate previously rejected software and AI applications for potential revival under the lowered eligibility threshold.", "Practice note: Optimize claim architecture to include hardware features and highlight overall technical contributions to the invention.", "What to monitor: Await detailed implementation guidelines from the UKIPO regarding the new intermediate assessment step."]
core_points_en: Abolition of the Aerotel test aligns UK software patent eligibility with the EPO's 'any hardware' standard.
Introduction of an intermediate assessment step to evaluate how excluded features contribute technically to the invention.
Retention of the domestic Pozzoli approach for inventive step, pending further clarification from the UKIPO.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Re-evaluate previously rejected software and AI applications for potential revival under the lowered eligibility threshold.
Practice note: Optimize claim architecture to include hardware features and highlight overall technical contributions to the invention.
What to monitor: Await detailed implementation guidelines from the UKIPO regarding the new intermediate assessment step.

items.title: EU Provisionally Approves Framework For New Genomic Technique Plants From 2028 – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 欧盟临时批准 2028 年新基因组技术植物监管框架
items.title_en: EU Provisionally Approves New Genomic Technique Plant Framework Effective 2028
items.summary: EU member states and the European Parliament have provisionally agreed a new regulatory framework for plants produced by new genomic techniques (NGTs). The
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟成员国与议会就新基因组技术（NGT）植物监管达成临时协议，确立双轨制市场准入：NGT1 适用简化验证，NGT2 沿用转基因规则。框架特别规定在满足透明度要求下保留 NGT 植物可专利性，为生物育种创新提供法律确定性，预计 2028 年全面实施。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union has reached a provisional agreement on a new regulatory framework for plants developed using new genomic techniques (NGTs). This legislation establishes a dual-track market access system, distinguishing between NGT1 plants, which undergo a simplified verification process similar to conventional breeding, and NGT2 plants, which remain subject to strict GMO regulations. Crucially, the framework preserves the patentability of NGT-derived plants, provided that specific transparency requirements regarding genetic modifications are met. This development offers significant legal certainty for the biotechnology sector, aiming to foster innovation while ensuring safety and traceability. The rules are scheduled for full implementation by 2028, fundamentally reshaping the landscape for agricultural biotechnology and intellectual property strategies within the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国农企应关注 NGT1 分类标准以利用简化通道进入欧洲市场。同时需调整专利布局策略，确保研发数据满足新规透明度要求，避免因合规瑕疵丧失专利权或面临市场准入障碍。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely analyze the classification criteria for NGT1 versus NGT2 categories to optimize product registration strategies and minimize compliance costs. Companies must also adapt their intellectual property portfolios by ensuring that research data and documentation strictly adhere to the new transparency mandates. Failure to maintain precise records may jeopardize patent rights or create barriers to market entry. Proactive alignment with these evolving standards will be essential for securing competitive advantages and maintaining robust IP protection in the European Union's emerging bio-economy sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["确立 NGT1 简化验证与 NGT2 严格管控的双轨市场准入机制", "明确符合透明度要求的新基因组技术植物仍具备可专利性", "新规预计于 2028 年正式实施，重塑欧洲农业生物技术格局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Establishes a dual-track market access mechanism with simplified verification for NGT1 plants and strict GMO controls for NGT2 varieties.", "Confirms that plants derived from new genomic techniques remain patentable if applicants meet mandatory transparency and disclosure obligations.", "The new regulatory regime is expected to enter into force in 2028, significantly altering the European agricultural biotechnology landscape."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["依据 NGT1/2 分类优化产品注册策略以降低合规成本", "完善研发记录以满足专利申请的透明度强制性要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Optimize product registration strategies based on NGT1/NGT2 classifications to reduce regulatory burdens and accelerate market access.", "Practice note: Strengthen internal R&D record-keeping systems to satisfy mandatory transparency requirements for patent applications involving genomic techniques.", "What to monitor: Track final legislative texts and implementing acts defining technical standards for transparency and verification processes."]
core_points_en: Establishes a dual-track market access mechanism with simplified verification for NGT1 plants and strict GMO controls for NGT2 varieties.
Confirms that plants derived from new genomic techniques remain patentable if applicants meet mandatory transparency and disclosure obligations.
The new regulatory regime is expected to enter into force in 2028, significantly altering the European agricultural biotechnology landscape.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Optimize product registration strategies based on NGT1/NGT2 classifications to reduce regulatory burdens and accelerate market access.
Practice note: Strengthen internal R&D record-keeping systems to satisfy mandatory transparency requirements for patent applications involving genomic techniques.
What to monitor: Track final legislative texts and implementing acts defining technical standards for transparency and verification processes.

items.title: An end to an emotional journey: The UK Supreme Court concludes the Emotional Perception saga - GJE
items.title_zh: 英国最高法院终结 Emotional Perception 专利案 saga
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Concludes Emotional Perception Case, Clarifying AI Patent Eligibility Standards
items.summary: Today, the UK Supreme Court concludes the Emotional Perception saga.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院今日对 Emotional Perception AI 有限公司诉专利局长一案作出终审判决，结束了这场长期的法律拉锯战。该案核心争议在于涉及人工智能神经网络的发明是否具备专利适格性。法院的裁决将明确英国在 AI 技术专利审查上的最终立场，为相关领域的创新和权利保护提供确定的法律框架。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has delivered its final judgment in the long-standing dispute between Emotional Perception AI Ltd and the Comptroller-General of Patents. This landmark decision resolves years of litigation concerning whether inventions utilizing artificial intelligence neural networks constitute patentable subject matter under UK law. The ruling provides critical clarity on the boundary between excluded computer programs and technical innovations involving AI. By establishing a definitive legal framework, the judgment significantly impacts how AI-related inventions are assessed for patent eligibility. This outcome offers necessary certainty for innovators and rights holders operating within the UK jurisdiction, shaping future strategies for protecting AI-driven technological advancements and ensuring alignment with updated judicial interpretations of patentability requirements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 企业在英布局需关注此判例对专利授权标准的影响。建议重新评估存量专利申请策略，针对涉及神经网络的发明调整权利要求撰写方式，以符合英国最新司法实践，规避被驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment fundamentally reshapes the landscape for AI patent protection in the UK. Rights holders should carefully review existing patent portfolios and pending applications involving neural networks to ensure compliance with the new eligibility criteria. Strategic adjustments to claim drafting may be necessary to emphasize technical contributions over pure algorithmic processes. Monitoring subsequent UKIPO examination guidelines will be essential to understand practical implementation. Companies must adapt their IP strategies to mitigate rejection risks while maximizing protection for AI innovations under this clarified legal framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院作出终审判决，终结长期诉讼", "案件聚焦人工智能神经网络发明的专利适格性", "裁决确立英国 AI 技术专利审查的最终法律标准"]
